\section*{Game Theory Basics}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{博弈论基础}
\end{tcolorbox}

Game theory is the science of interaction. This textbook, derived from courses taught by the author and developed over several years, is a comprehensive, straightforward introduction to the mathematics of non-cooperative games. It teaches what every game theorist should know: the important ideas and results on strategies, game trees, utility theory, imperfect information, and Nash equilibrium. The proofs of such results - in particular, existence of an equilibrium via fixed points and an elegant direct proof of the minimax theorem for zero-sum games - are presented in a self-contained, accessible way. Complementary to that are chapters on combinatorial games such as Go, and - as introductions to algorithmic game theory - traffic games and the geometry of two-player games. This detailed and lively text requires minimal mathematical background and includes many examples, exercises, and pictures. It is suitable for self-study or introductory courses in mathematics, computer science, or economics departments.

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈论是研究交互行为的科学。这本教材源自作者多年授课的课程内容，是对非合作博弈数学理论的全面且直接的介绍。它涵盖了每个博弈论学者都应了解的内容:关于策略、博弈树(game tree)、效用理论、不完全信息和纳什均衡(Nash equilibrium)的重要概念与成果。这些成果的证明——特别是通过不动点证明均衡(equilibrium)的存在性，以及对零和博弈(zero-sum game)极小极大定理(minimax theorem)的简洁直接证明——都以自成体系且易于理解的方式呈现。与之互补(complementary)的是关于组合博弈(如围棋)的章节，以及作为算法博弈论入门的交通博弈和双人博弈几何的内容。这本详细且生动的教材所需的数学背景极少，包含大量示例、练习和插图。它适合自学，也适用于数学、计算机科学或经济学专业的入门课程。
\end{tcolorbox}

Bernhard von Stengel, educated in Germany and the US, is a mathematical game theorist at the London School of Economics and Political Science, and an authority on computational and geometric methods for solving games. He chaired the 2016 World Congress of the Game Theory Society, and is Co-Editor of the International Journal of Game Theory.

\begin{tcolorbox}
伯恩哈德·冯·施滕格尔(Bernhard von Stengel)在德国和美国接受教育，是伦敦政治经济学院的数学博弈论学者，也是解决博弈问题的计算和几何方法方面的权威。他担任了2016年博弈论学会世界大会的主席，还是《国际博弈论杂志》的联合主编。
\end{tcolorbox}

"This looks like a fine introduction to game theory, inter alia emphasizing methods for computing equilibria, and mathematical aspects in general. Especially worthy of note is the chapter devoted to correlated equilibria, a topic of central importance not normally covered in introductory texts."

\begin{tcolorbox}
“这似乎是一本优秀的博弈论入门教材，尤其强调了计算均衡的方法以及一般的数学方面。特别值得注意的是关于相关均衡的章节，这一重要主题通常不在入门教材中涉及。”
\end{tcolorbox}

Robert Aumann, The Hebrew University of Jerusalem,

\begin{tcolorbox}
罗伯特·奥曼(Robert Aumann)，耶路撒冷希伯来大学
\end{tcolorbox}

Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences 2005

\begin{tcolorbox}
2005年诺贝尔经济学奖
\end{tcolorbox}

"This book is a delightful adventure into the mathematics of game theory. Without any heavy apparatus, it lets us into the secrets of a whole range of exciting results that are usually thought too advanced for the common herd. It is not only undergraduate students who will benefit from reading this book. Professional game theorists will find it very useful too."

\begin{tcolorbox}
“这本书是一场对博弈论数学的精彩探索。无需复杂的工具，它就让我们了解到一系列令人兴奋的成果背后的奥秘，这些成果通常被认为对普通读者来说过于高深。不仅本科生会从阅读本书中受益，专业的博弈论学者也会发现它非常有用。”
\end{tcolorbox}

Ken Binmore, University College London

\begin{tcolorbox}
肯·宾默尔(Ken Binmore)，伦敦大学学院
\end{tcolorbox}

"This is a rather reader-friendly, engaging, and polished superior creation. It illustrates, explains, motivates every definition, theorem, proof. Interesting and unique choice of topics, such as a delightful introductory chapter on combinatorial games. Highly recommended."

\begin{tcolorbox}
“这是一部非常便于阅读、引人入胜且精心打磨的佳作。它对每个定义、定理和证明都进行了说明、解释和动机阐述。主题选择有趣且独特，比如关于组合博弈的精彩入门章节。强烈推荐。”
\end{tcolorbox}

Aviezri Fraenkel, Weizmann Institute of Science, Israel

\begin{tcolorbox}
阿维兹里·弗兰克尔(Aviezri Fraenkel)，以色列魏茨曼科学研究所
\end{tcolorbox}

"A masterful presentation of mathematical game theory in all its beauty and elegance, from basic notions to advanced techniques. It fills the gaps left by the many textbooks that cover concepts and applications, but devote only the bare minimum to the mathematical tools and insights, without which game theory would not have become the success it is today."

\begin{tcolorbox}
“对数学博弈论进行了精彩而优雅的呈现，从基本概念到高级技术一应俱全。它填补了许多只涵盖概念和应用，却对数学工具和见解仅作最低限度介绍的教材的空白，没有这些数学工具和见解，博弈论就不会取得如今的成功。”
\end{tcolorbox}

Sergiu Hart, The Hebrew University of Jerusalem

\begin{tcolorbox}
塞尔久·哈特(Sergiu Hart)，耶路撒冷希伯来大学
\end{tcolorbox}

"Game theory is the child of mathematicians, as this textbook demonstrates through self-contained, elegant proofs of all seminal theorems. The lively and rigorous exposition of carefully selected models, such as bargaining, combinatorial, and congestion games (the latter two rarely the stuff of textbooks), explains its success far beyond mathematics. To reach deep results on both sides of the theory, Bernhard von Sten-gel's marvellous learning tool uses uncompromising, yet accessible mathematics, and chooses examples to maximal effect."

\begin{tcolorbox}
“博弈论是数学家的结晶，正如这本教材通过对所有开创性定理自成体系且优雅的证明所展示的那样。对精心挑选的模型(如讨价还价博弈、组合博弈和拥塞博弈(congestion game)，后两者很少出现在教材中)进行生动而严谨的阐述，解释了博弈论在数学之外的广泛成功。为了深入探究该理论的各个方面，伯恩哈德·冯·施滕格尔的这部出色的学习工具采用了严谨但易于理解的数学方法，并精心选择示例以达到最佳效果。”
\end{tcolorbox}

Hervé Moulin, University of Glasgow

\begin{tcolorbox}
埃尔韦·莫林(Hervé Moulin)，格拉斯哥大学
\end{tcolorbox}

"This book is a gem. The presentation is clear and well structured, often with nice geometric illustrations. It moves step by step from basics to powerful concepts, methods, and results. It is ideal for students of mathematics, computer science, and economics who are curious about what game theory is and how it can be used." Jörgen Weibull, Stockholm School of Economics

\begin{tcolorbox}
“这本书是一颗宝石。内容呈现清晰、结构合理，常有精美的几何插图。它循序渐进地从基础内容过渡到强大的概念、方法和成果。对于对博弈论是什么以及如何应用感兴趣的数学、计算机科学和经济学专业的学生来说，它是理想之选。” 约根·魏布尔(Jörgen Weibull)，斯德哥尔摩经济学院
\end{tcolorbox}

"An exceptionally lucid introduction to the fundamentals of game theory, enlivened by examples that are sure to captivate students."

\begin{tcolorbox}
“对博弈论基础进行了异常清晰的介绍，大量示例定会吸引学生的兴趣。”
\end{tcolorbox}

Peyton Young, University of Oxford

\begin{tcolorbox}
佩顿·扬，牛津大学
\end{tcolorbox}

"This is a rigorous, yet accessible introduction to mathematical non-cooperative game theory. In addition to the coverage of the basic concepts and results, it includes special and advanced topics and applications usually not contained in game theory textbooks, such as combinatorial games, congestion games, and inspection games. The special emphasis on algorithmic and computational techniques makes this textbook, just like its author, a valuable bridge between game theory and computer sciences."

\begin{tcolorbox}
"这是一本严谨且易懂的数学非合作博弈论入门书籍。除了涵盖基本概念和结果外，它还包含了博弈论教材中通常没有的特殊和高级主题及应用，如组合博弈、拥塞博弈(congestion game)和监管博弈(Inspection game)。对算法和计算技术的特别强调，使这本教材就像其作者一样，成为博弈论和计算机科学之间的宝贵桥梁。"
\end{tcolorbox}

Shmuel Zamir, The Hebrew University of Jerusalem

\begin{tcolorbox}
什穆埃尔·扎米尔，耶路撒冷希伯来大学
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Preface}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{前言}
\end{tcolorbox}

This book is an introduction to the mathematics of non-cooperative game theory. Each concept is explained in detail, starting from a main example, with a slow-paced proof of each theorem. The book has been designed and tested for self-study, and as an undergraduate course text with core chapters and optional chapters for different audiences. It has been developed over 15 years for a one-semester course on game theory at the London School of Economics and the distance learning program of the University of London, attended each year by about 200 third-year students in mathematics, economics, management, and other degrees. After studying this book, a student who started from first-year mathematics (the basics of linear algebra, analysis, and probability) will have a solid understanding of the most important concepts and theorems of non-cooperative game theory.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本书是对非合作博弈论数学知识的入门介绍。每个概念都从一个主要示例出发进行详细解释，并对每个定理进行循序渐进的证明。本书专为自学而设计并经过测试，也可作为本科课程教材，设有核心章节和供不同受众选择的可选章节。本书是作者在15年时间里，为伦敦政治经济学院的一学期博弈论课程以及伦敦大学远程学习项目编写的，每年约有200名数学、经济学、管理学及其他专业的三年级学生参加学习。学完本书后，具备一年级数学基础(线性代数、分析和概率论基础)的学生将扎实掌握非合作博弈论中最重要的概念和定理。
\end{tcolorbox}

The intended audience are primarily students in mathematics, computer science, and mathematical economics. For mathematicians, we provide complete self-contained proofs (in an economics course, these may be used as reliable background and reference material). For computer scientists, we introduce important ideas in algorithmic game theory, such as traffic equilibria, the "parity argument" for the existence and computation of Nash equilibria, and correlated equilibria. For economists, we use some important economic models as examples, such as Cournot's quantity competition, which is the oldest formal definition of a game. Commitment games are applied to Stackelberg leadership, and in Chapter 11 to the iterated-offers bargaining model. However, given the many good introductions to game theory for economists (for example, Gibbons, 1992, or Osborne, 2004), economic applications are not central to this book.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本书主要面向数学、计算机科学和数理经济学专业的学生。对于数学家，我们提供完整且自成体系的证明(在经济学课程中，这些证明可作为可靠的背景资料和参考材料)。对于计算机科学家，我们介绍算法博弈论中的重要思想，如交通均衡、用于证明纳什均衡(Nash equilibrium)存在性和计算的“奇偶性论证(parity argument)”以及相关均衡。对于经济学家，我们以一些重要的经济模型为例，如古诺产量竞争(Cournot's quantity competition)，这是最早对博弈的正式定义。承诺博弈(commitment game)被应用于斯塔克尔伯格领导模型(Stackelberg leadership)，在第11章中还应用于迭代报价议价模型。不过，鉴于已有许多面向经济学家的优秀博弈论入门书籍(如Gibbons，1992年或Osborne，2004年的著作)，经济应用并非本书的核心内容。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Aims and Contents}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{目标与内容}
\end{tcolorbox}

The first aim of this book is to let the student become fluent in game theory. The student will learn the modeling tools (such as game trees and the strategic form) and methods for analyzing games (finding their equilibria). This is provided in the core chapters on non-cooperative games: Chapter 3 on games in strategic form introduces important games such as the Prisoner's Dilemma or Matching Pennies, and the concept of Nash equilibrium (in the book nearly always just called "equilibrium" for brevity). Chapter 4 treats game trees with perfect information. Chapter 6 explains mixed strategies and mixed equilibrium. Game trees with imperfect information are covered in Chapter 10.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本书的首要目标是让学生熟练掌握博弈论。学生将学习建模工具(如博弈树(game tree)和策略形式(strategic form))以及分析博弈的方法(寻找博弈的均衡(equilibrium))。这些内容在关于非合作博弈的核心章节中有所呈现:第3章介绍策略形式的博弈，引入了诸如囚徒困境(Prisoner's Dilemma)或硬币匹配(Matching Pennies)等重要博弈，以及纳什均衡(Nash equilibrium)的概念(为简洁起见，本书中几乎总是简称为“均衡”)。第4章讨论具有完美信息(perfect information)的博弈树。第6章解释混合策略(mixed strategy)和混合均衡(mixed equilibrium)。第10章涵盖具有不完美信息的博弈树。
\end{tcolorbox}

A second aim is to provide the conceptual and mathematical foundations of the theory. Chapter 5 explains how the concept of expected utility represents a consistent preference for risky outcomes (and not "risk neutrality" for monetary payoffs, a common confusion). Chapter 7 proves Brouwer's fixed-point theorem, used by Nash (1951) to show the existence of an equilibrium point. Chapter 8 gives a self-contained two-page proof of von Neumann's minimax theorem for zero-sum games. These chapters form independent "modules" that can be used for reference and that can be omitted in a taught course that has less emphasis on mathematical proofs. If only one proof from these chapters is presented, it should be the short proof of the minimax theorem.

\begin{tcolorbox}
第二个目标是为该理论提供概念和数学基础。第5章解释了期望效用概念如何代表对风险结果的一致偏好(而非对货币收益的“风险中性”，这是常见的误解)。第7章证明了布劳威尔不动点定理，纳什(1951年)用该定理证明了均衡点的存在性。第8章用两页篇幅自成体系地证明了冯·诺伊曼针对零和博弈(zero-sum game)提出的极小极大定理(minimax theorem)。这些章节构成独立的“模块”，可用于参考，在不太注重数学证明的授课课程中可以省略。如果只展示这些章节中的一个证明，那应该是极小极大定理的简短证明。
\end{tcolorbox}

A third aim is to introduce ideas that every game theorist should know but which are not normally taught, and which make this book special. They are mathematical highlights, typically less known to economists:

\begin{tcolorbox}
第三个目标是介绍每个博弈论学者都应了解但通常不教授的思想，这也是本书的独特之处。这些都是数学亮点，经济学家通常不太了解:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item An accessible introduction to combinatorial games in Chapter 1, pioneered by Berlekamp, Conway, and Guy (2001-2004), with a focus on impartial games and the central game of Nim.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 第1章对组合博弈进行了通俗易懂的介绍，这一领域由Berlekamp、Conway和Guy(2001 - 2004年)开创，重点介绍无偏博弈(impartial game)和核心的尼姆游戏(Nim)。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Congestion games, with the famous Braess paradox where increasing network capacity can worsen congestion, and where an equilibrium is found with the help of a potential function (Chapter 2).
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 拥塞博弈(congestion game)，其中有著名的布雷斯悖论(Braess paradox)，即增加网络容量可能会加剧拥堵，并且可以借助势函数(potential function)找到均衡(第2章)。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item An elegant constructive proof of Sperner's lemma, used to prove Brouwer's fixed-point theorem, and the Freudenthal simplicial subdivision that works naturally in any dimension (Sections 7.4 and 7.7 in Chapter 7).
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 对斯佩纳引理(Sperner's lemma)进行了优雅的构造性证明，该引理用于证明布劳威尔不动点定理，还介绍了在任何维度都能自然适用的弗罗伊登塔尔单纯剖分(Freudenthal simplicial subdivision)(第7章第7.4节和第7.7节)。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item The geometry of two-player games (which is my own research specialty) and the algorithm by Lemke and Howson (1964) for finding a Nash equilibrium, which implies that generic games have an odd number of equilibria (Chapter 9).
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 两人博弈的几何性质(这是我自己的研究专长)以及莱姆克 - 豪森算法(Lemke and Howson，1964年)，用于寻找纳什均衡，该算法表明通有博弈(generic game)有奇数个均衡(第9章)。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item An introduction to correlated equilibria in Chapter 12, with an elegant existence proof due to Hart and Schmeidler (1989) that does not require a fixed-point theorem but only von Neumann's minimax theorem.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 第12章介绍了相关均衡(correlated equilibria)，并给出了一个优雅的存在性证明，该证明由哈特(Hart)和施迈德勒(Schmeidler)于1989年提出，不依赖不动点定理，仅使用了冯·诺伊曼的极小极大定理(minimax theorem)。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

These chapters are optional. I teach the course in variations but always include Chapter 1 on combinatorial games; they are much enjoyed by students, who would not encounter them in an introductory economics course on game theory. These chapters give also a short introduction to algorithmic game theory for computer science students.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这些章节为选学内容。我会以不同方式讲授这门课程，但始终会涵盖第1章关于组合博弈的内容；学生们很喜欢这些内容，因为在经济学专业的博弈论入门课程中他们不会接触到这些。这些章节还为计算机科学专业的学生简要介绍了算法博弈论。
\end{tcolorbox}

The general emphasis of the book is to teach methods, not philosophy. Game theorists tend to question and to justify the approaches they take, for example the concept of Nash equilibrium, and the assumed common knowledge of all players about the rules of the game. These questions are of course very important. In fact, these are probably the very issues that require a careful validation in a practical game-theoretic analysis. However, this problem is not remedied by a lengthy discussion of why one should play Nash equilibrium.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本书的重点是传授方法，而非探讨哲学。博弈论学者往往会质疑并论证他们所采用的方法，例如纳什均衡(Nash equilibrium)的概念，以及假设所有参与者对博弈规则具有共同知识(common knowledge)。这些问题当然非常重要。事实上，在实际的博弈论分析中，这些可能正是需要仔细验证的问题。然而，冗长地讨论为何要采用纳什均衡并不能解决这个问题。
\end{tcolorbox}

I think that a student of game theory should first learn the central models of the strategic and extensive form, and be fluent in analyzing them. That toolbox will then be useful when comparing different game-theoretic models and the solutions that they imply.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我认为，博弈论的学生首先应该学习战略式和扩展式的核心模型，并熟练掌握对它们的分析方法。当比较不同的博弈论模型及其所蕴含的解时，这些工具将非常有用。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Mathematical and Scholarly Level}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{数学与学术水平}
\end{tcolorbox}

General mathematical prerequisites are the basic notions of linear algebra (vectors and matrices), probability theory (independence, conditional probability), and analysis (continuity, closed sets). Rather than putting them in a rarely read appendix, important concepts are recalled where needed in text boxes labeled as Background material.

\begin{tcolorbox}
一般的数学先决条件包括线性代数(向量和矩阵)、概率论(独立性、条件概率(conditional probability))和分析学(连续性、闭集)的基本概念。重要的概念没有放在很少有人阅读的附录中，而是在需要时以“背景材料(background material)”的文本框形式回顾。
\end{tcolorbox}

For each chapter, the necessary mathematical background and the required previous chapters are listed in a first section on prerequisites and learning objectives.

\begin{tcolorbox}
每章的先决条件和学习目标部分会列出所需的数学背景和之前需要学习的章节。
\end{tcolorbox}

The mathematics of game theory is not technically difficult, but very conceptual, and requires therefore a certain mathematical maturity. For example, combinatorial games have a recursive structure, for which a generalization of the mathematical induction known for natural numbers is appropriate, called "top-down induction" and explained in Section 1.3.

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈论的数学知识在技术上并不难，但概念性很强，因此需要一定的数学成熟度。例如，组合博弈具有递归结构(recursive structure)，对于这种结构，自然数的数学归纳法的一种推广形式——“自上而下归纳(top - down induction)”是适用的，这在第1.3节中会进行解释。
\end{tcolorbox}

Game-theoretic concepts have precise and often unfamiliar mathematical definitions. In this book, each main concept is typically explained by means of a detailed introductory example. We use the format of definitions, theorems and proofs in order to be precise and to keep some technical details hidden in proofs. The proofs are detailed and complete and can be studied line by line. The main idea of each proof is conveyed by introductory examples, and geometric arguments are supported by pictures wherever possible.

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈论的概念具有精确且往往不常见的数学定义。在本书中，每个主要概念通常通过详细的引入性示例进行解释。我们采用定义、定理和证明的形式，以确保表述精确，并将一些技术细节隐藏在证明中。证明详细且完整，可以逐行研读。每个证明的主要思想通过引入性示例传达，并且在可能的情况下，用图形支持几何论证。
\end{tcolorbox}

Great attention is given to details that help avoid unnecessary confusions. For example, a random event with two real values \(x\) and \(y\) as outcomes will be described with a probability \(p\) assigned to the second outcome \(y\) , so that the interval \(\left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) for \(p\) corresponds naturally to the interval \(\left\lbrack  {x,y}\right\rbrack\) of the expectation \(\left( {1 - p}\right) x + {py}\) of that event.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们非常注重避免不必要的混淆。例如，一个有两个实数值 \(x\) 和 \(y\) 作为结果的随机事件，会将概率 \(p\) 分配给第二个结果 \(y\) ，这样 \(p\) 的区间 \(\left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) 自然对应于该事件期望 \(\left( {1 - p}\right) x + {py}\) 的区间 \(\left\lbrack  {x,y}\right\rbrack\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

This book emphasizes accessibility, not generality. I worked hard (and enjoyed) on presenting the most direct and overhead-free proof of each result. In studying these proofs, students may encounter for the first time important ideas from topology, convex analysis, and linear programming, and thus may become interested in the more general mathematical theory of these subjects.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本书强调易读性，而非一般性。我努力(并且乐在其中)为每个结果提供最直接、无冗余的证明。在学习这些证明的过程中，学生可能首次接触到拓扑学、凸分析和线性规划(linear programming)中的重要思想，从而可能对这些学科更一般的数学理论产生兴趣。
\end{tcolorbox}

Bibliographic references, historical discussions, and further extensions are deferred to a final section in each chapter, to avoid distractions in the main text. References to further reading may include popular science books that I found relevant and interesting. This book is primarily directed at undergraduate students and not at researchers, but I have tried to be historically accurate. For important works in French or German I have also cited English translations where I could find them.

\begin{tcolorbox}
参考文献、历史讨论和进一步的拓展内容放在每章的最后一节，以免干扰正文的阅读。推荐的进一步阅读材料可能包括我认为相关且有趣的科普书籍。本书主要面向本科生，而非研究人员，但我尽量保证历史信息的准确性。对于重要的法语或德语著作，只要能找到英文译本，我也会引用。
\end{tcolorbox}

References to statements inside this book are in upper case, like "Theorem 2.2", and to other works in lower case, like "theorem 13.6 of Roughgarden (2016)".

\begin{tcolorbox}
本书内的陈述引用使用大写，如“定理2.2”，对其他著作的引用使用小写，如“拉夫加登(Roughgarden，2016)的定理13.6”。
\end{tcolorbox}

I use both genders to refer to players in a game, in particular in two-player games to distinguish them more easily. Usage of the pronoun "she" has become natural, and (like "he") it may stand collectively for "he or she".

\begin{tcolorbox}
我在描述博弈中的参与者时会使用两种性别指代，特别是在两人博弈中，这样可以更轻松地区分参与者。使用代词“她”已经变得很自然，并且(和“他”一样)它可以泛指“他或她”。
\end{tcolorbox}

Each chapter concludes with a set of exercises. They test methods (such as how to find mixed equilibria), or the understanding of mathematical concepts used in proofs (in particular in Chapters 5 and 7). Complete solutions to the exercises are available from the publisher for registered lecturers.

\begin{tcolorbox}
每章结尾都有一组练习题。这些练习题用于检验方法(如如何找到混合均衡)，或对证明中使用的数学概念的理解(特别是第5章和第7章)。已注册的教师可以从出版商处获取练习题的完整解答。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Acknowledgments}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{致谢}
\end{tcolorbox}

Some of the material for this book has been adapted and developed from a course guide originally produced for the distance learning International Programme offered by the University of London (http://london.ac.uk/).Rahul Savani and George Zouros gave valuable comments on that first course guide. Many students, some of them anonymously, have pointed out errors and needs for clarification in the subsequent versions of lecture notes. Rahul Savani proof-read the final version with great care. Vanessa Wells drew the nice animal pictures on the cover and in Figures 8.1 and 9.6. I thank Robert Aumann, Paul Dütting, Aviezri Fraenkel, Sergiu Hart, Urban Larsson, Abraham Neyman, Richard Nowakowski, David Tranah, Peyton Young, and Shmuel Zamir for further suggestions.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本书的部分内容改编自伦敦大学(http://london.ac.uk/)远程学习国际项目最初编写的课程指南。拉胡尔·萨瓦尼(Rahul Savani)和乔治·佐罗斯(George Zouros)对第一版课程指南提出了宝贵意见。许多学生(有些匿名)指出了后续版本讲义中的错误，并提出了需要澄清的地方。拉胡尔·萨瓦尼仔细校对了最终版本。凡妮莎·韦尔斯(Vanessa Wells)绘制了封面以及图8.1和图9.6中可爱的动物图片。我感谢罗伯特·奥曼(Robert Aumann)、保罗·迪廷(Paul Dütting)、阿维兹里·弗伦克尔(Aviezri Fraenkel)、塞尔吉乌·哈特(Sergiu Hart)、乌尔班·拉尔森(Urban Larsson)、亚伯拉罕·内曼(Abraham Neyman)、理查德·诺瓦科夫斯基(Richard Nowakowski)、大卫·特拉纳(David Tranah)、佩顿·扬(Peyton Young)和什穆埃尔·扎米尔(Shmuel Zamir)提出的进一步建议。
\end{tcolorbox}

Martin Antonov is the current lead programmer of the Game Theory Explorer software (http://www.gametheoryexplorer.org), a joint project with Rahul Savani and (as part of the Gambit software) Theodore Turocy, for solving games in strategic and extensive form. From Game Theory Explorer, one can export games into the LaTeX formats used in this book for drawing payoff tables and game trees (available on request). 1

\begin{tcolorbox}
马丁·安东诺夫(Martin Antonov)是博弈论探索者软件(http://www.gametheoryexplorer.org)的现任首席程序员，该软件是与拉胡尔·萨瓦尼(Rahul Savani)以及(作为Gambit软件的一部分)西奥多·图罗西(Theodore Turocy)的联合项目，用于求解战略型和扩展型博弈。通过博弈论探索者软件，可以将博弈导出为本书用于绘制收益表和博弈树的LaTeX格式(可按需获取)。1
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Nim and Combinatorial Games}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{尼姆游戏与组合博弈}
\end{tcolorbox}

Combinatorial game theory is about perfect-information two-player games, such as Checkers, Go, Chess, or Nim, which are analyzed using their rules. It tries to answer who will win in a game position (assuming optimal play on both sides), and to quantify who is ahead and by how much. The topic has a rich mathematical theory that relates to discrete mathematics, algebra, and (not touched here) computational complexity, and highly original ideas specific to these games.

\begin{tcolorbox}
组合博弈论研究的是完全信息的两人博弈，如跳棋、围棋、国际象棋或尼姆游戏(Nim)，通过其规则对这些博弈进行分析。它试图回答在一个博弈位置(假设双方都进行最优策略)谁会获胜，并量化哪一方领先以及领先多少。该主题有丰富的数学理论，与离散数学、代数以及(此处未涉及)计算复杂性相关，还有针对这些博弈的极具原创性的思想。
\end{tcolorbox}

Combinatorial games are not part of "classical" game theory as used in economics. However, they nicely demonstrate that game theory is about rigorous, and often unfamiliar, mathematical concepts rather than complex techniques.

\begin{tcolorbox}
组合博弈并非经济学中“经典”博弈论的一部分。然而，它们很好地证明了博弈论涉及的是严谨且往往不熟悉的数学概念，而非复杂的技术。
\end{tcolorbox}

This chapter is only an introduction to combinatorial games. It presents the theory of impartial games where in any game position both players have the same allowed moves. We show the powerful and surprisingly simple result (Theorem 1.14), independently found by Sprague (1935) and Grundy (1939), that every impartial game is equivalent to a "Nim heap" of suitable size.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本章仅为组合博弈的入门介绍。它介绍了无偏博弈(impartial game)理论，在任何博弈位置，双方玩家都有相同的可行移动。我们展示了一个强大且惊人简单的结果(定理1.14)，该结果由斯普拉格(Sprague，1935年)和格伦迪(Grundy，1939年)独立发现，即每个无偏博弈都等价于一个合适大小的“尼姆堆”(Nim heap)。
\end{tcolorbox}

In Section 1.8 we give a short glimpse into the more general theory of partizan games, where the allowed moves may depend on the player (e.g., one player can move the white pieces on the game board and the other player the black pieces).

\begin{tcolorbox}
在1.8节中，我们简要介绍更一般的有偏博弈(partizan game)理论，其中可行移动可能取决于玩家(例如，一名玩家可以移动棋盘上的白子，另一名玩家可以移动黑子)。
\end{tcolorbox}

For a deeper treatment, the final Section 1.9 of this chapter lists some excellent textbooks on combinatorial games. They treat impartial games as a special case of general combinatorial games. In contrast, we first treat the simpler impartial games in full.

\begin{tcolorbox}
为了更深入地探讨，本章的最后一节1.9列出了一些关于组合博弈的优秀教科书。它们将无偏博弈作为一般组合博弈的特殊情况进行处理。相比之下，我们首先全面处理较简单的无偏博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{1.1 Prerequisites and Learning Outcomes}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{1.1 先决条件与学习成果}
\end{tcolorbox}

The combinatorial games that we consider are finite, and dealing with them uses a type of mathematical induction that we explain further in Section 1.3. It is helpful to have seen induction about the natural numbers before. Although not essential, is is useful to know the algebraic concept of an abelian group, in particular addition modulo 2. We assume familiarity with the binary system where numbers are written in base 2, using only the two digits 0 and 1, rather than in the familiar base 10 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们考虑的组合博弈是有限的，处理这些博弈使用一种数学归纳法，我们将在1.3节进一步解释。之前了解过关于自然数的归纳法会有所帮助。虽然不是必需的，但了解阿贝尔群(abelian group)的代数概念，特别是模2加法，会很有用。我们假设熟悉二进制(binary system)，即数字以2为基数书写，仅使用0和1这两个数字，而不是熟悉的以10为基数。
\end{tcolorbox}

After studying this chapter, you should be able to:

\begin{tcolorbox}
学习本章后，你应该能够:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item play Nim optimally;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 最优地玩尼姆游戏；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item explain the different concepts of options, game sums, equivalent games, Nim values, and the mex rule;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 解释选项(option)、博弈之和(game sum)、等价博弈、尼姆值(Nim value)和mex规则等不同概念；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item apply these concepts to play other impartial games like those described in the exercises;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 将这些概念应用于玩其他无偏博弈，如练习中描述的博弈；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item understand how some (not all) game positions in partizan games can be expressed as numbers that state how many moves the Left player is safely ahead, and why these numbers may be fractions with powers of two in the denominator.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 理解有偏博弈中的一些(并非全部)博弈位置如何表示为数字，这些数字表示左玩家(Left player)安全领先的移动步数，以及为什么这些数字可能是分母为2的幂的分数。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{1.2 Nim}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{1.2 尼姆游戏}
\end{tcolorbox}

Combinatorial games are two-player win-lose games of perfect information, that is, every player is perfectly informed about the state of play (unlike, for example, the card games Bridge or Poker that have hidden information). The games do not have chance moves like rolling dice or shuffling cards. When playing the game, the two players always alternate in making a move. Every play of the game ends with a win for one player and a loss for the other player (some games like Chess allow for a draw as an outcome, but not the games we consider here).

\begin{tcolorbox}
组合博弈是一种双人胜负博弈，具有完美信息，即每个玩家都完全了解游戏的状态(这与像桥牌或扑克这类具有隐藏信息的纸牌游戏不同)。这类游戏没有掷骰子或洗牌等随机动作。在游戏过程中，两名玩家总是轮流行动。每一局游戏的结果都是一名玩家获胜，另一名玩家失败(像国际象棋这类游戏允许平局，但我们这里讨论的游戏不包括这种情况)。
\end{tcolorbox}

The game has a (typically finite) number of positions, with well-defined rules that define the allowed moves to reach the next position. The rules are such that play will always come to an end because some player is unable to move. This is called the ending condition. We assume the normal play convention that a player unable to move loses. The alternative to normal play is misère play, where a player who is unable to move wins (so the previous player who has made the last move loses).

\begin{tcolorbox}
游戏有(通常是有限数量的)博弈位置，并有明确的规则来定义到达下一个位置所允许的行动。这些规则使得游戏总会结束，因为总会有玩家无法行动。这被称为终止条件。我们采用正常规则，即无法行动的玩家输掉游戏。与正常规则相对的是反向玩法，在反向玩法中，无法行动的玩家获胜(即上一个做出最后行动的玩家失败)。
\end{tcolorbox}

We study impartial games where the available moves in a game position do not depend on whose turn it is to move. If that is not the case, as in Chess where one player can only move the white pieces and the other player the black pieces, the game is called partizan.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们研究无偏博弈，在无偏博弈中，博弈位置的可用行动不取决于轮到谁行动。如果不是这种情况，例如在国际象棋中，一名玩家只能移动白子，另一名玩家只能移动黑子，这种游戏就被称为有偏博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

For impartial games, the game Nim plays a central role. A game position in Nim is given by some heaps of tokens, and a move is to remove some (at least one, possibly all) tokens from one of the heaps. The last player able to move wins the game, according to the normal play convention.

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于无偏博弈，尼姆游戏(Nim)起着核心作用。尼姆游戏的一个博弈位置由一些尼姆堆(Nim heap)的代币表示，一次行动是从其中一个尼姆堆中移除一些(至少一个，可能全部)代币。根据正常规则，最后能够行动的玩家赢得游戏。
\end{tcolorbox}

We analyze the Nim position1,2,3, which means three heaps with one, two, and three tokens, respectively. One possible move is to remove two tokens from the heap of size three, like here:

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们分析尼姆游戏位置1,2,3，这意味着有三个尼姆堆，分别有一、二和三个代币。一种可能的行动是从有三个代币的尼姆堆中移除两个代币，就像这里:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.7\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_14_324_291_1004_78_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

which we write as a move from1,2,3to1,2,1. Because the move can be made in any one heap, the order of the heap sizes does not matter, so the position1,2,1 could also be written as1,1,2. The options of a game position are the positions that can be reached by a single legal move (according to the game rules) from the player to move. We draw them with moves shown as downward lines, like here,

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们将其写为从1,2,3到1,2,1的行动。因为行动可以在任何一个尼姆堆上进行，所以尼姆堆大小的顺序无关紧要，因此位置1,2,1也可以写成1,1,2。一个博弈位置的选项是当前玩家通过一次合法行动(根据游戏规则)能够到达的位置。我们用向下的线表示行动，就像这里，
\end{tcolorbox}

(1.1)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.7\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_14_346_678_901_196_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

where the first option 2,3 is obtained by removing from1,2,3the entire heap of size 1, the second option 1, 1, 3 by removing one token from the heap of size 2, and so on. The game tree is obtained by continuing to draw all possible moves in this way until play ends (game trees are studied in much more detail in Chapter 4). We may conflate options with obvious equal meaning, such as the positions1,1,2 and 1, 2, 1 that can be reached from 1, 2, 2. However, we do not draw moves to the same position from two different predecessors, such as1,1,2that can be reached from1,1,3and1,2,2. Instead, such a position like1,1,2will be repeated in the game tree, so that every position has a unique history of moves.

\begin{tcolorbox}
其中第一个选项2,3是通过从1,2,3中移除整个大小为1的尼姆堆得到的，第二个选项1,1,3是通过从大小为2的尼姆堆中移除一个代币得到的，依此类推。博弈树(game tree)是通过继续以这种方式绘制所有可能的行动直到游戏结束得到的(在第4章将更详细地研究博弈树)。我们可以合并具有明显相同含义的选项，例如可以从1,2,2到达的位置1,1,2和1,2,1。然而，我们不会从两个不同的前一个位置绘制到同一个位置的行动，例如可以从1,1,3和1,2,2到达的1,1,2。相反，像1,1,2这样的位置会在博弈树中重复出现，这样每个位置都有唯一的行动历史(history of moves)。
\end{tcolorbox}

In an impartial game, the available moves in a game position are by definition independent of the player to move. A game position belongs therefore to exactly one of two possible outcome classes, namely it is either a winning or a losing position. "Winning" or "losing" applies to the player whose turn it is to move, assuming optimal play. A winning position means that the player can force a win with a suitable first "winning" move (and subsequent winning moves at all later positions). A losing position means that every move from the current position leads to a winning position of the other player, who can then force a win, so that the current player will lose.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在无偏博弈中，根据定义，博弈位置的可用行动与当前行动的玩家无关。因此，一个博弈位置恰好属于两种可能的结果类(outcome class)之一，即它要么是必胜局势(winning position)，要么是失败位置(losing position)。“必胜”或“失败”适用于轮到行动的玩家，前提是进行最优玩法。必胜局势意味着玩家可以通过合适的第一步“必胜步”(winning move)(以及后续在所有后续位置的必胜步)迫使获胜。失败位置意味着从当前位置的每一步行动都会导致另一名玩家处于必胜局势，然后该玩家可以迫使获胜，因此当前玩家将失败。
\end{tcolorbox}

When drawn in full with all possible subsequent moves, the Nim position 1,2,3has already a rather large game tree. However, we can tell from its options in (1.1) that1,2,3is a losing position, using the following simple observations of how to play optimally in a Nim position with at most two heaps:

\begin{tcolorbox}
当完整绘制出所有可能的后续行动时，尼姆游戏位置1,2,3的博弈树已经相当大。然而，我们可以从(1.1)中的选项判断出1,2,3是一个失败位置，使用以下关于在最多有两个尼姆堆的尼姆游戏位置中如何进行最优玩法的简单观察:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item If there are no Nim heaps left, this defines a losing position, by the normal play convention.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 如果没有尼姆堆剩余，根据正常规则，这定义为一个失败位置。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item A single Nim heap is a winning position; the winning move is to remove the entire heap.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 一个尼姆堆是一个必胜局势；必胜步是移除整个尼姆堆。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Two Nim heaps are a winning position if and only if the heaps have different sizes; the winning move is to equalize their sizes by suitably reducing the larger heap, which creates a losing position of two equal heaps. If the two heaps are equal, then a Nim move means reducing one of the heaps, so that the two heaps become unequal, which is a winning position; hence, two equal heaps define a losing position, as claimed.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 两个尼姆堆是必胜局势当且仅当这两个尼姆堆的大小不同；必胜步是通过适当地减少较大的尼姆堆使其大小相等，这会创建一个两个相等尼姆堆的失败位置。如果两个尼姆堆相等，那么一次尼姆游戏行动意味着减少其中一个尼姆堆，使得两个尼姆堆变得不相等，这是一个必胜局势；因此，两个相等的尼姆堆定义为一个失败位置，如所声称的那样。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

Every option of1,2,3shown in (1.1) is a winning position, because it allows moving to a two-heap losing position, as follows:

\begin{tcolorbox}
(1.1) 中所示的 1,2,3 的每个选项都是必胜局势，因为它允许移动到一个双堆失败位置，如下所示:
\end{tcolorbox}

(1.2)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.7\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_15_481_615_905_332_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

This picture shows sequences of two moves from position1,2,3, but not all of them, so this is not the top part of the full game tree. Only from the first position1,2,3, which is a losing position, all moves are shown. For the second move, only a single, winning move is shown, which leads again to a losing position. This suffices to prove that1,2,3is a losing position in Nim, by the following observation.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这张图展示了从位置 1,2,3 开始的两步走法序列，但不是所有走法，所以这不是完整博弈树的顶部部分。只有从第一个位置 1,2,3(这是一个失败位置)出发的所有走法都被展示出来了。对于第二步走法，只展示了一个必胜步，它又会导致一个失败位置。根据以下观察，这足以证明 1,2,3 在尼姆游戏中是一个失败位置。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 1.1. In an impartial game, a game position is losing if and only if all its options are winning positions. A game position is winning if and only if at least one of its options is a losing position; moving there is a winning move.

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理 1.1:在无偏博弈中，一个博弈位置是失败位置当且仅当它的所有选项都是必胜局势。一个博弈位置是必胜局势当且仅当它的至少一个选项是失败位置；移动到那个位置就是一个必胜步。
\end{tcolorbox}

This lemma applies also to the game position that has no options at all, so trivially ("vacuously") all these options are winning (because there aren't any) and hence the position is losing. The lemma can also be taken as a definition of losing and winning positions, which is not circular but recursive because the options of a game position are simpler in the sense of being closer to the end of any play in the game. In Section 1.3 we will make this precise, and will give a formal proof of Lemma 1.1.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这个引理也适用于根本没有选项的博弈位置，所以显然(“空”意义上)所有这些选项都是必胜的(因为根本没有选项)，因此该位置是失败位置。这个引理也可以作为失败位置和必胜局势的定义，它不是循环定义而是递归定义，因为一个博弈位置的选项在更接近游戏任何一轮结束的意义上更简单。在 1.3 节中，我们将对此进行精确阐述，并给出引理 1.1 的正式证明。
\end{tcolorbox}

The concepts of winning and losing position assume optimal play. Binmore (2007, p. 49) writes: "A dull art movie called Last Year in Marienbad consists largely of the characters playing Nim very badly." In that movie (from 1961, directed by Alain Resnais), they play several times misère Nim, always from the starting position1,3,5,7. There is a scene where three successive positions are

\begin{tcolorbox}
必胜局势和失败位置的概念假设了最优玩法。宾莫尔(2007 年，第 49 页)写道:“一部沉闷的艺术电影《去年在马里昂巴德》很大程度上是角色们把尼姆游戏玩得很糟糕的情节。”在那部电影(1961 年上映，由阿伦·雷乃执导)中，他们多次玩反向尼姆游戏，总是从起始位置 1,3,5,7 开始。有一个场景中，三个连续的位置是
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\text{ player I player II }
\]

\[
2,3,6 \rightarrow  2,3,5 \rightarrow  2,3,1
\]

and then player I, whose turn it is, eventually loses. We can conclude from this that player I indeed played badly. Namely, either2,3,1is a winning position, in which case player I failed to force a win. Or2,3,1is a losing position (which is in fact the case), in which case player I failed to move there from position 2,3,6.

\begin{tcolorbox}
然后轮到玩家 I 行动，最终玩家 I 输了。我们可以由此得出结论，玩家 I 确实玩得很糟糕。也就是说，要么 2,3,1 是一个必胜局势，在这种情况下玩家 I 未能确保获胜。要么 2,3,1 是一个失败位置(实际上就是这种情况)，在这种情况下玩家 I 未能从位置 2,3,6 移动到该位置。
\end{tcolorbox}

On the other hand, a game is more entertaining if it is not known how to play it optimally. In Chess, for example, which allows for a draw, one can show similarly to Lemma 1.1 that either White can force a win, or Black can force a win, or both players can force a draw. However, Chess is not yet "solved" in the sense that we know which outcome results from optimal play, which is why it is still an interesting game because players do not play perfectly.

\begin{tcolorbox}
另一方面，如果不知道如何最优地玩一个游戏，这个游戏会更有趣。例如在国际象棋中，存在和棋的情况，我们可以类似引理 1.1 那样证明，要么白方可以确保获胜，要么黑方可以确保获胜，要么双方都可以确保和棋。然而，国际象棋在我们知道最优玩法会导致何种结果的意义上还没有被“解决”，这就是为什么它仍然是一个有趣的游戏，因为玩家们不会完美地玩。
\end{tcolorbox}

Nim, however, has been solved by Bouton (1901). We demonstrate his winning strategy for the already formidable Nim position

\begin{tcolorbox}
然而，尼姆游戏已经被布顿(1901 年)解决了。我们将展示他针对已经很有挑战性的尼姆位置的必胜策略
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_16_547_726_554_226_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

which is4,5,9,14and a winning position. The trick is to write the heap sizes in the binary system. The following tables show that one winning move is to reduce the Nim heap of size 5 to size 3, which leads to the losing position4,3,9,14:

\begin{tcolorbox}
即 4,5,9,14，这是一个必胜局势。诀窍是将尼姆堆的大小用二进制表示。以下表格显示，一个必胜步是将大小为 5 的尼姆堆减少到大小为 3，这会导致失败位置 4,3,9,14:
\end{tcolorbox}

(1.3)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.7\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_16_376_1145_900_291_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

In (1.3), the top row represents the powers of 2 used to represent each heap size with binary digits, such as \(5 = 0 \cdot  8 + 1 \cdot  4 + 0 \cdot  2 + 1 \cdot  1\) . The bottom row is called the Nim sum of the heap sizes and is obtained by "addition modulo 2 without carry" for each column; that is, the Nim sum has a 0 if the number of 1 's in that column is even, and a 1 if that number is odd.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在 (1.3) 中，顶行代表用于用二进制数字表示每个尼姆堆大小的 2 的幂，例如 \(5 = 0 \cdot  8 + 1 \cdot  4 + 0 \cdot  2 + 1 \cdot  1\) 。底行称为尼姆堆大小的尼姆和，是通过对每一列进行“无进位模 2 加法”得到的；也就是说，如果该列中 1 的个数是偶数，尼姆和为 0，如果个数是奇数，尼姆和为 1。
\end{tcolorbox}

We claim that a Nim position is losing if and only if that Nim sum is zero for all columns, like on the right in (1.3); we call such a position a zero position. In order to show this, we have to show, in line with Lemma 1.1, that

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们声称，一个尼姆位置是失败位置当且仅当对于所有列，该尼姆和都为零，就像 (1.3) 右边那样；我们称这样的位置为零位置。为了证明这一点，我们必须根据引理 1.1 证明:
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) every move from a zero position leads to a position which is not zero and therefore winning, and

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 从一个零位置的每一步走法都会导致一个非零位置，因此是必胜局势，并且
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) from every winning position (with a Nim sum that is not zero) there is a move to a zero position.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 从每个必胜局势(尼姆和不为零)都有一步走法可以到达一个零位置。
\end{tcolorbox}

It easy to see (a): A move changes exactly one Nim heap, which corresponds to one row in the table of binary numbers, and changes at least one binary digit of the binary representation of the heap size. Therefore, the resulting Nim sum is also changed in the corresponding digits and becomes nonzero.

\begin{tcolorbox}
很容易看出 (a):一步走法恰好改变一个尼姆堆，这对应于二进制数表中的一行，并且至少改变尼姆堆大小二进制表示中的一个二进制数字。因此，得到的尼姆和在相应的数字上也会改变，并且变为非零。
\end{tcolorbox}

Condition (b) amounts to finding at least one winning move for a position that is not zero, like on the left in (1.3). Such a winning move is found by these steps:

\begin{tcolorbox}
条件 (b) 相当于为一个非零位置(如 (1.3) 左边的位置)找到至少一个必胜步。可以通过以下步骤找到这样的必胜步:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Choose the leftmost " 1 " column in the Nim sum (which exists because not all columns are 0 ), which represents the highest power of two used in the binary representation of the Nim sum, here \(4 = {2}^{2}\) .
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 选择尼姆和(Nim sum)中最左边的“1”列(该列存在是因为并非所有列都是 0)，它代表尼姆和的二进制表示中使用的最高 2 的幂次，这里是 \(4 = {2}^{2}\) 。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item By definition, there is an odd number of Nim heaps which use that power of two in their binary representation. In (1.3), these are the heaps of sizes 4, 5, and 14. Choose one of the corresponding rows; we choose the heap of size 5 .
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 根据定义，有奇数个尼姆堆(Nim heap)在其二进制表示中使用了该 2 的幂次。在 (1.3) 中，这些是大小为 4、5 和 14 的堆。选择其中一个对应的行；我们选择大小为 5 的堆。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item In the chosen row, "flip" the binary digits for each 1 in the Nim-sum, here for the columns 4 and 2; the resulting changes are shown with boxes on the right in (1.3). The largest of the changed powers of two in that row has digit 1, which is changed to 0 . Even if all subsequent digits were changed from 0 to 1, the resulting binary number gives a new, smaller integer (possibly zero, which will mean removing the entire heap). The winning move is to reduce the Nim heap to that smaller size, here from 5 to 3 .
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 在所选行中，对尼姆和中每个为 1 的二进制位进行“翻转”，这里是第 4 列和第 2 列；(1.3) 中右侧的方框显示了所做的更改。该行中被更改的 2 的幂次中最大的那个位原来是 1，现在将其更改为 0。即使所有后续位都从 0 变为 1，得到的二进制数也会是一个新的、更小的整数(可能为零，这意味着移除整个堆)。必胜步(winning move)是将尼姆堆的大小减小到那个更小的值，这里是从 5 减小到 3。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

As a result of these steps, the Nim sum is changed from 1 to 0 exactly in the columns where there was a 1 before, with the digits 0 untouched, so the new Nim sum has a 0 in each column. The resulting Nim position is therefore a zero position, as required in (b).

\begin{tcolorbox}
通过这些步骤，尼姆和恰好会在之前为 1 的列中从 1 变为 0，而 0 的位保持不变，因此新的尼姆和每一列都是 0。所以得到的尼姆博弈位置(game position)是一个零位置，符合 (b) 的要求。
\end{tcolorbox}

This winning strategy in Nim can be done with pen and paper, but is probably difficult to perform in one's head in actual play. For small heap sizes, a helpful mental image is to group the tokens in each Nim heap (now shown as dots) into distinct powers of two:

\begin{tcolorbox}
尼姆游戏(Nim)中的这个必胜策略可以用笔和纸来完成，但在实际游戏中可能很难在脑海中完成。对于较小的堆大小，一个有用的心理图像是将每个尼姆堆中的代币(现在用点表示)分组为不同的 2 的幂次:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.3\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_17_726_1477_477_343_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

(1.4)

This is an equivalent representation to the left table in (1.3), but without binary digits. It shows that the powers of two that occur an odd number of times are 4 and 2. A winning move has to change one heap so that afterwards they occur an even number of times. This can be done by changing the four dots in the heap of size 5 to two dots, as in \(:  :  \rightarrow   :\) . This is the winning move in (1.3) that removes two tokens from the heap of size 5 ; the same winning move can be made in the heap of size 4 . The third possible winning move is to remove six tokens (:: and :) from the heap of size 14. Caution: The resulting powers of two in a heap size must be distinct. The change \(:  :  \rightarrow   :\) is not allowed in the heap of size 14, because the intended result ::::: : : is not a binary representation because 2 appears twice; rather, the result of removing two tokens from the heap of size 14 is :::: :::, and then the overall created Nim position is not zero. Once the heap for the winning move is chosen, the number of tokens that have to be removed is unique.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这与 (1.3) 中左边的表格是等价的表示，但没有二进制位。它表明出现奇数次的 2 的幂次是 4 和 2。一个必胜步必须改变一个堆，使得之后这些幂次出现偶数次。这可以通过将大小为 5 的堆中的四个点变为两个点来实现，如 \(:  :  \rightarrow   :\) 所示。这就是 (1.3) 中从大小为 5 的堆中移除两个代币的必胜步；同样的必胜步也可以在大小为 4 的堆中进行。第三种可能的必胜步是从大小为 14 的堆中移除六个代币(:: 和 :)。注意:堆大小中得到的 2 的幂次必须是不同的。在大小为 14 的堆中不允许进行 \(:  :  \rightarrow   :\) 的更改，因为预期的结果 ::::: : : 不是一个二进制表示，因为 2 出现了两次；相反，从大小为 14 的堆中移除两个代币的结果是 :::: ::: ，然后整体得到的尼姆博弈位置不是零位置。一旦选择了用于必胜步的堆，必须移除的代币数量是唯一的。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{1.3 Top-down Induction}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{1.3 自上而下归纳(top-down induction)}
\end{tcolorbox}

When talking about combinatorial games, we will often use for brevity the word game for "game position". Every game \(G\) has finitely many options \({G}_{1},\ldots ,{G}_{m}\) that are reached from \(G\) by one of the allowed moves in \(G\) , as in this picture:

\begin{tcolorbox}
在讨论组合博弈时，为简洁起见，我们经常用“博弈”这个词来表示“博弈位置”。每个博弈 \(G\) 都有有限个选项 \({G}_{1},\ldots ,{G}_{m}\) ，这些选项可以通过 \(G\) 中允许的一步移动从 \(G\) 到达，如图所示:
\end{tcolorbox}

(1.5)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.3\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_18_628_862_391_188_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

If \(m = 0\) then \(G\) has no options. We denote the game with no options by 0, which by the normal play convention is a losing game. Otherwise the options of \(G\) are themselves games, defined by their respective options according to the rules of the game. In that way, any game is completely defined by its options. In short, the starting position defines the game completely.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果 \(m = 0\) ，那么 \(G\) 没有选项。我们用 0 表示没有选项的博弈，根据正常规则(normal play)约定，这是一个必败博弈。否则， \(G\) 的选项本身也是博弈，根据游戏规则由它们各自的选项来定义。这样，任何博弈都完全由其选项来定义。简而言之，起始位置完全定义了这个博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

We introduce a certain type of mathematical induction for games, which is applied to a partial order (see the background material text box on the next page).

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们为博弈引入一种特定类型的数学归纳法，它应用于一个偏序(partial order)(见下一页的背景材料(background material)文本框)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Consider a set \(S\) of games, defined, for example, by a starting game and all the games that can reached from it via any sequence of moves of the players. For two games \(G\) and \(H\) in \(S\) , call \(H\) simpler than \(G\) if there is a sequence of moves that leads from \(G\) to \(H\) . We allow \(G = H\) where this sequence is empty. The relation of being "simpler than" defines a partial order which for the moment we denote by \(\leq\) . Note that \(\leq\) is antisymmetric because it is not possible to reach \(G\) from \(G\) by a nonempty sequence of moves because this would violate the ending condition. The ending condition for games implies the following property:

\begin{tcolorbox}
考虑一个博弈集合 \(S\) ，例如，它由一个起始博弈以及所有可以通过玩家的任何移动序列从该起始博弈到达的博弈定义。对于 \(S\) 中的两个博弈 \(G\) 和 \(H\) ，如果存在一个从 \(G\) 到 \(H\) 的移动序列，就称 \(H\) 比 \(G\) 更简单。我们允许 \(G = H\) 这种移动序列为空的情况。“比……更简单”的关系定义了一个偏序，目前我们用 \(\leq\) 表示。注意， \(\leq\) 是反对称的(antisymmetric)，因为不可能通过非空的移动序列从 \(G\) 回到 \(G\) ，因为这会违反终止条件(ending condition)。博弈的终止条件意味着以下性质:
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\text{ Every nonempty subset of }S\text{ has a minimal element. } \tag{1.8}
\]

If there was a nonempty subset \(T\) of \(S\) without a minimal element, then we could produce an infinite play as follows: Start with some \(G\) in \(T\) . Because \(G\) is not minimal, there is some \(H\) in \(T\) with \(H < G\) , so there is some sequence of moves from \(G\) to \(H\) . Similarly, \(H\) is not minimal, so another game in \(T\) is reached from \(H\) . Continuing in this manner creates an infinite sequence of moves, which contradicts the ending condition.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果 \(S\) 存在一个非空子集 \(T\) 没有极小元，那么我们可以按照如下方式构造一个无限对局:从 \(T\) 中的某个 \(G\) 开始。因为 \(G\) 不是极小元，所以 \(T\) 中存在某个 \(H\) 满足 \(H < G\) ，因此存在从 \(G\) 到 \(H\) 的一系列走法。类似地， \(H\) 也不是极小元，所以从 \(H\) 又能到达 \(T\) 中的另一个对局。按照这种方式继续下去会产生一个无限的走法序列，这与终止条件相矛盾。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Background material: Partial orders}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{背景材料:偏序}
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 1.2 (Partial order, total order). A binary relation \(\leq\) on a set \(S\) is called a partial order if the following hold for all \(x,y,z\) in \(S\) :

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义 1.2(偏序、全序)。集合 \(S\) 上的二元关系 \(\leq\) 若对于 \(S\) 中的所有 \(x,y,z\) 都满足以下条件，则称其为偏序:
\end{tcolorbox}

\(x \leq  y\) and \(y \leq  z \Rightarrow  x \leq  z\;\left( { \leq  \text{ is transitive }}\right)\) ,

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(x \leq  y\) 和 \(y \leq  z \Rightarrow  x \leq  z\;\left( { \leq  \text{ is transitive }}\right)\) ，
\end{tcolorbox}

\(x \leq  x\) (≤ is reflexive), and

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(x \leq  x\) (≤ 是自反的)，并且
\end{tcolorbox}

\(x \leq  y\) and \(y \leq  x \Rightarrow  x = y\;\left( { \leq  \text{ is antisymmetric }}\right)\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(x \leq  y\) 和 \(y \leq  x \Rightarrow  x = y\;\left( { \leq  \text{ is antisymmetric }}\right)\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

If, in addition, for all \(x,y\) in \(S\)

\begin{tcolorbox}
此外，如果对于 \(S\) 中的所有 \(x,y\)
\end{tcolorbox}

\(x \leq  y\) or \(y \leq  x\;\left( { \leq  \text{ is total }}\right)\)

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(x \leq  y\) 或 \(y \leq  x\;\left( { \leq  \text{ is total }}\right)\)
\end{tcolorbox}

then \(\leq\) is called a total order .

\begin{tcolorbox}
那么 \(\leq\) 称为全序。
\end{tcolorbox}

For a given partial order \(\leq\) , we often say " \(x\) is less than or equal to \(y\) " if \(x \leq  y\) . We then define \(x < y\) (" \(x\) is less than \({y}^{\prime \prime }\) ) as follows:

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于给定的偏序 \(\leq\) ，如果 \(x \leq  y\) ，我们常说“ \(x\) 小于或等于 \(y\) ”。然后我们按如下方式定义 \(x < y\) (“ \(x\) 小于 \({y}^{\prime \prime }\) ”):
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
x < y\; \Leftrightarrow  \;x \leq  y\;\text{ and }\;x \neq  y. \tag{1.6}
\]

This relation \(<\) is also called the strict order that corresponds to \(\leq\) . Exercise 1.1 asks you to show how a partial order \(\leq\) can be defined from a relation \(<\) on \(S\) with suitable properties (transitivity and "irreflexivity") that define a strict order. Then \(\leq\) is obtained from \(<\) according to

\begin{tcolorbox}
这个关系 \(<\) 也称为与 \(\leq\) 对应的严格序。练习 1.1 要求你展示如何从集合 \(S\) 上具有合适性质(传递性和“非自反性”)的关系 \(<\) 定义一个偏序 \(\leq\) ，这些性质定义了一个严格序。然后根据以下方式从 \(<\) 得到 \(\leq\)
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
x \leq  y\; \Leftrightarrow  \;x < y\;\text{ or }\;x = y. \tag{1.7}
\]

An element \(x\) of \(S\) is called minimal if there is no \(y\) in \(S\) with \(y < x\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果 \(S\) 中不存在 \(y\) 满足 \(y < x\) ，则 \(S\) 中的元素 \(x\) 称为极小元。
\end{tcolorbox}

Any set \(S\) of rational or real numbers has the familiar relation \(\leq\) , which is a total order. The most important partial order that is usually not total is the set inclusion \(\subseteq\) on a set \(S\) of sets. Then elements \(x,y\) of \(S\) as used in Definition 1.2 are usually written with capital letters like \(A,B\) . The partial order is written with the symbol \(\subseteq\) where \(A \subseteq  B\) holds if \(A\) is a subset of \(B\) (which allows for \(A = B\) ). The inclusion order is not total because two sets may be incomparable, that is, neither \(A \subseteq  B\) nor \(B \subseteq  A\) hold, for example if \(A = \{ 1,2\}\) and \(B = \{ 2,3,4\}\) . If \(S\) is the set of all subsets of, say, the finite set \(\{ 1,\ldots ,n\}\) for some positive integer \(n\) , then \(S\) , partially ordered by \(\subseteq\) , has the empty set \(\varnothing\) as a unique minimal element. If \(S\) is the set of nonempty subsets of \(\{ 1,\ldots ,n\}\) , then its minimal elements are the singletons \(\{ 1\} ,\{ 2\} ,\ldots ,\{ n\}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
任何有理数或实数集 \(S\) 都有我们熟悉的关系 \(\leq\) ，它是一个全序。通常不是全序的最重要的偏序是集合族 \(S\) 上的集合包含关系 \(\subseteq\) 。那么定义 1.2 中使用的 \(S\) 的元素 \(x,y\) 通常用大写字母如 \(A,B\) 表示。偏序用符号 \(\subseteq\) 表示，其中如果 \(A\) 是 \(B\) 的子集(允许 \(A = B\) )，则 \(A \subseteq  B\) 成立。包含序不是全序，因为两个集合可能不可比较，即 \(A \subseteq  B\) 和 \(B \subseteq  A\) 都不成立，例如当 \(A = \{ 1,2\}\) 且 \(B = \{ 2,3,4\}\) 时。如果 \(S\) 是某个正整数 \(n\) 对应的有限集 \(\{ 1,\ldots ,n\}\) 的所有子集构成的集合，那么由 \(\subseteq\) 给出偏序的 \(S\) 有唯一的极小元，即空集 \(\varnothing\) 。如果 \(S\) 是 \(\{ 1,\ldots ,n\}\) 的非空子集构成的集合，那么它的极小元是单元素集 \(\{ 1\} ,\{ 2\} ,\ldots ,\{ n\}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

For any partial order with the property (1.8), the following theorem applies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于任何具有性质 (1.8) 的偏序，以下定理成立。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 1.3 (Top-down induction). Consider a set \(S\) with a partial order \(\leq\) that fulfills (1.8). Let \(P\left( x\right)\) be a statement that may be true or false for an element \(x\) of \(S\) , and assume that \(P\left( x\right)\) holds whenever \(P\left( y\right)\) holds for all \(y\) of \(S\) with \(y < x\) . Then \(P\left( x\right)\) is true for all \(x\) in \(S\) . In symbols, where " \(\forall x\) " means "for all elements \(x\) of \({S}^{\prime \prime }\) :

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理1.3(自上而下归纳)。考虑一个带有偏序 \(\leq\) 的集合 \(S\) ，该偏序满足 (1.8)。设 \(P\left( x\right)\) 是一个关于 \(S\) 中元素 \(x\) 的陈述，其可能为真也可能为假，并且假设只要对于 \(S\) 中所有满足 \(y < x\) 的 \(y\) ， \(P\left( y\right)\) 都成立时， \(P\left( x\right)\) 就成立。那么 \(P\left( x\right)\) 对于 \(S\) 中的所有 \(x\) 都为真。用符号表示，其中“ \(\forall x\) ”表示“对于 \({S}^{\prime \prime }\) 中的所有元素 \(x\) :
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\left( {\forall x : \left( {\forall y < x : P\left( y\right) }\right)  \Rightarrow  P\left( x\right) }\right)  \Rightarrow  \left( {\forall x : P\left( x\right) }\right) . \tag{1.9}
\]

Proof. Suppose, as assumed, that condition \(P\left( x\right)\) holds whenever it holds for all elements \(y\) of \(S\) with \(y < x\) . Consider the set \(T = \{ z \in  S \mid  P\left( z\right)\) is false \(\}\) . Then \(P\left( x\right)\) is true for all \(x\) in \(S\) if \(T\) is the empty set, so assume \(T\) is not empty. Let \(x\) be a minimal element of \(T\) , which exists by (1.8). Then all \(y\) in \(S\) with \(y < x\) do not belong to \(T\) , that is, \(P\left( y\right)\) is true. But, by assumption (1.9), this implies that \(P\left( x\right)\) is also true, which contradicts \(x \in  T\) . So \(T\) is empty, as claimed.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。假设，如所假定的，只要对于 \(S\) 中所有满足 \(y < x\) 的元素 \(y\) ，条件 \(P\left( x\right)\) 都成立。考虑集合 \(T = \{ z \in  S \mid  P\left( z\right)\) 为假 \(\}\) 。如果 \(T\) 是空集，那么对于 \(S\) 中的所有 \(x\) ， \(P\left( x\right)\) 都为真，所以假设 \(T\) 不是空集。设 \(x\) 是 \(T\) 的一个极小元(根据 (1.8) 它存在)。那么 \(S\) 中所有满足 \(y < x\) 的 \(y\) 都不属于 \(T\) ，即 \(P\left( y\right)\) 为真。但是，根据假设 (1.9)，这意味着 \(P\left( x\right)\) 也为真，这与 \(x \in  T\) 矛盾。所以，如所声称的， \(T\) 是空集。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 1.3 is called a method of induction because it assumes that a property \(P\left( x\right)\) can be inferred from the fact that \(P\left( y\right)\) is true for all \(y\) that are smaller than \(x\) . Then, assuming (1.8), the property holds for all \(x\) in \(S\) . An important application of this induction method is for proving the prime number decomposition theorem \(P\left( x\right)\) , which states that if \(x\) is an integer and \(x \geq  2\) , then \(x\) can be written as a product of prime numbers. In this induction proof, one first shows that \(x\) has a prime factor \(p\) , where either \(x = p\) (in which case we have shown \(P\left( x\right)\) and are done) or \(x = p \cdot  y\) for some integer \(y\) with \(2 \leq  y < x\) . In the latter case, \(P\left( y\right)\) holds by the "inductive hypothesis", that is, \(y = {p}_{1}{p}_{2}\cdots {p}_{k}\) for some prime numbers \({p}_{1},{p}_{2},\ldots ,{p}_{k}\) . Hence, \(x = p{p}_{1}{p}_{2}\cdots {p}_{k}\) , which also shows \(P\left( x\right)\) . By Theorem 1.3, \(P\left( x\right)\) holds throughout. The induction can be applied because any nonempty set of positive integers has a minimal element.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理1.3被称为一种归纳方法，因为它假设一个性质 \(P\left( x\right)\) 可以从以下事实推出:对于所有小于 \(x\) 的 \(y\) ， \(P\left( y\right)\) 都为真。然后，假设 (1.8) 成立，该性质对于 \(S\) 中的所有 \(x\) 都成立。这种归纳方法的一个重要应用是证明质数分解定理 \(P\left( x\right)\) ，该定理指出，如果 \(x\) 是一个整数且 \(x \geq  2\) ，那么 \(x\) 可以写成质数的乘积。在这个归纳证明中，首先证明 \(x\) 有一个质因数 \(p\) ，其中要么 \(x = p\) (在这种情况下我们就证明了 \(P\left( x\right)\) ，证明完成)，要么对于某个满足 \(2 \leq  y < x\) 的整数 \(y\) 有 \(x = p \cdot  y\) 。在后一种情况下，根据“归纳假设”， \(P\left( y\right)\) 成立，即对于某些质数 \({p}_{1},{p}_{2},\ldots ,{p}_{k}\) 有 \(y = {p}_{1}{p}_{2}\cdots {p}_{k}\) 。因此， \(x = p{p}_{1}{p}_{2}\cdots {p}_{k}\) ，这也证明了 \(P\left( x\right)\) 。根据定理1.3， \(P\left( x\right)\) 始终成立。可以应用这种归纳法是因为任何非空的正整数集合都有一个极小元。
\end{tcolorbox}

The method is called "top-down" induction because we start from the "top" (here the element \(x\) ) and use the property \(P\left( y\right)\) in some way for elements \(y\) that are smaller than \(x\) , where we typically encounter \(y\) without knowing how much smaller \(y\) is compared to \(x\) , as in the prime number decomposition theorem. In contrast, ordinary induction for natural numbers \(n\) proceeds typically by proving \(P\left( 1\right)\) and then the implication \(P\left( n\right)  \Rightarrow  P\left( {n + 1}\right)\) , that is, one step at a time, which then implies that \(P\left( n\right)\) holds for all natural numbers \(n\) . Top-down induction is more powerful; it also applies to partial orders and not just to the totally ordered natural numbers (where it is often called "strong induction").

\begin{tcolorbox}
这种方法被称为“自上而下”归纳法，因为我们从“顶部”(这里是元素 \(x\) )开始，并以某种方式对小于 \(x\) 的元素 \(y\) 使用性质 \(P\left( y\right)\) ，通常我们遇到 \(y\) 时并不知道它比 \(x\) 小多少，就像在质数分解定理中一样。相比之下，自然数 \(n\) 的普通归纳法通常是先证明 \(P\left( 1\right)\) ，然后证明蕴含关系 \(P\left( n\right)  \Rightarrow  P\left( {n + 1}\right)\) ，即一次一步，这意味着 \(P\left( n\right)\) 对所有自然数 \(n\) 都成立。自上而下归纳法更强大；它不仅适用于全序的自然数(在那里它通常被称为“强归纳法”)，也适用于偏序。
\end{tcolorbox}

Where did the base case go, which is normally proved separately in an induction proof? The base case states that \(P\left( x\right)\) holds for any minimal element \(x\) (if \(\leq\) is total, then there is only one minimal element). The answer is that the base case is covered by the assumption in (1.9): The left-hand side of the implication (1.9) says that

\begin{tcolorbox}
通常在归纳证明中单独证明的基础情况去哪里了呢？基础情况表明 \(P\left( x\right)\) 对任何极小元 \(x\) 都成立(如果 \(\leq\) 是全序的，那么只有一个极小元)。答案是基础情况包含在 (1.9) 的假设中:蕴含关系 (1.9) 的左边表示
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\left( {\forall y < x : P\left( y\right) }\right)  \Rightarrow  P\left( x\right)  \tag{1.10}
\]

holds for all \(x\) , in particular for all minimal \(x\) . But then \(\left( {\forall y < x : P\left( y\right) }\right)\) is vacuously true because there is no \(y\) with \(y < x\) . Therefore, for minimal \(x\) , condition (1.10) means that \(P\left( x\right)\) is true, which is the base case.

\begin{tcolorbox}
对所有 \(x\) 都成立，特别是对所有极小的 \(x\) 。但此时 \(\left( {\forall y < x : P\left( y\right) }\right)\) 显然为真，因为不存在满足 \(y < x\) 的 \(y\) 。因此，对于极小的 \(x\) ，条件 (1.10) 意味着 \(P\left( x\right)\) 为真，这就是基础情况。
\end{tcolorbox}

As a proof technique, we can often use Theorem 1.3 generally without any case distinctions about whether the considered elements are minimal or not. It also allows using a property about games recursively by referring to the same property applied to simpler games.

\begin{tcolorbox}
作为一种证明技巧，我们通常可以普遍使用定理 1.3 ，而无需区分所考虑的元素是否为极小元。它还允许通过引用应用于更简单博弈的相同性质来递归地使用关于博弈的性质。
\end{tcolorbox}

Using top-down induction, we can now prove Lemma 1.1, which states that any game \(G\) is either winning or losing. Assume that this holds for all games that are simpler than \(G\) , in particular the options of \(G\) . If all of these options are winning, then \(G\) is a losing position, because the other player can force a win no matter which move the player makes in \(G\) . If not all options of \(G\) are winning, then one of them, say \(H\) , is losing, and by moving to \(H\) the player forces a win, hence \(G\) is a winning position. This argument is not in any way deeper than what we said before. Theorem 1.3 merely asserts that it is rigorous (and that it uses the ending condition).

\begin{tcolorbox}
使用自上而下归纳法，我们现在可以证明引理 1.1 ，该引理指出任何博弈 \(G\) 要么是必胜局势，要么是失败位置。假设这对所有比 \(G\) 更简单的博弈都成立，特别是 \(G\) 的选项。如果所有这些选项都是必胜局势，那么 \(G\) 是一个失败位置，因为无论玩家在 \(G\) 中采取什么行动，另一个玩家都能迫使获胜。如果 \(G\) 的选项并非都是必胜局势，那么其中一个，比如 \(H\) ，是失败位置，通过移动到 \(H\) ，玩家可以迫使获胜，因此 \(G\) 是一个必胜局势。这个论证并不比我们之前所说的更深刻。定理 1.3 只是断言它是严谨的(并且它使用了终止条件)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{1.4 Game Sums and Equivalence of Games}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{1.4 博弈之和与博弈等价性}
\end{tcolorbox}

Combinatorial games often "decompose" into parts in which players can move independently, and the players then have to decide in which part to make their move. This is captured by the important concept of a sum of games.

\begin{tcolorbox}
组合博弈通常会“分解”成玩家可以独立行动的部分，然后玩家必须决定在哪个部分行动。这可以通过博弈之和这个重要概念来体现。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 1.4. Suppose that \(G\) and \(H\) are game positions with options (positions reached by one move) \({G}_{1},\ldots ,{G}_{k}\) and \({H}_{1},\ldots ,{H}_{m}\) , respectively. Then the options of the game sum \(G + H\) are

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义 1.4 。假设 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 是博弈位置，它们的选项(通过一步行动到达的位置)分别是 \({G}_{1},\ldots ,{G}_{k}\) 和 \({H}_{1},\ldots ,{H}_{m}\) 。那么博弈之和 \(G + H\) 的选项是
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{G}_{1} + H,\ldots ,{G}_{k} + H,\;G + {H}_{1},\ldots ,G + {H}_{m}\text{ . } \tag{1.11}
\]

The first list of options \({G}_{1} + H,\ldots ,{G}_{k} + H\) in (1.11) simply means that the player makes his move in \(G\) , the second list \(G + {H}_{1},\ldots ,G + {H}_{m}\) that he makes his move in \(H\) ; the other part of the game sum remains untouched. As an example, a Nim position is simply the game sum of its individual Nim heaps, because the player moves in exactly one of the heaps.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(1.11) 中的第一组选项 \({G}_{1} + H,\ldots ,{G}_{k} + H\) 仅仅意味着玩家在 \(G\) 中行动，第二组选项 \(G + {H}_{1},\ldots ,G + {H}_{m}\) 意味着玩家在 \(H\) 中行动；博弈之和的另一部分保持不变。例如，一个尼姆游戏位置仅仅是其各个尼姆堆的博弈之和，因为玩家恰好可以在其中一个堆中行动。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 1.4 is a recursive definition, because the game sum is defined in terms of its options, which are themselves game sums (but they are simpler games).

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义 1.4 是一个递归定义，因为博弈之和是根据其选项来定义的，而这些选项本身也是博弈之和(但它们是更简单的博弈)。
\end{tcolorbox}

The sum of games turns out to define an abelian group on the (appropriately defined) set of games. It is a commutative and associative operation: for any games \(G,H,J,\)

\begin{tcolorbox}
事实证明，博弈之和在(适当定义的)博弈集合上定义了一个阿贝尔群。它是一个满足交换律和结合律的运算:对于任何博弈 \(G,H,J,\)
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
G + H = H + G\;\text{ and }\;\left( {G + H}\right)  + J = G + \left( {H + J}\right) . \tag{1.12}
\]

The first condition (commutativity) holds because the order of the options of a game, used in (1.11), does not matter. The second condition (associativity) holds because both \(\left( {G + H}\right)  + J\) and \(G + \left( {H + J}\right)\) mean in effect that the player decides to move in \(G\) , in \(H\) , or in \(J\) , leaving the other two parts of the game sum unchanged. We can therefore assume the equalities (1.12). More generally, in a sum of several games \({G}_{1},\ldots ,{G}_{n}\) the player moves in exactly one of these games, which does not depend on how these games are arranged, so that we can write this sum unambiguously without parentheses as \({G}_{1} + \cdots  + {G}_{n}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
第一个条件(交换律)成立，因为在(1.11)中使用的博弈选项的顺序无关紧要。第二个条件(结合律)成立，因为 \(\left( {G + H}\right)  + J\) 和 \(G + \left( {H + J}\right)\) 实际上都意味着玩家决定在 \(G\) 、 \(H\) 或 \(J\) 中移动，而博弈之和的另外两部分保持不变。因此，我们可以假设等式(1.12)成立。更一般地，在几个博弈 \({G}_{1},\ldots ,{G}_{n}\) 的和中，玩家恰好只在其中一个博弈中移动，这与这些博弈的排列方式无关，所以我们可以明确地将这个和写成 \({G}_{1} + \cdots  + {G}_{n}\) 而无需括号。
\end{tcolorbox}

The losing game 0 which has no options is a zero (neutral element) for game sums: It fulfills \(G + 0 = G\) for any game \(G\) , because the game 0 is "invisible" when added to \(G\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
没有选项的必败博弈 0 是博弈之和的零元(中性元素):对于任何博弈 \(G\) ，它满足 \(G + 0 = G\) ，因为当 0 与 \(G\) 相加时，博弈 0 是“不可见的”。
\end{tcolorbox}

In order to obtain a group, every game \(G\) needs to have a "negative" game \(- G\) so that \(G + \left( {-G}\right)  = 0\) . However, this equality cannot hold as stated as soon as the game \(G\) has options, because then the game \(G + \left( {-G}\right)\) also has options but 0 has none. Instead, we need a more general condition

\begin{tcolorbox}
为了构成一个群，每个博弈 \(G\) 都需要有一个“负”博弈 \(- G\) ，使得 \(G + \left( {-G}\right)  = 0\) 成立。然而，一旦博弈 \(G\) 有选项，这个等式就不能按所述方式成立，因为此时博弈 \(G + \left( {-G}\right)\) 也有选项，而 0 没有选项。相反，我们需要一个更一般的条件
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
G + \left( {-G}\right)  \equiv  0, \tag{1.13}
\]

where \(G \equiv  H\) means that the two games \(G\) and \(H\) are equivalent, according to the following definition.

\begin{tcolorbox}
其中 \(G \equiv  H\) 表示根据以下定义，两个博弈 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 是等价的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 1.5. Two games \(G,H\) are called equivalent, written \(G \equiv  H\) , if and only if for any other game \(J\) , the game sum \(G + J\) is losing if and only if \(H + J\) is losing.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义 1.5:当且仅当对于任何其他博弈 \(J\) ，博弈之和 \(G + J\) 是必败的当且仅当 \(H + J\) 是必败的时，两个博弈 \(G,H\) 被称为等价的，记为 \(G \equiv  H\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 1.6. The relation \(\equiv\) in Definition 1.5 is indeed an equivalence relation between games, that is, it is reflexive, symmetric and transitive.

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理 1.6:定义 1.5 中的关系 \(\equiv\) 确实是博弈之间的一种等价关系(关系的等价性)，即它是自反的、对称的和传递的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Reflexivity means \(G \equiv  G\) for any game \(G\) , which holds trivially. Symmetry means that if \(G \equiv  H\) then \(H \equiv  G\) , which holds also by definition. Suppose that \(G \equiv  H\) and \(H \equiv  K\) for games \(G,H,K\) . Transitivity means that then \(G \equiv  K\) . Consider any other game \(J\) . If \(G + J\) is losing then \(H + J\) is losing because \(G \equiv  H\) , and then \(K + J\) is losing because \(H \equiv  K\) . Conversely, if \(K + J\) is losing then \(H + J\) is losing because \(H \equiv  K\) , and then \(G + J\) is losing because \(G \equiv  H\) . Hence, \(G + J\) is losing if and only if \(K + J\) is losing, that is, \(G \equiv  K\) . This shows that \(\equiv\) is transitive.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明:自反性意味着对于任何博弈 \(G\) 有 \(G \equiv  G\) ，这显然成立。对称性意味着如果 \(G \equiv  H\) 则 \(H \equiv  G\) ，这也根据定义成立。假设对于博弈 \(G,H,K\) 有 \(G \equiv  H\) 和 \(H \equiv  K\) 。传递性意味着 \(G \equiv  K\) 。考虑任何其他博弈 \(J\) 。如果 \(G + J\) 是必败的，那么由于 \(G \equiv  H\) ， \(H + J\) 是必败的，又因为 \(H \equiv  K\) ， \(K + J\) 是必败的。反之，如果 \(K + J\) 是必败的，那么由于 \(H \equiv  K\) ， \(H + J\) 是必败的，再由于 \(G \equiv  H\) ， \(G + J\) 是必败的。因此， \(G + J\) 是必败的当且仅当 \(K + J\) 是必败的，即 \(G \equiv  K\) 。这表明 \(\equiv\) 是传递的。
\end{tcolorbox}

By taking \(J = 0\) in Definition 1.5, if the games \(G\) and \(H\) are equivalent then either both are losing or both are winning. Equivalence of \(G\) and \(H\) means that the games \(G\) and \(H\) always have the same outcome (winning or losing) and that this property is preserved in any game sum \(G + J\) and \(H + J\) for any other game \(J\) . This is much stronger than just belonging to the same outcome class. For example, if \(G\) and \(H\) are Nim heaps of different sizes, then both games are winning but they are not equivalent. We state this as a lemma.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在定义 1.5 中取 \(J = 0\) ，如果博弈 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 是等价的，那么它们要么都是必败的，要么都是必胜的。 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 等价意味着博弈 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 总是有相同的结果(必胜或必败)，并且对于任何其他博弈 \(J\) ，这个性质在任何博弈之和 \(G + J\) 和 \(H + J\) 中都保持不变。这比仅仅属于同一个结果类要强得多。例如，如果 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 是不同大小的尼姆堆(Nim heap)，那么这两个博弈都是必胜的，但它们并不等价。我们将此表述为一个引理。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 1.7. Two Nim heaps are equivalent if and only if they have equal size.

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理 1.7:两个尼姆堆(Nim heap)等价当且仅当它们的大小相等。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Let \(G\) and \(H\) be two Nim heaps of different sizes, and consider \(J = G\) in Definition 1.5. Then \(G + G\) is a losing game, but \(H + G\) is winning. This shows that \(G\) and \(H\) are not equivalent. If \(G\) and \(H\) are Nim heaps of equal size then they are the same game and therefore equivalent.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明:设 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 是两个不同大小的尼姆堆(Nim heap)，考虑定义 1.5 中的 \(J = G\) 。那么 \(G + G\) 是一个必败博弈，但 \(H + G\) 是必胜的。这表明 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 不等价。如果 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 是大小相等的尼姆堆(Nim heap)，那么它们是同一个博弈，因此是等价的。
\end{tcolorbox}

However, any two losing games are equivalent, because they are all equivalent to the game 0 that has no options.

\begin{tcolorbox}
然而，任意两个必败游戏都是等价的，因为它们都等价于没有选项(option)的游戏 0。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 1.8. \(G\) is a losing game if and only if \(G \equiv  0\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理 1.8。 \(G\) 是必败游戏当且仅当 \(G \equiv  0\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. If \(G \equiv  0\) then \(G\) is losing because 0 is losing, using \(J = 0\) in Definition 1.5. So it remains to prove the "only if" part.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。若 \(G \equiv  0\) ，则根据定义 1.5 中的 \(J = 0\) ，由于 0 是必败游戏，所以 \(G\) 是必败的。因此，只需证明“仅当”部分。
\end{tcolorbox}

Let \(G\) be a losing game. We want to show \(G \equiv  0\) , that is, for any other game \(J\) , the game \(G + J\) is losing if and only if \(0 + J\) is losing. Because \(0 + J = J\) , this amounts to the two implications

\begin{tcolorbox}
设 \(G\) 是必败游戏。我们要证明 \(G \equiv  0\) ，即对于任意其他游戏 \(J\) ，游戏 \(G + J\) 是必败的当且仅当 \(0 + J\) 是必败的。因为 \(0 + J = J\) ，这等价于两个蕴含关系
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
G + J\text{ is losing } \Rightarrow  J\text{ is losing } \tag{1.14}
\]

and (we add the assumption that \(G\) is losing for symmetry)

\begin{tcolorbox}
并且(为了对称，我们添加假设 \(G\) 是失败位置)
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
G\text{ and }J\text{ are losing } \Rightarrow  G + J\text{ is losing. } \tag{1.15}
\]

Condition (1.15) is rather obvious: If \(G\) and \(J\) are both losing games, there is no way to win the game sum \(G + J\) because the second player always has a counter-move. We prove (1.15) formally by top-down induction. Let \(G\) and \(J\) be losing. We want to show that \(G + J\) is losing, which means that every option of \(G + J\) is a winning game. Any such option is of the form \({G}^{\prime } + J\) where is \({G}^{\prime }\) is an option of \(G\) , or of the form \(G + {J}^{\prime }\) where \({J}^{\prime }\) is an option of \(J\) . Consider the first type of option \({G}^{\prime } + J\) . Because \(G\) is a losing game, its option \({G}^{\prime }\) is a winning position, so \({G}^{\prime }\) has some option \({G}^{\prime \prime }\) that is a losing position. Because \({G}^{\prime \prime }\) is a losing game that is simpler than \(G\) , we can assume that (1.15) holds, that is, \({G}^{\prime \prime }\) and \(J\) are losing \(\Rightarrow  {G}^{\prime \prime } + J\) is losing. So the option \({G}^{\prime } + J\) is winning because it has the losing option \({G}^{\prime \prime } + J\) , and this was an arbitrary option of \(G + J\) with a move to \({G}^{\prime } + J\) in the first game \(G\) of the sum \(G + J\) . The same applies for any option of \(G + J\) of the form \(G + {J}^{\prime }\) where \({J}^{\prime }\) is an option of \(J\) . So all options of \(G + J\) are winning games, and \(G + J\) is therefore a losing game, as claimed. This concludes the proof of (1.15).

\begin{tcolorbox}
条件 (1.15) 相当明显:如果 \(G\) 和 \(J\) 都是失败的博弈，那么就没有办法在博弈之和 \(G + J\) 中获胜，因为第二个玩家总是有应对的策略。我们通过自上而下归纳(top-down induction)正式证明 (1.15)。设 \(G\) 和 \(J\) 是失败的。我们要证明 \(G + J\) 是失败的，这意味着 \(G + J\) 的每个选项(option)都是必胜局势(winning position)。任何这样的选项都具有 \({G}^{\prime } + J\) 的形式，其中 \({G}^{\prime }\) 是 \(G\) 的一个选项，或者具有 \(G + {J}^{\prime }\) 的形式，其中 \({J}^{\prime }\) 是 \(J\) 的一个选项。考虑第一种类型的选项 \({G}^{\prime } + J\) 。因为 \(G\) 是一个失败的博弈，它的选项 \({G}^{\prime }\) 是一个必胜局势，所以 \({G}^{\prime }\) 有某个选项 \({G}^{\prime \prime }\) 是失败位置。因为 \({G}^{\prime \prime }\) 是一个比 \(G\) 更简单的失败博弈，我们可以假设 (1.15) 成立，即 \({G}^{\prime \prime }\) 和 \(J\) 是失败的， \(\Rightarrow  {G}^{\prime \prime } + J\) 是失败的。所以选项 \({G}^{\prime } + J\) 是必胜的，因为它有失败的选项 \({G}^{\prime \prime } + J\) ，并且这是博弈之和 \(G + J\) 的第一个博弈 \(G\) 中向 \({G}^{\prime } + J\) 移动的 \(G + J\) 的任意一个选项。同样的情况适用于 \(G + J\) 的任何形式为 \(G + {J}^{\prime }\) 的选项，其中 \({J}^{\prime }\) 是 \(J\) 的一个选项。所以 \(G + J\) 的所有选项都是必胜的博弈，因此 \(G + J\) 是一个失败的博弈，正如所声称的那样。这就完成了 (1.15) 的证明。
\end{tcolorbox}

We now show (1.14). Suppose that \(G + J\) is losing but that \(J\) is winning. Then there is a winning move to some option \({J}^{\prime }\) of \(J\) where \({J}^{\prime }\) is a losing game. But then by (1.15), which we just proved, this implies that \(G + {J}^{\prime }\) is a losing game, which is an option of \(G + J\) , and would represent a winning move from \(G + J\) , contradicting that \(G + J\) is losing. This shows that (1.14) holds, which completes the proof.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们现在证明 (1.14)。假设 \(G + J\) 是失败的，但 \(J\) 是必胜的。那么存在一个必胜步(winning move)到 \(J\) 的某个选项 \({J}^{\prime }\) ，其中 \({J}^{\prime }\) 是一个失败的博弈。但根据我们刚刚证明的 (1.15)，这意味着 \(G + {J}^{\prime }\) 是一个失败的博弈，它是 \(G + J\) 的一个选项，并且代表了从 \(G + J\) 出发的一个必胜步，这与 \(G + J\) 是失败的相矛盾。这表明 (1.14) 成立，从而完成了证明。
\end{tcolorbox}

The next lemma shows that adding a game preserves equivalence between two games \(G\) and \(H\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
下一个引理表明，添加一个博弈可以保持两个博弈 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 之间的等价性。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 1.9. For all games \(G,H,K\) ,

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理 1.9. 对于所有的博弈 \(G,H,K\) ，
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
G \equiv  H \Rightarrow  G + K \equiv  H + K. \tag{1.16}
\]

Proof. Let \(G \equiv  H\) . We show that \(G + K \equiv  H + K\) , that is, \(\left( {G + K}\right)  + J\) is losing \(\Leftrightarrow\)  \(\left( {H + K}\right)  + J\) is losing for any other game \(J\) . By (1.12), this means \(G + \left( {K + J}\right)\) is losing \(\Leftrightarrow  H + \left( {K + J}\right)\) is losing, which holds because \(G\) and \(H\) are equivalent.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。设 \(G \equiv  H\) 。我们证明 \(G + K \equiv  H + K\) ，即对于任何其他博弈 \(J\) ， \(\left( {G + K}\right)  + J\) 是失败的 \(\Leftrightarrow\)  \(\left( {H + K}\right)  + J\) 是失败的。根据 (1.12)，这意味着 \(G + \left( {K + J}\right)\) 是失败的 \(\Leftrightarrow  H + \left( {K + J}\right)\) 是失败的，这是成立的，因为 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 是等价的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Using equivalence, any losing game \(Z\) can take the role of a zero (neutral) element for game sums, that is,

\begin{tcolorbox}
利用等价性，任何失败的博弈 \(Z\) 都可以充当博弈之和的零(中性)元素，即
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
Z\text{ is losing } \Rightarrow  G + Z \equiv  G \tag{1.17}
\]

for any game \(G\) . The reason is that any move in the component \(Z\) of the game sum \(G + Z\) has an immediate counter-move back to a losing position, which will eventually become the game 0 without options. Therefore, adding \(Z\) does not affect if \(G\) is winning or losing, and this is also preserved when adding any other game \(J\) . In particular, \(Z \equiv  0\) for any losing game \(Z\) , by taking \(G = 0\) in (1.17). We state (1.17) as a lemma, which is proved easily with the help of the previous lemmas.

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于任意博弈 \(G\) 。原因在于，博弈之和 \(G + Z\) 的分量 \(Z\) 中的任何一步行动都有一个直接的反制行动，可回到失败位置，最终该博弈将变为没有选项的博弈 0。因此，添加 \(Z\) 不会影响 \(G\) 是必胜还是必败，并且在添加任何其他博弈 \(J\) 时，这一性质也会保留。特别地，对于任何失败博弈 \(Z\) ，通过在 (1.17) 中取 \(G = 0\) ，有 \(Z \equiv  0\) 。我们将 (1.17) 表述为一个引理，借助之前的引理很容易证明该引理。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 1.10. Let \(Z\) be a losing game. Then \(G + Z \equiv  G\) for any game \(G\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理 1.10。设 \(Z\) 为失败博弈。那么对于任意博弈 \(G\) ，有 \(G + Z \equiv  G\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. By Lemma 1.8, \(Z \equiv  0\) , and by (1.16) \(G + Z = Z + G \equiv  0 + G = G\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。根据引理 1.8，有 \(Z \equiv  0\) ，根据 (1.16) 有 \(G + Z = Z + G \equiv  0 + G = G\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The next lemma implies that any game \(G\) has a negative game \(- G\) so that (1.13) holds. For impartial games, this negative game is \(G\) itself, that is, \(G + G\) is always a losing position. This is the copycat principle for impartial games: the second player always has a move left, by copying the move of the first player. If \({G}^{\prime }\) is an option of \(G\) and the first player moves in \(G + G\) to \({G}^{\prime } + G\) or \(G + {G}^{\prime }\) , then the second player responds by moving to \({G}^{\prime } + {G}^{\prime }\) . This continues until the first player has run out of moves and loses.

\begin{tcolorbox}
下一个引理表明，任意博弈 \(G\) 都有一个负博弈 \(- G\) ，使得 (1.13) 成立。对于无偏博弈，这个负博弈就是 \(G\) 本身，即 \(G + G\) 总是一个失败位置。这就是无偏博弈的模仿原则:第二名玩家总是可以通过模仿第一名玩家的行动来保留一步行动。如果 \({G}^{\prime }\) 是 \(G\) 的一个选项，并且第一名玩家在 \(G + G\) 中行动到 \({G}^{\prime } + G\) 或 \(G + {G}^{\prime }\) ，那么第二名玩家通过行动到 \({G}^{\prime } + {G}^{\prime }\) 来回应。这一过程会一直持续，直到第一名玩家无步可走并失败。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 1.11 (The copycat principle). \(G + G \equiv  0\) for any impartial game \(G\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理 1.11(模仿原则)。对于任意无偏博弈 \(G\) ，有 \(G + G \equiv  0\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Let \(G\) be an impartial game. By Lemma 1.8, we have to show that \(G + G\) is a losing game. We prove this by top-down induction, by showing that every option of \(G + G\) is a winning game. Any such option of \(G + G\) is of the form \({G}^{\prime } + G\) or \(G + {G}^{\prime }\) for an option \({G}^{\prime }\) of \(G\) . Then the next player has the winning move to the game \({G}^{\prime } + {G}^{\prime }\) which is losing, by the inductive assumption because \({G}^{\prime } + {G}^{\prime }\) is simpler than \(G + G\) . So \(G + G\) is indeed a losing game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。设 \(G\) 为无偏博弈。根据引理 1.8，我们需要证明 \(G + G\) 是一个失败博弈。我们通过自上而下归纳法来证明这一点，即证明 \(G + G\) 的每个选项都是必胜博弈。 \(G + G\) 的任何这样的选项都具有 \({G}^{\prime } + G\) 或 \(G + {G}^{\prime }\) 的形式，其中 \({G}^{\prime }\) 是 \(G\) 的一个选项。然后，下一名玩家有一个必胜步行动到博弈 \({G}^{\prime } + {G}^{\prime }\) ，根据归纳假设， \({G}^{\prime } + {G}^{\prime }\) 是失败的，因为 \({G}^{\prime } + {G}^{\prime }\) 比 \(G + G\) 更简单。所以 \(G + G\) 确实是一个失败博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 1.10 states that any losing game in a game sum is irrelevant for the outcome class of that sum. For example, by Lemma 1.11 a losing game of this sort may be the sum of two equal games in a larger game sum. This can simplify game sums substantially. For example, in the Nim position1,2,3,5,5,5, the game sum of two Nim heaps of size 5 is a losing game, and the position1,2,3is also losing, as we saw earlier, so the overall position is equivalent to a single Nim heap of size 5 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理 1.10 表明，博弈之和中的任何失败博弈对于该和的结果类都是无关紧要的。例如，根据引理 1.11，这种失败博弈可能是一个更大博弈之和中两个相等博弈的和。这可以大大简化博弈之和。例如，在尼姆游戏位置 1,2,3,5,5,5 中，两个大小为 5 的尼姆堆的博弈之和是一个失败博弈，并且如我们之前所见，位置 1,2,3 也是失败的，所以整个位置等价于一个大小为 5 的单个尼姆堆。
\end{tcolorbox}

The following condition is very useful to prove that two games are equivalent.

\begin{tcolorbox}
以下条件对于证明两个博弈等价非常有用。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 1.12. Two impartial games \(G,H\) are equivalent if and only if \(G + H \equiv  0\) . Proof. If \(G \equiv  H\) , then by (1.16) and Lemma 1.11, \(G + H \equiv  H + H \equiv  0\) . Conversely, \(G + H \equiv  0\) implies \(G \equiv  G + H + H \equiv  0 + H = H\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理 1.12。两个无偏博弈 \(G,H\) 等价当且仅当 \(G + H \equiv  0\) 。证明。如果 \(G \equiv  H\) ，那么根据 (1.16) 和引理 1.11，有 \(G + H \equiv  H + H \equiv  0\) 。反之， \(G + H \equiv  0\) 意味着 \(G \equiv  G + H + H \equiv  0 + H = H\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The following lemma states that two games \(G\) and \(H\) are equivalent if all their options are equivalent.

\begin{tcolorbox}
以下引理表明，如果两个博弈 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 的所有选项都等价，那么这两个博弈等价。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 1.13. Consider two games \(G\) and \(H\) so that for every option of \(G\) there is an equivalent option of \(H\) and vice versa. Then \(G \equiv  H\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理 1.13。考虑两个博弈 \(G\) 和 \(H\) ，使得对于 \(G\) 的每个选项，都有 \(H\) 的一个等价选项，反之亦然。那么 \(G \equiv  H\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. We show that \(G + H\) is losing, by showing that every option of \(G + H\) is a winning game. Consider such an option \({G}^{\prime } + H\) where \({G}^{\prime }\) is an option of \(G\) . By assumption, there is an option \({H}^{\prime }\) of \(H\) with \({G}^{\prime } \equiv  {H}^{\prime }\) , that is, \({G}^{\prime } + {H}^{\prime } \equiv  0\) by Lemma 1.12, so \({G}^{\prime } + {H}^{\prime }\) is losing. Moving there defines a winning move in \({G}^{\prime } + H\) . By the same argument, any option \(G + {H}^{\prime }\) of \(G + H\) is winning.
