Hence, every option of \(G + H\) is winning, so \(G + H\) is a losing game. Therefore, \(G + H \equiv  0\) , and \(G \equiv  H\) by Lemma 1.12.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。我们通过证明 \(G + H\) 的每个选项都是必胜博弈，来证明 \(G + H\) 是失败博弈。考虑这样一个选项 \({G}^{\prime } + H\) ，其中 \({G}^{\prime }\) 是 \(G\) 的一个选项。根据假设，存在 \(H\) 的一个选项 \({H}^{\prime }\) 满足 \({G}^{\prime } \equiv  {H}^{\prime }\) ，即根据引理 1.12 有 \({G}^{\prime } + {H}^{\prime } \equiv  0\) ，所以 \({G}^{\prime } + {H}^{\prime }\) 是失败博弈。移动到该位置定义了 \({G}^{\prime } + H\) 中的一个必胜步。通过相同的论证， \(G + H\) 的任何选项 \(G + {H}^{\prime }\) 都是必胜的。因此， \(G + H\) 的每个选项都是必胜的，所以 \(G + H\) 是一个失败博弈。所以， \(G + H \equiv  0\) ，并且根据引理 1.12 有 \(G \equiv  H\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Note that Lemma 1.13 states only a sufficient condition for the equivalence of \(G\) and \(H\) . Games can be equivalent without that property. For example, \(G + G\) is equivalent to 0, but \(G + G\) has in general many options and 0 has none.

\begin{tcolorbox}
注意，引理 1.13 仅陈述了 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 等价的一个充分条件。博弈在不满足该性质的情况下也可能等价。例如， \(G + G\) 等价于 0，但一般来说 \(G + G\) 有很多选项，而 0 没有选项。
\end{tcolorbox}

We conclude this section with a comment on the abelian group of impartial games. The group operation is the sum + of games. In order for the group to work, we have to identify equivalent games, which are "compatible" with game sums by Lemma 1.9. Any losing game (which is equivalent to the game 0 with no options) is a zero element of the group operation. Every game is its own negative. Essentially (although we will not prove it that way), in our context the only groups with this property are given by the set of binary vectors with component-wise addition modulo 2. This may serve as an intuition why the optimal strategy of Nim as in (1.3) uses the binary representation based on powers of two (and not powers of three, say). Moreover, Nim plays a central game for all impartial games. We will prove this directly, without any reference to abelian groups, in the next section.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们以对无偏博弈的阿贝尔群(abelian group)的评论来结束本节。群运算为博弈的和 +。为了使该群成立，我们必须识别等价博弈，根据引理 1.9，这些等价博弈与博弈之和是“相容的”。任何失败博弈(它等价于没有选项的博弈 0)是群运算的零元。每个博弈都是其自身的逆元。本质上(尽管我们不会这样证明)，在我们的上下文中，具有此性质的唯一群是由按分量模 2 加法的二进制向量集给出的。这可以解释为什么如 (1.3) 中尼姆游戏(Nim)的最优策略使用基于 2 的幂(而不是 3 的幂等)的二进制表示。此外，尼姆游戏在所有无偏博弈中起着核心作用。我们将在下一节直接证明这一点，而不涉及阿贝尔群。
\end{tcolorbox}

A second comment concerns misère games, where the last player to move loses. One losing position in misère Nim is a single heap with one token in it. However, adding such a heap \(Z\) , say, to a game \(G\) completely reverses the outcome class of \(G\) because the token can just be removed, so we certainly do not have \(G + Z \equiv  G\) as in Lemma 1.10, no matter how the equivalence \(\equiv\) is defined. Also, 1,1is a winning position but2,2is a losing position in misère Nim, so there is no easy copycat principle for misère games. Although misère Nim is well understood (see Exercise 1.3), general misère games are not. In contrast, game sums have a nice structure with the normal play convention.

\begin{tcolorbox}
第二个评论涉及反常博弈(misère games)，即最后行动的玩家失败的博弈。反向尼姆游戏(misère Nim)中的一个失败位置是只有一个筹码的单个尼姆堆(Nim heap)。然而，例如，将这样的一个堆 \(Z\) 添加到博弈 \(G\) 中会完全反转 \(G\) 的结果类，因为那个筹码可以被移除，所以无论等价关系 \(\equiv\) 如何定义，我们肯定不会有引理 1.10 中的 \(G + Z \equiv  G\) 。此外，在反向尼姆游戏中，(1,1) 是一个必胜局势，但 (2,2) 是一个失败位置，所以反常博弈没有简单的模仿原则(copycat principle)。尽管反向尼姆游戏已被很好地理解(见练习 1.3)，但一般的反常博弈并非如此。相比之下，在正常规则(normal play)约定下，博弈之和具有良好的结构。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{1.5 Nim, Poker Nim, and the Mex Rule}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{1.5 尼姆游戏、扑克尼姆游戏(Poker Nim)和 mex 规则(mex rule)}
\end{tcolorbox}

In this section, we prove a sweeping statement (Theorem 1.14): Every impartial game is equivalent to some Nim heap. Note that this means a single Nim heap, even if the game itself is rather complicated, for example a general Nim position, which is a game sum of several Nim heaps. This can proved without any reference to playing Nim optimally. As we will see at the end of this section with the help of Figure 1.2, the so-called mex rule that underlies Theorem 1.14 can be used to discover the role of powers of two for playing sums of Nim heaps.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在本节中，我们证明一个全面的陈述(定理 1.14):每个无偏博弈都等价于某个尼姆堆。注意，这意味着即使博弈本身相当复杂，例如一般的尼姆游戏位置(它是几个尼姆堆的博弈之和)，也等价于单个尼姆堆。这可以在不涉及尼姆游戏最优策略的情况下证明。正如我们将在本节末尾借助图 1.2 看到的，作为定理 1.14 基础的所谓 mex 规则可用于发现 2 的幂在尼姆堆之和博弈中的作用。
\end{tcolorbox}

We use the following notation for Nim heaps. If \(G\) is a single Nim heap with \(n\) tokens, \(n \geq  0\) , then we denote this game by \(* n\) . This game is completely specified by its \(n\) options, and they are defined recursively as follows:

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们对尼姆堆使用以下符号表示。如果 \(G\) 是一个有 \(n\) 个筹码的单个尼姆堆， \(n \geq  0\) ，那么我们用 \(* n\) 表示这个博弈。这个博弈完全由它的 \(n\) 个选项确定，并且它们递归定义如下:
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\text{ options of } * n : \; * 0, * 1, * 2,\ldots , * \left( {n - 1}\right) \text{ . } \tag{1.18}
\]

Note that \(* 0\) is the empty heap with no tokens, that is, \(* 0 = 0\) ; we will normally continue to just write 0 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
注意， \(* 0\) 是没有筹码的空堆，即 \(* 0 = 0\) ；我们通常仍只写 0。
\end{tcolorbox}

We can use (1.18) as the definition of \(* n\) . For example, the game \(* 4\) is defined by its options \(* 0, * 1, * 2, * 3\) . It is very important to include \(* 0\) in that list of options, because it means that *4 has a winning move. Condition (1.18) is a recursive definition of the game \(* n\) , because its options are also defined by reference to such games \(* k\) , for numbers \(k\) smaller than \(n\) . This game fulfills the ending condition because the heap gets successively smaller in any sequence of moves.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们可以使用 (1.18) 作为 \(* n\) 的定义。例如，博弈 \(* 4\) 由其选项 \(* 0, * 1, * 2, * 3\) 定义。在这些选项列表中包含 \(* 0\) 非常重要，因为这意味着 *4 有一个必胜步。条件 (1.18) 是博弈 \(* n\) 的递归定义，因为它的选项也是通过参考此类博弈 \(* k\) 来定义的，其中数字 \(k\) 小于 \(n\) 。这个博弈满足终止条件，因为在任何移动序列中，堆的大小会依次减小。
\end{tcolorbox}

A general Nim position is a game sum of several Nim heaps. Earlier we had written such a position by just listing the sizes of the Nim heaps, such as1,2,3in (1.1). The fancy way to write this is now \(* 1 +  * 2 +  * 3\) , a sum of games.

\begin{tcolorbox}
一般的尼姆游戏局势是几个尼姆堆的博弈之和。早些时候，我们只是列出尼姆堆的大小来表示这样的局势，比如 (1.1) 中的 1, 2, 3。现在更正式的写法是 \(* 1 +  * 2 +  * 3\) ，即博弈的和。
\end{tcolorbox}

The game of Poker Nim is a variation of Nim. Suppose that each player is given, at the beginning of the game, some extra "reserve" tokens. Like Nim, the game is played with heaps of tokens. In a move, a player can choose, as in ordinary Nim, a heap and remove some tokens, which he can add to his reserve tokens. A second, new kind of move is to add some of the player's reserve tokens to some heap (or even to create an entire new heap with these tokens). These two kinds of moves are the only ones allowed.

\begin{tcolorbox}
扑克尼姆游戏是尼姆游戏的一种变体。假设在游戏开始时，每个玩家都会得到一些额外的“储备”代币。和尼姆游戏一样，这个游戏也是用代币堆来玩的。在一次移动中，玩家可以像普通尼姆游戏那样，选择一个堆并移除一些代币，然后将这些代币添加到自己的储备代币中。第二种新的移动方式是将玩家的一些储备代币添加到某个堆中(甚至可以用这些代币创建一个全新的堆)。这两种移动方式是唯一允许的操作。
\end{tcolorbox}

Suppose that there are three heaps, of sizes 1, 2, 5, and that the game has been going on for some time, so that both players have accumulated substantial reserves of tokens. It is player I's turn, who moves to1,2,3because that is a good move in ordinary Nim. But then player II adds 50 tokens to the heap of size 2, creating the position1,52,3, which seems complicated.

\begin{tcolorbox}
假设现在有三个堆，大小分别为 1、2、5，并且游戏已经进行了一段时间，两个玩家都积累了大量的储备代币。现在轮到玩家 I 行动，他移动到 1, 2, 3，因为这在普通尼姆游戏中是一步好棋。但随后玩家 II 向大小为 2 的堆中添加了 50 个代币，形成了 1, 52, 3 的局势，这看起来很复杂。
\end{tcolorbox}

What should player I do? After a moment's thought, he just removes the 50 tokens that player II has just added to the heap, reverting to the previous position. Player II may keep adding tokens, but will eventually run out of them, no matter how many she acquires in between, and then player I can proceed as in ordinary Nim.

\begin{tcolorbox}
玩家 I 应该怎么做呢？稍加思考后，他只需移除玩家 II 刚刚添加到堆中的 50 个代币，恢复到之前的局势。玩家 II 可能会继续添加代币，但无论她中途获得多少代币，最终都会用完，然后玩家 I 就可以像在普通尼姆游戏中那样继续行动。
\end{tcolorbox}

Hence, a player who can win in a position in ordinary Nim can still win in Poker Nim. He replies to the opponent's heap-reducing moves just as he would in ordinary \(\mathrm{{Nim}}\) , and reverses the effect of any heap-increasing move by using a heap-reducing move to restore the heap to the same size again. Strictly speaking, the ending condition is violated in Poker Nim because in theory the game could go on forever. However, a player in a winning position wants to end the game with his victory, and never has to put back any tokens; then the losing player will eventually run out of reserve tokens that she can add to a heap, so that the game terminates.

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此，在普通尼姆游戏中能在某个局势获胜的玩家，在扑克尼姆游戏中仍然可以获胜。他对对手减少堆大小的行动的回应方式与在普通 \(\mathrm{{Nim}}\) 游戏中一样，并且可以通过减少堆大小的行动来抵消对手增加堆大小的行动，使堆恢复到原来的大小。严格来说，扑克尼姆游戏违反了终止条件，因为从理论上讲，游戏可能会永远进行下去。然而，处于必胜局势的玩家希望以胜利结束游戏，并且永远不必放回任何代币；那么失败的玩家最终会用完可以添加到堆中的储备代币，从而使游戏终止。
\end{tcolorbox}

Consider now an impartial game where the options of player I are games that are equivalent to the Nim heaps \(* 0, * 1, * 2, * 5, * 9\) . This can be regarded as a rather peculiar Nim heap of size 3 which can be reduced to any of the sizes0,1,2, but which can also be increased to size 5 or 9 . The Poker Nim argument shows that this extra freedom is of no use to turn a losing game into a winning game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
现在考虑一个无偏博弈，玩家 I 的选项是与尼姆堆 \(* 0, * 1, * 2, * 5, * 9\) 等价的博弈。这可以被看作是一个相当特殊的大小为 3 的尼姆堆，它可以被减小到 0、1、2 中的任何一个大小，但也可以增加到 5 或 9。扑克尼姆游戏的论证表明，这种额外的自由度并不能将一个必败的游戏变成一个必胜的游戏。
\end{tcolorbox}

The mex rule says that if the options of a game \(G\) are equivalent to Nim heaps with sizes from a set \(S\) (like \(S = \{ 0,1,2,5,9\}\) above), then \(G\) is equivalent to a Nim heap of size \(m\) , where \(m\) is the smallest nonnegative integer not contained in \(S\) . This number \(m\) is written \(\operatorname{mex}\left( S\right)\) , where \(m\) ex stands for minimum excluded number. That is,

\begin{tcolorbox}
mex 规则指出，如果一个博弈 \(G\) 的选项等价于大小来自集合 \(S\) 的尼姆堆(就像上面的 \(S = \{ 0,1,2,5,9\}\) )，那么 \(G\) 等价于一个大小为 \(m\) 的尼姆堆，其中 \(m\) 是 \(S\) 中不包含的最小非负整数。这个数字 \(m\) 写作 \(\operatorname{mex}\left( S\right)\) ，其中 \(m\) ex 代表最小排除数。也就是说，
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
m = \operatorname{mex}\left( S\right)  = \min \{ k \geq  0 \mid  k \notin  S\} .
\]

For example, \(\operatorname{mex}\left( {\{ 0,1,2,3,5,6\} }\right)  = 4,\operatorname{mex}\left( {\{ 1,2,3,4,5\} }\right)  = 0\) , and \(\operatorname{mex}\left( \varnothing \right)  = 0\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
例如， \(\operatorname{mex}\left( {\{ 0,1,2,3,5,6\} }\right)  = 4,\operatorname{mex}\left( {\{ 1,2,3,4,5\} }\right)  = 0\) ，以及 \(\operatorname{mex}\left( \varnothing \right)  = 0\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

If \(G \equiv   * m\) for an impartial game \(G\) , then \(m\) is called the Nim value of \(G\) . The Nim value is unique because Nim heaps of different sizes are not equivalent by Lemma 1.7. Because the concept has been discovered independently by Sprague (1935) and Grundy (1939), other common names for the Nim value are also Sprague-Grundy value, sg-value, or Grundy value.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果对于一个无偏博弈 \(G\) 有 \(G \equiv   * m\) ，那么 \(m\) 被称为 \(G\) 的尼姆值(Nim value)。尼姆值是唯一的，因为根据引理 1.7，不同大小的尼姆堆(Nim heap)并不等价。由于这一概念由斯普拉格(Sprague，1935 年)和格鲁迪(Grundy，1939 年)独立发现，尼姆值的其他常见名称还有斯普拉格 - 格鲁迪值(Sprague - Grundy value)、sg 值(sg - value)或格鲁迪值(Grundy value)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 1.14 (The mex rule). Consider an impartial game G. Then \(G\) has Nim value \(m\) (that is, \(G \equiv   * m\) ), where \(m\) is uniquely determined as follows: For each option \(H\) of \(G\) , let \(H\) have Nim value \({s}_{H}\) , and let \(S = \left\{  {{s}_{H} \mid  H\text{ is an option of }G}\right\}\) . Then \(m = \operatorname{mex}\left( S\right)\) , that is, \(G \equiv   * \left( {\operatorname{mex}\left( S\right) }\right)\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理 1.14(mex 规则)。考虑一个无偏博弈 G。那么 \(G\) 具有尼姆值 \(m\) (即 \(G \equiv   * m\) )，其中 \(m\) 由以下方式唯一确定:对于 \(G\) 的每个选项 \(H\) ，设 \(H\) 具有尼姆值 \({s}_{H}\) ，并设 \(S = \left\{  {{s}_{H} \mid  H\text{ is an option of }G}\right\}\) 。那么 \(m = \operatorname{mex}\left( S\right)\) ，即 \(G \equiv   * \left( {\operatorname{mex}\left( S\right) }\right)\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. The statement is true if \(S = \varnothing\) because then \(G = 0 =  * 0\) . Assume, per top-down induction, that each option \(H\) of \(G\) has Nim value \({s}_{H}\) , where \({s}_{H}\) is itself the mex of the Nim values of the options of \(H\) , and that \(S\) is the set of these Nim values \({s}_{H}\) as described. Let \(m = \operatorname{mex}\left( S\right)\) . We show \(G +  * m \equiv  0\) , which proves that \(G \equiv   * m\) by Lemma 1.12. The game \(G +  * m\) is losing if each of its options is winning.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。如果 \(S = \varnothing\) ，该陈述为真，因为此时 \(G = 0 =  * 0\) 。假设根据自上而下归纳(top - down induction)， \(G\) 的每个选项 \(H\) 都有尼姆值 \({s}_{H}\) ，其中 \({s}_{H}\) 本身是 \(H\) 的选项的尼姆值的 mex，并且 \(S\) 是如前所述的这些尼姆值 \({s}_{H}\) 的集合。设 \(m = \operatorname{mex}\left( S\right)\) 。我们证明 \(G +  * m \equiv  0\) ，根据引理 1.12，这证明了 \(G \equiv   * m\) 。如果一个博弈 \(G +  * m\) 的每个选项都是必胜局势，那么这个博弈就是失败位置。
\end{tcolorbox}

First, consider such an option of the form \(G +  * h\) for some \(h < m\) , that is, the player moves in the Nim heap \(* m\) of the game sum \(G +  * m\) . Then \(h \in  S\) because \(m = \operatorname{mex}\left( S\right)\) , and by construction of \(S\) there is an option \(H\) of \(G\) so that \(h = {s}_{H}\) , that is, \(H \equiv   * h\) . Hence, the other player has the counter-move from \(G +  * h\) to the losing position \(H +  * h\) , which shows that \(G +  * h\) is indeed winning.

\begin{tcolorbox}
首先，考虑对于某个 \(h < m\) 形式为 \(G +  * h\) 的这样一个选项，即玩家在博弈之和 \(G +  * m\) 的尼姆堆 \(* m\) 中移动。那么 \(h \in  S\) ，因为 \(m = \operatorname{mex}\left( S\right)\) ，并且根据 \(S\) 的构造， \(G\) 有一个选项 \(H\) 使得 \(h = {s}_{H}\) ，即 \(H \equiv   * h\) 。因此，另一个玩家有从 \(G +  * h\) 到失败位置 \(H +  * h\) 的应对策略，这表明 \(G +  * h\) 确实是必胜局势。
\end{tcolorbox}

Second, consider an option of \(G +  * m\) of the form \(H +  * m\) where \(H\) is an option of \(G\) , where \(H \equiv   * {s}_{H}\) and \({s}_{H} \in  S\) , and either \({s}_{H} < m\) or \({s}_{H} > m\) (the case \({s}_{H} = m\) is excluded because \(m \notin  S\) ). If \({s}_{H} < m\) , then the other player counters by moving from \(H +  * m\) to the losing position \(H +  * {s}_{H}\) . If \({s}_{H} > m\) , we use the inductive hypothesis that \({s}_{H}\) is the mex of the Nim values of the options of \(H\) , so one of these Nim values is \(m\) , with \({H}^{\prime } \equiv   * m\) for an option \({H}^{\prime }\) of \(H\) . Then the other player counters by moving from \(H +  * m\) to the losing position \({H}^{\prime } +  * m\) (this is the Poker Nim argument). This shows that \(H +  * m\) is also a winning position. Hence, \(G +  * m\) is a losing game and \(G +  * m \equiv  0\) , as required.

\begin{tcolorbox}
其次，考虑 \(G +  * m\) 的一个形式为 \(H +  * m\) 的选项，其中 \(H\) 是 \(G\) 的一个选项，其中 \(H \equiv   * {s}_{H}\) 且 \({s}_{H} \in  S\) ，并且要么 \({s}_{H} < m\) 要么 \({s}_{H} > m\) (排除 \({s}_{H} = m\) 的情况，因为 \(m \notin  S\) )。如果 \({s}_{H} < m\) ，那么另一个玩家通过从 \(H +  * m\) 移动到失败位置 \(H +  * {s}_{H}\) 来应对。如果 \({s}_{H} > m\) ，我们使用归纳假设，即 \({s}_{H}\) 是 \(H\) 的选项的尼姆值的 mex，所以这些尼姆值之一是 \(m\) ，对于 \(H\) 的一个选项 \({H}^{\prime }\) 有 \({H}^{\prime } \equiv   * m\) 。然后另一个玩家通过从 \(H +  * m\) 移动到失败位置 \({H}^{\prime } +  * m\) 来应对(这是扑克尼姆游戏(Poker Nim)的论证)。这表明 \(H +  * m\) 也是一个必胜局势。因此， \(G +  * m\) 是一个失败的博弈，并且如要求的那样 \(G +  * m \equiv  0\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

A special case of the preceding theorem is that \(\operatorname{mex}\left( S\right)  = 0\) , which means that all options of \(G\) are equivalent to positive Nim heaps, so they are all winning positions, or that \(G\) has no options at all. Then \(G\) is a losing game, and indeed \(G \equiv   * 0\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
前面定理的一个特殊情况是 \(\operatorname{mex}\left( S\right)  = 0\) ，这意味着 \(G\) 的所有选项都等价于正的尼姆堆，所以它们都是必胜局势，或者 \(G\) 根本没有选项。那么 \(G\) 是一个失败的博弈，实际上 \(G \equiv   * 0\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\Rightarrow\) The digraph game in Exercise 1.9 is excellent for understanding the mex rule.

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\Rightarrow\) 练习 1.9 中的有向图博弈非常适合理解 mex 规则。
\end{tcolorbox}

Note that the mex rule has nothing to do with game sums (apart from the way Theorem 1.14 is proved, which is irrelevant). The mex rule evaluates the options of a game \(G\) , and they are not added when looking at them. If the options are themselves game sums (for example for the game Kayles in (1.22) below or the game in Exercise 1.7), then this is a separate matter that is used to find the Nim value of each option, and is specific to the rules of that game. The mex rule is general.

\begin{tcolorbox}
请注意，mex 规则与博弈之和无关(除了定理 1.14 的证明方式，这并不相关)。mex 规则评估博弈 \(G\) 的选项，并且在考虑这些选项时不会将它们相加。如果这些选项本身就是博弈之和(例如下面 (1.22) 中的凯尔斯博弈或练习 1.7 中的博弈)，那么这是一个单独的问题，用于找出每个选项的尼姆值，并且特定于该博弈的规则。mex 规则是通用的。
\end{tcolorbox}

By Theorem 1.14, any impartial game can be played like Nim, provided the Nim values of the positions of the game are known. The Nim values of the positions can be evaluated recursively by the mex rule. This is illustrated in the Rook-move game in Figure 1.1. Place a rook on a Chess board that extends arbitrarily to the right and bottom but ends at the left and at the top. In one move, the rook is moved either horizontally to the left or vertically upwards, for any number of squares (at least one) as long as it stays on the board. The first player who can no longer move loses, when the rook is on the top left square of the board. We number the rows and columns of the board by \(0,1,2,\ldots\) starting from the top left.

\begin{tcolorbox}
根据定理 1.14，任何无偏博弈都可以像尼姆游戏一样进行，前提是知道该博弈局势的尼姆值。局势的尼姆值可以通过 mex 规则递归地评估。这在图 1.1 的战车移动游戏中得到了说明。将一个战车放在一个国际象棋棋盘上，该棋盘向右和向下任意延伸，但在左边和顶部结束。在一步移动中，战车可以水平向左或垂直向上移动任意数量的方格(至少一个)，只要它留在棋盘上。当战车位于棋盘的左上角方格时，第一个无法再移动的玩家失败。我们从左上角开始用 \(0,1,2,\ldots\) 对棋盘的行和列进行编号。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 1.2 gives the Nim values for the positions of the rook on the Chess board. The top left square is equivalent to 0 because the rook can no longer move. The square below only allows reaching the square with 0 on it, so it is equivalent to *1 because \(\operatorname{mex}\left( {\{ 0\} }\right)  = 1\) . The square below gets *2 because its options are equivalent to 0 and *1. From any square in the leftmost column in Figure 1.2, the rook can only move upwards, so any such square in column 0 and row \(i\) corresponds obviously to a Nim heap \(* i\) . Similarly, the topmost row 0 has entry \(* j\) in column \(j\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 1.2 给出了战车在国际象棋棋盘上局势的尼姆值。左上角的方格等价于 0，因为战车无法再移动。下面的方格只能到达值为 0 的方格，所以它等价于 *1，因为 \(\operatorname{mex}\left( {\{ 0\} }\right)  = 1\) 。再下面的方格得到 *2，因为它的选项等价于 0 和 *1。在图 1.2 中最左边的列中，战车只能向上移动，所以第 0 列和第 \(i\) 行的任何这样的方格显然对应于一个尼姆堆 \(* i\) 。类似地，最上面的第 0 行在第 \(j\) 列有条目 \(* j\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_29_332_203_730_571_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 1.1 Rook-move game, where the player may move the rook on the Chess board in the direction of the arrows.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 1.1 战车移动游戏，玩家可以在国际象棋棋盘上沿箭头方向移动战车。
\end{tcolorbox}

In general, a position on the board is evaluated knowing all Nim values for the squares to the left and the top of it, which are the options of that position. As an example, consider the square in row 3 and column 2. To the left of that square, the entries \(* 3\) and \(* 2\) are found, and to the top the entries \(* 2, * 3,0\) . So the square itself is equivalent to \(* 1\) because \(\operatorname{mex}\left( {\{ 0,2,3\} }\right)  = 1\) . The square in row 3 and column 5, where the rook is placed in Figure 1.1, gets entry *6 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
一般来说，知道棋盘上某个方格左边和上边所有方格的尼姆值(这些是该局势的选项)，就可以评估该方格的局势。例如，考虑第 3 行和第 2 列的方格。在该方格的左边，找到条目 \(* 3\) 和 \(* 2\) ，在上面找到条目 \(* 2, * 3,0\) 。所以该方格本身等价于 \(* 1\) ，因为 \(\operatorname{mex}\left( {\{ 0,2,3\} }\right)  = 1\) 。在图 1.1 中放置战车的第 3 行和第 5 列的方格得到条目 *6。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.6\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_29_583_1139_772_772_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 1.2 Equivalent Nim heaps \(* n\) for positions of the Rook-move game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 1.2 战车移动游戏局势的等价尼姆堆 \(* n\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 1.2 shows that the only losing squares on the Chess board are on the diagonal, which are equivalent to the losing game 0 . It is also easy to see directly that the diagonal squares are exactly the losing positions of the Rook-move game: These include the top left square where the rook can no longer move. Whenever the rook is on the diagonal the next move has to move the rook away from the diagonal, which is a winning position because at the next move the rook can always be moved back to the diagonal.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 1.2 显示，国际象棋棋盘上唯一的失败方格在对角线上，它们等价于失败的博弈 0。也很容易直接看出，对角线上的方格恰好是战车移动游戏的失败位置:这些包括战车无法再移动的左上角方格。每当战车在对角线上时，下一步移动必须将战车移离对角线，这是一个必胜局势，因为下一步总是可以将战车移回到对角线上。
\end{tcolorbox}

The astute reader will have noticed that the Rook-move game is just Nim with two heaps. A rook positioned in row \(i\) and column \(j\) can either reduce \(i\) by moving up, or \(j\) by moving left. So this position is the sum of Nim heaps \(* i +  * j\) . It is a losing position if and only if \(i = j\) , where the rook is on the diagonal.

\begin{tcolorbox}
敏锐的读者会注意到，战车移动游戏只是有两个堆的尼姆游戏。位于第 \(i\) 行和第 \(j\) 列的战车可以通过向上移动减少 \(i\) ，或者通过向左移动减少 \(j\) 。所以这个局势是尼姆堆 \(* i +  * j\) 的和。当且仅当 \(i = j\) (即战车在对角线上)时，这是一个失败位置。
\end{tcolorbox}

In addition to distinguishing winning and losing positions, the Nim values become very useful as soon as we place multiple rooks on the board, assuming that they are allowed to share any square, which corresponds to a sum of Rook-move games. For example, it is not obvious that two rooks, one placed in row 2 and column 0, and another in row 4 and column 6, define a losing position because both squares have Nim value 2 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
除了区分获胜位置和失败位置之外，一旦我们在棋盘上放置多个战车，尼姆值就变得非常有用。假设允许它们共用任何一个方格，这对应于多个战车移动游戏的博弈之和。例如，一个战车放在第2行第0列，另一个放在第4行第6列，这两个战车构成一个失败位置并不明显，因为这两个方格的尼姆值都是2。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 1.14 states that any impartial game is equivalent to a single Nim heap of a certain size. In particular, the sum of two Nim heaps is equivalent to a single Nim heap. Assuming we don't know how to play Nim using the binary system as explained with the example (1.3), so far we only know that the game sum of two Nim heaps is winning if they have different sizes, and losing if they are equal. Figure 1.2 represents the computation of the Nim values of a pair of Nim heaps \(* i +  * j\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理1.14指出，任何无偏博弈都等价于一个特定大小的单一尼姆堆。特别地，两个尼姆堆的博弈之和等价于一个单一尼姆堆。假设我们不知道如何像示例(1.3)中那样使用二进制来玩尼姆游戏，到目前为止，我们只知道两个尼姆堆的博弈之和在它们大小不同时是获胜的，在它们大小相等时是失败的。图1.2展示了一对尼姆堆 \(* i +  * j\) 的尼姆值的计算。
\end{tcolorbox}

There is a certain pattern to these Nim values: Clearly, they are symmetric when reflected at the diagonal because rows and columns can be interchanged. In a single row (or column), all the values are different because for any set \(S\) of integers, \(\operatorname{mex}\left( S\right)\) does not belong to \(S\) , so the value in a square is certainly different from the values to the left of that square. Now consider some power of two, say \({2}^{k}\) , and look at the first \({2}^{k}\) rows and columns, which are each numbered \(0,1,\ldots ,{2}^{k} - 1\) . This quadratic top left part of the table (for example, the top left \(4 \times  4\) squares if \(k = 2\) ) only has the entries \(0,1,\ldots ,{2}^{k} - 1\) in each row and column in some permutation. The role of powers of two for computing the Nim value of a sum of two Nim heaps is made precise in the next section, and will explain the use of the binary representation as in (1.3).

\begin{tcolorbox}
这些尼姆值有一定的规律:显然，当沿对角线反射时它们是对称的，因为行和列可以互换。在单行(或单列)中，所有的值都不同，因为对于任何整数集合 \(S\) ， \(\operatorname{mex}\left( S\right)\) 不属于 \(S\) ，所以一个方格中的值肯定与该方格左侧的值不同。现在考虑2的某个幂，比如 \({2}^{k}\) ，并查看前 \({2}^{k}\) 行和列，它们的编号都是 \(0,1,\ldots ,{2}^{k} - 1\) 。表格的这个左上角方形部分(例如，如果 \(k = 2\) ，则是左上角的 \(4 \times  4\) 个方格)在每行和每列中都只有 \(0,1,\ldots ,{2}^{k} - 1\) 以某种排列形式出现。在下一节中，将精确说明2的幂在计算两个尼姆堆之和的尼姆值时的作用，并解释如(1.3)中那样使用二进制表示的原因。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{1.6 Sums of Nim Heaps}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{1.6 尼姆堆的和}
\end{tcolorbox}

In this section, we derive how to compute the Nim value for a general Nim position, which is a sum of different Nim heaps. This will be the Nim sum that we have defined using the binary representation, now cast in the language of game sums and equivalent games, and without assuming the binary representation.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在本节中，我们将推导如何计算一般尼姆局面(即不同尼姆堆的和)的尼姆值。这将是我们用二进制表示法定义的尼姆和，现在用博弈之和和等价博弈的语言来表述，并且不假设使用二进制表示法。
\end{tcolorbox}

For example, we know that \(* 1 +  * 2 +  * 3 \equiv  0\) , so by Lemma 1.12, \(* 1 +  * 2\) is equivalent to *3. In general, however, the sizes of the Nim heaps cannot simply be added to obtain the equivalent Nim heap, because \(* 2 +  * 3\) is also equivalent to \(* 1\) , and \(* 1 +  * 3\) is equivalent to \(* 2\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
例如，我们知道 \(* 1 +  * 2 +  * 3 \equiv  0\) ，所以根据引理1.12， \(* 1 +  * 2\) 等价于 *3。然而，一般来说，不能简单地将尼姆堆的大小相加来得到等价的尼姆堆，因为 \(* 2 +  * 3\) 也等价于 \(* 1\) ，并且 \(* 1 +  * 3\) 等价于 \(* 2\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

If \(* k \equiv   * n +  * m\) , then we call \(k\) the Nim sum of \(n\) and \(m\) , written \(k = n \oplus  m\) . The following theorem states that the Nim sum of distinct powers of two is their arithmetic sum. For example, \(1 = {2}^{0}\) and \(2 = {2}^{1}\) , so \(1 \oplus  2 = 1 + 2 = 3\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果 \(* k \equiv   * n +  * m\) ，那么我们称 \(k\) 为 \(n\) 和 \(m\) 的尼姆和，记为 \(k = n \oplus  m\) 。以下定理表明，不同2的幂的尼姆和就是它们的算术和。例如， \(1 = {2}^{0}\) 且 \(2 = {2}^{1}\) ，所以 \(1 \oplus  2 = 1 + 2 = 3\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 1.15. Let \(n \geq  1\) , and \(n = {2}^{a} + {2}^{b} + {2}^{c} + \cdots\) , where \(a > b > c > \cdots  \geq  0\) . Then

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理1.15。设 \(n \geq  1\) ，且 \(n = {2}^{a} + {2}^{b} + {2}^{c} + \cdots\) ，其中 \(a > b > c > \cdots  \geq  0\) 。那么
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
* n \equiv   * \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * \left( {2}^{b}\right)  +  * \left( {2}^{c}\right)  + \cdots . \tag{1.19}
\]

We first discuss the implications of this theorem, and then prove it. The expression \(n = {2}^{a} + {2}^{b} + {2}^{c} + \cdots\) is an arithmetic sum of distinct powers of two. Any \(n\) is uniquely given as such a sum. It amounts to the binary representation of \(n\) , which, if \(n < {2}^{a + 1}\) , gives \(n\) as the sum of all powers \({2}^{a},{2}^{a - 1},{2}^{a - 2},\ldots ,{2}^{0}\) where each power of two is multiplied with 0 or 1, the binary digit for the respective position. For example,

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们首先讨论这个定理的含义，然后证明它。表达式 \(n = {2}^{a} + {2}^{b} + {2}^{c} + \cdots\) 是不同 2 的幂的算术和。任何 \(n\) 都唯一地表示为这样的和。这相当于 \(n\) 的二进制表示，如果 \(n < {2}^{a + 1}\) ，则将 \(n\) 表示为所有幂 \({2}^{a},{2}^{a - 1},{2}^{a - 2},\ldots ,{2}^{0}\) 的和，其中每个 2 的幂都乘以 0 或 1，即相应位置的二进制数字。例如，
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
9 = 8 + 1 = 1 \cdot  {2}^{3} + 0 \cdot  {2}^{2} + 0 \cdot  {2}^{1} + 1 \cdot  {2}^{0},
\]

so that 9 in decimal is written as 1001 in binary. Theorem 1.15 uses only the distinct powers of two \({2}^{a},{2}^{b},{2}^{c},\ldots\) that correspond to the digits 1 in the binary representation of \(n\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此十进制的 9 在二进制中写成 1001。定理 1.15 仅使用与 \(n\) 的二进制表示中数字 1 对应的不同 2 的幂 \({2}^{a},{2}^{b},{2}^{c},\ldots\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The right-hand side of (1.19) is a game sum. Equation (1.19) states that the single Nim heap \(* n\) is equivalent to a game sum of Nim heaps whose sizes are distinct powers of two. If we visualize the tokens in a Nim heap as dots like in (1.4), then an example of this equation is

\begin{tcolorbox}
(1.19) 式的右边是一个博弈之和。等式 (1.19) 表明，单个尼姆堆 \(* n\) 等价于大小为不同 2 的幂的尼姆堆的博弈之和。如果我们像 (1.4) 那样将尼姆堆中的代币可视化为点，那么这个等式的一个例子是
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\text{ *14 = ::::::::: ≡ ::::: + :: + : }
\]

for \(n = {14}\) . In addition to \(* n\) , consider a second Nim heap \(* m\) and represent it as its equivalent game sum of several Nim heaps, all of which have a size that is a power of two. Then \(* n +  * m\) is a game sum of many such heaps, where equal heaps cancel out in pairs because a game sum of two identical games is losing and can be omitted. The remaining heap sizes are all distinct powers of two, which can be added to give the size of the single Nim heap \(* k\) that is equivalent to \(* n +  * m\) . As an example, let \(n = 9 = 8 + 1\) and \(m = {14} = 8 + 4 + 2\) . Then \(* n +  * m \equiv   * 8 +  * 1 +  * 8 +  * 4 +  * 2 \equiv   * 4 +  * 2 +  * 1 \equiv   * 7\) , which we can also write as \(9 \oplus  {14} = 7\) . In particular, \(* 9 +  * {14} +  * 7\) is a losing game, which would be very laborious to show without the theorem.

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于 \(n = {14}\) 。除了 \(* n\) 之外，考虑第二个尼姆堆 \(* m\) ，并将其表示为几个尼姆堆的等价博弈之和，所有这些尼姆堆的大小都是 2 的幂。那么 \(* n +  * m\) 是许多这样的堆的博弈之和，其中相等的堆会成对抵消，因为两个相同博弈的博弈之和是必败的，可以省略。剩下的堆的大小都是不同的 2 的幂，将它们相加可以得到与 \(* n +  * m\) 等价的单个尼姆堆 \(* k\) 的大小。例如，设 \(n = 9 = 8 + 1\) 且 \(m = {14} = 8 + 4 + 2\) 。那么 \(* n +  * m \equiv   * 8 +  * 1 +  * 8 +  * 4 +  * 2 \equiv   * 4 +  * 2 +  * 1 \equiv   * 7\) ，我们也可以写成 \(9 \oplus  {14} = 7\) 。特别地， \(* 9 +  * {14} +  * 7\) 是一个必败的博弈，如果没有这个定理，证明起来会非常繁琐。
\end{tcolorbox}

One consequence of Theorem 1.15 is that the Nim sum of two integers never exceeds their arithmetic sum. Moreover, if both integers are less than some power of two, then so is their Nim sum.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理 1.15 的一个推论是，两个整数的尼姆和永远不会超过它们的算术和。此外，如果两个整数都小于某个 2 的幂，那么它们的尼姆和也小于该 2 的幂。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 1.16. Suppose that Theorem 1.15 holds for any \(n\) with \(n < {2}^{d}\) . Let \(0 \leq  p,q < {2}^{d}\) . Then \(* p +  * q \equiv   * r\) where \(0 \leq  r < {2}^{d}\) , that is, \(r = p \oplus  q < {2}^{d}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理 1.16。假设定理 1.15 对于任何满足 \(n < {2}^{d}\) 的 \(n\) 都成立。设 \(0 \leq  p,q < {2}^{d}\) 。那么 \(* p +  * q \equiv   * r\) 其中 \(0 \leq  r < {2}^{d}\) ，即 \(r = p \oplus  q < {2}^{d}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Both \(p\) and \(q\) are sums of distinct powers of two, all smaller than \({2}^{d}\) . By Theorem 1.15, \(r\) is also a sum of such powers of two, where those powers that appear in both \(p\) and \(q\) , say \({2}^{k}\) , cancel out in the game sum because \(* \left( {2}^{k}\right)  +  * \left( {2}^{k}\right)  \equiv  0\) . Hence, the resulting sum \(r\) is also less than \({2}^{d}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。 \(p\) 和 \(q\) 都是小于 \({2}^{d}\) 的不同 2 的幂的和。根据定理 1.15， \(r\) 也是这样的 2 的幂的和，其中那些同时出现在 \(p\) 和 \(q\) 中的幂，比如 \({2}^{k}\) ，在博弈之和中会抵消，因为 \(* \left( {2}^{k}\right)  +  * \left( {2}^{k}\right)  \equiv  0\) 。因此，得到的和 \(r\) 也小于 \({2}^{d}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The following proof may be best understood by considering it along with an example, say \(n = 7\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
通过结合一个例子，比如 \(n = 7\) ，来考虑下面的证明可能会更容易理解。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof of Theorem 1.15. We proceed by induction. Consider some \(n\) , and assume that the theorem holds for all smaller \(n\) . Let \(n = {2}^{a} + q\) where \(q = {2}^{b} + {2}^{c} + \cdots\) . If \(q = 0\) , the claim holds trivially ( \(n\) is just a single power of two), so let \(q > 0\) . We have \(q < {2}^{a}\) . By inductive assumption, \(* q \equiv   * \left( {2}^{b}\right)  +  * \left( {2}^{c}\right)  + \cdots\) , so all we have to prove is that \(* n \equiv   * \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * q\) in order to show (1.19). We show this using Lemma 1.13, that is, by showing that each option of \(* n\) is equivalent to some option of the game \(* \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * q\) and vice versa. The options of \(* n\) are \(0, * 1, * 2,\ldots , * \left( {n - 1}\right)\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理 1.15 的证明。我们采用归纳法进行证明。考虑某个 \(n\) ，并假设该定理对所有更小的 \(n\) 都成立。设 \(n = {2}^{a} + q\) ，其中 \(q = {2}^{b} + {2}^{c} + \cdots\) 。如果 \(q = 0\) ，该命题显然成立( \(n\) 只是 2 的单一幂次)，所以设 \(q > 0\) 。我们有 \(q < {2}^{a}\) 。根据归纳假设， \(* q \equiv   * \left( {2}^{b}\right)  +  * \left( {2}^{c}\right)  + \cdots\) ，所以为了证明 (1.19)，我们只需证明 \(* n \equiv   * \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * q\) 。我们使用引理 1.13 来证明这一点，即通过证明 \(* n\) 的每个选项都等同于博弈 \(* \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * q\) 的某个选项，反之亦然。 \(* n\) 的选项是 \(0, * 1, * 2,\ldots , * \left( {n - 1}\right)\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The options of \(* \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * q\) are of two kinds, depending on whether the player moves in the Nim heap \(* \left( {2}^{a}\right)\) or \(* q\) . The first kind of options are given by

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(* \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * q\) 的选项有两种类型，取决于玩家是在尼姆堆(Nim heap) \(* \left( {2}^{a}\right)\) 还是 \(* q\) 中移动。第一种类型的选项由以下给出
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
0 +  * q \equiv   * {r}_{0}
\]

\[
* 1 +  * q \equiv   * {r}_{1} \tag{1.20}
\]

\[
\vdots
\]

\[
* \left( {{2}^{a} - 1}\right)  +  * q \equiv   * {r}_{{2}^{a} - 1}
\]

where the equivalence of \(* i +  * q\) with some Nim heap \(* {r}_{i}\) , for \(0 \leq  i < {2}^{a}\) , holds by the inductive assumption even if we did not know the mex rule. Moreover, by Lemma 1.16 (with \(d = a\) , because for \(n < {2}^{a}\) Theorem 1.15 holds by the inductive assumption), both \(i\) and \(q\) are less than \({2}^{a}\) , and therefore also \({r}_{i} < {2}^{a}\) . On the right-hand side in (1.20), there are \({2}^{a}\) many Nim heaps \(* {r}_{i}\) for \(0 \leq  i < {2}^{a}\) . We claim they are all different, so that these options form exactly the set \(\{ 0, * 1, * 2,\ldots\) , \(\left. {*\left( {{2}^{a} - 1}\right) }\right\}\) . Namely, by adding the game \(* q\) to the heap \(* {r}_{i}\) ,(1.16) implies \(* {r}_{i} +  * q \equiv\)  \(* i +  * q +  * q \equiv   * i\) , so that \(* {r}_{i} \equiv   * {r}_{j}\) implies \(* {r}_{i} +  * q \equiv   * {r}_{j} +  * q\) , that is, \(* i \equiv   * j\) and hence \(i = j\) by Lemma 1.7, for \(0 \leq  i,j < {2}^{a}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
其中，对于 \(0 \leq  i < {2}^{a}\) ， \(* i +  * q\) 与某个尼姆堆(Nim heap) \(* {r}_{i}\) 的等价性，即使我们不知道 mex 规则，根据归纳假设也成立。此外，根据引理 1.16(对于 \(d = a\) ，因为对于 \(n < {2}^{a}\) ，定理 1.15 根据归纳假设成立)， \(i\) 和 \(q\) 都小于 \({2}^{a}\) ，因此 \({r}_{i} < {2}^{a}\) 也小于 \({2}^{a}\) 。在 (1.20) 的右侧，对于 \(0 \leq  i < {2}^{a}\) 有 \({2}^{a}\) 个尼姆堆(Nim heap) \(* {r}_{i}\) 。我们声称它们都是不同的，所以这些选项恰好构成集合 \(\{ 0, * 1, * 2,\ldots\) ， \(\left. {*\left( {{2}^{a} - 1}\right) }\right\}\) 。即，通过将博弈 \(* q\) 添加到堆 \(* {r}_{i}\) 中，(1.16) 意味着 \(* {r}_{i} +  * q \equiv\)  \(* i +  * q +  * q \equiv   * i\) ，所以 \(* {r}_{i} \equiv   * {r}_{j}\) 意味着 \(* {r}_{i} +  * q \equiv   * {r}_{j} +  * q\) ，即 \(* i \equiv   * j\) ，因此根据引理 1.7，对于 \(0 \leq  i,j < {2}^{a}\) 有 \(i = j\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The second kind of options of \(* \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * q\) are of the form

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(* \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * q\) 的第二种类型的选项形式为
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
* \left( {2}^{a}\right)  + 0 \equiv   * \left( {{2}^{a} + 0}\right)
\]

\[
* \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * 1 \equiv   * \left( {{2}^{a} + 1}\right)
\]

\[
\vdots
\]

\[
* \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * \left( {q - 1}\right)  \equiv   * \left( {{2}^{a} + q - 1}\right) ,
\]

where the heap sizes on the right-hand sides are given again by the inductive assumption. These heaps form the set \(\left\{  {*\left( {2}^{a}\right) , * \left( {{2}^{a} + 1}\right) ,\ldots , * \left( {n - 1}\right) }\right\}\) . Together with the first kind of options, they are exactly the options of \(* n\) . This shows that the options of \(* n\) and of the game sum \(* \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * q\) are indeed equivalent, which completes the proof.

\begin{tcolorbox}
其中右侧的堆大小再次由归纳假设给出。这些堆构成集合 \(\left\{  {*\left( {2}^{a}\right) , * \left( {{2}^{a} + 1}\right) ,\ldots , * \left( {n - 1}\right) }\right\}\) 。与第一种类型的选项一起，它们恰好是 \(* n\) 的选项。这表明 \(* n\) 的选项和博弈之和(game sum) \(* \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * q\) 的选项确实是等价的，从而完成了证明。
\end{tcolorbox}

In the proof of Theorem 1.15, if \(n = 7\) then \(n = {2}^{a} + q\) with \(a = 2\) and \(q = 3\) . Then the Nim heaps \(* {r}_{0}, * {r}_{1}, * {r}_{2}, * {r}_{3}\) in (1.20) are the entries in the first \({2}^{a}\) rows 0,1,2,3in column \(q = 3\) of the Rook-move table in Figure 1.2.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在定理1.15的证明中，如果 \(n = 7\) ，那么 \(n = {2}^{a} + q\) ，其中 \(a = 2\) 且 \(q = 3\) 。那么(1.20)中的尼姆堆 \(* {r}_{0}, * {r}_{1}, * {r}_{2}, * {r}_{3}\) 是图1.2中战车移动表第 \(q = 3\) 列前 \({2}^{a}\) 行0、1、2、3中的元素。
\end{tcolorbox}

This step in the proof of Theorem 1.15, which is based on Lemma 1.16, also explains to some extent why powers of two appear in the computation of Nim sums. The options of moving from \(* \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * q\) in (1.20) neatly produce exactly the numbers \(0,1,\ldots ,{2}^{a} - 1\) , which would not work when replacing \({2}^{a}\) with something else.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理1.15证明中的这一步基于引理1.16，它在一定程度上也解释了为什么2的幂次会出现在尼姆和的计算中。在(1.20)中从 \(* \left( {2}^{a}\right)  +  * q\) 移动的选项恰好能产生数字 \(0,1,\ldots ,{2}^{a} - 1\) ，如果用其他东西代替 \({2}^{a}\) ，这就行不通了。
\end{tcolorbox}

The Nim sum of any set of numbers can be obtained by writing each number as a sum of distinct powers of two and then canceling repetitions in pairs. For the example in (1.3) and (1.4), we have

\begin{tcolorbox}
任何一组数字的尼姆和可以通过将每个数字写成不同2的幂次之和，然后成对消去重复项来得到。对于(1.3)和(1.4)中的例子，我们有
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
4 \oplus  5 \oplus  9 \oplus  {14} = 4 \oplus  \left( {4 + 1}\right)  \oplus  \left( {8 + 1}\right)  \oplus  \left( {8 + 4 + 2}\right)  = 4 + 2 = 6. \tag{1.21}
\]

Hence, the Nim position4,5,9,14is equivalent to a single Nim heap of size 6 . This equivalence holds because adding this Nim heap to the game sum of the four Nim heaps produces a losing game:

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此，尼姆局势4、5、9、14等价于一个大小为6的单个尼姆堆。这种等价关系成立是因为将这个尼姆堆加到四个尼姆堆的博弈之和中会产生一个失败的博弈:
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\left( {*4 +  * 5 +  * 9 +  * {14}}\right)  +  * 6 \equiv  0.
\]

The same can be seen from the left table in (1.3). That is, Nim sums amount to writing the heap sizes in binary and adding the binary digits separately in each column (power of 2) modulo 2. The column entry of the Nim sum is 0 if the respective power of 2 appears an even number of times (all pairs cancel) and 1 if it appears an odd number of times.

\begin{tcolorbox}
从(1.3)中的左表也可以看出这一点。也就是说，尼姆和相当于将堆的大小写成二进制形式，并在每一列(2的幂次)中分别对二进制数字进行模2加法。如果相应的2的幂次出现偶数次(所有对都消去)，尼姆和的列元素为0；如果出现奇数次，则为1。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{1.7 Finding Nim Values}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{1.7 寻找尼姆值}
\end{tcolorbox}

In this section, we analyze some impartial games using the mex rule in Theorem 1.14.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在本节中，我们使用定理1.14中的mex规则来分析一些无偏博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

A game similar to the Rook-move game is the Queen-move game shown in Figure 1.3 where the rook is replaced by a Chess queen, which may move horizontally, vertically, and diagonally (left or up). The squares on the main diagonal are therefore no longer losing positions. This game can also be played with two heaps of tokens where in one move, the player may either remove tokens from one heap as in Nim, or reduce both heaps by the same number of tokens (so this is no longer a sum of two Nim heaps!). In order to illustrate that we are not just interested in the winning and losing squares, we add to this game a Nim heap of size 4 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
与战车移动游戏类似的一个博弈是图1.3所示的皇后移动博弈，其中战车被国际象棋中的皇后所取代，皇后可以水平、垂直和对角(向左或向上)移动。因此，主对角线上的方格不再是失败位置。这个博弈也可以用两堆代币来玩，在一次移动中，玩家可以像在尼姆游戏中那样从一堆中移除代币，或者从两堆中移除相同数量的代币(所以这不再是两个尼姆堆的和！)。为了说明我们不只是对胜负方格感兴趣，我们给这个博弈添加一个大小为4的尼姆堆。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_34_264_189_1042_405_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 1.3 Game sum of a Queen-move game and a Nim heap of size four. The player may either move the queen in the direction of the arrows or take some of the four tokens from the heap.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图1.3 皇后移动博弈和一个大小为4的尼姆堆的博弈之和。玩家可以要么沿箭头方向移动皇后，要么从堆中取走四个代币中的一些。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 1.4 shows the equivalent Nim heaps for the positions of the Queen-move game, determined by the mex rule. The square in row 3 and column 4 occupied by the queen in Figure 1.3 has entry *2. So a winning move is to remove two tokens from the Nim heap to turn it into the heap *2, creating the losing position *2 + *2 . Because 2 is the mex of the Nim values of the options of the queen, these may include (as in Poker Nim) higher Nim values. Indeed, the queen can reach two positions equivalent to *4, in row 3 column 1, and row 0 column 4 . If the queen moves there this creates the game sum \(* 4 +  * 4\) which is losing, so these are two further winning moves in Figure 1.3.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图1.4显示了由mex规则确定的皇后移动博弈局势的等价尼姆堆。图1.3中皇后所在的第3行第4列的方格元素为*2。所以一个必胜步是从尼姆堆中取走两个代币，使其变成堆*2，形成失败局势*2 + *2。因为2是皇后选项的尼姆值的mex，这些选项可能包括(如在扑克尼姆游戏中)更高的尼姆值。实际上，皇后可以到达两个等价于*4的局势，即第3行第1列和第0行第4列。如果皇后移动到那里，就会形成博弈和 \(* 4 +  * 4\) ，这是一个失败的博弈，所以在图1.3中这是另外两个必胜步。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_34_271_1234_617_396_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 1.4 Equivalent Nim heaps for positions of the Queen-move game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图1.4 皇后移动博弈局势的等价尼姆堆。
\end{tcolorbox}

The impartial game Kayles is played as follows: Given a row of \(n\) bowling pins (numbered \(1,2,\ldots ,n\) ), a move knocks out one or two consecutive pins, as in this example of 5 pins where pins 2 and 3 are knocked out:

\begin{tcolorbox}
无偏博弈凯尔斯博弈的玩法如下:给定一排 \(n\) 个保龄球瓶(编号为 \(1,2,\ldots ,n\) )，一次移动可以击倒一个或两个连续的球瓶，就像这个有5个球瓶的例子，其中第2和第3个球瓶被击倒:
\end{tcolorbox}

(1.22)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_34_545_1824_563_140_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

If this game is called \({K}_{n}\) , then knocking out a single pin \(p\) creates the game sum \({K}_{p - 1} + {K}_{n - p}\) , and knocking out pins \(p\) and \(p + 1\) (in the picture, \(p = 2\) and \(n = 5\) ) creates the game sum \({K}_{p - 1} + {K}_{n - p - 1}\) (here \({K}_{1} + {K}_{2}\) ). The options of \({K}_{n}\) are these game sums for all possible \(p\) , where \(p\) only needs to be considered up to the middle pin due to the symmetry of the row of pins. Note that in this game, options happen to be sums of games. For the mex rule, only their Nim value is important. The following picture shows all options of Kayles with 1, 2, or 3 pins:

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果这个博弈被称为 \({K}_{n}\) ，那么击倒单个球瓶 \(p\) 会形成博弈之和 \({K}_{p - 1} + {K}_{n - p}\) ，而击倒球瓶 \(p\) 和 \(p + 1\) (在图中是 \(p = 2\) 和 \(n = 5\) )会形成博弈之和 \({K}_{p - 1} + {K}_{n - p - 1}\) (这里是 \({K}_{1} + {K}_{2}\) )。 \({K}_{n}\) 的选项是所有可能的 \(p\) 对应的这些博弈之和，由于球瓶行的对称性， \(p\) 只需要考虑到中间的球瓶即可。注意，在这个博弈中，选项恰好是博弈的和。对于 mex 规则，只有它们的尼姆值是重要的。下图展示了有 1、2 或 3 个球瓶的凯尔斯博弈的所有选项:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_35_669_376_582_374_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

which shows \({K}_{1} \equiv   * \operatorname{mex}\left( 0\right)  =  * 1,{K}_{2} \equiv   * \operatorname{mex}\left( {1,0}\right)  =  * 2\) , and \({K}_{3} \equiv   * \operatorname{mex}\left( {2,1,1 \oplus  1}\right)  =\)  \(* 3\) because \(1 \oplus  1 = 0\) . The options of Kayles with 4 pins are

\begin{tcolorbox}
这表明 \({K}_{1} \equiv   * \operatorname{mex}\left( 0\right)  =  * 1,{K}_{2} \equiv   * \operatorname{mex}\left( {1,0}\right)  =  * 2\) ，并且 \({K}_{3} \equiv   * \operatorname{mex}\left( {2,1,1 \oplus  1}\right)  =\)  \(* 3\) ，因为 \(1 \oplus  1 = 0\) 。有 4 个球瓶的凯尔斯博弈的选项是
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.7\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_35_509_896_905_124_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

and therefore \({K}_{4} \equiv   * \operatorname{mex}\left( {3,2,1 \oplus  2,1 \oplus  1}\right)  =  * \operatorname{mex}\left( {3,2,3,0}\right)  =  * 1\) which leads to an unexpected winning move for 6 pins:

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此 \({K}_{4} \equiv   * \operatorname{mex}\left( {3,2,1 \oplus  2,1 \oplus  1}\right)  =  * \operatorname{mex}\left( {3,2,3,0}\right)  =  * 1\) ，这导致了有 6 个球瓶时一个意想不到的必胜步:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_35_674_1167_580_123_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

To check this, it is illustrative to show directly that \({K}_{1} + {K}_{4}\) is a losing position, by showing that all its options are winning by finding for each one a counter-move to a losing position.

\begin{tcolorbox}
为了验证这一点，通过为 \({K}_{1} + {K}_{4}\) 的每个选项找到一个走向失败位置的应对策略，直接证明所有选项都是必胜的，从而说明 \({K}_{1} + {K}_{4}\) 是一个失败位置，这是很有启发性的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Further impartial games are described in the exercises.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习中描述了更多的无偏博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{1.8 A Glimpse of Partizan Games}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{1.8 有偏博弈一瞥}
\end{tcolorbox}

A combinatorial game is called partizan if the available moves in a game position may be different for the two players. These games have a rich theory of which we sketch the first concepts here, in particular the four outcome classes, and numbers as strengths of positions in certain games, pioneered by Conway (2001).

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果在一个博弈位置中，两个玩家可用的行动可能不同，那么这个组合博弈就被称为有偏博弈。这些博弈有丰富的理论，我们在这里概述其最初的概念，特别是四种结果类，以及在某些博弈中作为位置强度的数字，这是由康威(2001 年)开创的。
\end{tcolorbox}

The two players are called Left and Right. Consider the game Domineering, given by a board of squares (the starting position is typically rectangular but does not have to be), where in a move Left may occupy two vertically adjacent free squares with a vertical domino, and Right two horizontally adjacent squares with a horizontal domino. (At least in lower case, the letter "l" is more vertical than the letter "r" to remember this.) Hence, starting from a 3-row, 2-column board, we have, up to symmetry,

\begin{tcolorbox}
两个玩家分别称为左玩家和右玩家。考虑纵横棋博弈，它由一个方格棋盘给出(起始位置通常是矩形，但不必如此)，在每一步中，左玩家可以用一个垂直的多米诺骨牌占据两个垂直相邻的空格，右玩家可以用一个水平的多米诺骨牌占据两个水平相邻的空格。(至少小写字母“l”比字母“r”更垂直，这样可以记住这一点。)因此，从一个 3 行 2 列的棋盘开始，在考虑对称性的情况下，我们有
\end{tcolorbox}

(1.23)This game is very different from the impartial game Cram (see Exercise 1.4) where each player may place a domino in either orientation. As (1.23) shows, in Domineering the options of Left and Right are usually different. As before, we assume the normal play convention that a player who can no longer move loses.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这个博弈与无偏博弈塞砖棋(见练习 1.4)非常不同，在塞砖棋中，每个玩家可以以任意方向放置多米诺骨牌。如(1.23)所示，在纵横棋中，左玩家和右玩家的选项通常是不同的。和之前一样，我们假设采用正常规则，即无法再行动的玩家输。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.7\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_36_310_275_941_125_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

We always assume optimal play. In (1.23), this means Right would choose the first option and place the domino in the middle, after which Left can no longer move, whereas the second option that leaves a \(2 \times  2\) board would provide Left with two further moves.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们总是假设玩家采取最优策略。在(1.23)中，这意味着右玩家会选择第一个选项，将多米诺骨牌放在中间，之后左玩家就无法再行动了，而第二个选项留下一个 \(2 \times  2\) 棋盘会给左玩家提供两步额外的行动。
\end{tcolorbox}

An impartial game can only be losing or winning as stated in Lemma 1.1. For partizan games, there are four possible outcome classes, which are denoted by the following calligraphic letters:

\begin{tcolorbox}
如引理 1.1 所述，无偏博弈只有失败或必胜两种情况。对于有偏博弈，有四种可能的结果类，用以下花体字母表示:
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\mathcal{L}\) : Left wins no matter who moves first.

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\mathcal{L}\) :无论谁先行动，左玩家必胜。
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\mathcal{R}\) : Right wins no matter who moves first.

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\mathcal{R}\) :无论谁先行动，右玩家必胜。
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\mathcal{P}\) : The first player to move loses, so the previous player wins.

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\mathcal{P}\) :先行动的玩家失败，因此前一个玩家获胜。
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\mathcal{N}\) : The first player to move wins. (Sometimes called the "next player", although this is as ambiguous as "next Friday", because it is the current player who wins.)

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\mathcal{N}\) :先行动的玩家获胜。(有时称为“下一玩家”，尽管这和“下周五”一样模棱两可，因为实际上是当前玩家获胜。)
\end{tcolorbox}

Every game belongs to exactly one of these outcome classes. The \(\mathcal{P}\) -positions are what we have called losing positions, and \(\mathcal{N}\) -positions are what we have called winning positions. For partizan games we have to consider the further outcome classes \(\mathcal{L}\) and \(\mathcal{R}\) . In Domineering, a single vertical strip of at least two squares, in its smallest form -, belongs to \(\mathcal{L}\) , whereas a horizontal strip such as belongs to \(\mathcal{R}\) . The starting \(3 \times  2\) board in (1.23) belongs to \(\mathcal{N}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
每个博弈都恰好属于这些结果类中的某一类。 \(\mathcal{P}\) 局势就是我们所说的失败位置，而 \(\mathcal{N}\) 局势就是我们所说的必胜局势。对于有偏博弈(partizan game)，我们还需要考虑另外的结果类 \(\mathcal{L}\) 和 \(\mathcal{R}\) 。在纵横棋(Domineering)中，一个至少有两个方格的单个垂直条，其最小形式为 - ，属于 \(\mathcal{L}\) 类，而像这样的水平条则属于 \(\mathcal{R}\) 类。(1.23) 中的起始 \(3 \times  2\) 棋盘属于 \(\mathcal{N}\) 类。
\end{tcolorbox}

The first move of Right in (1.23) creates two disjoint \(1 \times  2\) games which represent, as before, a game sum, here \(\rightarrow\) the game to the four outcome classes, games \(G\) and \(H\) are called equivalent, written \(G \equiv  H\) , if and only if \(G + J\) and \(H + J\) always belong to the same outcome class, for any other game \(J\) ; this extends Definition 1.5. Every losing game (any game in \(\mathcal{P}\) ) is equivalent to the zero game 0 that has no options, as in Lemma 1.8, by the same argument. As before, Lemma 1.10 holds, that is, adding a losing game does not change the outcome class of a game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在 (1.23) 中，Right 的第一步行动创造了两个不相交的 \(1 \times  2\) 博弈，和之前一样，这代表了一个博弈之和(game sum)，这里 \(\rightarrow\) 将博弈划分为四个结果类，如果且仅当对于任何其他博弈 \(J\) ， \(G + J\) 和 \(H + J\) 总是属于同一结果类时，博弈 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 被称为等价的，记为 \(G \equiv  H\) ；这扩展了定义 1.5。每个失败博弈( \(\mathcal{P}\) 中的任何博弈)都等价于没有选项的零博弈 0，就像引理 1.8 中那样，依据相同的论证。和之前一样，引理 1.10 成立，即添加一个失败博弈不会改变一个博弈的结果类。
\end{tcolorbox}

For any partizan game \(G\) , there is a negative game \(- G\) so that \(G + \left( {-G}\right)  \equiv  0\) as stated in (1.13). This negative game is obtained by exchanging the options of Left and Right, and doing so recursively for their options. For Domineering, the negative of any game position \(G\) (as shown by the squares that have not yet been occupied by dominos) is obtained by turning the board by 90 degrees. For example,

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于任何有偏博弈 \(G\) ，存在一个负博弈 \(- G\) ，使得如 (1.13) 中所述的 \(G + \left( {-G}\right)  \equiv  0\) 成立。这个负博弈是通过交换 Left 和 Right 的选项，并对它们的选项进行递归交换得到的。对于纵横棋(Domineering)，任何博弈位置 \(G\) 的负博弈(由尚未被多米诺骨牌(domino)占据的方格表示)是通过将棋盘旋转 90 度得到的。例如，
\end{tcolorbox}

(1.24)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_37_712_349_510_153_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

because any move by a player in one component can be copied by the other player by the corresponding move in the other component. In that way, the second player always has a move left and has won when the position reached is \(0 + 0\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
因为一个组件中玩家的任何行动都可以被另一个玩家在另一个组件中进行相应的行动所复制。通过这种方式，第二个玩家总是有行动可做，并且当达到 \(0 + 0\) 局势时获胜。
\end{tcolorbox}

Formally, a game \(G\) is defined by two sets of games \({\mathcal{G}}^{L}\) and \({\mathcal{G}}^{R}\) which define the options of Left and Right, respectively. Then \(G\) is written as \(\left\{  {{\mathcal{G}}^{L} \mid  {\mathcal{G}}^{R}}\right\}\) . In concrete examples, one lists directly the elements of the sets \({\mathcal{G}}^{L}\) and \({\mathcal{G}}^{R}\) rather than using set notation. For example, if \({\mathcal{G}}^{L} = {\mathcal{G}}^{R} = \varnothing\) , then we write this game as \(\{ \left| {\} \text{ rather than }\{ \varnothing }\right| \varnothing \}\) . This is the simplest possible game that has no options for either player, which we have denoted by 0 :

\begin{tcolorbox}
形式上，一个博弈 \(G\) 由两组博弈 \({\mathcal{G}}^{L}\) 和 \({\mathcal{G}}^{R}\) 定义，它们分别定义了 Left 和 Right 的选项。那么 \(G\) 可以写成 \(\left\{  {{\mathcal{G}}^{L} \mid  {\mathcal{G}}^{R}}\right\}\) 。在具体例子中，人们直接列出集合 \({\mathcal{G}}^{L}\) 和 \({\mathcal{G}}^{R}\) 的元素，而不是使用集合符号。例如，如果 \({\mathcal{G}}^{L} = {\mathcal{G}}^{R} = \varnothing\) ，那么我们将这个博弈写成 \(\{ \left| {\} \text{ rather than }\{ \varnothing }\right| \varnothing \}\) 。这是可能的最简单的博弈，任何玩家都没有选项，我们用 0 来表示:
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
0 = \{  \mid  \} \text{ . } \tag{1.25}
\]

The options in a game are defined recursively. We can write (1.23) as

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈中的选项是递归定义的。我们可以将 (1.23) 写成
\end{tcolorbox}

(1.26)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_37_583_1122_755_129_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

and continue to define

\begin{tcolorbox}
并继续定义
\end{tcolorbox}

(1.27)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_37_684_1328_556_127_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

where the first option of Left is to put the domino in the top left which leaves Right with no further move, the second (inferior) option to put the domino at the bottom; Right has only one choice. Furthermore,

\begin{tcolorbox}
其中 Left 的第一个选项是将多米诺骨牌(domino)放在左上角，这使得 Right 没有进一步的行动，第二个(较差的)选项是将多米诺骨牌放在底部；Right 只有一种选择。此外，
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\square  = \left\{  {0|}\right\}  ,\;\square \square  = \left\{  {0|0}\right\}  , \tag{1.28}
\]

which are the positions where Left respectively Right have exactly one move left and the other player none. The first of Right's options in (1.26) can also be defined by the recursive definition of game sums as

\begin{tcolorbox}
这些是 Left 或 Right 分别只剩下一步行动，而另一个玩家没有行动的局势。(1.26) 中 Right 的第一个选项也可以通过博弈之和的递归定义来定义为
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\square \square  + \square \square  = \left\{  {\square \square }\right\}  ,
\]

and the second of Right's options in (1.26) is defined by

\begin{tcolorbox}
(1.26) 中 Right 的第二个选项定义为
\end{tcolorbox}

(1.29)The definitions (1.25) to (1.29) are recursive by referring to simpler games, with the shown board configurations as suitable placeholders. It only matters which player eventually loses in subsequent optimal play.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义 (1.25) 到 (1.29) 通过引用更简单的博弈进行递归定义，所示的棋盘配置作为合适的占位符。重要的是在后续的最优玩法中哪个玩家最终失败。
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\text{ 田 = \{B|□ \textbackslash }.}
\]

In (1.27), Left has the dominated option [1] that Left should never move to because it unnecessarily gives Right an extra move. Dominated options can be omitted, which simplifies the recursive description of a game in terms of its

\begin{tcolorbox}
在(1.27)中，先手玩家(左方)有一个被占优选项[1]，先手玩家永远不应该选择这个选项，因为这会不必要地让后手玩家(右方)多走一步。被占优选项可以被省略，这能简化博弈根据其选项进行的递归描述。
\end{tcolorbox}

options. Note: there are no dominated options in the board configuration 1 in

\begin{tcolorbox}
注意:在无偏博弈塞砖棋(Cram)的棋盘布局1中，没有被占优选项。
\end{tcolorbox}

the impartial game Cram. In Cram, this configuration is equivalent to a Nim heap \(* 2\) of size two with its two options \(* 1\) and 0, neither of which dominates the other. For example, in the game sum \(* 2 +  * 1\) , the move to \(* 1 +  * 1\) is a winning move which requires the option *1 of *2. Impartial games are exactly the games \(\left\{  {{\mathcal{G}}^{L} \mid  {\mathcal{G}}^{R}}\right\}\) where \({\mathcal{G}}^{L} = {\mathcal{G}}^{R}\) , and the option sets themselves contain only impartial games. The simplest impartial game is 0 by (1.25), the next simplest is \(\{ 0 \mid  0\}\) which is the Nim heap *1 of size one, which is also denoted by *(just a star).

\begin{tcolorbox}
在塞砖棋(Cram)中，这种布局等价于一个大小为2的尼姆堆(Nim heap) \(* 2\) ，它有两个选项 \(* 1\) 和0，且这两个选项彼此都不占优。例如，在博弈之和 \(* 2 +  * 1\) 中，移动到 \(* 1 +  * 1\) 是一个必胜步(winning move)，这需要用到*2的选项*1。无偏博弈(impartial game)恰好是那些满足 \(\left\{  {{\mathcal{G}}^{L} \mid  {\mathcal{G}}^{R}}\right\}\) (其中 \({\mathcal{G}}^{L} = {\mathcal{G}}^{R}\) )的博弈，并且选项集本身只包含无偏博弈。根据(1.25)，最简单的无偏博弈是0，次简单的是 \(\{ 0 \mid  0\}\) ，它是大小为1的尼姆堆*1，也可以用*(仅一个星号)表示。
\end{tcolorbox}

An important new concept for partizan games is that of a number, which applies to some (but not all) partizan games. If a game is identified with a number, then this number states how many moves player Left is safely ahead in the game. The simplest numbers are integers, but they can also be certain types of fractions. For example, the Domineering position - can be identified with the integer 1 . Furthermore, \(1 + 1 = 2\) , that is, the game sum of numbers corresponds to the arithmetic sum of these numbers. Similarly, the Domineering position identified with -1, in agreement with \(1 + \left( {-1}\right)  \equiv  0\) . For a positive integer \(n\) , if Left is \(n\) moves ahead and makes one of these moves, then Left is still \(n - 1\) moves ahead afterwards. By denoting the game itself by \(n\) , this gives rise to the recursive definition

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于有偏博弈(partizan game)，一个重要的新概念是数的概念，它适用于某些(但不是所有)有偏博弈。如果一个博弈可以用一个数来表示，那么这个数表示先手玩家(左方)在博弈中安全领先的步数。最简单的数是整数，但它们也可以是某些类型的分数。例如，纵横棋(Domineering)的局面 - 可以用整数1表示。此外， \(1 + 1 = 2\) ，即数的博弈之和对应于这些数的算术和。类似地，纵横棋(Domineering)中对应于 -1的局面，与 \(1 + \left( {-1}\right)  \equiv  0\) 一致。对于正整数 \(n\) ，如果先手玩家(左方)领先 \(n\) 步并走了其中一步，那么之后先手玩家(左方)仍然领先 \(n - 1\) 步。通过用 \(n\) 表示博弈本身，这就产生了递归定义
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
n = \{ n - 1 \mid  \} , \tag{1.30}
\]

which has its base case \(1 = \{ 0 \mid  \}\) as in (1.28). Games that are negative numbers are defined as negative games \(- n\) of positive numbers \(n\) . In the game \(- n\) , player Right is ahead by \(n\) moves.

\begin{tcolorbox}
它的基础情况是 \(1 = \{ 0 \mid  \}\) ，如(1.28)所示。负整数对应的博弈被定义为正整数对应博弈的负博弈(negative game) \(- n\) 。在博弈 \(- n\) 中，后手玩家(右方)领先 \(n\) 步。
\end{tcolorbox}

Caution: No impartial game is a number (except for the game 0 ). Nim heaps as defined in (1.18) are very different, and obey different rules such as \(* n +  * n \equiv  0\) . (Nim heaps are sometimes called nimbers to distinguish them from numbers.)

\begin{tcolorbox}
注意:除了博弈0之外，没有无偏博弈可以用数来表示。(1.18)中定义的尼姆堆(Nim heap)非常不同，并且遵循不同的规则，如 \(* n +  * n \equiv  0\) 。(尼姆堆有时被称为尼姆数(nimber)，以区别于数。)
\end{tcolorbox}

In order to define a number more generally, we need a partial order \(\geq\) on games (which is not the "simpler than" order), defined as follows:

\begin{tcolorbox}
为了更一般地定义数，我们需要在博弈上定义一个偏序(partial order) \(\geq\) (它不是“比……更简单”的顺序)，定义如下:
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
G \geq  H \Leftrightarrow  \left( {\forall J : H + J \in  \mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{N} \Rightarrow  G + J \in  \mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{N},}\right.  \tag{1.31}
\]

\[
\forall J : H + J \in  \mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{P} \Rightarrow  G + J \in  \mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{P})
\]

where a game belongs to \(\mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{N}\) if Left wins by moving first, and to \(\mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{P}\) if Left wins by moving second. With this understanding, (1.31) can be written as

\begin{tcolorbox}
其中，如果先手玩家(左方)先走能获胜，则一个博弈属于 \(\mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{N}\) ；如果先手玩家(左方)后走能获胜，则属于 \(\mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{P}\) 。有了这个理解，(1.31)可以写成
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
G \geq  H \Leftrightarrow  \left( {\forall J : \text{ Left wins in }H + J \Rightarrow  \text{ Leftwins in }G + J}\right) .
\]

That is, \(G \geq  H\) if replacing \(H\) by \(G\) never hurts Left, no matter what the context (always assuming optimal play). Then \(\geq\) is a partial order on games because it is obviously reflexive and transitive, and antisymmetric up to equivalence of games:

\begin{tcolorbox}
也就是说，如果无论在什么情况下(始终假设是最优玩法)，用 \(G\) 替换 \(H\) 都不会对先手玩家(左方)造成不利，那么就有 \(G \geq  H\) 。那么 \(\geq\) 是博弈上的一个偏序，因为它显然是自反的(reflexive)和传递的(transitive)，并且在博弈等价的意义下是反对称的(antisymmetric):
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 1.17. \(G \geq  H\) and \(H \geq  G \Leftrightarrow  G \equiv  H\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理1.17. \(G \geq  H\) 且 \(H \geq  G \Leftrightarrow  G \equiv  H\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Recall that \(G \equiv  H\) means that \(G + J\) and \(H + J\) belong to the same outcome class \(\mathcal{L},\mathcal{R},\mathcal{P},\mathcal{N}\) for any game \(J\) , so " \(\Leftarrow\) " is obvious. To show " \(\Rightarrow\) ", assume \(G \geq  H\) and \(H \geq  G\) , and let \(J\) be any game. If \(H + J \in  \mathcal{L}\) then \(G + J \in  \left( {\mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{N}}\right)  \cap  \left( {\mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{P}}\right)  = \mathcal{L}\) by (1.31), because the outcome classes are disjoint. By symmetry, if \(G + J \in  \mathcal{L}\) then \(H + J \in  \mathcal{L}\) . Next, consider the outcome class \(\mathcal{P}\) : If \(H + J \in  \mathcal{P}\) then \(G + J \in  \mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{P}\) by (1.31). If we had \(G + J \in  \mathcal{L}\) then \(H + J \in  \mathcal{L}\) as just shown, but \(H + J \in  \mathcal{P}\) which is disjoint from \(\mathcal{L}\) , so we have \(G + J \in  \mathcal{P}\) . By symmetry, if \(G + J \in  \mathcal{P}\) then \(H + J \in  \mathcal{P}\) . Similar arguments apply for the outcome classes \(\mathcal{R}\) and \(\mathcal{N}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。回想一下， \(G \equiv  H\) 意味着对于任何博弈 \(J\) ， \(G + J\) 和 \(H + J\) 属于同一个结果类 \(\mathcal{L},\mathcal{R},\mathcal{P},\mathcal{N}\) ，所以 “ \(\Leftarrow\) ” 是显然的。为了证明 “ \(\Rightarrow\) ”，假设 \(G \geq  H\) 且 \(H \geq  G\) ，并设 \(J\) 为任意博弈。如果 \(H + J \in  \mathcal{L}\) ，那么由 (1.31) 可得 \(G + J \in  \left( {\mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{N}}\right)  \cap  \left( {\mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{P}}\right)  = \mathcal{L}\) ，因为结果类是不相交的。由对称性可知，如果 \(G + J \in  \mathcal{L}\) ，那么 \(H + J \in  \mathcal{L}\) 。接下来，考虑结果类 \(\mathcal{P}\) :如果 \(H + J \in  \mathcal{P}\) ，那么由 (1.31) 可得 \(G + J \in  \mathcal{L} \cup  \mathcal{P}\) 。如果 \(G + J \in  \mathcal{L}\) ，那么正如刚才所证 \(H + J \in  \mathcal{L}\) ，但 \(H + J \in  \mathcal{P}\) 与 \(\mathcal{L}\) 不相交，所以 \(G + J \in  \mathcal{P}\) 。由对称性可知，如果 \(G + J \in  \mathcal{P}\) ，那么 \(H + J \in  \mathcal{P}\) 。类似的论证适用于结果类 \(\mathcal{R}\) 和 \(\mathcal{N}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Define \(G < H\) as \(H \geq  G\) and not \(G \equiv  H\) . This allows the following recursive definition of a partizan game that is a number.

\begin{tcolorbox}
将 \(G < H\) 定义为 \(H \geq  G\) 且非 \(G \equiv  H\) 。这允许对作为数字的有偏博弈进行如下递归的定义。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 1.18. A game \(G = \left\{  {{\mathcal{G}}^{L} \mid  {\mathcal{G}}^{R}}\right\}\) is a number if all left options \({G}^{L} \in  {\mathcal{G}}^{L}\) of \(G\) and all right options \({G}^{R} \in  {\mathcal{G}}^{R}\) of \(G\) are numbers and fulfill \({G}^{L} < G < {G}^{R}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义 1.18。一个博弈 \(G = \left\{  {{\mathcal{G}}^{L} \mid  {\mathcal{G}}^{R}}\right\}\) 是一个数字，如果 \(G\) 的所有左选项 \({G}^{L} \in  {\mathcal{G}}^{L}\) 和 \(G\) 的所有右选项 \({G}^{R} \in  {\mathcal{G}}^{R}\) 都是数字，并且满足 \({G}^{L} < G < {G}^{R}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The simplest number is \(0 = \{  \mid  \}\) because it has no left or right options, so the condition \({G}^{L} < G < {G}^{R}\) for \(G = 0\) holds vacuously. Similarly, the game defined in (1.30) for a positive integer \(n\) is a number because its left option fulfills \(n - 1 < n\) , and it has no right options.

\begin{tcolorbox}
最简单的数字是 \(0 = \{  \mid  \}\) ，因为它没有左选项或右选项，所以对于 \(G = 0\) 的条件 \({G}^{L} < G < {G}^{R}\) 显然成立。类似地，对于正整数 \(n\) 在 (1.30) 中定义的博弈是一个数字，因为它的左选项满足 \(n - 1 < n\) ，并且它没有右选项。
\end{tcolorbox}

The intuition of the inequalities \({G}^{L} < G < {G}^{R}\) in Definition 1.18 is that by moving in a number, a player gives up a safe advantage and thereby weakens his position, so moving to a left option \({G}^{L}\) weakens the position of Left ( \({G}^{L} < G\) ), and moving to a right option \({G}^{R}\) weakens the position of Right \(\left( {G < {G}^{R}}\right)\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义 1.18 中不等式 \({G}^{L} < G < {G}^{R}\) 的直观理解是，在一个数字博弈中移动时，玩家放弃了安全优势，从而削弱了自己的局面，因此移动到左选项 \({G}^{L}\) 会削弱左方( \({G}^{L} < G\) )的局面，而移动到右选项 \({G}^{R}\) 会削弱右方 \(\left( {G < {G}^{R}}\right)\) 的局面。
\end{tcolorbox}

The advantage that is given up by moving in a number can be small, because games can also be numbers that are fractions. For example, the Domineering

\begin{tcolorbox}
在一个数字博弈中移动所放弃的优势可能很小，因为博弈也可以是分数形式的数字。例如，纵横棋
\end{tcolorbox}

position 1 in (1.27) is given by \(\{ 0, - 1 \mid  1\}\) (or, because -1 is dominated, \(\{ 0 \mid  1\}\) ),

\begin{tcolorbox}
(1.27) 中的局面 1 由 \(\{ 0, - 1 \mid  1\}\) 给出(或者，因为 -1 被占优，由 \(\{ 0 \mid  1\}\) 给出)。
\end{tcolorbox}

where \(0 = \{ \left| \right| \}\) and \(1 = \{ 0 \mid  \}\) according to (1.30), and is therefore a number by by Definition 1.18. That number is the fraction \(\frac{1}{2}\) , that is, Left is half a move ahead in this game, because \(\frac{1}{2} + \frac{1}{2} + \left( {-1}\right)  \equiv  0\) , that is,

\begin{tcolorbox}
其中 \(0 = \{ \left| \right| \}\) 和 \(1 = \{ 0 \mid  \}\) 根据 (1.30)，因此根据定义 1.18 它是一个数。那个数是分数 \(\frac{1}{2}\) ，也就是说，在这个博弈中先手玩家(左玩家)领先半手，因为 \(\frac{1}{2} + \frac{1}{2} + \left( {-1}\right)  \equiv  0\) ，即
\end{tcolorbox}

(1.32)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.3\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_39_738_1874_453_124_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

This can be inferred from the (essentially unique) sequence of optimal moves

\begin{tcolorbox}
这可以从(本质上唯一的)最优走法序列推断出来
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.7\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_39_519_2054_943_129_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.7\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_40_377_199_944_127_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

which shows that Left as starting player loses, and similarly that Right as starting player loses.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这表明左玩家作为起始玩家会失败，同样，右玩家作为起始玩家也会失败。
\end{tcolorbox}

In terms of recursively defined games, starting from the definition of \(0 = \{  \mid  \}\) , (1.32) states

\begin{tcolorbox}
就递归定义的博弈而言，从 \(0 = \{  \mid  \}\) 的定义开始，(1.32) 表明
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \left( {-1}\right)  \equiv  0. \tag{1.33}
\]

This holds for the same reason as for the Domineering moves in (1.32), just a bit more abstract. Recall that \(- 1 = \{  \mid  0\}\) (which is one safe move for Right). In the game sum \(\{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \{  \mid  0\}\) in (1.33), Leftc an only move in the component \(\{ 0 \mid  1\}\) , so that the next game is \(0 + \{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \{  \mid  0\}  = \{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \{  \mid  0\}\) . Here Right has a choice, moving either in the first component to \(1 + \{  \mid  0\}\) , which is \(1 + \left( {-1}\right)  = 0\) and a loss for Left who moves next, or in the second component (using his safe move) to \(\{ 0 \mid  1\}  + 0 = \{ 0 \mid  1\}\) , which is a win for Left. Clearly, Right should choose the former. Hence, Left as starting player loses in \(\{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \{ 0 \mid  1\}\) . By a very similar reasoning, Right as starting player loses, which proves (1.33).

\begin{tcolorbox}
这与 (1.32) 中纵横棋(Domineering)走法的原因相同，只是更抽象一些。回想一下 \(- 1 = \{  \mid  0\}\) (这对右玩家来说是一步安全走法)。在 (1.33) 的博弈之和 \(\{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \{  \mid  0\}\) 中，左玩家只能在组件 \(\{ 0 \mid  1\}\) 中走棋，所以下一个博弈状态是 \(0 + \{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \{  \mid  0\}  = \{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \{  \mid  0\}\) 。在这里，右玩家有一个选择，要么在第一个组件中走到 \(1 + \{  \mid  0\}\) ，这是 \(1 + \left( {-1}\right)  = 0\) 且对接下来走棋的左玩家来说是失败局面，要么在第二个组件中(使用他的安全走法)走到 \(\{ 0 \mid  1\}  + 0 = \{ 0 \mid  1\}\) ，这对左玩家来说是胜利局面。显然，右玩家应该选择前者。因此，左玩家作为起始玩家在 \(\{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \{ 0 \mid  1\}  + \{ 0 \mid  1\}\) 中失败。通过非常类似的推理，右玩家作为起始玩家也会失败，这就证明了 (1.33)。
\end{tcolorbox}

As an extension of this argument, there is a game that can be identified with the fraction \(\frac{1}{{2}^{p}}\) for every positive integer \(p\) , defined recursively as follows:

\begin{tcolorbox}
作为这个论证的扩展，对于每个正整数 \(p\) ，都有一个可以与分数 \(\frac{1}{{2}^{p}}\) 对应的博弈，其递归定义如下:
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\frac{1}{{2}^{p}} = \left\{  {0\left| {\;\frac{1}{{2}^{p - 1}}}\right. }\right\}  \;\text{ and hence }\;\frac{-1}{{2}^{p}} = \left\{  {\left. \frac{-1}{{2}^{p - 1}}\right| \;0}\right\}  . \tag{1.34}
\]

In order to show

\begin{tcolorbox}
为了证明
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\frac{1}{{2}^{p}} + \frac{1}{{2}^{p}} + \frac{-1}{{2}^{p - 1}} \equiv  0, \tag{1.35}
\]

one can see that in this game sum it is better for both players to move in the component \(\frac{1}{{2}^{p}}\) rather than in \(\frac{-1}{{2}^{p - 1}}\) where the player gives up more, similar to the reasoning for (1.33). Hence, no matter who starts, after two moves the position (1.35) becomes the losing position \(\frac{1}{{2}^{p - 1}} + \frac{-1}{{2}^{p - 1}}\) , which shows (1.35).

\begin{tcolorbox}
可以发现，在这个博弈之和中，对于两个玩家来说，在组件 \(\frac{1}{{2}^{p}}\) 中走棋比在 \(\frac{-1}{{2}^{p - 1}}\) 中走棋更好，因为在 \(\frac{-1}{{2}^{p - 1}}\) 中玩家会放弃更多，这与 (1.33) 的推理类似。因此，无论谁先开始，两步之后局面 (1.35) 就会变成失败位置 \(\frac{1}{{2}^{p - 1}} + \frac{-1}{{2}^{p - 1}}\) ，这就证明了 (1.35)。
\end{tcolorbox}

The following is the more general, recursive definition of a game that is a fraction with a power of two in its denominator.

\begin{tcolorbox}
以下是一个更一般的、递归定义的博弈，其值是分母为 2 的幂的分数。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 1.19. Let \(p\) be a positive integer and \(m\) be an odd integer. Define the game \(\frac{m}{{2}^{p}}\) by

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义 1.19。设 \(p\) 是一个正整数， \(m\) 是一个奇数。通过以下方式定义博弈 \(\frac{m}{{2}^{p}}\)
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\frac{m}{{2}^{p}} = \left\{  {\left. \frac{m - 1}{{2}^{p}}\right| \;\frac{m + 1}{{2}^{p}}}\right\}  . \tag{1.36}
\]

On the right-hand side of (1.36), \(m - 1\) and \(m + 1\) are even integers, and we assume that the resulting fractions are maximally canceled as fractions normally are, so they have a smaller denominator, or are integers as in (1.30). Hence, (1.36) is a recursive definition. An example of (1.36) is \(\frac{17}{8} = \left\{  {\frac{16}{8}\left| {\;\frac{18}{8}}\right. }\right\}   = \left\{  {2\left| {\;\frac{9}{4}}\right. }\right\}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
在 (1.36) 的右边， \(m - 1\) 和 \(m + 1\) 是偶数，并且我们假设得到的分数像通常那样进行了最大程度的约分，所以它们的分母更小，或者像 (1.30) 中那样是整数。因此，(1.36) 是一个递归定义。(1.36) 的一个例子是 \(\frac{17}{8} = \left\{  {\frac{16}{8}\left| {\;\frac{18}{8}}\right. }\right\}   = \left\{  {2\left| {\;\frac{9}{4}}\right. }\right\}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Equation (1.33) justifies the definition \(\frac{1}{2} = \{ 0 \mid  1\}\) . In a similar way, one has to show that the game \(\frac{m}{{2}^{p}}\) in (1.36) is a number and that

\begin{tcolorbox}
方程 (1.33) 证明了定义 \(\frac{1}{2} = \{ 0 \mid  1\}\) 的合理性。类似地，必须证明 (1.36) 中的博弈 \(\frac{m}{{2}^{p}}\) 是一个数，并且
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\frac{m}{{2}^{p}} + \frac{m}{{2}^{p}} + \frac{-m}{{2}^{p - 1}} \equiv  0 \tag{1.37}
\]

This is done by using the recursive definition of \(G = \frac{m}{{2}^{p}}\) in (1.36) as \(G = \left\{  {{G}^{L} \mid  {G}^{R}}\right\}\) with \({G}^{L} = \frac{m - 1}{{2}^{p - 1}}\) and \({G}^{R} = \frac{m + 1}{{2}^{p - 1}}\) . According to Definition 1.18, we also have to show \({G}^{L} < G\) and \(G < {G}^{R}\) . For details we refer to chapters 4 and 5 of Albert, Nowakowski, and Wolfe (2007). Exercise 1.14 asks to compute some numbers that represent positions of the game Black-White Hackenbush, as special cases of (1.37).

\begin{tcolorbox}
这可以通过使用 (1.36) 中 \(G = \frac{m}{{2}^{p}}\) 的递归定义来完成，即 \(G = \left\{  {{G}^{L} \mid  {G}^{R}}\right\}\) 且 \({G}^{L} = \frac{m - 1}{{2}^{p - 1}}\) 和 \({G}^{R} = \frac{m + 1}{{2}^{p - 1}}\) 。根据定义 1.18，我们还必须证明 \({G}^{L} < G\) 和 \(G < {G}^{R}\) 。具体细节请参考 Albert、Nowakowski 和 Wolfe(2007 年)的第 4 章和第 5 章。练习 1.14 要求计算一些代表黑白哈肯布什游戏(Black - White Hackenbush)位置的数，作为 (1.37) 的特殊情况。
\end{tcolorbox}

Using Definition 1.19, every rational number with a denominator \({2}^{p}\) for a positive integer \(p\) can be represented as a game. In that game, a player who makes a move gives up a safe advantage of \(\frac{1}{{2}^{p}}\) of a move. In a game sum of numbers, the player will move in the component with largest dominator where he gives up least. A game sum may have components that are not numbers, such as Nim heaps. The number avoidance theorem states that a player should move first in those components, and last in components that are numbers.

\begin{tcolorbox}
使用定义 1.19，分母为 \({2}^{p}\) (其中 \(p\) 为正整数)的每个有理数都可以表示为一个博弈。在该博弈中，进行一步行动的玩家会放弃 \(\frac{1}{{2}^{p}}\) 步的安全优势。在数的博弈之和中，玩家会在分母最大的分量中行动，因为在那里他放弃的最少。博弈之和可能包含非数的分量，例如尼姆堆(Nim heap)。避数定理指出，玩家应先在这些非数的分量中行动，最后在数的分量中行动。
\end{tcolorbox}

Not all partizan games are numbers. Indeed, there are games which are not numbers because moving there confers an extra advantage. For example, the \(2 \times  2\) Domineering board in (1.29) is equal to \(\{ 1 \mid   - 1\}\) . This is called a hot game because moving there gives the player an extra advantage in the form of additional moves. The \(3 \times  2\) Domineering board, which by (1.26) we found to be equal to \(\left\{  {\frac{1}{2} \mid   - 2}\right\}\) , is also a hot game. A number, in contrast, is a cold game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
并非所有有偏博弈(partizan game)都是数。实际上，有些博弈不是数，因为在这些博弈中行动会带来额外的优势。例如，(1.29) 中的 \(2 \times  2\) 纵横棋(Domineering)棋盘等于 \(\{ 1 \mid   - 1\}\) 。这被称为热情博弈(hot game)，因为在那里行动会以额外步数的形式给玩家带来额外的优势。由 (1.26) 可知等于 \(\left\{  {\frac{1}{2} \mid   - 2}\right\}\) 的 \(3 \times  2\) 纵横棋棋盘也是热情博弈。相反，数是冷静博弈(cold game)。
\end{tcolorbox}

If there are several hot components in a game sum, analyzing their temperature is of interest to decide where to move first. This has been applied to some situations of the board game Go with disconnected components. In Chess, different moves are rarely about disconnected parts of the game. However, Chess has the related concept of tempo of development which can outweigh a material advantage.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果博弈之和中有几个热情分量，分析它们的热度对于决定先在何处行动很有意义。这已应用于一些具有不相连部分的围棋局面。在国际象棋中，不同的走法很少涉及游戏的不相连部分。然而，国际象棋有相关的发展节奏概念，这可能会超过物质优势。
\end{tcolorbox}

This concludes our peek into the theory of partizan games. Further comments on this topic, with literature references, are given in the next section.

\begin{tcolorbox}
至此，我们对有偏博弈理论的初步探讨结束。下一节将给出关于该主题的进一步评论以及文献参考。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{1.9 Further Reading}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{1.9 进一步阅读}
\end{tcolorbox}

An undergraduate textbook on combinatorial games is Albert, Nowakowski, and Wolfe (2007), from which we have adopted the name "top-down induction". This book starts from many examples of games, considers in depth the game Dots and Boxes, and treats the theory of short games where (as we have assumed throughout) every position has only finitely many options and no game position is ever revisited (which in "loopy" games is allowed and could lead to a draw). An authoritative graduate textbook is Siegel (2013), which gives excellent overviews before treating each topic in depth, including newer research developments. It stresses the concept of equivalence of games that we have treated in Section 1.4.

\begin{tcolorbox}
Albert、Nowakowski 和 Wolfe(2007 年)编写的一本关于组合博弈的本科教材，我们采用了其中“自上而下归纳(top - down induction)”这一名称。这本书从许多博弈的例子入手，深入探讨了点格棋(Dots and Boxes)博弈，并处理了短博弈理论，在该理论中(正如我们始终假设的那样)每个位置只有有限个选项，并且不会重复访问任何博弈位置(在“循环”博弈中这是允许的，并且可能导致平局)。一本权威的研究生教材是 Siegel(2013 年)的著作，它在深入处理每个主题之前给出了精彩的概述，包括较新的研究进展。它强调了我们在 1.4 节中讨论的博弈等价概念。
\end{tcolorbox}

The classic text on combinatorial game theory is Winning Ways by Berlekamp, Conway, and Guy (2001-2004). Its wit, vivid colour drawings, and the wealth of games it considers make this a very attractive original source. However, the mathematics is hard, and it helps to know first what the topic is about; for example, equivalence of games is rather implicit and written as equality.

\begin{tcolorbox}
关于组合博弈理论的经典著作是 Berlekamp、Conway 和 Guy(2001 - 2004 年)的《Winning Ways》。其诙谐的语言、生动的彩色插图以及所涉及的大量博弈，使其成为极具吸引力的原始资料。然而，其数学内容较难，并且最好先了解该主题的大致内容；例如，博弈的等价性相当隐晦，被写成了相等。
\end{tcolorbox}

All these books consider directly partizan games. We have chosen to focus on the simpler impartial games, apart from our short introduction to partizan games in Section 1.8. For a more detailed study of games as numbers see Albert, Nowakowski, and Wolfe (2007, Section 5.1), where Definition 5.12 is our Definition 1.18.

\begin{tcolorbox}
所有这些书籍都直接讨论有偏博弈。除了在 1.8 节中对有偏博弈的简要介绍外，我们选择专注于较简单的无偏博弈(impartial game)。若要更详细地研究将博弈表示为数的内容，请参阅 Albert、Nowakowski 和 Wolfe(2007 年，第 5.1 节)，其中定义 5.12 就是我们的定义 1.18。
\end{tcolorbox}

The winning strategy for the game Nim based on the binary system was first described by Bouton (1901). The Queen move game is due to Wythoff (1907), described as an extension of Nim. The observation that every impartial game is equivalent to a Nim heap is independently due to Sprague (1935) and Grundy (1939). This is therefore also called the Sprague-Grundy theory of impartial games, and Nim values are also called "Sprague-Grundy values" or just "Grundy values".

\begin{tcolorbox}
基于二进制的尼姆游戏(Nim)必胜策略最早由布顿(Bouton，1901年)提出。皇后移动游戏由维托夫(Wythoff，1907年)提出，他将其描述为尼姆游戏的扩展。每个无偏博弈(impartial game)都等同于一个尼姆堆(Nim heap)这一发现分别归功于斯普拉格(Sprague，1935年)和格鲁迪(Grundy，1939年)。因此，这也被称为无偏博弈的斯普拉格 - 格鲁迪理论(Sprague - Grundy theory)，尼姆值(Nim value)也被称为“斯普拉格 - 格鲁迪值(Sprague - Grundy values)”或简称“格鲁迪值(Grundy values)”。
\end{tcolorbox}

The approach in Section 1.5 to the mex rule is inspired by Berlekamp, Conway, and Guy (2001-2004), chapter 4, which describes Poker Nim, the Rook and Queen move games, Northcott's game (Exercise 1.8), and chapter 5 with Kayles and Lasker's Nim (called Split-Nim in Exercise 1.12). Chomp (Exercises 1.5 and 1.10) was described by Gale (1974); for a generalization see Gale and Neyman (1982). The digraph game in Exercise 1.9 is another great example for understanding the mex rule, due to Fraenkel (1996).

\begin{tcolorbox}
1.5节中对mex规则的处理方法受到了伯利坎普(Berlekamp)、康威(Conway)和盖伊(Guy)(2001 - 2004年)所著书籍第4章的启发，该章介绍了扑克尼姆游戏(Poker Nim)、车和皇后移动游戏、诺斯科特游戏(练习1.8)，第5章介绍了凯尔斯博弈(Kayles)和拉斯克尼姆游戏(在练习1.12中称为分裂尼姆游戏，Split - Nim)。巧取游戏(Chomp，练习1.5和1.10)由盖尔(Gale，1974年)提出；关于其推广可参考盖尔和内曼(Gale and Neyman，1982年)的研究。练习1.9中的有向图博弈(digraph game)是理解mex规则的另一个很好的例子，它由弗伦克尔(Fraenkel，1996年)提出。
\end{tcolorbox}

The systematic study of sums of partizan games led John Conway, one of the authors of Winning Ways, to the insight that it is possible to define in an elegant way real numbers (in fact, a generalization called surreal numbers) as the strengths of positions in certain games. A number is a game \(G\) as in Definition 1.18, but where the sets \({\mathcal{G}}^{L}\) and \({\mathcal{G}}^{R}\) of left options \({G}^{L}\) and right options \({G}^{R}\) are allowed to be infinite. The condition \({G}^{L} < G < {G}^{R}\) generalizes "Dedekind cuts", a standard construction of the real numbers. The original ideas are described in Conway (2001), known as ONAG ("On Numbers and Games"), which is another classic. For an introduction see Conway (1977).

\begin{tcolorbox}
对有偏博弈(partizan game)之和的系统研究使《制胜之道》(Winning Ways)的作者之一约翰·康威(John Conway)认识到，可以用一种优雅的方式将实数(实际上是一种称为超实数的推广)定义为某些博弈中局势的强度。一个数是如定义1.18中的博弈 \(G\) ，但左选项 \({G}^{L}\) 的集合 \({\mathcal{G}}^{L}\) 和右选项 \({G}^{R}\) 的集合 \({\mathcal{G}}^{R}\) 可以是无限集。条件 \({G}^{L} < G < {G}^{R}\) 推广了“戴德金分割(Dedekind cut)”，这是实数的一种标准构造方法。原始思想在康威(Conway，2001年)所著的《论数字与博弈》(ONAG，“On Numbers and Games”)中有所描述，这也是一本经典著作。关于入门介绍可参考康威(Conway，1977年)的书。
\end{tcolorbox}

An informative and entertaining mathematical study of parlour games, with detailed historical notes, is Bewersdorff (2005). Combinatorial games are treated in part II of that book.

\begin{tcolorbox}
贝沃斯多夫(Bewersdorff，2005年)对客厅游戏进行了既有知识性又有趣味性的数学研究，并附有详细的历史注释。该书第二部分讨论了组合博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{1.10 Exercises for Chapter 1}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{1.10 第1章练习}
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.1. For this exercise, be careful to use only the definitions and not your intuitions that are familiar to you about the symbols \(\leq\) and \(<\) . Consider a set \(S\) with a binary relation \(<\) that is transitive and fulfills for all \(x\) in \(S\)

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习1.1。在做本题时，要注意仅使用定义，而不要依赖你对符号 \(\leq\) 和 \(<\) 的熟悉直觉。考虑一个具有二元关系 \(<\) 的集合 \(S\) ，该关系是传递的，并且对于 \(S\) 中的所有 \(x\) 都满足
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\text{ not }x < x\;\text{ ( } < \text{ is irreflexive). } \tag{1.38}
\]

Define the relation \(\leq\) on \(S\) by (1.7), that is, \(x \leq  y \Leftrightarrow  x < y\) or \(x = y\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
通过(1.7)式在 \(S\) 上定义关系 \(\leq\) ，即 \(x \leq  y \Leftrightarrow  x < y\) 或 \(x = y\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Show that \(\leq\) is a partial order, and that \(<\) is obtained from \(\leq\) via (1.6), that is, \(x < y \Leftrightarrow  x \leq  y\) and \(x \neq  y\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 证明 \(\leq\) 是一个偏序(partial order)，并且 \(<\) 可通过(1.6)式从 \(\leq\) 得到，即 \(x < y \Leftrightarrow  x \leq  y\) 且 \(x \neq  y\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Show that if \(<\) is obtained from a partial order \(\leq\) via (1.6), then \(<\) is transitive and irreflexive, and (1.7) holds, that is, \(x \leq  y \Leftrightarrow  x < y\) or \(x = y\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 证明如果 \(<\) 是通过(1.6)式从一个偏序 \(\leq\) 得到的，那么 \(<\) 是传递且非自反的，并且(1.7)式成立，即 \(x \leq  y \Leftrightarrow  x < y\) 或 \(x = y\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.2. Consider the game Nim with heaps of tokens. The players alternately remove some tokens from one of the heaps. The player to remove the last token wins. Try to prove part (a) without reference to the binary representation for playing Nim optimally.

\begin{tcolorbox}
