练习1.2。考虑有若干堆代币的尼姆游戏。玩家轮流从其中一堆中取走一些代币。取走最后一个代币的玩家获胜。在证明(a)部分时，尽量不参考尼姆游戏的最优玩法的二进制表示。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) For all positions with three heaps, where one of the heaps has only one token, describe exactly the losing positions. Justify your answer, for example with a proof by induction, or by theorems on Nim. [Hint: Start with the easy cases to find the pattern.]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 对于所有三堆代币的局势，其中有一堆只有一个代币，准确描述失败位置(losing position)。说明你的答案的合理性，例如用归纳法证明，或者使用尼姆游戏的定理。[提示:从简单情况入手寻找规律。]
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Consider a Nim game with three heaps whose sizes are consecutive integers, for example2,3,4. Show that the only case where this is a losing position is 1,2,3and that otherwise this is always a winning position. [Hint: Use (a).]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 考虑一个三堆代币的尼姆游戏，其堆的大小是连续整数，例如2、3、4。证明这种局势是失败位置的唯一情况是1、2、3，否则总是必胜局势(winning position)。[提示:使用(a)的结论。]
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) Determine all initial winning moves for Nim with three heaps of sizes 8, 11, and 13, using the binary representation of the heap sizes.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) 使用堆大小的二进制表示，确定三堆大小分别为8、11和13的尼姆游戏的所有初始必胜步(winning move)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.3. Misère Nim is played just like Nim but where the last player to move loses. A losing position is therefore a single Nim heap with a single token in it (which the player then has to take). Another losing position is given by three heaps with a single token each.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习1.3。反向尼姆游戏(misère Nim)的玩法与尼姆游戏(Nim)相同，但最后一个行动的玩家输。因此，一个包含单个棋子的单个尼姆堆(Nim heap)就是一个失败位置(losing position)(玩家必须取走该棋子)。另一个失败位置是三个各含一个棋子的尼姆堆。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Determine the winning and losing positions of misère Nim with one or two heaps, and the winning moves from a winning position.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 确定有一个或两个尼姆堆的反向尼姆游戏的必胜局势(winning position)和失败位置，以及从必胜局势出发的必胜步(winning move)。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Are three heaps with sizes1,2,3winning or losing in misère Nim?

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 在反向尼姆游戏中，大小为1、2、3的三个尼姆堆是必胜局势还是失败位置？
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) Try to describe the losing positions in misère Nim for any number of heaps. [Hint: They are closely related to the losing positions in Nim with normal play.]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) 尝试描述任意数量尼姆堆的反向尼姆游戏的失败位置。[提示:它们与正常规则(normal play)的尼姆游戏的失败位置密切相关。]
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.4. The impartial game Cram is played on a board of \(m \times  n\) squares, where players alternately place a domino on the board which covers two adjacent squares that are free (not yet occupied by a domino), vertically or horizontally. The

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习1.4。无偏博弈(impartial game)塞砖棋(Cram)在一个 \(m \times  n\) 方格的棋盘上进行，玩家轮流在棋盘上放置一块多米诺骨牌(domino)，该骨牌覆盖两个相邻的空格(尚未被多米诺骨牌占据)，可以是垂直或水平放置。
\end{tcolorbox}

first player who cannot place a domino any more loses. Example play for a \(2 \times  3\) board:

\begin{tcolorbox}
第一个无法再放置多米诺骨牌的玩家输。 \(2 \times  3\) 棋盘的示例玩法:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.7\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_44_357_317_897_124_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

(a) Who will win in \(3 \times  3\mathrm{{Cram}}\) ? [Hint: Use the symmetry of the game to investigate possible moves, and remember that it suffices to find one winning strategy.]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 在 \(3 \times  3\mathrm{{Cram}}\) 的情况下谁会赢？[提示:利用游戏的对称性来研究可能的走法，并记住找到一种必胜策略就足够了。]
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Who will win in \(m \times  n\) Cram when both \(m\) and \(n\) are even? (That player has a simple and precisely definable winning strategy which you have to find.)

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 当 \(m\) 和 \(n\) 都是偶数时，在 \(m \times  n\) 的塞砖棋中谁会赢？(该玩家有一个简单且可精确定义的必胜策略，你必须找到它。)
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) Who will win in \(m \times  n\) Cram when \(m\) is odd and \(n\) is even? (Easy with (b).)

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) 当 \(m\) 是奇数且 \(n\) 是偶数时，在 \(m \times  n\) 的塞砖棋中谁会赢？(利用(b)的结果很容易得出。)
\end{tcolorbox}

Note (not a question): Because of the known answers from (b) and (c), this game is more interesting for "real play" on an \(m \times  n\) board where both \(m\) and \(n\) are odd. Play it with your friends on a \(5 \times  5\) board, for example. The situation often decomposes into independent parts, like contiguous fields of2,3,4,5,6squares, that have a known winner, which may help you analyse the situation.

\begin{tcolorbox}
注意(不是问题):由于从(b)和(c)中已知答案，这个游戏在 \(m \times  n\) 棋盘上“实际玩”时更有趣，其中 \(m\) 和 \(n\) 都是奇数。例如，和你的朋友在 \(5 \times  5\) 棋盘上玩这个游戏。局势通常会分解成独立的部分，比如2、3、4、5、6个方格的连续区域，这些区域的获胜者是已知的，这可能有助于你分析局势。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.5. Consider the following game Chomp: A rectangular array of \(m \times  n\) dots is given, in \(m\) rows and \(n\) columns, like \(3 \times  4\) in the next picture on the left. A dot in row \(i\) (from the top) and column \(j\) (from left) is named(i, j). A move consists in picking a dot(i, j)and removing it and all other dots to the right and below it, which means removing all dots \(\left( {{i}^{\prime },{j}^{\prime }}\right)\) with \({i}^{\prime } \geq  i\) and \({j}^{\prime } \geq  j\) , as shown for \(\left( {i,j}\right)  = \left( {2,3}\right)\) in the middle picture, resulting in the picture on the right:

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习1.5。考虑以下巧取游戏(Chomp):给定一个由 \(m \times  n\) 个点组成的矩形阵列，有 \(m\) 行和 \(n\) 列，就像下一张图左边的 \(3 \times  4\) 那样。位于第 \(i\) 行(从顶部开始)和第 \(j\) 列(从左边开始)的点被命名为(i, j)。一步行动包括选择一个点(i, j)并移除它以及它右边和下方的所有其他点，这意味着移除所有满足 \({i}^{\prime } \geq  i\) 且 \({j}^{\prime } \geq  j\) 的点 \(\left( {{i}^{\prime },{j}^{\prime }}\right)\) ，如中间图中 \(\left( {i,j}\right)  = \left( {2,3}\right)\) 所示，结果如右图所示:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.7\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_44_324_1314_985_174_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Player I is the first player to move, players alternate, and the last player who removes a dot loses.

\begin{tcolorbox}
玩家I是第一个行动的玩家，玩家轮流行动，最后一个移除点的玩家输。
\end{tcolorbox}

One can think of these dots as (real) cookies: a move is to eat a cookie and all those to the right and below it, but the top left cookie is poisoned.

\begin{tcolorbox}
可以把这些点想象成(真正的)饼干:一步行动就是吃掉一块饼干以及它右边和下方的所有饼干，但左上角的饼干是有毒的。
\end{tcolorbox}

In the following questions, do not rush into any quick and false application of Nim values. Part (c) has an elegant but tricky answer.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在以下问题中，不要急于快速且错误地应用尼姆值(Nim value)。(c)部分有一个巧妙但棘手的答案。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Assuming optimal play, determine the winning player and a winning move for Chomp of size \(2 \times  2\) , size \(2 \times  3\) , size \(2 \times  n\) , and size \(m \times  m\) , where \(m \geq  3\) . Justify your answers.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 假设进行最优玩法，确定大小为 \(2 \times  2\) 、大小为 \(2 \times  3\) 、大小为 \(2 \times  n\) 和大小为 \(m \times  m\) 的巧取游戏的获胜玩家和必胜步，其中 \(m \geq  3\) 。证明你的答案。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) As it is described here, Chomp is a misère game where the last player to make a move loses. Suppose we want to play the same game but so that the normal play convention applies, where the last player to move wins. How should the array of dots be changed to achieve this? (Normal play with the array as given would be a boring game, with the obvious winning move of taking the top left dot(1,1).)

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 如这里所描述的，巧取游戏是一个反常规则的游戏，最后一个行动的玩家输。假设我们想玩同样的游戏，但采用正常规则，即最后一个行动的玩家赢。为了实现这一点，点的阵列应该如何改变？(对于给定的阵列采用正常规则会是一个无聊的游戏，明显的必胜步是取走左上角的点(1,1)。)
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) Show that when Chomp is played for a game of any size \(m \times  n\) , player I can always win (unless \(m = n = 1\) ). [Hint: You only have to show that a winning move exists, but you do not have to describe that winning move.]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) 证明当进行任意大小为 \(m \times  n\) 的巧取游戏时，玩家一总能获胜(除非 \(m = n = 1\) )。[提示:你只需证明存在必胜步，但无需描述该必胜步。]
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.6.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 1.6。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Complete the entries of equivalent Nim heaps for the Queen-move game in columns 5 and 6, rows 0 to 3, in the table in Figure 1.4 (assuming the queen can be anywhere on the board).

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 完成图 1.4 表格中第 5 列和第 6 列、第 0 行到第 3 行的皇后移动博弈等效尼姆堆的条目(假设皇后可以在棋盘上的任意位置)。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Describe all winning moves in the game sum of the Queen-move game and the Nim heap in Figure 1.3.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 描述图 1.3 中皇后移动博弈和尼姆堆的博弈之和游戏中的所有必胜步。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.7. Consider the game Cram from Exercise 1.4, played on a \(1 \times  n\) board for \(n \geq  2\) . Let \({D}_{n}\) be the Nim value of that game, so that the starting position of the \(1 \times  n\) board is equivalent to a Nim heap of size \({D}_{n}\) . For example, \({D}_{2} = 1\) because the \(1 \times  2\) board is equivalent to \(* 1\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 1.7。考虑练习 1.4 中的塞砖棋游戏，在 \(1 \times  n\) 的棋盘上进行，其中 \(n \geq  2\) 。设 \({D}_{n}\) 为该游戏的尼姆值，使得 \(1 \times  n\) 棋盘的起始位置等效于大小为 \({D}_{n}\) 的尼姆堆。例如， \({D}_{2} = 1\) ，因为 \(1 \times  2\) 棋盘等效于 \(* 1\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) How is \({D}_{n}\) computed from smaller values \({D}_{k}\) for \(k < n\) ? [Hint: Use sums of games and the mex rule. The notation for Nim sums is \(\oplus\) , where \(a \oplus  b = c\) if and only if \(* a +  * b \equiv   * c\) .]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 如何从 \(k < n\) 时的较小值 \({D}_{k}\) 计算 \({D}_{n}\) ？[提示:使用博弈之和与 mex 规则。尼姆和的表示法为 \(\oplus\) ，其中当且仅当 \(* a +  * b \equiv   * c\) 时 \(a \oplus  b = c\) 。]
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Give the values of \({D}_{n}\) up to \(n = {10}\) (or more, if you are ambitious - higher values come at \(n = {16}\) , and at some point they even repeat, but before you detect that you will probably have run out of patience). For which values of \(n\) in \(\{ 1,\ldots ,{10}\}\) is Cram on a \(1 \times  n\) board a losing game?

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 给出 \({D}_{n}\) 直到 \(n = {10}\) 的值(如果你有雄心壮志，也可以给出更多值——更高的值在 \(n = {16}\) 出现，并且在某个时候它们甚至会重复，但在你发现这一点之前，你可能已经失去耐心了)。对于 \(\{ 1,\ldots ,{10}\}\) 中的哪些 \(n\) 值， \(1 \times  n\) 棋盘上的塞砖棋是必败游戏？
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.8. Consider the following game on a rectangular board where a white and a black counter are placed in each row, like in this example:

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 1.8。考虑在矩形棋盘上进行的以下游戏，每行放置一个白色和一个黑色棋子，例如:
\end{tcolorbox}

(*)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.3\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_45_750_1464_385_393_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Player I is White and starts, and player II is Black. Players take turns. In a move, a player moves a counter of his color to any other square within its row, but may not jump over the other counter. For example, in (*) above, in row 8 White may move from e8 to any of the squares \(\mathrm{c}8,\mathrm{\;d}8,\mathrm{\;f}8,\mathrm{\;g}8\) , or h8. The player who can no longer move loses.

\begin{tcolorbox}
玩家一是白方且先行动，玩家二是黑方。玩家轮流行动。在一次行动中，玩家将自己颜色的棋子移动到其所在行的任何其他方格，但不能跳过另一个棋子。例如，在上面的 (*) 中，在第 8 行，白方可以从 e8 移动到 \(\mathrm{c}8,\mathrm{\;d}8,\mathrm{\;f}8,\mathrm{\;g}8\) 中的任何一个方格，或 h8。无法再移动的玩家输。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Who will win in the following position?

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 在以下局面中谁会获胜？
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.3\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_46_610_203_387_396_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

(b) Show that White can win in position (*) above. Give at least two winning moves from that position. Justify your answers. [Hint: Compare this with another game that is not impartial and that also violates the ending condition, but that nevertheless is close to Nim and ends in finite time when played well.]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 证明白方在上面的 (*) 局面中可以获胜。从该局面给出至少两种必胜步。说明你的答案的合理性。[提示:将此游戏与另一个非无偏博弈且也违反终止条件的游戏进行比较，但该游戏仍然接近尼姆游戏，并且如果玩得好，会在有限时间内结束。]
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.9. Consider the following network (in technical terms, a directed graph or "digraph"). Each circle, here marked with one of the letters A to P, represents a node of the network. Some of these nodes (here A, F, G, H, and K) have counters on them. There can be any number of counters on a node, like currently the two counters on \(\mathrm{H}\) . In a move, one of the counters is moved to a neighbouring node in the direction of the arrow as indicated, for example from \(\mathrm{F}\) to \(\mathrm{I}\) (but not from \(\mathrm{F}\) to C, nor directly from F to D, say). Players alternate, and the first player no longer able to move loses.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 1.9。考虑以下网络(用技术术语来说，是一个有向图或“digraph”)。每个圆圈，这里用字母 A 到 P 之一标记，表示网络的一个节点。其中一些节点(这里是 A、F、G、H 和 K)上有棋子。一个节点上可以有任意数量的棋子，例如目前 \(\mathrm{H}\) 上有两个棋子。在一次行动中，其中一个棋子沿着箭头方向移动到相邻节点，例如从 \(\mathrm{F}\) 到 \(\mathrm{I}\) (但不能从 \(\mathrm{F}\) 到 C，也不能直接从 F 到 D)。玩家轮流行动，第一个无法再移动的玩家输。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_46_450_1208_742_541_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

(a) Explain why this game fulfills the ending condition.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 解释为什么这个游戏满足终止条件。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Who is winning in the above position? If it is the first player to move, describe all possible winning moves. Justify your answer.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 在上述局面中谁会获胜？如果是先手玩家行动，描述所有可能的必胜步。说明你的答案的合理性。
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) How does the answer to (b) change when the arrow from \(\mathrm{J}\) to \(\mathrm{K}\) is reversed so that it points from \(\mathrm{K}\) to \(\mathrm{J}\) instead?

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) 当从 \(\mathrm{J}\) 到 \(\mathrm{K}\) 的箭头反转，使其从 \(\mathrm{K}\) 指向 \(\mathrm{J}\) 时，(b) 的答案会如何变化？
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.10. Consider the game Chomp from Exercise 1.5 of size \(2 \times  4\) , in a game sum with a Nim heap of size 4.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 1.10。考虑练习 1.5 中大小为 \(2 \times  4\) 的巧取游戏，与大小为 4 的尼姆堆进行博弈之和。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_47_564_191_757_147_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

What are the winning moves of the starting player I, if any? [Hint: Represent Chomp as a game in the normal play convention (see Exercise 1.5(b), by changing the dot pattern), so that the losing player is not the player who takes the "poisoned cookie", but the player who can no longer move. This will simplify finding the various Nim values.]

\begin{tcolorbox}
先手玩家 I 是否有必胜步？如果有，是哪些？[提示:将巧取游戏(Chomp)表示为正常规则下的博弈(见练习 1.5(b)，通过改变点模式)，这样输的玩家不是拿到“有毒曲奇”的玩家，而是无法继续行动的玩家。这将简化计算各种尼姆值(Nim value)的过程。]
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.11. In \(m \times  n\) Cram (see Exercise 1.4), a rectangular board of \(m \times  n\) squares is given. The two players alternately place a domino either horizontally or vertically on two unoccupied adjacent squares, which then become occupied. The last player to be able to place a domino wins.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 1.11。在 \(m \times  n\) 塞砖棋(Cram)游戏中(见练习 1.4)，给定一个由 \(m \times  n\) 个方格组成的矩形棋盘。两名玩家交替地将一块多米诺骨牌(domino)水平或垂直放置在两个未被占据的相邻方格上，放置后这两个方格就被占据了。最后能够放置多米诺骨牌的玩家获胜。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Find the Nim value (size of the equivalent Nim heap) of \(2 \times  3\) Cram.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 求出 \(2 \times  3\) 塞砖棋的尼姆值(等效尼姆堆(Nim heap)的大小)。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Find all winning moves, if any, for the game sum of a \(2 \times  3\) Cram game and a \(1 \times  4\) Cram game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 对于一个 \(2 \times  3\) 塞砖棋游戏和一个 \(1 \times  4\) 塞砖棋游戏的博弈之和(game sum)，如果存在必胜步，找出所有的必胜步。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.12. Consider the following variant of Nim called Split-Nim, which is played with heaps of tokens as in Nim. Like in Nim, a player can remove some tokens from one of the heaps, or else split a heap into two (not necessarily equal) new heaps. For example, a heap of size 4 can be reduced to size 3, 2, 1, or 0 as in ordinary Nim, or be split into two heaps of sizes 1 and 3, respectively, or into two heaps of sizes 2 and 2. As usual, the last player to be able to move wins.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 1.12。考虑尼姆游戏(Nim)的一个变体，称为分裂尼姆游戏(Split - Nim)，它和尼姆游戏一样使用代币堆进行游戏。和尼姆游戏一样，玩家可以从其中一个代币堆中移除一些代币，或者将一个代币堆分裂成两个(不一定相等)新的代币堆。例如，一个大小为 4 的代币堆可以像普通尼姆游戏那样减少到大小为 3、2、1 或 0，或者分裂成大小分别为 1 和 3 的两个代币堆，或者分裂成大小为 2 和 2 的两个代币堆。和往常一样，最后能够行动的玩家获胜。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Find the Nim values (size of equivalent Nim heaps) of the single Split-Nim heaps of size1,2,3, and 4, respectively.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 分别求出大小为 1、2、3 和 4 的单个分裂尼姆代币堆的尼姆值(等效尼姆堆的大小)。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Find all winning moves, if any, when Split-Nim is played with three heaps of sizes1,2,3.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 当分裂尼姆游戏使用大小为 1、2、3 的三个代币堆进行时，如果存在必胜步，找出所有的必胜步。
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) Find all winning moves, if any, when Split-Nim is played with three heaps of sizes 1, 2, 4 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) 当分裂尼姆游戏使用大小为 1、2、4 的三个代币堆进行时，如果存在必胜步，找出所有的必胜步。
\end{tcolorbox}

(d) Determine if the following statement is true or false: "Two heaps in Split-Nim are equivalent if and only if they have equal size." Justify your answer.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(d) 判断以下陈述的真假:“在分裂尼姆游戏中，两个代币堆等效当且仅当它们的大小相等。”证明你的答案。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.13. The impartial game Gray Hackenbush is played on a figure consisting of nodes and edges that are connected to these nodes or to the ground (the ground is the dashed line in the pictures below). A move is to remove an edge, and with it all the edges that are then no longer connected to the ground. For example, in the leftmost figure below, one can remove any edge in one of the three stalks. Removing the second edge from the stalk that consists of three edges takes the topmost edge with it, leaving only a single edge. All edges are colored gray, which means they can be removed by either player, so this is an impartial game. As usual, players alternate and the last player able to move wins.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 1.13。无偏博弈灰色哈肯布什游戏(Gray Hackenbush)在一个由节点(node)和连接到这些节点或地面的边(edge)组成的图形上进行(地面在下面的图片中用虚线表示)。一次行动是移除一条边，同时移除所有不再与地面相连的边。例如，在下面最左边的图形中，玩家可以移除三根茎中的任何一条边。移除由三条边组成的茎中的第二条边时，会同时移除最上面的边，只留下一条边。所有的边都被涂成灰色，这意味着任何玩家都可以移除它们，所以这是一个无偏博弈。和往常一样，玩家交替行动，最后能够行动的玩家获胜。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Compute the Nim values for the following three Gray Hackenbush figures (using what you know about Nim, and the mex rule):

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 计算以下三个灰色哈肯布什图形的尼姆值(使用你所知道的尼姆游戏和 mex 规则):
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_48_272_202_1098_205_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

(b) Compute the Nim values for the following four Gray Hackenbush figures:

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 计算以下四个灰色哈肯布什图形的尼姆值:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_48_263_474_1112_229_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

(c) Based on the answer in (b), give a rule to compute the Nim value of a "tree" that is constructed by putting several "branches" with known Nim values on top of a "stem" of size \(n\) , for example \(n = 2\) in the picture below, where three branches of heights3,2, and 4 are put on top of the "stem". Prove the correctness of that rule (you may find it advantageous to glue the branches together at the bottom, as in (b)). Use this rule to find the Nim value of the rightmost picture.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) 根据 (b) 的答案，给出一个规则来计算一个“树”的尼姆值，这个“树”是通过将几个已知尼姆值的“分支”放在一个大小为 \(n\) 的“茎”上构建的，例如下面图片中的 \(n = 2\) ，其中高度为 3、2 和 4 的三个分支被放在“茎”的顶部。证明该规则的正确性(你可能会发现像 (b) 中那样将分支在底部粘在一起会更有利)。使用这个规则来求出最右边图片的尼姆值。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_48_276_1093_1162_313_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Exercise 1.14. This exercise is about partizan games as treated in Section 1.8.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 1.14。本练习是关于第 1.8 节中讨论的有偏博弈(partizan game)。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Complete the proof of (1.32) by showing that Right as starting player loses.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 通过证明右玩家(Right player)为先手时会输来完成 (1.32) 的证明。
\end{tcolorbox}

The partizan game (Black-White) Hackenbush is played on a figure consisting of nodes and black or white edges that are connected to these nodes or to the ground, as in the pictures below. In a move the Left player removes a black edge and the Right player a white edge; if no such edge exists then the player loses. After such a move, all nodes and edges no longer connected to the ground disappear as well. For example, in a Hackenbush figure with a white edge on top of a black edge, Left can remove the bottom (black) edge and creates the zero game, whereas Right can remove the top (white) edge and leaves a single black edge.

\begin{tcolorbox}
有偏博弈(黑白)哈肯布什游戏((Black - White) Hackenbush)在一个由节点和连接到这些节点或地面的黑色或白色边组成的图形上进行，如下方图片所示。在一次行动中，左玩家(Left player)移除一条黑色边，右玩家移除一条白色边；如果不存在这样的边，那么该玩家输。在这样的行动之后，所有不再与地面相连的节点和边也会消失。例如，在一个黑色边上面有一条白色边的哈肯布什图形中，左玩家可以移除底部(黑色)边并形成零博弈，而右玩家可以移除顶部(白色)边并留下一条黑色边。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_48_253_2008_1136_181_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

(b) Recall that for a combinatorial game \(G\) its negative is a game \(- G\) so that \(G + \left( {-G}\right)  \equiv  0\) . Describe the negative of a Hackenbush figure, and give an example. [Hint: Compare (1.24).]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 回顾一下，对于一个组合博弈 \(G\) ，它的负博弈是一个博弈 \(- G\) ，使得 \(G + \left( {-G}\right)  \equiv  0\) 。描述哈肯布什图形的负博弈，并给出一个例子。[提示:参考 (1.24)。]
\end{tcolorbox}

We consider Hackenbush stalks, which are just paths of edges of either color. It can be shown that any such stalk is a number, with some examples considered here.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们考虑哈肯布什杆，它们只是由任意一种颜色的边组成的路径。可以证明，任何这样的杆都代表一个数字，这里会考虑一些例子。
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) Draw the game that represents the number 3 (where Left is 3 moves ahead).

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) 画出表示数字 3 的博弈图(其中先手玩家已经进行了 3 步)。
\end{tcolorbox}

(d) Find the numbers represented by the following Hackenbush stalks. Justify your answers. It helps to study and understand (1.36), and to consider only undominated moves of the players; also, compare the strength of the Left (black) player for these games to get an idea about the answer:

\begin{tcolorbox}
(d) 找出以下哈肯布什杆所代表的数字。证明你的答案。研究并理解 (1.36) 会有帮助，并且只考虑玩家的非劣势移动；此外，比较这些博弈中先手(黑色)玩家的实力，以了解答案:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_49_614_742_759_238_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

where you can use as shorthand the following notation for these games:

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于这些博弈，你可以使用以下简写符号:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_49_653_1101_677_122_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

(e) Construct a Hackenbush game (preferably a single stalk) that represents the number \(\frac{7}{4}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(e) 构造一个哈肯布什博弈(最好是单个杆)，它代表数字 \(\frac{7}{4}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 1.15. (Albert, Nowakowski, and Wolfe, 2007, Prep Problems 4.1 and 4.2.)

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 1.15。(Albert、Nowakowski 和 Wolfe，2007 年，预备问题 4.1 和 4.2。)
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Show that if \(G\) and \(H\) are partizan games and Left wins when moving second in \(G\) and in \(H\) , then Left wins when moving second in \(G + H\) . [Hint: Express what it means for Left to win when moving second in terms of outcome classes, as in (1.31).]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 证明如果 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 是有偏博弈，并且先手玩家在 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 中后手移动时获胜，那么先手玩家在 \(G + H\) 中后手移动时也获胜。[提示:用结果类来表示先手玩家后手移动获胜的含义，如 (1.31) 所示。]
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Give an example of games \(G\) and \(H\) so that Left wins when moving first in \(G\) and in \(H\) , but cannot win in \(G + H\) , even if Left can choose to move first or second in the game sum \(G + H\) . [Hint: Can both games be numbers? Or impartial games?] 2

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 给出博弈 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 的一个例子，使得先手玩家在 \(G\) 和 \(H\) 中先手移动时获胜，但在先手玩家可以选择在博弈之和 \(G + H\) 中先手或后手移动的情况下，也无法在 \(G + H\) 中获胜。[提示:这两个博弈都能是数字博弈吗？或者是无偏博弈吗？] 2
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Congestion Games}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{拥塞博弈}
\end{tcolorbox}

Congestion means that a shared resource, such as a road, becomes more costly when more people use it. In a congestion game, multiple players decide on which resource to use, with the aim to minimize their cost. This interaction defines a game because the cost depends on what the other players do.

\begin{tcolorbox}
拥塞是指当更多人使用共享资源(如道路)时，该资源的使用成本会增加。在拥塞博弈中，多个玩家决定使用哪种资源，目的是最小化他们的成本。这种相互作用构成了一个博弈，因为成本取决于其他玩家的选择。
\end{tcolorbox}

We present congestion networks as a model of traffic where individual users choose routes from an origin to a destination. Each edge of the network is congested by creating a cost to each user that weakly increases with the number of users of that edge. The central result (Theorem 2.2) states that every congestion game has an equilibrium where no user can individually reduce her cost by changing her chosen route.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们将拥塞网络作为交通模型，其中单个用户从起点到终点选择路线。网络的每条边都会产生拥塞，使得每个用户的成本随着使用该边的用户数量的增加而弱增加。核心结果(定理 2.2)表明，每个拥塞博弈都存在一个均衡，在该均衡下，没有用户可以通过改变其选择的路线来单独降低其成本。
\end{tcolorbox}

Like Chapter 1, this is another introductory chapter to an active and diverse area of game theory, where we can quickly show some important and nontrivial results. It also gives an introduction to the game-theoretic concepts of strategies and equilibrium before we develop the general theory.

\begin{tcolorbox}
与第 1 章一样，这是博弈论中一个活跃且多样化领域的另一章导论，我们可以快速展示一些重要且非平凡的结果。在我们发展一般理论之前，它还介绍了博弈论中的策略和均衡概念。
\end{tcolorbox}

Equilibrium is the result of selfish routing by the users who individually minimize their costs. This is typically not the socially optimal outcome, which could have a lower average cost. A simple example is the "Pigou network" (see Section 2.2), named after the English economist Arthur Pigou, who introduced the concept of an externality (such as congestion) to economics. Even more surprisingly, the famous Braess paradox, threated in Section 2.3, shows that adding capacity to a road network can worsen equilibrium congestion.

\begin{tcolorbox}
均衡是用户为了最小化自身成本而进行自私路由的结果。这通常不是社会最优结果，社会最优结果可能具有更低的平均成本。一个简单的例子是“庇古网络”(见第 2.2 节)，它以英国经济学家亚瑟·庇古的名字命名，他将外部性(如拥塞)的概念引入了经济学。更令人惊讶的是，著名的布雷斯悖论(在第 2.3 节讨论)表明，增加道路网络的容量可能会加剧均衡拥塞。
\end{tcolorbox}

In Section 2.4 we give the general definition of congestion networks. In Section 2.5 we prove that every congestion game has an equilibrium. This is proved with a cleverly chosen potential function, which is a single function that simultaneously reflects the change in cost of every user when she changes her strategy. Its minimum over all strategy choices therefore defines an equilibrium.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在第 2.4 节中，我们给出拥塞网络的一般定义。在第 2.5 节中，我们证明每个拥塞博弈都存在一个均衡。这是通过巧妙选择的势函数来证明的，势函数是一个单一的函数，它同时反映了每个用户改变策略时其成本的变化。因此，它在所有策略选择上的最小值定义了一个均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

In Section 2.6, we explain the wider context of the presented model. It is the discrete model of atomic (non-splittable) flow with finitely many individual users who choose their traffic routes. As illustrated by the considered examples, the resulting equilibria are often not unique when the "last" user can optimally choose between more than one edge. The limit of many users is the continuous model of splittable flow where users can be infinitesmally small fractions of a "mass" of users who want to travel from an origin to a destination. In this model the equilibrium is often unique, but its existence proof is more technical and not given here.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在第 2.6 节中，我们解释所提出模型的更广泛背景。它是原子流(不可拆分)的离散模型，有有限数量的单个用户选择他们的交通路线。正如所考虑的例子所示，当“最后一个”用户可以在多条边上进行最优选择时，得到的均衡通常不是唯一的。大量用户的极限情况是可拆分流的连续模型，其中用户可以是想要从起点到终点旅行的“用户群体”的无穷小部分。在这个模型中，均衡通常是唯一的，但它的存在性证明更具技术性，这里不给出。
\end{tcolorbox}

We also mention the Price of Anarchy that compares the worst equilibrium cost with the socially optimal cost. For further details we give references in Section 2.7.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们还提到了无政府代价(Price of Anarchy)，它用于比较最差均衡成本与社会最优成本。更多详细信息请参考2.7节中的参考文献。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{2.1 Prerequisites and Learning Outcomes}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{2.1 先决条件与学习成果}
\end{tcolorbox}

There are no particular prequisites for this chapter. We use the terminology of directed graphs with nodes and their connecting edges (with possibly several "parallel" edges that connect the same two nodes); they are formally defined in Section 2.4. Standard calculus is used to find the minimum of a function of one variable. In Section 2.5 the flow through a network, given by the collection of flows \({f}_{e}\) through each edge \(e\) , is denoted by the letter \(f\) , which is here a variable.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本章没有特定的先决条件。我们使用有向图的术语，包括节点及其连接的边(可能存在连接相同两个节点的多条“平行”边)；它们在2.4节中有正式定义。使用标准微积分来求单变量函数的最小值。在2.5节中，网络中的流由每条边 \(e\) 上的流 \({f}_{e}\) 集合给出，用字母 \(f\) 表示，这里它是一个变量。
\end{tcolorbox}

After studying this chapter, you should be able to:

\begin{tcolorbox}
学习本章后，你应该能够:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item understand the concept of congestion networks with their cost functions, and how edges in the network have a flow that depends on users' chosen paths;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 理解拥塞网络(congestion network)及其成本函数的概念，以及网络中的边如何具有取决于用户所选路径的流；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item find an equilibrium flow in a network, and the socially optimal flow, and compare them;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 找出网络中的均衡流(equilibrium flow)和社会最优流，并对它们进行比较；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item understand how a potential function helps finding an equilibrium.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 理解势函数(potential function)如何帮助找到均衡。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{2.2 Introduction: The Pigou Network}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{2.2 引言:庇古网络(Pigou network)}
\end{tcolorbox}

Getting around at rush hour takes much longer than at less busy times. Commuters take buses where people have to queue to board. There are more buses that fill the bus lanes, and cars are in the way and clog the streets because only a limited number can pass each green light. Commuters can take the bus, walk, or ride a bike, and cars can choose different routes. Congestion, which slows everything down, depends on how many people use certain forms of transport, and which route they choose. We assume that people try to choose an optimal route, but what is optimal depends on what others do. This is an interactive situation, which we call a game. We describe a mathematical model that makes the rules of this game precise.

\begin{tcolorbox}
高峰时段出行比非繁忙时段要花费长得多的时间。通勤者乘坐公交车时需要排队上车。更多的公交车挤满了公交专用道，而汽车也会阻碍交通，使街道堵塞，因为每个绿灯期间只有有限数量的车辆能够通过。通勤者可以选择乘坐公交车、步行或骑自行车，汽车也可以选择不同的路线。拥堵会使一切变慢，它取决于有多少人使用特定的交通方式以及他们选择的路线。我们假设人们会尝试选择最优路线，但什么是最优路线取决于其他人的选择。这是一种交互式的情况，我们称之为博弈。我们将描述一个数学模型，精确地定义这个博弈的规则。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 2.1 shows an example of a congestion network. This particular network has two nodes, an origin \(o\) and a destination \(d\) , and two edges that both connect \(o\) to \(d\) . Suppose there are several users of the network who all want to travel from \(o\) to \(d\) and can choose either edge. Each edge has a \(\operatorname{cost}c\left( x\right)\) associated with it, which is a function that describes how costly it is for each user to use that edge when it has a flow or load of \(x\) users. The top edge has constant cost \(c\left( x\right)  = 2\) , and the bottom edge has \(\operatorname{cost}c\left( x\right)  = x\) . The cost could for example be travel time, or incorporate additional monetary costs. The cost is the same for all users of the edge. The cost for each user of the top edge, no matter how many people use it, is always 2, whereas the bottom edge has cost 1 for one user, 2 for two users, and so on (and zero cost for no users, but then there is no one to profit from taking that zero-cost route).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图2.1展示了一个拥塞网络的示例。这个特定的网络有两个节点，一个起点 \(o\) 和一个终点 \(d\) ，以及两条都连接 \(o\) 到 \(d\) 的边。假设网络中有多个用户，他们都想从 \(o\) 前往 \(d\) ，并且可以选择任意一条边。每条边都有一个与之关联的 \(\operatorname{cost}c\left( x\right)\) ，它是一个函数，描述了当该边有 \(x\) 个用户的流或负载时，每个用户使用该边的成本。上边的边具有恒定成本 \(c\left( x\right)  = 2\) ，下边的边具有 \(\operatorname{cost}c\left( x\right)  = x\) 。成本例如可以是旅行时间，或者包含额外的货币成本。对于该边的所有用户来说，成本是相同的。无论有多少人使用上边的边，每个用户的成本始终是2，而下边的边对于一个用户的成本是1，对于两个用户的成本是2，依此类推(对于没有用户的情况，成本为零，但此时没有人能从选择这条零成本路线中受益)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.3\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_52_198_201_455_316_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 2.1 A congestion network with two nodes \(o\) and \(d\) that define the origin and destination of all users, with two parallel edges between them which have different costs \(c\left( x\right)\) that depend on the usage \(x\) of the respective edge.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图2.1 一个拥塞网络，有两个节点 \(o\) 和 \(d\) ，它们定义了所有用户的起点和终点，节点之间有两条平行边，其成本 \(c\left( x\right)\) 取决于相应边的使用情况 \(x\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Suppose the network in Figure 2.1 is used by two users. They can either both use the top edge, or choose a different edge each, or both use the bottom edge. If both use the top edge, both pay cost 2, but if one of them switches to the bottom edge, that user will travel more cheaply and only pay cost 1 on that edge. We say that this situation is not in equilibrium because at least one user can improve her cost by changing her action. Note that we only consider the unilateral deviation of a single user in this scenario. If both would simultaneously switch to the bottom edge, then this edge would be congested with \(x = 2\) and again incur cost 2, which is no improvement to the situation that both use the top edge.

\begin{tcolorbox}
假设图2.1中的网络有两个用户使用。他们可以都使用上边的边，或者各自选择不同的边，或者都使用下边的边。如果他们都使用上边的边，两人都需支付成本2，但如果其中一人切换到下边的边，该用户的出行成本会更低，只需支付成本1。我们说这种情况不是均衡状态，因为至少有一个用户可以通过改变自己的行为来降低成本。注意，在这个场景中我们只考虑单个用户的单方面偏离(deviation)。如果两人同时切换到下边的边，那么这条边会因 \(x = 2\) 而变得拥堵，成本又会变为2，这与两人都使用上边的边的情况相比并没有改善。
\end{tcolorbox}

If the two users use different edges, this is an equilibrium. Namely, the user of the bottom edge currently has cost 1 but would pay cost 2 by switching to the top edge, and clearly she has no incentive to do so. And the user of the top edge, when switching from the top edge, would increase the load on the bottom edge from 1 to 2 with the resulting new cost 2 which is also no improvement for her. This proves the equilibrium property.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果两个用户使用不同的边，这就是一种均衡状态。具体来说，使用下边边的用户当前成本为1，但如果切换到上边的边，成本将变为2，显然她没有动机这么做。而使用上边边的用户，如果从上边的边切换，会使下边边的负载从1增加到2，新的成本也为2，这对她来说也没有改善。这就证明了均衡性质。
\end{tcolorbox}

Finally, the situation where both users use the bottom edge is also an equilibrium. Both have cost 2, but that is not worse than the cost on the top edge. So this network, with two users, has two equilibria.

\begin{tcolorbox}
最后，两个用户都使用底边的情况也是一种均衡。两者的成本均为2，但这并不比顶边的成本更差。因此，这个有两个用户的网络有两种均衡状态。
\end{tcolorbox}

The underlying behavior of the users in this game is called selfish routing. That is, every user tries to find the route that is least costly for her. Equilibrium is obtained when every user is in a situation where she cannot find a better route on her own.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这个博弈中用户的潜在行为被称为自私路由。也就是说，每个用户都试图找到对自己成本最低的路线。当每个用户都处于无法自行找到更好路线的情况时，就达到了均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

Is selfish routing always socially optimal, as measured by the average cost per user? In this example, it is better if one user chooses the top edge and the other the bottom edge, with costs 2 and 1, respectively (or 1.5 on average), which is clearly socially optimal. The other equilibrium where both use the bottom edge has the higher cost 2 (paid by both users).

\begin{tcolorbox}
以每个用户的平均成本来衡量，自私路由是否总是符合社会最优呢？在这个例子中，如果一个用户选择顶边，另一个用户选择底边，成本分别为2和1(平均为1.5)，这显然是社会最优的。另一种两个用户都使用底边的均衡状态下，成本更高，为2(两个用户都支付这个成本)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.3\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_53_337_332_454_314_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 2.2 The same Pigou network as in Figure 2.1, but with a constant cost \(c\left( x\right)  =\) 100 on the top edge. We assume 100 users want to travel from \(o\) to \(d\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图2.2 与图2.1相同的庇古网络(Pigou network)，但顶边的固定成本 \(c\left( x\right)  =\) 为100。我们假设100个用户想从 \(o\) 前往 \(d\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

However, selfish routing may lead to a situation where the average user is worse off than in the socially optimal flow through the network. Figure 2.2 is identical to Figure 2.1 except that the top edge has a constant cost of 100 . It is called the Pigou network. Assume that there are 100 users who want to travel from \(o\) to \(d\) . It is easy to see that there are only two equilibria in this congestion game, namely one where 99 users use the bottom edge and only 1 the top edge, or another where all 100 users use the bottom edge. In all other cases, at most 98 users use the bottom edge, so that any one of the remaining users on the top edge would pay at most 99 by switching to the bottom edge, which improves her cost. In other words, the bottom edge will be "saturated" with users until its usage costs reaches or nearly reaches the cost of the top edge.

\begin{tcolorbox}
然而，自私路由可能导致一种情况，即与通过网络的社会最优流相比，普通用户的情况更糟。图2.2与图2.1相同，只是顶边的固定成本为100。它被称为庇古网络(Pigou network)。假设100个用户想从 \(o\) 前往 \(d\) 。很容易看出，在这个拥塞博弈(congestion game)中只有两种均衡状态，即一种是99个用户使用底边，只有1个用户使用顶边，另一种是100个用户都使用底边。在所有其他情况下，最多98个用户使用底边，因此顶边上剩余的任何一个用户通过切换到底边最多只需支付99，这降低了她的成本。换句话说，底边将被用户“饱和”，直到其使用成本达到或接近顶边的成本。
\end{tcolorbox}

The average cost for each user in these equilibria is found as follows. In the equilibrium where all users use the bottom edge the average cost is 100 . In the equilibrium where one user uses the top edge and 99 the bottom edge the average cost is99.01, computed as \(\frac{99}{100}{99} + \frac{1}{100}{100} = \frac{99}{100}{99} + \frac{1}{100}\left( {{99} + 1}\right)  = \frac{{99} + 1}{100}{99} + \frac{1}{100}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
这些均衡状态下每个用户的平均成本计算如下。在所有用户都使用底边的均衡状态下，平均成本为100。在一个用户使用顶边、99个用户使用底边的均衡状态下，平均成本为99.01，计算方式为 \(\frac{99}{100}{99} + \frac{1}{100}{100} = \frac{99}{100}{99} + \frac{1}{100}\left( {{99} + 1}\right)  = \frac{{99} + 1}{100}{99} + \frac{1}{100}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

In contrast, if the flow from \(o\) to \(d\) is split equally between the two edges, then 50 users will use the top edge and each pay cost 100, and 50 users will use the bottom edge and each pay cost 50 . So the average cost is \(\frac{50}{100}{100} + \frac{50}{100}{50} = \frac{1}{2}\left( {{100} + {50}}\right)  = {75}\) . We show that this 50-50 split has the optimal average cost. Namely, suppose that \(y\) users use the bottom edge and correspondingly \({100} - y\) use the top edge. Then their average cost is \(\frac{{100} - y}{100}{100} + \frac{y}{100}y\) , which equals \({100} - y + \frac{1}{100}{y}^{2}\) . Assume first that \(y\) can take any real value. Then the derivative of this function of \(y\) is \(- 1 + \frac{2}{100}y\) , which is zero if and only if \(y = {50}\) , which is an integer and gives the unique minimum of the function. Equivalently, \({100} - y + \frac{1}{100}{y}^{2} = {100} + \frac{1}{100}{\left( y - {50}\right) }^{2} - \frac{2500}{100}\) , which has its smallest value 75 for \(y = {50}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
相比之下，如果从 \(o\) 到 \(d\) 的流在两条边上平均分配，那么50个用户将使用顶边，每个用户支付成本100，50个用户将使用底边，每个用户支付成本50。因此平均成本为 \(\frac{50}{100}{100} + \frac{50}{100}{50} = \frac{1}{2}\left( {{100} + {50}}\right)  = {75}\) 。我们证明这种50 - 50的分配方式具有最优平均成本。即，假设 \(y\) 个用户使用底边，相应地 \({100} - y\) 个用户使用顶边。那么他们的平均成本为 \(\frac{{100} - y}{100}{100} + \frac{y}{100}y\) ，这等于 \({100} - y + \frac{1}{100}{y}^{2}\) 。首先假设 \(y\) 可以取任何实数值。那么这个关于 \(y\) 的函数的导数为 \(- 1 + \frac{2}{100}y\) ，当且仅当 \(y = {50}\) 时该导数为零， \(y = {50}\) 是一个整数，并且使该函数取得唯一最小值。等价地， \({100} - y + \frac{1}{100}{y}^{2} = {100} + \frac{1}{100}{\left( y - {50}\right) }^{2} - \frac{2500}{100}\) ，当 \(y = {50}\) 时其最小值为75。
\end{tcolorbox}

However, note that the 50-50 split in this example is definitely not an equilibrium, because any user of the top edge would switch to the bottom edge. This would continue until that edge no longer has a lower cost. The socially optimal 50-50 split would have to be enforced in some way (for example by limiting the usage of the bottom edge), or would require new incentives that "change the game", for example a congestion charge.

\begin{tcolorbox}
然而，请注意，在这个例子中 50 - 50 的分流绝对不是一种均衡状态，因为顶边(top edge)的任何用户都会切换到底边(bottom edge)。这种情况会一直持续，直到该边的成本不再更低。社会最优的 50 - 50 分流必须以某种方式来实施(例如，限制底边的使用)，或者需要有新的激励措施来“改变博弈规则”，例如征收拥堵费。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{2.3 The Braess Paradox}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{2.3 布雷斯悖论(Braess paradox)}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_54_219_362_1213_399_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 2.3 The Braess paradox. Each edge has a cost function \(c\left( x\right)\) that depends on the flow \(x\) on that edge (the flow may be different for different edges). The right congestion network has one extra edge \({uv}\) with zero cost compared to the left congestion network. For 100 users, the equilibrium flow in the left network is optimal, whereas in the right network it is worse, due to the extra capacity.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 2.3 布雷斯悖论(Braess paradox)。每条边(edge)都有一个成本函数 \(c\left( x\right)\) ，该函数取决于该边上的流(flow) \(x\) (不同边上的流可能不同)。与左侧的拥塞网络(congestion network)相比，右侧的拥塞网络多了一条成本为零的边 \({uv}\) 。对于 100 个用户来说，左侧网络中的均衡流(equilibrium flow)是最优的，而在右侧网络中，由于额外的容量，均衡流反而更差。
\end{tcolorbox}

We describe a famous congestion game that shows an unexpected effect of selfish routing. Figure 2.3 shows two congestion networks. In both cases, we assume that there are 100 users who all want to travel from the origin \(o\) to the destination \(d\) . In the left network in Figure 2.3, there are only two routes, across the top and across the bottom. Suppose that \(y\) users take the bottom route via the intermediate node \(u\) , and \({100} - y\) users take the top route via node \(v\) . Both routes use two edges, whose costs are added. With this usage distribution on the edges, the bottom route incurs cost \(y + {100}\) and the top route incurs cost \({100} + \left( {{100} - y}\right)\) . In equilibrium, both costs should be equal or nearly equal in order to not incentivize any user to switch routes. That is, if \(y = {50}\) , then both routes cost 150, and any switch would only lead to a higher cost on the other route. If, for example, \(y = {51}\) , then the bottom route would cost 151 and the top route would cost 149 . Then a user on the bottom route could switch to the top route which has a new, lower cost of 150 . So the only equilibrium is an equal split between the two routes. It is easy to see that this is also the optimal flow through the network.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们描述一个著名的拥塞博弈(congestion game)，它展示了自私路由的意外效果。图 2.3 展示了两个拥塞网络(congestion network)。在这两种情况下，我们假设共有 100 个用户，他们都想从起点 \(o\) 前往终点 \(d\) 。在图 2.3 的左侧网络中，只有两条路线，即顶部路线和底部路线。假设 \(y\) 个用户选择经过中间节点 \(u\) 的底部路线， \({100} - y\) 个用户选择经过节点 \(v\) 的顶部路线。两条路线都使用两条边，其成本相加。根据边上的这种使用分布，底部路线的成本为 \(y + {100}\) ，顶部路线的成本为 \({100} + \left( {{100} - y}\right)\) 。在均衡状态下，为了不激励任何用户切换路线，两条路线的成本应该相等或近乎相等。也就是说，如果 \(y = {50}\) ，那么两条路线的成本均为 150，并且任何切换只会导致另一条路线的成本更高。例如，如果 \(y = {51}\) ，那么底部路线的成本为 151，顶部路线的成本为 149。那么，底部路线上的一个用户可以切换到顶部路线，此时顶部路线的新成本更低，为 150。因此，唯一的均衡状态是两条路线的用户数量平均分配。很容易看出，这也是通过该网络的最优流(flow)。
\end{tcolorbox}

The right picture in Figure 2.3 has an extra edge \({uv}\) (from \(u\) to \(v\) ). This edge provides a "shortcut" with a new route from \(o\) via \(u\) and \(v\) to \(d\) along the edges \({ou}\) , \({uv},{vd}\) . Suppose that \(z\) users take this new route, that \(y - z\) users for \(y \geq  z\) take the bottom route from \(o\) via \(u\) to \(d\) (so that \(y\) users use the edge \({ou}\) , and \(y - z\) users use the edge \({ud}\) ), and that \({100} - y\) users take the top route from \(o\) to \(v\) to \(d\) . The resulting usage of the edge \({ov}\) is then \({100} - y\) and of the edge \({vd}\) is \({100} - y + z\) . The left diagram in Figure 2.3 corresponds to the case where the edge \({uv}\) is blocked and therefore \(z = 0\) . Now consider the previous equilibrium flow where \(y = {50}\) and \(z = 0\) and suppose that the shortcut \({uv}\) is opened. In this situation, the cost of getting from \(o\) to \(v\) via the shortcut is currently 50, much lower than 100 along the edge \({ov}\) , and similarly getting from \(u\) to \(d\) via \(v\) is also cheaper than via the edge \({ud}\) with cost 100 . This is not in equilibrium. In fact, the only equilibrium flow is given with at most one user on edge \({ov}\) and at most one user on the edge \({ud}\) , and everybody else using the shortcut route, in the worst case with \(y = z = {100}\) where everyone takes the shortcut and nobody uses the edges ov and \({ud}\) with constant costs 100. Then the overall cost of traveling from \(o\) to \(d\) is now \({100} + 0 + {100} = {200}\) , whereas it was 150 beforehand.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图2.3中的右图有一条额外的边 \({uv}\) (从 \(u\) 到 \(v\) )。这条边提供了一条“捷径”，即从 \(o\) 经 \(u\) 和 \(v\) 沿着边 \({ou}\) 、 \({uv},{vd}\) 到 \(d\) 的新路线。假设 \(z\) 个用户选择这条新路线， \(y \geq  z\) 对应的 \(y - z\) 个用户选择从 \(o\) 经 \(u\) 到 \(d\) 的底部路线(这样就有 \(y\) 个用户使用边 \({ou}\) ， \(y - z\) 个用户使用边 \({ud}\) )，并且 \({100} - y\) 个用户选择从 \(o\) 经 \(v\) 到 \(d\) 的顶部路线。那么，边 \({ov}\) 的使用量为 \({100} - y\) ，边 \({vd}\) 的使用量为 \({100} - y + z\) 。图2.3中的左图对应边 \({uv}\) 被阻塞的情况，因此 \(z = 0\) 。现在考虑之前的均衡流，其中 \(y = {50}\) 且 \(z = 0\) ，并假设捷径 \({uv}\) 开通了。在这种情况下，经捷径从 \(o\) 到 \(v\) 的成本目前为50，远低于沿边 \({ov}\) 的100，同样，经 \(v\) 从 \(u\) 到 \(d\) 也比经成本为100的边 \({ud}\) 便宜。这不是均衡状态。实际上，唯一的均衡流是边 \({ov}\) 上最多有一个用户，边 \({ud}\) 上最多有一个用户，其他所有人都使用捷径路线，在最坏的情况下， \(y = z = {100}\) 即每个人都走捷径，没有人使用成本恒定为100的边ov和 \({ud}\) 。那么，现在从 \(o\) 到 \(d\) 的总出行成本为 \({100} + 0 + {100} = {200}\) ，而之前是150。
\end{tcolorbox}

On closer inspection, the new edge \({uv}\) with zero cost creates two Pigou networks as shown on the right, if one identifies the two nodes \(u\) and \(v\) . The first Pigou network goes from \(o\) to destination \(v\) via two routes, namely along edge ov with constant cost 100, and along the edges ou and \({uv}\) with \(\operatorname{cost}c\left( x\right)  = x\) , all of which is incurred on the first edge \({ou}\) . The second Pigou network starts at node \(u\) with destination \(d\) , also via two routes, one along the edge \({ud}\) with cost 100, and the other along edges \({uv}\) and \({vd}\) , of which only the second edge is costly. Like in the Pigou network, neither of the edges ov and \({ud}\) with constant cost is used in selfish routing (except possibly by one user).

\begin{tcolorbox}
仔细观察，如果将两个节点 \(u\) 和 \(v\) 视为同一节点，那么成本为零的新边 \({uv}\) 会形成两个庇古网络(Pigou network)，如右图所示。第一个庇古网络从 \(o\) 经两条路线到达目的地 \(v\) ，即沿成本恒定为100的边ov，以及沿边ou和 \({uv}\) 且 \(\operatorname{cost}c\left( x\right)  = x\) ，所有这些成本都产生在第一条边 \({ou}\) 上。第二个庇古网络从节点 \(u\) 出发，目的地为 \(d\) ，同样有两条路线，一条沿成本为100的边 \({ud}\) ，另一条沿边 \({uv}\) 和 \({vd}\) ，其中只有第二条边有成本。与庇古网络一样，在自私路由中，成本恒定的边ov和 \({ud}\) 都不会被使用(最多可能有一个用户使用)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_55_952_605_639_403_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

The Pigou network shows that a selfish equilibrium flow may be more costly than a centrally planned socially optimal flow. The added zero-cost edge \({uv}\) in Figure 2.3 goes beyond this observation, because it shows that adding extra capacity to the network can make congestion worse in equilibrium because the new shortcut destroys the balance between the previous two routes. This is called Braess's paradox, after Braess (1968).

\begin{tcolorbox}
庇古网络表明，自私的均衡流可能比中央计划的社会最优流成本更高。图2.3中新增的零成本边 \({uv}\) 进一步说明了这一点，因为它表明在网络中增加额外容量可能会使均衡状态下的拥塞情况恶化，因为新的捷径破坏了之前两条路线之间的平衡。这被称为布雷斯悖论(Braess's paradox)，以布雷斯(Braess，1968)的名字命名。
\end{tcolorbox}

Similarly, closing a road may reduce traffic congestion. This has also been observed in practice. Most evidence on this is anecdotal, but at least some urban planners seem to be aware of this possible effect. In Seoul (Korea), a large expressway over the central Cheonggyecheon riverbed was dismantled in 2002 and replaced by a long recreational park (Hong, Song, and Wu, 2007, p. 235). Traffic improved, although certainly due to many factors in this complex project.

\begin{tcolorbox}
同样，关闭一条道路可能会减少交通拥堵。这在实践中也得到了证实。关于这一点的大多数证据都是轶事性的，但至少一些城市规划者似乎意识到了这种可能的影响。在首尔(韩国)，2002年拆除了清溪川中央河床上的一条大型高速公路，并将其改造成了一个长长的休闲公园(Hong、Song和Wu，2007，第235页)。尽管这个复杂项目的交通改善肯定是由多种因素造成的，但交通状况确实得到了改善。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{2.4 Definition of Congestion Games}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{2.4 拥塞博弈(congestion game)的定义}
\end{tcolorbox}

In this section, we give a general definition of congestion games and of the concept of an equilibrium. A congestion network has the following components:

\begin{tcolorbox}
在本节中，我们给出拥塞博弈和均衡概念的一般定义。拥塞网络(congestion network)具有以下组成部分:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item A finite set of nodes.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 一个有限的节点(node)集合。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item A finite collection \(E\) of edges. Each edge \(e\) is an ordered pair, written as \({uv}\) , from some node \(u\) to some node \(v\) , which is graphically drawn as an arrow from \(u\) to \(v\) . Parallel edges (that is, with the same pair \({uv}\) ) are allowed (hence the edges form a "collection" \(E\) rather than a set, which would not allow for such repetitions), as in Figure 2.1.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 边(edge)的有限集合 \(E\) 。每条边 \(e\) 是一个有序对，记为 \({uv}\) ，从某个节点 \(u\) 到某个节点 \(v\) ，在图形上表示为从 \(u\) 到 \(v\) 的箭头。允许存在平行边(parallel edges)(即具有相同的有序对 \({uv}\) )(因此边构成一个“集合” \(E\) 而非集合，集合不允许有这样的重复)，如图 2.1 所示。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Each edge \(e\) in \(E\) has a cost function \({c}_{e}\) that gives a value \({c}_{e}\left( x\right)\) when there are \(x\) users on edge \(e\) , which describes the same cost to each user for using \(e\) . Each cost function is weakly increasing, that is, \(x \leq  y\) implies \({c}_{e}\left( x\right)  \leq  {c}_{e}\left( y\right)\) .
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 图 \(E\) 中的每条边 \(e\) 都有一个成本函数 \({c}_{e}\) ，当边 \(e\) 上有 \(x\) 个用户时，该函数给出一个值 \({c}_{e}\left( x\right)\) ，它描述了每个用户使用边 \(e\) 的相同成本。每个成本函数都是弱递增的，即 \(x \leq  y\) 意味着 \({c}_{e}\left( x\right)  \leq  {c}_{e}\left( y\right)\) 。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item A number \(N\) of users of the network. Each user \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) has an origin \({o}_{i}\) and destination \({d}_{i}\) , which are two nodes in the network, which may or may not be the same for all users (if they are the same, they are usually called \(o\) and \(d\) as in the above examples).
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 网络的用户数量为 \(N\) 。每个用户 \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) 都有一个起点 \({o}_{i}\) 和一个终点 \({d}_{i}\) ，它们是网络中的两个节点，对于所有用户来说，这两个节点可能相同也可能不同(如果相同，通常像上述示例中那样称为 \(o\) 和 \(d\) )。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

The underlying structure of nodes and edges is called a directed graph or digraph (where edges are sometimes called "arcs"). In such a digraph, a path \(P\) from \(u\) to \(v\) is a sequence of distinct nodes \({u}_{0},{u}_{1},\ldots ,{u}_{m}\) for \(m \geq  0\) where \({u}_{k}{u}_{k + 1}\) is an edge for \(0 \leq  k < m\) , and \(u = {u}_{0}\) and \(v = {u}_{m}\) . For any such edge \(e = {u}_{k}{u}_{k + 1}\) for \(0 \leq  k < m\) we write \(e \in  P\) . Note that a node may appear at most once in a path. Every user \(i\) chooses a path (which we have earlier also called a "route") from her origin \({o}_{i}\) to her destination \({d}_{i}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
节点(node)和边(edge)的底层结构称为有向图(digraph)(边有时也称为“弧”)。在这样的有向图中，从 \(u\) 到 \(v\) 的路径(path) \(P\) 是一个不同节点的序列 \({u}_{0},{u}_{1},\ldots ,{u}_{m}\) ，其中 \(m \geq  0\) ， \({u}_{k}{u}_{k + 1}\) 是一条边， \(0 \leq  k < m\) ，且 \(u = {u}_{0}\) 和 \(v = {u}_{m}\) 。对于任何这样的边 \(e = {u}_{k}{u}_{k + 1}\) ，其中 \(0 \leq  k < m\) ，我们记为 \(e \in  P\) 。注意，一个节点在一条路径中最多出现一次。每个用户 \(i\) 都从她的起点 \({o}_{i}\) 选择一条路径(我们之前也称之为“路线”)到她的终点 \({d}_{i}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item A strategy of user \(i\) is a path \({P}_{i}\) from \({o}_{i}\) to \({d}_{i}\) .
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 用户 \(i\) 的一个策略是从 \({o}_{i}\) 到 \({d}_{i}\) 的一条路径 \({P}_{i}\) 。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Given a strategy \({P}_{i}\) for each user \(i\) , the load on or flow through an edge \(e\) is defined as \({f}_{e} = \left| \left\{  {i \mid  e \in  {P}_{i}}\right\}  \right|\) , which is the number of chosen paths that contain \(e\) , that is, the number of users on \(e\) . The cost to user \(i\) for her strategy \({P}_{i}\) , given that the other users have chosen their strategies, is then
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 给定每个用户 \(i\) 的一个策略 \({P}_{i}\) ，边 \(e\) 上的负载或流量(flow)定义为 \({f}_{e} = \left| \left\{  {i \mid  e \in  {P}_{i}}\right\}  \right|\) ，即包含边 \(e\) 的所选路径的数量，也就是边 \(e\) 上的用户数量。在其他用户已经选择了他们的策略的情况下，用户 \(i\) 采用她的策略 \({P}_{i}\) 的成本为
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {P}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right) . \tag{2.1}
\]

The congestion game obtained from this network is defined by the strategies of the users (or "players"), and by the resulting cost to each user when all strategies are known. Each user tries, on her own, to minimize her cost. Every edge \(e\) has a certain flow \({f}_{e}\) , which is number of users on \(e\) , and creates the cost \({c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) which is the same for every user of that edge. Users may have different total costs when they use different strategies (that is, different paths; these paths necessarily differ if they do not have the same origin and destination).

\begin{tcolorbox}
从这个网络得到的拥塞博弈(congestion game)由用户(或“玩家”)的策略以及当所有策略都已知时每个用户产生的成本来定义。每个用户都独自尝试最小化她的成本。每条边 \(e\) 都有一定的流量 \({f}_{e}\) ，即边 \(e\) 上的用户数量，并产生成本 \({c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) ，该成本对于使用该边的每个用户都是相同的。当用户采用不同的策略(即不同的路径；如果他们的起点和终点不同，这些路径必然不同)时，他们可能有不同的总成本。
\end{tcolorbox}

Now consider what happens when user \(i\) changes her strategy \({P}_{i}\) to a different strategy \({Q}_{i}\) . Some edges of \({Q}_{i}\) are the same as before, which define the set \({Q}_{i} \cap  {P}_{i}\) . Other edges in \({Q}_{i}\) , those not in \({P}_{i}\) , are new, which define the set \({Q}_{i} \smallsetminus  {P}_{i}\) . Clearly, every edge in \({Q}_{i}\) belongs to exactly one of these two sets. On any edge in \({Q}_{i} \cap  {P}_{i}\) , the flow and hence the cost remains the same as before, but every edge \(e\) in \({Q}_{i} \smallsetminus  {P}_{i}\) has user \(i\) as one additional user and therefore an increased flow of \({f}_{e} + 1\) . We say \({P}_{i}\) is a best response of user \(i\) to the remaining strategies \({P}_{j}\) for \(j \neq  i\) if for every alternative strategy \({Q}_{i}\) of user \(i\)

\begin{tcolorbox}
现在考虑当用户 \(i\) 将其策略 \({P}_{i}\) 改变为不同的策略 \({Q}_{i}\) 时会发生什么。 \({Q}_{i}\) 中的一些边与之前相同，这些边定义了集合 \({Q}_{i} \cap  {P}_{i}\) 。 \({Q}_{i}\) 中其他不在 \({P}_{i}\) 里的边是新的，这些边定义了集合 \({Q}_{i} \smallsetminus  {P}_{i}\) 。显然， \({Q}_{i}\) 中的每条边恰好属于这两个集合之一。在 \({Q}_{i} \cap  {P}_{i}\) 中的任何一条边上，流(flow)以及因此产生的成本(cost)都与之前相同，但 \({Q}_{i} \smallsetminus  {P}_{i}\) 中的每条边 \(e\) 都有用户 \(i\) 作为一个额外的用户，因此流增加了 \({f}_{e} + 1\) 。我们说，如果对于用户 \(i\) 的每个替代策略 \({Q}_{i}\) ， \({P}_{i}\) 是用户 \(i\) 对其余策略 \({P}_{j}\) 的最优反应(best response)
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {P}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)  \leq  \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {Q}_{i} \cap  {P}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)  + \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {Q}_{i} \smallsetminus  {P}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {{f}_{e} + 1}\right) . \tag{2.2}
\]

The left-hand side of this inequality is the cost to user \(i\) when she uses strategy \({P}_{i}\) , and the right-hand side is the possibly changed cost when she deviates to strategy \({Q}_{i}\) . Then \({P}_{i}\) is a best response to the other strategies if no deviation gives rise to a lower cost. (When user \(i\) changes from \({P}_{i}\) to \({Q}_{i}\) , some edges of \({P}_{i}\) may no longer be used in \({Q}_{i}\) and therefore have a reduced flow; this may help other users of those edges, but is irrelevant for user \(i\) .) Note that the concept of best response concerns only deviations of a single user.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这个不等式的左边是用户 \(i\) 使用策略 \({P}_{i}\) 时的成本，右边是她偏离到策略 \({Q}_{i}\) 时可能改变的成本。那么，如果没有偏离能产生更低的成本， \({P}_{i}\) 就是对其他策略的最优反应。(当用户 \(i\) 从 \({P}_{i}\) 改变到 \({Q}_{i}\) 时， \({P}_{i}\) 中的一些边可能在 \({Q}_{i}\) 中不再使用，因此流会减少；这可能对那些边的其他用户有帮助，但与用户 \(i\) 无关。)请注意，最优反应的概念仅涉及单个用户的偏离(deviation)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 2.1. The strategies \({P}_{1},\ldots ,{P}_{N}\) of all \(N\) users define an equilibrium if each strategy is a best response to the other strategies, that is, if (2.2) holds for all \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义 2.1。如果每个策略都是对其他策略的最优反应，即如果 (2.2) 对所有 \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) 都成立，那么所有 \(N\) 个用户的策略 \({P}_{1},\ldots ,{P}_{N}\) 定义了一个均衡(equilibrium)。
\end{tcolorbox}

We have seen examples of equilibria in congestion games in the previous sections. Next, we prove that such an equilibrium always exists.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们在前面的章节中已经看到了拥塞博弈(congestion game)中均衡的例子。接下来，我们证明这样的均衡总是存在的。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{2.5 Existence of Equilibrium in a Congestion Game}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{2.5 拥塞博弈中均衡的存在性}
\end{tcolorbox}

The following is the central theorem of this chapter. It is proved with the help of a potential function \(\Phi\) . The potential function is constructed in such a way that it defines for each edge the increase in cost created by each additional user on the edge, as explained further after the proof.

\begin{tcolorbox}
以下是本章的核心定理。它是借助势函数(potential function) \(\Phi\) 来证明的。势函数的构造方式是，它为每条边定义了该边上每个额外用户所产生的成本增加，证明后会进一步解释。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 2.2. Every congestion game (as obtained from a congestion network) has at least one equilibrium.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理 2.2。每个拥塞博弈(从拥塞网络(congestion network)得到)至少有一个均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Suppose the \(N\) strategies of the users are \({P}_{1},\ldots ,{P}_{N}\) , which defines a flow \({f}_{e}\) on each edge \(e \in  E\) , namely the number of users \(i\) with \(e \in  {P}_{i}\) . We call this the flow \(f\) induced by these strategies. We now define the following function \(\Phi \left( f\right)\) of this flow by

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。假设用户的 \(N\) 个策略是 \({P}_{1},\ldots ,{P}_{N}\) ，这在每条边 \(e \in  E\) 上定义了一个流 \({f}_{e}\) ，即具有 \(e \in  {P}_{i}\) 的用户数量 \(i\) 。我们称这为由这些策略诱导的流 \(f\) 。我们现在通过以下方式定义这个流的函数 \(\Phi \left( f\right)\)
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\Phi \left( f\right)  = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  E}}\left( {{c}_{e}\left( 1\right)  + {c}_{e}\left( 2\right)  + \cdots  + {c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right) }\right) . \tag{2.3}
\]

Suppose that user \(i\) changes her path \({P}_{i}\) to \({Q}_{i}\) . We call the resulting new flow \({f}^{{Q}_{i}}\) . We will prove that

\begin{tcolorbox}
假设用户 \(i\) 将其路径 \({P}_{i}\) 改变为 \({Q}_{i}\) 。我们称由此产生的新流为 \({f}^{{Q}_{i}}\) 。我们将证明
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\Phi \left( {f}^{{Q}_{i}}\right)  - \Phi \left( f\right)  = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {Q}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}^{{Q}_{i}}\right)  - \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {P}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right) . \tag{2.4}
\]

The right-hand side is the difference in costs to user \(i\) between her strategy \({Q}_{i}\) and her strategy \({P}_{i}\) , according to (2.1). Her cost for the flow \({f}^{{Q}_{i}}\) has been written on the right-hand side of (2.2) in terms of the original flow \(f\) (when she uses \({P}_{i}\) ) as

\begin{tcolorbox}
根据 (2.1)，右边是用户 \(i\) 在其策略 \({Q}_{i}\) 和策略 \({P}_{i}\) 之间的成本差异。她在流 \({f}^{{Q}_{i}}\) 下的成本在 (2.2) 的右边已经根据原始流 \(f\) (当她使用 \({P}_{i}\) 时)写成了
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {Q}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}^{{Q}_{i}}\right)  = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {Q}_{i} \cap  {P}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)  + \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {Q}_{i} \smallsetminus  {P}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {{f}_{e} + 1}\right) , \tag{2.5}
\]

and her cost for the original flow \(f\) can be expressed similarly, namely as

\begin{tcolorbox}
并且她在原始流 \(f\) 下的成本可以类似地表示，即表示为
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {P}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)  = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {P}_{i} \cap  {Q}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)  + \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {P}_{i} \smallsetminus  {Q}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right) . \tag{2.6}
\]

Hence, the right-hand side of (2.4) is

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此，(2.4) 的右边是
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {Q}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}^{{Q}_{i}}\right)  - \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {P}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)  = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {Q}_{i} \smallsetminus  {P}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {{f}_{e} + 1}\right)  - \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{e \in  {P}_{i} \smallsetminus  {Q}_{i}}}{c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right) . \tag{2.7}
\]

We claim that because of (2.3) (which is why \(\Phi\) is defined that way), the right-hand side of (2.7) is equal to the left-hand side \(\Phi \left( {f}^{{Q}_{i}}\right)  - \Phi \left( f\right)\) of (2.4). Namely, by changing her path from \({P}_{i}\) to \({Q}_{i}\) , user \(i\) increases the flow on any new edge \(e\) in \({Q}_{i} \smallsetminus  {P}_{i}\) from \({f}_{e}\) to \({f}_{e} + 1\) , and thus adds the term \({c}_{e}\left( {{f}_{e} + 1}\right)\) to the sum in (2.3). Similarly, for any edge \(e\) in \({P}_{i} \smallsetminus  {Q}_{i}\) which is in \({P}_{i}\) but no longer in \({Q}_{i}\) , the flow \({f}^{{Q}_{i}}\left( e\right)\) is reduced from \({f}_{e}\) to \({f}_{e} - 1\) , so that the term \({c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) has to be subtracted from the sum in (2.3). This shows (2.4).

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们断言，由于(2.3)(这就是 \(\Phi\) 如此定义的原因)，(2.7)的右侧等于(2.4)的左侧 \(\Phi \left( {f}^{{Q}_{i}}\right)  - \Phi \left( f\right)\) 。即，用户 \(i\) 将其路径从 \({P}_{i}\) 改变为 \({Q}_{i}\) 时，会使 \({Q}_{i} \smallsetminus  {P}_{i}\) 中任意新边 \(e\) 上的流从 \({f}_{e}\) 增加到 \({f}_{e} + 1\) ，从而在(2.3)的求和项中加上项 \({c}_{e}\left( {{f}_{e} + 1}\right)\) 。类似地，对于 \({P}_{i} \smallsetminus  {Q}_{i}\) 中存在于 \({P}_{i}\) 但不再存在于 \({Q}_{i}\) 中的任意边 \(e\) ，流 \({f}^{{Q}_{i}}\left( e\right)\) 从 \({f}_{e}\) 减少到 \({f}_{e} - 1\) ，因此必须从(2.3)的求和项中减去项 \({c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) 。这就证明了(2.4)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Equation (2.4) states that any change in cost to any user \(i\) when she changes her strategy, and thus the flow, is the same as the change in \(\Phi\) . Consider now a flow that achieves the global minimum of \(\Phi \left( f\right)\) for all possible flows \(f\) that result from users’ strategies \({P}_{1},\ldots ,{P}_{N}\) . There are only finitely many such strategy combinations, so this minimum exists. For such a minimum, no change in flow will reduce \(\Phi \left( f\right)\) any further, and hence no individual player can reduce her cost. Therefore, the players’ strategies for such a minimum of \(\Phi\) define an equilibrium.

\begin{tcolorbox}
方程(2.4)表明，任意用户 \(i\) 改变其策略(从而改变流)时，其成本的任何变化都与 \(\Phi\) 的变化相同。现在考虑一个流，它在所有由用户策略 \({P}_{1},\ldots ,{P}_{N}\) 产生的可能流 \(f\) 中实现了 \(\Phi \left( f\right)\) 的全局最小值。这样的策略组合只有有限多个，所以这个最小值存在。对于这样的最小值，流的任何变化都不会使 \(\Phi \left( f\right)\) 进一步减小，因此没有单个玩家可以降低其成本。因此，玩家对于 \(\Phi\) 这样一个最小值的策略定义了一个均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

The function \(\Phi\) introduced in the proof of Theorem 2.2 is of special interest. Its definition (2.3) can be thought of being obtained in the following way. Consider an edge \(e\) , and assume that there are no users of the network yet. Then \(c\left( 1\right)\) is the cost when one user travels on edge \(e\) . Next, \(c\left( 2\right)\) is the cost when a second user travels on edge \(e\) , and so on until \(c\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) for the full flow \({f}_{e}\) on edge \(e\) when all users are present (recall that \({f}_{e}\) is the number of users of the edge). However, the sum \({c}_{e}\left( 1\right)  + {c}_{e}\left( 2\right)  + \cdots  + {c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) in (2.3) is not the combined cost to all users of edge \(e\) , because the load \({f}_{e}\) creates the cost \(c\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) for every user of the edge, so their combined cost is \({f}_{e} \cdot  c\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) , which is larger than the sum \({c}_{e}\left( 1\right)  + {c}_{e}\left( 2\right)  + \cdots  + {c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) . Rather, this sum accumulates the additional cost created by each new user of the edge.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理2.2证明中引入的函数 \(\Phi\) 特别值得关注。它的定义(2.3)可以通过以下方式得到。考虑一条边 \(e\) ，并假设网络中还没有用户。那么 \(c\left( 1\right)\) 是一个用户在边 \(e\) 上通行时的成本。接着， \(c\left( 2\right)\) 是第二个用户在边 \(e\) 上通行时的成本，依此类推，直到所有用户都在时边 \(e\) 上的全流 \({f}_{e}\) 对应的 \(c\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) (回想一下， \({f}_{e}\) 是该边的用户数量)。然而，(2.3)中的和 \({c}_{e}\left( 1\right)  + {c}_{e}\left( 2\right)  + \cdots  + {c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) 并不是边 \(e\) 上所有用户的总费用，因为负载 \({f}_{e}\) 为该边的每个用户产生成本 \(c\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) ，所以他们的总费用是 \({f}_{e} \cdot  c\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) ，这比和 \({c}_{e}\left( 1\right)  + {c}_{e}\left( 2\right)  + \cdots  + {c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) 要大。相反，这个和累积了该边每个新用户所产生的额外成本。
\end{tcolorbox}

Because of this definition, the function \(\Phi\) has the crucial property (2.4) that any change of strategy by a single user incurs a cost change for that user that equals the change of \(\Phi\) . A single function \(\Phi\) achieves this for all users simultaneously. A function \(\Phi\) with this property (2.4) is called a potential function, and a game that has such a potential function is called a potential game. By the preceding proof, any potential game has an equilibrium, which can be found via the minimum of the potential function.

\begin{tcolorbox}
由于这个定义，函数 \(\Phi\) 具有关键性质(2.4)，即单个用户的任何策略改变所导致的该用户的成本变化等于 \(\Phi\) 的变化。单个函数 \(\Phi\) 能同时为所有用户实现这一点。具有这种性质(2.4)的函数 \(\Phi\) 被称为势函数，具有这种势函数的博弈被称为势博弈。根据前面的证明，任何势博弈都存在一个均衡，这个均衡可以通过势函数的最小值找到。
\end{tcolorbox}

The property (2.4) can also be used in the reverse direction to find an equilibrium. Namely, a smaller value of the potential function \(\Phi\) can be found by identifying one user who can reduce her cost by changing her strategy. So we change her strategy accordingly and reduce the value of the potential function. This can be seen as an iterative process where every user (one at a time) adapts her strategy until no user can find further improvements, which then defines an equilibrium. This clearly occurs for any equilibrium. Such an equilibrium may only represent a "local" minimum of the potential function in the sense that it does not allow further reduction by changing a single user's strategy at a time. In the example in Figure 2.1, there are two equilibria that have different values of the potential function.

\begin{tcolorbox}
性质 (2.4) 也可以反向使用来寻找均衡。即，可以通过识别出一个能够通过改变策略降低自身成本的用户，来找到势函数 \(\Phi\) 的更小值。因此，我们相应地改变该用户的策略，从而降低势函数的值。这可以看作一个迭代过程，每个用户(一次一个)调整自己的策略，直到没有用户能找到进一步的改进方法，此时就确定了一个均衡。显然，任何均衡都会出现这种情况。从一次改变单个用户的策略无法进一步降低势函数值的意义上来说，这样的均衡可能仅仅代表势函数的一个“局部”最小值。在图 2.1 的例子中，有两个势函数值不同的均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{2.6 Atomic and Splittable Flow, Price of Anarchy}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{2.6 原子流与可拆分流、无政府代价}
\end{tcolorbox}

The presented model of congestion games with finitely many users on a network is only one of many ways of modeling congestion by selfish routing.

\begin{tcolorbox}
所提出的网络上有限多个用户的拥塞博弈模型，只是通过自私路由对拥塞进行建模的众多方法之一。
\end{tcolorbox}

First, the representation of costs in (2.1) is a simplification in the sense that it assumes that user \(i\) creates a "flow" of 1 along its chosen path, and thus contributes to the congestion on every edge on that path. This model does not take any temporal aspects into account, like the fact that the user can be only on one edge at a time but not everywhere on the path at once. Nevertheless, this is not completely unrealistic because everywhere along her chosen route, the user will create congestion at some point in time together with the other users. The flow model is very appropriate for internet routing, for example, with a continuous flow of data packets that consume bandwidth along the taken route.

\begin{tcolorbox}
首先，(2.1) 中成本的表示是一种简化，因为它假设用户 \(i\) 沿着其选择的路径创建了一个大小为 1 的“流”，从而对该路径上的每条边的拥塞都有贡献。这个模型没有考虑任何时间因素，例如用户一次只能在一条边上，而不能同时在路径上的所有位置。然而，这并非完全不现实，因为在用户选择的路线上的任何位置，用户都会在某个时刻与其他用户一起造成拥塞。例如，对于互联网路由，数据分组沿着所选路线持续流动并消耗带宽，这种流模型就非常合适。
\end{tcolorbox}

The specific model that we consider here is called atomic (or non-splittable) flow where single users decide on their paths from their origin to their destination.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们这里考虑的特定模型称为原子(或不可拆分)流，其中单个用户决定他们从起点到终点的路径。
\end{tcolorbox}

In contrast, splittable flow means that a flow unit is just some "mass" of users of which any parts can be routed along different paths. For example, in Figure 2.1 the flow of 2 from \(o\) to \(d\) could be split into a flow of 0.01 on the top edge and a flow of 1.99 on the bottom edge. This would still not be an equilibrium because any one user from the 0.01 fraction of users on the top edge could improve her cost by moving to the bottom edge (each user is negligibly small). Splittable flows are in some sense simpler because there will be no minor variation of equilibria, such as the distinct equilibria with a flow of either \(y = {100}\) or \(y = {99}\) on the bottom edge in Figure 2.2; the only equilibrium flow would be \(y = {100}\) . The equilibrium condition is then simply that there is no alternative path with currently smaller cost, without taking into account that there is an increased cost by switching to another path, as in the term \({c}_{e}\left( {{f}_{e} + 1}\right)\) in (2.2) where the increase of \({f}_{e}\) by 1 results from that switch. In the splittable flow model, users have negligible size, so there is no such increase. It can be shown that the cost of an equilibrium flow in a splittable flow network is unique (however, there may be several equilibria with that cost, for example with an undetermined flow across two parallel edges with the same constant cost). For splittable flow, the proof of Theorem 2.2 can be amended with a potential function \(\Phi\) defined with an integral \({\int }_{0}^{{f}_{e}}{c}_{e}\left( x\right) {dx}\) that replaces the sum \({c}_{e}\left( 1\right)  + {c}_{e}\left( 2\right)  + \cdots  + {c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) in (2.3); see Roughgarden (2016, section 13.2.2).

\begin{tcolorbox}
相比之下，可拆分流意味着一个流单元只是一些“群体”用户，其任何部分都可以沿着不同的路径进行路由。例如，在图 2.1 中，从 \(o\) 到 \(d\) 的流量为 2 的流可以拆分为顶部边上流量为 0.01 和底部边上流量为 1.99。这仍然不是一个均衡，因为顶部边上 0.01 部分的任何一个用户都可以通过移动到底部边来降低自己的成本(每个用户的影响可以忽略不计)。从某种意义上说，可拆分流更简单，因为不会有均衡的微小变化，例如图 2.2 中底部边上流量为 \(y = {100}\) 或 \(y = {99}\) 的不同均衡；唯一的均衡流量将是 \(y = {100}\) 。那么均衡条件就简单地是，不存在当前成本更低的替代路径，而无需考虑切换到另一条路径会增加成本，就像 (2.2) 中的项 \({c}_{e}\left( {{f}_{e} + 1}\right)\) 那样，其中 \({f}_{e}\) 增加 1 就是由这种切换导致的。在可拆分流模型中，用户的规模可以忽略不计，因此不会有这样的增加。可以证明，可拆分流网络中均衡流的成本是唯一的(然而，可能存在多个具有该成本的均衡，例如在两条具有相同恒定成本的平行边上存在未确定的流量)。对于可拆分流，定理 2.2 的证明可以用一个由积分 \({\int }_{0}^{{f}_{e}}{c}_{e}\left( x\right) {dx}\) 定义的势函数 \(\Phi\) 进行修正，该积分替代了 (2.3) 中的求和 \({c}_{e}\left( 1\right)  + {c}_{e}\left( 2\right)  + \cdots  + {c}_{e}\left( {f}_{e}\right)\) ；参见拉夫加登(2016 年，第 13.2.2 节)。
\end{tcolorbox}

We also mention the Price of Anarchy in this context. This is defined, for a specific congestion network, as the ratio between the worst average cost to a user in an equilibrium and the optimal average cost in the social optimum. The "anarchy" refers to the selfishness in the routing game where each user chooses her optimal route on her own. In the Pigou network, the Price of Anarchy is \(\frac{100}{75} = \frac{4}{3}\) . For splittable flow, it can be shown that this is the worst possible Price of Anarchy when the cost function on each edge is affine, that is, of the form \(c\left( x\right)  = {ax} + b\) for suitable nonnegative real numbers \(a\) and \(b\) (which depend on the edge). (For a proof of this result see Roughgarden and Tardos, 2002, theorem 4.5, or Roughgarden, 2016, chapter 11.) In other words, for affine cost functions, selfish routing is never more than \(1/3\) more costly than what could be achieved with central planning. For non-splittable flow (which has been the topic of this chapter), the Price of Anarchy for affine cost functions can be shown to be at most \(\frac{5}{2}\) (Roughgarden,2016, theorem 12.3). Exercise 2.3 describes a network where this bad ratio is actually achieved, so \(\frac{5}{2}\) as the Price of Anarchy for this model is tight.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们还会在这个背景下提及无政府代价(Price of Anarchy)。对于特定的拥塞网络(congestion network)，它被定义为均衡(equilibrium)状态下用户的最差平均成本(cost)与社会最优(social optimum)状态下的最优平均成本之比。“无政府”指的是路由博弈中的自私行为，即每个用户自行选择其最优路线。在庇古网络(Pigou network)中，无政府代价为 \(\frac{100}{75} = \frac{4}{3}\) 。对于可拆分流(splittable flow)，可以证明，当每条边(edge)的成本函数为仿射函数，即对于合适的非负实数 \(a\) 和 \(b\) (取决于具体的边)，具有 \(c\left( x\right)  = {ax} + b\) 的形式时，这是可能出现的最差无政府代价。(关于此结果的证明，请参阅Roughgarden和Tardos(2002年)的定理4.5，或Roughgarden(2016年)的第11章。)换句话说，对于仿射成本函数，自私路由的成本永远不会比中央规划所能达到的成本高出 \(1/3\) 以上。对于不可拆分流(本章的主题)，可以证明仿射成本函数的无政府代价至多为 \(\frac{5}{2}\) (Roughgarden，2016年，定理12.3)。练习2.3描述了一个实际上能达到这个糟糕比率的网络，因此在这个模型中 \(\frac{5}{2}\) 作为无政府代价是紧的。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{2.7 Further Reading}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{2.7 延伸阅读}
\end{tcolorbox}

We have used material from chapter 13 of the book by Roughgarden (2016), in particular theorem 13.6 and its notation for our proof of Theorem 2.2. Exercise 2.3 is figure 12.3 of that book, rotated so as to display its symmetry. The Pigou network has been described qualitatively by Pigou (1920, p. 194).

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们参考了Roughgarden(2016年)所著书籍第13章的内容，特别是定理13.6及其符号，用于我们对定理2.2的证明。练习2.3是那本书中图12.3旋转后的图形，以展示其对称性。庇古网络已由庇古(1920年，第194页)进行了定性描述。
\end{tcolorbox}

The Braess paradox is due to Braess (1968). Exercise 2.2 discusses the actual network used by Braess. Hong, Song, and Wu (2007) do not mention the Braess paradox in the Cheonggyecheon urban regeneration project, but show some nice "before" and "after" pictures on page 235, and add some Fengshui to this book.

\begin{tcolorbox}
布雷斯悖论(Braess paradox)由布雷斯(1968年)提出。练习2.2讨论了布雷斯实际使用的网络。Hong、Song和Wu(2007年)在清溪川城市再生项目中未提及布雷斯悖论，但在第235页展示了一些不错的“前后”对比图片，并为这本书增添了一些风水元素。
\end{tcolorbox}

The proof of Theorem 2.2 for atomic selfish routing with the help of a potential function \(\Phi\) was originally described by Rosenthal (1973). More general games where an equilibrium is found with the help of a potential function are the potential games studied by Monderer and Shapley (1996).

\begin{tcolorbox}
借助势函数 \(\Phi\) 对原子自私路由的定理2.2进行的证明最初由罗森塔尔(Rosenthal，1973年)提出。在更一般的博弈中，借助势函数找到均衡的博弈是由蒙德勒(Monderer)和夏普利(Shapley，1996年)研究的势博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

The model of splittable flow is due to Wardrop (1952), and its equilibrium is often called a Wardrop equilibrium.

\begin{tcolorbox}
可拆分流模型由沃德罗普(Wardrop，1952年)提出，其均衡通常被称为用户均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{2.8 Exercises for Chapter 2}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{2.8 第2章练习}
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 2.1. Consider the following congestion network with origin and destination nodes \(o\) and \(d\) , and an intermediate node \(v\) . There are ten individual users who want to travel from \(o\) to \(d\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习2.1。考虑以下拥塞网络，其起点和终点节点分别为 \(o\) 和 \(d\) ，中间节点为 \(v\) 。有十个个体用户想从 \(o\) 前往 \(d\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_61_674_728_579_290_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

As shown, the cost on the bottom edge is \(c\left( x\right)  = x + 2\) when it has \(x\) users, where \(x + y = {10}\) . The edge from \(o\) to \(v\) has cost function \(c\left( y\right)  = y\) . The top edge from \(v\) to \(d\) has constant cost 3, and the lower edge from \(v\) to \(d\) has cost function \(c\left( z\right)  = z\) if \(z\) of the \(y\) users use that edge, where \(0 \leq  z \leq  y\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
如图所示，当底部的边有 \(x\) 个用户时，其成本为 \(c\left( x\right)  = x + 2\) ，其中 \(x + y = {10}\) 。从 \(o\) 到 \(v\) 的边的成本函数为 \(c\left( y\right)  = y\) 。从 \(v\) 到 \(d\) 的顶部边的成本恒定为3，从 \(v\) 到 \(d\) 的底部边，如果 \(y\) 个用户中有 \(z\) 个使用该边，其成本函数为 \(c\left( z\right)  = z\) ，其中 \(0 \leq  z \leq  y\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Find all equilibria of this congestion game (note that \(x,y\) and \(z\) are integers), and their resulting average cost per user. [Hint: Consider first what \(z\) can be in an equilibrium.]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 找出这个拥塞博弈的所有均衡(注意 \(x,y\) 和 \(z\) 是整数)，以及每个用户的最终平均成本。[提示:首先考虑在均衡状态下 \(z\) 可能的取值。]
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) For the same network, find the equilibrium when \(x,y,z\) can be fractional for a total flow of 10 from \(o\) to \(d\) (so this flow is now splittable). Compare its average cost with the costs of the equilibria in (a).

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 对于同一个网络，当从 \(o\) 到 \(d\) 的总流量为10且 \(x,y,z\) 可以取分数值时(即现在的流量是可拆分的)，找出均衡。将其平均成本与(a)中均衡的成本进行比较。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 2.2. Consider the following two congestion networks with the indicated cost functions for a flow \(x\) on the respective edge; for example, \({50} + x\) is short for \(c\left( x\right)  = {50} + x\) . Note that different edges may have different flows \(x\) that depend on the chosen routes by the users.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习2.2。考虑以下两个拥塞网络，各边的流量 \(x\) 对应的成本函数已标明；例如， \({50} + x\) 是 \(c\left( x\right)  = {50} + x\) 的简写。注意，不同的边可能有不同的流量 \(x\) ，这取决于用户选择的路线。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_61_359_1773_1214_393_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Suppose there are six users who want to travel from \(o\) to \(d\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
假设有六个用户想从 \(o\) 前往 \(d\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Find the equilibrium flow in the network on the left.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 找出左边网络中的均衡流量。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) In the network on the right, start from the equilibrium flow in (a) and improve the route of one user at a time until you find an equilibrium flow. Compare the equilibrium costs in both networks.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 在右边的网络中，从(a)中的均衡流量开始，每次改进一个用户的路线，直到找到一个均衡流量。比较两个网络中的均衡成本。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 2.3. Consider the following congestion network with three nodes. As shown, an edge has the cost function \(c\left( x\right)  = x\) if the flow (number of users) on that edge is \(x\) , except for the edges \({vu}\) and \({wu}\) where \(c\left( x\right)  = 0\) . The table on the right shows four users \(i = 1,2,3,4\) with different origins \({o}_{i}\) and destinations \({d}_{i}\) . In this network, each user has two possible routes from her origin to her destination.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习2.3。考虑以下具有三个节点的拥塞网络。如图所示，如果某条边的流量(用户数量)为 \(x\) ，则该边的成本函数为 \(c\left( x\right)  = x\) ，但边 \({vu}\) 和 \({wu}\) 除外，其中 \(c\left( x\right)  = 0\) 。右边的表格显示了四个用户 \(i = 1,2,3,4\) ，他们有不同的起点 \({o}_{i}\) 和终点 \({d}_{i}\) 。在这个网络中，每个用户从其起点到终点有两条可能的路线。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_62_298_740_1051_421_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Find two equilibria of this congestion game, one with low cost and one with high cost. Explain why the equilibrium property holds in each case. Compare the costs of the two equilibria. What is the socially optimal (possibly non-equilibrium) flow?

\begin{tcolorbox}
找出这个拥塞博弈的两个均衡，一个是低成本均衡，一个是高成本均衡。解释在每种情况下均衡性质为何成立。比较这两个均衡的成本。社会最优(可能是非均衡)流量是多少？
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{3}

\section*{Games in Strategic Form}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{策略形式的博弈}
\end{tcolorbox}

In this chapter we start with the systematic development of non-cooperative game theory. Its most basic model is the game in strategic form, the topic of this chapter. The available actions of each player, called strategies, are assumed as given. The players choose their strategies simultaneously and independently, and receive individual payoffs that represent their preferences for strategy profiles (combinations of strategies).

\begin{tcolorbox}
在本章中，我们开始系统地发展非合作博弈论。其最基本的模型是策略形式的博弈，这也是本章的主题。假设每个玩家可用的行动(称为策略)是已知的。玩家们同时且独立地选择他们的策略，并获得代表他们对策略组合(策略的组合)偏好的个体收益。
\end{tcolorbox}

For two players, a game in strategic form is a table where one player chooses a row and the other player a column, with two payoffs in each cell of the table. We present a number of standard games such as the Prisoner's Dilemma, the Quality game, Chicken, the Battle of the Sexes, and Matching Pennies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于两个玩家的情况，策略形式的博弈是一个表格，其中一个玩家选择行，另一个玩家选择列，表格的每个单元格中有两个收益。我们介绍一些标准博弈，如囚徒困境、产品质量博弈、胆小鬼博弈、性别战和硬币匹配 (博弈)。
\end{tcolorbox}

The central concept of equilibrium is a profile of strategies that are mutual best responses. A game may have one, several, or no equilibria. As shown in Chapter 6, allowing for mixed (randomized) strategies will ensure that every finite game has an equilibrium, as shown by Nash (1951). An equilibrium without randomization as considered in the present chapter is also known as a pure Nash equilibrium.

\begin{tcolorbox}
均衡的核心概念是一组相互为最优反应(best response)的策略组合。一个博弈可能有一个、多个或没有均衡。正如第6章所示，引入混合(随机化)策略将确保每个有限博弈都有一个均衡，这是由纳什(1951)证明的。本章所考虑的无随机化的均衡也被称为纯纳什均衡(Nash equilibrium)。
\end{tcolorbox}

A strategy dominates another strategy if the player strictly prefers it for any fixed strategies of the other players. Dominated strategies are never played in equilibrium and can therefore be eliminated from the game. If iterated elimination of dominated strategies results in a unique strategy profile, the game is called dominance solvable. We illustrate this with the "Cournot duopoly" of quantity competition.
