(In Section 4.7 in Chapter 4, we will change this game to a commitment game, known as Stackelberg leadership.)

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果对于其他参与者的任何固定策略，某一参与者严格偏好某一策略而非另一策略，那么该策略就占优于另一策略。被占优策略(dominated strategy)在均衡中永远不会被采用，因此可以从博弈中剔除。如果重复剔除劣势策略(iterated elimination of dominated)得到唯一的策略组合，那么该博弈被称为占优可解(dominance solvable)博弈。我们用产量竞争的“古诺双寡头博弈(Cournot duopoly)”来说明这一点。(在第4章的4.7节中，我们将把这个博弈转变为一个承诺博弈(commitment game)，即斯塔克尔伯格领导博弈。)
\end{tcolorbox}

A strategy weakly dominates another strategy if the player weakly prefers it for any fixed strategies of the other players, and in at least one case strictly prefers it. Eliminating a weakly dominated strategy does not introduce new equilibria, but may lose equilibria, which reduces the understanding of the game. Unless one is interested in finding just one equilibrium of the game, one should therefore not eliminate weakly dominated strategies, nor iterate that process.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果对于其他参与者的任何固定策略，某一参与者弱偏好某一策略而非另一策略，并且至少在一种情况下严格偏好该策略，那么该策略就弱占优于另一策略。剔除一个弱被占优策略(weakly dominated strategy)不会引入新的均衡，但可能会丢失一些均衡，这会降低对博弈的理解。因此，除非只对找到博弈的一个均衡感兴趣，否则不应该剔除弱被占优策略，也不应该重复这个过程。
\end{tcolorbox}

The final Section 3.8 shows that symmetric \(N\) -player games with two strategies per player always have an equilibrium.

\begin{tcolorbox}
最后的3.8节表明，每个参与者有两种策略的对称(symmetric) \(N\) 人博弈总是存在一个均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{3.1 Prerequisites and Learning Outcomes}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{3.1 先决条件和学习成果}
\end{tcolorbox}

This is a first core chapter of the book. The previous Chapter 2 has introduced the concepts of strategies and equilibrium for the special congestion games and is therefore useful but not a prerequisite. We deal mostly with finite sets. You should be thoroughly familiar with Cartesian products \({S}_{1} \times  \cdots  \times  {S}_{N}\) of sets \({S}_{1},\ldots ,{S}_{N}\) . The Cournot game on intervals is analyzed with basic calculus.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这是本书的第一个核心章节。前一章(第2章)介绍了特殊拥塞博弈(congestion game)的策略和均衡概念，因此有帮助但不是先决条件。我们主要处理有限集。你应该熟悉集合 \({S}_{1},\ldots ,{S}_{N}\) 的笛卡尔积 \({S}_{1} \times  \cdots  \times  {S}_{N}\) 。区间上的古诺博弈用基础微积分进行分析。
\end{tcolorbox}

After studying this chapter, you should

\begin{tcolorbox}
学习本章后，你应该
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item know the components of a game in strategic form: strategies, strategy profiles, and payoffs;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 了解策略形式(strategic form)博弈的组成部分:策略、策略组合和收益；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item be able to write down and correctly interpret tables for two-player games;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 能够写出并正确解释两人博弈的表格；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item understand the concept of an equilibrium, use this term correctly (and its plural "equilibria"), and know how it relates to strategies, partial strategy profiles, and best responses;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 理解均衡的概念，正确使用该术语(及其复数形式“equilibria”)，并知道它与策略、部分策略组合(partial strategy profile)和最优反应的关系；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item be familiar with common \(2 \times  2\) games such as the Prisoner’s Dilemma or the Stag Hunt, and understand how they differ;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 熟悉常见的 \(2 \times  2\) 博弈，如囚徒困境(Prisoner’s Dilemma)或猎鹿博弈(Stag Hunt)，并理解它们的区别；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item know the difference between dominance and weak dominance and why dominated strategies can be eliminated in a complete equilibrium analysis but weakly dominated strategies cannot;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 了解占优和弱占优的区别，以及为什么在完整的均衡分析中可以剔除被占优策略，但不能剔除弱被占优策略；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item know the Cournot quantity game, also when strategy sets are real intervals;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 了解古诺产量博弈，包括策略集为实数区间的情况；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item understand symmetry in games with two players and more than two players.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 理解两人及多人博弈中的对称性。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{3.2 Games in Strategic Form}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{3.2 策略形式的博弈}
\end{tcolorbox}

A game in strategic form is the fundamental model of non-cooperative game theory. The game has \(N\) players, \(N \geq  1\) , and each player \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) has a nonempty set \({S}_{i}\) of strategies. If each player \(i\) chooses a strategy \({s}_{i}\) from \({S}_{i}\) , the resulting \(N\) -tuple \(s = \left( {{s}_{1},\ldots ,{s}_{n}}\right)\) is called a strategy profile. The game is specified by assigning to each strategy profile \(s\) a real-valued payoff \({u}_{i}\left( s\right)\) to each player \(i\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
策略形式的博弈是非合作博弈论的基本模型。该博弈有 \(N\) 个参与者， \(N \geq  1\) ，并且每个参与者 \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) 都有一个非空的策略集 \({S}_{i}\) 。如果每个参与者 \(i\) 从 \({S}_{i}\) 中选择一个策略 \({s}_{i}\) ，那么得到的 \(N\) 元组 \(s = \left( {{s}_{1},\ldots ,{s}_{n}}\right)\) 被称为一个策略组合。该博弈通过为每个策略组合 \(s\) 给每个参与者 \(i\) 分配一个实值收益 \({u}_{i}\left( s\right)\) 来确定。
\end{tcolorbox}

The payoffs represent each player’s preference. For two strategy profiles \(s\) and \(\widehat{s}\) , player \(i\) strictly prefers \(s\) to \(\widehat{s}\) if \({u}_{i}\left( s\right)  > {u}_{i}\left( \widehat{s}\right)\) , and is indifferent between \(s\) and \(\widehat{s}\) if \({u}_{i}\left( s\right)  = {u}_{i}\left( \widehat{s}\right)\) . If \({u}_{i}\left( s\right)  \geq  {u}_{i}\left( \widehat{s}\right)\) then player \(i\) weakly prefers \(s\) to \(\widehat{s}\) . Player \(i\) is only interested in maximizing his own payoff, and not interested in the payoffs to other players (other than in trying to anticipate their actions); any "social" concern a player has about a particular outcome has to be (and could be) built into his own payoff. All players know the available strategies and payoffs of the other players, and know that they know them, etc. (that is, the game is "common knowledge").

\begin{tcolorbox}
收益代表了每个玩家的偏好。对于两个策略组合 \(s\) 和 \(\widehat{s}\) ，如果 \({u}_{i}\left( s\right)  > {u}_{i}\left( \widehat{s}\right)\) ，玩家 \(i\) 严格偏好 \(s\) 而非 \(\widehat{s}\) ；如果 \({u}_{i}\left( s\right)  = {u}_{i}\left( \widehat{s}\right)\) ，玩家 \(i\) 对 \(s\) 和 \(\widehat{s}\) 无差异。如果 \({u}_{i}\left( s\right)  \geq  {u}_{i}\left( \widehat{s}\right)\) ，则玩家 \(i\) 弱偏好 \(s\) 而非 \(\widehat{s}\) 。玩家 \(i\) 只关心最大化自己的收益，而不关心其他玩家的收益(除了试图预测他们的行动)；玩家对特定结果的任何“社会”考量都必须(也可以)纳入他自己的收益中。所有玩家都知道其他玩家可用的策略和收益，并且知道他们都知道这些，以此类推(也就是说，该博弈是“共同知识”)。
\end{tcolorbox}

The game is played as follows: The players choose their strategies simultaneously (without knowing what the other players choose), and receive their respective payoffs for the resulting strategy profile. They cannot enter into any binding agreements about what they should play (which is why the game is called "noncooperative"). Furthermore, the game is assumed to be played only once, and therefore also called a one-shot game. Playing the same game (or a varying game) many times leads to the much more advanced theory of repeated games.

\begin{tcolorbox}
该博弈的进行方式如下:玩家同时选择他们的策略(不知道其他玩家的选择)，并根据所产生的策略组合获得各自的收益。他们不能就应该采取的行动达成任何有约束力的协议(这就是为什么该博弈被称为“非合作博弈”)。此外，假设该博弈只进行一次，因此也被称为一次性博弈。多次进行相同的博弈(或不同的博弈)会引出更为高深的重复博弈理论。
\end{tcolorbox}

Much of this book is concerned with two-player games. The players are typically named 1 and 2 or I and II. Then the strategic form is conveniently represented by a table. The rows of the table represent the strategies of player I (also called the row player), and the columns represent the strategies of player II (the column player). A strategy profile is a strategy pair, that is, a row and a column, with a corresponding cell of the table that contains two payoffs, one for player I and the other for player II.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本书大部分内容涉及两人博弈。玩家通常被命名为 1 和 2 或 I 和 II。然后，策略形式可以方便地用一个表格来表示。表格的行代表玩家 I(也称为行玩家)的策略，列代表玩家 II(列玩家)的策略。一个策略组合是一个策略对，即一行和一列，表格中对应的单元格包含两个收益，一个是玩家 I 的，另一个是玩家 II 的。
\end{tcolorbox}

If \(m\) and \(n\) are positive integers, then an \(m \times  n\) game is a two-player game in strategic form with \(m\) strategies for player I (the rows of the table) and \(n\) strategies for player II (the columns of the table).

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果 \(m\) 和 \(n\) 是正整数，那么一个 \(m \times  n\) 博弈是一个策略形式的两人博弈，玩家 I 有 \(m\) 个策略(表格的行)，玩家 II 有 \(n\) 个策略(表格的列)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Many interesting game-theoretic observations apply already to \(2 \times  2\) games. Figure 3.1(a) shows the famous Prisoner's Dilemma game. Each player has two strategies, called \(C\) and \(D\) , which stand for "cooperate" and "defect". The payoffs are as follows: If both players choose \(C\) , then both get payoff 2. If both players choose \(D\) , then both get payoff 1 . If the two players choose different strategies, then the player who chooses \(C\) gets payoff 0 and the player who chooses \(D\) gets the highest possible payoff 3.

\begin{tcolorbox}
许多有趣的博弈论观察结果已经适用于 \(2 \times  2\) 博弈。图 3.1(a) 展示了著名的囚徒困境博弈。每个玩家有两个策略，分别称为 \(C\) 和 \(D\) ，分别代表“合作”和“背叛”。收益情况如下:如果两个玩家都选择 \(C\) ，则两人都获得收益 2。如果两个玩家都选择 \(D\) ，则两人都获得收益 1。如果两个玩家选择不同的策略，那么选择 \(C\) 的玩家获得收益 0，选择 \(D\) 的玩家获得最高可能的收益 3。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_66_289_1504_1062_362_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 3.1 Prisoner's Dilemma game (a), its symmetry shown by reflection along the dotted diagonal line in (b).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 3.1 囚徒困境博弈 (a)，其对称性在 (b) 中通过沿虚线对角线反射显示。
\end{tcolorbox}

In each cell of the payoff table, the payoff to the row player is shown in the lower left corner and the payoff to the column player in the upper right corner. There are several very good reasons for this display:

\begin{tcolorbox}
在收益表的每个单元格中，行玩家的收益显示在左下角，列玩家的收益显示在右上角。这种显示方式有几个很好的理由:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item There is no ambiguity to whom the payoff belongs. The payoff close to the left is near the strategy labels for the rows, the payoff close to the top is near the labels for the columns; this subdivision is also shown at the top left of the table that shows the two player names, here I and II.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 收益归属明确。靠近左边的收益靠近行的策略标签，靠近顶部的收益靠近列的标签；这种划分也在表格的左上角显示，那里显示了两个玩家的名称，这里是 I 和 II。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item It is immediately apparent if the game is symmetric (that is, it stays the same game when exchanging the players), by reflecting the table along the diagonal line which exchanges the players, their strategies (rows and columns), and the payoffs in each cell, as shown in Figure 3.1(b). This reflection works because the rows are listed from top to bottom and the columns from left to right.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 通过沿对角线反射表格(交换玩家、他们的策略(行和列)以及每个单元格中的收益)，可以立即看出该博弈是否是对称的(即，交换玩家后博弈保持不变)，如图 3.1(b) 所示。这种反射可行是因为行是从上到下列出的，列是从左到右列出的。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item An \(m \times  n\) game is specified by two \(m \times  n\) matrices \(A\) and \(B\) which contain the payoffs to players I and II. Because the payoffs are staggered in each cell, these two matrices are easy to identify separately so that one can focus on the payoffs of a single player.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 一个 \(m \times  n\) 博弈由两个 \(m \times  n\) 矩阵 \(A\) 和 \(B\) 确定，它们分别包含玩家 I 和玩家 II 的收益。由于每个单元格中的收益是错开的，这两个矩阵很容易分别识别，这样就可以专注于单个玩家的收益。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

The only unfamiliar aspect of putting the row player's payoff at the bottom left of a cell is that it is below the column player's payoff, where the usual convention is to consider rows first and columns second. However, the above reasons do not depend on this convention and treat rows and columns symmetrically. Another common display (usually for typesetting reasons) that uses the convention "rows first, columns second" is

\begin{tcolorbox}
将行参与者的收益置于单元格左下角唯一不熟悉的方面在于，它位于列参与者的收益下方，而通常的惯例是先考虑行，再考虑列。然而，上述原因并不依赖于这一惯例，而是对称地(symmetric)对待行和列。另一种采用“先考虑行，再考虑列”这一惯例的常见显示方式(通常出于排版原因)是
\end{tcolorbox}

(3.1)where the payoffs are not staggered and therefore harder to separate, and where the symmetry of a game is less apparent. We will always use the staggered payoff display as in Figure 3.1.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在这种显示方式中，收益没有错开，因此更难区分，而且博弈的对称性也不太明显。我们将始终使用如图3.1所示的错开收益显示方式。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\adjustbox{max width=\textwidth}{
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
\multirow{3}{*}{AI \(C\)  \(D\)} & \(C\) & \(D\) \\
\cline{2-3}
 & 2, 2 & 0,3 \\
\cline{2-3}
 & 3,0 & 1,1 \\
\cline{1-3}
\hline
\end{tabular}
}
\end{center}

\begin{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\adjustbox{max width=\textwidth}{
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
\multirow{3}{*}{人工智能(AI) \(C\)  \(D\)} & \(C\) & \(D\) \\
\cline{2-3}
 & 2, 2 & 0,3 \\
\cline{2-3}
 & 3,0 & 1,1 \\
\cline{1-3}
\hline
\end{tabular}
}
\end{center}

\end{tcolorbox}

The story behind the name "Prisoner's Dilemma" is that of two prisoners held suspect of a serious crime. There is no judicial evidence for this crime except if one of the prisoners testifies against the other. If one of them testifies (choosing " \(D\) "), he will be rewarded with immunity from prosecution (payoff 3), whereas the other will serve a long prison sentence (payoff 0 ). If both testify, their punishment will be less severe (payoff 1 for each). If they both "cooperate" with each other by not testifying at all (choosing " \(C\) "), they will only be imprisoned briefly for some minor charge that can be held against them (payoff 2 for each). The "defection" from that mutually beneficial outcome is to testify, which gives a higher payoff no matter what the other prisoner does. However, the resulting payoff is lower to both. This constitutes their "dilemma".

\begin{tcolorbox}
“囚徒困境”这一名称背后的故事涉及两名因涉嫌严重犯罪而被关押的囚犯。除了其中一名囚犯指证另一名囚犯之外，没有其他司法证据证明这一罪行。如果其中一人指证(选择“ \(D\) ”)，他将获得免于起诉的奖励(收益为3)，而另一人将面临长期监禁(收益为0)。如果两人都指证，他们的刑罚会较轻(每人收益为1)。如果他们通过完全不指证而相互“合作”(选择“ \(C\) ”)，他们只会因一些可指控的轻微罪行而被短期监禁(每人收益为2)。从这种互利结果的“背叛”行为就是指证，无论另一名囚犯怎么做，指证都会带来更高的收益。然而，最终两人的收益都会降低。这就构成了他们的“困境”。
\end{tcolorbox}

In the Prisoner's Dilemma, both players always have an incentive to defect, so that the strategy pair(D, D)will be the predicted outcome of playing the game, even though both players are worse off than if they played(C, C). The reason is that the non-cooperative model assumes that the players cannot enforce an agreement to play(C, C)(if they could, for example by signing a contract of some sort, this would have to be a separate strategy in the game). The Prisoner's Dilemma shows that individual incentives may jeopardize a common good. For example, in an arms race between two nations, \(C\) and \(D\) may represent the choices between restricted and unlimited arms build-up. The Prisoner's Dilemma can also be extended to many players. For example, it may be in every fisher's interest not to overfish so that fish stocks do not decline, by introducing a fishing quota that limits each fisher's catch. If adopted collectively, this would benefit all. However, without an enforcement of fishing quotas, each single fisher who exceeds his quota will barely contribute to overfishing but strongly benefit for himself. Collectively, they destroy their fish stock this way. This is also called the "tragedy of the commons". It applies similarly to many types of actions that collectively harm the environment (and each participant) but are individually beneficial.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在囚徒困境中，两名玩家总是有背叛的动机，因此策略组合(D，D)将是这场博弈的预期结果，尽管如果他们选择(C，C)，两名玩家的情况都会更好。原因在于非合作模型假设玩家无法执行达成的选择(C，C)的协议(例如，如果他们可以通过签署某种合同来达成协议，那么这在博弈中必须是一种单独的策略)。囚徒困境表明个人动机可能会损害公共利益。例如，在两个国家的军备竞赛中， \(C\) 和 \(D\) 可能代表限制军备建设和无限制军备建设之间的选择。囚徒困境也可以扩展到多个玩家。例如，通过引入限制每个渔民捕捞量的捕捞配额，不进行过度捕捞以避免鱼类资源减少可能符合每个渔民的利益。如果集体采纳这一措施，将对所有人都有益。然而，如果不执行捕捞配额，每个超过配额的渔民对过度捕捞的影响微乎其微，但自己却能获得巨大利益。从总体上看，他们这样做会破坏鱼类资源。这也被称为“公地悲剧”。这同样适用于许多对环境(以及每个参与者)造成集体损害但对个人有益的行为。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{3.3 Best Responses and Equilibrium}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{3.3 最优反应与均衡}
\end{tcolorbox}

Consider the game in Figure 3.2(a). This is the Prisoner's Dilemma game, with further graphical information added to help the analysis of the game. Namely, certain payoffs are surrounded by boxes that indicate that they are best-response payoffs.

\begin{tcolorbox}
考虑图3.2(a)中的博弈。这是囚徒困境博弈，并添加了更多图形信息以帮助分析该博弈。具体来说，某些收益被方框包围，表明它们是最优反应收益。
\end{tcolorbox}

A best response is a strategy of a player that is optimal for that player assuming it is known what all other players do. In a two-player game, there is only one other player. Hence, for the row player, a best response is found for each column. In the Prisoner’s Dilemma game, the best response of the row player against column \(C\) is row \(D\) because his payoff when playing \(D\) is 3 rather than 2 when playing \(C\) (so in the cell for row \(D\) and column \(C\) , the payoff 3 to player \(\mathrm{I}\) is put in a box), and the best response against column \(D\) is also row \(D\) (with payoff 1, in a box, which is larger than payoff 0 for row \(C\) ). Similarly, the best response of the column player against row \(C\) is column \(D\) , and against row \(D\) is column \(D\) , as shown by the boxes around the payoffs for the column player. Because the game is symmetric, the best-response payoffs are also symmetric.

\begin{tcolorbox}
最优反应是指在已知其他所有玩家的行动的情况下，对某个玩家而言最优的策略。在两人博弈中，只有另一名玩家。因此，对于行玩家来说，要为每一列找到最优反应。在囚徒困境博弈中，行玩家针对列 \(C\) 的最优反应是行 \(D\) ，因为他选择 \(D\) 时的收益是3，而选择 \(C\) 时的收益是2(因此在行 \(D\) 和列 \(C\) 的单元格中，玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 的收益3被放在方框中)，并且针对列 \(D\) 的最优反应也是行 \(D\) (收益为1，放在方框中，大于行 \(C\) 的收益0)。同样，列玩家针对行 \(C\) 的最优反应是列 \(D\) ，针对行 \(D\) 的最优反应也是列 \(D\) ，列玩家的收益周围的方框显示了这一点。由于该博弈是对称的，最优反应收益也是对称的。
\end{tcolorbox}

The game in Figure 3.2(b) has payoffs that are nearly identical to those of the Prisoner's Dilemma game, except for the payoff to player II for the top right cell, which is changed from 3 to 1 . Because the game is not symmetric, we name the strategies of the players differently, here \(T\) and \(B\) for the row player (for "top" and "bottom") and \(l\) and \(r\) for the column player ("left" and "right"). To distinguish them more easily, we often write the strategies of player I in upper case and those of player II in lower case.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.2(b)中的博弈收益与囚徒困境博弈的收益几乎相同，除了右上角单元格中玩家II的收益从3变为1。由于该博弈不是对称的，我们对玩家的策略进行了不同的命名，这里行玩家的策略是 \(T\) 和 \(B\) (分别表示“上”和“下”)，列玩家的策略是 \(l\) 和 \(r\) (分别表示“左”和“右”)。为了更方便地区分它们，我们通常将玩家I的策略用大写字母表示，将玩家II的策略用小写字母表示。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_69_383_194_1101_363_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 3.2 Prisoner's Dilemma game (a) with best-response payoffs shown by boxes, and the same done for the Quality game (b).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.2 囚徒困境博弈(a)，其中最优反应收益用方框表示，产品质量博弈(b)也是如此。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 3.2(b) is called the Quality game. It may describe the situation of player I as a restaurant owner, who can provide food of good quality (strategy \(T\) ) or bad quality(B), and a potential customer, player II, who may decide to eat there(l)or not(r). The customer prefers \(l\) to \(r\) only if the quality is good. However, whatever player II does, player I is better off by choosing \(B\) . These preferences are shown by the best-response boxes. The best response of player II to row \(T\) is different from her best response to row \(B\) . The best response of player I to either column is always row \(B\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.2(b)被称为产品质量博弈。它可以描述参与者I作为餐厅老板的情况，老板可以提供高质量的食物(策略 \(T\) )或低质量的食物(B)，而潜在客户，即参与者II，可以决定在那里就餐(l)或不在那里就餐(r)。只有当食物质量好时，客户才会偏好 \(l\) 而非 \(r\) 。然而，无论参与者II怎么做，参与者I选择 \(B\) 都会更好。这些偏好通过最优反应框显示出来。参与者II对行 \(T\) 的最优反应与她对行 \(B\) 的最优反应不同。参与者I对任意列的最优反应始终是行 \(B\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

In both games in Figure 3.2, consider the bottom right cell of the table, which corresponds to the strategy pair(D, D)in the Prisoner’s Dilemma game and to (B, r)in the Quality game. In each game, this is the only cell that has both payoffs surrounded by a box. This means that the two strategies that define that cell are mutual best responses. Such a strategy pair is called an equilibrium, which is the central solution concept for non-cooperative games.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在图3.2的两个博弈中，考虑表格的右下角单元格，在囚徒困境博弈中对应策略组合(D，D)，在产品质量博弈中对应(B，r)。在每个博弈中，这个单元格是唯一两个收益都被框起来的单元格。这意味着定义该单元格的两个策略是相互的最优反应。这样的策略组合被称为均衡，它是非合作博弈的核心解概念。
\end{tcolorbox}

We define the concepts of best response and equilibrium for a general \(N\) -player game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们为一般的 \(N\) 人博弈定义最优反应和均衡的概念。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 3.1. Consider a game in strategic form with \(N\) players where player \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) has the strategy set \({S}_{i}\) and receives payoff \({u}_{i}\left( s\right)\) for each strategy profile \(s = \left( {{s}_{1},\ldots ,{s}_{N}}\right)\) in \({S}_{1} \times  \cdots  \times  {S}_{N}\) . For player \(i\) , consider a partial profile

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义3.1。考虑一个具有 \(N\) 个参与者的策略形式博弈，其中参与者 \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) 的策略集为 \({S}_{i}\) ，并且对于 \({S}_{1} \times  \cdots  \times  {S}_{N}\) 中的每个策略组合 \(s = \left( {{s}_{1},\ldots ,{s}_{N}}\right)\) ，参与者 \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) 获得收益 \({u}_{i}\left( s\right)\) 。对于参与者 \(i\) ，考虑其他参与者的部分策略组合
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{s}_{-i} = \left( {{s}_{1},\ldots ,{s}_{i - 1},{s}_{i + 1},\ldots ,{s}_{N}}\right)  \tag{3.2}
\]

of strategies of the other players, given by their strategies \({s}_{j}\) for \(j \neq  i\) . For any strategy \({s}_{i}\) in \({S}_{i}\) , this partial profile is extended to a full strategy profile denoted by \(s = \left( {{s}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)\) . Then \({s}_{i}\) is a best response to \({s}_{-i}\) if

\begin{tcolorbox}
由其他参与者在 \(j \neq  i\) 时的策略 \({s}_{j}\) 给出。对于 \({S}_{i}\) 中的任何策略 \({s}_{i}\) ，这个部分策略组合可以扩展为一个完整的策略组合，记为 \(s = \left( {{s}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)\) 。那么，如果满足以下条件，则 \({s}_{i}\) 是对 \({s}_{-i}\) 的最优反应
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{u}_{i}\left( s\right)  = {u}_{i}\left( {{s}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)  \geq  {u}_{i}\left( {{\bar{s}}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right) \;\text{ for all }{\bar{s}}_{i} \in  {S}_{i}. \tag{3.3}
\]

An equilibrium of the game is a strategy profile \(s\) where each strategy is a best response to the other strategies in that profile, that is, (3.3) holds for all players \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈的一个均衡是一个策略组合 \(s\) ，其中每个策略都是对该组合中其他策略的最优反应，即，对于所有参与者 \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) ，式(3.3)都成立。
\end{tcolorbox}

The notation (3.2) for a partial profile \({s}_{-i}\) of strategies of the players other than player \(i\) is very common. For a two-player game \(\left( {N = 2}\right)\) , it is just a strategy of the other player. The notation \(\left( {{s}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)\) creates a profile \(s\) for all players, where \({s}_{i}\) is assumed to inserted at the \(i\) th position (as the strategy of player \(i\) ) to define the strategy profile \(s\) . The condition (3.3) states that \({s}_{i}\) is a best response to \({s}_{-i}\) because player \(i\) cannot get a better payoff by changing to a different strategy \({\bar{s}}_{i}\) . That payoff may stay the same (in which case \({\bar{s}}_{i}\) is also a best response to \({s}_{-i}\) ). Moreover, only unilateral changes by a single player are considered, not joint changes by several players, as for example, a change from(D, D)to(C, C)in the Prisoner’s Dilemma. An equilibrium, as a strategy profile of mutual best responses, is therefore a profile where no player can profitably gain by unilaterally changing his strategy, that is, with the remaining strategies kept fixed.

\begin{tcolorbox}
除参与者 \(i\) 之外的其他参与者的部分策略组合 \({s}_{-i}\) 的表示法(3.2)非常常见。对于两人博弈 \(\left( {N = 2}\right)\) ，它只是另一个参与者的一个策略。表示法 \(\left( {{s}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)\) 为所有参与者创建了一个策略组合 \(s\) ，其中假设 \({s}_{i}\) 被插入到第 \(i\) 个位置(作为参与者 \(i\) 的策略)来定义策略组合 \(s\) 。条件(3.3)表明 \({s}_{i}\) 是对 \({s}_{-i}\) 的最优反应，因为参与者 \(i\) 通过改变为不同的策略 \({\bar{s}}_{i}\) 无法获得更好的收益。收益可能保持不变(在这种情况下， \({\bar{s}}_{i}\) 也是对 \({s}_{-i}\) 的最优反应)。此外，只考虑单个参与者的单方面改变，而不考虑多个参与者的联合改变，例如，在囚徒困境中从(D，D)变为(C，C)。因此，作为相互最优反应的策略组合，均衡是一个没有参与者可以通过单方面改变其策略而获利的组合，即，其余策略保持不变。
\end{tcolorbox}

Some notes on terminology: An equilibrium is often called Nash equilibrium, named after John Nash (1951) who proved that an "equilibrium point" always exists for finite strategy sets that are extended to include randomized or "mixed" strategies (see Chapter 6). In distinction to such a "mixed equilibrium", an equilibrium according to Definition 3.1 is often called a pure equilibrium where players choose their strategies deterministically. The plural of "equilibrium" is equilibria.

\begin{tcolorbox}
关于术语的一些说明:均衡通常被称为纳什均衡(Nash equilibrium)，以约翰·纳什(John Nash，1951)的名字命名，他证明了对于扩展到包括随机或“混合”策略的有限策略集(见第6章)，“均衡点”总是存在的。与这种“混合均衡(mixed equilibrium)”不同，根据定义3.1的均衡通常被称为纯策略均衡(pure equilibrium)，其中参与者确定性地选择他们的策略。“equilibrium”的复数形式是“equilibria”。
\end{tcolorbox}

The congestion games studied in Chapter 2 are \(N\) -player games, where the strategy set of each player \(i\) is the set of paths from her origin \({o}_{i}\) to her destination \({d}_{i}\) , and a player's payoff is the negative of her cost as it results from the cost of using the edges on such a path, depending on their usage by all players. Exercise 3.2 considers a three-player game where the strategic form is given directly by a suitable "three-dimensional" table.

\begin{tcolorbox}
第2章中研究的拥塞博弈(congestion game)是 \(N\) 人博弈，其中每个参与者 \(i\) 的策略集是从其起点 \({o}_{i}\) 到其终点 \({d}_{i}\) 的路径集合，而参与者的收益是其成本的负值，该成本源于使用这条路径上的边的成本，这取决于所有参与者对这些边的使用情况。练习3.2考虑了一个三人博弈，其策略形式(strategic form)由一个合适的“三维”表格直接给出。
\end{tcolorbox}

Why is equilibrium such an important concept? The analysis of a game should describe what players will (or should) do when following their preferences, taking into account that the other players do the same. A recommendation of a strategy to each player defines a strategy profile. This profile should be an equilibrium because otherwise this recommendation would be self-defeating, that is, at least one player would have an incentive to deviate from her recommended strategy to some other strategy. In that sense, equilibrium is a necessary condition of "stability" for a game-theoretic "solution". This does not mean that an equilibrium will always exist, or that the players will always reach an equilibrium when they adapt their strategies to be best responses. In practice, people may not play an equilibrium because the game is only a simplified model of the interactive situation, and even in a game with clear rules (such as Chess) it may be too difficult to play perfectly.

\begin{tcolorbox}
为什么均衡(equilibrium)是如此重要的概念呢？对博弈的分析应该描述参与者在遵循自身偏好的情况下会(或应该)怎么做，同时要考虑到其他参与者也会这么做。为每个参与者推荐一种策略就定义了一个策略组合。这个组合应该是一个均衡，否则这个推荐就会自相矛盾，也就是说，至少有一个参与者会有动机从推荐给她的策略转向其他策略。从这个意义上说，均衡是博弈论“解”的“稳定性”的必要条件。这并不意味着均衡总是存在，也不意味着参与者在调整策略以达到最优反应(best response)时总能达到均衡。在实践中，人们可能不会达到均衡，因为博弈只是对互动情况的简化模型，即使在规则明确的博弈(如国际象棋)中，要完美地进行博弈也可能太难了。
\end{tcolorbox}

Much of this book is about determining the equilibria of a game, as the first step in a game-theoretic analysis. A separate argument is then which of these equilibria, if any, make sense as a practical recommendation of how to play. Another insight may be that an equilibrium has undesirable properties, like in the Prisoner's Dilemma or in the Braess paradox in Figure 2.3.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本书的大部分内容是关于确定博弈的均衡，这是博弈论分析的第一步。然后另一个问题是，如果存在多个均衡，那么其中哪些均衡可以作为如何进行博弈的实际建议。另一个见解可能是，一个均衡可能有不理想的性质，比如在囚徒困境或图2.3中的布雷斯悖论(Braess paradox)中。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{3.4 Games with Multiple Equilibria}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{3.4 具有多个均衡的博弈}
\end{tcolorbox}

We consider several well-known \(2 \times  2\) games that have more than one equilibrium. The game of Chicken is shown in Figure 3.3. It is symmetric, as shown by the dotted line, and we have directly marked the best-response payoffs with boxes; recall that neither markup is part of the game description but already part of its analysis. The two strategies \(A\) and \(C\) stand for "aggressive" and "cautious" behavior, for example of two car drivers that drive towards each other on a narrow road. The aggressive strategy is only advantageous (with payoff 2) if the other player is cautious but leads to a crash (payoff 0 ) if the other player is aggressive, whereas a cautious strategy always gives payoff 1 to the player who uses it. This game has two equilibria,(C, A)and(A, C), because the best response to aggressive behavior is to be cautious and vice versa.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们考虑几个著名的具有多个均衡的对称博弈(symmetric game)。胆小鬼博弈(Chicken)如图3.3所示。它是对称的，如虚线所示，我们已经直接用方框标记出了最优反应的收益；请记住，这两种标记都不是博弈描述的一部分，而是其分析的一部分。两种策略 \(A\) 和 \(C\) 分别代表“激进”和“谨慎”的行为，例如两个司机在狭窄道路上相向行驶的情况。如果另一个参与者采取谨慎策略，激进策略才是有利的(收益为2)，但如果另一个参与者也采取激进策略，就会导致碰撞(收益为0)，而谨慎策略总是给采取该策略的参与者带来收益1。这个博弈有两个均衡，(C, A)和(A, C)，因为对激进行为的最优反应是谨慎，反之亦然。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.2\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_71_397_997_353_357_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 3.3 The symmetric game of Chicken.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.3 对称的胆小鬼博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

The game known as the Battle of the Sexes is shown in Figure 3.4(a); its rather antiquated gender stereotypes should not be taken too seriously. In this scenario, player I and player II are a couple who each decide (simultaneously and independently, which is rather unrealistic) whether to go to a concert(C)or to a sports event(S). The players have different payoffs arising from which event they go to, but that payoff is zero if they have to attend the event alone. The game has two equilibria:(C, C)where they both go to the concert, or(S, S)where they both go to the sports event. Their preferences between these events differ, however.

\begin{tcolorbox}
被称为性别战(Battle of the Sexes)的博弈如图3.4(a)所示；其相当过时的性别刻板印象不应被太当真。在这个场景中，参与者I和参与者II是一对情侣，他们各自决定(同时且独立地，这相当不现实)是去听音乐会(C)还是去看体育赛事(S)。参与者从他们去的活动中获得不同的收益，但如果他们必须独自参加活动，收益就是零。这个博弈有两个均衡:(C, C)，即他们都去听音乐会；或者(S, S)，即他们都去看体育赛事。然而，他们对这些活动的偏好不同。
\end{tcolorbox}

The Battle of the Sexes game is not symmetric when written as in Figure 3.4(a), because the payoffs for the strategy pairs(C, C)and(S, S)on the diagonal are not the same for both players, which is clearly necessary for symmetry. However, changing the order of the strategies of one player, for example of player I as shown in Figure 3.4(b), makes this a game with a symmetric payoff structure; for a true symmetric game, one would also have to exchange the strategy names \(C\) and \(S\) of the strategies of player I, which would not represent the actions that player I takes. The game in Figure 3.4(b) is very similar to Chicken. However, because of the meaning of the strategies, the Battle of the Sexes is a coordination game (both players benefit from choosing the same action), whereas Chicken is an "anti-coordination" game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如图3.4(a)所示写出的性别战博弈不是对称的，因为对角线上的策略对(C, C)和(S, S)的收益对两个参与者来说并不相同，而这显然是对称的必要条件。然而，改变一个参与者的策略顺序，例如如图3.4(b)所示改变参与者I的策略顺序，会使这个博弈具有对称的收益结构；对于真正的对称博弈，还必须交换参与者I的策略名称 \(C\) 和 \(S\) ，但这就不能代表参与者I实际采取的行动了。图3.4(b)中的博弈与胆小鬼博弈非常相似。然而，由于策略的含义，性别战是一个协调博弈(coordination game)(两个参与者都从选择相同的行动中受益)，而胆小鬼博弈是一个“反协调”博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_72_244_195_1105_362_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 3.4 The Battle of the Sexes game (a), whose payoff structure is symmetric if the strategies of one player are exchanged, as in (b).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.4 性别战博弈(a)，如果交换一个参与者的策略，其收益结构就会对称，如图(b)所示。
\end{tcolorbox}

The Stag Hunt game in Figure 3.5 models a conflict between cooperation and safety. Two hunters individually decide between \(S\) , to hunt a stag (a male deer), or \(H\) , to hunt a hare. Each can catch the hare, without the help of the other player, and then receives payoff 1 . In order to catch the stag they have to cooperate - if the other hunter goes for the hare then the stag hunter's payoff is zero. If they both hunt the stag they succeed and each get payoff 3 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.5中的猎鹿博弈(Stag Hunt)模型展示了合作与安全之间的冲突。两名猎人各自要在 \(S\) (猎鹿，雄鹿)和 \(H\) (猎兔)之间做出选择。每个猎人无需他人帮助就能捕获野兔，从而获得收益1。要捕获鹿，他们必须合作 — 如果另一个猎人去猎兔，那么猎鹿者的收益为零。如果两人都猎鹿，他们就能成功，且各自获得收益3。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.2\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_72_258_1187_352_357_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 3.5 The Stag Hunt game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.5 猎鹿博弈(Stag Hunt)。
\end{tcolorbox}

This symmetric game has two symmetric equilibria,(S, S)and(H, H). The first equilibrium has a much higher payoff to both, but it requires trusting that the other player will also play \(S\) , because playing \(H\) is completely safe no matter what the other player does. The Stag Hunt game is sometimes called the "Trust Dilemma" for that reason.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这个对称博弈(symmetric game)有两个对称均衡(symmetric equilibrium)，即(S, S)和(H, H)。第一个均衡为双方带来的收益要高得多，但这需要信任对方也会选择 \(S\) ，因为无论对方怎么做，选择 \(H\) 都是完全安全的。出于这个原因，猎鹿博弈有时也被称为“信任困境(Trust Dilemma)”。
\end{tcolorbox}

It would be useful if game theory gave guidance of how to select between multiple equilibria. In the Stag Hunt game, it seems very reasonable to recommend (S, S), because both players get more than when playing(H, H), and it is strictly better to play \(S\) if the other player also plays \(S\) . However, that reasoning alone is tenuous, because it also applies in the following variation of the game. Suppose each payoff 1 in Figure 3.5 is replaced by 2.9 and these payoffs are in millions of dollars, so a player may get 3 million dollars by playing \(S\) (and risk getting nothing if the other player plays \(H\) ) or safely get 2.9 million dollars by playing \(H\) . Here the risk-free choice seems clearly better.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果博弈论能为如何在多个均衡中进行选择提供指导，那将非常有用。在猎鹿博弈中，推荐选择(S, S)似乎非常合理，因为与选择(H, H)相比，双方的收益都更高，而且如果对方也选择 \(S\) ，那么选择 \(S\) 会更好。然而，仅靠这种推理是站不住脚的，因为它同样适用于该博弈的以下变体。假设图3.5中每个收益1都被替换为290万，且这些收益以百万美元为单位，那么玩家选择 \(S\) 可能获得300万美元(但如果对方选择 \(H\) ，则有一无所获的风险)，或者选择 \(H\) 安全地获得290万美元。在这里，无风险的选择显然更好。
\end{tcolorbox}

There are a number of different approaches to the problem of equilibrium selection. Typically, an equilibrium is meant to fulfill additional refinement criteria which are meant to be generally applicable (for example that there should not be another equilibrium with higher payoff to both players, although this may conflict with the inherent "riskiness" of an equilibrium, as the Stag Hunt game demonstrates). This generality is often debated, because it may require that the players use the same theoretical thought process to arrive at the supposedly "right" equilibrium. We leave this problem open, and are content with just identifying when a game has multiple equilibria.

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于均衡选择(equilibrium selection)问题，有多种不同的解决方法。通常，一个均衡需要满足额外的精炼标准，这些标准应具有普遍适用性(例如，不应存在另一个能让双方玩家获得更高收益的均衡，不过，正如猎鹿博弈所示，这可能与均衡固有的“风险性”相冲突)。这种普遍性常常引发争议，因为这可能要求玩家使用相同的理论思维过程来达成所谓“正确”的均衡。我们暂不讨论这个问题，只满足于识别一个博弈何时存在多个均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{3.5 Dominated Strategies}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{3.5 被占优策略(dominated strategy)}
\end{tcolorbox}

In the Quality game in Figure 3.2(b), strategy \(B\) gives a better payoff to the row player than strategy \(T\) , no matter what the other player does. We say \(B\) dominates \(T\) , by which we always mean \(B\) strictly dominates \(T\) . The following definition states the concepts of strict dominance, weak dominance, and payoff equivalence for a general \(N\) -player game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在图3.2(b)的产品质量博弈(Quality game)中，无论对方玩家选择什么策略，行玩家选择策略 \(B\) 的收益都比选择策略 \(T\) 更高。我们说 \(B\) 占优 \(T\) ，这里我们始终指的是 \(B\) 严格占优 \(T\) 。以下定义阐述了一般 \(N\) 人博弈中严格占优、弱占优和收益等价(payoff equivalent)的概念。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 3.2. Consider an \(N\) -player game with strategy sets \({S}_{i}\) and payoff functions \({u}_{i}\) for each player \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) , let \({s}_{i}\) and \({t}_{i}\) be two strategies of some player \(i\) , and let \({S}_{-i} = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{j = 1,j \neq  i}}^{N}{S}_{j}\) be the set of partial profiles of the other players. Then

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义3.2。考虑一个 \(N\) 人博弈，每个玩家 \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) 的策略集为 \({S}_{i}\) ，收益函数为 \({u}_{i}\) ，设 \({s}_{i}\) 和 \({t}_{i}\) 是某个玩家 \(i\) 的两个策略， \({S}_{-i} = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{j = 1,j \neq  i}}^{N}{S}_{j}\) 是其他玩家的部分策略组合集合。那么
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item \({t}_{i}\) dominates (or strictly dominates) \({s}_{i}\) if
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 如果满足以下条件，则 \({t}_{i}\) 占优(或严格占优) \({s}_{i}\)
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{u}_{i}\left( {{t}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)  > {u}_{i}\left( {{s}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right) \;\text{ for all }{s}_{-i} \in  {S}_{-i}, \tag{3.4}
\]

\begin{itemize}
\item \({t}_{i}\) is payoff equivalent to \({s}_{i}\) if
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 如果满足以下条件，则 \({t}_{i}\) 与 \({s}_{i}\) 收益等价
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{u}_{i}\left( {{t}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)  = {u}_{i}\left( {{s}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right) \;\text{ for all }{s}_{-i} \in  {S}_{-i},
\]

\begin{itemize}
\item \({t}_{i}\) weakly dominates \({s}_{i}\) if \({t}_{i}\) and \({s}_{i}\) are not payoff equivalent and
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 如果 \({t}_{i}\) 和 \({s}_{i}\) 收益不等价，且满足以下条件，则 \({t}_{i}\) 弱占优 \({s}_{i}\)
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{u}_{i}\left( {{t}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)  \geq  {u}_{i}\left( {{s}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right) \;\text{ for all }{s}_{-i} \in  {S}_{-i}. \tag{3.5}
\]

The condition (3.4) of strict dominance is particularly easy to check in a two-player game. For example, if \(i\) is the column player, then \({s}_{i}\) and \({t}_{i}\) are two columns, and \({s}_{-i}\) is an arbitrary row, and we look at the payoffs of the column player. Then (3.4) states that, row by row, each entry in column \({t}_{i}\) is greater than the respective entry in column \({s}_{i}\) . For the columns \(D\) and \(C\) in the Prisoner’s Dilemma game in Figure 3.2(a), this can be written as \(\left( \begin{array}{l} 3 \\  1 \end{array}\right)  > \left( \begin{array}{l} 2 \\  0 \end{array}\right)\) , which is true. However, for the Quality game in Figure 3.2(b), the two payoff columns for \(r\) and \(l\) are \(\left( \begin{array}{l} 1 \\  1 \end{array}\right)\) and \(\left( \begin{array}{l} 2 \\  0 \end{array}\right)\) where this does not hold, and neither \(r\) dominates \(l\) nor \(l\) dominates \(r\) . However, for both games (which have the same payoffs to the row player) the bottom row dominates the top row because \(\left( \begin{array}{ll} 3 & 1 \end{array}\right)  > \left( \begin{array}{ll} 2 & 0 \end{array}\right)\) (we compare two vectors component by component).

\begin{tcolorbox}
在两人博弈中，严格占优的条件(3.4)特别容易验证。例如，如果 \(i\) 是列参与者，那么 \({s}_{i}\) 和 \({t}_{i}\) 是两列， \({s}_{-i}\) 是任意一行，我们观察列参与者的收益。那么(3.4)表明，逐行来看，列 \({t}_{i}\) 中的每个元素都大于列 \({s}_{i}\) 中的相应元素。对于图3.2(a)中囚徒困境博弈里的列 \(D\) 和 \(C\) ，这可以写成 \(\left( \begin{array}{l} 3 \\  1 \end{array}\right)  > \left( \begin{array}{l} 2 \\  0 \end{array}\right)\) ，这是成立的。然而，对于图3.2(b)中的产品质量博弈， \(r\) 和 \(l\) 的两个收益列分别是 \(\left( \begin{array}{l} 1 \\  1 \end{array}\right)\) 和 \(\left( \begin{array}{l} 2 \\  0 \end{array}\right)\) ，这种情况不成立， \(r\) 既不占优 \(l\) ， \(l\) 也不占优 \(r\) 。不过，对于这两个博弈(行参与者的收益相同)，底行占优顶行，因为 \(\left( \begin{array}{ll} 3 & 1 \end{array}\right)  > \left( \begin{array}{ll} 2 & 0 \end{array}\right)\) (我们逐分量比较两个向量)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Note that " \({t}_{i}\) dominates \({s}_{i}\) " does not mean that \({t}_{i}\) is "always better" than \({s}_{i}\) . In the Prisoner’s Dilemma, \(D\) dominates \(C\) , but playing \(D\) can result in payoff 1 and playing \(C\) in payoff 2, just not for the same strategy of the other player.

\begin{tcolorbox}
请注意，“ \({t}_{i}\) 占优 \({s}_{i}\) ”并不意味着 \({t}_{i}\) 总是比 \({s}_{i}\) “更好”。在囚徒困境中， \(D\) 占优 \(C\) ，但选择 \(D\) 可能得到收益1，选择 \(C\) 可能得到收益2，只是这不是在另一个参与者采用相同策略的情况下。
\end{tcolorbox}

Weak dominance is essentially the same as strict dominance where the strict inequality in (3.4) is replaced by a weak inequality in (3.5). However, it should not be equality throughout (which would mean payoff equivalence). That is, \({t}_{i}\) weakly dominates \({s}_{i}\) if (3.5) holds and \({u}_{i}\left( {{t}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)  > {u}_{i}\left( {{s}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)\) for at least one partial profile \({s}_{-i} \in  {S}_{-i}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
弱占优本质上与严格占优相同，只是(3.4)中的严格不等式被(3.5)中的弱不等式所取代。然而，不能始终取等号(这意味着收益等价)。也就是说，如果(3.5)成立，并且对于至少一个部分策略组合 \({s}_{-i} \in  {S}_{-i}\) 有 \({u}_{i}\left( {{t}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)  > {u}_{i}\left( {{s}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)\) ，那么 \({t}_{i}\) 弱占优 \({s}_{i}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.2\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_74_258_1052_354_358_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 3.6 A game where column \(r\) weakly dominates column \(l\) . The weakly dominated strategy \(l\) is part of the equilibrium(T, l).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.6 一个列 \(r\) 弱占优列 \(l\) 的博弈。弱被占优策略 \(l\) 是均衡(T，l)的一部分。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 3.6 shows a game where the strategy \(T\) of player I has two best responses, \(l\) and \(r\) , because they result in the same payoff for player II (in fact for player I as well, but player I does not choose between columns). Against row \(B\) , player II’s payoff for \(l\) is worse than for \(r\) , so \(l\) is weakly dominated by \(r\) . Nevertheless, \(l\) is part of an equilibrium, namely(T, l), in fact with a higher payoff to player II than the equilibrium(B, r), so player II arguably has an advantage when "threatening" to play \(l\) . Therefore, one should not disregard weakly dominated strategies when analyzing a game, where one is typically interested in finding all its equilibria.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.6展示了一个博弈，其中参与者I的策略 \(T\) 有两个最优反应， \(l\) 和 \(r\) ，因为它们给参与者II带来相同的收益(实际上给参与者I也带来相同收益，但参与者I不选择列)。对于行 \(B\) ，参与者II选择 \(l\) 的收益比选择 \(r\) 的收益差，所以 \(l\) 被 \(r\) 弱占优。然而， \(l\) 是一个均衡，即(T，l)的一部分，实际上给参与者II带来的收益比均衡(B，r)更高，所以可以说参与者II在“威胁”选择 \(l\) 时具有优势。因此，在分析博弈时，不应该忽视弱被占优策略，因为通常人们感兴趣的是找出博弈的所有均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

In contrast, in an equilibrium a player never plays a strictly dominated strategy, because he can play instead the strategy that dominates it. The following proposition considers what happens when a weakly or strictly dominated strategy is removed from the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
相比之下，在均衡中，参与者永远不会选择严格被占优策略，因为他可以选择占优该策略的策略。下面的命题考虑了从博弈中移除弱被占优或严格被占优策略时会发生什么。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proposition 3.3. Consider an \(N\) -player game \(G\) in strategic form with strategy sets \({S}_{1},\ldots ,{S}_{N}\) . Let \({s}_{i}\) and \({t}_{i}\) be strategies of player \(i\) . Suppose \({t}_{i}\) weakly dominates or is payoff equivalent to \({s}_{i}\) . Consider the game \({G}^{\prime }\) with the same payoff functions as \(G\) but where \({S}_{i}\) is replaced by \({S}_{i} - \left\{  {s}_{i}\right\}\) . Then:

\begin{tcolorbox}
命题3.3。考虑一个具有策略集 \({S}_{1},\ldots ,{S}_{N}\) 的 \(N\) 人策略形式博弈 \(G\) 。设 \({s}_{i}\) 和 \({t}_{i}\) 是参与者 \(i\) 的策略。假设 \({t}_{i}\) 弱占优于 \({s}_{i}\) 或与 \({s}_{i}\) 收益等价。考虑与 \(G\) 具有相同收益函数，但 \({S}_{i}\) 被 \({S}_{i} - \left\{  {s}_{i}\right\}\) 替代的博弈 \({G}^{\prime }\) 。那么:
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Any equilibrium of \({G}^{\prime }\) is an equilibrium of \(G\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) \({G}^{\prime }\) 的任何均衡都是 \(G\) 的均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) If \({t}_{i}\) dominates \({s}_{i}\) , then \(G\) and \({G}^{\prime }\) have the same equilibria.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 如果 \({t}_{i}\) 占优于 \({s}_{i}\) ，那么 \(G\) 和 \({G}^{\prime }\) 具有相同的均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. To show (a), consider an equilibrium of \({G}^{\prime }\) , which is a valid strategy profile in \(G\) . If player \(i\) could profitably deviate to strategy \({s}_{i}\) in \(G\) , then, clearly, player \(i\) could also profitably deviate to \({t}_{i}\) in \({G}^{\prime }\) , which violates the equilibrium property. The equilibrium property holds for the players other than player \(i\) because they have the same strategies and payoffs in \(G\) and \({G}^{\prime }\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。为了证明 (a)，考虑 \({G}^{\prime }\) 的一个均衡，它是 \(G\) 中的一个有效策略组合。如果参与者 \(i\) 在 \(G\) 中可以通过偏离到策略 \({s}_{i}\) 获得收益，那么显然，参与者 \(i\) 在 \({G}^{\prime }\) 中也可以通过偏离到 \({t}_{i}\) 获得收益，这违反了均衡性质。除参与者 \(i\) 之外的其他参与者的均衡性质成立，因为他们在 \(G\) 和 \({G}^{\prime }\) 中有相同的策略和收益。
\end{tcolorbox}

To show (b), suppose \({t}_{i}\) dominates \({s}_{i}\) . Then the dominated strategy \({s}_{i}\) is never part of an equilibrium \(s = \left( {{s}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)\) because of player \(i\) ’s profitable deviation to \({t}_{i}\) according to (3.4). Any strategy profile in \(G\) that does not contain \({s}_{i}\) is also a strategy profile of \({G}^{\prime }\) . If that profile is an equilibrium of \(G\) , then it is also an equilibrium of \({G}^{\prime }\) , which has fewer deviation possibilities. Any equilibrium in \({G}^{\prime }\) is an equilibrium in \(G\) by (a). Hence, \(G\) and \({G}^{\prime }\) have the same equilibria, as claimed.

\begin{tcolorbox}
为了证明 (b)，假设 \({t}_{i}\) 占优于 \({s}_{i}\) 。根据 (3.4)，由于参与者 \(i\) 可以通过偏离到 \({t}_{i}\) 获得收益，被占优策略 \({s}_{i}\) 永远不会是均衡 \(s = \left( {{s}_{i},{s}_{-i}}\right)\) 的一部分。 \(G\) 中任何不包含 \({s}_{i}\) 的策略组合也是 \({G}^{\prime }\) 的一个策略组合。如果该组合是 \(G\) 的一个均衡，那么它也是 \({G}^{\prime }\) 的一个均衡，因为 \({G}^{\prime }\) 的偏离可能性更少。根据 (a)， \({G}^{\prime }\) 中的任何均衡也是 \(G\) 中的均衡。因此，如所声称的， \(G\) 和 \({G}^{\prime }\) 具有相同的均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

As explained before with Figure 3.6, eliminating a weakly dominated strategy may result in losing equilibria of a game. Proposition 3.3(a) shows that this does not introduce new equilibria. Hence, deleting a weakly dominated strategy may be considered acceptable when trying to find only one equilibrium of a game. However, this normally distorts the analysis of the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如之前结合图3.6所解释的，剔除一个弱被占优策略可能会导致博弈的均衡丢失。命题3.3(a)表明这不会引入新的均衡。因此，当试图仅找到博弈的一个均衡时，剔除一个弱被占优策略可以被认为是可接受的。然而，这通常会扭曲对博弈的分析。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proposition 3.3(b) shows that removing a (strictly) dominated strategy from a game does not change its equilibria. Because a payoff-maximizing player should not play a dominated strategy, it does make sense to eliminate a dominated strategy from the game by removing it from the player's strategy set. This changes the game, and the smaller game may have new dominated strategies that were not dominated before. Removing such a dominated strategy again, and continuing this process until all remaining strategies are undominated, is called iterated elimination of dominated strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
命题3.3(b)表明，从博弈中剔除一个(严格)被占优策略不会改变其均衡。因为一个追求收益最大化的参与者不应该采用被占优策略，所以从参与者的策略集中剔除被占优策略从而将其从博弈中消除是有意义的。这会改变博弈，并且更小的博弈可能会有之前未被占优的新的被占优策略。再次剔除这样的被占优策略，并持续这个过程直到所有剩余策略都未被占优，这被称为重复剔除劣势策略。
\end{tcolorbox}

In contrast, eliminating weakly dominated strategies is problematic because it may lose equilibria, and introduces further issues when iterated. As shown in Exercise 3.1, the order in which weakly dominated strategies are eliminated generally matters. The process may get stuck because weak domination turns into payoff equivalence, unless one also removes a payoff equivalent strategy, with a rather arbitrary decision as to which strategy should be eliminated. In addition, even if the elimination of weakly dominated strategies results in a single strategy profile, that profile may depend on the elimination order.

\begin{tcolorbox}
相比之下，剔除弱被占优策略是有问题的，因为它可能会丢失均衡，并且在重复操作时会引入更多问题。如练习3.1所示，剔除弱被占优策略的顺序通常是重要的。这个过程可能会陷入停滞，因为弱占优会变成收益等价，除非同时剔除一个收益等价的策略，而关于应该剔除哪个策略的决策相当随意。此外，即使剔除弱被占优策略得到了一个单一的策略组合，该组合也可能依赖于剔除顺序。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 3.7 demonstrates iterated elimination of dominated strategies for the Quality game. In the first step, the top row \(T\) is dominated by \(B\) and removed to create a \(1 \times  2\) game. In that game, column \(r\) dominates \(l\) (which is not true in the original \(2 \times  2\) game). After eliminating \(l\) , a single cell remains that corresponds to the strategy profile(B, r)which is also the unique equilibrium of the original game. The following proposition shows that this holds generally.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.7展示了产品质量博弈的重复剔除劣势策略过程。第一步，顶行 \(T\) 被 \(B\) 占优并被剔除，形成一个 \(1 \times  2\) 博弈。在那个博弈中，列 \(r\) 占优于 \(l\) (在原始的 \(2 \times  2\) 博弈中并非如此)。剔除 \(l\) 后，剩下一个单元格，对应策略组合 (B, r)，它也是原始博弈的唯一均衡。以下命题表明这通常是成立的。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_76_214_233_1224_365_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 3.7 Iterated elimination of dominated strategies in the Quality game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.7 产品质量博弈中的重复剔除劣势策略。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proposition 3.4. Consider a game G that by a finite iterated elimination of dominated strategies is reduced to a single strategy profile \(s\) (that is, every player has only one strategy). Then \(s\) is the unique equilibrium of \(G\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
命题3.4。考虑一个博弈G，通过有限次重复剔除劣势策略将其简化为单一的策略组合 \(s\) (即每个参与者只有一种策略)。那么 \(s\) 是 \(G\) 的唯一均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. As assumed, eliminate a dominated strategy from \(G\) , and continue this process in finitely many steps to arrive at a single strategy for each player. The final game only has one strategy profile \(s\) , which is trivially an equilibrium because no player can deviate. By Proposition 3.3, the previous game has the same unique equilibrium \(s\) , and by going backwards in the produced sequence of games, this eventually holds for the original game \(G\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。根据假设，从 \(G\) 中剔除一个(被占优)策略，并在有限步骤内继续此过程，为每个参与者得到单一策略。最终的博弈只有一个策略组合 \(s\) ，这显然是一个均衡，因为没有参与者可以偏离。根据命题3.3，前一个博弈有相同的唯一均衡 \(s\) ，通过在生成的博弈序列中逆向推导，这最终对原始博弈 \(G\) 也成立。
\end{tcolorbox}

If finite iterated elimination of dominated strategies ends in a unique strategy profile, the game is said to be dominance solvable, with the final strategy profile as its solution, which is also the unique equilibrium of the game. The following lemma states the reassuring fact that the order in which dominated strategies are eliminated does not matter, and that several dominated strategies can be removed simultaneously.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果有限次重复剔除劣势策略以唯一的策略组合结束，则称该博弈是占优可解的，最终的策略组合即为其解，这也是该博弈的唯一均衡。以下引理表明了一个令人放心的事实，即剔除(被占优)策略的顺序无关紧要，并且可以同时剔除多个(被占优)策略。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 3.5. Consider an \(N\) -player game \(G\) in strategic form with strategy sets \({S}_{1},\ldots\) , \({S}_{N}\) , let \({s}_{i}\) and \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) be two dominated strategies of player \(i\) and player \(j\) , respectively, possibly the same player \(\left( {i = j}\right)\) . Let \({G}^{\prime }\) be the (otherwise unchanged) game obtained from \(G\) by removing \({s}_{i}\) from \({S}_{i}\) , and let \(\widehat{G}\) be the game obtained from \(G\) by removing \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) from \({S}_{j}\) . Then \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) is dominated in \({G}^{\prime }\) , and \({s}_{i}\) is dominated in \(\widehat{G}\) , and removing \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) from \({G}^{\prime }\) and removing \({s}_{i}\) from \(\widehat{G}\) results in the same game obtained by removing directly \({s}_{i}\) and \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) from \(G\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理3.5。考虑一个具有策略集 \({S}_{1},\ldots\) 、 \({S}_{N}\) 的 \(N\) 个参与者的策略形式博弈 \(G\) ，设 \({s}_{i}\) 和 \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) 分别是参与者 \(i\) 和参与者 \(j\) 的两个(被占优)策略，可能是同一个参与者 \(\left( {i = j}\right)\) 。设 \({G}^{\prime }\) 是从 \(G\) 中通过从 \({S}_{i}\) 移除 \({s}_{i}\) 得到的(其他方面不变)博弈，设 \(\widehat{G}\) 是从 \(G\) 中通过从 \({S}_{j}\) 移除 \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) 得到的博弈。那么 \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) 在 \({G}^{\prime }\) 中是(被占优)策略， \({s}_{i}\) 在 \(\widehat{G}\) 中是(被占优)策略，并且从 \({G}^{\prime }\) 移除 \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) 和从 \(\widehat{G}\) 移除 \({s}_{i}\) 得到的博弈与直接从 \(G\) 移除 \({s}_{i}\) 和 \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) 得到的博弈相同。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Suppose \({s}_{i}\) is dominated by \({t}_{i}\) and \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) is dominated by \({\widehat{t}}_{j}\) . If \({\widehat{t}}_{j} \neq  {s}_{i}\) , then \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) is also dominated in \({G}^{\prime }\) by \({\widehat{t}}_{j}\) because any partial profile \({s}_{-j}\) in \({G}^{\prime }\) is also a partial profile in \(G\) where the dominating inequality holds. If \({\widehat{t}}_{j} = {s}_{i}\) (which requires \(i = j\) ), then \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) is also dominated in \({G}^{\prime }\) (by \({t}_{i}\) , because domination is clearly transitive), as claimed. Similarly, if \({t}_{i} \neq  {\widehat{s}}_{j}\) , then \({s}_{i}\) is dominated in \(\widehat{G}\) by \({t}_{i}\) , and if \({t}_{i} = {\widehat{s}}_{j}\) , then \({s}_{i}\) is dominated in \(\widehat{G}\) by \({\widehat{t}}_{j}\) . Removing the dominated strategies clearly results in the same game with both \({s}_{i}\) and \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) removed directly from \(G\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。假设 \({s}_{i}\) 被 \({t}_{i}\) 占优， \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) 被 \({\widehat{t}}_{j}\) 占优。如果 \({\widehat{t}}_{j} \neq  {s}_{i}\) ，那么 \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) 在 \({G}^{\prime }\) 中也被 \({\widehat{t}}_{j}\) 占优，因为 \({G}^{\prime }\) 中的任何部分策略组合 \({s}_{-j}\) 也是 \(G\) 中的部分策略组合，在其中占优不等式成立。如果 \({\widehat{t}}_{j} = {s}_{i}\) (这需要 \(i = j\) )，那么 \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) 在 \({G}^{\prime }\) 中也被 \({t}_{i}\) 占优(因为占优显然是传递的)，如所声称的那样。类似地，如果 \({t}_{i} \neq  {\widehat{s}}_{j}\) ，那么 \({s}_{i}\) 在 \(\widehat{G}\) 中被 \({t}_{i}\) 占优，如果 \({t}_{i} = {\widehat{s}}_{j}\) ，那么 \({s}_{i}\) 在 \(\widehat{G}\) 中被 \({\widehat{t}}_{j}\) 占优。显然，剔除(被占优)策略会得到与直接从 \(G\) 剔除 \({s}_{i}\) 和 \({\widehat{s}}_{j}\) 相同的博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

Iterated elimination of strictly dominated strategies has two uses. First, it may simplify the game, ideally until only one strategy profile remains, which is the unique equilibrium of the game (which of course only works if the game has only one equilibrium). Second, playing an equilibrium strategy assumes that the other players also play their equilibrium strategies. However, a player will always prefer a dominating strategy over the dominated strategy irrespective of the strategies of the other players. In that sense, choosing a dominating strategy is more "robust" in terms of assumptions about what the other players do. This applies certainly to a strategy that is dominating in the original game; iterated elimination of dominated strategies does require the "rationality" assumption that the other players maximize their payoff (in Figure 3.7, player II chooses \(r\) under the assumption that player I will not play \(T\) ).

\begin{tcolorbox}
重复剔除严格被占优策略有两个用途。首先，它可以简化博弈，理想情况下，直到只剩下一个策略组合，这就是该博弈的唯一均衡(当然，这只有在博弈只有一个均衡时才有效)。其次，选择均衡策略假设其他参与者也会选择他们的均衡策略。然而，无论其他参与者选择什么策略，参与者总是会更喜欢占优策略而不是(被占优)策略。从这个意义上说，选择占优策略在对其他参与者行为的假设方面更“稳健”。这当然适用于在原博弈中占优的策略；重复剔除劣势策略确实需要“理性”假设，即其他参与者会最大化他们的收益(在图3.7中，参与者II假设参与者I不会选择 \(r\) 而选择 \(T\) )。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{3.6 The Cournot Duopoly of Quantity Competition}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{3.6 产量竞争的古诺双寡头博弈}
\end{tcolorbox}

In this section, we discuss an economic model of competition between two competing firms who choose the quantity of their product, where the price decreases with the total quantity on the market. It is also called a Cournot duopoly named after Augustin Cournot (1838) who proposed this model, nowadays described as a game with an equilibrium. In this game, the strategy sets of the players are infinite. Finite versions of the game are dominance solvable, and in principle also the infinite version, as we describe at the end of this section.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在本节中，我们讨论一个经济模型，该模型涉及两家相互竞争的企业之间的竞争，它们各自选择产品的产量，而市场价格会随着市场上的总产量的增加而下降。这个模型也被称为古诺双寡头博弈(Cournot duopoly)，以奥古斯丁·古诺(Augustin Cournot，1838 年)的名字命名，他提出了这个模型，如今该模型被描述为具有均衡的博弈。在这个博弈中，参与者的策略集是无限的。该博弈的有限版本是占优可解的，原则上无限版本也是如此，我们将在本节末尾进行描述。
\end{tcolorbox}

The players I and II are two firms who choose a nonnegative quantity of producing some good up to some upper bound \(M\) , say, so \({S}_{1} = {S}_{2} = \left\lbrack  {0,M}\right\rbrack\) . Let \(x\) and \(y\) be the strategies chosen by player I and II, respectively. For simplicity there are no costs of production, and the total quantity \(x + y\) is sold at a price \({12} - \left( {x + y}\right)\) per unit, which is also the firm’s profit per unit, so the payoffs \(a\left( {x,y}\right)\) and \(b\left( {x,y}\right)\) to players I and II are given by

\begin{tcolorbox}
参与者 I 和 II 是两家企业，它们选择生产某种商品的非负产量，最高可达某个上限 \(M\) ，比如说，因此 \({S}_{1} = {S}_{2} = \left\lbrack  {0,M}\right\rbrack\) 。设 \(x\) 和 \(y\) 分别是参与者 I 和 II 选择的策略。为简单起见，假设没有生产成本，总产量 \(x + y\) 以每单位 \({12} - \left( {x + y}\right)\) 的价格出售，这也是企业每单位的利润，因此参与者 I 和 II 的收益 \(a\left( {x,y}\right)\) 和 \(b\left( {x,y}\right)\) 由下式给出
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\text{ payoff to }\mathrm{I} : \;a\left( {x,y}\right)  = x \cdot  \left( {{12} - y - x}\right) \text{ , } \tag{3.6}
\]

\[
\text{ payoff to II : }\;b\left( {x,y}\right)  = y \cdot  \left( {{12} - x - y}\right) \text{ . }
\]

\(\Rightarrow\) Exercise 3.3 describes an extension that incorporates production costs.

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\Rightarrow\) 练习3.3描述了一个包含生产成本的扩展模型。
\end{tcolorbox}

The game (3.6) is clearly symmetric in \(x\) and \(y\) . Figure 3.8 shows a finite version of this game where the players' strategies are restricted to the four quantities 0 ,

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈(3.6)在 \(x\) 和 \(y\) 上显然是对称的。图3.8展示了这个博弈的一个有限版本，其中参与者的策略被限制在四个产量水平上:0 ，
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_78_196_198_617_621_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 3.8 Symmetric duopoly game between two firms who both have four strategies 0,3 , 4, 6 with payoffs (3.6) if player I chooses \(x\) and player II chooses \(y\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.8 两家企业之间的对称双寡头博弈，两家企业都有四个策略:0、3、4、6，收益由(3.6)式确定。如果参与者 I 选择 \(x\) ，参与者 II 选择 \(y\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

3,4, or 6 . The payoffs are determined by (3.6). For example, for \(x = 3\) and \(y = 4\) player I gets payoff 15 and player II gets 20. Best-response payoffs, as determined by this payoff table, are marked by boxes. The game has the unique equilibrium (4,4)where each player gets payoff 16 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
3、4 或 6。收益由(3.6)式确定。例如，当 \(x = 3\) 和 \(y = 4\) 时，参与者 I 的收益为15，参与者 II 的收益为20。根据这个收益表确定的最优反应收益用方框标记。该博弈有唯一的均衡(4,4)，此时每个参与者的收益为16。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_78_207_1111_1213_491_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 3.9 The game in Figure 3.8 after eliminating for both players the dominated strategy 0, then strategy 6 (dominated by 4), then strategy 3 (dominated by 4), which shows that the game is dominance solvable.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.9 图3.8中的博弈，在为两个参与者剔除(被占优)策略 0，然后剔除策略 6(被 4 占优)，接着剔除策略 3(被 4 占优)之后，这表明该博弈是占优可解的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 3.9 shows that the game can be solved by iterated elimination of dominated strategies. In the \(4 \times  4\) game, strategy 0 is dominated by 3 or 4, and after eliminating it directly for both players one obtains the \(3 \times  3\) game shown on the left in Figure 3.9. In that game, strategy 6 is dominated by 4, and after eliminating this strategy for both players the middle \(2 \times  2\) game is reached. This game has the structure of a Prisoner's Dilemma game because 4 dominates 3 (so that 3 will be eliminated), but the final strategy pair of undominated strategies (4,4) gives both players a lower payoff than the strategy pair(3,3). Here the Prisoner’s Dilemma arises in an economic context: The two firms could cooperate by equally splitting the optimal "monopoly" quantity 6, but in response the other player would "defect" and choose 4 rather than 3 . If both players do this, the total quantity 8 reduces the price to 4 and both players have an overall lower payoff.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.9表明，该博弈可以通过重复剔除劣势策略来求解。在 \(4 \times  4\) 博弈中，策略 0 被 3 或 4 占优，在为两个参与者直接剔除该策略后，得到图3.9左侧所示的 \(3 \times  3\) 博弈。在那个博弈中，策略 6 被 4 占优，在为两个参与者剔除该策略后，得到中间的 \(2 \times  2\) 博弈。这个博弈具有囚徒困境博弈的结构，因为 4 占优 3(因此 3 将被剔除)，但最终未被占优的策略对(4,4)给两个参与者带来的收益低于策略对(3,3)。这里的囚徒困境出现在经济背景中:两家企业可以通过平均分配最优的“垄断”产量 6 来进行合作，但作为回应，另一个参与者会“背叛”并选择 4 而不是 3。如果两个参与者都这样做，总产量 8 会使价格降至 4，两个参与者的总体收益都会降低。
\end{tcolorbox}

The game can also be analyzed when each player has the real interval \(\left\lbrack  {0,M}\right\rbrack\) as a strategy set. We can assume that \(M = {12}\) because if a player chooses a higher quantity than 12 then the price \({12} - x - y\) would be negative and the player would be better off by producing nothing. Player I’s best response to \(y\) is the quantity \(x\) that maximizes \(a\left( {x,y}\right)\) , where

\begin{tcolorbox}
当每个玩家将实数区间 \(\left\lbrack  {0,M}\right\rbrack\) 作为策略集时，也可以对该博弈进行分析。我们可以假设 \(M = {12}\) ，因为如果一个玩家选择的产量高于 12，那么价格 \({12} - x - y\) 将为负，此时该玩家不生产会更好。玩家 I 对 \(y\) 的最优反应是使 \(a\left( {x,y}\right)\) 最大化的产量 \(x\) ，其中
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
a\left( {x,y}\right)  = x \cdot  \left( {{12} - y - x}\right)  =  - {\left( x - \frac{{12} - y}{2}\right) }^{2} + {\left( \frac{{12} - y}{2}\right) }^{2} \tag{3.7}
\]

which clearly has its maximum at \(x = \frac{{12} - y}{2} = 6 - y/2\) , where \(x \geq  0\) because \(y \leq  {12}\) . (When just looking at \(x \cdot  \left( {{12} - y - x}\right)\) , this is a parabola with its apex at the midpoint between its zeros at \(x = 0\) and \(x = {12} - y\) . The maximum is also found by solving \(\left. {\frac{d}{dx}a\left( {x,y}\right)  = 0\text{ . }}\right)\) Note that the best response to \(y\) is unique. Because the game is symmetric, player II’s best response \(y\) to \(x\) is given by \(y = 6 - x/2\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
显然，它在 \(x = \frac{{12} - y}{2} = 6 - y/2\) 处取得最大值，其中 \(x \geq  0\) ，因为 \(y \leq  {12}\) 。(仅考虑 \(x \cdot  \left( {{12} - y - x}\right)\) 时，这是一条抛物线，其顶点位于零点 \(x = 0\) 和 \(x = {12} - y\) 的中点。也可以通过求解 \(\left. {\frac{d}{dx}a\left( {x,y}\right)  = 0\text{ . }}\right)\) 来找到最大值。注意，对 \(y\) 的最优反应是唯一的。由于该博弈是对称的，玩家 II 对 \(x\) 的最优反应 \(y\) 由 \(y = 6 - x/2\) 给出。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_79_344_1070_662_668_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 3.10 Best-response functions \(x = 6 - y/2\) and \(y = 6 - x/2\) of players \(\mathrm{I}\) and II for \(x,y \in  \left\lbrack  {0,{12}}\right\rbrack\) in the duopoly game (3.6).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 3.10 在双头垄断博弈 (3.6) 中，玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 和玩家 II 对于 \(x,y \in  \left\lbrack  {0,{12}}\right\rbrack\) 的最优反应函数 \(x = 6 - y/2\) 和 \(y = 6 - x/2\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

In order to obtain an equilibrium(x, y), the strategies \(x\) and \(y\) have to be mutual best responses, that is, \(x = 6 - y/2\) and \(y = 6 - x/2\) . This pair of linear equations has the unique solution \(x = y = 4\) , which is the unique equilibrium of the game, shown as the intersection point(4,4)of the best-response lines in Figure 3.10. It coincides with the equilibrium of the \(4 \times  4\) game in Figure 3.8 where 4 was one of the considered four strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
为了得到一个均衡 (x, y)，策略 \(x\) 和 \(y\) 必须是相互的最优反应，即 \(x = 6 - y/2\) 且 \(y = 6 - x/2\) 。这对线性方程有唯一解 \(x = y = 4\) ，它是该博弈的唯一均衡，如图 3.10 中最优反应线的交点 (4,4) 所示。它与图 3.8 中 \(4 \times  4\) 博弈的均衡一致，在图 3.8 中，4 是所考虑的四个策略之一。
\end{tcolorbox}

In the remainder of this section, we explain how even the game with infinite strategy sets \(\left\lbrack  {0,M}\right\rbrack\) is (to an arbitarily close approximation) dominance solvable, by iteratively eliminating whole intervals of dominated strategies from the strategy sets. The first step already happened when we chose \(M = {12}\) : In principle, player \(\mathrm{I}\) could choose \({x}^{\prime } > {12}\) , but then \(a\left( {{x}^{\prime },y}\right)  = {x}^{\prime } \cdot  \left( {{12} - y - {x}^{\prime }}\right)  < 0\) because \(y \geq  0\) , which is worse than \(a\left( {0,y}\right)\) for any \(y\) . That is, if \({x}^{\prime } > {12}\) then \({x}^{\prime }\) is strictly dominated by \(x = 0\) . The same applies to the other player, so we can assume that both strategy sets are equal to the interval \(\left\lbrack  {0,{12}}\right\rbrack\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
在本节的剩余部分，我们将解释如何通过从策略集中迭代消除整个区间的(被占优)策略，使得即使是具有无限策略集 \(\left\lbrack  {0,M}\right\rbrack\) 的博弈也是(任意接近地近似)占优可解的。当我们选择 \(M = {12}\) 时，第一步就已经完成了:原则上，玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 可以选择 \({x}^{\prime } > {12}\) ，但此时 \(a\left( {{x}^{\prime },y}\right)  = {x}^{\prime } \cdot  \left( {{12} - y - {x}^{\prime }}\right)  < 0\) ，因为 \(y \geq  0\) ，对于任何 \(y\) 来说，这都比 \(a\left( {0,y}\right)\) 更差。也就是说，如果 \({x}^{\prime } > {12}\) ，那么 \({x}^{\prime }\) 被 \(x = 0\) 严格占优。同样的情况也适用于另一个玩家，所以我们可以假设两个策略集都等于区间 \(\left\lbrack  {0,{12}}\right\rbrack\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The following proposition states that when player II is assumed to play \(y\) from an interval \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) , then there is another interval \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack   = \left\lbrack  {6 - \frac{B}{2},6 - \frac{A}{2}}\right\rbrack\) that contains all possible best responses of player I (condition (a)), and so that every strategy \({x}^{\prime }\) of player I outside the interval \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) is dominated by either \({B}^{\prime }\) or \({A}^{\prime }\) (conditions (b) and (c)). After eliminating these dominated strategies, we can assume that player I only plays strategies from \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) . If both players are initially considering strategies from the same interval \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) , then condition (d) shows when this leads to a sequence of successively narrower intervals, which we will discuss afterwards.

\begin{tcolorbox}
以下命题表明，当假设玩家 II 从区间 \(y\) 中选择策略 \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) 时，存在另一个区间 \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack   = \left\lbrack  {6 - \frac{B}{2},6 - \frac{A}{2}}\right\rbrack\) 包含玩家 I 的所有可能的最优反应(条件 (a))，并且区间 \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) 之外玩家 I 的每个策略 \({x}^{\prime }\) 都被 \({B}^{\prime }\) 或 \({A}^{\prime }\) 占优(条件 (b) 和 (c))。在消除这些(被占优)策略后，我们可以假设玩家 I 仅从 \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) 中选择策略。如果两个玩家最初都在考虑来自同一区间 \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) 的策略，那么条件 (d) 表明何时会导致一系列逐渐变窄的区间，我们将在后面讨论。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proposition 3.6. Consider the game with payoffs (3.6) where \(y \in  \left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) with \(0 \leq  A \leq\)  \(B \leq  {12}\) . Let \({A}^{\prime } = 6 - \frac{B}{2}\) and \({B}^{\prime } = 6 - \frac{A}{2}\) . Then

\begin{tcolorbox}
命题 3.6. 考虑具有收益 (3.6) 的博弈，其中 \(y \in  \left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) 满足 \(0 \leq  A \leq\)  \(B \leq  {12}\) 。设 \({A}^{\prime } = 6 - \frac{B}{2}\) 且 \({B}^{\prime } = 6 - \frac{A}{2}\) 。那么
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Each \(x\) in \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) is player \({I}^{\prime }\) s unique best response to some \(y\) in \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 区间 \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) 中的每个 \(x\) 都是玩家 \({I}^{\prime }\) 对区间 \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) 中某个 \(y\) 的唯一最优反应。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) If \({x}^{\prime } > {B}^{\prime }\) then strategy \({x}^{\prime }\) of player \(I\) is strictly dominated by \(x = {B}^{\prime }\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 如果 \({x}^{\prime } > {B}^{\prime }\) ，那么玩家 \(I\) 的策略 \({x}^{\prime }\) 被 \(x = {B}^{\prime }\) 严格占优。
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) If \({x}^{\prime } < {A}^{\prime }\) then strategy \({x}^{\prime }\) of player \(I\) is strictly dominated by \(x = {A}^{\prime }\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) 如果 \({x}^{\prime } < {A}^{\prime }\) ，那么玩家 \(I\) 的策略 \({x}^{\prime }\) 被 \(x = {A}^{\prime }\) 严格占优。
\end{tcolorbox}

(d) The interval \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) has half the length of the interval \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) , and \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack   \subseteq  \left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) if and only if

\begin{tcolorbox}
(d) 区间 \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) 的长度是区间 \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) 长度的一半，并且当且仅当 \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack   \subseteq  \left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\)
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
4 \in  \left\lbrack  {A + \frac{1}{3}\left( {B - A}\right) ,A + \frac{2}{3}\left( {B - A}\right) }\right\rbrack  , \tag{3.8}
\]

and if (3.8) holds then it also holds with \({A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }\) instead of \(A,B\) . \(y = A\) and \(y = B\) (solid curves) and an intermediate case (dashed curve). Player I’s best response to \(y\) is \(x = \frac{{12} - y}{2}\) and therefore between \(\frac{{12} - B}{2}\) and \(\frac{{12} - A}{2}\) , that is, in the interval \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) , shown by the long solid vertical lines in Figure 3.11. The mapping \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack   \rightarrow  \left\lbrack  {6 - \frac{B}{2},6 - \frac{A}{2}}\right\rbrack  ,y \mapsto  6 - \frac{y}{2}\) is a bijection, which shows (a).

\begin{tcolorbox}
并且如果 (3.8) 成立，那么用 \({A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }\) 代替 \(A,B\) 时它也成立。 \(y = A\) 和 \(y = B\) (实线曲线)以及一个中间情况(虚线曲线)。玩家 I 对 \(y\) 的最优反应是 \(x = \frac{{12} - y}{2}\) ，因此在 \(\frac{{12} - B}{2}\) 和 \(\frac{{12} - A}{2}\) 之间，即在区间 \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) 内，如图 3.11 中的长实线垂直线所示。映射 \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack   \rightarrow  \left\lbrack  {6 - \frac{B}{2},6 - \frac{A}{2}}\right\rbrack  ,y \mapsto  6 - \frac{y}{2}\) 是一个双射，这表明了 (a)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.6\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_80_203_1479_791_555_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 3.11 The dashed curve shows the payoff \(x \cdot  \left( {{12} - y - x}\right)\) to player I in Proposition 3.6 (negative payoffs are not shown). Its maximum is at \(\frac{{12} - y}{2}\) . Here \({A}^{\prime } = \frac{{12} - B}{2}\) and \({B}^{\prime } = \frac{{12} - A}{2}\)

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.11 虚线曲线展示了命题3.6中参与者I的收益 \(x \cdot  \left( {{12} - y - x}\right)\) (负收益未显示)。其最大值位于 \(\frac{{12} - y}{2}\) 处。这里 \({A}^{\prime } = \frac{{12} - B}{2}\) 和 \({B}^{\prime } = \frac{{12} - A}{2}\)
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Assume that player II plays some \(y\) in \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) . According to (3.7), the payoff to player I as a function of \(x\) is a parabola. Figure 3.11 shows the two extreme cases

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。假设参与者II在 \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) 中选择某个 \(y\) 。根据式(3.7)，参与者I的收益作为 \(x\) 的函数是一条抛物线。图3.11展示了两种极端情况
\end{tcolorbox}

The case \(y = A\) is best possible for player I, which is the largest parabola in Figure 3.11. The figure shows that if \({x}^{\prime } > {B}^{\prime } = \frac{{12} - A}{2}\) then player \(\mathrm{I}\) is certainly better off playing \(x = {B}^{\prime }\) because for each \(y \geq  A\) the dashed parabola slopes downwards (possibly even with negative values) from \({B}^{\prime }\) to \({x}^{\prime }\) . This intuition for (b) is formally shown as follows. Let \({x}^{\prime } > {B}^{\prime }\) . We want to show that \(a\left( {{B}^{\prime },y}\right)  > a\left( {{x}^{\prime },y}\right)\) , which by (3.7) is equivalent to

\begin{tcolorbox}
情况 \(y = A\) 对参与者I来说是最有利的，这对应图3.11中最大的抛物线。该图表明，如果 \({x}^{\prime } > {B}^{\prime } = \frac{{12} - A}{2}\) ，那么参与者 \(\mathrm{I}\) 选择 \(x = {B}^{\prime }\) 肯定更有利，因为对于每个 \(y \geq  A\) ，虚线抛物线从 \({B}^{\prime }\) 到 \({x}^{\prime }\) 是向下倾斜的(甚至可能取负值)。对于(b)的这种直观理解正式证明如下。设 \({x}^{\prime } > {B}^{\prime }\) 。我们要证明 \(a\left( {{B}^{\prime },y}\right)  > a\left( {{x}^{\prime },y}\right)\) ，根据式(3.7)，这等价于
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
- {\left( {B}^{\prime } - \frac{{12} - y}{2}\right) }^{2} >  - {\left( {x}^{\prime } - \frac{{12} - y}{2}\right) }^{2}\text{ . } \tag{3.9}
\]

With

\begin{tcolorbox}
有了
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{x}^{\prime } = {B}^{\prime } + c\;\left( {c > 0}\right) ,\;y = A + d\;\left( {d \geq  0}\right)
\]

and therefore \(\frac{{12} - y}{2} = 6 - \frac{A + d}{2} = {B}^{\prime } - \frac{d}{2}\) the inequality (3.9) is equivalent to

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此 \(\frac{{12} - y}{2} = 6 - \frac{A + d}{2} = {B}^{\prime } - \frac{d}{2}\) ，不等式(3.9)等价于
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{\left( {B}^{\prime } - \left( {B}^{\prime } - \frac{d}{2}\right) \right) }^{2} < {\left( {B}^{\prime } + c - \left( {B}^{\prime } - \frac{d}{2}\right) \right) }^{2},
\]

that is, to \({\left( d/2\right) }^{2} < {\left( c + d/2\right) }^{2}\) which is true. This shows (b).

\begin{tcolorbox}
即 \({\left( d/2\right) }^{2} < {\left( c + d/2\right) }^{2}\) ，这是成立的。这证明了(b)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Similarly, the case \(y = B\) is worst possible for player \(\mathrm{I}\) , which is the smallest parabola in Figure 3.11. Then if \({x}^{\prime } < {A}^{\prime } = \frac{{12} - B}{2}\) then player I is better off playing \(x = {A}^{\prime }\) because for each \(y \leq  B\) the parabola slopes upwards from \({x}^{\prime }\) to \({A}^{\prime }\) . Formally, let \({x}^{\prime } < {A}^{\prime }\) . We want to show that \(a\left( {{A}^{\prime },y}\right)  > a\left( {{x}^{\prime },y}\right)\) , that is,

\begin{tcolorbox}
类似地，情况 \(y = B\) 对参与者 \(\mathrm{I}\) 来说是最不利的，这对应图3.11中最小的抛物线。那么如果 \({x}^{\prime } < {A}^{\prime } = \frac{{12} - B}{2}\) ，参与者I选择 \(x = {A}^{\prime }\) 更有利，因为对于每个 \(y \leq  B\) ，抛物线从 \({x}^{\prime }\) 到 \({A}^{\prime }\) 是向上倾斜的。正式地，设 \({x}^{\prime } < {A}^{\prime }\) 。我们要证明 \(a\left( {{A}^{\prime },y}\right)  > a\left( {{x}^{\prime },y}\right)\) ，即
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
- {\left( {A}^{\prime } - \frac{{12} - y}{2}\right) }^{2} >  - {\left( {x}^{\prime } - \frac{{12} - y}{2}\right) }^{2}\text{ . } \tag{3.10}
\]

Now we set

\begin{tcolorbox}
现在我们设
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{x}^{\prime } = {A}^{\prime } - c\;\left( {c > 0}\right) ,\;y = B - d\;\left( {d \geq  0}\right)
\]

and therefore \(\frac{{12} - y}{2} = 6 - \frac{B - d}{2} = {A}^{\prime } + \frac{d}{2}\) . Then (3.10) is equivalent to

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此 \(\frac{{12} - y}{2} = 6 - \frac{B - d}{2} = {A}^{\prime } + \frac{d}{2}\) 。那么式(3.10)等价于
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{\left( {A}^{\prime } - \left( {A}^{\prime } + \frac{d}{2}\right) \right) }^{2} < {\left( {A}^{\prime } - c - \left( {A}^{\prime } + \frac{d}{2}\right) \right) }^{2},
\]

that is, to \({\left( -d/2\right) }^{2} < {\left( -c - d/2\right) }^{2}\) or again \({\left( d/2\right) }^{2} < {\left( c + d/2\right) }^{2}\) which is true. This shows (c).

\begin{tcolorbox}
即 \({\left( -d/2\right) }^{2} < {\left( -c - d/2\right) }^{2}\) 或者再次得到 \({\left( d/2\right) }^{2} < {\left( c + d/2\right) }^{2}\) ，这是成立的。这证明了(c)。
\end{tcolorbox}

To show (d), clearly \({B}^{\prime } - {A}^{\prime } = \left( {B - A}\right) /2\) . The condition \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack   \subseteq  \left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) is equivalent to \(A \leq  {A}^{\prime } = 6 - \frac{B}{2}\) and \({B}^{\prime } = 6 - \frac{A}{2} \leq  B\) , which has the following equivalent statements:

\begin{tcolorbox}
为了证明(d)，显然 \({B}^{\prime } - {A}^{\prime } = \left( {B - A}\right) /2\) 。条件 \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack   \subseteq  \left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) 等价于 \(A \leq  {A}^{\prime } = 6 - \frac{B}{2}\) 和 \({B}^{\prime } = 6 - \frac{A}{2} \leq  B\) ，它有以下等价表述:
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
A + \frac{B}{2} \leq  6 \leq  B + \frac{A}{2},
\]

\[
A + \frac{A + \left( {B - A}\right) }{2} \leq  6 \leq  A + \left( {B - A}\right)  + \frac{A}{2},
\]

\[
\frac{3}{2}A + \frac{\left( B - A\right) }{2} \leq  6 \leq  \frac{3}{2}A + \left( {B - A}\right) ,
\]

that is,

\begin{tcolorbox}
即
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
A + \frac{1}{3}\left( {B - A}\right)  \leq  4 \leq  A + \frac{2}{3}\left( {B - A}\right)  \tag{3.11}
\]

which is stated in (3.8). Assuming that (3.11) holds, we want to show the same inequalities with \({A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }\) instead of \(A,B\) , that is,

\begin{tcolorbox}
这在(3.8)中有所陈述。假设(3.11)成立，我们想证明用 \({A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }\) 代替 \(A,B\) 时的相同不等式，即
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{A}^{\prime } + \frac{1}{3}\left( {{B}^{\prime } - {A}^{\prime }}\right)  \leq  4 \leq  {A}^{\prime } + \frac{2}{3}\left( {{B}^{\prime } - {A}^{\prime }}\right) ,
\]

\[
{B}^{\prime } - \frac{2}{3}\left( {{B}^{\prime } - {A}^{\prime }}\right)  \leq  4 \leq  {B}^{\prime } - \frac{1}{3}\left( {{B}^{\prime } - {A}^{\prime }}\right) ,
\]

\[
6 - \frac{A}{2} - \frac{2}{3}\left( {{B}^{\prime } - {A}^{\prime }}\right)  \leq  4 \leq  6 - \frac{A}{2} - \frac{1}{3}\left( {{B}^{\prime } - {A}^{\prime }}\right)
\]

or

\begin{tcolorbox}
或者
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
2 \leq  \frac{A}{2} + \frac{2}{3}\left( {{B}^{\prime } - {A}^{\prime }}\right) ,\;\frac{A}{2} + \frac{1}{3}\left( {{B}^{\prime } - {A}^{\prime }}\right)  \leq  2
\]

which when multiplied by 2 and using \(2\left( {{B}^{\prime } - {A}^{\prime }}\right)  = B - A\) is equivalent to (3.11) which we assumed to be true. This completes the proof of (d).

\begin{tcolorbox}
将其乘以 2 并使用 \(2\left( {{B}^{\prime } - {A}^{\prime }}\right)  = B - A\) 后，等价于我们假设为真的(3.11)。这就完成了(d)的证明。
\end{tcolorbox}

By Proposition 3.6, we can apply iterated elimination of dominated strategies to the game (3.6) as follows. Initially, assume that both players consider any strategy in the interval \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack   = \left\lbrack  {0,{12}}\right\rbrack\) . Then \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack   = \left\lbrack  {0,6}\right\rbrack\) . Any strategy \({x}^{\prime }\) of player I outside \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) (which here means only \({x}^{\prime } > 6\) ) is dominated by \(x = {B}^{\prime }\) . (However, as stated in (a), any strategy in \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) is not dominated because it is a best response to some \(y\) in \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) , which shows that the elimination at this stage is best possible.) Similarly, because the game is symmetric, any strategy \({y}^{\prime }\) of player II outside \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) is dominated by \(y = {B}^{\prime }\) . We eliminate these dominated strategies, so that the new strategy sets will be \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) . Condition (3.8) states that 4 is in the middle third of the interval \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) , which is (just about) true if \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack   = \left\lbrack  {0,{12}}\right\rbrack\) where that middle third is \(\left\lbrack  {4,8}\right\rbrack\) . Furthermore,(d) states that then the same holds for \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) (indeed,4 is in the middle third \(\left\lbrack  {2,4}\right\rbrack\) of the interval \(\left\lbrack  {0,6}\right\rbrack\) ). We can now repeat the elimination of dominated strategies with \({A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }\) instead of \(A,B\) , and each time the length \(B - A\) of the considered interval is reduced by half. The strategy \(x = 4\) and \(y = 4\) belongs to all these intervals. In fact, any other strategy will sooner or later be outside a sufficiently small interval and will therefore be eliminated, although there is no bound on the number of elimination rounds for strategies that are very close to 4 . In the end, only the strategy pair(4,4)survives, which is the unique equilibrium of the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
根据命题 3.6，我们可以对博弈(3.6)应用重复剔除劣势策略(iterated elimination of dominated strategies)，具体如下。最初，假设两个参与者都考虑区间 \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack   = \left\lbrack  {0,{12}}\right\rbrack\) 内的任何策略。然后 \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack   = \left\lbrack  {0,6}\right\rbrack\) 。参与者 I 在 \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) 之外的任何策略 \({x}^{\prime }\) (这里仅指 \({x}^{\prime } > 6\) )都被 \(x = {B}^{\prime }\) 占优。(然而，如(a)中所述， \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) 内的任何策略都不被占优，因为它是对 \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) 中某个 \(y\) 的最优反应(best response)，这表明此阶段的剔除是最优的。)类似地，由于该博弈是对称的(symmetric)，参与者 II 在 \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) 之外的任何策略 \({y}^{\prime }\) 都被 \(y = {B}^{\prime }\) 占优。我们剔除这些被占优策略(dominated strategy)，使得新的策略集将是 \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) 。条件(3.8)表明 4 在区间 \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack\) 的中间三分之一处，如果 \(\left\lbrack  {A,B}\right\rbrack   = \left\lbrack  {0,{12}}\right\rbrack\) 成立，这(几乎)是正确的，其中中间三分之一是 \(\left\lbrack  {4,8}\right\rbrack\) 。此外，(d)表明对于 \(\left\lbrack  {{A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }}\right\rbrack\) 同样成立(实际上，4 在区间 \(\left\lbrack  {0,6}\right\rbrack\) 的中间三分之一 \(\left\lbrack  {2,4}\right\rbrack\) 处)。我们现在可以用 \({A}^{\prime },{B}^{\prime }\) 代替 \(A,B\) 重复剔除劣势策略，并且每次所考虑区间的长度 \(B - A\) 都会减半。策略 \(x = 4\) 和 \(y = 4\) 属于所有这些区间。实际上，任何其他策略迟早都会处于足够小的区间之外，因此会被剔除，尽管对于非常接近 4 的策略，剔除轮数没有上限。最终，只有策略对(4,4)留存下来，这是该博弈的唯一均衡(equilibrium)。
\end{tcolorbox}

This shows that the quantity duopoly game (3.6) is dominance solvable (if one allows for an unlimited number of elimination rounds, otherwise up to any desired degree of accuracy). Dominance solvability is considered a stronger solution concept than equilibrium.
