The equilibrium itself, of course, can be computed directly as the solution(x, y)to the two equations \(x = 6 - y/2\) and \(y = 6 - x/2\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
这表明产量双头垄断博弈(3.6)是占优可解的(dominance solvable)(如果允许进行无限轮次的剔除，否则可达到任何期望的精度)。占优可解性被认为是比均衡更强的解概念。当然，均衡本身可以直接作为两个方程 \(x = 6 - y/2\) 和 \(y = 6 - x/2\) 的解(x, y)来计算。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{3.7 Games without a Pure-Strategy Equilibrium}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{3.7 没有纯策略均衡的博弈}
\end{tcolorbox}

Not every game has an equilibrium in pure strategies. Figure 3.12 shows two well-known examples. In Matching Pennies, the two players reveal a penny which can show Heads(H)or Tails(T). If the pennies match, then player I wins the other player's penny, otherwise player II. No strategy pair can be stable because the losing player would always deviate.

\begin{tcolorbox}
并非每个博弈都存在纯策略均衡。图3.12展示了两个著名的例子。在硬币匹配(博弈)中，两名玩家各出示一枚硬币，硬币可能正面(H)朝上或反面(T)朝上。如果两枚硬币的正反面相同，则玩家I赢得对方的硬币；否则，玩家II赢得硬币。不存在稳定的策略组合，因为输的玩家总会改变策略。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_83_416_550_1084_490_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 3.12 Matching Pennies (left) and Rock-Paper-Scissors (right).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图3.12 硬币匹配(左)和石头剪刀布(右)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Rock-Paper-Scissors is a \(3 \times  3\) game where both players choose simultaneously one of their three strategies Rock(R), Paper(P), or Scissors(S). Rock loses to Paper, Paper loses to Scissors, and Scissors lose to Rock, and it is a draw otherwise. No two strategies are best responses to each other. Like Matching Pennies, this is a zero-sum game because the payoffs in any cell of the table sum to zero. Unlike Matching Pennies, Rock-Paper-Scissors is symmetric. Hence, when both players play the same strategy (the cells on the diagonal), they get the same payoff, which is zero because the game is zero-sum.

\begin{tcolorbox}
石头剪刀布是一种零和博弈，两名玩家同时从石头(R)、布(P)、剪刀(S)这三种策略中选择其一。石头输给布，布输给剪刀，剪刀输给石头；若双方选择相同，则为平局。没有两种策略能互为最优反应。和硬币匹配(博弈)一样，这是一个零和博弈，因为表格中任意单元格的收益之和为零。与硬币匹配(博弈)不同的是，石头剪刀布是对称博弈。因此，当两名玩家采用相同的策略(对角线上的单元格)时，他们的收益相同，由于这是零和博弈，所以收益为零。
\end{tcolorbox}

The game-theoretic recommendation is to play randomly in games like Matching Pennies or Rock-Paper-Scissors that have no equilibrium, according to certain probabilities that depend on the payoffs. As we will see in Chapter 6, any finite game has an equilibrium when players are allowed to use randomized strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈论的建议是，在像硬币匹配(博弈)或石头剪刀布这类不存在均衡的博弈中，玩家应根据取决于收益的特定概率随机选择策略。正如我们将在第6章看到的，当允许玩家使用随机策略时，任何有限博弈都存在均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{3.8 Symmetric Games with Two Strategies per Player}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{3.8 每个玩家有两种策略的对称博弈}
\end{tcolorbox}

In this section, we consider \(N\) -player games that, somewhat surprisingly, always have a pure equilibrium, namely symmetric games where each player has only two strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在本节中，我们考虑n人博弈，有些令人惊讶的是，这类博弈总是存在纯策略均衡，即每个玩家只有两种策略的对称博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

An \(N\) -player game is symmetric if each player has the same set of strategies, and if the game stays the same after any permutation (shuffling) of the players and, correspondingly, their payoffs. For two players, this means that the game stays the same when exchanging the players and their payoffs, visualized by reflection along the diagonal.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果每个玩家都有相同的策略集，并且在对玩家及其相应的收益进行任何排列(洗牌)后，博弈保持不变，那么该n人博弈就是对称的。对于两名玩家来说，这意味着当交换玩家及其收益时，博弈保持不变，这可以通过沿对角线反射来直观表示。
\end{tcolorbox}

We now consider symmetric \(N\) -player games where each player has two strategies, which we call 0 and 1 . Normally, any combination of these strategies defines a separate strategy profile, so there are \({2}^{N}\) profiles, and each of them specifies a payoff to each player, so the game is defined by \(N \cdot  {2}^{N}\) payoffs. If the game is symmetric, vastly fewer payoffs are needed. Then a strategy profile is determined by how many players choose 1, say \(k\) players (where \(0 \leq  k \leq  N\) ), and then the remaining \(N - k\) players choose 0, so the profile can be written as

\begin{tcolorbox}
现在我们考虑对称的n人博弈，其中每个玩家有两种策略，我们称之为0和1。通常，这些策略的任何组合都定义了一个单独的策略组合，因此有n + 1个组合，并且每个组合都为每个玩家指定了一个收益，所以该博弈由2(n + 1)个收益来定义。如果该博弈是对称的，则需要的收益数量会大大减少。那么，一个策略组合由选择1的玩家数量决定，假设为k名玩家(其中0 ≤ k ≤ n)，然后剩下的n - k名玩家选择0，所以该组合可以写成
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\left( {\underset{k}{\underbrace{1,\ldots ,1}},\underset{N - k}{\underbrace{0,\ldots ,0}}}\right) . \tag{3.12}
\]

Because the game is symmetric, any profile where \(k\) players choose 1 has to give the same payoff as (3.12) to any player who chooses 1, and a second payoff to any player who chooses 0 . Hence, we need only two payoffs for these profiles (3.12) when \(1 \leq  k \leq  N - 1\) . When \(k = 0\) then the profile is \(\left( {0,\ldots ,0}\right)\) and all players play the same strategy 0 and only one payoff is needed, and similarly when \(k = N\) where all players play 1. Therefore, a symmetric \(N\) -player game with two strategies per player is specified by only \({2N}\) payoffs.

\begin{tcolorbox}
因为该博弈是对称的，任何有k名玩家选择1的组合，对于选择1的任何玩家来说，其收益必须与(3.12)中的收益相同，对于选择0的任何玩家来说，有另一个收益。因此，当0 < k < n时，对于这些组合(3.12)，我们只需要两个收益。当k = 0时，组合为(0, …, 0)，所有玩家都采用相同的策略0，只需要一个收益；同样，当k = n时，所有玩家都采用策略1。因此，每个玩家有两种策略的对称n人博弈仅由n + 2个收益来定义。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proposition 3.7. Consider a symmetric \(N\) -player game where each player has two strategies, 0 and 1 . Then this game has a pure equilibrium. The strategy profile \(\left( {1,1,\ldots ,1}\right)\) is the unique equilibrium of the game if and only if 1 dominates 0 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
命题3.7。考虑一个对称的n人博弈，其中每个玩家有两种策略，0和1。那么这个博弈存在纯策略均衡。当且仅当策略1占优于策略0时，策略组合(1, …, 1)是该博弈的唯一均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. If 1 dominates 0, then \(\left( {1,1,\ldots ,1}\right)\) is clearly the unique equilibrium of the game, so suppose this not the case. Because 0 is not dominated and there are only two strategies, for some profile the payoff when playing 0 is greater than or equal to the payoff when playing 1, that is, 0 is a best response to the remaining strategies. Consider such a profile (3.12) where 0 is a best response, with the smallest number \(k\) of players who play 1, where \(0 \leq  k < N\) . Then this profile is an equilibrium: By assumption and symmetry, 0 is a best response to the strategies of the other players for every player who plays 0 . If 1 was not a best response for every player who plays 1, then such a player would obtain a higher payoff when changing his strategy to 0 . This change would result in a profile where \(k - 1\) players play 1, the remaining players play 0, and 0 is a best response, which contradicts the smallest of choice of \(k\) . This shows that the game has an equilibrium.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。如果策略1占优于策略0，那么 \(\left( {1,1,\ldots ,1}\right)\) 显然是该博弈的唯一均衡，因此假设并非如此。由于策略0未被占优，且只有两种策略，对于某些策略组合而言，选择策略0的收益大于或等于选择策略1的收益，即策略0是其余策略的最优反应。考虑这样一个策略组合(3.12)，其中策略0是最优反应，且选择策略1的玩家数量 \(k\) 最少，其中 \(0 \leq  k < N\) 。那么这个策略组合就是一个均衡:根据假设和对称性，对于每个选择策略0的玩家来说，策略0是对其他玩家策略的最优反应。如果对于每个选择策略1的玩家来说，策略1不是最优反应，那么这样的玩家将策略改为0时会获得更高的收益。这种改变会导致一个新的策略组合，其中有 \(k - 1\) 个玩家选择策略1，其余玩家选择策略0，并且策略0是最优反应，这与 \(k\) 的最小选择相矛盾。这表明该博弈存在一个均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

We consider this proposition for symmetric games when \(N = 2\) . The symmetric Prisoner's Dilemma (Figure 3.1) has a single equilibrium, and it indeed uses a dominating strategy. The equilibrium is symmetric, that is, both players play the same strategy. The symmetric Stag Hunt game (Figure 3.5) has two symmetric equilibria. The symmetric Chicken game (Figure 3.3) has two equilibria which are not symmetric. Clearly, in any symmetric two-player game, non-symmetric equilibria come in pairs, here(A, C)and(C, A), obtained by exchanging the players and their strategies. Chicken does not have a symmetric pure equilibrium. The \(3 \times  3\) game of Rock-Paper-Scissors (Figure 3.12) is symmetric but does not have a pure equilibrium. This shows that Proposition 3.7 cannot be generalized to symmetric games with more than two strategies per player.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们考虑当 \(N = 2\) 时对称博弈的这一命题。对称的囚徒困境(图3.1)有一个单一均衡，并且确实使用了占优策略。该均衡是对称的，即两个玩家选择相同的策略。对称的猎鹿博弈(图3.5)有两个对称均衡。对称的胆小鬼博弈(图3.3)有两个非对称的均衡。显然，在任何对称的两人博弈中，非对称均衡是成对出现的，这里是(A，C)和(C，A)，通过交换玩家及其策略得到。胆小鬼博弈没有对称的纯策略均衡。 \(3 \times  3\) 的石头剪刀布博弈(图3.12)是对称的，但没有纯策略均衡。这表明命题3.7不能推广到每个玩家有两种以上策略的对称博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\Rightarrow\) Exercise 3.4 describes a game related to Proposition 3.7.

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\Rightarrow\) 练习3.4描述了一个与命题3.7相关的博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{3.9 Further Reading}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{3.9 延伸阅读}
\end{tcolorbox}

The display of staggered payoffs in the lower left and upper right of each cell in the payoff table is due to Thomas Schelling. In 2005, he received, together with Robert Aumann, the Nobel memorial prize in Economic Sciences (officially: The Sveriges Riksbank Prize in Economic Sciences in Memory of Alfred Nobel) "for having enhanced our understanding of conflict and cooperation through game-theory analysis." According to Dixit and Nalebuff (1991, p. 90), he said with excessive modesty: "If I am ever asked whether I ever made a contribution to game theory, I shall answer yes. Asked what it was, I shall say the invention of staggered payoffs in a matrix."

\begin{tcolorbox}
收益表中每个单元格左下角和右上角交错显示收益的方式归功于托马斯·谢林(Thomas Schelling)。2005年，他与罗伯特·奥曼(Robert Aumann)共同获得了诺贝尔经济学奖(正式名称:瑞典中央银行纪念阿尔弗雷德·诺贝尔经济学奖)，“因为他们通过博弈论分析增进了我们对冲突与合作的理解”。根据迪克西特(Dixit)和奈尔伯夫(Nalebuff)(1991年，第90页)的说法，他过分谦虚地说:“如果有人问我是否对博弈论做出过贡献，我会回答是的。如果问我贡献是什么，我会说是发明了矩阵中的交错收益。”
\end{tcolorbox}

The strategic form is taught in every course on non-cooperative game theory (it is sometimes called the "normal form", now less used in game theory because "normal" is an overworked term in mathematics). Osborne (2004) gives careful historical explanations of the games considered in this chapter, including the original duopoly model of Cournot (1838), and many others. Our Exercise 3.4 is taken from that book. Gibbons (1992) shows that the Cournot game is dominance solvable, with less detail than our proof of Proposition 3.6. Both Osborne and Gibbons disregard Schelling and use comma-separated payoffs as in (3.1).

\begin{tcolorbox}
策略形式在每一门非合作博弈论课程中都会讲授(它有时被称为“标准型”，现在在博弈论中较少使用，因为“标准”在数学中是一个被过度使用的术语)。奥斯本(Osborne，2004年)对本章所考虑的博弈进行了细致的历史解释，包括古诺(Cournot，1838年)最初的双头垄断模型以及许多其他模型。我们的练习3.4取自那本书。吉本斯(Gibbons，1992年)表明古诺博弈是占优可解的，但细节不如我们对命题3.6的证明。奥斯本和吉本斯都忽略了谢林，使用如(3.1)中用逗号分隔的收益。
\end{tcolorbox}

The Cournot game in Section 3.6 is also a potential game with a strictly concave potential function, which has a unique maximum and therefore a unique equilibrium. Neyman (1997) showed that it is also a unique correlated equilibrium (see Chapter 12). Potential games (Monderer and Shapley, 1996) generalize games with a potential function such as the congestion games considered in Section 2.5.

\begin{tcolorbox}
3.6节中的古诺博弈也是一个具有严格凹势函数的势博弈，该势函数有唯一的最大值，因此有唯一的均衡。内曼(Neyman，1997年)表明它也是唯一的相关均衡(见第12章)。势博弈(蒙德勒(Monderer)和沙普利(Shapley)，1996年)推广了具有势函数的博弈，如2.5节中考虑的拥塞博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

A classic survey of equilibrium refinements is van Damme (1987). Proposition 3.7 seems to have been shown first by Cheng, Reeves, Vorobeychik, and Wellman (2004, theorem 1).

\begin{tcolorbox}
关于均衡精炼的经典综述是范·达姆(van Damme，1987年)的著作。命题3.7似乎最早由程(Cheng)、里夫斯(Reeves)、沃罗贝奇克(Vorobeychik)和韦尔曼(Wellman)(2004年，定理1)证明。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{3.10 Exercises for Chapter 3}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{3.10 第3章练习}
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 3.1. Consider the following \(3 \times  3\) game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习3.1。考虑以下 \(3 \times  3\) 博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.3\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_86_575_351_488_483_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

(a) Identify all pairs of strategies where one strategy weakly dominates the other.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a)找出所有一个策略弱占优于另一个策略的策略对。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Assume you are allowed to remove a weakly dominated strategy of some player. Do so, and repeat this process (of iterated elimination of weakly dominated strategies) until you find a single strategy pair of the original game. (Remember that two payoff equivalent strategies do not weakly dominate each other!)

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 假设允许你剔除某个参与者的一个弱被占优策略。进行此操作，并重复这个过程(即迭代剔除弱被占优策略)，直到你找到原博弈的一个单一策略组合。(记住，两个收益等价的策略不会相互弱占优！)
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) Find such an iterated elimination of weakly dominated strategies that results in a strategy pair other than the one found in (b), where both strategies, and the payoffs to the players, are different.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) 找出一种迭代剔除弱被占优策略的方法，使其得到一个与 (b) 中不同的策略组合，其中两个策略以及参与者的收益都不同。
\end{tcolorbox}

(d) What are the equilibria (in pure strategies) of the game?

\begin{tcolorbox}
(d) 该博弈的(纯策略)均衡是什么？
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 3.2. Consider the following three-player game in strategic form.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 3.2。考虑以下策略形式的三人博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.2\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_86_435_1415_356_351_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.2\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_86_862_1414_353_351_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

III: \(L\) III: \(R\)

\begin{tcolorbox}
参与者 III: \(L\) 参与者 III: \(R\)
\end{tcolorbox}

Each player has two strategies: Player I has the strategies \(T\) and \(B\) (the top and bottom row), player II has the strategies \(l\) and \(r\) (the left and right column in each \(2 \times  2\) panel), and player III has the strategies \(L\) and \(R\) (the right or left panel). The payoffs to the players in each cell are given as triples of numbers to players I, II, III.

\begin{tcolorbox}
每个参与者有两个策略:参与者 I 有策略 \(T\) 和 \(B\) (顶行和底行)，参与者 II 有策略 \(l\) 和 \(r\) (每个 \(2 \times  2\) 面板中的左列和右列)，参与者 III 有策略 \(L\) 和 \(R\) (右面板或左面板)。每个单元格中参与者的收益以参与者 I、II、III 的数字三元组形式给出。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Identify all pairs of strategies where one strategy strictly, or weakly, dominates the other. [Hint: Make sure you understand what dominance means for more than two players. Be careful to consider the correct payoffs.]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 找出所有一个策略严格或弱占优另一个策略的策略对。[提示:确保你理解多于两个参与者时占优的含义。注意考虑正确的收益。]
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Apply iterated elimination of strictly dominated strategies to this game. What are the equilibria of the game?

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 对这个博弈应用迭代剔除严格被占优策略的方法。该博弈的均衡是什么？
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 3.3. Consider the duopoly game as studied in Section 3.6 where player I and II produce nonnegative quantities \(x\) and \(y\) and the unit price on the market is \({12} - x - y\) . In addition, the players now have a cost of production, which is a cost of 1 per unit for player I and a cost of 2 per unit for player II. Write down the payoff to the players as a modification of (3.6), find their best response functions, and determine the equilibrium of this game. What are the players' profits in equilibrium?

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 3.3。考虑第 3.6 节中研究的双头垄断博弈，其中参与者 I 和 II 生产非负产量 \(x\) 和 \(y\) ，市场上的单位价格是 \({12} - x - y\) 。此外，现在参与者有生产成本，参与者 I 的单位成本为 1，参与者 II 的单位成本为 2。将参与者的收益写成 (3.6) 的修正形式，找出他们的最优反应函数，并确定这个博弈的均衡。均衡时参与者的利润是多少？
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 3.4. (Osborne, 2004, Exercise 33.1, Contributing to a public good.)

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 3.4。(奥斯本，2004 年，练习 33.1，为公共品做贡献。)
\end{tcolorbox}

Each of \(N\) people chooses whether or not to contribute a fixed amount towards the provision of a public good. The good is provided if and only if at least \(K\) people contribute, where \(2 \leq  K \leq  N\) . If the good is not provided, contributions are not refunded. Each person strictly ranks outcomes from best to worst as follows:

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(N\) 个人中的每个人都选择是否为提供一种公共品贡献一笔固定的金额。当且仅当至少 \(K\) 个人做出贡献时，该公共品才会被提供，其中 \(2 \leq  K \leq  N\) 。如果公共品未被提供，贡献不会退还。每个人对结果的严格排序从最好到最差如下:
\end{tcolorbox}

(i) any outcome in which the good is provided and she does not contribute,

\begin{tcolorbox}
(i) 公共品被提供且她没有做出贡献的任何结果，
\end{tcolorbox}

(ii) any outcome in which the good is provided and she contributes,

\begin{tcolorbox}
(ii) 公共品被提供且她做出贡献的任何结果，
\end{tcolorbox}

(iii) any outcome in which the good is not provided and she does not contribute,

\begin{tcolorbox}
(iii) 公共品未被提供且她没有做出贡献的任何结果，
\end{tcolorbox}

(iv) any outcome in which the good is not provided and she contributes.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(iv) 公共品未被提供且她做出贡献的任何结果。
\end{tcolorbox}

Formulate this situation as a strategic-form game and find its equilibria. You need to know the number of players, the strategies that a player has available, and the players' preferences for strategy profiles. Is there a symmetry here? Is there an equilibrium in which more than \(K\) people contribute? One in which \(K\) people contribute? One in which fewer than \(K\) people contribute? 4

\begin{tcolorbox}
将这种情况表述为一个策略形式的博弈并找出其均衡。你需要知道参与者的数量、参与者可用的策略以及参与者对策略组合的偏好。这里有对称性吗？是否存在一个均衡，其中超过 \(K\) 个人做出贡献？一个 \(K\) 个人做出贡献的均衡？一个少于 \(K\) 个人做出贡献的均衡？4
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Game Trees with Perfect Information}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{完美信息博弈树}
\end{tcolorbox}

This chapter considers game trees, the second main way for defining a noncooperative game in addition to the strategic form. In a game tree, players move sequentially and (in the case of perfect information studied in this chapter) are aware of the previous moves of the other players. In contrast, in a strategic-form game players move simultaneously. In this "dynamic" setting, a play means a specific run of the game given by a sequence of actions of the players.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本章将探讨博弈树，它是除策略形式之外定义非合作博弈的第二种主要方式。在博弈树中，玩家按顺序行动，并且(在本章研究的完美信息情况下)知晓其他玩家之前的行动。相比之下，在策略形式的博弈中，玩家同时行动。在这种“动态”设定下，一次博弈指的是由玩家的一系列行动所构成的特定博弈过程。
\end{tcolorbox}

A game tree is described by a tree whose nodes represent possible states of play. Some nodes are chance nodes where the next node is determined randomly according to a known probability distribution. A decision node belongs to a player who makes a move to determine the next node. Play ends at a terminal node or leaf of the game tree where each player receives a payoff.

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈树由一棵树来描述，其节点代表可能的博弈状态。一些节点是机会节点，在这些节点上，下一个节点根据已知的概率分布随机确定。决策节点属于某个玩家，该玩家通过行动来确定下一个节点。博弈在博弈树的终端节点或叶节点结束，此时每个玩家会获得一个收益。
\end{tcolorbox}

Game trees can be solved by backward induction, where one finds optimal moves for each player, given that all future moves have already been determined. Backward induction starts with the decision nodes closest to the leaves, and assumes that a player always makes a payoff-maximizing move. Such a move is in general not unique, so that the players' moves determined by backward induction may be interdependent (see Figure 4.4).

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈树可以通过逆向归纳法求解，即在已知所有未来行动都已确定的情况下，为每个玩家找到最优行动。逆向归纳法从最接近叶节点的决策节点开始，并假设玩家总是做出使收益最大化的行动。一般来说，这样的行动并非唯一，因此通过逆向归纳法确定的玩家行动可能相互依赖(见图4.4)。
\end{tcolorbox}

A strategy in a game tree is a derived concept. A strategy defines a move at every decision node of the player and therefore a plan of action for every possible state of play. Any strategy profile therefore defines a payoff to each player (which may be an expected payoff if there are chance moves), which defines the strategic form of the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈树中的策略是一个派生概念。策略定义了玩家在每个决策节点的行动，因此是针对每种可能博弈状态的行动计划。因此，任何策略组合都为每个玩家定义了一个收益(如果存在机会行动，可能是期望效用)，这就定义了博弈的策略形式。
\end{tcolorbox}

Because every strategy is a combination of moves, the number of strategies in a game tree is very large. Reduced strategies reduce this number to some extent. In a reduced strategy, moves at decision nodes that cannot be reached due to an earlier own of the player are left unspecified.

\begin{tcolorbox}
由于每个策略都是行动的组合，博弈树中的策略数量非常多。精简策略在一定程度上减少了这个数量。在精简策略中，由于玩家先前的行动而无法到达的决策节点上的行动不做具体规定。
\end{tcolorbox}

Backward induction defines a strategy profile which is an equilibrium of the game (Theorem 4.6). This is called a subgame-perfect equilibrium (SPE) because it defines an equilibrium in every subgame. (In a game tree with perfect information, a subgame is just a subtree of the game. In games with imperfect information, treated in Chapter 10, an SPE can in general not be found by backward induction.)

\begin{tcolorbox}
逆向归纳法定义了一个策略组合，它是博弈的一个均衡(定理4.6)。这被称为子博弈完美均衡(SPE)，因为它在每个子博弈中都定义了一个均衡。(在具有完美信息的博弈树中，子博弈只是博弈的一个子树。在第10章讨论的不完美信息博弈中，一般不能通过逆向归纳法找到子博弈完美均衡。)
\end{tcolorbox}

In Section 4.7, we consider commitment games, which are two-player games where a given strategic-form game is changed to a game tree where one player moves first and the other player is informed about the first player's move and can react to that move. It is assumed that the second player always chooses a best response, as in an SPE. This changes the game, typically to the advantage of the first mover. We study the commitment game for the Quality game and for the Cournot duopoly game of quantity competition from Section 3.6, where the commitment game is known as a Stackelberg leadership game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在4.7节中，我们将考虑承诺博弈，这是一种两人博弈，其中给定的策略形式博弈被转变为一个博弈树，其中一个玩家先行动，另一个玩家知晓第一个玩家的行动并可以对此做出反应。假设第二个玩家总是选择最优反应，就像在子博弈完美均衡中一样。这改变了博弈，通常对先行动者有利。我们研究产品质量博弈和第3.6节中的古诺双寡头产量竞争博弈的承诺博弈，其中承诺博弈被称为斯塔克尔伯格领导博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

Every game tree can be converted to strategic form. A general game in strategic form can only be represented by a tree with extra components that model imperfect information. Game trees with imperfect information will be treated in Chapter 10 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
每个博弈树都可以转换为策略形式。一般的策略形式博弈只能由带有模拟不完美信息的额外组件的树来表示。不完美信息的博弈树将在第10章讨论。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{4.1 Prerequisites and Learning Outcomes}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{4.1 先决条件和学习成果}
\end{tcolorbox}

Chapter 3 is a prerequisite for this chapter. Chapter 1 is not, although combinatorial games are sequential games with perfect information as considered here, and therefore illustrative examples. We will recall the mathematical concepts of directed graphs and trees, and of the expected value of a random variable, as background material.

\begin{tcolorbox}
第3章是本章的先决条件。虽然组合博弈也是这里所考虑的具有完美信息的序贯博弈，是具有说明性的例子，但第1章不是先决条件。我们将回顾有向图与树图以及随机变量的期望值等数学概念，作为背景材料。
\end{tcolorbox}

After studying this chapter, you should be able to

\begin{tcolorbox}
学习本章后，你应该能够
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item interpret game trees with their moves and payoffs;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 解读博弈树及其行动和收益；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item create the strategies of a player as combinations of moves, and know how to count them;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 将玩家的行动组合成策略，并知道如何计算策略的数量；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item combine strategies into reduced strategies, which identify moves at decision nodes that are unreachable due to an earlier own move of the player, and understand where the corresponding "wildcard" symbol "*" is placed in the move list that represents the strategy;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 将策略组合成精简策略，识别由于玩家先前的行动而无法到达的决策节点上的行动，并理解在表示策略的行动列表中，相应的“通配符”符号“*”应放在何处；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item write down the strategic form of a game tree, with players' payoffs in the correct place in each cell, and with computed expected payoffs if needed;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 写出博弈树的策略形式，将玩家的收益正确地放置在每个单元格中，并在需要时计算期望效用；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item explain backward induction and why it requires full (unreduced) strategies, which may not be unique;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 解释逆向归纳法(backward induction)以及为什么它需要完整(未简化)的策略，而这些策略可能并非唯一；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item construct any equilibrium, even if not subgame-perfect, directly from the game tree, also for more than two players, as in Exercise 4.5;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 直接从博弈树(game tree)构建任何均衡(equilibrium)，即使不是子博弈完美均衡，也适用于两个以上的参与者，如练习4.5所示；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item create the commitment game from a game in strategic form, and compare its subgame-perfect equilibria with the equilibria in the strategic form.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 从策略形式(strategic form)的博弈中构建承诺博弈(commitment game)，并将其子博弈完美均衡与策略形式中的均衡进行比较。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{4.2 Definition of Game Trees}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{4.2 博弈树的定义}
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 4.1 shows an example of a game tree. We always draw trees downwards, with the starting node, called the root, at the top. (Conventions on drawing game trees vary. Sometimes trees are drawn from the bottom upwards, sometimes from left to right, and sometimes with the root at the center with edges in any direction.)

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.1展示了一个博弈树的示例。我们通常将树向下绘制，起始节点(称为根节点)位于顶部。(绘制博弈树的约定各不相同。有时树是从下向上绘制，有时是从左向右绘制，有时根节点位于中心，边可以指向任何方向。)
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.6\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_90_204_533_775_575_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 4.1 Example of a game tree. The square node indicates a chance move. At a leaf of the tree, the top payoff is to player I, the bottom payoff to player II.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.1 博弈树示例。方形节点表示随机行动。在树的叶节点(leaf)处，顶部的收益属于参与者I，底部的收益属于参与者II。
\end{tcolorbox}

The nodes of the tree denote states of play (which have been called "positions" in the combinatorial games considered in Chapter 1). Nodes are connected by lines, called edges. An edge from a node \(u\) to a child node \(v\) (where \(v\) is drawn below \(u\) ) indicates a possible move in the game. This may be a move of a "personal" player, for example move \(X\) of player I in Figure 4.1. Then \(u\) is also called a decision node. Alternatively, \(u\) is a chance node, like the node \(u\) that follows move \(b\) of player II in Figure 4.1. We draw decision nodes as small filled circles and chance nodes as squares. After a chance node \(u\) , the next node \(v\) is determined by a random choice made with the probability associated with the edge that leads from \(u\) to \(v\) . In Figure 4.1, these probabilities are \(\frac{1}{3}\) for the left move and \(\frac{2}{3}\) for the right move.

\begin{tcolorbox}
树的节点(node)表示博弈状态(在第1章所考虑的组合博弈中，这些状态被称为“位置”)。节点通过称为边(edge)的线连接。从节点 \(u\) 到子节点 \(v\) (其中 \(v\) 绘制在 \(u\) 下方)的边表示博弈中的一个可能行动。这可能是“个人”参与者的行动，例如图4.1中参与者I的行动 \(X\) 。此时 \(u\) 也称为决策节点。或者， \(u\) 是一个随机节点，如图4.1中参与者II行动 \(b\) 之后的节点 \(u\) 。我们将决策节点绘制为小实心圆，将随机节点绘制为方形。在随机节点 \(u\) 之后，下一个节点 \(v\) 由与从 \(u\) 到 \(v\) 的边相关联的概率随机确定。在图4.1中，向左行动的概率为 \(\frac{1}{3}\) ，向右行动的概率为 \(\frac{2}{3}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

At a terminal node or leaf of the game tree, every player gets a payoff. In Figure 4.1, leaves are not explicitly drawn, but the payoffs given instead, with the top payoff to player I and the bottom payoff to player II.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在博弈树的终端节点(terminal node)或叶节点处，每个参与者都会获得一个收益。在图4.1中，叶节点未明确绘制，而是直接给出了收益，顶部的收益属于参与者I，底部的收益属于参与者II。
\end{tcolorbox}

It does not matter how the tree is drawn, only how the nodes are connected by edges, as summarized in the background material on directed graphs and trees. The following is the formal definition of a game tree.

\begin{tcolorbox}
树的绘制方式并不重要，重要的是节点如何通过边连接，这在有向图与树图(directed graphs and trees)的背景材料(background material)中已有总结。以下是博弈树的正式定义。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 4.2. A game tree is a tree with the following additional information:

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义4.2. 博弈树是具有以下额外信息的树:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item A finite set of \(N\) players.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 有限个 \(N\) 参与者。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Each non-terminal node of the tree is either a decision node and belongs to one of the \(N\) players, or is a chance node. Background material: Directed graphs and trees
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 树的每个非终端节点要么是决策节点，属于 \(N\) 个参与者之一，要么是随机节点。背景材料:有向图与树图
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

A directed graph (or digraph) is given by a finite set of nodes and a finite set of edges. Every edge is an ordered pair of nodes \(u\) and \(v\) , written as \({uv}\) . A walk from \(u\) to \(v\) is a sequence of nodes \({u}_{0},{u}_{1},\ldots ,{u}_{m}\) for \(m \geq  0\) where \({u}_{k}{u}_{k + 1}\) is an edge for \(0 \leq  k < m\) , and \(u = {u}_{0}\) and \(v = {u}_{m}\) . If these nodes are all distinct, the walk is called a path.

\begin{tcolorbox}
有向图(digraph)由有限个节点和有限条边组成。每条边是节点 \(u\) 和 \(v\) 的有序对，记为 \({uv}\) 。从 \(u\) 到 \(v\) 的行走(walk)是节点序列 \({u}_{0},{u}_{1},\ldots ,{u}_{m}\) ，其中 \(m \geq  0\) 且 \({u}_{k}{u}_{k + 1}\) 是边， \(0 \leq  k < m\) ， \(u = {u}_{0}\) 且 \(v = {u}_{m}\) 。如果这些节点都不同，则该行走称为路径(path)。
\end{tcolorbox}

A (directed) tree is a digraph with a distinguished node, the root, so that for every node \(u\) there is a unique walk from the root to \(u\) . If \({uv}\) is an edge of the tree, then \(v\) is called a child of \(u\) and \(u\) is called the parent of \(v\) . In a tree, a node \(v\) is a descendant of a node \(u\) if there is a nonempty walk from \(u\) to \(v\) . A node without a child is called a leaf or terminal node of the tree.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(有向)树是具有一个特殊节点(根节点)的有向图，使得对于每个节点 \(u\) ，从根节点到 \(u\) 都有唯一的行走。如果 \({uv}\) 是树的一条边，则 \(v\) 称为 \(u\) 的子节点， \(u\) 称为 \(v\) 的父节点。在树中，如果从节点 \(u\) 到节点 \(v\) 存在非空行走，则 \(v\) 是 \(u\) 的后代节点(descendant)。没有子节点的节点称为树的叶节点或终端节点。
\end{tcolorbox}

We represent trees graphically with the root at the top and edges drawn downwards from a parent to a child.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们以图形方式表示树，根节点位于顶部，边从父节点向下绘制到子节点。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 4.1. In a tree, the unique walk from the root to a node is a path. Every node has a unique parent, except for the root which has no parent.

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理4.1. 在树中，从根节点到节点的唯一行走是一条路径。每个节点都有唯一的父节点，除了根节点没有父节点。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. If the walk \({u}_{0},{u}_{1},\ldots ,{u}_{m}\) from the root \({u}_{0}\) to the node \({u}_{m}\) had a repeated node \({u}_{i} = {u}_{j}\) for some \(i < j\) , we could remove the nodes \({u}_{i + 1},\ldots ,{u}_{j}\) from the walk and obtain a second walk from \({u}_{0}\) to \({u}_{m}\) , which is not allowed. Hence, the walk has no repeated nodes and is therefore a path.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。如果从根节点 \({u}_{0}\) 到节点 \({u}_{m}\) 的行走 \({u}_{0},{u}_{1},\ldots ,{u}_{m}\) 存在某个 \(i < j\) 使得有重复节点 \({u}_{i} = {u}_{j}\) ，我们可以从该行走中移除节点 \({u}_{i + 1},\ldots ,{u}_{j}\) ，并得到从 \({u}_{0}\) 到 \({u}_{m}\) 的第二条行走路径，这是不允许的。因此，该行走没有重复节点，所以它是一条路径。
\end{tcolorbox}

Consider a tree node \(u\) which is not the root, and let \({vu}\) be the last edge on the unique path from the root to \(u\) . If there was a second edge \({wu}\) with \(w \neq  v\) , then the path from the root to \(w\) extended by \(u\) would give a second path from the root to \(u\) , which is not allowed. Hence, \(v\) is the unique parent of \(u\) . If the root had a parent \(v\) , the path from the root to \(v\) could be continued to a walk from the root to itself, which would be a second walk in addition to the empty path that consists of a single node. Again, this is not allowed.

\begin{tcolorbox}
考虑一个非根节点的树节点 \(u\) ，设 \({vu}\) 是从根节点到 \(u\) 的唯一路径上的最后一条边。如果存在第二条边 \({wu}\) 满足 \(w \neq  v\) ，那么从根节点到 \(w\) 再经过 \(u\) 的路径将构成从根节点到 \(u\) 的第二条路径，这是不允许的。因此， \(v\) 是 \(u\) 的唯一父节点。如果根节点有一个父节点 \(v\) ，从根节点到 \(v\) 的路径可以继续延伸为从根节点到其自身的一条行走路径，这将是除了由单个节点组成的空路径之外的第二条行走路径。同样，这也是不允许的。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item For a decision node \(u\) , every edge \({uv}\) to a child \(v\) of \(u\) is assigned a different move. These moves are called the moves at \(u\) .
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 对于一个决策节点 \(u\) ，从该节点到其子节点 \(v\) 的每条边 \({uv}\) 都被赋予一个不同的行动。这些行动被称为在节点 \(u\) 处的行动。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item For a chance node \(u\) , the edges \({uv}\) to the children \(v\) of \(u\) are assigned probabilities (that is, nonnegative reals that sum to 1).
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 对于一个机会节点 \(u\) ，从该节点到其子节点 \(v\) 的边 \({uv}\) 被赋予概率(即非负实数且其和为 1)。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Every leaf of the game tree has an \(N\) -tuple of real-valued payoffs, one for each player.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 博弈树的每个叶节点(leaf)都有一个 \(N\) 元实值收益向量，每个玩家对应一个收益值。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

The game tree, with its decision nodes, moves, chance probabilities and payoffs, is known to the players, and defines the game completely. The game is played by starting at the root. At a decision node, the respective player chooses a move, which determines the next node. At a chance node, the move is made randomly according to the given probabilities. Play ends when a leaf is reached, where all players receive their payoffs.

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈树及其决策节点、行动、机会概率和收益情况对玩家是已知的，并且完全定义了该博弈。博弈从根节点开始进行。在决策节点处，相应的玩家选择一个行动，该行动决定了下一个节点。在机会节点处，根据给定的概率随机进行行动。当到达叶节点时，博弈结束，此时所有玩家获得他们的收益。
\end{tcolorbox}

Players are interested in maximizing their own payoffs. If the outcome of playing the game is random, then the players are assumed to be interested in maximizing their expected payoffs.

\begin{tcolorbox}
玩家们关心的是最大化他们自己的收益。如果博弈的结果是随机的，那么假设玩家们关心的是最大化他们的期望效用(expected payoff)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Background material: Expected value of a finite random variable}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{背景材料(background material):有限随机变量的期望值(expected value)}
\end{tcolorbox}

A random variable \(U\) takes real values that depend on a random event. Let \(U\) take finitely many values \({u}_{1},\ldots ,{u}_{k}\) that occur with probabilities \({p}_{1},\ldots ,{p}_{k}\) , respectively. Then the expected value or expectation of \(U\) is defined as

\begin{tcolorbox}
随机变量 \(U\) 取依赖于随机事件的实数值。设 \(U\) 取有限个值 \({u}_{1},\ldots ,{u}_{k}\) ，这些值分别以概率 \({p}_{1},\ldots ,{p}_{k}\) 出现。那么 \(U\) 的期望值或期望定义为
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathbb{E}\left\lbrack  U\right\rbrack   = {p}_{1}{u}_{1} + \cdots  + {p}_{k}{u}_{k}. \tag{4.1}
\]

Because the probabilities \({p}_{1},\ldots ,{p}_{k}\) sum to 1, the expected value is the weighted average of the values \({u}_{i}\) with their probabilities \({p}_{i}\) as weights.

\begin{tcolorbox}
因为概率 \({p}_{1},\ldots ,{p}_{k}\) 之和为 1，所以期望值是以概率 \({p}_{i}\) 为权重的 \({u}_{i}\) 值的加权平均值。
\end{tcolorbox}

As a special case, if the random variable is deterministic and takes only a single value \({u}_{1}\) (with probability \({p}_{1} = 1\) ), then that value is the expected value, \(\mathbb{E}\left\lbrack  U\right\rbrack   = {u}_{1}.\)

\begin{tcolorbox}
作为一种特殊情况，如果随机变量是确定性的，并且只取单个值 \({u}_{1}\) (概率为 \({p}_{1} = 1\) )，那么该值就是期望值 \(\mathbb{E}\left\lbrack  U\right\rbrack   = {u}_{1}.\)
\end{tcolorbox}

In Figure 4.1, the expected payoff to player I after the chance move is \(\frac{1}{3}0 + \frac{2}{3}3 = 2\) , and to player II it is \(\frac{1}{3}{15} + \frac{2}{3}0 = 5\) . In this game, the chance node could therefore be replaced by a leaf with payoff 2 for player I and payoff 5 for player II. These expected payoffs reflect the players' preferences for random outcomes; Chapter 5 discusses in detail the assumptions that underly the use of such payoffs.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在图 4.1 中，机会行动之后玩家 I 的期望效用(expected payoff)是 \(\frac{1}{3}0 + \frac{2}{3}3 = 2\) ，玩家 II 的期望效用是 \(\frac{1}{3}{15} + \frac{2}{3}0 = 5\) 。在这个博弈中，机会节点因此可以用一个叶节点(leaf)来代替，该叶节点给玩家 I 的收益为 2，给玩家 II 的收益为 5。这些期望效用反映了玩家对随机结果的偏好(preference)；第 5 章详细讨论了使用此类收益的假设条件。
\end{tcolorbox}

In a two-player game, we normally name the players I and II and refer to player I as "he" and to player II as "she". Moves of player I are typically in upper case and of player II in lower case.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在两人博弈中，我们通常将玩家命名为玩家 I 和玩家 II，并将玩家 I 称为“他”，将玩家 II 称为“她”。玩家 I 的行动通常用大写字母表示，玩家 II 的行动通常用小写字母表示。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 4.2 demonstrates the tree property that every node is reached by a unique path from the root. This fails to hold in the left picture (a) for the middle leaf. Such a structure may make sense in a game. For example, the figure could represent two people in a pub where first player I chooses to pay for the drinks (move \(P\) ) or to accept (move \(A\) ) that the first round is paid by player II. In the second round, player II may then decide to either accept not to pay (move \(a\) ) or to pay(p). Then the players may only care how often, but not when, they have paid a round, with the middle payoff pair(1,1)for two possible "histories" of moves. The tree property prohibits states of play with more than one history, because the history is represented by the unique path from the root. Even if certain differences in the game history do not matter, these states of play are distinguished, mostly as a matter of mathematical convenience. The correct representation of the above game is shown in the right picture, Figure 4.2(b).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.2展示了博弈树(game tree)的一个特性，即从根节点出发，每个节点都有唯一的路径可达。在左图(a)中，中间的叶节点(leaf)不满足这一特性。这样的结构在博弈中可能是合理的。例如，该图可以表示酒吧里的两个人，首先玩家I可以选择为酒水买单(行动 \(P\) )，或者接受(行动 \(A\) )由玩家II支付第一轮的费用。在第二轮，玩家II可以决定要么接受不买单(行动 \(a\) )，要么买单(p)。然后玩家可能只关心他们买单的次数，而不关心买单的时间，中间的收益对(1,1)对应着两种可能的行动“历史”。博弈树的特性禁止出现具有多个历史的游戏状态，因为历史是由从根节点出发的唯一路径表示的。即使博弈历史中的某些差异无关紧要，这些游戏状态也会被区分开来，这主要是为了数学处理的方便。上述博弈的正确表示如图4.2(b)右图所示。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_93_391_218_1138_465_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 4.2 The left picture (a) is not a tree because the leaf where both players receive payoff 1 is reachable by two different paths. The correct game tree representation is shown on the right in (b).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.2 左图(a)不是一棵博弈树，因为两个玩家收益都为1的叶节点可以通过两条不同的路径到达。正确的博弈树表示如右图(b)所示。
\end{tcolorbox}

Game trees are also called extensive games with perfect information. Perfect information means that a player always knows the state of play and therefore the complete history of play up to then. Game trees can be enhanced with an additional structure that represents "imperfect information", which is the topic of Chapter 10. Imperfect information is required to represent simultaneous moves. In the game trees considered in the present chapter, players move sequentially and observe each other's moves.

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈树也被称为具有完美信息(perfect information)的扩展式博弈。完美信息意味着玩家总是知道游戏的状态，因此也知道到那时为止的完整游戏历史。博弈树可以通过额外的结构来表示“不完美信息”，这是第10章的主题。不完美信息用于表示同时行动。在本章所考虑的博弈树中，玩家按顺序行动并观察彼此的行动。
\end{tcolorbox}

Note how game trees differ from the combinatorial games in Chapter 1:

\begin{tcolorbox}
注意博弈树与第1章中的组合博弈有何不同:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item A combinatorial game can be described very compactly, in particular when it is given as a sum of games. For general game trees, such game sums are usually not considered.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 组合博弈可以非常简洁地描述，特别是当它表示为博弈之和(game sum)时。对于一般的博弈树，通常不考虑这种博弈之和。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item The "rules" in a game tree are much more flexible: more than two players and chance moves are allowed, players do not have to alternate, and payoffs do not just stand for "win" or "lose".
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 博弈树中的“规则”更加灵活:允许有两个以上的玩家和随机行动，玩家不必轮流行动，收益也不只是代表“赢”或“输”。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item The flexibility of game trees comes at a price, though: The game description is much longer than a combinatorial game. For example, a simple instance of Nim may require a huge game tree. Regularities like the mex rule do not apply to general game trees.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 然而，博弈树的灵活性是有代价的:博弈的描述比组合博弈长得多。例如，一个简单的尼姆游戏(Nim)可能需要一个巨大的博弈树。像mex规则(mex rule)这样的规律并不适用于一般的博弈树。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{4.3 Backward Induction}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{4.3 逆向归纳法(backward induction)}
\end{tcolorbox}

Which moves should the players choose in a game tree? "Optimal" play should maximize a player's payoff. This can be decided irrespective of other players' actions when the player is the last player to move. In the game in Figure 4.3(a), player II maximizes her payoff by move \(r\) . Going backward in time, player I makes his move \(T\) or \(B\) at the root of the game tree, where he will receive either 1 or 2, assuming the described future behavior of player II. Consequently, he will choose \(B\) , and play ends with payoff 2 to each player. These chosen moves are shown in Figure 4.3(b) with arrows on the edges; similar to the boxes that we put around best-response payoffs in a strategic-form game, this is additional information to help the analysis of the game and not part of the game specification.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在博弈树中，玩家应该选择哪些行动呢？“最优”行动应该使玩家的收益最大化。当玩家是最后行动的玩家时，这可以不考虑其他玩家的行动来决定。在图4.3(a)的博弈中，玩家II通过行动 \(r\) 使自己的收益最大化。逆向推导，玩家I在博弈树的根节点选择行动 \(T\) 或 \(B\) ，假设玩家II按上述方式行动，他将获得1或2的收益。因此，他会选择 \(B\) ，游戏结束时每个玩家的收益为2。这些选择的行动在图4.3(b)中用边上的箭头表示；类似于我们在战略式博弈中给最优反应收益加上的方框，这是帮助分析博弈的额外信息，而不是博弈规范的一部分。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_94_285_533_1056_463_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 4.3 A two-player game tree (a) with backward induction moves (b).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.3 一个两人博弈树(a)及逆向归纳法行动(b)。
\end{tcolorbox}

This process is called backward induction: Starting with the decision nodes closest to the leaves, a player's move is chosen that maximizes that player's payoff at the node. In general, a move is chosen in this way for each decision node provided all subsequent moves have already been decided. Eventually, this will determine a move for every decision node, and hence for the entire game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这个过程称为逆向归纳法:从最接近叶节点的决策节点开始，为每个决策节点选择一个能使该节点上玩家收益最大化的行动。一般来说，只要所有后续行动都已经确定，就以这种方式为每个决策节点选择一个行动。最终，这将为每个决策节点确定一个行动，从而为整个博弈确定行动。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_94_203_1399_672_624_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 4.4 The game tree in Figure 4.1 with the chance move replaced by its expected payoffs, and backward induction moves that depend on player II's choice between \(b\) (white arrow, then two possible choices \(X\) or \(Y\) by player I) and \(c\) (black arrow, unique choice \(X\) by player \(\mathrm{I}\) ).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.4 图4.1中的博弈树，随机行动被其期望效用(expected payoff)所取代，逆向归纳法行动取决于玩家II在 \(b\) (白色箭头，然后玩家I有两种可能的选择 \(X\) 或 \(Y\) )和 \(c\) (黑色箭头，玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 唯一的选择 \(X\) )之间的选择。
\end{tcolorbox}

The move selected by backward induction is not necessarily unique, if there is more than one move that gives maximal payoff to the player. This may influence the choice of moves earlier in the tree. In the game in Figure 4.4, backward induction chooses either move \(b\) or move \(c\) for player II, both of which give her payoff 5 (which is an expected payoff for move \(b\) in the original game in Figure 4.1) that exceeds her payoff 4 for move \(a\) . At the rightmost node, player I chooses \(Q\) . This determines the preceding move \(d\) by player II which gives her the higher payoff 3 as opposed to 2 (via move \(Q\) ). In turn, this means that player I, when choosing between \(X,Y\) , or \(Z\) at the root of the game tree, will get payoff 2 for \(Y\) and payoff 1 for \(Z\) . The payoff when he chooses \(X\) depends on the choice of player II: if that is \(b\) , then player I gets 2, and he can choose either \(X\) or \(Y\) , both of which give him maximal payoff 2. These choices are shown with white arrows in Figure 4.4. If player II chooses \(c\) , however, then the payoff to player I is 4 when choosing \(X\) , so this is the unique optimal move.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果有不止一种行动能为玩家带来最大效用，那么逆向归纳法(backward induction)所选择的行动不一定是唯一的。这可能会影响博弈树(game tree)中早期的行动选择。在图4.4的博弈中，逆向归纳法为玩家II选择行动 \(b\) 或行动 \(c\) ，这两种行动都能为她带来效用5(这是图4.1原始博弈中行动 \(b\) 的期望效用(expected payoff))，超过了她选择行动 \(a\) 时的效用4。在最右侧的节点(node)，玩家I选择 \(Q\) 。这决定了玩家II的前一个行动 \(d\) ，该行动为她带来了更高的效用3，而不是2(通过行动 \(Q\) )。反过来，这意味着玩家I在博弈树的根节点选择 \(X,Y\) 或 \(Z\) 时，选择 \(Y\) 能得到效用2，选择 \(Z\) 能得到效用1。他选择 \(X\) 时的效用取决于玩家II的选择:如果玩家II选择 \(b\) ，那么玩家I得到2，他可以选择 \(X\) 或 \(Y\) ，这两种选择都能为他带来最大效用2。这些选择在图4.4中用白色箭头表示。然而，如果玩家II选择 \(c\) ，那么玩家I选择 \(X\) 时的效用是4，所以这是唯一的最优行动。
\end{tcolorbox}

To summarize, the possible combinations of moves that can arise in Figure 4.1 by backward induction are, by listing the moves for each player: \(\left( {{XQ},{bd}}\right) ,\left( {{XQ},{cd}}\right)\) , and(YQ, bd). Note that \(Q\) and \(d\) are always chosen, but that \(Y\) can only be chosen in combination with the move \(b\) by player II.

\begin{tcolorbox}
综上所述，通过逆向归纳法(backward induction)在图4.1中可能出现的行动组合，按每个玩家的行动列出为: \(\left( {{XQ},{bd}}\right) ,\left( {{XQ},{cd}}\right)\) 和(YQ, bd)。请注意， \(Q\) 和 \(d\) 总是会被选择，但 \(Y\) 只能与玩家II的行动 \(b\) 组合选择。
\end{tcolorbox}

We do not try to argue that player II "should not" choose \(b\) because of the "risk" that player I might choose \(Y\) . This would be a "refinement" of backward induction that we do not pursue.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们并不试图争辩说，由于参与者I可能选择 \(b\) 的“风险”，参与者II“不应该”选择 \(Y\) 。这将是我们不探讨的逆向归纳法(backward induction)的一种“精炼”。
\end{tcolorbox}

The moves determined by backward induction are therefore, in general, not unique, and possibly interdependent.

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此，一般来说，逆向归纳法(backward induction)所确定的行动并非唯一，并且可能相互依赖。
\end{tcolorbox}

Backward induction gives a unique recommendation to each player if there is always only one move that gives maximal payoff. This applies to generic games. A generic game is a game where the payoff parameters are real numbers with no special dependency between them (like two payoffs being equal). In particular, it should be allowed to replace them with values nearby. For example, the payoffs may be given in some practical scenario which has some "noise" that effects the precise value of each payoff. Then two such payoffs are equal with probability zero, and so this case can be disregarded. In generic games, the optimal move is always unique, so that backward induction gives a unique result.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果总是只有一种行动能带来最大效用，那么逆向归纳法(backward induction)会为每个参与者提供唯一的建议。这适用于通有博弈(generic game)。通有博弈是指效用参数为实数且它们之间没有特殊依赖关系(如两个效用相等)的博弈。特别是，应该允许用附近的值替换它们。例如，效用可能是在某个存在影响每个效用精确值的“噪声”的实际场景中给出的。那么，两个这样的效用相等的概率为零，因此这种情况可以忽略不计。在通有博弈中，最优行动总是唯一的，因此逆向归纳法(backward induction)会给出唯一的结果。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{4.4 Strategies in Game Trees}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{4.4 博弈树(game tree)中的策略}
\end{tcolorbox}

In a game in strategic form, each player's strategies are assumed as given. For a game tree, a strategy is a derived concept. The best way to think of a strategy in a game tree is as a plan of action for every decision node.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在策略形式(strategic form)的博弈中，每个参与者的策略被视为给定的。对于博弈树(game tree)，策略是一个派生概念。将博弈树(game tree)中的策略视为每个决策节点(node)的行动计划是最好的理解方式。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 4.3. In a game tree, a strategy of a player specifies a move for every decision node of that player.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义4.3. 在博弈树(game tree)中，参与者的策略为该参与者的每个决策节点(node)指定一个行动。
\end{tcolorbox}

Hence, if a player has \(k\) decision nodes, a strategy of that player is a \(k\) -tuple of moves. For simplicity of notation, we often write down a strategy as a list of moves, one for each decision node of the player (and not as a comma-separated tuple surrounded by parentheses). In the game tree in Figure 4.1, the strategies of player I are \({XP},{XQ},{YP},{YQ},{ZP},{ZQ}\) . The strategies of player II are \({ad},{ae},{bd}\) , be, \({cd},{ce}\) . When specifying a strategy in that way, this must be done with respect to a fixed order of the decision nodes in the tree, in order to identify each move uniquely. This matters when a move name appears more than once, for example in Figure 4.2(b). In that tree, the strategies of player II are \({aa},{ap},{pa},{pp}\) , with the understanding that the first move refers to the left decision node and the second move to the right decision node of player II.

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此，如果一个参与者有 \(k\) 个决策节点(node)，那么该参与者的策略就是一个由 \(k\) 个行动组成的元组。为了符号表示的简便，我们通常将策略写成一个行动列表，每个行动对应参与者的一个决策节点(node)(而不是用括号括起来、用逗号分隔的元组)。在图4.1的博弈树(game tree)中，参与者I的策略是 \({XP},{XQ},{YP},{YQ},{ZP},{ZQ}\) 。参与者II的策略是 \({ad},{ae},{bd}\) 、be、 \({cd},{ce}\) 。以这种方式指定策略时，必须按照树中决策节点(node)的固定顺序进行，以便唯一地识别每个行动。当一个行动名称出现多次时，这一点很重要，例如在图4.2(b)中。在该树中，参与者II的策略是 \({aa},{ap},{pa},{pp}\) ，要理解为第一个行动对应参与者II的左决策节点(node)，第二个行动对应右决策节点(node)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Backward induction defines a move for every decision node of the game tree, and therefore for every decision node of each player, which in turn gives a strategy for each player. The result of backward induction is therefore a strategy profile.

\begin{tcolorbox}
逆向归纳法(backward induction)为博弈树(game tree)的每个决策节点(node)定义一个行动，因此也为每个参与者的每个决策节点(node)定义一个行动，进而为每个参与者给出一个策略。因此，逆向归纳法(backward induction)的结果是一个策略组合。
\end{tcolorbox}

With a strategy for each player, we can define the strategic form of the game tree, which is determined as follows. Consider a strategy profile, and assume that players move according to their strategies. If there are no chance moves, their play leads to a unique leaf. If there are chance moves, a strategy profile may lead to a probability distribution on the leaves of the game tree, with resulting expected payoffs. For chance moves that occur earlier in the tree, the computation of expected payoffs is more complicated than in Figure 4.1; see Figure 4.8 for an example. In general, any strategy profile defines an expected payoff to each player; this includes the deterministic case where payoffs and expected payoffs conincide, as mentioned after (4.1).

\begin{tcolorbox}
有了每个参与者的策略，我们就可以定义博弈树(game tree)的策略形式(strategic form)，其确定方式如下。考虑一个策略组合，并假设参与者按照他们的策略行动。如果没有随机行动，他们的行动会导致一个唯一的叶节点(leaf)。如果有随机行动，一个策略组合可能会导致博弈树(game tree)的叶节点(leaf)上出现一个概率分布，从而产生期望效用(expected payoff)。对于在树中较早出现的随机行动，期望效用(expected payoff)的计算比图4.1中的情况更复杂；见图4.8的示例。一般来说，任何策略组合都会为每个参与者定义一个期望效用(expected payoff)；这包括如(4.1)之后所提到的效用和期望效用(expected payoff)一致的确定性情况。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 4.4. The strategic form of a game tree is defined by the set of strategies for each player according to Definition 4.3, and the expected payoff to each player resulting from each strategy profile.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义4.4. 博弈树(game tree)的策略形式(strategic form)由根据定义4.3为每个参与者设定的策略集以及每个策略组合为每个参与者带来的期望效用(expected payoff)来定义。
\end{tcolorbox}

We first consider the simple game tree in Figure 4.3, which is shown in Figure 4.5 together with its strategic form (already encountered in Figure 3.6), which is a \(2 \times  2\) game. In this game, each player has only one decision node, so a strategy is the same as a move. (In general, a strategy specifies several moves, so do not use the terms "strategy" and "move" interchangeably!) Note also that we write the payoff to player I at the top at each leaf of the game tree, but at the bottom left of a cell in the payoff table.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们首先考虑图4.3中的简单博弈树(game tree)，它与它的策略形式(已经在图3.6中见过)一起显示在图4.5中，这是一个 \(2 \times  2\) 博弈。在这个博弈中，每个参与者只有一个决策节点(node)，因此策略与行动相同。(一般来说，策略指定多个行动，因此不要将“策略”和“行动”这两个术语互换使用！)还要注意，我们在博弈树(game tree)的每个叶节点(leaf)的顶部写出参与者I的效用，但在效用表中一个单元格的左下角写出。
\end{tcolorbox}

The game tree in Figure 4.3 has only three leaves (and no chance moves) but the strategic form has four cells. Therefore, the payoffs for one of the leaves appear twice, namely for the strategy pairs(T, l)and(T, r). The reason is that the leftmost leaf is reached immediately after move \(T\) of player \(\mathrm{I}\) , and then the move \(l\) or \(r\) of player II does not matter.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.3中的博弈树(game tree)只有三个叶节点(leaf)(且没有随机行动)，但策略形式有四个单元格。因此，其中一个叶节点(leaf)的效用出现了两次，即对于策略对(T, l)和(T, r)。原因是，在参与者 \(\mathrm{I}\) 采取行动 \(T\) 后立即到达最左边的叶节点(leaf)，然后参与者II的行动 \(l\) 或 \(r\) 就无关紧要了。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_97_422_221_1083_459_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 4.5 Game tree (a) of the Threat game with backward induction moves, and its strategic form (b) with best responses. Note: In the game tree, the payoffs to player I are shown at the top, but in the strategic form they appear at the bottom left of each cell.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.5 带有逆向归纳法步骤的威胁博弈的博弈树(a)及其带有最优反应的策略形式(b)。注意:在博弈树中，玩家I的收益显示在顶部，但在策略形式中，它们出现在每个单元格的左下角。
\end{tcolorbox}

The game tree and the strategic form specify the same game, but are played differently. In the game tree, play proceeds sequentially. In the strategic form, the players choose their strategies simultaneously, and then they play according to these "plans of action".

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈树和策略形式描述的是同一个博弈，但玩法不同。在博弈树中，博弈按顺序进行。在策略形式中，玩家同时选择他们的策略，然后根据这些“行动计划”进行博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

In the game in Figure 4.5, backward induction specifies the unique strategy profile(B, r), which is also an equilibrium (which holds generally, see Theorem 4.6 below). The game has a second equilibrium(T, l), which can also be seen from the game tree: If player II chooses \(l\) , then player I’s best response is \(T\) . Player II can threaten this suboptimal move because she never has to make it when player I chooses \(T\) , as he is incentivized to do. The game is also known as the Threat game for that reason. Player II gets a higher payoff in this equilibrium(T, r). We will distinguish equilibria that are obtained by backward induction and those that are not, as discussed in Section 4.6.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在图4.5的博弈中，逆向归纳法确定了唯一的策略组合(B，r)，这也是一个均衡(一般情况下都成立，见下面的定理4.6)。该博弈还有第二个均衡(T，l)，这也可以从博弈树中看出:如果玩家II选择 \(l\) ，那么玩家I的最优反应是 \(T\) 。玩家II可以威胁采取这种次优行动，因为当玩家I选择 \(T\) 时(他有动机这么做)，她永远不必采取这一行动。出于这个原因，该博弈也被称为威胁博弈。在这个均衡(T，r)中，玩家II获得了更高的收益。我们将区分通过逆向归纳法得到的均衡和未通过逆向归纳法得到的均衡，如4.6节所述。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 4.6 shows the strategic form of the game tree in Figure 4.1. In that game, players I and II both have two decision nodes with three and two moves, respectively, and therefore \(3 \times  2\) strategies each. The strategic form has 36 cells, which have many repetitions because the game tree has only eight (or, after contracting the two chance outcomes as in Figure 4.4, seven) leaves.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.6显示了图4.1中博弈树的策略形式。在该博弈中，玩家I和玩家II分别有两个决策节点，分别有三种和两种行动，因此每个玩家有 \(3 \times  2\) 种策略。策略形式有36个单元格，其中有很多重复，因为博弈树只有八个叶节点(或者，如图4.4那样合并两个随机结果后，有七个叶节点)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{4.5 Reduced Strategies}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{4.5 精简策略}
\end{tcolorbox}

In Figure 4.6, the two rows \({XP}\) and \({XQ}\) have identical payoffs for both players, and similarly the two rows \({YP}\) and \({YQ}\) . The reason is that the strategies \({XP}\) and \({XQ}\) specify that player I chooses \(X\) at his first decision node and either \(P\) or \(Q\) at his second decision node. However, after choosing \(X\) , the second decision node cannot be reached. The second decision node can only be reached if player I chooses \(Z\) as his first move, and then the two strategies \({ZP}\) and \({ZQ}\) may lead to different leaves (if player II chooses \(e\) , as in her three strategies \({ae},{be}\) , or \({ce}\) ). Because player I himself chooses between \(X,Y\) , or \(Z\) , it makes sense to replace both strategies \({XP}\) and \({XQ}\) by a less specific "plan of action" \(X *\) that precribes only move \(X\) . Similarly, the two strategies \({YP}\) and \({YQ}\) are collapsed into \(Y\) *. We always use the star " \(*\) " as a placeholder. It stands for any unspecified move at the respective unreachable decision node, to identify the node where this move is left unspecified.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在图4.6中，两行 \({XP}\) 和 \({XQ}\) 对两个玩家来说收益相同，同样，两行 \({YP}\) 和 \({YQ}\) 也是如此。原因是策略 \({XP}\) 和 \({XQ}\) 规定玩家I在他的第一个决策节点选择 \(X\) ，在他的第二个决策节点选择 \(P\) 或 \(Q\) 。然而，在选择 \(X\) 之后，无法到达第二个决策节点。只有当玩家I将 \(Z\) 作为他的第一步行动时，才能到达第二个决策节点，然后两种策略 \({ZP}\) 和 \({ZQ}\) 可能会导致不同的叶节点(如果玩家II选择 \(e\) ，就像她的三种策略 \({ae},{be}\) 或 \({ce}\) 那样)。因为玩家I自己在 \(X,Y\) 或 \(Z\) 之间进行选择，所以用一个不太具体的“行动计划” \(X *\) 来取代策略 \({XP}\) 和 \({XQ}\) 是有意义的，该“行动计划”只规定行动 \(X\) 。同样，两种策略 \({YP}\) 和 \({YQ}\) 合并为 \(Y\) *。我们总是使用星号“ \(*\) ”作为占位符。它代表在相应无法到达的决策节点上的任何未指定的行动，以确定该行动未被指定的节点。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.7\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_98_355_195_909_889_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 4.6 Strategic form of the extensive game in Figure 4.1. The less redundant reduced strategic form is shown in Figure 4.7 below.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.6 图4.1中扩展式博弈的策略形式。冗余较少的精简策略型如图4.7所示。
\end{tcolorbox}

Leaving moves at unreachable nodes unspecified in this manner defines a reduced strategy according to the following definition. Because the resulting expected payoffs remain uniquely defined, tabulating these reduced strategies and the payoff for the resulting reduced strategy profiles gives the reduced strategic form of the game. The reduced strategic form of the game tree of Figure 4.1 is shown in Figure 4.7, with indicated best-response payoffs.

\begin{tcolorbox}
以这种方式对无法到达的节点上的行动不做具体规定，根据以下定义确定了一个精简策略。由于由此产生的期望效用仍然是唯一确定的，将这些精简策略和由此产生的精简策略组合的收益制成表格，就得到了博弈的精简策略型。图4.1中博弈树的精简策略型如图4.7所示，并标明了最优反应收益。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 4.5. In a game tree, a reduced strategy of a player specifies a move for every decision node of that player, except for those decision nodes that are unreachable due to an earlier own move, where the move is replaced by "*". A reduced strategy profile is a tuple of reduced strategies, one for each player of the game. The reduced strategic form of a game tree lists all reduced strategies for each player, and tabulates the expected payoff to each player for each reduced strategy profile.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义4.5。在博弈树中，玩家的精简策略为该玩家的每个决策节点指定一个行动，但由于玩家自己先前的行动而无法到达的决策节点除外，在这些节点上，行动用“*”代替。精简策略组合是一个精简策略的元组，每个玩家对应一个。博弈树的精简策略型列出了每个玩家的所有精简策略，并将每个精简策略组合下每个玩家的期望效用制成表格。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.7\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_99_503_197_898_622_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 4.7 Reduced strategic form of the extensive game in Figure 4.1. The star * stands for an arbitrary move at the second decision node of player I, which is not reachable after move \(X\) or \(Y\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.7 图4.1中扩展式博弈的精简策略型。星号*代表玩家I在第二个决策节点上的任意行动，在行动 \(X\) 或 \(Y\) 之后无法到达该节点。
\end{tcolorbox}

The preceding definition generalizes Definitions 4.3 and 4.4. It is important that the only moves that are left unspecified are at decision nodes which are unreachable due to an earlier own move of the player. A reduced strategy must not disregard a move because another player may not move there, because that possibility cannot be excluded by looking only at the player's own moves. In the game tree in Figure 4.1, for example, no reduction is possible for the strategies of player II, because neither of her moves at one decision node precludes a move at her other decision node. Therefore, the reduced strategies of player II in that game are the same as her (unreduced, original) strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
上述定义对定义 4.3 和 4.4 进行了推广。重要的是，唯一未明确指定的行动是在由于玩家自身先前的行动而无法到达的决策节点处。一个精简策略不能因为另一个玩家可能不会在某个节点行动就忽略该行动，因为仅通过查看玩家自身的行动无法排除这种可能性。例如，在图 4.1 的博弈树中，玩家 II 的策略无法进行精简，因为她在一个决策节点的任何行动都不会排除她在另一个决策节点的行动。因此，在该博弈中玩家 II 的精简策略与她的(未精简的、原始的)策略相同。
\end{tcolorbox}

In Definition 4.5, reduced strategies are defined without any reference to payoffs, only by the structure of the game tree. One could derive the reduced strategic form in Figure 4.7 from the strategic form in Figure 4.6 by identifying the strategies that have identical payoffs for both players and replacing them by a single reduced strategy. Some authors define the reduced strategic form in terms of this elimination of duplicate strategies in the strategic form. (Sometimes, dominated strategies are also eliminated when defining the reduced strategic form.) We define the reduced strategic form without any reference to payoffs because strategies may have identical payoffs by accident, due to special payoffs at the leaves of the game (this does not occur in generic games). Moreover, we prefer that reduced strategies refer to the structure of the game tree, not to some relationship of the payoffs, which is a different aspect of the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在定义 4.5 中，精简策略的定义与收益无关，仅取决于博弈树的结构。可以通过识别对两个玩家都有相同收益的策略，并用一个单一的精简策略替换它们，从图 4.6 的策略形式推导出图 4.7 的精简策略型。一些作者根据策略形式中重复策略的消除来定义精简策略型。(有时，在定义精简策略型时也会消除(被占优) 策略。)我们在定义精简策略型时不考虑收益，因为由于博弈叶节点的特殊收益，策略可能偶然具有相同的收益(这在通有博弈中不会发生)。此外，我们更倾向于让精简策略参考博弈树的结构，而不是收益的某种关系，这是博弈的不同方面。
\end{tcolorbox}

The following is a recipe for filling, reasonably quickly, the payoff table for the reduced strategic form (or, if one wishes, the full strategic form) of a game tree like Figure 4.1:

\begin{tcolorbox}
以下是一个相对快速地填写类似图 4.1 这样的博弈树的精简策略型(或者，如果愿意，完整策略型)收益表的方法:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item For each player, choose a fixed order of the decision nodes, where nodes that come earlier in the tree should come first. If the player has \(k\) decision nodes, a (reduced) strategy has \(k\) "slots" to define a move, one for each decision node.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 为每个玩家选择一个固定的决策节点顺序，树中较早出现的节点应排在前面。如果玩家有 \(k\) 个决策节点，一个(精简)策略有 \(k\) 个“空位”来定义一个行动，每个决策节点对应一个空位。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Generate all (reduced) strategies. A reduced strategy defines for each slot a move, or an unspecified move " \(*\) " if the respective decision node is unreachable due to an earlier move of the same player. For two players, list the (reduced) strategies of player I as rows and of player II as columns.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 生成所有(精简)策略。一个精简策略为每个空位定义一个行动，如果相应的决策节点由于同一玩家先前的行动而无法到达，则定义为未指定的行动“ \(*\) ”。对于两个玩家，将玩家 I 的(精简)策略列为行，玩家 II 的(精简)策略列为列。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item For each leaf of the game tree, consider the path from the root to the leaf. Assume first that there are no chance moves on that path. For each player, consider the moves of that player on that path. For example, the leftmost leaf in Figure 4.1 with payoffs(3,4)has move \(X\) for player I and move \(a\) for player II.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 对于博弈树的每个叶节点，考虑从根节点到该叶节点的路径。首先假设该路径上没有随机行动。对于每个玩家，考虑该玩家在该路径上的行动。例如，图 4.1 中最左边的叶节点，收益为(3,4)，玩家 I 的行动为 \(X\) ，玩家 II 的行动为 \(a\) 。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item For each player, identify all strategies that agree with the moves on the path to the leaf. For the leftmost leaf in Figure 4.1 and the reduced strategic form in Figure 4.7, these strategies are given by the reduced strategy \(X *\) for player \(\mathrm{I}\) (or the unreduced strategies \({XP}\) and \({XQ}\) in Figure 4.6), and the strategies \({ad}\) and ae for player II. For all these strategy pairs, enter the payoff pair, here (3,4), in the respective cells. Repeat this for each leaf.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 对于每个玩家，识别所有与到达叶节点路径上的行动一致的策略。对于图 4.1 中最左边的叶节点和图 4.7 中的精简策略型，这些策略对于玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 由精简策略 \(X *\) 给出(或者在图 4.6 中是未精简策略 \({XP}\) 和 \({XQ}\) )，对于玩家 II 是策略 \({ad}\) 和 ae 。对于所有这些策略对，将收益对(这里是(3,4))填入相应的单元格。对每个叶节点重复此操作。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

As another example, the leaf with payoffs(2,3)that directly follows move \(Y\) is reached by move \(Y\) of player I (which defines row \(Y *\) in the reduced strategic form in Figure 4.7), and no moves of player II, so all strategies of player II agree with these moves; that is, the entire row \(Y *\) is filled with the payoff pair(2,3).

\begin{tcolorbox}
再举一个例子，直接跟随行动 \(Y\) 且收益为(2,3)的叶节点，是由玩家 I 的行动 \(Y\) 到达的(该行动在图 4.7 的精简策略型中定义了行 \(Y *\) )，玩家 II 没有行动，所以玩家 II 的所有策略都与这些行动一致；也就是说，整行 \(Y *\) 都填入收益对(2,3)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item If there are chance moves on the path from the root to the leaf, let \(p\) be the product of chance probabilities on that path. Multiply each payoff at the leaf with \(p\) , and add the result to the current payoffs in the respective cells that are determined as before (assuming the payoffs in each cell are initially zero). For an example see Figure 4.8.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 如果从根节点到叶节点的路径上有随机行动，令 \(p\) 为该路径上随机概率的乘积。将叶节点的每个收益乘以 \(p\) ，并将结果加到如前所述确定的相应单元格的当前收益中(假设每个单元格的初始收益为零)。示例见图 4.8。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

This recipe for creating the strategic form of a game tree is much less laborious than considering each strategy pair and identifying to which leaf it leads (or leaves, in case there are chance moves).

\begin{tcolorbox}
这种创建博弈树策略型的方法比考虑每对策略并确定它通向哪个叶节点(或者，如果有随机行动，则是哪些叶节点)要省力得多。
\end{tcolorbox}

However, it should be clear that the strategic form is typically substantially larger than the game tree. It is therefore often easier to directly analyze the game tree rather than the strategic form.

\begin{tcolorbox}
然而，应该清楚的是，策略型通常比博弈树大得多。因此，直接分析博弈树通常比分析策略型更容易。
\end{tcolorbox}

Strategies are combinations of moves, so for every additional decision node of a player, each move at that node can be combined with move combinations already considered. The number of move combinations therefore grows exponentially with

\begin{tcolorbox}
策略是行动的组合，因此对于玩家的每个额外决策节点，该节点的每个行动都可以与已经考虑过的行动组合相结合。因此，行动组合的数量会随着
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_101_390_219_1139_459_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 4.8 Creating the strategic form by iterating through tree leaves when there are chance moves, which lead to expected payoffs.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.8 当存在导致期望效用的随机行动时，通过遍历博弈树的叶节点来创建策略形式。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_101_369_844_644_379_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 4.9 Game tree (without payoffs) with \(m\) moves \({C}_{1},\ldots ,{C}_{m}\) of player I at the root of the game tree. After each move \({C}_{i}\) , player II has two moves \({l}_{i}\) and \({r}_{i}\) . Player II has \({2}^{m}\) strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.9 博弈树(无效用值)，玩家I在博弈树的根节点有 \(m\) 个行动 \({C}_{1},\ldots ,{C}_{m}\) 。在玩家I的每次行动 \({C}_{i}\) 之后，玩家II有两个行动 \({l}_{i}\) 和 \({r}_{i}\) 。玩家II有 \({2}^{m}\) 个策略。
\end{tcolorbox}

the number of decision nodes of the player, because it is the product of the numbers of moves at each node. In the game in Figure 4.9, for example, where \(m\) possible initial moves of player I are followed each time by two possible moves of player II, the number of strategies of player II is \({2}^{m}\) . Moreover, this is also the number of reduced strategies of player II because no reduction is possible. This shows that even the reduced strategic form can be exponentially large in the size of the game tree. If a player's move is preceded by an own earlier move, the reduced strategic form is smaller because then that move can be left unspecified in any reduced strategy that does not choose the preceding own move.

\begin{tcolorbox}
玩家的决策节点数量，因为它是每个节点的行动数量的乘积。例如，在图4.9的博弈中，玩家I有 \(m\) 种可能的初始行动，每次之后玩家II都有两种可能的行动，玩家II的策略数量是 \({2}^{m}\) 。此外，这也是玩家II的精简策略的数量，因为无法进行精简。这表明，即使是精简策略型在博弈树的规模上也可能呈指数级增长。如果一个玩家的行动之前有自己的早期行动，那么精简策略型会更小，因为在任何不选择前面自己行动的精简策略中，该行动可以不被确定。
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\Rightarrow\) Exercise 4.4 is about reduced strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\Rightarrow\) 练习4.4是关于精简策略的。
\end{tcolorbox}

In the reduced strategic form, an equilibrium is defined, as in Definition 3.1, as a profile of reduced strategies, each of which is a best response to the others. In this context, we will for brevity sometimes refer to reduced strategies simply as "strategies". This is justified because, when looking at the reduced strategic form, the concepts of dominance and equilibrium can be applied directly to the reduced strategic form, for example to the table that defines a two-player game. Then "strategy" means simply a row or column of that table. The term "reduced strategy" is only relevant when referring to the game tree.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在精简策略型中，如同定义3.1一样，均衡被定义为一个精简策略的组合，其中每个策略都是对其他策略的最优反应。在这种情况下，为了简洁起见，我们有时会简单地将精简策略称为“策略”。这是合理的，因为在考虑精简策略型时，占优和均衡的概念可以直接应用于精简策略型，例如应用于定义两人博弈的表格。那么“策略”就简单地意味着该表格中的一行或一列。“精简策略”这个术语仅在提及博弈树时才相关。
\end{tcolorbox}

The equilibria in Figure 4.7 are identified as those pairs of (reduced) strategies that are best responses to each other, with both payoffs surrounded by a box in the respective cell of the table. These equilibria in reduced strategies are \(\left( {X*,{bd}}\right)\) , \(\left( {X*,{cd}}\right) ,\left( {X*,{ce}}\right)\) , and \(\left( {Y*,{bd}}\right)\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.7中的均衡被确定为那些(精简)策略对，它们彼此是最优反应，并且在表格的相应单元格中，两个效用值都被框起来。这些精简策略的均衡是 \(\left( {X*,{bd}}\right)\) 、 \(\left( {X*,{cd}}\right) ,\left( {X*,{ce}}\right)\) 和 \(\left( {Y*,{bd}}\right)\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{4.6 Subgame-Perfect Equilibrium (SPE)}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{4.6 子博弈完美均衡(SPE)}
\end{tcolorbox}

In this section, we consider the relationship between backward induction and equilibrium. The concept of equilibrium is based on the strategic form because it applies to a strategy profile. In a game tree, the strategies in that profile are assumed to be chosen by the players before play starts, and the concept of best response assumes that the other players play the moves in the strategy profile.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在本节中，我们考虑逆向归纳法和均衡之间的关系。均衡的概念基于策略形式，因为它适用于一个策略组合。在博弈树中，假设该组合中的策略是玩家在游戏开始前选择的，并且最优反应的概念假设其他玩家按照策略组合中的行动进行游戏。
\end{tcolorbox}

With the game tree as the given specification of the game, it is often desirable to keep its sequential interpretation. The strategies chosen by the players should therefore also express some "sequential rationality" as expressed by backward induction. That is, the moves in a strategy profile should be optimal for any part of the game, including subtrees that cannot be reached due to earlier moves, possibly by other players, like the little tree that starts with the decision node of player II in the Threat game in Figure 4.3(a).

\begin{tcolorbox}
以博弈树作为博弈的给定规范，通常希望保留其顺序解释。因此，玩家选择的策略也应该表达出逆向归纳法所表达的某种“顺序理性”。也就是说，策略组合中的行动对于博弈的任何部分都应该是最优的，包括由于早期行动(可能是其他玩家的行动)而无法到达的子树，就像图4.3(a)中威胁博弈中以玩家II的决策节点开始的小树一样。
\end{tcolorbox}

In a game tree, a subgame is any subtree of the game, given by a node of the tree as the root of the subtree and all its descendants. (Note: This definition of a subgame applies only to games with perfect information, which are the game trees considered so far. In extensive games with imperfect information, which we consider in Chapter 10, a subtree is a subgame only if all players know that they are in that subtree; see Section 10.10.) A strategy profile that defines an equilibrium for every subgame is called a subgame-perfect equilibrium or SPE.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在博弈树中，子博弈是博弈的任何子树，由树的一个节点作为子树的根节点及其所有后代节点给出。(注意:这个子博弈的定义仅适用于具有完美信息的博弈，这是我们目前考虑的博弈树。在第10章中我们将考虑的不完美信息扩展式博弈中，一个子树只有在所有玩家都知道他们处于该子树时才是一个子博弈；见第10.10节。)一个为每个子博弈定义均衡的策略组合被称为子博弈完美均衡或SPE。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Theorem 4.6. Backward induction defines an SPE.}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{定理4.6. 逆向归纳法定义了一个子博弈完美均衡。}
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Recall the process of backward induction: Starting with the nodes closest to the leaves, consider a decision node \(u\) , say, with the assumption that all moves at descendants of \(u\) have already been selected. Among the moves at \(u\) , select a move that maximizes the expected payoff to the player who moves at \(u\) . (Expected payoffs must be regarded if there are chance moves after \(u\) .) In that manner, a move is selected for every decision node, which determines an entire strategy profile.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。回顾逆向归纳法的过程:从最接近叶节点的节点开始，考虑一个决策节点 \(u\) ，假设 \(u\) 的所有后代节点的行动都已经被选择。在 \(u\) 的行动中，选择一个能使在 \(u\) 行动的玩家的期望效用最大化的行动。(如果在 \(u\) 之后有随机行动，则必须考虑期望效用。)以这种方式，为每个决策节点选择一个行动，从而确定一个完整的策略组合。
\end{tcolorbox}

We prove the theorem inductively (see Figure 4.10): Consider a non-terminal node \(u\) of the game tree, which may be a decision node (as in the backward induction process), or a chance node. Suppose that the moves at \(u\) are \({c}_{1},{c}_{2},\ldots ,{c}_{k}\) , which lead to subtrees \({T}_{1},{T}_{2},\ldots ,{T}_{k}\) of the game tree. If \(u\) is a chance node, then instead of the moves we have probabilities \({p}_{1},{p}_{2},\ldots ,{p}_{k}\) . Assume, as inductive hypothesis, that the moves selected so far define an SPE in each of these trees. (As the "base case" of the induction, this is certainly true for each subtree \({T}_{1},{T}_{2},\ldots ,{T}_{k}\) that is just a leaf of the tree; if all subtrees are leaves, then \(u\) is a "last" decision node considered first in the backward induction process.) The induction step is completed if one shows that, by selecting the move at \(u\) , one obtains an SPE for the subgame with root \(u\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们用归纳法证明该定理(见图4.10):考虑博弈树(game tree)的一个非终端节点(non-terminal node) \(u\) ，它可能是一个决策节点(如在逆向归纳法(backward induction)过程中)，也可能是一个机会节点。假设在节点 \(u\) 的行动为 \({c}_{1},{c}_{2},\ldots ,{c}_{k}\) ，这些行动会导向博弈树的子树(subtree) \({T}_{1},{T}_{2},\ldots ,{T}_{k}\) 。如果 \(u\) 是一个机会节点，那么我们有的不是行动，而是概率 \({p}_{1},{p}_{2},\ldots ,{p}_{k}\) 。作为归纳假设，假定到目前为止所选择的行动在每一棵子树中都定义了一个子博弈完美均衡(SPE)。(作为归纳的“基础情形”，对于每一棵只是树的叶节点(leaf)的子树 \({T}_{1},{T}_{2},\ldots ,{T}_{k}\) ，这肯定是成立的；如果所有子树都是叶节点，那么 \(u\) 就是逆向归纳法过程中首先考虑的“最后”决策节点。)如果能证明通过在节点 \(u\) 选择行动，可以得到以 \(u\) 为根节点的子博弈(subgame)的一个子博弈完美均衡，那么归纳步骤就完成了。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.8\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_103_399_219_1136_459_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 4.10 Induction step for proving Theorem 4.6 when the next node \(u\) belongs to chance (left) or to player \(i\) (right).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图4.10 当下一个节点 \(u\) 属于机会(左图)或属于参与者 \(i\) (右图)时，证明定理4.6的归纳步骤。
\end{tcolorbox}

First, suppose that \(u\) is a chance node, so that the next node is chosen according to the fixed probabilities specified in the game tree. Then backward induction does not select a move for \(u\) , and the inductive step holds trivially: For every player, the payoff in the subgame that starts at \(u\) is the expectation of the payoffs for the subgames \({T}_{l}\) weighted with the probabilities \({p}_{l}\) for \(1 \leq  l \leq  k\) , and if a player could improve on that payoff, she would have to do so by changing her moves within at least one subtree \({T}_{l}\) , which, by inductive hypothesis, she cannot.

\begin{tcolorbox}
首先，假设 \(u\) 是一个机会节点，这样下一个节点是根据博弈树中指定的固定概率来选择的。那么逆向归纳法不会为 \(u\) 选择行动，并且归纳步骤显然成立:对于每个参与者来说，从 \(u\) 开始的子博弈中的收益是子博弈 \({T}_{l}\) 的收益以概率 \({p}_{l}\) 对 \(1 \leq  l \leq  k\) 进行加权后的期望值，而且如果一个参与者能够提高该收益，她就必须通过改变至少一棵子树 \({T}_{l}\) 内的行动来实现，根据归纳假设，她无法做到这一点。
\end{tcolorbox}

Second, suppose that \(u\) is a decision node, let \(i\) be the player to move at \(u\) , and consider a player \(j\) other than player \(i\) . Again, for player \(j\) , the moves in the subgame that starts at \(u\) are completely specified. Irrespective of what move player \(i\) selects at \(u\) , player \(j\) cannot improve her payoff because that would mean she could improve her payoff already in some subtree \({T}_{l}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
其次，假设 \(u\) 是一个决策节点，设 \(i\) 是在节点 \(u\) 行动的参与者，并考虑除参与者 \(i\) 之外的另一个参与者 \(j\) 。同样，对于参与者 \(j\) 来说，从 \(u\) 开始的子博弈中的行动是完全确定的。无论参与者 \(i\) 在节点 \(u\) 选择什么行动，参与者 \(j\) 都无法提高她的收益，因为这意味着她已经可以在某棵子树 \({T}_{l}\) 中提高她的收益了。
\end{tcolorbox}

Third, consider player \(i\) who moves at \(u\) . Backward induction selects a move for \(u\) that is best for player \(i\) , given the remaining moves. Suppose player \(i\) could improve his payoff by choosing some move \({c}_{l}\) and additionally change his moves in the subtree \({T}_{l}\) . But the resulting improved payoff would only be the improved payoff in \({T}_{l}\) , that is, the player could already get a better payoff in \({T}_{l}\) itself. This contradicts the inductive assumption that the moves selected so far define an SPE for \({T}_{l}\) . This completes the induction.

\begin{tcolorbox}
第三，考虑在节点 \(u\) 行动的参与者 \(i\) 。逆向归纳法会为节点 \(u\) 选择一个在其余行动给定的情况下对参与者 \(i\) 来说最优的行动。假设参与者 \(i\) 可以通过选择某个行动 \({c}_{l}\) 并额外改变他在子树 \({T}_{l}\) 中的行动来提高他的收益。但由此得到的提高后的收益仅仅是子树 \({T}_{l}\) 中提高后的收益，也就是说，该参与者本来就可以在子树 \({T}_{l}\) 中获得更好的收益。这与到目前为止所选择的行动为子树 \({T}_{l}\) 定义了一个子博弈完美均衡的归纳假设相矛盾。这样就完成了归纳。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 4.6 has two important consequences. In backward induction, each move can be chosen deterministically, so that backward induction determines a profile of pure strategies. Theorem 4.6 therefore implies that game trees have equilibria, and it is not necessary to consider randomized strategies. Secondly, subgame-perfect equilibria exist. For game trees, we can use "SPE" synonymously with "strategy profile obtained by backward induction".

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理4.6有两个重要的推论。在逆向归纳法中，每个行动都可以确定性地选择，因此逆向归纳法确定了一个纯策略组合。因此，定理4.6意味着博弈树存在均衡(equilibrium)，而且没有必要考虑随机策略。其次，子博弈完美均衡是存在的。对于博弈树，我们可以将“子博弈完美均衡(SPE)”与“通过逆向归纳法得到的策略组合”同义使用。
\end{tcolorbox}

Corollary 4.7. Every game tree with perfect information has a pure-strategy equilibrium.

\begin{tcolorbox}
推论4.7:每个具有完美信息(perfect information)的博弈树都有一个纯策略均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

Corollary 4.8. Every game tree with perfect information has an SPE.

\begin{tcolorbox}
推论4.8:每个具有完美信息的博弈树都有一个子博弈完美均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

An SPE can only be described as a profile of unreduced (that is, fully specified) strategies. Recall that in the game in Figure 4.1, the SPE are \(\left( {{XQ},{bd}}\right) ,\left( {{XQ},{cd}}\right)\) , and(YQ, bd). The reduced strategic form of the game in Figure 4.7 shows the equilibria \(\left( {X*,{bd}}\right) ,\left( {X*,{cd}}\right)\) , and \(\left( {Y*,{bd}}\right)\) . However, we cannot call any of these an SPE because they leave the second move of player I unspecified, as indicated by the \(*\) symbol. In this case, replacing \(*\) by \(Q\) results in all cases in an SPE. The full strategies are necessary to determine if they define an equilibrium in every subgame. As seen in Figure 4.7, the game has, in addition, the equilibrium \(\left( {X*,{ce}}\right)\) . This is not subgame-perfect because it prescribes the move \(e\) , which is not part of an equilibrium in the subgame that starts with the decision node where player II chooses between \(d\) and \(e\) : Replacing \(*\) by \(P\) would not be the best response by player I to \(e\) in that subgame, and when replacing \(*\) by \(Q\) , player II’s best response would not be \(e\) . This property can only be seen in the game tree, not even in the unreduced strategic form in Figure 4.6, because it concerns an unreached part of the game tree due to the earlier move \(X\) of player \(\mathrm{I}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
子博弈完美均衡(SPE)只能被描述为未简化(即完全明确)策略的组合。回顾图4.1中的博弈，子博弈完美均衡是 \(\left( {{XQ},{bd}}\right) ,\left( {{XQ},{cd}}\right)\) 和(YQ, bd)。图4.7中博弈的精简策略型展示了均衡 \(\left( {X*,{bd}}\right) ,\left( {X*,{cd}}\right)\) 和 \(\left( {Y*,{bd}}\right)\) 。然而，我们不能将这些中的任何一个称为子博弈完美均衡，因为它们没有明确玩家I的第二步行动，如 \(*\) 符号所示。在这种情况下，在所有情况下将 \(*\) 替换为 \(Q\) 会得到一个子博弈完美均衡。要确定它们是否在每个子博弈中都定义了一个均衡，完整的策略是必要的。如图4.7所示，该博弈还有均衡 \(\left( {X*,{ce}}\right)\) 。这不是子博弈完美的，因为它规定了行动 \(e\) ，而这不是以玩家II在 \(d\) 和 \(e\) 之间进行选择的决策节点开始的子博弈中的均衡的一部分:在该子博弈中，将 \(*\) 替换为 \(P\) 不是玩家I对 \(e\) 的最优反应，并且当将 \(*\) 替换为 \(Q\) 时，玩家II的最优反应不会是 \(e\) 。这个性质只能在博弈树中看到，即使在图4.6的未简化策略形式中也看不到，因为它涉及到由于玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 更早的行动 \(X\) 而未到达的博弈树部分。
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\Rightarrow\) Exercise 4.3 is very instructive for understanding subgame-perfect equilibria. Exercise 4.5 studies a three-player game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\Rightarrow\) 练习4.3对于理解子博弈完美均衡非常有启发性。练习4.5研究了一个三人博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{4.7 Commitment Games}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{4.7 承诺博弈}
\end{tcolorbox}

The main difference between game trees and games in strategic form is that in a game tree the players move sequentially and are aware of any previous moves of the other players. In contrast, in a strategic-form game the players move simultaneously. The difference between these two descriptions becomes striking when changing a two-player game in strategic form to a commitment game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈树和策略形式的博弈之间的主要区别在于，在博弈树中，玩家按顺序行动，并且知道其他玩家之前的任何行动。相比之下，在策略形式的博弈中，玩家同时行动。当将一个两人的策略形式博弈转变为承诺博弈时，这两种描述之间的差异变得非常明显。
\end{tcolorbox}

The commitment game is a new game obtained from a two-player game in strategic form. One of the players (we always choose player I) moves first by choosing one of his strategies. Then, unlike in the strategic form, player II is informed about the move of player I and moves second. Player II chooses one of her original strategies, which can depend on the move of player I. The resulting payoffs to both players are as specified in the original game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
承诺博弈是从一个两人的策略形式博弈中得到的新博弈。其中一个玩家(我们总是选择玩家I)先行动，选择他的一个策略。然后，与策略形式不同的是，玩家II会得知玩家I的行动，然后再行动。玩家II选择她原来的一个策略，这个策略可以取决于玩家I的行动。两个玩家最终获得的收益与原博弈中规定的相同。
\end{tcolorbox}

An example is shown in Figure 4.11. The game in strategic form in (a) is the Quality game in Figure 3.2(b). The commitment game, shown in (b) and (c), is a game tree with perfect information, where player I moves first and player II moves second. The original strategies \(T\) and \(B\) of player \(\mathrm{I}\) become his moves \(T\) and \(B\) .
