On the graph of the function (as a subset of \({\mathbb{R}}^{2}\) ) we show three white dots that are the points \(\left( {x,u\left( x\right) }\right) ,\left( {\left( {1 - \lambda }\right) x + \lambda {x}^{\prime },u\left( {\left( {1 - \lambda }\right) x + \lambda {x}^{\prime }}\right) }\right)\) , and \(\left( {{x}^{\prime },u\left( {x}^{\prime }\right) }\right)\) . The black dot is the convex combination \(\left( {1 - \lambda }\right)  \cdot  \left( {x,u\left( x\right) }\right)  + \lambda  \cdot  \left( {{x}^{\prime },u\left( {x}^{\prime }\right) }\right)\) of the outer white dots, which is below the middle white dot, as asserted in (5.46). This holds generally and shows that \(u\) is concave.

\begin{tcolorbox}
例如，图5.5展示了定义在 \(u\left( x\right)  = \sqrt{x}\) 上的函数 \(\left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) 。在该函数的图(作为 \({\mathbb{R}}^{2}\) 的子集)上，我们展示了三个白色点，分别是点 \(\left( {x,u\left( x\right) }\right) ,\left( {\left( {1 - \lambda }\right) x + \lambda {x}^{\prime },u\left( {\left( {1 - \lambda }\right) x + \lambda {x}^{\prime }}\right) }\right)\) 和 \(\left( {{x}^{\prime },u\left( {x}^{\prime }\right) }\right)\) 。黑色点是外部白色点的凸组合 \(\left( {1 - \lambda }\right)  \cdot  \left( {x,u\left( x\right) }\right)  + \lambda  \cdot  \left( {{x}^{\prime },u\left( {x}^{\prime }\right) }\right)\) ，正如(5.46)中所断言的，它位于中间白色点的下方。这一情况普遍成立，表明 \(u\) 是凹函数。
\end{tcolorbox}

If we have equality in (5.46), then that part of the graph of the function is itself a line segment. If we never have equality, that is, (5.46) holds as a strict inequality whenever \(x \neq  {x}^{\prime }\) and \(0 < \lambda  < 1\) , then the function \(u\) is said to be strictly concave.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果(5.46)式取等号，那么函数图的那部分本身就是一条线段。如果永远不取等号，即只要 \(x \neq  {x}^{\prime }\) 和 \(0 < \lambda  < 1\) 时，(5.46)式以严格不等式成立，那么函数 \(u\) 被称为严格凹函数。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.6\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_142_275_562_859_643_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 5.5 Example of a concave function \(u\left( x\right)  = \sqrt{x}\) on the interval \(\left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图5.5 区间 \(\left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) 上凹函数 \(u\left( x\right)  = \sqrt{x}\) 的示例。
\end{tcolorbox}

It is easy to see that a function that is twice differentiable is concave if and only if its second derivative is always nonnegative, and strictly concave if its second derivative is always positive (as in the case of the square root function \(\sqrt{x}\) for \(x > 0\) ). Furthermore, it can be shown that, except at the endpoints of the interval that is the domain of the function, concavity implies continuity.

\begin{tcolorbox}
很容易看出，一个二次可微的函数是凹函数当且仅当它的二阶导数始终是非负的；如果它的二阶导数始终为正(例如对于 \(x > 0\) 时的平方根函数 \(\sqrt{x}\) )，则该函数是严格凹函数。此外，可以证明，除了函数定义域区间的端点外，凹性意味着连续性。
\end{tcolorbox}

The following observation states that risk aversion is equivalent to the concavity of the player's utility function. For simple lotteries its proof is almost immediate.

\begin{tcolorbox}
以下观察表明，风险厌恶等价于玩家的效用函数是凹函数。对于简单彩票，其证明几乎是直接的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proposition 5.5. Let \(X\) be a real interval and let \(u : X \rightarrow  \mathbb{R}\) be an expected-utility function. Consider any lottery \(P\) over \(X\) with expected value \(\widehat{x} = \mathbb{E}\left( P\right)\) . Then the player is risk averse, that is, always \(P \precsim  \widehat{x}\) , if and only if \(u\) is concave.

\begin{tcolorbox}
命题5.5。设 \(X\) 是一个实数区间， \(u : X \rightarrow  \mathbb{R}\) 是一个期望效用函数。考虑 \(X\) 上的任意彩票 \(P\) ，其期望值为 \(\widehat{x} = \mathbb{E}\left( P\right)\) 。那么玩家是风险厌恶的，即总是 \(P \precsim  \widehat{x}\) ，当且仅当 \(u\) 是凹函数。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Because \(X\) is a real interval, any lottery \(P\) over \(X\) is a random variable that has an expectation \(\widehat{x} = \mathbb{E}\left( P\right)\) , where \(\widehat{x} \in  X\) . Consider first (see (5.16)) a simple lottery \(P = x{ \land  }_{\lambda }{x}^{\prime }\) for \(\lambda  \in  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) and \(x,{x}^{\prime } \in  X\) , where \(\widehat{x} = \mathbb{E}\left( P\right)  = \left( {1 - \lambda }\right) x + \lambda {x}^{\prime }\) . Then \(P \precsim  \widehat{x}\) is equivalent to \(\mathbb{E}\left( {u,P}\right)  = \left( {1 - \lambda }\right) u\left( x\right)  + {\lambda u}\left( {x}^{\prime }\right)  \leq  u\left( \widehat{x}\right)\) , which is exactly the concavity condition (5.46).

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。因为 \(X\) 是一个实数区间， \(X\) 上的任何彩票 \(P\) 都是一个具有期望值 \(\widehat{x} = \mathbb{E}\left( P\right)\) 的随机变量，其中 \(\widehat{x} \in  X\) 。首先考虑(见(5.16))一个简单彩票 \(P = x{ \land  }_{\lambda }{x}^{\prime }\) ，其中 \(\lambda  \in  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) 且 \(x,{x}^{\prime } \in  X\) ，这里 \(\widehat{x} = \mathbb{E}\left( P\right)  = \left( {1 - \lambda }\right) x + \lambda {x}^{\prime }\) 。那么 \(P \precsim  \widehat{x}\) 等价于 \(\mathbb{E}\left( {u,P}\right)  = \left( {1 - \lambda }\right) u\left( x\right)  + {\lambda u}\left( {x}^{\prime }\right)  \leq  u\left( \widehat{x}\right)\) ，这正是凹性条件(5.46)。
\end{tcolorbox}

It remains to show that if \(u\) is concave then \(P \precsim  \mathbb{E}\left( P\right)\) for any lottery \(P\) that assigns positive probabilities \({p}_{1},\ldots ,{p}_{n}\) to outcomes \({x}_{1},\ldots ,{x}_{n}\) for \(n \geq  3\) . As inductive hypothesis, assume this is true for \(n - 1\) instead of \(n\) (we have just shown it for \(n = 2\) ). Write \(P\) as \(Q{ \land  }_{{p}_{n}}{x}_{n}\) where \(Q\) assigns probability \(\frac{{p}_{i}}{1 - {p}_{n}}\) to \({x}_{i}\) for \(1 \leq  i \leq  n - 1\) . By inductive hypothesis, \(\mathbb{E}\left( {Q,u}\right)  \leq  u\left( {\mathbb{E}\left( Q\right) }\right)\) . Therefore (where the second inequality holds because \(u\) is concave),

\begin{tcolorbox}
接下来需要证明，如果 \(u\) 是凹的，那么对于任何将正概率 \({p}_{1},\ldots ,{p}_{n}\) 分配给结果 \({x}_{1},\ldots ,{x}_{n}\) (其中 \(n \geq  3\) )的彩票 \(P\) ，有 \(P \precsim  \mathbb{E}\left( P\right)\) 。作为归纳假设，假设对于 \(n - 1\) 而非 \(n\) 这是成立的(我们刚刚已经证明了对于 \(n = 2\) 是成立的)。将 \(P\) 写为 \(Q{ \land  }_{{p}_{n}}{x}_{n}\) ，其中 \(Q\) 将概率 \(\frac{{p}_{i}}{1 - {p}_{n}}\) 分配给 \({x}_{i}\) (对于 \(1 \leq  i \leq  n - 1\) )。根据归纳假设， \(\mathbb{E}\left( {Q,u}\right)  \leq  u\left( {\mathbb{E}\left( Q\right) }\right)\) 。因此(其中第二个不等式成立是因为 \(u\) 是凹的)
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathbb{E}\left( {P,u}\right)  = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{{n - 1}}{p}_{i}u\left( {x}_{i}\right)  + {p}_{n}{x}_{n}
\]

\[
= \left( {1 - {p}_{n}}\right) \mathbb{E}\left( {Q,u}\right)  + {p}_{n}{x}_{n}
\]

\[
\leq  \left( {1 - {p}_{n}}\right) u\left( {\mathbb{E}\left( Q\right) }\right)  + {p}_{n}{x}_{n}
\]

\[
\leq  u\left( {\left( {1 - {p}_{n}}\right) \mathbb{E}\left( Q\right)  + {p}_{n}{x}_{n}}\right)  = u\left( {\mathbb{E}\left( P\right) }\right) ,
\]

which completes the proof.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这就完成了证明。
\end{tcolorbox}

We stress again that risk aversion requires real-valued outcomes, such as money, where the expectation of a lottery is itself an outcome which can be compared with the lottery.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们再次强调，风险厌恶要求结果是实值的，例如金钱，在这种情况下，彩票的期望本身就是一个可以与彩票进行比较的结果。
\end{tcolorbox}

Beyond concavity, risk aversion is not easy to quantify. If the player has a wealth of \(\widehat{x}\) dollars and is risk averse, he will prefer \(\widehat{x}\) to an equal gamble between \(\widehat{x} - {100}\) and \(\widehat{x} + {100}\) dollars. The player should therefore be willing to pay a "risk premium" \(\pi\) so that he is indifferent between safely receiving \(\widehat{x} - \pi\) and the gamble. For the utility function \(u\left( x\right)  =  - {e}^{-{cx}}\) for some \(c > 0\) , it is easy to see that \(\pi\) does not depend on \(\widehat{x}\) , and that this is up to positive-affine transformations the only concave utility function with this property. If the values of \(\widehat{x}\) are realistic, and the lottery itself (like winning or losing 100 dollars) is comparable to the risks under consideration, then estimating the risk premium \(\pi\) may be a useful approach to constructing the utility function.

\begin{tcolorbox}
除了凹性之外，风险厌恶并不容易量化。如果玩家拥有 \(\widehat{x}\) 美元的财富并且是风险厌恶的，他会更喜欢确定的 \(\widehat{x}\) 美元，而不是在 \(\widehat{x} - {100}\) 美元和 \(\widehat{x} + {100}\) 美元之间进行等概率的赌博。因此，玩家应该愿意支付一个“风险溢价” \(\pi\) ，使得他对安全地获得 \(\widehat{x} - \pi\) 美元和进行赌博无差异。对于效用函数 \(u\left( x\right)  =  - {e}^{-{cx}}\) (对于某个 \(c > 0\) )，很容易看出 \(\pi\) 不依赖于 \(\widehat{x}\) ，并且在正仿射变换下，这是唯一具有此性质的凹效用函数。如果 \(\widehat{x}\) 的值是现实的，并且彩票本身(例如赢或输 100 美元)与所考虑的风险具有可比性，那么估计风险溢价 \(\pi\) 可能是构建效用函数的一种有用方法。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{5.10 Discussion and Further Reading}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{5.10 讨论与进一步阅读}
\end{tcolorbox}

The consistency axioms that imply the existence of an expected-utility function are sometimes called "rationality axioms". A player is called "rational" when he acts in accordance with his own consistent preferences. In game theory, further assumptions are that all players know the entire game, including the preferences of all players, that everybody is rational, and often that this is common knowledge (everybody knows that everybody knows, and so on). For more detailed discussions see Aumann and Brandenburger (1995), Aumann and Dreze (2008), and the book by Perea (2012).

\begin{tcolorbox}
暗示期望效用函数存在的一致性公理有时被称为“理性公理”。当一个玩家按照自己一致的偏好行动时，他被称为“理性的”。在博弈论中，进一步的假设是所有玩家都了解整个博弈，包括所有玩家的偏好，每个人都是理性的，并且通常这是共同知识(每个人都知道每个人都知道，依此类推)。更详细的讨论请参见奥曼和布兰登伯格(1995 年)、奥曼和德雷兹(2008 年)以及佩雷亚(2012 年)的书。
\end{tcolorbox}

Game theory, and economic theory in general, is often criticized for these idealized assumptions. It should be clear that the assumptions are part of the mathematical model, which may not be particularly realistic, and need to be questioned when considering the conclusions of a game-theoretic analysis. These conclusions already pose difficulties when a solution (such as equilibrium) is not unique. But even if unique, the conclusions are normative (stating what players should do) rather than descriptive (stating what players actually do).

\begin{tcolorbox}
博弈论以及一般的经济理论常常因这些理想化的假设而受到批评。应该明确的是，这些假设是数学模型的一部分，可能并不特别现实，在考虑博弈论分析的结论时需要对其进行质疑。当一个解(如均衡)不唯一时，这些结论就已经存在困难。但即使解是唯一的，这些结论也是规范的(陈述玩家应该做什么)而非描述性的(陈述玩家实际做了什么)。
\end{tcolorbox}

A further source of misunderstanding game theory is the use of words for mathematical concepts that have strong connotations elsewhere. It is clear that a game tree is not an actual tree but describes a sequence of decision "branches", so "tree" can be used as a neutral word. However, the word "utility" suggests an intrinsic strength of preference associated with the "usefulness" of an outcome. As argued in this chapter, it should be considered as a mathematical tool for representing a preference for risky outcomes.

\begin{tcolorbox}
对博弈论产生误解的另一个原因是，用于数学概念的词汇在其他地方有强烈的内涵。显然，博弈树并不是真正的树，而是描述了一系列决策“分支”，因此“树”可以作为一个中性的词汇。然而，“效用”这个词暗示了与结果的“有用性”相关的内在偏好强度。正如本章所论述的，它应该被视为一种用于表示对风险结果偏好的数学工具。
\end{tcolorbox}

Because of the connotations of the word "utility", we instead use the more neutral and active term "payoff" in the context of games in this book. However, it is useful to remember that payoffs represent a preference and can therefore be scaled with positive-affine transformations.

\begin{tcolorbox}
由于“效用”这个词的内涵，在本书的博弈背景下，我们改用更中性和积极的术语“收益”。然而，记住收益代表一种偏好，因此可以通过正仿射变换进行缩放是很有用的。
\end{tcolorbox}

A number of classic books on game theory, such as Luce and Raiffa (1957) and Myerson (1991), start with the rationality assumptions that justify the use of expected payoffs. Unusually, Osborne and Rubinstein (1994) even define games in terms of preference relations rather than payoffs.

\begin{tcolorbox}
许多关于博弈论的经典著作，如卢斯和雷法(1957 年)以及迈尔森(1991 年)的著作，都始于证明使用期望效用合理性的理性假设。不同寻常的是，奥斯本和鲁宾斯坦(1994 年)甚至用偏好关系而非效用来定义博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

The concept of expected utility is generally attributed to Daniel Bernoulli (1738), who proposed the logarithm as a utility function for lotteries that involve money. For an informative and entertaining historical account see Szpiro (2020), and also Szpiro (2010) on approaches to group decision making, the Condorcet Paradox, and social choice in general. The consistency axioms for an expected-utility function are due to, and often named after, von Neumann and Morgenstern (1947). The paradox in Figure 5.2 has been described by Allais (1953). For a short history of the discussion around these axioms, and further references, see Moscati (2016).

\begin{tcolorbox}
期望效用的概念通常归功于丹尼尔·伯努利(1738 年)，他提出用对数作为涉及货币的彩票的效用函数。关于这一概念的信息丰富且有趣的历史记载，可参见斯皮罗(2020 年)的著作，关于群体决策方法、孔多塞悖论和一般社会选择的内容也可参见斯皮罗(2010 年)的著作。期望效用函数的一致性公理由冯·诺依曼和摩根斯坦(1947 年)提出，并且通常以他们的名字命名。图 5.2 中的悖论由阿莱(1953 年)描述。关于围绕这些公理的讨论的简短历史及更多参考文献，可参见莫斯卡蒂(2016 年)的著作。
\end{tcolorbox}

A classic book on utility theory, which connects axioms about preferences and their representation with a utility function, is Fishburn (1970). An abstract approach to preferences, due to Herstein and Milnor (1953), is called a "mixture set" where two lotteries (including safe outcomes) \(P\) and \(Q\) and a probability \(\alpha\) are combined into a new lottery \(\left( {1 - \alpha }\right) P + {\alpha Q}\) as in (5.15), with suitable axioms about these mixtures of \(P,Q,\alpha\) . Rather than considering such abstract "mixtures", we have kept their meaning as lotteries, so that the algebraic manipulations, for example as used in the proof of Theorem 5.4, stay concrete.

\begin{tcolorbox}
一本关于效用理论的经典著作是菲什伯恩(1970 年)的作品，它将关于偏好的公理及其用效用函数表示的内容联系起来。赫斯坦和米尔诺(1953 年)提出的一种关于偏好的抽象方法被称为“混合集”，其中两个彩票(包括确定结果) \(P\) 和 \(Q\) 以及一个概率 \(\alpha\) 按照(5.15)式组合成一个新的彩票 \(\left( {1 - \alpha }\right) P + {\alpha Q}\) ，并且有关于这些 \(P,Q,\alpha\) 混合的合适公理。我们没有考虑这种抽象的“混合”，而是保留了它们作为彩票的含义，这样代数运算，例如在定理 5.4 的证明中使用的运算，就保持具体。
\end{tcolorbox}

Utility theory is not a good descriptive theory of human decision making, as documented and extensively studied by the psychologists Daniel Kahneman and Amos Tversky. Their collaboration and research is vividly described in the popular-science book by Lewis (2016). The example (5.10) of intransitive preferences is due to Tversky (1969).

\begin{tcolorbox}
效用理论并不是一个很好的描述人类决策的理论，心理学家丹尼尔·卡尼曼和阿莫斯·特沃斯基对此进行了记录和广泛研究。刘易斯(2016 年)的科普书籍生动地描述了他们的合作与研究。非传递偏好的例子(5.10)由特沃斯基(1969 年)提出。
\end{tcolorbox}

An important paper on risk aversion is Pratt (1964).

\begin{tcolorbox}
一篇关于风险厌恶的重要论文是普拉特(1964 年)的作品。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{5.11 Exercises for Chapter 5}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{5.11 第五章练习}
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 5.1. Find positive weights \(w\) in a utility function of the form \(u\left( {{c}_{1},{c}_{2}}\right)  =\)  \({c}_{1} + w \cdot  {c}_{2}\) to create at least four different strict rankings among the candidates \(x,y,z\) in (5.10). [Hint: Experiment with some values of \(w\) and vary them. What are the values of \(u\left( {{c}_{1},{c}_{2}}\right)\) for the three candidates as a function of \(w\) ?]

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 5.1。在形式为 \(u\left( {{c}_{1},{c}_{2}}\right)  =\)  \({c}_{1} + w \cdot  {c}_{2}\) 的效用函数中找到正权重 \(w\) ，以使(5.10)中的候选人 \(x,y,z\) 之间至少有四种不同的严格排序。[提示:尝试一些 \(w\) 的值并改变它们。作为 \(w\) 的函数，三个候选人的 \(u\left( {{c}_{1},{c}_{2}}\right)\) 值是多少？]
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 5.2. Complete the proof of Proposition 5.2 for the third case \({x}_{1} \prec  z\) in (5.21), to show that \(v\left( z\right)  = {au}\left( z\right)  + b\) . As stated in the proof, choose a simple lottery \(Q = {x}_{0}{ \land  }_{\gamma }z\) so that the player is indifferent between that lottery and \({x}_{1}\) , and proceed similarly to (5.23) using (5.18) and (5.20).

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 5.2。完成命题 5.2 中(5.21)式第三种情况 \({x}_{1} \prec  z\) 的证明，以表明 \(v\left( z\right)  = {au}\left( z\right)  + b\) 。如证明中所述，选择一个简单彩票 \(Q = {x}_{0}{ \land  }_{\gamma }z\) ，使得玩家对该彩票和 \({x}_{1}\) 无差异，然后使用(5.18)式和(5.20)式，按照(5.23)式的方式进行推导。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 5.3.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 5.3。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Prove the equivalence (5.25), including the inequalities, for real numbers \(A,B,C\) that fulfill \(A < C\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 对于满足 \(A < C\) 的实数 \(A,B,C\) ，证明等价关系(5.25)，包括其中的不等式。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Prove \(\left( {1 - \alpha }\right) \mathbb{E}\left( {u,P}\right)  + \alpha \mathbb{E}\left( {u,R}\right)  = \mathbb{E}\left( {u,\left( {1 - \alpha }\right) P + {\alpha R}}\right)\) for two lotteries \(P\) and \(R\) , which is the second equation in (5.30).

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 证明两个彩票 \(P\) 和 \(R\) 的 \(\left( {1 - \alpha }\right) \mathbb{E}\left( {u,P}\right)  + \alpha \mathbb{E}\left( {u,R}\right)  = \mathbb{E}\left( {u,\left( {1 - \alpha }\right) P + {\alpha R}}\right)\) 关系，这是(5.30)式中的第二个等式。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Mixed Equilibrium}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{混合均衡}
\end{tcolorbox}

A game in strategic form does not always have an equilibrium in which each player chooses her strategy deterministically. As we describe in this chapter, Nash (1951) showed that any finite strategic-form game has an equilibrium if players are allowed to use mixed strategies. A mixed strategy is an actively randomized choice of the player's given "pure" strategies according to certain probabilities. A profile of mixed strategies is called a mixed equilibrium if no player can improve her expected payoff by unilaterally changing her strategy.

\begin{tcolorbox}
策略形式的博弈并不总是存在每个玩家都确定性地选择其策略的均衡。正如我们在本章中所描述的，纳什(1951 年)证明了，如果允许玩家使用混合策略，任何有限策略形式的博弈都存在均衡。混合策略是玩家根据一定概率对给定的“纯”策略进行主动随机选择。如果没有玩家可以通过单方面改变其策略来提高其期望效用，那么一个混合策略组合就被称为混合均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

In this chapter, we briefly recall from Chapter 5 how a player's payoffs represent her preference for random outcomes via an expected-utility function. This is illustrated with a single-player decision problem whether to comply or not with a legal requirement, which is then turned into a game. The game is known as an Inspection game between an inspector as player I and an inspectee as player II. This game has no equilibrium in pure strategies. However, active randomization comes up naturally in this game, because the inspector can and will choose to inspect only some of the time. The mixed equilibrium in this game demonstrates many aspects of general mixed equilibria, in particular that a player's randomization probabilities depend on the payoffs of the other player.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在本章中，我们简要回顾第5章的内容，即玩家的收益如何通过期望效用函数来表示她对随机结果的偏好。这通过一个单人决策问题来说明，即是否遵守一项法律要求，然后将其转化为一个博弈。这个博弈被称为监管博弈，玩家I是监管者，玩家II是被监管者。这个博弈在纯策略中不存在均衡。然而，在这个博弈中自然会出现主动随机化，因为监管者可以而且只会选择在某些时候进行检查。这个博弈中的混合均衡展示了一般混合均衡的许多方面，特别是一个玩家的随机化概率取决于另一个玩家的收益。
\end{tcolorbox}

We then turn to general two-player games in strategic form, already studied in Chapter 3, here called bimatrix games. The payoff matrices for the two players are convenient for representing expected payoffs via multiplication with vectors of probabilities that are the players' mixed strategies. These mixed strategies, as vectors of probabilities, have a geometric interpretation, which we study in detail (and even more so in Chapter 9). Mixing with probabilities is geometrically equivalent to taking convex combinations.

\begin{tcolorbox}
然后我们转向策略形式的一般两人博弈，在第3章中已经研究过，这里称为双矩阵博弈。两个玩家的收益矩阵便于通过与玩家混合策略的概率向量相乘来表示期望收益。这些作为概率向量的混合策略具有几何解释，我们将详细研究(在第9章会研究得更多)。从几何角度看，用概率进行混合等同于取凸组合。
\end{tcolorbox}

In a two-player game we usually name the players I and II, and denote their pure strategies as rows \(i = 1,\ldots ,m\) and columns \(j = 1,\ldots ,n\) , respectively. In \(N\) -player games, which we only consider for general definitions, the players are numbered as players \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) , which is the most commonly used notation.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在两人博弈中，我们通常将玩家命名为I和II，并分别将他们的纯策略表示为行 \(i = 1,\ldots ,m\) 和列 \(j = 1,\ldots ,n\) 。在 \(N\) 人博弈中，我们仅考虑一般定义，玩家编号为 \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) ，这是最常用的表示法。
\end{tcolorbox}

The best-response condition gives a convenient, finite condition for when a mixed strategy is a best response to the mixed strategy of the other player (or against the mixed strategies of all other players in a game with more than two players). Namely, only the pure strategies that are best responses are allowed to be played with positive probability by a best-response mixed strategy. These pure best responses must have maximal, and hence equal, expected payoff. This is stated as a "trivial condition" by Nash (1951, p. 287), and it is indeed not hard to prove (see Proposition 6.1). However, the best-response condition is central for understanding and computing mixed-strategy equilibria.

\begin{tcolorbox}
最优反应条件为混合策略何时是对另一个玩家的混合策略(或在多人博弈中对所有其他玩家的混合策略)的最优反应提供了一个方便的有限条件。即，最优反应混合策略只允许以正概率采用那些作为最优反应的纯策略。这些纯最优反应必须具有最大的，因此是相等的期望收益。纳什(1951，第287页)将其表述为一个“平凡条件”，而且确实不难证明(见命题6.1)。然而，最优反应条件对于理解和计算混合策略均衡至关重要。
\end{tcolorbox}

In Section 6.5, we state and prove Nash's theorem that every game has an equilibrium. We follow Nash's original proof (for two players, to simplify notation), and state and briefly motivate Brouwer's fixed-point theorem used in that proof. In Chapter 7 we prove the fixed-point theorem itself.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在6.5节中，我们陈述并证明纳什定理，即每个博弈都存在一个均衡。我们遵循纳什的原始证明(为简化表示，针对两人博弈)，并陈述并简要说明该证明中使用的布劳威尔不动点定理。在第7章中，我们将证明不动点定理本身。
\end{tcolorbox}

The important final Sections 6.6-6.8 show how to find equilibria in bimatrix games, in particular when one player has only two strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
重要的最后6.6 - 6.8节展示了如何在双矩阵博弈中找到均衡，特别是当一个玩家只有两种策略时。
\end{tcolorbox}

The special and interesting case of zero-sum games is treated in Chapter 8 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
零和博弈这一特殊且有趣的情况将在第8章中讨论。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{6.1 Prerequisites and Learning Objectives}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{6.1 先决条件和学习目标}
\end{tcolorbox}

Prerequisites are Chapter 3 and knowing that payoffs are only unique up to positive-affine transformations, as discussed in Chapter 5. Fluency with matrix multiplication is useful, which we will recall as background material. We also need the algebraic representation of line segments explained in Figure 5.4.

\begin{tcolorbox}
先决条件是熟悉第3章的内容，并知道收益仅在正仿射变换下是唯一的，如第5章所讨论的。熟练掌握矩阵乘法会很有用，我们将把它作为背景材料进行回顾。我们还需要图5.4中解释的线段的代数表示。
\end{tcolorbox}

After studying this chapter, you should be able to:

\begin{tcolorbox}
学习本章后，你应该能够:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item explain what mixed strategies and mixed equilibria are;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 解释什么是混合策略和混合均衡；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item state the main theorems about mixed strategies, namely the best-response condition, and Nash's theorem on the existence of mixed equilibria;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 陈述关于混合策略的主要定理，即最优反应条件和纳什关于混合均衡存在性的定理；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item (as an important skill) find all mixed equilibria of small bimatrix games, using the "difference trick" and, where necessary, the upper-envelope method;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item (作为一项重要技能)使用“差分技巧”，必要时使用上包络法，找出小型双矩阵博弈的所有混合均衡；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item give the definition of a degenerate game, and find all equilibria of small degenerate games.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 给出退化博弈的定义，并找出小型退化博弈的所有均衡。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{6.2 Compliance Inspections}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{6.2 合规检查}
\end{tcolorbox}

In this section, we consider a two-player Inspection game where randomized or mixed strategies occur naturally. Against a mixed strategy, the other player faces a lottery, which is evaluated according to its expected payoffs, as studied in detail in the preceding Chapter 5.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在本节中，我们考虑一个两人监管博弈，其中随机或混合策略自然出现。面对一个混合策略，另一个玩家面临一场彩票，根据其期望收益对其进行评估，如前一章第5章详细研究的那样。
\end{tcolorbox}

To recap this briefly, Figure 6.1 shows a game with a single player who can decide to comply with a legal requirement, like buying a train ticket, or to cheat otherwise. The payoff when she chooses to comply is 0 . Cheating involves a 10 percent chance of getting caught and having to pay a penalty, stated as the negative payoff -90, and otherwise a 90 percent chance of gaining a payoff of 10 . With these numbers, cheating leads to a random outcome with an expected payoff of \({0.9} \cdot  {10} + {0.1} \cdot  \left( {-{90}}\right)\) , which is zero, so that the player is indifferent between her two moves. In fact, assuming that the two outcomes with payoffs 0 and 10 have been set as origin and unit of the utility function, the utility of -90 for "getting caught" is determined by this indifference. If the player is indifferent when the probability of getting caught is only four percent, then -90 has to be replaced by \(- u\) so that

\begin{tcolorbox}
简要回顾一下，图6.1展示了一个单人博弈，该玩家可以选择遵守法律要求(如购买火车票)，或者选择违规。她选择遵守时的收益为0。违规有10\%的概率被抓住并需支付罚款，此时收益记为 -90，另外有90\%的概率获得10的收益。根据这些数值，违规会导致一个随机结果，期望效用为 \({0.9} \cdot  {10} + {0.1} \cdot  \left( {-{90}}\right)\) ，即0，因此玩家对这两种选择无差异。实际上，假设收益为0和10的两种结果已被设定为效用函数的原点和单位，那么“被抓住”的效用 -90 就由这种无差异决定。如果玩家在被抓住的概率仅为4\%时无差异，那么 -90 必须替换为 \(- u\) ，使得
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
0 = {0.96} \cdot  {10} + {0.04} \cdot  \left( {-u}\right) ,
\]

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.2\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_148_338_201_352_373_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 6.1 One-player decision problem between comply and cheat. With the stated payoffs, the player is indifferent.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图6.1 遵守与违规之间的单人决策问题。在给定的收益下，玩家无差异。
\end{tcolorbox}

which is equivalent to \(u = {9.6}/{0.04} = {240}\) . Also, recall that the origin and unit of the utility function are arbitrary and can be changed by a positive-affine transformation. For example, \(0,{10}, - {90}\) could be replaced by \(0,1, - 9\) or \(- 1,0, - {10}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
这等同于 \(u = {9.6}/{0.04} = {240}\) 。此外，请记住，效用函数的原点和单位是任意的，可以通过正仿射变换进行改变。例如， \(0,{10}, - {90}\) 可以替换为 \(0,1, - 9\) 或 \(- 1,0, - {10}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

In practice, the preference for risk of a player may not be known. A game-theoretic analysis should be carried out for different choices of the payoff parameters in order to test how much they influence the results.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在实践中，玩家的风险偏好可能是未知的。为了测试收益参数对结果的影响程度，应该针对不同的收益参数选择进行博弈论分析。
\end{tcolorbox}

We now consider the Inspection game. For player II, called inspectee, the strategy comply means again to fulfill some legal obligation (such as buying a transport ticket, or paying tax). The inspectee has an incentive to violate this obligation by choosing to cheat. Player I, the inspector, should verify that the inspectee complies, but doing so requires inspections which are costly. If the inspector inspects and catches the inspectee cheating, then the inspectee has to pay a penalty.

\begin{tcolorbox}
现在我们考虑监管博弈。对于玩家II，即被监管者，“遵守”策略再次意味着履行某些法律义务(如购买交通票或纳税)。被监管者有动机通过选择“违规”来违反这项义务。玩家I，即监管者，应该核实被监管者是否遵守规定，但这样做需要进行检查，会产生成本。如果监管者进行检查并抓住被监管者违规，那么被监管者必须支付罚款。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 6.2 shows possible payoffs for the Inspection game. The outcome that defines the reference payoff zero to both players is that the inspector chooses Don't Inspect and the inspectee chooses to comply. Without inspection, the inspectee prefers to cheat, represented by her payoff 10, with negative payoff -10 to the inspector. The inspector may also decide to Inspect. If the inspectee complies, inspection leaves her payoff 0 unchanged, while the inspector incurs a cost with a negative payoff of -1 . If the inspectee cheats, however, inspection will result in a penalty (payoff -90 for player II) and still create a certain amount of hassle for player I (payoff -6).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图6.2展示了监管博弈的可能收益情况。为两个玩家定义参考收益为0的结果是，监管者选择“不检查”，被监管者选择“遵守”。如果不进行检查，被监管者更倾向于违规，其收益为10，而监管者的收益为 -10。监管者也可以决定“检查”。如果被监管者遵守规定，检查不会改变她的收益0，而监管者会产生成本，收益为 -1。然而，如果被监管者违规，检查将导致被监管者支付罚款(玩家II的收益为 -90)，并且仍然会给玩家I带来一定的麻烦(收益为 -6)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_149_335_196_542_401_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 6.2 Inspection game between the inspector (player I) and inspectee (player II).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图6.2 监管者(玩家I)与被监管者(玩家II)之间的监管博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

In all cases, player I would strongly prefer if player II complied, but this is outside of player I's control. However, the inspector prefers to inspect if the inspectee cheats (because -6 is better than -10 ), as shown by the best-response box for payoff -6 in Figure 6.2. If the inspector always preferred Don't Inspect, then this would be a dominating strategy and be part of a (unique) equilibrium where the inspectee cheats.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在所有情况下，玩家I非常希望玩家II遵守规定，但这不在玩家I的控制范围内。然而，如图6.2中收益 -6 的最优反应框所示，如果被监管者违规，监管者更倾向于检查(因为 -6 比 -10 好)。如果监管者总是倾向于“不检查”，那么这将是一个占优策略，并且会成为被监管者违规的(唯一)均衡的一部分。
\end{tcolorbox}

The best-response payoffs in Figure 6.2 show that this game has no equilibrium in pure strategies. If any player settles on a deterministic choice (like Don't Inspect by player I), then the best reponse of the other player is unique (here to cheat by player II), to which the original choice is not a best reponse (player I prefers to inspect when the other player cheats, against which player II in turn prefers to comply). The strategies in an equilibrium must be best responses to each other, so in this game this fails for any pair of pure strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图6.2中的最优反应收益表明，这个博弈在纯策略中没有均衡。如果任何一个玩家确定了一个确定性的选择(如玩家I选择“不检查”)，那么另一个玩家的最优反应是唯一的(这里是玩家II选择违规)，而最初的选择并不是对这个最优反应的最优反应(当另一个玩家违规时，玩家I更倾向于检查，而玩家II则更倾向于遵守)。均衡中的策略必须是相互的最优反应，因此在这个博弈中，任何一对纯策略都无法满足这一条件。
\end{tcolorbox}

What should the players do in the game of Figure 6.2? One possibility is that they prepare for the worst, that is, choose a so-called max-min strategy. A max-min strategy maximizes the player's worst payoff against all possible choices of the opponent. The max-min strategy (as a pure strategy) for player \(\mathrm{I}\) is to inspect (where the inspector guarantees himself payoff -6), and for player II it is to comply (which guarantees her a payoff of 0 ). However, this is not an equilibrium and hence not a stable recommendation to the two players, because player I could switch his strategy to Don't Inspect and improve his payoff.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在图6.2的博弈中，玩家应该怎么做呢？一种可能性是他们为最坏的情况做准备，即选择所谓的极大极小策略。极大极小策略是在对手所有可能的选择下，最大化玩家的最差收益。玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 的极大极小策略(作为纯策略)是检查(监管者可以保证自己的收益为 -6)，而玩家II的极大极小策略是遵守(这可以保证她的收益为0)。然而，这不是一个均衡，因此也不是给两个玩家的稳定建议，因为玩家I可以将他的策略改为“不检查”来提高他的收益。
\end{tcolorbox}

A mixed strategy of player I in this game is to inspect only with a certain probability. In the context of inspections, randomizing is also a practical approach that reduces costs. Even if an inspection is not certain, a sufficiently high chance of being caught should deter the inspectee from cheating.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在这个博弈中，参与者I的混合策略(mixed strategy)是以一定概率进行检查。在检查的情境下，随机化也是一种降低成本(cost)的实用方法。即使检查不是必然的，被抓住的可能性足够高也应该能阻止被检查者作弊。
\end{tcolorbox}

A mixed strategy of player I is given by a probability \(p\) for Inspect, and thus \(1 - p\) for Don’t Inspect. Instead of his two pure strategies, player I can now choose a probability \(p \in  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) . This includes his pure strategies Don’t Inspect when \(p = 0\) and Inspect when \(p = 1\) . A mixed strategy of player II is given by a probability \(q\) for cheat and \(1 - q\) for comply. Her choice between two pure strategies is thereby extended to any choice of \(q \in  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) . In effect, this means the players now each have an infinite strategy set \(\left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) with payoffs computed as expected payoffs. This new game is called the mixed extension of the original \(2 \times  2\) game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
参与者I的混合策略(mixed strategy)是为“检查”设定一个概率 \(p\) ，因此“不检查”的概率为 \(1 - p\) 。现在，参与者I可以选择一个概率 \(p \in  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) ，而不是他的两种纯策略。这包括当 \(p = 0\) 时的纯策略“不检查”和当 \(p = 1\) 时的纯策略“检查”。参与者II的混合策略(mixed strategy)是为“作弊”设定一个概率 \(q\) ，为“遵守”设定一个概率 \(1 - q\) 。这样，她在两种纯策略之间的选择就扩展到了对 \(q \in  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) 的任何选择。实际上，这意味着参与者现在各自有一个无限的策略集 \(\left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) ，其收益按期望效用(expected payoff)计算。这个新的博弈被称为原 \(2 \times  2\) 博弈的混合扩展(mixed extension)。
\end{tcolorbox}

When does the strategy pair \(\left( {p,q}\right)  \in  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack   \times  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) define an equilibrium (which will be called a mixed equilibrium)? These strategies have to be best responses to each other. Suppose player I chooses \(p\) and player II has to determine her best response. We first consider her possible pure responses. Her expected payoff for comply is \(\left( {1 - p}\right)  \cdot  0 + p \cdot  0 = 0\) , which is independent of \(p\) . Her expected payoff for cheat is \(\left( {1 - p}\right)  \cdot  {10} + p \cdot  \left( {-{90}}\right)  = {10} - {100p}\) . In effect, player II faces the decision situation in Figure 6.1 with 0.9 and 0.1 replaced by \(1 - p\) and \(p\) . Clearly, she prefers comply when \(0 > {10} - {100p}\) , that is, \(p > {0.1}\) , and cheat when \(0 < {10} - {100p}\) , that is, \(p < {0.1}\) , and she is indifferent if and only if \(0 = {10} - {100p}\) , that is, \(p = {0.1}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
策略对 \(\left( {p,q}\right)  \in  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack   \times  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) 何时构成一个均衡(equilibrium)(将其称为混合均衡(mixed equilibrium))？这些策略必须是彼此的最优反应(best response)。假设参与者I选择 \(p\) ，参与者II必须确定她的最优反应。我们首先考虑她可能的纯反应。她选择“遵守”的期望效用(expected payoff)是 \(\left( {1 - p}\right)  \cdot  0 + p \cdot  0 = 0\) ，这与 \(p\) 无关。她选择“作弊”的期望效用(expected payoff)是 \(\left( {1 - p}\right)  \cdot  {10} + p \cdot  \left( {-{90}}\right)  = {10} - {100p}\) 。实际上，参与者II面临着图6.1中的决策情形，其中0.9和0.1分别被 \(1 - p\) 和 \(p\) 取代。显然，当 \(0 > {10} - {100p}\) 时，即 \(p > {0.1}\) 时，她更倾向于“遵守”；当 \(0 < {10} - {100p}\) 时，即 \(p < {0.1}\) 时，她更倾向于“作弊”；当且仅当 \(0 = {10} - {100p}\) 时，即 \(p = {0.1}\) 时，她无差异。
\end{tcolorbox}

In the mixed extension, player II can also choose to mix by choosing \(q\) so that \(0 < q < 1\) . We claim that this only happens when she is indifferent. Namely, suppose that \(p\) is very low, for example \(p = {0.01}\) , so that cheat gives an expected payoff of \({0.99} \cdot  {10} + {0.01} \cdot  \left( {-{90}}\right)  = 9\) . With a mixed strategy \(q\) , player II’s expected payoff is \(0 \cdot  \left( {1 - q}\right)  + {9q} = {9q}\) , which is clearly maximized for \(q = 1\) , which is her pure best response cheat. Similarly, if \(p\) is high, for example \(p = {0.2}\) , then cheat gives an expected payoff of \({0.8} \cdot  {10} + {0.2} \cdot  \left( {-{90}}\right)  =  - {10}\) . With a mixed strategy \(q\) , player II’s expected payoff is \(0 \cdot  \left( {1 - q}\right)  - {10q} =  - {10q}\) , which is maximized for \(q = 0\) , which is her pure best response comply. Only when comply and cheat give the same expected payoffs, player II’s expected payoff does not depend on \(q\) and she can in fact choose any \(q \in  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) as a best response. Figure 6.3 shows a drawing of the best-response probability \(q\) against \(p\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
在混合扩展中，玩家 II 也可以通过选择 \(q\) 进行混合，使得 \(0 < q < 1\) 。我们断言，只有当她无差异时才会出现这种情况。也就是说，假设 \(p\) 非常低，例如 \(p = {0.01}\) ，那么作弊的期望效用为 \({0.99} \cdot  {10} + {0.01} \cdot  \left( {-{90}}\right)  = 9\) 。采用混合策略 \(q\) 时，玩家 II 的期望效用为 \(0 \cdot  \left( {1 - q}\right)  + {9q} = {9q}\) ，显然当 \(q = 1\) 时该期望效用达到最大化，这是她的纯最优反应——作弊。类似地，如果 \(p\) 很高，例如 \(p = {0.2}\) ，那么作弊的期望效用为 \({0.8} \cdot  {10} + {0.2} \cdot  \left( {-{90}}\right)  =  - {10}\) 。采用混合策略 \(q\) 时，玩家 II 的期望效用为 \(0 \cdot  \left( {1 - q}\right)  - {10q} =  - {10q}\) ，当 \(q = 0\) 时期望效用达到最大化，这是她的纯最优反应——遵守规则。只有当遵守规则和作弊带来相同的期望效用时，玩家 II 的期望效用才不依赖于 \(q\) ，实际上她可以选择任何 \(q \in  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) 作为最优反应。图 6.3 展示了最优反应概率 \(q\) 相对于 \(p\) 的关系图。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_150_248_1432_598_587_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 6.3 Best-response mixed-strategy probability \(q\) for cheat (black line) of player II in the Inspection game, against the mixed-strategy probability \(p\) for Inspect of player I. The mixed-strategy square is drawn so that its corners match the four cells in Figure 6.2.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 6.3 监管博弈中玩家 II 作弊的最优反应混合策略概率 \(q\) (黑线)与玩家 I 检查的混合策略概率 \(p\) 的关系图。混合策略方块的绘制使得其角点与图 6.2 中的四个单元格相对应。
\end{tcolorbox}

In summary, the only case where player II can possibly randomize between her pure strategies is if both strategies give her the same expected payoff. As stated and proved formally in Proposition 6.1 below, it is never optimal for a player to assign a positive probability to a pure strategy that is inferior to other pure strategies, for given mixed strategies of the other players.

\begin{tcolorbox}
综上所述，玩家 II 可能在其纯策略之间进行随机选择的唯一情况是，两种策略给她带来相同的期望效用。正如下面命题 6.1 所陈述和正式证明的那样，对于给定的其他玩家的混合策略，玩家对劣于其他纯策略的纯策略赋予正概率永远不是最优的。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_151_339_379_1246_589_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 6.4 Left: Best-response mixed-strategy probability \(p\) (gray line) of player I against \(q\) . Right: The intersection of the two best-response lines determines the unique mixed equilibrium where \(p = {0.1}\) and \(q = {0.2}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 6.4 左图:玩家 I 相对于 \(q\) 的最优反应混合策略概率 \(p\) (灰线)。右图:两条最优反应线的交点确定了唯一的混合均衡，其中 \(p = {0.1}\) 且 \(q = {0.2}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Similarly, consider a mixed strategy of player II where she cheats with probability \(q\) . The expected payoffs for player I’s pure strategies are \(- {10q}\) for Don’t Inspect and \(\left( {-1}\right) \left( {1 - q}\right)  - {6q} =  - 1 - {5q}\) for Inspect. If player I plays Inspect with probability \(p\) , then his expected payoff is therefore \(\left( {-{10q}}\right) \left( {1 - p}\right)  + \left( {-1 - {5q}}\right) p =  - {10q} + p\left( {{5q} - 1}\right)\) . This is a linear function of \(p\) , and has its maximum at \(p = 0\) if \({5q} - 1 < 0\) , that is, \(q < {0.2}\) , and at \(p = 1\) if \({5q} - 1 > 0\) , that is, \(q > {0.2}\) , and only if \(q = {0.2}\) does it not depend on \(p\) so that \(p \in  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) can then be chosen arbitrarily. This is shown in the left diagram in Figure 6.4.

\begin{tcolorbox}
类似地，考虑玩家 II 的一种混合策略，她以概率 \(q\) 作弊。玩家 I 的纯策略的期望效用分别为:不检查时为 \(- {10q}\) ，检查时为 \(\left( {-1}\right) \left( {1 - q}\right)  - {6q} =  - 1 - {5q}\) 。如果玩家 I 以概率 \(p\) 选择检查，那么他的期望效用为 \(\left( {-{10q}}\right) \left( {1 - p}\right)  + \left( {-1 - {5q}}\right) p =  - {10q} + p\left( {{5q} - 1}\right)\) 。这是关于 \(p\) 的线性函数，若 \({5q} - 1 < 0\) ，即 \(q < {0.2}\) ，则在 \(p = 0\) 处达到最大值；若 \({5q} - 1 > 0\) ，即 \(q > {0.2}\) ，则在 \(p = 1\) 处达到最大值；只有当 \(q = {0.2}\) 时，它才不依赖于 \(p\) ，此时 \(p \in  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack\) 可以任意选择。这在图 6.4 的左图中有所展示。
\end{tcolorbox}

The right diagram in Figure 6.4 shows the intersection of the two best-response lines with the unique mixed equilibrium of the game at \(p = {0.1}\) and \(q = {0.2}\) when the two mixed strategies are mutual best responses. It is similar to the best-response diagram in Figure 3.10 for the Cournot duopoly, except for a quarter clock-wise turn so that the vertical and horizontal parameters \(p\) and \(q\) match the rows and columns in the \(2 \times  2\) game in Figure 6.2.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图6.4中的右图展示了当两个混合策略互为最优反应时，两条最优反应线的交点，该交点对应着博弈在 \(p = {0.1}\) 和 \(q = {0.2}\) 处的唯一混合均衡(mixed equilibrium)。它与图3.10中用于古诺双寡头博弈(Cournot duopoly)的最优反应示意图(best-response diagram)相似，只是顺时针旋转了四分之一圈，以便垂直和水平参数 \(p\) 和 \(q\) 与图6.2中 \(2 \times  2\) 博弈的行和列相匹配。
\end{tcolorbox}

In the unique mixed equilibrium with \(p = {0.1}\) and \(q = {0.2}\) of the Inspection game, the expected payoffs are

\begin{tcolorbox}
在监管博弈(Inspection game)的 \(p = {0.1}\) 和 \(q = {0.2}\) 唯一混合均衡(mixed equilibrium)中，期望效用(expected payoff)为
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
- {10q} =  - 1\left( {1 - q}\right)  - {6q} =  - 2\;\text{ for player }\mathrm{I},
\]

\[
0 = {10}\left( {1 - p}\right)  - {90p} = 0\;\text{ for player II. }
\]

The equilibrium probabilities \(q\) and \(p\) are (independently) determined by these equations. It is noteworthy that they depend on the opponent's payoffs and not on the player's own payoffs (which can therefore be changed as long as the circular preference structure in Figure 6.2 remains intact). For example, one would expect that a more severe penalty than -90 for being caught lowers the probability of cheating in equilibrium. In fact, it does not. What does change is the probability \(p\) of inspection, which is reduced until the inspectee is indifferent.

\begin{tcolorbox}
均衡概率 \(q\) 和 \(p\) 由这些方程(独立地)确定。值得注意的是，它们取决于对手的收益，而非玩家自身的收益(因此，只要图6.2中的循环偏好(preference)结构保持不变，玩家自身的收益就可以改变)。例如，人们可能认为，被抓后处以比 -90 更严厉的惩罚会降低均衡状态下作弊的概率。实际上并非如此。真正改变的是检查的概率 \(p\) ，该概率会降低，直到被检查者感到无差异为止。
\end{tcolorbox}

This dependence on opponent payoffs is the most counter-intuitive property of mixed equilibrium. Moreover, mixing itself seems paradoxical when the player is indifferent in equilibrium. If player II, for example, can equally comply or cheat, why should she randomize? In particular, she could comply and get payoff zero for certain, which is simpler and safer. The answer is that precisely because there is no incentive to choose one strategy over the other, a player can mix, and only in that case can there be an equilibrium. If player II would comply for certain, then the only optimal choice of player \(\mathrm{I}\) is not to inspect, but then complying is not optimal, so this is not an equilibrium.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这种对对手收益的依赖是混合均衡(mixed equilibrium)最违反直觉的特性。此外，当玩家在均衡状态下感到无差异时，混合策略(mixed strategy)本身似乎也自相矛盾。例如，如果玩家 II 可以同样地选择遵守规则或作弊，那她为什么要随机选择呢？特别是，她可以选择遵守规则并确定获得零收益，这样既简单又安全。答案是，正是因为没有动机去选择一种策略而非另一种策略，玩家才可以采用混合策略，而且只有在这种情况下才可能存在均衡。如果玩家 II 确定会遵守规则，那么玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 的唯一最优选择就是不进行检查，但这样一来，遵守规则就不是最优选择了，所以这不是一个均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{6.3 Bimatrix Games}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{6.3 双矩阵博弈(bimatrix game)}
\end{tcolorbox}

In the following, we discuss mixed equilibria for general games in strategic form. Most ideas can be explained, with much simpler notation, for two-player games. Then the strategies of player I and player II are the rows and columns of a table with two payoffs in each cell, so that the game is defined by two payoff matrices. Some basic facts about matrix manipulation are in the background material.

\begin{tcolorbox}
接下来，我们将讨论策略形式下一般博弈的混合均衡。对于两人博弈，大多数概念可以用更简单的符号来解释。此时，玩家I和玩家II的策略分别对应一个表格的行和列，每个单元格中有两个收益值，因此该博弈由两个收益矩阵定义。有关矩阵运算的一些基本事实可在背景材料中找到。
\end{tcolorbox}

Background material: Matrix multiplication

\begin{tcolorbox}
背景材料:矩阵乘法
\end{tcolorbox}

If \(A\) is an \(m \times  k\) matrix and \(B\) is an \(k \times  n\) matrix, then their matrix product \(A \cdot  B\) (or \({AB}\) for short) is the \(m \times  n\) matrix \(C\) with entries

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果 \(A\) 是一个 \(m \times  k\) 矩阵， \(B\) 是一个 \(k \times  n\) 矩阵，那么它们的矩阵乘积 \(A \cdot  B\) (或简记为 \({AB}\) )是一个 \(m \times  n\) 矩阵 \(C\) ，其元素为
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{c}_{ij} = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{s = 1}}^{k}{a}_{is}{b}_{sj}\;\left( {1 \leq  i \leq  m,\;1 \leq  j \leq  n}\right) . \tag{6.1}
\]

When writing down a matrix product \({AB}\) it is assumed to be defined, that is, \(A\) has as many columns as \(B\) has rows. Matrix multiplication is associative, that is, \(\left( {AB}\right) C = A\left( {BC}\right)\) , written as \({ABC}\) , for matrices \(A,B,C\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
在写下矩阵乘积 \({AB}\) 时，默认该乘积是有定义的，即 \(A\) 的列数等于 \(B\) 的行数。矩阵乘法满足结合律，即对于矩阵 \(A,B,C\) ，有 \(\left( {AB}\right) C = A\left( {BC}\right)\) ，可写成 \({ABC}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

When dealing with matrices, we use the following notation throughout. All matrices considered here are real matrices (with entries in \(\mathbb{R}\) ). The set of \(m \times  n\) matrices is \({\mathbb{R}}^{m \times  n}\) . The transpose of an \(m \times  n\) matrix \(A\) is written \({A}^{\top }\) , which is the \(n \times  m\) matrix with entries \({a}_{ji}\) if \({a}_{ij}\) are the entries of \(A\) , for \(1 \leq  i \leq  m,1 \leq  j \leq  n\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
在处理矩阵时，我们始终使用以下符号。这里考虑的所有矩阵都是实矩阵(元素属于 \(\mathbb{R}\) )。 \(m \times  n\) 矩阵的集合记为 \({\mathbb{R}}^{m \times  n}\) 。 \(m \times  n\) 矩阵 \(A\) 的转置记为 \({A}^{\top }\) ，它是一个 \(n \times  m\) 矩阵，若 \(A\) 的元素为 \({a}_{ij}\) ，则 \({A}^{\top }\) 的元素为 \({a}_{ji}\) ，其中 \(1 \leq  i \leq  m,1 \leq  j \leq  n\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

All vectors are column vectors, so if \(x \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) then \(x\) is an \(n \times  1\) matrix and \({x}^{\top }\) is the corresponding row vector in \({\mathbb{R}}^{1 \times  n}\) , and row vectors are easily identified by this transposition sign. The components of a vector \(x \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) are \({x}_{1},\ldots ,{x}_{n}\) . We write the scalar product of two vectors \(x,y\) in \({\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) as the matrix product \({x}^{\top }y\) . In pictures (with a square for each matrix entry, here for \(n = 4\) ):

\begin{tcolorbox}
所有向量均为列向量，因此如果 \(x \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) ，那么 \(x\) 是一个 \(n \times  1\) 矩阵， \({x}^{\top }\) 是 \({\mathbb{R}}^{1 \times  n}\) 中对应的行向量，通过这个转置符号可以很容易识别出行向量。向量 \(x \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) 的分量为 \({x}_{1},\ldots ,{x}_{n}\) 。我们将 \({\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) 中两个向量 \(x,y\) 的标量积写成矩阵乘积 \({x}^{\top }y\) 。用图形表示(每个矩阵元素用一个方块表示，这里是 \(n = 4\) ):
\end{tcolorbox}

(6.2)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.3\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_153_753_327_424_155_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

We use matrix multiplication where possible. In particular, scalars are treated like \(1 \times  1\) matrices. A column vector \(x\) is multiplied with a scalar \(\lambda\) from the right, and a row vector \({x}^{\top }\) is multiplied with a scalar \(\lambda\) from the left,

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们尽可能使用矩阵乘法。特别地，标量被视为 \(1 \times  1\) 矩阵。列向量 \(x\) 从右侧与标量 \(\lambda\) 相乘，行向量 \({x}^{\top }\) 从左侧与标量 \(\lambda\) 相乘。
\end{tcolorbox}

(6.3)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.7\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_153_513_657_901_158_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

For more details see the background material on visualizing matrix multiplication.

\begin{tcolorbox}
更多详细信息请参阅矩阵乘法可视化的背景材料。
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\Rightarrow\) Exercise 6.1 compares \({x}^{\top }y\) and \(x{y}^{\top }\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\Rightarrow\) 练习6.1比较了 \({x}^{\top }y\) 和 \(x{y}^{\top }\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Furthermore, we use the special vectors0and1with all components equal to 0 and 1, respectively, where their dimension depends on the context. Inequalities like \(x \geq  \mathbf{0}\) hold for all components; note that the resulting order \(\leq\) between vectors is only a partial order.

\begin{tcolorbox}
此外，我们使用特殊向量0和1，它们的所有分量分别等于0和1，其维度取决于具体情境。像 \(x \geq  \mathbf{0}\) 这样的不等式对所有分量都成立；注意，向量之间由此产生的序 \(\leq\) 只是一个偏序。
\end{tcolorbox}

Recall that a game in strategic form is specified by a finite set of "pure" strategies for each player, and a payoff for each player for each strategy profile, which is a tuple of strategies, one for each player. The game is played by each player independently and simultaneously choosing one strategy, whereupon the players receive their respective payoffs.

\begin{tcolorbox}
回顾一下，策略形式的博弈由每个玩家的一组有限“纯”策略以及每个策略组合(即每个玩家各选一个策略所构成的元组)下每个玩家的收益来确定。该博弈通过每个玩家独立且同时选择一个策略来进行，然后玩家获得各自的收益。
\end{tcolorbox}

In this chapter we focus on two-player games. Unless specified otherwise, we assume that player I has \(m\) strategies and player II has \(n\) strategies. The \(m\) strategies of the row player I are denoted by \(i = 1,\ldots ,m\) , and the \(n\) strategies of the column player II are denoted by \(j = 1,\ldots ,n\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
在本章中，我们主要关注两人博弈。除非另有说明，我们假设玩家I有 \(m\) 个策略，玩家II有 \(n\) 个策略。行玩家I的 \(m\) 个策略用 \(i = 1,\ldots ,m\) 表示，列玩家II的 \(n\) 个策略用 \(j = 1,\ldots ,n\) 表示。
\end{tcolorbox}

An \(m \times  n\) two-player game in strategic form is also called a bimatrix game (A, B). Here, \(A\) and \(B\) are the two \(m \times  n\) payoff matrices. The \(m\) rows are the pure strategies \(i\) of player I and the \(n\) columns are the pure strategies \(j\) of player II. For a row \(i\) and column \(j\) , the matrix entry \({a}_{ij}\) of \(A\) is the payoff to player \(\mathrm{I}\) , and the matrix entry \({b}_{ij}\) of \(B\) is the payoff to player II. The staggered payoffs in a table such as Figure 3.1 for the Prisoner’s Dilemma show \(A\) as the matrix of lower-left payoffs in each cell, and \(B\) as the matrix of upper-right payoffs in each cell, here

\begin{tcolorbox}
一个处于策略形式(strategic form)的 \(m \times  n\) 两人博弈也被称为双矩阵博弈(bimatrix game)(A, B)。这里， \(A\) 和 \(B\) 是两个 \(m \times  n\) 收益矩阵。 \(m\) 行是玩家 I 的纯策略 \(i\) ， \(n\) 列是玩家 II 的纯策略 \(j\) 。对于第 \(i\) 行和第 \(j\) 列， \(A\) 的矩阵元素 \({a}_{ij}\) 是玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 的收益， \(B\) 的矩阵元素 \({b}_{ij}\) 是玩家 II 的收益。像图 3.1 中囚徒困境(Prisoner’s Dilemma)表格里的交错收益(staggered payoffs)显示， \(A\) 是每个单元格左下角收益的矩阵， \(B\) 是每个单元格右上角收益的矩阵，这里
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
A = \left( \begin{array}{ll} 2 & 0 \\  3 & 1 \end{array}\right) ,\;B = \left( \begin{array}{ll} 2 & 3 \\  0 & 1 \end{array}\right) .
\]

This bimatrix game is symmetric because \(B = {A}^{\top }\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
这个双矩阵博弈是对称的(symmetric)，因为 \(B = {A}^{\top }\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Background material: Visualizing matrix multiplication

\begin{tcolorbox}
背景材料(background material):矩阵乘法的可视化(visualizing matrix multiplication)
\end{tcolorbox}

In a matrix product \(C = {AB}\) , the entry \({c}_{ij}\) of \(C\) in row \(i\) and column \(j\) is the scalar product of the \(i\) th row of \(A\) with the \(j\) th column of \(B\) , according to (6.2) and (6.1). There are two useful ways of visualizing the product \({AB}\) . Let \(A \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{m \times  k}\) and \(x \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{k}\) . Then \({Ax} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) . Let \(A = \left\lbrack  {{A}_{1}\cdots {A}_{k}}\right\rbrack\) , that is, \({A}_{1},\ldots ,{A}_{k}\) are the columns of \(A\) . Then \({Ax} = {A}_{1}{x}_{1} + \cdots  + {A}_{k}{x}_{k}\) , that is, \({Ax}\) is the linear combination of the columns of \(A\) with the components \({x}_{1},\ldots ,{x}_{k}\) of \(x\) as coefficients. In pictures for \(m = 3,k = 4\) :

\begin{tcolorbox}
在矩阵乘积 \(C = {AB}\) 中，根据(6.2)和(6.1)， \(C\) 中位于第 \(i\) 行和第 \(j\) 列的元素 \({c}_{ij}\) 是 \(A\) 的第 \(i\) 行与 \(B\) 的第 \(j\) 列的标量积(scalar product)。有两种直观理解乘积 \({AB}\) 的有用方法。设 \(A \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{m \times  k}\) 和 \(x \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{k}\) 。那么 \({Ax} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) 。设 \(A = \left\lbrack  {{A}_{1}\cdots {A}_{k}}\right\rbrack\) ，即 \({A}_{1},\ldots ,{A}_{k}\) 是 \(A\) 的列。那么 \({Ax} = {A}_{1}{x}_{1} + \cdots  + {A}_{k}{x}_{k}\) ，即 \({Ax}\) 是以 \(x\) 的分量 \({x}_{1},\ldots ,{x}_{k}\) 为系数的 \(A\) 的列的线性组合。对于 \(m = 3,k = 4\) 的图示如下:
\end{tcolorbox}

(6.4)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_154_517_623_616_165_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Let \(B \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{k \times  n}\) and \(B = \left\lbrack  {{B}_{1}\cdots {B}_{n}}\right\rbrack\) . Then the \(j\) th column of \({AB}\) is the linear combination \(A{B}_{j}\) of the columns of \(A\) with the components of \({B}_{j}\) as coefficients. We can visualize the columns of \({AB}\) as follows (for \(n = 2\) ):

\begin{tcolorbox}
设 \(B \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{k \times  n}\) 和 \(B = \left\lbrack  {{B}_{1}\cdots {B}_{n}}\right\rbrack\) 。那么 \({AB}\) 的第 \(j\) 列是以 \({B}_{j}\) 的分量为系数的 \(A\) 的列的线性组合 \(A{B}_{j}\) 。我们可以如下直观理解 \({AB}\) 的列(对于 \(n = 2\) ):
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.6\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_154_395_970_859_155_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

The second view of \({AB}\) is the same but using rows. Let \(y \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{k}\) , so that \({y}^{\top }B\) is a row vector in \({\mathbb{R}}^{1 \times  n}\) , given as the linear combination \({y}_{1}{b}_{1}^{\top } + \cdots  + {y}_{k}{b}_{k}^{\top }\) of the rows \({b}_{1}^{\top },\ldots ,{b}_{k}^{\top }\) of \(B\) (which we can write as \({B}^{\top } = \left\lbrack  {{b}_{1}\cdots {b}_{k}}\right\rbrack\) ),

\begin{tcolorbox}
对 \({AB}\) 的第二种理解方式相同，但使用行。设 \(y \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{k}\) ，使得 \({y}^{\top }B\) 是 \({\mathbb{R}}^{1 \times  n}\) 中的一个行向量，它表示为 \(B\) 的行 \({b}_{1}^{\top },\ldots ,{b}_{k}^{\top }\) 的线性组合 \({y}_{1}{b}_{1}^{\top } + \cdots  + {y}_{k}{b}_{k}^{\top }\) (我们可以写成 \({B}^{\top } = \left\lbrack  {{b}_{1}\cdots {b}_{k}}\right\rbrack\) )，
\end{tcolorbox}

(6.5)

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_154_456_1305_740_171_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Let \({A}^{\top } = \left\lbrack  {{a}_{1}\cdots {a}_{m}}\right\rbrack\) . Then the \(i\) th row of \({AB}\) is the linear combination \({a}_{i}^{\top }B\) of the rows of \(B\) with the components of the \(i\) th row \({a}_{i}^{\top }\) of \(A\) as coefficients:

\begin{tcolorbox}
设 \({A}^{\top } = \left\lbrack  {{a}_{1}\cdots {a}_{m}}\right\rbrack\) 。那么 \({AB}\) 的第 \(i\) 行是以 \(A\) 的第 \(i\) 行 \({a}_{i}^{\top }\) 的分量为系数的 \(B\) 的行的线性组合 \({a}_{i}^{\top }B\) :
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.6\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_154_398_1610_856_155_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

It is useful to acquire some routine in these manipulations. Most common is multiplication of a matrix with a column vector from the right as in (6.4) or with a row vector from the left as in (6.5).

\begin{tcolorbox}
熟悉这些操作是很有用的。最常见的是矩阵与列向量右乘，如式(6.4)所示；或者矩阵与行向量左乘，如式(6.5)所示。
\end{tcolorbox}

A mixed strategy is a randomized strategy of a player. It is defined as a lottery (probability distribution) on the set of pure strategies of that player. This is played as an "active randomization", where the player selects each pure strategy according to its probability. When the other player considers a best response to such a mixed strategy, she is assumed to know the probabilities but not the outcome of the random choice, and bases her decision on the resulting expected payoffs.

\begin{tcolorbox}
混合策略(mixed strategy)是玩家的一种随机策略。它被定义为该玩家纯策略集上的一种彩票(概率分布)。这是一种“主动随机化”玩法，玩家根据概率选择每种纯策略。当另一位玩家考虑对这种混合策略的最优反应(best response)时，假定她知道概率但不知道随机选择的结果，并根据由此产生的期望效用(expected payoff)来做出决策。
\end{tcolorbox}

A pure strategy is considered as a special mixed strategy, which chooses the pure strategy with probability one (in the same way as a deterministic outcome is considered a "random" outcome that occurs with probability one).

\begin{tcolorbox}
纯策略被视为一种特殊的混合策略，它以概率1选择该纯策略(就像确定性结果被视为以概率1发生的“随机”结果一样)。
\end{tcolorbox}

A mixed strategy is determined by the probabilities that it assigns to the player’s pure strategies. For player I, a mixed strategy \(x\) is therefore identified with the \(m\) -tuple of probabilities \({x}_{1},{x}_{2},\ldots ,{x}_{m}\) that it assigns to the pure strategies \(1,2,\ldots ,m\) of player I, which we write as the (column) vector \(x \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) . Similarly, a mixed strategy \(y\) of player II is an \(n\) -tuple of probabilities \({y}_{1},\ldots ,{y}_{n}\) for playing the pure strategies \(j = 1,\ldots ,n\) , written as \(y \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
混合策略由它分配给玩家纯策略的概率决定。对于玩家I，混合策略 \(x\) 因此由它分配给玩家I的纯策略 \(1,2,\ldots ,m\) 的 \(m\) 维(dimension)概率元组 \({x}_{1},{x}_{2},\ldots ,{x}_{m}\) 确定，我们将其写为(列)向量 \(x \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) 。类似地，玩家II的混合策略 \(y\) 是玩纯策略 \(j = 1,\ldots ,n\) 的 \(n\) 维概率元组 \({y}_{1},\ldots ,{y}_{n}\) ，写为 \(y \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The sets of mixed strategies of player I and player II are denoted by \(X\) and \(Y\) . The probabilities \({x}_{i}\) in a mixed strategy \(x\) are nonnegative and sum to 1, where we can write the conditions \({x}_{i} \geq  0\) for \(1 \leq  i \leq  m\) and \({x}_{1} + \cdots  + {x}_{m} = 1\) as \(x \geq  \mathbf{0}\) and \({\mathbf{1}}^{\top }x = 1\) , where in this context \(\mathbf{1} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) to match the dimension of \(x\) ; when we similarly write \({y}_{1} + \cdots  + {y}_{n} = 1\) as \({\mathbf{1}}^{\top }y = 1\) then \(\mathbf{1} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) . That is,

\begin{tcolorbox}
玩家I和玩家II的混合策略集分别用 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) 表示。混合策略 \(x\) 中的概率 \({x}_{i}\) 是非负的，且总和为1，我们可以将 \(1 \leq  i \leq  m\) 和 \({x}_{1} + \cdots  + {x}_{m} = 1\) 的条件 \({x}_{i} \geq  0\) 写为 \(x \geq  \mathbf{0}\) 和 \({\mathbf{1}}^{\top }x = 1\) ，在这种情况下 \(\mathbf{1} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) 以匹配 \(x\) 的维度；当我们类似地将 \({y}_{1} + \cdots  + {y}_{n} = 1\) 写为 \({\mathbf{1}}^{\top }y = 1\) 时，则 \(\mathbf{1} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) 。即，
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
X = \left\{  {x \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{m} \mid  x \geq  \mathbf{0},{\mathbf{1}}^{\top }x = 1}\right\}  ,\;Y = \left\{  {y \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n} \mid  y \geq  \mathbf{0},{\mathbf{1}}^{\top }y = 1}\right\}  . \tag{6.6}
\]

For \(x \in  X\) and \(y \in  Y\) , the expected payoff to player \(\mathrm{I}\) is \({x}^{\top }{Ay}\) , and the expected payoff to player II is \({x}^{\top }{By}\) . It is best to think of \({x}^{\top }{Ay}\) as \({x}^{\top }\left( {Ay}\right)\) , that is, as the scalar product of \(x\) with \({Ay}\) . For each row \(i\) , the entry of \({Ay}\) in row \(i\) is \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) , given by

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于 \(x \in  X\) 和 \(y \in  Y\) ，玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 的期望效用是 \({x}^{\top }{Ay}\) ，玩家II的期望效用是 \({x}^{\top }{By}\) 。最好将 \({x}^{\top }{Ay}\) 看作 \({x}^{\top }\left( {Ay}\right)\) ，即 \(x\) 与 \({Ay}\) 的标量积(scalar product)。对于每一行 \(i\) ， \({Ay}\) 中第 \(i\) 行的元素是 \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) ，由下式给出
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{\left( Ay\right) }_{i} = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{j = 1}}^{n}{a}_{ij}{y}_{j}\;\text{ for }1 \leq  i \leq  m. \tag{6.7}
\]

That is, \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) is the expected payoff to player \(I\) when he plays row \(i\) . Therefore, \({x}^{\top }{Ay}\) is the expected payoff to player I when the players use \(x\) and \(y\) , because

\begin{tcolorbox}
即， \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) 是玩家 \(I\) 选择第 \(i\) 行时的期望效用。因此， \({x}^{\top }{Ay}\) 是当玩家使用 \(x\) 和 \(y\) 时玩家I的期望效用，因为
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{x}^{\top }\left( {Ay}\right)  = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}{x}_{i}{\left( Ay\right) }_{i} = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}{x}_{i}\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{j = 1}}^{n}{a}_{ij}{y}_{j} = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{j = 1}}^{n}{x}_{i}{a}_{ij}{y}_{j}. \tag{6.8}
\]

Because the players choose their pure strategies \(i\) and \(j\) independently, the probability that they choose the pure strategy pair(i, j)is the product \({x}_{i}{y}_{j}\) of these probabilities, which is the coefficient of the payoff \({a}_{ij}\) in (6.8).

\begin{tcolorbox}
由于玩家独立地选择他们的纯策略 \(i\) 和 \(j\) ，他们选择纯策略对(i, j)的概率是这些概率的乘积 \({x}_{i}{y}_{j}\) ，这就是式(6.8)中收益 \({a}_{ij}\) 的系数。
\end{tcolorbox}

Analogously, \({x}^{\top }{By}\) is the expected payoff to player II when the players use the mixed strategies \(x\) and \(y\) . Here, it is best to read this as \(\left( {{x}^{\top }B}\right) y\) . The row vector \({x}^{\top }B\) is the vector of expected payoffs \({\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{j}\) to player II for her columns \(j\) . Multiplied with the column probabilities \({y}_{j}\) , the sum over all columns \(j\) is the expected payoff to player II, which in analogy to (6.8) is given by

\begin{tcolorbox}
类似地， \({x}^{\top }{By}\) 是当玩家使用混合策略 \(x\) 和 \(y\) 时玩家 II 的期望效用。在这里，最好将其理解为 \(\left( {{x}^{\top }B}\right) y\) 。行向量 \({x}^{\top }B\) 是玩家 II 对于她的列 \(j\) 的期望效用 \({\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{j}\) 的向量。与列概率 \({y}_{j}\) 相乘后，所有列 \(j\) 的总和就是玩家 II 的期望效用，类似于 (6.8)，其由下式给出
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\left( {{x}^{\top }B}\right) y = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{j = 1}}^{n}{\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{j}{y}_{j} = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{j = 1}}^{n}\left( {\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}{x}_{i}{b}_{ij}}\right) {y}_{j} = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{j = 1}}^{n}\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}{x}_{i}{b}_{ij}{y}_{j}.
\]

\section*{6.4 The Best-Response Condition}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{6.4 最优反应条件}
\end{tcolorbox}

A mixed-strategy equilibrium is a profile of mixed strategies such that no player can improve his expected payoff by unilaterally changing his own strategy. In a two-player game, an equilibrium is a pair(x, y)of mixed strategies such that \(x\) is a best response to \(y\) and vice versa. It does not seem easy to decide if \(x\) is a best response to \(y\) among all possible mixed strategies, that is, if \(x\) maximizes \({x}^{\top }{Ay}\) for all \(x\) in \(X\) , because \(X\) is an infinite set. However, the following proposition, known as the best-response condition, shows how to recognise this. This proposition is not difficult but important. We discuss it afterwards.

\begin{tcolorbox}
混合策略均衡是一种混合策略组合，使得没有玩家可以通过单方面改变自己的策略来提高其期望效用。在两人博弈中，均衡是一对混合策略 (x, y)，使得 \(x\) 是对 \(y\) 的最优反应，反之亦然。要判断 \(x\) 是否是所有可能的混合策略中对 \(y\) 的最优反应，即 \(x\) 是否能使 \({x}^{\top }{Ay}\) 对于 \(X\) 中的所有 \(x\) 都达到最大，似乎并不容易，因为 \(X\) 是一个无限集。然而，下面这个被称为最优反应条件的命题展示了如何识别这一点。这个命题并不难但很重要。我们随后会讨论它。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proposition 6.1 (Best-response condition, two players). Consider an \(m \times  n\) bimatrix game(A, B)and mixed strategies \(x \in  X\) and \(y \in  Y\) . Let

\begin{tcolorbox}
命题 6.1(两人博弈的最优反应条件)。考虑一个 \(m \times  n\) 双矩阵博弈 (A, B) 以及混合策略 \(x \in  X\) 和 \(y \in  Y\) 。设
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
u = \max \left\{  {{\left( Ay\right) }_{i} \mid  1 \leq  i \leq  m}\right\}  ,\;v = \max \left\{  {{\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{j} \mid  1 \leq  j \leq  n}\right\}  . \tag{6.9}
\]

Then

\begin{tcolorbox}
那么
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\left( {\forall \bar{x} \in  X : {x}^{\top }{Ay} \geq  {\bar{x}}^{\top }{Ay}}\right) \; \Leftrightarrow  \;{x}^{\top }{Ay} = u, \tag{6.10}
\]

that is, \(x\) is a best response to \(y\) if and only if \({x}^{\top }{Ay} = u\) . This is equivalent to

\begin{tcolorbox}
也就是说， \(x\) 是对 \(y\) 的最优反应，当且仅当 \({x}^{\top }{Ay} = u\) 。这等价于
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{x}_{i} > 0 \Rightarrow  {\left( Ay\right) }_{i} = u\;\left( {1 \leq  i \leq  m}\right) . \tag{6.11}
\]

Similarly, \(y\) is a best response to \(x\) if and only if \({x}^{\top }{By} = v\) , which is equivalent to

\begin{tcolorbox}
类似地， \(y\) 是对 \(x\) 的最优反应，当且仅当 \({x}^{\top }{By} = v\) ，这等价于
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{y}_{j} > 0 \Rightarrow  {\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{j} = v\;\left( {1 \leq  j \leq  n}\right) . \tag{6.12}
\]

Proof. Recall that \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) is the \(i\) th component of \({Ay}\) , which is the expected payoff to player I when he plays row \(i\) , as in (6.7). The maximum of these expected payoffs for the pure strategies of player \(\mathrm{I}\) is \(u\) . Then

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。回顾 \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) 是 \({Ay}\) 的第 \(i\) 个分量，如 (6.7) 所示，它是玩家 I 选择第 \(i\) 行时的期望效用。玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 的纯策略的这些期望效用的最大值是 \(u\) 。那么
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{x}^{\top }{Ay} = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}{x}_{i}{\left( Ay\right) }_{i} = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}{x}_{i}\left( {u - \left( {u - {\left( Ay\right) }_{i}}\right) }\right.
\]

\[
= \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}{x}_{i}u - \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}{x}_{i}\left( {u - {\left( Ay\right) }_{i}}\right)
\]

\[
= u - \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}{x}_{i}\left( {u - {\left( Ay\right) }_{i}}\right) . \tag{6.13}
\]

Because \({x}_{i} \geq  0\) and \(u - {\left( Ay\right) }_{i} \geq  0\) for any row \(i\) , we have \(\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}{x}_{i}\left( {u - {\left( Ay\right) }_{i}}\right)  \geq  0\) and therefore \({x}^{\top }{Ay} \leq  u\) . The expected payoff \({x}^{\top }{Ay}\) achieves the maximum \(u\) if and only if \(\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}{x}_{i}\left( {u - {\left( Ay\right) }_{i}}\right)  = 0\) , that is, if \({x}_{i} > 0\) implies \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i} = u\) , which shows (6.11). Condition (6.12) is shown analogously.

\begin{tcolorbox}
因为对于任何行 \(i\) 都有 \({x}_{i} \geq  0\) 和 \(u - {\left( Ay\right) }_{i} \geq  0\) ，我们有 \(\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}{x}_{i}\left( {u - {\left( Ay\right) }_{i}}\right)  \geq  0\) ，因此 \({x}^{\top }{Ay} \leq  u\) 。期望效用 \({x}^{\top }{Ay}\) 达到最大值 \(u\) 当且仅当 \(\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 1}}^{m}{x}_{i}\left( {u - {\left( Ay\right) }_{i}}\right)  = 0\) ，即如果 \({x}_{i} > 0\) 意味着 \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i} = u\) ，这就证明了 (6.11)。条件 (6.12) 的证明类似。
\end{tcolorbox}

We repeat this argument using vector notation. First, the scalar product of two nonnegative vectors is nonnegative: for \(x,w \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) ,

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们用向量符号重复这个论证。首先，两个非负向量的标量积是非负的:对于 \(x,w \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) ，
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
x \geq  \mathbf{0},\;w \geq  \mathbf{0}\; \Rightarrow  \;{x}^{\top }w \geq  0. \tag{6.14}
\]

Furthermore,(6.9) implies \(\mathbf{1}u \geq  {Ay}\) , that is, \(w = \mathbf{1}u - {Ay} \geq  \mathbf{0}\) . Hence,

\begin{tcolorbox}
此外，(6.9) 意味着 \(\mathbf{1}u \geq  {Ay}\) ，即 \(w = \mathbf{1}u - {Ay} \geq  \mathbf{0}\) 。因此，
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{x}^{\top }{Ay} = {x}^{\top }\left( {\mathbf{1}u - \left( {\mathbf{1}u - {Ay}}\right) }\right)  = {x}^{\top }\mathbf{1}u - {x}^{\top }\left( {\mathbf{1}u - {Ay}}\right)
\]

\[
= u - {x}^{\top }\left( {\mathbf{1}u - {Ay}}\right) \; \leq  u \tag{6.15}
\]

(note \({x}^{\top }\mathbf{1}u = \left( {{x}^{\top }\mathbf{1}}\right) u = 1 \cdot  u = u\) , which shows the advantage of using matrix products consistently). Second, (6.14) can be extended to

\begin{tcolorbox}
(注意 \({x}^{\top }\mathbf{1}u = \left( {{x}^{\top }\mathbf{1}}\right) u = 1 \cdot  u = u\) ，这显示了始终使用矩阵乘积的优势)。其次，(6.14) 可以扩展到
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
x \geq  \mathbf{0},\;w \geq  \mathbf{0}\; \Rightarrow  \;\left( {{x}^{\top }w = 0\; \Leftrightarrow  \;{x}_{i}{w}_{i} = 0\;\text{ for }1 \leq  i \leq  m}\right)
\]

so that \({x}^{\top }{Ay} = u\) in (6.15) if and only if \({x}_{i} = 0\) or \(u = {\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) for \(1 \leq  i \leq  m\) , which says the same as (6.11).

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此，当且仅当对于 \(1 \leq  i \leq  m\) 有 \({x}_{i} = 0\) 或 \(u = {\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) 时，(6.15) 式中的 \({x}^{\top }{Ay} = u\) 成立，这与 (6.11) 式表述相同。
\end{tcolorbox}

The mixed strategy \(x\) is a best response to \(y\) if \(x\) gives maximum expected payoff \({x}^{\top }{Ay}\) to player I among all mixed strategies in \(X\) , as in the left statement in (6.10). In contrast, the payoff \(u\) in (6.9) is the maximum expected payoff \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) among all pure strategies \(i\) of player I. According to (6.10), these payoffs are the same, so a player cannot improve his payoff by mixing. This is intuitive because mixing creates a weighted average \({x}^{\top }\left( {Ay}\right)\) of the expected payoffs \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) with the probabilities \({x}_{i}\) as weights as in (6.8).

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果混合策略 \(x\) 在 \(X\) 中的所有混合策略里，能为参与者 I 带来最大期望效用 \({x}^{\top }{Ay}\) ，如 (6.10) 式左侧表述的那样，那么 \(x\) 就是对 \(y\) 的最优反应。相比之下，(6.9) 式中的收益 \(u\) 是参与者 I 在所有纯策略 \(i\) 中的最大期望效用 \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) 。根据 (6.10) 式，这些收益是相同的，所以参与者无法通过混合策略来提高其收益。这是符合直觉的，因为混合策略会如 (6.8) 式那样，以概率 \({x}_{i}\) 为权重，对期望效用 \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) 进行加权平均 \({x}^{\top }\left( {Ay}\right)\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Furthermore,(6.11) states that \(x\) is a best response to \(y\) if and only if \(x\) only chooses pure best responses with positive probability \({x}_{i}\) . The condition whether a pure strategy is a best response is easy to check, because one only has to compute the \(m\) expected payoffs \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) for \(i = 1,\ldots ,m\) . For example, if player I has three pure strategies \(\left( {m = 3}\right)\) , and the expected payoffs in (6.7) are \({\left( Ay\right) }_{1} = 4,{\left( Ay\right) }_{2} = 4\) , and \({\left( Ay\right) }_{3} = 3\) , then only the first two strategies are pure best responses. If these expected payoffs are 3, 5, and 3, then only the second strategy is a best response. Clearly, at least one pure best response exists, because the numbers \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) in (6.11) take their maximum \(u\) for at least one \(i\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
此外，(6.11) 式表明，当且仅当 \(x\) 仅以正概率 \({x}_{i}\) 选择纯最优反应时， \(x\) 才是对 \(y\) 的最优反应。判断一个纯策略是否为最优反应的条件很容易验证，因为只需要计算 \(i = 1,\ldots ,m\) 的 \(m\) 个期望效用 \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) 即可。例如，如果参与者 I 有三个纯策略 \(\left( {m = 3}\right)\) ，且 (6.7) 式中的期望效用分别为 \({\left( Ay\right) }_{1} = 4,{\left( Ay\right) }_{2} = 4\) 和 \({\left( Ay\right) }_{3} = 3\) ，那么只有前两个策略是纯最优反应。如果这些期望效用分别为 3、5 和 3，那么只有第二个策略是最优反应。显然，至少存在一个纯最优反应，因为 (6.11) 式中的数字 \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) 至少会在一个 \(i\) 处取得最大值 \(u\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The best-response condition is the central tool for finding mixed-strategy equilibria, which we describe here for an example; a related approach is described in Section 6.6. Consider the following \(2 \times  3\) game(A, B), where we already marked the best-response payoffs and give the two payoff matrices (as determined by the table):

\begin{tcolorbox}
最优反应条件是寻找混合策略均衡的核心工具，我们在此通过一个例子来进行说明；相关方法将在 6.6 节介绍。考虑以下 \(2 \times  3\) 博弈 (A, B)，我们已经标记出了最优反应收益，并给出了两个收益矩阵(由表格确定):
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
A = \left( \begin{array}{lll} 0 & 4 & 0 \\  2 & 2 & 1 \end{array}\right) ,\;B = \left( \begin{array}{lll} 3 & 0 & 2 \\  1 & 6 & 4 \end{array}\right) . \tag{6.16}
\]

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.3\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_157_359_1713_500_353_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

This game has no equilibrium in pure strategies. For any pure strategy, its unique best response is a pure strategy of the other player, which does not result in an equilibrium, so that both players have to play mixed strategies that are not pure. We try to find a mixed equilibrium(x, y), were \(x = {\left( {x}_{1},{x}_{2}\right) }^{\top }\) and \(y = {\left( {y}_{1},{y}_{2},{y}_{3}\right) }^{\top }\) . (When writing down specific vectors, we will from now on omit the transposition sign for brevity and just write \(x = \left( {{x}_{1},{x}_{2}}\right)\) , for example.)

\begin{tcolorbox}
这个博弈不存在纯策略均衡。对于任何纯策略，其唯一的最优反应是另一个参与者的纯策略，这不会导致均衡，因此两个参与者都必须采用非纯的混合策略。我们尝试寻找一个混合均衡 (x, y)，其中 \(x = {\left( {x}_{1},{x}_{2}\right) }^{\top }\) 且 \(y = {\left( {y}_{1},{y}_{2},{y}_{3}\right) }^{\top }\) 。(为了简洁起见，从现在起，在写下具体向量时，我们将省略转置符号，例如只写 \(x = \left( {{x}_{1},{x}_{2}}\right)\) 。)
\end{tcolorbox}

Because player I has only two pure strategies, both have to be played with positive probability, that is, \({x}_{1} > 0\) and \({x}_{2} > 0\) . According to the best-response condition (6.11), player I has to be indifferent between his two rows, that is,

\begin{tcolorbox}
由于参与者 I 只有两个纯策略，这两个策略都必须以正概率被采用，即 \({x}_{1} > 0\) 且 \({x}_{2} > 0\) 。根据最优反应条件 (6.11)，参与者 I 必须对他的两行策略无差异，即
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{\left( Ay\right) }_{1} = {\left( Ay\right) }_{2} = u,\;4{y}_{2} = 2{y}_{1} + 2{y}_{2} + {y}_{3} = u,\;2{y}_{2} = 2{y}_{1} + {y}_{3}. \tag{6.17}
\]

We have already seen that this cannot be achieved when \(y\) is a pure strategy, so we now consider any pair of strategies that are played with positive probability under \(y\) . Suppose first that these are the first two columns, that is, \({y}_{1} > 0\) and \({y}_{2} > 0\) and \({y}_{3} = 0\) . Then the unique solution to (6.17) (and \({y}_{1} + {y}_{2} = 1\) ) is \({y}_{1} = {y}_{2} = \frac{1}{2}\) with \(u = 2\) . In turn, by (6.12) player II has to be indifferent between her first two columns, that is,

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们已经看到，当 \(y\) 是纯策略时，这无法实现，因此我们现在考虑在 \(y\) 下以正概率采用的任意一对策略。首先假设这些策略是前两列，即 \({y}_{1} > 0\) 、 \({y}_{2} > 0\) 和 \({y}_{3} = 0\) 。那么，(6.17)(以及 \({y}_{1} + {y}_{2} = 1\) )的唯一解是 \({y}_{1} = {y}_{2} = \frac{1}{2}\) ，其中 \(u = 2\) 。继而，根据(6.12)，玩家 II 必须对她的前两列无差异，即
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{1} = {\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{2} = v,\;3{x}_{1} + {x}_{2} = 6{x}_{2},\;3{x}_{1} = 5{x}_{2} \tag{6.18}
\]

which has the unique solution \({x}_{1} = \frac{5}{8},{x}_{2} = \frac{3}{8}\) and \({\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{1} = {\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{2} = \frac{18}{8} = \frac{9}{4}\) . However, we also have to make sure that \(\frac{9}{4}\) is the best-response payoff to player II, a consideration that is not necessary in \(2 \times  2\) games where it suffices that both players are indifferent between their two strategies. Here we have \({\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{3} = \frac{22}{8} = \frac{11}{4} > \frac{9}{4}\) . That is, even though player II is indifferent between her first two columns, which is necessary because \({y}_{1} > 0\) and \({y}_{2} > 0\) , these are not pure best responses against \(x = \left( {\frac{5}{8},\frac{3}{8}}\right)\) as required by (6.12). So there is no mixed equilibrium where player \(\mathrm{L}\) plays her first two columns.

\begin{tcolorbox}
其唯一解为 \({x}_{1} = \frac{5}{8},{x}_{2} = \frac{3}{8}\) 和 \({\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{1} = {\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{2} = \frac{18}{8} = \frac{9}{4}\) 。然而，我们还必须确保 \(\frac{9}{4}\) 是玩家 II 的最优反应收益，在 \(2 \times  2\) 博弈中这一考虑并非必要，在这类博弈中，只要两个玩家对他们的两种策略无差异即可。这里我们有 \({\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{3} = \frac{22}{8} = \frac{11}{4} > \frac{9}{4}\) 。也就是说，尽管玩家 II 对她的前两列无差异，这是因为 \({y}_{1} > 0\) 和 \({y}_{2} > 0\) 所必需的，但根据(6.12)的要求，这些并非针对 \(x = \left( {\frac{5}{8},\frac{3}{8}}\right)\) 的纯最优反应。因此，不存在玩家 \(\mathrm{L}\) 采用她的前两列的混合均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

Next, suppose player II chooses her first and third column, with \({y}_{1} > 0,{y}_{2} = 0\) and \({y}_{3} > 0\) . However, then the right equation in (6.17) states \(0 = 2{y}_{1} + {y}_{3}\) , which is not possible. Intuitively, when player II plays only the first and third column in (6.16), then the bottom row \(D\) is the unique best response and player I cannot be made indifferent.

\begin{tcolorbox}
接下来，假设玩家 II 选择她的第一列和第三列，其中 \({y}_{1} > 0,{y}_{2} = 0\) 和 \({y}_{3} > 0\) 。然而，此时(6.17)中的右侧方程表明 \(0 = 2{y}_{1} + {y}_{3}\) ，这是不可能的。直观地说，当玩家 II 在(6.16)中仅采用第一列和第三列时，那么底行 \(D\) 是唯一的最优反应，并且无法使玩家 I 无差异。
\end{tcolorbox}

This leaves the final possibility that \({y}_{1} = 0\) and \({y}_{2} > 0\) and \({y}_{3} > 0\) where (6.17) becomes \(2{y}_{2} = {y}_{3}\) and therefore \({y}_{2} = \frac{1}{3},{y}_{3} = \frac{2}{3}\) . Similar to (6.18), we now need

\begin{tcolorbox}
这就只剩下最后一种可能性，即 \({y}_{1} = 0\) 、 \({y}_{2} > 0\) 和 \({y}_{3} > 0\) ，此时(6.17)变为 \(2{y}_{2} = {y}_{3}\) ，因此 \({y}_{2} = \frac{1}{3},{y}_{3} = \frac{2}{3}\) 。与(6.18)类似，我们现在需要
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{2} = {\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{3} = v,\;6{x}_{2} = 2{x}_{1} + 4{x}_{2},\;2{x}_{1} = 2{x}_{2} \tag{6.19}
\]

which has the unique solution \({x}_{1} = {x}_{2} = \frac{1}{2}\) with \({\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{2} = {\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{3} = v = 3\) where \(v\) is indeed the maximum of expected column payoffs because \({\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{1} = 2 < 3\) . We have found the mixed equilibrium \(x = \left( {\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}}\right) ,y = \left( {0,\frac{1}{3},\frac{2}{3}}\right)\) . There is no equilibrium where player II chooses all three columns with positive probability because this implies the column indifferences (6.18) and (6.19), which have contradictory solutions \(x\) . The mixed equilibrium is therefore unique.

\begin{tcolorbox}
其唯一解为 \({x}_{1} = {x}_{2} = \frac{1}{2}\) ，其中 \({\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{2} = {\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{3} = v = 3\) ，由于 \({\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{1} = 2 < 3\) ， \(v\) 确实是预期列收益的最大值。我们已经找到了混合均衡 \(x = \left( {\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}}\right) ,y = \left( {0,\frac{1}{3},\frac{2}{3}}\right)\) 。不存在玩家 II 以正概率选择所有三列的均衡，因为这意味着列无差异条件(6.18)和(6.19)，而它们的解 \(x\) 相互矛盾。因此，混合均衡是唯一的。
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\Rightarrow\) Exercise 6.2 asks you to find all mixed equilibria of a \(3 \times  2\) game, which can be done similarly and illustrates the best-response condition. It also motivates methods for finding mixed equilibria that we develop in Section 6.6.

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\Rightarrow\) 练习 6.2 要求你找出一个 \(3 \times  2\) 博弈的所有混合均衡，这可以用类似的方法完成，并能说明最优反应条件。它还为我们在第 6.6 节中开发的寻找混合均衡的方法提供了动机。
\end{tcolorbox}

The best-response condition applies also to a game with more than two players, which we explain in the remainder of this section. In Definition 3.1 we defined a commonly used notation and the concepts of best response and equilibrium for an \(N\) -player game. The following definition defines its mixed extension. For the finite strategy set \({S}_{i}\) of player \(i\) , we denote the set of mixed strategies over \({S}_{i}\) by \(\Delta \left( {S}_{i}\right)\) , in agreement with Definition 5.1.

\begin{tcolorbox}
最优反应条件同样适用于有两个以上参与者的博弈，我们将在本节的剩余部分对此进行解释。在定义3.1中，我们为一个 \(N\) 参与者博弈定义了常用符号以及最优反应和均衡的概念。以下定义给出了它的混合扩展。对于参与者 \(i\) 的有限策略集 \({S}_{i}\) ，根据定义5.1，我们用 \(\Delta \left( {S}_{i}\right)\) 表示 \({S}_{i}\) 上的混合策略集。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 6.2 (Mixed extension). Consider an \(N\) -player game in strategic form with finite strategy set \({S}_{i}\) for player \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) and payoff \({u}_{i}\left( s\right)\) for each strategy profile \(s = \left( {{s}_{1},\ldots ,{s}_{N}}\right)\) in \(S = {S}_{1} \times  \cdots  \times  {S}_{N}\) . Let \({X}_{i} = \Delta \left( {S}_{i}\right)\) be the set of mixed strategies \({\sigma }_{i}\) of player \(i\) , where \({\sigma }_{i}\left( {s}_{i}\right)\) is the probability that \({\sigma }_{i}\) selects the pure strategy \({s}_{i} \in  {S}_{i}\) . (If \({\sigma }_{i}\left( {s}_{i}\right)  = 1\) for some \({s}_{i}\) then \({\sigma }_{i}\) is considered the same as the pure strategy \({s}_{i}\) .) Then the mixed extension of this game is the \(N\) -player game in strategic form with the (infinite) strategy sets \({X}_{1},\ldots ,{X}_{N}\) for the \(N\) players, where for a mixed-strategy profile \(\sigma  = \left( {{\sigma }_{1},\ldots ,{\sigma }_{N}}\right)\) player \(i\) receives the expected payoff

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义6.2(混合扩展)。考虑一个策略形式的 \(N\) 参与者博弈，其中参与者 \(i = 1,\ldots ,N\) 的有限策略集为 \({S}_{i}\) ，对于 \(S = {S}_{1} \times  \cdots  \times  {S}_{N}\) 中的每个策略组合 \(s = \left( {{s}_{1},\ldots ,{s}_{N}}\right)\) ，其支付为 \({u}_{i}\left( s\right)\) 。设 \({X}_{i} = \Delta \left( {S}_{i}\right)\) 为参与者 \(i\) 的混合策略 \({\sigma }_{i}\) 的集合，其中 \({\sigma }_{i}\left( {s}_{i}\right)\) 是 \({\sigma }_{i}\) 选择纯策略 \({s}_{i} \in  {S}_{i}\) 的概率。(如果对于某个 \({s}_{i}\) 有 \({\sigma }_{i}\left( {s}_{i}\right)  = 1\) ，则 \({\sigma }_{i}\) 被视为与纯策略 \({s}_{i}\) 相同。)那么，该博弈的混合扩展是一个策略形式的 \(N\) 参与者博弈，其(无限)策略集为 \({X}_{1},\ldots ,{X}_{N}\) ，对于所有 \(N\) 个参与者，在混合策略组合 \(\sigma  = \left( {{\sigma }_{1},\ldots ,{\sigma }_{N}}\right)\) 下，参与者 \(i\) 获得期望效用
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{U}_{i}\left( \sigma \right)  = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{s \in  S}}{u}_{i}\left( s\right) \mathop{\prod }\limits_{{k = 1}}^{N}{\sigma }_{k}\left( {s}_{k}\right) .
\]

An equilibrium (or Nash equilibrium) \(\sigma\) of the mixed extension is called a mixed equilibrium of the original game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
混合扩展的一个均衡(或纳什均衡) \(\sigma\) 被称为原博弈的混合均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

The following proposition extends the best-response condition to an \(N\) -player game. As in the case of two players, it allows us to verify that a mixed-strategy profile is an equilibrium by the condition that a player's mixed strategy only selects pure best responses with positive probability.

\begin{tcolorbox}
以下命题将最优反应条件扩展到了一个 \(N\) 参与者博弈中。与两个参与者的情况一样，它使我们能够通过“参与者的混合策略仅以正概率选择纯最优反应”这一条件来验证一个混合策略组合是否为均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proposition 6.3 (Best-response condition, \(N\) players). Consider the mixed extension of an \(N\) -player game with the notation in Definition 6.2. Let \({\sigma }_{i} \in  {X}_{i}\) be a mixed strategy of player \(i\) , let \({\sigma }_{-i}\) be a partial profile of mixed strategies of the other \(N - 1\) players, and let

\begin{tcolorbox}
命题6.3(最优反应条件， \(N\) 个参与者)。考虑一个 \(N\) 参与者博弈的混合扩展，采用定义6.2中的符号。设 \({\sigma }_{i} \in  {X}_{i}\) 为参与者 \(i\) 的混合策略，设 \({\sigma }_{-i}\) 为其他 \(N - 1\) 个参与者的混合策略的部分组合，并且设
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{v}_{i} = \max \left\{  {{U}_{i}\left( {{s}_{i},{\sigma }_{-i}}\right)  \mid  {s}_{i} \in  {S}_{i}}\right\}
\]

be the best possible expected payoff against \({\sigma }_{-i}\) for a pure strategy \({s}_{i}\) of player \(i\) . Then \({\sigma }_{i}\) is a best response to \({\sigma }_{-i}\) if and only if

\begin{tcolorbox}
为参与者 \(i\) 的纯策略 \({s}_{i}\) 针对 \({\sigma }_{-i}\) 所能获得的最大期望效用。那么，当且仅当
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{\sigma }_{i}\left( {s}_{i}\right)  > 0 \Rightarrow  {U}_{i}\left( {{s}_{i},{\sigma }_{-i}}\right)  = {v}_{i}\;\left( {{s}_{i} \in  {S}_{i}}\right) ,
\]

that is, if \({\sigma }_{i}\) only selects pure best responses \({s}_{i}\) against \({\sigma }_{i}\) with positive probability. Proof. The proof is the same as for Proposition 6.1 with the pure strategy \({s}_{i}\) (where \(i\) denotes a player) instead of row \(i\) (where \(i\) denotes a pure strategy) and probability \({\sigma }_{i}\left( {s}_{i}\right)\) instead of \({x}_{i}\) in (6.11). The crucial condition is that the expected payoff to player \(i\) is linear in his mixed-strategy probabilities \({\sigma }_{i}\left( {s}_{i}\right)\) , because for a mixed-strategy profile \(\sigma\) ,

\begin{tcolorbox}
