这些向量仿射无关，因为在 (7.4) 中考虑的 \({\mathbb{R}}^{1 + d}\) 中对应的向量 \({z}^{i} = \left( \begin{matrix} 1 \\  {v}^{i}\left( k\right)  \end{matrix}\right)\) 线性无关，因为 \(\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 0}}^{d}{z}^{i}{\lambda }_{i} = \mathbf{0}\) 意味着 \({\lambda }_{d} = 0\) ，然后 \({\lambda }_{d - 1} = 0\) ，依此类推，直到 \({\lambda }_{0} = 0\) 。因此，它们的凸包(convex hull)定义了一个单纯形(simplex)，我们称之为 \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Let \(k = 1\) . The vertices of \({T}_{d}\left( 1\right)\) are defined by starting from the all-zero vector \({v}^{0}\) and changing one coordinate at a time from 0 to 1 until we arrive at the all-one vector \({v}^{d} = \mathbf{1} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) . In order to construct \({T}_{d}\left( 1\right)\) we have chosen the coordinates in their natural order \(1,\ldots ,d\) . As a generalization, consider an arbitrary order \(\pi \left( 1\right) ,\ldots ,\pi \left( d\right)\) of the coordinates, that is, a permutation \(\pi\) of \(1,\ldots ,d\) (a bijection on the set \(\{ 1,\ldots ,d\} )\) . We denote the set of all permutations of \(1,\ldots ,d\) by \(\operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) . For \(\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) , let \({v}_{\pi }^{0} = \mathbf{0} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) , and for \(i = 1,\ldots ,d\) define

\begin{tcolorbox}
设 \(k = 1\) 。 \({T}_{d}\left( 1\right)\) 的顶点是从全零向量 \({v}^{0}\) 开始定义的，每次将一个坐标从 0 变为 1，直到得到全 1 向量 \({v}^{d} = \mathbf{1} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) 。为了构造 \({T}_{d}\left( 1\right)\) ，我们按自然顺序 \(1,\ldots ,d\) 选择坐标。作为推广，考虑坐标的任意顺序 \(\pi \left( 1\right) ,\ldots ,\pi \left( d\right)\) ，即 \(1,\ldots ,d\) 的一个排列(permutation) \(\pi\) (集合 \(\{ 1,\ldots ,d\} )\) 上的双射)。我们用 \(\operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) 表示 \(1,\ldots ,d\) 的所有排列的集合。对于 \(\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) ，设 \({v}_{\pi }^{0} = \mathbf{0} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) ，对于 \(i = 1,\ldots ,d\) 定义
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{v}_{\pi }^{i} = {v}_{\pi }^{i - 1} + {e}_{\pi \left( i\right) }. \tag{7.26}
\]

That is, the \(i\) th vector \({v}_{\pi }^{i}\) is obtained from the previous vector by adding the unit vector \({e}_{\pi \left( i\right) }\) , which changes the coordinate at position \(\pi \left( i\right)\) from 0 to 1 . For example, if \(\left( {\pi \left( 1\right) ,\ldots ,\pi \left( d\right) }\right)  = \left( {2,3,1,4}\right)\) , then

\begin{tcolorbox}
也就是说，第 \(i\) 个向量 \({v}_{\pi }^{i}\) 是通过在前一个向量上加上单位向量 \({e}_{\pi \left( i\right) }\) 得到的，这将位置 \(\pi \left( i\right)\) 处的坐标从 0 变为 1 。例如，如果 \(\left( {\pi \left( 1\right) ,\ldots ,\pi \left( d\right) }\right)  = \left( {2,3,1,4}\right)\) ，那么
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{v}_{\pi }^{0} = \left( \begin{array}{l} 0 \\  0 \\  0 \\  0 \end{array}\right) ,{v}_{\pi }^{1} = \left( \begin{array}{l} 0 \\  1 \\  0 \\  0 \end{array}\right) ,{v}_{\pi }^{2} = \left( \begin{array}{l} 0 \\  1 \\  1 \\  0 \end{array}\right) ,{v}_{\pi }^{3} = \left( \begin{array}{l} 1 \\  1 \\  1 \\  0 \end{array}\right) ,{v}_{\pi }^{4} = \left( \begin{array}{l} 1 \\  1 \\  1 \\  1 \end{array}\right) . \tag{7.27}
\]

This defines a "monotonic path" of vertices of \({C}_{d}\) that starts at the all-zero vector0 and ends at the all-one vector1. (It is easy to see that the vertices of the unit cube \({C}_{d}\) are the \({2}^{d}\) vectors in \({\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) where each component is either 0 or 1 .) The convex hull of these vectors defines the simplex

\begin{tcolorbox}
这定义了 \({C}_{d}\) 的顶点的一条“单调路径(path)”，它从全零向量 0 开始，到全 1 向量 1 结束。(很容易看出，单位立方体(unit cube) \({C}_{d}\) 的顶点是 \({\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) 中的 \({2}^{d}\) 个向量，其中每个分量要么是 0 要么是 1 。)这些向量的凸包(convex hull)定义了单纯形
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{T}_{d}^{\pi } = \operatorname{conv}\left\{  {{v}_{\pi }^{0},{v}_{\pi }^{1},\ldots ,{v}_{\pi }^{d}}\right\}  .
\]

The following lemma describes this simplex in terms of \(d + 1\) linear inequalities rather than as a convex hull of vertices.

\begin{tcolorbox}
以下引理用 \(d + 1\) 个线性不等式而不是顶点的凸包(convex hull)来描述这个单纯形(simplex)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 7.21. Let \(x \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) and \(\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) . Then \(x \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) if and only if

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理7.21。设 \(x \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) 和 \(\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) 。那么当且仅当
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
1 \geq  {x}_{\pi \left( 1\right) } \geq  {x}_{\pi \left( 2\right) } \geq  \cdots  \geq  {x}_{\pi \left( d\right) } \geq  0. \tag{7.28}
\]

Proof. Suppose (7.28) holds. Define \({y}_{0},{y}_{1},\ldots ,{y}_{d}\) by

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。假设 (7.28) 成立。通过以下方式定义 \({y}_{0},{y}_{1},\ldots ,{y}_{d}\)
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{y}_{0} = 1 - {x}_{\pi \left( 1\right) }
\]

\[
{y}_{1} = {x}_{\pi \left( 1\right) } - {x}_{\pi \left( 2\right) }
\]

\[
\vdots  \tag{7.29}
\]

\[
{y}_{d - 1} = {x}_{\pi \left( {d - 1}\right) } - {x}_{\pi \left( d\right) }
\]

\[
{y}_{d} = {x}_{\pi \left( d\right) }
\]

which implies

\begin{tcolorbox}
这意味着
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{x}_{\pi \left( j\right) } = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = j}}^{d}{y}_{i}\;\left( {1 \leq  j \leq  d}\right)
\]

and by (7.29) and (7.28)

\begin{tcolorbox}
并且由 (7.29) 和 (7.28)
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 0}}^{d}{y}_{i} = 1,\;{y}_{i} \geq  0\;\left( {1 \leq  i \leq  d}\right) . \tag{7.30}
\]

By (7.26), as the example (7.27) illustrates, the components of the vectors \({v}_{\pi }^{i}\) are given by

\begin{tcolorbox}
由(7.26)式，正如示例(7.27)所示，向量 \({v}_{\pi }^{i}\) 的分量由下式给出
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{\left( {v}_{\pi }^{i}\right) }_{\pi \left( j\right) } = \left\{  {\begin{array}{ll} 0 & \text{ if }i < j \\  1 & \text{ if }i \geq  j \end{array}\;\left( {0 \leq  i \leq  d,1 \leq  j \leq  d}\right) .}\right.
\]

Hence, by (7.29),

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此，由(7.29)式
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 0}}^{d}{\left( {v}_{\pi }^{i}\right) }_{\pi \left( j\right) }{y}_{i} = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = j}}^{d}{y}_{i} = {x}_{\pi \left( j\right) }\;\left( {1 \leq  j \leq  d}\right)
\]

and therefore

\begin{tcolorbox}
所以
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
x = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{i = 0}}^{d}{v}_{\pi }^{i}{y}_{i} \tag{7.31}
\]

That is, \(x\) is by (7.30) a convex combination of the vectors \({v}_{\pi }^{0},\ldots ,{v}_{\pi }^{d}\) , which shows that \(x \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) . Moreover, \({y}_{0},\ldots ,{y}_{d}\) in (7.29) are the barycentric coordinates of \(x\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
也就是说，由(7.30)式可知， \(x\) 是向量 \({v}_{\pi }^{0},\ldots ,{v}_{\pi }^{d}\) 的凸组合，这表明 \(x \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 。此外，(7.29)式中的 \({y}_{0},\ldots ,{y}_{d}\) 是 \(x\) 的重心坐标。
\end{tcolorbox}

Conversely, any vertex \(x = {v}_{\pi }^{i}\) of \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) for \(0 \leq  i \leq  d\) fulfills (7.28), where the only inequality that holds strictly (if \(0 < i < d\) ) is when the component of \(x\) changes from 1 to 0 . Therefore, the inequalities (7.28) also hold for any convex combination of these vertices, that is, for any element of \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
反之，对于 \(0 \leq  i \leq  d\) ， \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 的任何顶点 \(x = {v}_{\pi }^{i}\) 都满足(7.28)式，其中唯一严格成立的不等式(如果 \(0 < i < d\) )是当 \(x\) 的分量从 1 变为 0 时。因此，不等式(7.28)对于这些顶点的任何凸组合也成立，即对于 \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 的任何元素都成立。
\end{tcolorbox}

The simplex \({T}_{d}\left( 1\right)\) is \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) for the identity permutation \(\pi\) . It is easy to see that \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)  = \left\{  {{xk} \mid  x \in  {T}_{d}\left( 1\right) }\right\}\) and therefore by Lemma 7.21

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于恒等排列 \(\pi\) ，单纯形 \({T}_{d}\left( 1\right)\) 是 \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 。容易看出 \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)  = \left\{  {{xk} \mid  x \in  {T}_{d}\left( 1\right) }\right\}\) ，因此根据引理 7.21
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
z \in  {T}_{d}\left( k\right) \; \Leftrightarrow  \;k \geq  {z}_{1} \geq  {z}_{2} \geq  \cdots  \geq  {z}_{d} \geq  0. \tag{7.32}
\]

Lemma 7.22. The set \(\left\{  {{T}_{d}^{\pi } \mid  \pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right) }\right\}\) is a triangulation of the unit cube \({C}_{d}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理 7.22. 集合 \(\left\{  {{T}_{d}^{\pi } \mid  \pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right) }\right\}\) 是单位立方体 \({C}_{d}\) 的一个三角剖分。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Let \(x = {\left( {x}_{1},\ldots ,{x}_{d}\right) }^{\top } \in  {C}_{d}\) , that is, \(1 \geq  {x}_{i} \geq  0\) for \(1 \leq  i \leq  d\) , and let \(\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) . Sort the components \({x}_{i}\) of \(x\) so that (7.28) holds (the permutation \(\pi\) is not unique if some components of \(x\) are equal to each other). By Lemma 7.21, \(x \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) . This shows \({C}_{d} = \mathop{\bigcup }\limits_{{\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right) }}{T}_{d}^{\pi }\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明. 设 \(x = {\left( {x}_{1},\ldots ,{x}_{d}\right) }^{\top } \in  {C}_{d}\) ，即对于 \(1 \leq  i \leq  d\) 有 \(1 \geq  {x}_{i} \geq  0\) ，并设 \(\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) 。对 \(x\) 的分量 \({x}_{i}\) 进行排序，使得(7.28)式成立(如果 \(x\) 的某些分量彼此相等，则排列 \(\pi\) 不唯一)。根据引理 7.21， \(x \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 。这表明 \({C}_{d} = \mathop{\bigcup }\limits_{{\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right) }}{T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

If \(\pi ,{\pi }^{\prime } \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) and \(\pi  \neq  {\pi }^{\prime }\) then \({T}_{d}^{\pi } \nsubseteq  {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) , by taking some \(x \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) with strict inequalities in (7.28) where clearly \(x \notin  {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) . It remains to show that the intersection of any two sub-simplices \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) and \({T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) is a face of both. Any \(x\) in \({T}_{d}^{\pi } \cap  {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) fulfills (7.28) as well as

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果 \(\pi ,{\pi }^{\prime } \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) 且 \(\pi  \neq  {\pi }^{\prime }\) ，那么通过选取(7.28)式中某些严格不等式对应的 \(x \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) (显然 \(x \notin  {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) )，有 \({T}_{d}^{\pi } \nsubseteq  {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) 。还需证明任意两个子单纯形 \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 和 \({T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) 的交集是它们两者的一个面。 \({T}_{d}^{\pi } \cap  {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) 中的任何 \(x\) 都满足(7.28)式以及
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{x}_{{\pi }^{\prime }\left( 1\right) } \geq  {x}_{{\pi }^{\prime }\left( 2\right) } \geq  \cdots  \geq  {x}_{{\pi }^{\prime }\left( d\right) }. \tag{7.33}
\]

Any inequality that holds in reverse order in (7.28) and (7.33) has to hold as an equality. This includes any implied equality; for example, if for \(d = 3\) we have \({x}_{1} \geq  {x}_{2} \geq  {x}_{3}\) and \({x}_{2} \geq  {x}_{3} \geq  {x}_{1}\) , then this implies \({x}_{1} = {x}_{3}\) as well as \({x}_{2} = {x}_{3}\) . The intersection \({T}_{d}^{\pi } \cap  {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) is then characterized by (7.28) where some inequalities are replaced by equalities. The barycentric coordinates \({y}_{1},\ldots ,{y}_{d - 1}\) in (7.29) of \(x\) that correspond to these equalities are then set to zero, which means that the corresponding vertex is not used in the representation of \(x\) in (7.31). Hence, \(x\) is only a convex combination of the remaining vertices. These are the vertices of \({T}_{d}^{\pi } \cap  {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) , which is therefore a common face of the two sub-simplices \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) and \({T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) , as claimed. It may be the case that all inequalities in (7.28) are equalities, so that the common face has just the vertices0and1.

\begin{tcolorbox}
任何在(7.28)和(7.33)中反向成立的不等式都必须以等式形式成立。这包括任何隐含的等式；例如，如果对于 \(d = 3\) 我们有 \({x}_{1} \geq  {x}_{2} \geq  {x}_{3}\) 和 \({x}_{2} \geq  {x}_{3} \geq  {x}_{1}\) ，那么这意味着 \({x}_{1} = {x}_{3}\) 以及 \({x}_{2} = {x}_{3}\) 。交集 \({T}_{d}^{\pi } \cap  {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) 随后由(7.28)表征，其中一些不等式被等式取代。在(7.29)中 \(x\) 的与这些等式对应的重心坐标(barycentric coordinates) \({y}_{1},\ldots ,{y}_{d - 1}\) 随后被设为零，这意味着在(7.31)中 \(x\) 的表示不使用相应的顶点。因此， \(x\) 只是剩余顶点的凸组合(convex combination)。这些是 \({T}_{d}^{\pi } \cap  {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) 的顶点，因此它是两个子单纯形(sub - simplex) \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 和 \({T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) 的公共面(face)，正如所声称的那样。可能会出现(7.28)中的所有不等式都是等式的情况，使得公共面只有顶点0和1。
\end{tcolorbox}

There are \(d\) ! many permutations \(\pi\) of the numbers \(1,\ldots ,d\) . Any two simplices \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) and \({T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) have the same shape and size because they only differ in the order of their coordinates, and they intersect in a set of dimension \(d - 1\) or lower which has volume zero in \({\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) . Because the unit cube \({C}_{d}\) has volume 1, Lemma 7.22 implies that each simplex \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) has volume \(\frac{1}{d!}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
数字 \(1,\ldots ,d\) 有 \(d\) ! 种排列(permutation) \(\pi\) 。任意两个单纯形(simplex) \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 和 \({T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) 具有相同的形状和大小，因为它们仅在坐标顺序上有所不同，并且它们的交集是一个维度(dimension)为 \(d - 1\) 或更低的集合，该集合在 \({\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) 中的体积为零。由于单位立方体(unit cube) \({C}_{d}\) 的体积为1，引理7.22意味着每个单纯形 \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 的体积为 \(\frac{1}{d!}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

We now use the triangulation of the unit cube \({C}_{d}\) in Lemma 7.22 to construct a triangulation of the simplex \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) in (7.25), for some integer \(k \geq  1\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
现在，我们使用引理7.22中单位立方体 \({C}_{d}\) 的三角剖分(triangulation)来构造(7.25)中单纯形 \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 的三角剖分，其中 \(k \geq  1\) 为某个整数。
\end{tcolorbox}

For a polytope \(P\) in \({\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) such as \({C}_{d}\) or \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) and an integer "grid" vector \(g =\)  \({\left( {g}_{1},\ldots ,{g}_{d}\right) }^{\top } \in  {\mathbb{Z}}^{d}\) , let \(g + P = \{ g + x \mid  x \in  P\}\) . Let

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于 \({\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) 中的有界多面体(polytope) \(P\) ，例如 \({C}_{d}\) 或 \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) ，以及一个整数“网格”向量 \(g =\)  \({\left( {g}_{1},\ldots ,{g}_{d}\right) }^{\top } \in  {\mathbb{Z}}^{d}\) ，设 \(g + P = \{ g + x \mid  x \in  P\}\) 。设
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\sum  = \left\{  {g + {T}_{d}^{\pi } \mid  \pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right) ,g \in  {\mathbb{Z}}^{d},g + {T}_{d}^{\pi } \subseteq  {T}_{d}\left( k\right) }\right\}  . \tag{7.34}
\]

In other words, we consider any simplex \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) of the triangulation of \({C}_{d}\) , translate it by adding some integer vector \(g\) , and if the resulting set \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) is a subset of \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) then this is a sub-simplex in \(\sum\) . By (7.32) this can only hold if \(0 \leq  {g}_{i} \leq  k\) for \(1 \leq  i \leq  d\) because otherwise \(g + \mathbf{0} \notin  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) (where \(\mathbf{0} \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) ), so \(\sum\) is finite. Figure 7.17 shows \(\sum\) for \(d = 3\) and \(k = 2\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
换句话说，我们考虑 \({C}_{d}\) 的三角剖分中的任意单纯形 \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) ，通过加上某个整数向量 \(g\) 对其进行平移，如果得到的集合 \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 是 \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 的子集，那么这就是 \(\sum\) 中的一个子单纯形。根据(7.32)，只有当 \(0 \leq  {g}_{i} \leq  k\) 对于 \(1 \leq  i \leq  d\) 成立时才会这样，因为否则 \(g + \mathbf{0} \notin  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) (其中 \(\mathbf{0} \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) )，所以 \(\sum\) 是有限的。图7.17展示了 \(\sum\) 在 \(d = 3\) 和 \(k = 2\) 时的情况。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_212_193_349_1262_579_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 7.17 The Freudenthal triangulation \(\sum\) of the three-dimensional simplex \({T}_{3}\left( k\right)\) with vertices \(\left( {0,0,0}\right) ,\left( {k,0,0}\right) ,\left( {k,k,0}\right)\) and(k, k, k)for \(k = 2\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图7.17 三维单纯形 \({T}_{3}\left( k\right)\) 的弗赖登塔尔三角剖分 \(\sum\) ，其顶点为 \(\left( {0,0,0}\right) ,\left( {k,0,0}\right) ,\left( {k,k,0}\right)\) 以及对于 \(k = 2\) 的 (k, k, k)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 7.23. \(\sum\) as defined in (7.34) is a triangulation of \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理7.23。如 (7.34) 所定义的 \(\sum\) 是 \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 的一个三角剖分。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. By (7.34), \(\mathop{\bigcup }\limits_{{S \in  \sum }}S \subseteq  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) . To show the converse inclusion, let \(z \in  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) . We want to find \(g \in  {\mathbb{Z}}^{d}\) and \(\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) so that \(z \in  g + {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) , and crucially, \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi } \subseteq  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) as required in (7.34). If this holds then \(z = g + x\) for some \(x \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) , which implies

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。由 (7.34) 可知， \(\mathop{\bigcup }\limits_{{S \in  \sum }}S \subseteq  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 。为证明反向包含关系，设 \(z \in  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 。我们要找到 \(g \in  {\mathbb{Z}}^{d}\) 和 \(\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) 使得 \(z \in  g + {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) ，并且至关重要的是，满足 (7.34) 中要求的 \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi } \subseteq  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 。如果这成立，那么对于某个 \(x \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 有 \(z = g + x\) ，这意味着
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{z}_{i} = {g}_{i} + {x}_{i}\;\left( {{g}_{i} \in  \mathbb{Z},{x}_{i} \in  \left\lbrack  {0,1}\right\rbrack  ,1 \leq  i \leq  d}\right) . \tag{7.35}
\]

In (7.35), the choice of \({g}_{i}\) and \({x}_{i}\) is not unique if \({z}_{i} \in  \mathbb{Z}\) because then either \({x}_{i} = 0\) or \({x}_{i} = 1\) are possible. Then we normally choose \({x}_{i} = 0\) , except when \({z}_{i} = k\) where we choose \({g}_{i} = k - 1\) and \({x}_{i} = 1\) , to ensure that \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi } \subseteq  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) . With \(\left\lfloor  {z}_{i}\right\rfloor   = \max \left\{  {t \in  \mathbb{Z} \mid  t \leq  {z}_{i}}\right\}\) , this is achieved by

\begin{tcolorbox}
在 (7.35) 中，如果 \({z}_{i} \in  \mathbb{Z}\) ， \({g}_{i}\) 和 \({x}_{i}\) 的选择不是唯一的，因为此时 \({x}_{i} = 0\) 或 \({x}_{i} = 1\) 都是可能的。那么我们通常选择 \({x}_{i} = 0\) ，除非当 \({z}_{i} = k\) 时我们选择 \({g}_{i} = k - 1\) 和 \({x}_{i} = 1\) ，以确保 \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi } \subseteq  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 。对于 \(\left\lfloor  {z}_{i}\right\rfloor   = \max \left\{  {t \in  \mathbb{Z} \mid  t \leq  {z}_{i}}\right\}\) ，这可通过
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{g}_{i} = \left\{  {\begin{array}{ll} \left\lfloor  {z}_{i}\right\rfloor  & \text{ if }{z}_{i} < k \\  k - 1 & \text{ if }{z}_{i} = k, \end{array}\;{x}_{i} = {z}_{i} - {g}_{i}\;\left( {1 \leq  i \leq  d}\right) ,}\right.  \tag{7.36}
\]

which clearly implies (7.35).

\begin{tcolorbox}
这显然意味着 (7.35)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Similarly, the integral parts \({g}_{i}\) of \({z}_{i}\) fulfill

\begin{tcolorbox}
类似地， \({z}_{i}\) 的整数部分 \({g}_{i}\) 满足
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{g}_{i} \geq  {g}_{i + 1}\;\left( {1 \leq  i < d}\right)  \tag{7.37}
\]

because if \({z}_{i} = k\) then \({z}_{i + 1} \leq  k\) and \({g}_{i} = k - 1 \geq  {g}_{i + 1}\) , and if \({z}_{i} < k\) then by (7.36) we have \({x}_{i} < 1\) so that \({g}_{i} < {g}_{i + 1}\) would imply \({z}_{i} = {g}_{i} + {x}_{i} < {g}_{i} + 1 \leq  {g}_{i + 1} \leq  {z}_{i + 1}\) , which contradicts (7.32).

\begin{tcolorbox}
因为如果 \({z}_{i} = k\) ，那么 \({z}_{i + 1} \leq  k\) 且 \({g}_{i} = k - 1 \geq  {g}_{i + 1}\) ，并且如果 \({z}_{i} < k\) ，那么由 (7.36) 我们有 \({x}_{i} < 1\) ，使得 \({g}_{i} < {g}_{i + 1}\) 意味着 \({z}_{i} = {g}_{i} + {x}_{i} < {g}_{i} + 1 \leq  {g}_{i + 1} \leq  {z}_{i + 1}\) ，这与 (7.32) 矛盾。
\end{tcolorbox}

Given (7.35), we want to find \(\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) so that \(x \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) and \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi } \subseteq  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) . The second condition is not automatic: For example, if \(d = 2\) and \(k = 1\) then \(\sum  = \left\{  {{T}_{2}\left( 1\right) }\right\}\) and for \(z = x = \left( {\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}}\right)\) both \({T}_{2}\left( 1\right)\) and \({T}_{2}^{\pi }\left( 1\right)\) for \(\left( {\pi \left( 1\right) ,\pi \left( 2\right) }\right)  = \left( {2,1}\right)\) contain \(x\) , but \(\mathbf{0} + {T}_{2}^{\pi }\left( 1\right)  \notin  \sum\) . The following is a safe choice of \(\pi\) . Sort the components \({x}_{i}\) of \(x\) with a permutation \(\pi\) so that (7.28) holds and so that

\begin{tcolorbox}
给定式(7.35)，我们希望找到 \(\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) 使得 \(x \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 且 \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi } \subseteq  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 。第二个条件并非自动满足:例如，如果 \(d = 2\) 且 \(k = 1\) ，那么 \(\sum  = \left\{  {{T}_{2}\left( 1\right) }\right\}\) ，并且对于 \(z = x = \left( {\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}}\right)\) ， \({T}_{2}\left( 1\right)\) 和 \({T}_{2}^{\pi }\left( 1\right)\) 在 \(\left( {\pi \left( 1\right) ,\pi \left( 2\right) }\right)  = \left( {2,1}\right)\) 时都包含 \(x\) ，但 \(\mathbf{0} + {T}_{2}^{\pi }\left( 1\right)  \notin  \sum\) 。以下是 \(\pi\) 的一个稳妥选择。用一个排列 \(\pi\) 对 \(x\) 的分量 \({x}_{i}\) 进行排序，使得式(7.28)成立，并且使得
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
1 \leq  h < j \leq  d,\;{x}_{\pi \left( h\right) } = {x}_{\pi \left( j\right) }\; \Rightarrow  \;\pi \left( h\right)  < \pi \left( j\right) . \tag{7.38}
\]

This can always be achieved, if necessary by exchanging \(\pi \left( h\right)\) and \(\pi \left( j\right)\) if \({x}_{\pi \left( h\right) } =\)  \({x}_{\pi \left( j\right) }\) , which maintains (7.28). Then \(x \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) by Lemma 7.21. We want to show that \(g + y \in  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) for any \(y \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) , that is, according to (7.32),

\begin{tcolorbox}
这总能实现，如有必要，若 \({x}_{\pi \left( h\right) } =\)  \({x}_{\pi \left( j\right) }\) ，可交换 \(\pi \left( h\right)\) 和 \(\pi \left( j\right)\) ，这能保持式(7.28)成立。然后根据引理 7.21 有 \(x \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 。我们想证明对于任意 \(y \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 都有 \(g + y \in  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) ，即根据式(7.32)
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{g}_{i} + {y}_{i} \geq  {g}_{i + 1} + {y}_{i + 1}\;\left( {1 \leq  i < d}\right)  \tag{7.39}
\]

(by (7.36), we have \(k \geq  {g}_{1} + {y}_{1}\) and \({g}_{d} + {y}_{d} \geq  0\) ). By (7.37), condition (7.39) holds if \({y}_{i} \geq  {y}_{i + 1}\) . Otherwise, let \(y \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) and suppose that \({y}_{i} < {y}_{i + 1}\) for some \(1 \leq  i < d\) . Let \(i + 1 = \pi \left( h\right)\) and \(i = \pi \left( j\right)\) . Then \({y}_{\pi \left( h\right) } = {y}_{i + 1} > {y}_{i} = {y}_{\pi \left( j\right) }\) , which implies \(h < j\) by Lemma 7.21. Then \({x}_{\pi \left( h\right) } \geq  {x}_{\pi \left( j\right) }\) , where \({x}_{\pi \left( h\right) } = {x}_{\pi \left( j\right) }\) would imply \(i + 1 = \pi \left( h\right)  <\)  \(\pi \left( j\right)  = i\) by (7.38) which is not possible, that is, \({x}_{i + 1} = {x}_{\pi \left( h\right) } > {x}_{\pi \left( j\right) } = {x}_{i}\) . By (7.37), either \({g}_{i} = {g}_{i + 1}\) or \({g}_{i} > {g}_{i + 1}\) . If \({g}_{i} = {g}_{i + 1}\) then \({z}_{i + 1} = {g}_{i + 1} + {x}_{i + 1} > {g}_{i} + {x}_{i} = {z}_{i}\) , which violates (7.32). Hence, \({g}_{i} + {y}_{i} \geq  {g}_{i} \geq  {g}_{i + 1} + 1 \geq  {g}_{i + 1} + {y}_{i + 1}\) . This shows (7.39), and therefore, as claimed, \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi } \subseteq  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) . That is, the sub-simplices in \(\sum\) cover \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(由(7.36)，我们有 \(k \geq  {g}_{1} + {y}_{1}\) 和 \({g}_{d} + {y}_{d} \geq  0\) )。由(7.37)，若 \({y}_{i} \geq  {y}_{i + 1}\) ，则条件(7.39)成立。否则，令 \(y \in  {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) ，并假设对于某个 \(1 \leq  i < d\) 有 \({y}_{i} < {y}_{i + 1}\) 。令 \(i + 1 = \pi \left( h\right)\) 且 \(i = \pi \left( j\right)\) 。那么 \({y}_{\pi \left( h\right) } = {y}_{i + 1} > {y}_{i} = {y}_{\pi \left( j\right) }\) ，根据引理7.21这意味着 \(h < j\) 。接着 \({x}_{\pi \left( h\right) } \geq  {x}_{\pi \left( j\right) }\) ，其中 \({x}_{\pi \left( h\right) } = {x}_{\pi \left( j\right) }\) 由(7.38)将意味着 \(i + 1 = \pi \left( h\right)  <\)  \(\pi \left( j\right)  = i\) ，而这是不可能的，即 \({x}_{i + 1} = {x}_{\pi \left( h\right) } > {x}_{\pi \left( j\right) } = {x}_{i}\) 。由(7.37)，要么 \({g}_{i} = {g}_{i + 1}\) ，要么 \({g}_{i} > {g}_{i + 1}\) 。若 \({g}_{i} = {g}_{i + 1}\) ，则 \({z}_{i + 1} = {g}_{i + 1} + {x}_{i + 1} > {g}_{i} + {x}_{i} = {z}_{i}\) ，这与(7.32)矛盾。因此， \({g}_{i} + {y}_{i} \geq  {g}_{i} \geq  {g}_{i + 1} + 1 \geq  {g}_{i + 1} + {y}_{i + 1}\) 。这表明了(7.39)，因此，如所声称的， \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi } \subseteq  {T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 。也就是说， \(\sum\) 中的子单形覆盖 \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

In order to show that \(\sum\) is a triangulation of \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) , it remains to show that no sub-simplex is a subset of another, and that the intersection of two sub-simplices is a face of both. Both properties are shown similarly to Lemma 7.22, which implies them for two sub-simplices \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) and \({g}^{\prime } + {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) if \(g = {g}^{\prime }\) . If \(g \neq  {g}^{\prime }\) then the two sub-simplices are subsets of two different cubes \(g + {C}_{d}\) and \({g}^{\prime } + {C}_{d}\) which have disjoint non-empty interiors, so neither sub-simplex is a subset of the other. The intersection of \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) and \({g}^{\prime } + {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) is a subset of \(\left( {g + {C}_{d}}\right)  \cap  \left( {{g}^{\prime } + {C}_{d}}\right)\) , which is non-empty only if \(\left| {{g}_{i} - {g}_{i}^{\prime }}\right|  \leq  1\) for \(1 \leq  i \leq  d\) . By Lemma 7.21, \(z \in  \left( {g + {T}_{d}^{\pi }}\right)  \cap  \left( {{g}^{\prime } + {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}}\right)\) if and only if \(z = g + x = {g}^{\prime } + {x}^{\prime }\) so that (7.28) holds and, analogously,

\begin{tcolorbox}
为了证明 \(\sum\) 是 \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 的一个三角剖分，还需证明没有子单纯形是另一个子单纯形的子集，并且两个子单纯形的交集是两者的一个面。这两个性质的证明与引理7.22类似，若 \(g = {g}^{\prime }\) ，则该引理对两个子单纯形 \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 和 \({g}^{\prime } + {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) 成立。若 \(g \neq  {g}^{\prime }\) ，则这两个子单纯形是两个不同立方体 \(g + {C}_{d}\) 和 \({g}^{\prime } + {C}_{d}\) 的子集，这两个立方体的非空内部不相交，因此没有一个子单纯形是另一个的子集。 \(g + {T}_{d}^{\pi }\) 和 \({g}^{\prime } + {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}\) 的交集是 \(\left( {g + {C}_{d}}\right)  \cap  \left( {{g}^{\prime } + {C}_{d}}\right)\) 的子集，仅当对于 \(1 \leq  i \leq  d\) 有 \(\left| {{g}_{i} - {g}_{i}^{\prime }}\right|  \leq  1\) 时，该交集才非空。根据引理7.21，当且仅当 \(z = g + x = {g}^{\prime } + {x}^{\prime }\) 时 \(z \in  \left( {g + {T}_{d}^{\pi }}\right)  \cap  \left( {{g}^{\prime } + {T}_{d}^{{\pi }^{\prime }}}\right)\) ，从而 (7.28) 成立，并且类似地
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
1 \geq  {x}_{{\pi }^{\prime }\left( 1\right) }^{\prime } \geq  {x}_{{\pi }^{\prime }\left( 2\right) }^{\prime } \geq  \cdots  \geq  {x}_{{\pi }^{\prime }\left( d\right) }^{\prime } \geq  0. \tag{7.40}
\]

If \({g}_{i} = {g}_{i}^{\prime } + 1\) , then the equation \({z}_{i} = {g}_{i} + {x}_{i} = {g}_{i}^{\prime } + {x}_{i}^{\prime }\) imposes the additional equations \({x}_{i} = 1\) and \({x}_{i}^{\prime } = 0\) (and similarly \({x}_{i} = 0\) and \({x}_{i}^{\prime } = 1\) if \({g}_{i} + 1 = {g}_{i}^{\prime }\) ). For those subscripts \(i\) where \({g}_{i} = {g}_{i}^{\prime }\) we have \({x}_{i} = {x}_{i}^{\prime }\) , which induce further equations in (7.28) and (7.40) if these components of \(x\) and \({x}^{\prime }\) appear in reverse order in these inequalities. As argued in the proof of Lemma 7.22, these equations mean that certain barycentric coordinates \({y}_{0},{y}_{1},\ldots ,{y}_{d}\) in (7.29) are zero (which may now include \({y}_{0}\) if \({x}_{\pi \left( 1\right) } = 1\) and \({y}_{d}\) if \({x}_{\pi \left( d\right) } = 0\) ), which excludes the corresponding vertices of the two sub-simplices from their intersection. The remaining vertices (if any) are then the vertices of the intersection, which is therefore a common face of both sub-simplices. This completes the proof of the triangulation property.

\begin{tcolorbox}
若 \({g}_{i} = {g}_{i}^{\prime } + 1\) ，则方程 \({z}_{i} = {g}_{i} + {x}_{i} = {g}_{i}^{\prime } + {x}_{i}^{\prime }\) 施加了额外的方程 \({x}_{i} = 1\) 和 \({x}_{i}^{\prime } = 0\) (类似地，若 \({g}_{i} + 1 = {g}_{i}^{\prime }\) ，则有 \({x}_{i} = 0\) 和 \({x}_{i}^{\prime } = 1\) )。对于那些满足 \({g}_{i} = {g}_{i}^{\prime }\) 的下标 \(i\) ，我们有 \({x}_{i} = {x}_{i}^{\prime }\) ，如果 \(x\) 和 \({x}^{\prime }\) 的这些分量在这些不等式中以相反顺序出现，那么这会在 (7.28) 和 (7.40) 中引出更多方程。正如在引理7.22的证明中所论述的，这些方程意味着 (7.29) 中某些重心坐标 \({y}_{0},{y}_{1},\ldots ,{y}_{d}\) 为零(如果 \({x}_{\pi \left( 1\right) } = 1\) ，现在可能包括 \({y}_{0}\) ；如果 \({x}_{\pi \left( d\right) } = 0\) ，可能包括 \({y}_{d}\) )，这就将两个子单纯形的相应顶点排除在它们的交集之外。剩下的顶点(如果有的话)就是交集的顶点，因此该交集是两个子单纯形的公共面。这就完成了三角剖分性质的证明。
\end{tcolorbox}

The simplex \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) has \(d + 1\) vertices \({v}^{i}\left( k\right)\) in (7.25) that are vertices of a cube in \({\mathbb{R}}^{k}\) with edge length \(k\) . The triangulation \(\sum\) in (7.34) subdivides \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) into simplices \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) (which triangulate the unit cube \({C}_{d}\) ) shifted by integer vectors \(g \in  {\mathbb{Z}}^{d}\) . The goal is to obtain an arbitrarily fine triangulation of the unit simplex \(\Delta\) , as used in the KKM lemma (Theorem 7.12). This achieved as follows: When mapping \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) to \(\Delta\) with the affine bijection \(J\) in Lemma 7.3, we obtain a corresponding triangulation \(\{ J\left( T\right)  \mid  T \in  \sum \}\) of \(\Delta\) . For each sub-simplex \(J\left( {g + {T}_{d}^{\pi }}\right)\) of that triangulation, the maximum of the distances between its vertices only depends on \(\pi\) , not on \(g\) . That maximum (over all \(\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) and all vertex pairs) is proportional to \(\frac{1}{k}\) because \(J\) is affine and \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)  = \left\{  {{xk} \mid  x \in  {T}_{d}\left( 1\right) }\right\}\) . For any \(\varepsilon  > 0\) , one can therefore choose \(k\) large enough so that no two points in any sub-simplex of the triangulation of \(\Delta\) have distance greater than \(\varepsilon\) , which gives the desired \(\varepsilon\) -fine triangulation.

\begin{tcolorbox}
单纯形 \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 在 (7.25) 中有 \(d + 1\) 个顶点 \({v}^{i}\left( k\right)\) ，这些顶点是 \({\mathbb{R}}^{k}\) 中边长为 \(k\) 的立方体的顶点。(7.34) 中的三角剖分 \(\sum\) 将 \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 细分为单纯形 \({T}_{d}^{\pi }\) (这些单纯形对单位立方体 \({C}_{d}\) 进行三角剖分)，并通过整数向量 \(g \in  {\mathbb{Z}}^{d}\) 进行平移。目标是得到单位单纯形 \(\Delta\) 的任意精细的三角剖分，如 KKM 引理(定理 7.12)中所使用的那样。这可以通过以下方式实现:当使用引理 7.3 中的仿射双射 \(J\) 将 \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)\) 映射到 \(\Delta\) 时，我们得到 \(\Delta\) 的相应三角剖分 \(\{ J\left( T\right)  \mid  T \in  \sum \}\) 。对于该三角剖分的每个子单纯形 \(J\left( {g + {T}_{d}^{\pi }}\right)\) ，其顶点之间距离的最大值仅取决于 \(\pi\) ，而不取决于 \(g\) 。该最大值(在所有 \(\pi  \in  \operatorname{Perm}\left( d\right)\) 和所有顶点对上)与 \(\frac{1}{k}\) 成正比，因为 \(J\) 是仿射的且 \({T}_{d}\left( k\right)  = \left\{  {{xk} \mid  x \in  {T}_{d}\left( 1\right) }\right\}\) 。因此，对于任何 \(\varepsilon  > 0\) ，可以选择足够大的 \(k\) ，使得 \(\Delta\) 的三角剖分的任何子单纯形中的任意两点之间的距离都不大于 \(\varepsilon\) ，这就得到了所需的 \(\varepsilon\) - 精细三角剖分。
\end{tcolorbox}

The proof of Brouwer's fixed-point Theorem 7.20 is thereby complete.

\begin{tcolorbox}
布劳威尔不动点定理 7.20 的证明至此完成。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{7.8 Further Reading}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{7.8 进一步阅读}
\end{tcolorbox}

The fixed-point theorem is stated at the end of Brouwer (1911, Satz 4). In this and related papers Brouwer developed and used the concept of "degree" (number of inverse images of a point) of a simplicial approximation of a continuous function. For a detailed discussion see Brouwer (1976). A historical survey of the wide-ranging impact of Brouwer's fixed-point theorem and its subsequent extensions is Park (1999).

\begin{tcolorbox}
不动点定理在布劳威尔(1911 年，命题 4)的论文结尾处被提出。在这篇以及相关论文中，布劳威尔发展并使用了连续函数的单纯逼近的“度”(一个点的原像数量)的概念。详细讨论见布劳威尔(1976 年)。关于布劳威尔不动点定理及其后续扩展的广泛影响的历史综述见帕克(1999 年)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Brouwer's fixed-point theorem is a major theorem in topology. The basic notion of topology is continuity, but more primitively topology can be seen as a qualitative study of "position". The first paper published in that regard is the solution by Euler (1741) to the problem of the "bridges of Königsberg" (see also Biggs, Lloyd, and Wilson, 1976). Euler's paper marks the foundation of both graph theory and topology; it introduces the concept of a graph, and the degree of a node plays a central role. It is fitting that Sperner's lemma relates to the even number of odd-degree nodes in a graph.

\begin{tcolorbox}
布劳威尔不动点定理是拓扑学中的一个重要定理。拓扑学的基本概念是连续性，但更本质地说，拓扑学可以被视为对“位置”的定性研究。在这方面发表的第一篇论文是欧拉(1741 年)对“哥尼斯堡七桥问题”的解答(另见比格斯、劳埃德和威尔逊，1976 年)。欧拉的论文标志着图论和拓扑学的基础；它引入了图的概念，并且节点的度起着核心作用。斯佩纳引理与图中奇数度节点的偶数性相关是恰当的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 7.10 is due to Sperner (1928), and was used by Knaster, Kuratowski, and Mazurkiewicz (1929) to prove the fixed-point theorem on a simplex with the help of their Theorem 7.12. As we mentioned in the proof of Theorem 7.10, these proofs use the counting modulo two in equation (7.12). The term "parity argument" has been coined by Papadimitriou (1994).

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理 7.10 归功于斯佩纳(1928 年)，并且被克纳斯特、库拉托夫斯基和马祖尔克维奇(1929 年)用于借助他们的定理 7.12 证明单纯形上的不动点定理。正如我们在定理 7.10 的证明中所提到的，这些证明使用了方程 (7.12) 中的模 2 计数。“奇偶性论证”这一术语由帕帕迪米特里欧(1994 年)创造。
\end{tcolorbox}

The path-following argument is due to Cohen (1967). Cohen's proof is not given in enough detail to see that the path may also come back to several dimensions further below, as in the example in Figure 7.10. For a more precise description see Kuhn (1969). We have defined the "internal" nodes of the graph as the sub-facets in \({C}^{\prime }\) rather than as the elements of \(A\) in (7.11). When staying in a fixed dimension, it is more natural to consider only sub-simplices, the elements of \(D\) and \(A\) , as nodes of a graph, which are joined by an edge if they have a common sub-facet in \({C}^{\prime }\) . However, for the construction of \({G}_{2}\) , in particular, the edges are more easily seen as line segments and the nodes as vertices in \({\sum }_{2}\) . A key argument in the construction of \(G\) in (7.13) is that the end nodes of some paths in a graph \({G}_{n}\) have different dimension, and we change to the higher dimension only at the end node.

\begin{tcolorbox}
路径跟踪的论证归功于科恩(Cohen，1967)。科恩的证明没有提供足够详细的内容，让人无法看到路径也可能回到低几个维度的情况，如图7.10中的示例所示。更精确的描述请参阅库恩(Kuhn，1969)。我们将图的“内部”节点定义为 \({C}^{\prime }\) 中的子极面，而不是(7.11)中 \(A\) 的元素。当处于固定维度时，更自然的做法是仅将单纯剖分中的子单纯形，即 \(D\) 和 \(A\) 的元素，视为图的节点，如果它们在 \({C}^{\prime }\) 中有一个公共子极面，则用一条边将它们连接起来。然而，特别是对于 \({G}_{2}\) 的构造，边更容易被看作线段，节点更容易被看作 \({\sum }_{2}\) 中的顶点。(7.13)中 \(G\) 构造的一个关键论证是，图 \({G}_{n}\) 中某些路径的端点具有不同的维度，并且我们仅在端点处转换到更高的维度。
\end{tcolorbox}

The image of "seaview hotels" to prove the Bolzano-Weierstrass theorem is taken from Bryant (1990).

\begin{tcolorbox}
用于证明波尔查诺 - 魏尔施特拉斯定理的“海景酒店”的例子取自布莱恩特(Bryant，1990)。
\end{tcolorbox}

The Freudenthal simplicial subdivision is due to Freudenthal (1942) and has been re-discovered several times, for example by Kuhn (1960).

\begin{tcolorbox}
弗罗伊登塔尔单纯剖分归功于弗罗伊登塔尔(Freudenthal，1942)，并且被多次重新发现，例如库恩(Kuhn，1960)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{7.9 Exercises for Chapter 7}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{7.9 第7章练习}
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 7.1. Let \(C = \left\{  {\left( {x,y}\right)  \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{2} \mid  {x}^{2} + {y}^{2} \leq  1}\right\}\) , and consider the following functions \(g,h,f : C \rightarrow  C\) :

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习7.1。设 \(C = \left\{  {\left( {x,y}\right)  \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{2} \mid  {x}^{2} + {y}^{2} \leq  1}\right\}\) ，并考虑以下函数 \(g,h,f : C \rightarrow  C\) :
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
g\left( {x,y}\right)  = \left( {x,\sqrt{1 - {x}^{2}}}\right) ,\;h\left( {x,y}\right)  = \left( {-y,x}\right) ,\;f\left( {x,y}\right)  = h\left( {g\left( {x,y}\right) }\right) .
\]

Let \(G = g\left( C\right)  = \{ g\left( {x,y}\right)  \mid  \left( {x,y}\right)  \in  C\}\) . Describe the geometric shape of \(C\) and \(G\) , and how the functions \(g\) and \(h\) act. Does each of the functions \(g,h,f\) have a fixed point in \(C\) ? Justify why, or why not. Determine all fixed points, if any, of each of \(g,h\) , and \(f\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
设 \(G = g\left( C\right)  = \{ g\left( {x,y}\right)  \mid  \left( {x,y}\right)  \in  C\}\) 。描述 \(C\) 和 \(G\) 的几何形状，以及函数 \(g\) 和 \(h\) 的作用方式。每个函数 \(g,h,f\) 在 \(C\) 中是否有不动点？说明原因。如果有，确定 \(g,h\) 和 \(f\) 每个函数的所有不动点。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 7.2. For a general polytope \(P\) , a vertex of \(P\) is defined as an extreme point of \(P\) , that is, it is not a convex combination of other points of \(P\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习7.2。对于一般的有界多面体 \(P\) ， \(P\) 的顶点被定义为 \(P\) 的极点，即它不是 \(P\) 中其他点的凸组合。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Show that for a simplex \(S\) given as the convex hull of the affinely independent points \({v}^{1},\ldots ,{v}^{d}\) , each point \({v}^{i}\) is indeed a vertex of \(S\) according to this definition.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a)证明对于由仿射无关点 \({v}^{1},\ldots ,{v}^{d}\) 的凸包给出的单纯形 \(S\) ，根据这个定义，每个点 \({v}^{i}\) 确实是 \(S\) 的一个顶点。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Show that the set of vertices of a simplex is unique.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b)证明单纯形的顶点集是唯一的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 7.3. Give examples of

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习7.3。给出以下例子
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) two simplices whose intersection is not a simplex;

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a)两个单纯形，它们的交集不是单纯形；
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) a nonempty convex set that has no extreme points;

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b)一个非空凸集，它没有极点；
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) a bounded convex set that has at least one extreme point but that is not equal to the convex hull of its extreme points.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c)一个有界凸集，它至少有一个极点，但它不等于其极点的凸包。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 7.4. Show that the following generalization of Sperner's lemma (Theorem 7.10) is false: Let \(S\) be a simplex with vertices \({v}^{1},\ldots ,{v}^{d}\) , let \(\sum\) be a triangulation of \(S\) , and consider a labeling of the vertices in \(V\) so that \({v}_{i}\) has label \(i\) for \(1 \leq  i \leq  d\) . Then \(\sum\) has at least one completely labeled sub-simplex.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习7.4。证明以下斯佩纳引理(定理7.10)的推广是错误的:设 \(S\) 是一个顶点为 \({v}^{1},\ldots ,{v}^{d}\) 的单纯形，设 \(\sum\) 是 \(S\) 的一个三角剖分，并考虑 \(V\) 中顶点的一个标签，使得 \({v}_{i}\) 具有标签 \(i\) ，其中 \(1 \leq  i \leq  d\) 。那么 \(\sum\) 至少有一个完全标记的子单纯形。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{8}

\section*{Zero-Sum Games}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{零和博弈}
\end{tcolorbox}

Zero-sum games are games of two players where the interests of the players are directly opposed: One player's loss is the other player's gain. Competitions between two players in sports or in parlor games can be thought of as zero-sum games. The combinatorial games studied in Chapter 1 are also zero-sum games.

\begin{tcolorbox}
零和博弈是两个玩家的博弈，玩家的利益直接对立:一个玩家的损失就是另一个玩家的收益。体育比赛或室内游戏中两个玩家之间的竞争可以被视为零和博弈。第1章中研究的组合博弈也是零和博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

In a zero-sum game, the sum of payoffs to the players in any outcome of playing the game is zero. An \(m \times  n\) bimatrix game(A, B)is zero-sum if \(B =  - A\) . It is completely specified by the matrix \(A\) of payoffs to the row player, and the game is therefore often called a matrix game. Consequently, we can analyse matrix games just like bimatrix games. However, zero-sum games have additional strong properties that do not hold for general bimatrix games. These properties are, which are the subject of this chapter, are:

\begin{tcolorbox}
在零和博弈中，博弈任何结果下参与者的收益总和为零。若 \(B =  - A\) ，则 \(m \times  n\) 双矩阵博弈 (A, B) 是零和博弈。它完全由行参与者的收益矩阵 \(A\) 确定，因此该博弈通常被称为矩阵博弈。因此，我们可以像分析双矩阵博弈一样分析矩阵博弈。然而，零和博弈具有一般双矩阵博弈所不具备的额外强大性质。本章的主题就是这些性质，它们是:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item In a zero-sum game, an equilibrium strategy is a max-min strategy that maximizes the worst-case expected payoff to the player, and is therefore independent of what the opponent does.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
 - 在零和博弈中，均衡策略是一种极大极小策略，它能使参与者的最坏情况期望效用最大化，因此与对手的行动无关。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Every equilibrium has the same unique payoff to the players, which is called the value of the game.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
 - 每个均衡给参与者带来的收益都是相同且唯一的，这被称为博弈的值。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item If a player has several equilibrium strategies, these are exchangeable and can be used in any equilibrium. Moreover, the set of a player's equilibrium strategies is convex.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
 - 如果一个参与者有多个均衡策略，这些策略是可交换的，并且可以在任何均衡中使用。此外，参与者的均衡策略集是凸集。
\end{tcolorbox}

Apart from these special properties, we treat zero-sum games in a chapter of its own, because:

\begin{tcolorbox}
除了这些特殊性质外，我们单独用一章来讨论零和博弈，原因如下:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Historically, the main result about zero-sum games of von Neumann (1928) precedes the equilibrium concept of Nash (1951) for games with general payoffs.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
 - 从历史上看，冯·诺伊曼(1928 年)关于零和博弈的主要结果早于纳什(1951 年)针对一般收益博弈提出的均衡概念。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item The concept of equilibrium as "no incentive to deviate" has a stronger form in a zero-sum game, namely that a player can make his strategy public without a disadvantage. As the games of Matching Pennies and Rock-Paper-Scissors show (see Figure 3.12), this requires the concept of mixed strategies. (Zero-sum games may not always require mixed strategies, for example if they have perfect information like the games in Chapters 1 and 4. A game where one player has only two strategies is dominance solvable, see Exercise 8.2.)
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
 - “没有偏离动机”这一均衡概念在零和博弈中有更强的形式，即参与者可以公开自己的策略而不会处于劣势。正如硬币匹配 (博弈) 和石头剪刀布博弈所示(见图 3.12)，这需要混合策略的概念。(零和博弈并不总是需要混合策略，例如，如果它们像第 1 章和第 4 章中的博弈那样具有完美信息。如果一个参与者只有两种策略，那么该博弈是占优可解的，见练习 8.2。)
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item This chapter is mathematically self-contained, with an independent two-page proof of von Neumann's minimax theorem, which (see Proposition 8.3) is equivalent to the existence of an equilibrium in a zero-sum game. The minimax theorem is less powerful than Brouwer's fixed-point theorem, and slightly less powerful than the duality theorem of linear programming, but will be useful for proving the existence of "correlated equilibria" in Chapter 12.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
 - 本章在数学上是自足的，独立用两页篇幅证明了冯·诺伊曼的极小极大定理，该定理(见命题 8.3)等价于零和博弈中均衡的存在性。极小极大定理的效力不如布劳威尔不动点定理，也略逊于线性规划的对偶定理，但在第 12 章证明“相关均衡”的存在性时会很有用。
\end{tcolorbox}

In Section 8.2, we analyze the soccer penalty as an introductory example of a zero-sum game, and show how to find a max-min strategy graphically (Figures 8.2 and 8.3).

\begin{tcolorbox}
在 8.2 节中，我们以足球点球为例介绍零和博弈，并展示如何通过图形找到极大极小策略(图 8.2 和 8.3)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Section 8.3 states the important properties of zero-sum games. The central concept is that of a max-min strategy, which is a (typically mixed) strategy that maximizes the minimum expected payoff to the player for all possible responses of the opponent. This (rather paranoid) approach can be applied to any game, but in a zero-sum game means that the other player uses a best response, and is therefore justified.

\begin{tcolorbox}
8.3 节阐述了零和博弈的重要性质。核心概念是极大极小策略，它是一种(通常是混合的)策略，能使参与者在对手所有可能反应下的最小期望效用最大化。这种(相当偏执的)方法可以应用于任何博弈，但在零和博弈中意味着另一个参与者使用最优反应，因此是合理的。
\end{tcolorbox}

The payoffs to the maximizing row player in a matrix game are costs to the column player who wants to minimize them. In terms of these costs (rather than the payoffs to the column player), a max-min strategy is called a min-max strategy of the minimizer. If the max-min payoff equals the min-max cost, then the max-min strategy and min-max strategy define an equilibrium, and an equilibrium is such a pair of strategies (Proposition 8.3). Because finite games have an equilibrium, the maxmin \(=\) minmax equality holds, known as the minimax theorem of von Neumann (1928) (Theorem 8.3). It also defines the unique equilibrium payoff (to the maximizer, and cost to the minimizer) as the value of the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
矩阵博弈中最大化收益的行参与者的收益，对于想要最小化这些收益的列参与者来说就是成本。就这些成本(而非列参与者的收益)而言，极大极小策略被称为最小值点的极小极大策略。如果极大极小收益等于极小极大成本，那么极大极小策略和极小极大策略就定义了一个均衡，并且均衡就是这样一对策略(命题 8.3)。因为有限博弈存在均衡，所以极大极小 \(=\) 极小极大等式成立，这就是冯·诺伊曼(1928 年)的极小极大定理(定理 8.3)。它还将唯一的均衡收益(对最大值点而言)和成本(对最小值点而言)定义为博弈的值。
\end{tcolorbox}

In Section 8.4 we give a short proof of the minimax theorem that is independent of the existence of an equilibrium. It connects two related optimization problems that are a special case of pair of "dual linear programs". We consider them only for this special case, where we prove directly, by induction, that the two problems have the same optimal value.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在 8.4 节中，我们给出了一个不依赖于均衡存在性的极小极大定理的简短证明。它将两个相关的优化问题联系起来，这两个问题是一对“对偶线性规划”的特殊情况。我们仅针对这个特殊情况考虑它们，在这种情况下，我们通过归纳法直接证明这两个问题具有相同的最优值。
\end{tcolorbox}

In zero-sum game, an equilibrium strategy is the same as a max-min strategy (in terms of the player's own payoff), also called an optimal strategy, which does not depend on what the other player does, a very strong property. Further properties of optimal strategies are discussed in Section 8.5.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在零和博弈中，均衡策略与极大极小策略(就参与者自身的收益而言)相同，也称为最优策略，它不依赖于其他参与者的行动，这是一个非常强的性质。最优策略的其他性质将在 8.5 节中讨论。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{8.1 Prerequisites and Learning Outcomes}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{8.1 先决条件和学习成果}
\end{tcolorbox}

As prerequisites we require the definition of bimatrix games and mixed strategies from Chapter 6, and manipulating matrices and linear inequalities between vectors. The proof of the minimax theorem in Section 8.4 uses that a continuous function on a compact domain assumes its minimum and maximum.

\begin{tcolorbox}
作为先决条件，我们需要第6章中双矩阵博弈(bimatrix game)和混合策略(mixed strategy)的定义，以及矩阵运算和向量间的线性不等式知识。8.4节中极小极大定理(minimax theorem)的证明用到了紧域上的连续函数能取到最小值和最大值这一性质。
\end{tcolorbox}

After studying this chapter, you should be able to:

\begin{tcolorbox}
学习本章后，你应该能够:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Explain the concept of max-min strategies and their importance for zero-sum games, and apply this to given games.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 解释极大极小策略(max - min strategy)的概念及其对零和博弈(zero - sum game)的重要性，并将其应用于给定的博弈中。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Find max-min and min-max strategies in small matrix games like in Exercise 8.1, and exploit the "maxmin = minmax" theorem to find the (max-min or min-max) strategy that is easier to find via the lower or upper envelope of expected payoffs.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 像在练习8.1中那样，在小型矩阵博弈(matrix game)中找到极大极小和极小极大策略(min - max strategy)，并利用“极大极小值 = 极小极大值”定理，通过期望效用(expected payoff)的下包络或上包络来找到更容易计算的(极大极小或极小极大)策略。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item State and prove the equivalence of existence of an equilibrium and the minimax theorem in a zero-sum game (Proposition 8.3).
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 陈述并证明零和博弈中均衡(equilibrium)的存在性与极小极大定理的等价性(命题8.3)。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item State the main properties of zero-sum games: independence of "optimal strategies" from the what the opponent does, and its implications (unique value, exchangeability, and convexity of the set of equilibrium strategies).
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 陈述零和博弈的主要性质:“最优策略”(optimal strategy)与对手的行动无关，以及其影响(唯一值、可交换性和均衡策略集的凸性)。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Explain the main idea behind the induction proof of the minimax theorem in Section 8.3.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 解释8.3节中极小极大定理归纳证明背后的主要思想。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{8.2 Example: Soccer Penalty}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{8.2 示例:足球点球}
\end{tcolorbox}

A zero-sum game is a bimatrix game(A, B)with \(B =  - A\) . Because it is completely specified by the matrix \(A\) of payoffs to the row player \(\mathrm{I}\) , it is also called a matrix game (with payoff matrix) \(A\) . When the players choose row \(i\) and column \(j\) , respectively, player II pays the matrix entry \({a}_{ij}\) to player I, which is therefore a cost that player II wants to minimize, also in expectation when player II faces a mixed strategy. Player I is called the maximizer and player II the minimizer.

\begin{tcolorbox}
零和博弈是一个双矩阵博弈(A, B)，其中 \(B =  - A\) 。由于它完全由行玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 的收益矩阵 \(A\) 确定，因此它也被称为(具有收益矩阵的)矩阵博弈 \(A\) 。当玩家分别选择行 \(i\) 和列 \(j\) 时，玩家II向玩家I支付矩阵元素 \({a}_{ij}\) ，因此这是玩家II想要最小化的成本(cost)，当玩家II面对混合策略时，在期望意义上也是如此。玩家I被称为最大值点(maximizer)，玩家II被称为最小值点(minimizer)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 8.1 shows the penalty kick in soccer (football in Britain) as a zero-sum game between striker and goalkeeper. For simplicity, assume that the striker's possible strategies are \(L\) and \(R\) , that is, to kick into the left or right corner, and that the goalkeeper’s strategies are \(l,w\) , and \(r\) , where \(l\) and \(r\) mean that he jumps immediately into the left or right corner, respectively, and \(w\) that he waits and sees where the ball goes and jumps afterwards. In Figure 8.2, we give an example of resulting probabilities that the striker scores a goal, depending on the choices of the two players. Clearly, the striker (player I) tries to maximize and the goalkeeper (player II) to minimize that probability, and we only need this payoff to player I to specify the game. We call the maximizer "Max" and the minimizer "min" (where we keep our tradition of using lower case letters for the column player).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图8.1将足球(在英国称为football)中的点球大战展示为前锋和守门员之间的零和博弈。为简单起见，假设前锋的可能策略是 \(L\) 和 \(R\) ，即踢向左侧或右侧角落，而守门员的策略是 \(l,w\) 和 \(r\) ，其中 \(l\) 和 \(r\) 分别表示他立即跳向左侧或右侧角落， \(w\) 表示他等待观察球的去向然后再跳跃。在图8.2中，我们给出了一个根据双方玩家的选择，前锋得分的概率示例。显然，前锋(玩家I)试图最大化该概率，而守门员(玩家II)试图最小化该概率，我们只需要这个给玩家I的收益就能确定这个博弈。我们称最大值点为“Max”，最小值点为“min”(我们沿用用小写字母表示列玩家的传统)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_219_340_202_1242_534_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 8.1 Strategies for striker and goalkeeper in the soccer Penalty game. In addition to jumping left or right, the goalkeeper can wait(w). An extra strategy for the striker of shooting down the middle is considered in Exercise 8.1.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图8.1 足球点球大战中前锋和守门员的策略。除了向左或向右跳跃，守门员还可以等待(w)。练习8.1中考虑了前锋向中间射门的额外策略。
\end{tcolorbox}

Because the players care about the probability that a goal is scored, this payoff (to player I, cost to player II) automatically fulfills the condition of an expected-utility function, because an expected probability is just a probability. (This is a nice "practical" example of the construction of the values of an expected-utility function as probabilities, see Figure 5.1.) In a general zero-sum game, payoffs and costs are not necessarily defined via probabilities, but are nevertheless assumed to represent the preference of both players with respect to taking expectations. This is a stronger assumption than in a non-zero-sum game where the payoffs can be adjusted independently to reflect different attitudes to risk.

\begin{tcolorbox}
因为玩家关心进球的概率，所以这个收益(给玩家I，成本给玩家II)自动满足期望效用函数(expected - utility function)的条件，因为期望概率就是一个概率。(这是一个很好的将期望效用函数的值构建为概率的“实际”例子，见图5.1。)在一般的零和博弈中，收益和成本不一定通过概率来定义，但仍然假设它们代表了两个玩家在取期望方面的偏好(preference)。这比非零和博弈中的假设更强，在非零和博弈中，收益可以独立调整以反映不同的风险态度。
\end{tcolorbox}

The top left payoff table in Figure 8.2 shows the game as a bimatrix game with two payoffs per cell, and the pure best-response payoffs marked as usual with boxes. The top right shows the more concise matrix game with only one number per cell. A best-response payoff for the maximizer is shown with a box, a best-response (minimal) cost for the minimizer with a circle. This represents the same information as the two payoffs per cell, but more concisely.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图8.2左上角的收益表将该博弈展示为一个双矩阵博弈，每个单元格有两个收益，纯最优反应(best response)收益像往常一样用方框标记。右上角展示了更简洁的矩阵博弈，每个单元格只有一个数字。最大值点的最优反应收益用方框表示，最小值点的最优反应(最小)成本用圆圈表示。这与每个单元格有两个收益所表示的信息相同，但更简洁。
\end{tcolorbox}

The best responses show that the game has no pure-strategy equilibrium. This is immediate from the sports situation: obviously, the goalkeeper's best response to a kick into the right or left corner is to jump into that corner, whereas the striker would rather kick into the opposite corner to where the goalkeeper jumps. We therefore have to find a mixed equilibrium, and active randomization is obviously advantageous for both players.

\begin{tcolorbox}
最优反应表明该博弈没有纯策略均衡。从体育情境中可以立即看出这一点:显然，守门员对踢向右侧或左侧角落的球的最优反应是跳向那个角落，而前锋更愿意踢向守门员跳跃方向的对角。因此，我们必须找到一个混合均衡(mixed equilibrium)，显然主动随机化对两个玩家都有利。
\end{tcolorbox}

We first find the equilibria of the game as we would do this for a \(2 \times  3\) game, except that we only use the payoffs to player I. It is more than appropriate to use the goalpost method for solving the Penalty game! The bottom picture in Figure 8.2 shows the expected payoffs to player I as a function of his probability, say \(p\) , of choosing \(R\) , for the reponses \(l,w\) , and \(r\) of player II. These payoffs to player I are costs to player II, so the best-response costs to player II are given by the minimum of these lines, which defines the lower envelope shown by bold line segments. There are two intersection points on the lower envelope, of the lines for \(l\) and \(w\) when \(p = \frac{1}{3}\) , and of the lines for \(w\) and \(r\) when \(p = \frac{3}{5}\) . These intersection points are easily found with the difference trick (where it does not matter if one considers costs or payoffs, because the differences are the same in absolute value): Namely, \(l\) and \(w\) have cost difference 0.2 in row \(L\) and 0.4 in row \(R\) , so that \(L\) and \(R\) should be played in the ratio \({0.4} : {0.2}\) , that is, for \(p = \frac{1}{3}\) , to get indifference between \(l\) and \(w\) . The indifference between \(w\) and \(r\) when \(p = \frac{3}{5}\) is found similarly.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们首先像求解双矩阵博弈(bimatrix game)那样找出该博弈的均衡，只不过我们仅考虑玩家I的效用。使用球门柱法(goalpost method)来求解点球博弈再合适不过了！图8.2的下图展示了玩家I的期望效用，它是玩家I选择 \(R\) 的概率(设为 \(p\) )的函数，对应玩家II的策略 \(l,w\) 和 \(r\) 。玩家I的这些效用对玩家II来说就是成本，因此玩家II的最优反应成本由这些直线的最小值决定，这就定义了由粗线段表示的下包络(lower envelope)。在下包络上有两个交点，当 \(p = \frac{1}{3}\) 时， \(l\) 和 \(w\) 对应的直线相交；当 \(p = \frac{3}{5}\) 时， \(w\) 和 \(r\) 对应的直线相交。使用差分技巧(difference trick)很容易找到这些交点(在这种情况下，考虑成本还是效用并不重要，因为差值的绝对值是相同的):即， \(l\) 和 \(w\) 在第 \(L\) 行的成本差为0.2，在第 \(R\) 行的成本差为0.4，因此应该以 \({0.4} : {0.2}\) 的比例选择 \(L\) 和 \(R\) ，也就是当 \(p = \frac{1}{3}\) 时，使得玩家I在 \(l\) 和 \(w\) 之间无差异(indifference)。同理可找到当 \(p = \frac{3}{5}\) 时， \(w\) 和 \(r\) 之间的无差异情况。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=1.0\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_220_188_190_1276_1040_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 8.2 Top left: the Penalty game as a zero-sum bimatrix game with scoring probabilities as payoffs to the striker (player I), which are the negative of the payoffs to the goalkeeper (player II). Top right: the same game as a matrix game, which shows only the payoffs to the maximizer, which are costs to the minimizer. A box indicates a best-response payoff to the maximizer, a circle a best-response (minimal) cost to the minimizer. Bottom: lower envelope of best-response costs as a function of the mixed strategy(1 - p, p)of Max. For \(p = \widehat{p} = \frac{1}{3}\) this is his max-min strategy.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图8.2 左上方:点球博弈作为一个零和双矩阵博弈，得分概率作为前锋(玩家I)的效用，而这是守门员(玩家II)效用的负值。右上方:同样的博弈表示为矩阵博弈(matrix game)，只显示了最大值点(maximizer)的效用，而这是最小值点(minimizer)的成本。方框表示最大值点的最优反应效用，圆圈表示最小值点的最优反应(最小)成本。下方:作为最大值点混合策略(1 - p, p)的函数，最优反应成本的下包络。对于 \(p = \widehat{p} = \frac{1}{3}\) ，这是他的极大极小策略(max - min strategy)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Hence, player II’s best response to the mixed strategy(1 - p, p)is \(l\) for \(0 \leq  p \leq  \frac{1}{3}\) , and \(w\) for \(\frac{1}{3} \leq  p \leq  \frac{3}{5}\) , and \(r\) for \(\frac{3}{5} \leq  p \leq  1\) . Only for \(p = \frac{1}{3}\) and for \(p = \frac{3}{5}\) can player II mix, and only in the first case, when \(l\) and \(w\) are best responses, can player I, in turn, be made indifferent. This gives the unique mixed equilibrium of the game, \(\left( {\left( {\frac{2}{3},\frac{1}{3}}\right) ,\left( {\frac{1}{6},\frac{5}{6},0}\right) }\right)\) . The resulting expected costs for the three columns \(l,w,r\) are \(\frac{2}{3},\frac{2}{3},\frac{4}{5}\) , where indeed player II assigns positive probability only to the smallest-cost columns \(l\) and \(w\) . The expected payoffs for both rows are \(\frac{2}{3}\) . (This unique equilibrium payoff of the game is the value of the game defined in Theorem 8.4.)

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此，玩家II对混合策略(1 - p, p)的最优反应是:当 \(0 \leq  p \leq  \frac{1}{3}\) 时为 \(l\) ，当 \(\frac{1}{3} \leq  p \leq  \frac{3}{5}\) 时为 \(w\) ，当 \(\frac{3}{5} \leq  p \leq  1\) 时为 \(r\) 。只有当 \(p = \frac{1}{3}\) 和 \(p = \frac{3}{5}\) 时，玩家II可以采用混合策略，并且只有在第一种情况下，即当 \(l\) 和 \(w\) 是最优反应时，才能让玩家I反过来达到无差异。这就得到了该博弈唯一的混合均衡(mixed equilibrium) \(\left( {\left( {\frac{2}{3},\frac{1}{3}}\right) ,\left( {\frac{1}{6},\frac{5}{6},0}\right) }\right)\) 。三列 \(l,w,r\) 对应的期望成本为 \(\frac{2}{3},\frac{2}{3},\frac{4}{5}\) ，实际上玩家II只对成本最小的列 \(l\) 和 \(w\) 赋予正概率。两行的期望效用均为 \(\frac{2}{3}\) 。(该博弈唯一的均衡效用就是定理8.4中定义的博弈值。)
\end{tcolorbox}

So far, our analysis is not new. We now consider the lower envelope of expected payoffs to player I as a function of his own mixed strategy \(x = \left( {1 - p,p}\right)\) from that player's own perspective: It represents the worst possible expected payoff to player I, given the possible responses of player II. It is easy to see that it suffices to look at pure responses of player II (see also Proposition 8.2 below). That is, against the mixed strategy \(x\) the minimum is found over all pure responses of the minimizer, here columns \(l,w\) , and \(r\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
到目前为止，我们的分析并无新意。现在我们从玩家I自身的角度，考虑玩家I的期望效用作为其自身混合策略 \(x = \left( {1 - p,p}\right)\) 的函数的下包络:它表示在玩家II可能的反应下，玩家I可能获得的最差期望效用。很容易看出，只需要考虑玩家II的纯策略反应即可(也可参见下面的命题8.2)。也就是说，对于混合策略 \(x\) ，最小值是在最小值点的所有纯策略反应(这里是列 \(l,w\) 和 \(r\) )中找到的。
\end{tcolorbox}

If player I wants to "secure" the maximum of this worst possible payoff, he should maximize over the lower envelope with a suitable choice of \(p\) . This maximum is easily found for \(\widehat{p} = \frac{1}{3}\) , shown by a dashed line in Figure 8.2. The strategy \(\widehat{x} = \left( {1 - \widehat{p},\widehat{p}}\right)\) is called the max-min strategy of the maximizer, which we found here graphically as the highest point on the lower envelope. The "hat" accent \(\widehat{x}\) may be thought of as representing that "peak".

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果玩家I想“确保”这种最差可能收益的最大值，他应该通过合适地选择 \(p\) 来最大化下包络。对于 \(\widehat{p} = \frac{1}{3}\) ，这个最大值很容易找到，如图8.2中的虚线所示。策略 \(\widehat{x} = \left( {1 - \widehat{p},\widehat{p}}\right)\) 被称为最大值点的极大极小策略，我们在这里通过图形将其确定为下包络上的最高点。“帽子”符号 \(\widehat{x}\) 可以被认为代表那个“峰值”。
\end{tcolorbox}

The max-min strategy \(\left( {1 - \widehat{p},\widehat{p}}\right)\) is one of the intersection points on the lower envelope. The intersection point at \(p = \frac{3}{5}\) has a lower payoff to the maximizer because the best-response line \(w\) slopes downwards from \(\frac{1}{3}\) to \(\frac{3}{5}\) . Hence, \(p = \frac{3}{5}\) is not a max-min strategy. As we show in Proposition 8.3 below, in a zero-sum game an equilibrium strategy is the same as a max-min strategy. Therefore, unlike in a bimatrix game, \(p = \frac{3}{5}\) does not need to be checked as a possible equilibrium strategy.

\begin{tcolorbox}
极大极小策略 \(\left( {1 - \widehat{p},\widehat{p}}\right)\) 是下包络上的一个交点。在 \(p = \frac{3}{5}\) 处的交点给最大值点带来的收益较低，因为最优反应线 \(w\) 从 \(\frac{1}{3}\) 到 \(\frac{3}{5}\) 是向下倾斜的。因此， \(p = \frac{3}{5}\) 不是极大极小策略。正如我们在下面的命题8.3中所示，在零和博弈中，均衡策略与极大极小策略是相同的。因此，与双矩阵博弈不同，不需要将 \(p = \frac{3}{5}\) 作为可能的均衡策略进行检验。
\end{tcolorbox}

The max-min payoff is well defined and does not depend on what player II does, because we always take the minimum over player II's possible responses. The maximum (and thus the max-min payoff) is unique, but there may be more than one choice of the max-min strategy \(\widehat{x}\) . An example is shown in Figure 8.3 where both \(L\) and \(R\) have the same payoff 0.6 in column \(w\) (and are therefore both best responses to \(w\) , so that this game is degenerate according to Definition 6.7). Then the lower envelope has its maximum 0.6 anywhere for \(\widehat{p} \in  \left\lbrack  {\frac{1}{5},\frac{2}{3}}\right\rbrack\) , which defines the possible max-min strategies \(\widehat{x} = \left( {1 - \widehat{p},\widehat{p}}\right)\) ; the endpoints of the interval are found with the difference trick for the indifferences between \(l\) and \(w\) , and \(w\) and \(r\) , respectively. On the side of player II, the unique best response against \(\widehat{p} = \frac{1}{2}\) (for example) is the pure strategy \(w\) . Therefore, without any further considerations, \(w\) is the unique equilibrium strategy of player II in any equilibrium; this is a very useful consequence of Proposition 8.3, as we will explain.

\begin{tcolorbox}
极大极小收益是明确定义的，并且不依赖于玩家II的行动，因为我们总是取玩家II可能反应的最小值。最大值(也就是极大极小收益)是唯一的，但极大极小策略 \(\widehat{x}\) 可能有不止一种选择。图8.3展示了一个例子，其中 \(L\) 和 \(R\) 在列 \(w\) 中的收益都是0.6(因此它们都是对 \(w\) 的最优反应，根据定义6.7，这个博弈是退化的)。那么下包络在 \(\widehat{p} \in  \left\lbrack  {\frac{1}{5},\frac{2}{3}}\right\rbrack\) 的任何位置都有最大值0.6，这就确定了可能的极大极小策略 \(\widehat{x} = \left( {1 - \widehat{p},\widehat{p}}\right)\) ；该区间的端点分别通过差分技巧来确定 \(l\) 和 \(w\) 以及 \(w\) 和 \(r\) 之间的无差异来得到。在玩家II这边，针对 \(\widehat{p} = \frac{1}{2}\) (例如)的唯一最优反应是纯策略 \(w\) 。因此，无需进一步考虑， \(w\) 是玩家II在任何均衡中的唯一均衡策略；正如我们将解释的，这是命题8.3的一个非常有用的结果。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_222_204_216_1238_574_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 8.3 The Penalty game with the same scoring probability 0.6 when the column player chooses \(w\) . Then the game is degenerate, and the max-min strategy \(\left( {1 - \widehat{p},\widehat{p}}\right)\) is not unique, \(\widehat{p} \in  \left\lbrack  {\frac{1}{5},\frac{2}{3}}\right\rbrack\)

\begin{tcolorbox}
图8.3 当列玩家选择 \(w\) 时，罚球博弈的得分概率相同，均为0.6。此时博弈是退化的，极大极小策略 \(\left( {1 - \widehat{p},\widehat{p}}\right)\) 不是唯一的， \(\widehat{p} \in  \left\lbrack  {\frac{1}{5},\frac{2}{3}}\right\rbrack\)
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\Rightarrow\) Exercise 8.1 is a variant of the Penalty game where the striker is more likely to score, and in part (b) has a third strategy \(M\) of shooting down the middle.

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\Rightarrow\) 练习8.1是罚球博弈的一个变体，其中射手更有可能得分，并且在(b)部分有第三种策略 \(M\) ，即向中间射门。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{8.3 Max-Min and Min-Max Strategies}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{8.3 极大极小和极小极大策略}
\end{tcolorbox}

The important new concept for zero-sum games is that of a max-min strategy. This concept applies in principle to any game, where we focus on two-player games. A max-min strategy is a strategy of a player that is defined in terms of the player's own payoffs. It also depends on the strategy set of the player. In the following definition, we assume general (nonempty) strategy sets \(X\) and \(Y\) , which are normally, but don't have to be, the mixed-strategy sets in (6.6). A min-max strategy is a strategy of the other player.

\begin{tcolorbox}
零和博弈的一个重要新概念是极大极小策略。这个概念原则上适用于任何博弈，这里我们关注两人博弈。极大极小策略是根据玩家自身收益来定义的玩家策略。它也依赖于玩家的策略集。在下面的定义中，我们假设一般的(非空)策略集 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) ，它们通常但不必须是(6.6)中的混合策略集。极小极大策略是另一个玩家的策略。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 8.1 (Max-min strategy, min-max strategy). Consider a two-player game with strategy set \(X\) for player I and \(Y\) for player II, and payoff \(a\left( {x,y}\right)\) to player I for \(x \in  X\) and \(y \in  Y\) . A max-min strategy \(\widehat{x} \in  X\) of player I fulfills

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义8.1(极大极小策略，极小极大策略)。考虑一个双人博弈，玩家I的策略集为 \(X\) ，玩家II的策略集为 \(Y\) ，当玩家I选择 \(x \in  X\) 且玩家II选择 \(y \in  Y\) 时，玩家I的收益为 \(a\left( {x,y}\right)\) 。玩家I的极大极小策略 \(\widehat{x} \in  X\) 满足
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}a\left( {\widehat{x},y}\right)  = \mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}a\left( {x,y}\right) , \tag{8.1}
\]

assuming these minima and maxima exist, which defines the max-min payoff to player I. A min-max strategy \(\widehat{y} \in  Y\) of player II fulfills

\begin{tcolorbox}
假设这些最小值和最大值存在，这就定义了玩家I的极大极小收益。玩家II的极小极大策略 \(\widehat{y} \in  Y\) 满足
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}a\left( {x,\widehat{y}}\right)  = \mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}a\left( {x,y}\right) , \tag{8.2}
\]

which defines the min-max payoff to player I.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这就定义了玩家I的极小极大收益。
\end{tcolorbox}

Consider first a finite game, for example the game at the top right in Figure 8.2 with the \(2 \times  3\) payoff matrix \(A\) to the row player \(I\) . With the strategy sets \(X = \{ L,R\}\) and \(Y = \{ l,w,r\}\) in Definition 8.1, the minima and maxima in (8.1) and (8.2) clearly exist. Because player I cannot randomize, he has to consider the minimum payoff against \(L\) (which is 0.5) or \(R\) (which is 0.4, both indicated by the circles), so his max-min strategy is \(L\) with max-min payoff 0.5 . For the min-max strategy, the column player II has to consider the maximum payoff to player I (indicated by the boxes) against \(l,w\) , and \(r\) , which is 1.0,0.7, and 1.0, respectively, so the min-max strategy is \(w\) with min-max payoff 0.7. Here, max-min and min-max payoff are different.

\begin{tcolorbox}
首先考虑一个有限博弈，例如图8.2右上角的博弈，行玩家 \(I\) 的 \(2 \times  3\) 收益矩阵为 \(A\) 。根据定义8.1中的策略集 \(X = \{ L,R\}\) 和 \(Y = \{ l,w,r\}\) ，式(8.1)和(8.2)中的最小值和最大值显然存在。由于玩家I不能随机化策略，他必须考虑针对 \(L\) (收益为0.5)或 \(R\) (收益为0.4，两者都用圆圈表示)的最小收益，因此他的极大极小策略是 \(L\) ，极大极小收益为0.5。对于极小极大策略，列玩家II必须考虑玩家I针对 \(l,w\) 和 \(r\) 的最大收益(用方框表示)，分别为1.0、0.7和1.0，因此极小极大策略是 \(w\) ，极小极大收益为0.7。这里，极大极小收益和极小极大收益不同。
\end{tcolorbox}

We normally consider the mixed extension of a finite game. Then \(X\) and \(Y\) are the sets of mixed strategies, and the payoff to player I in Definition 8.1 is given by \(a\left( {x,y}\right)  = {x}^{\top }{Ay}\) . With the goalpost diagram in Figure 8.2, we have identified the (mixed) max-min strategy of player \(\mathrm{I}\) as \(\widehat{x} = \left( {\frac{2}{3},\frac{1}{3}}\right)\) with max-min payoff \(\frac{2}{3}\) . We will shortly see that the min-max strategy player II is her equilibrium strategy \(\left( {\frac{1}{6},\frac{5}{6},0}\right)\) , with the min-max payoff to player I also given by \(\frac{2}{3}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们通常考虑有限博弈的混合扩展。此时 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) 是混合策略集，定义8.1中玩家I的收益由 \(a\left( {x,y}\right)  = {x}^{\top }{Ay}\) 给出。根据图8.2中的门柱图，我们已确定玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 的(混合)极大极小策略为 \(\widehat{x} = \left( {\frac{2}{3},\frac{1}{3}}\right)\) ，极大极小收益为 \(\frac{2}{3}\) 。我们很快会看到，玩家II的极小极大策略是她的均衡策略 \(\left( {\frac{1}{6},\frac{5}{6},0}\right)\) ，玩家I的极小极大收益同样为 \(\frac{2}{3}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The rationale behind a max-min strategy is that it gives a guaranteed (expected) payoff to the player, irrespective of what the other player does. (A max-min strategy is therefore sometimes called a "security strategy".) If player I plays \(x\) , his worst-case payoff is \(\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}a\left( {x,y}\right)\) , which is maximized by player I’s max-min strategy \(\widehat{x}\left( {8.1}\right)\) and gives him his max-min payoff. In a game with general payoffs, player II may not necessarily want to play the minimizing strategy \(y\) against \(x\) in \(\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}a\left( {x,y}\right)\) . However, in a zero-sum game, this strategy \(y\) is player II’s best response to \(x\) , so this is what player I should expect.

\begin{tcolorbox}
极大极小策略背后的基本原理是，无论另一个玩家采取什么行动，它都能为玩家提供有保证的(期望)收益。(因此，极大极小策略有时也被称为“安全策略”。)如果玩家I选择 \(x\) ，他的最坏情况收益是 \(\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}a\left( {x,y}\right)\) ，玩家I的极大极小策略 \(\widehat{x}\left( {8.1}\right)\) 使该收益最大化，并为他带来极大极小收益。在一般收益的博弈中，玩家II不一定想在 \(\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}a\left( {x,y}\right)\) 中针对 \(x\) 采用最小化策略 \(y\) 。然而，在零和博弈中，该策略 \(y\) 是玩家II对 \(x\) 的最优反应，因此这是玩家I应该预期到的。
\end{tcolorbox}

In a min-max strategy \(\widehat{y}\) in (8.2), player II chooses \(\widehat{y}\) under the assumption that player I chooses a best response \(x\) to achieve the payoff \(\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}a\left( {x,\widehat{y}}\right)\) . By playing \(\widehat{y}\) , player II minimizes this payoff. In a game with general payoffs, this is a rather malevolent action by player II and not justified by any security considerations in terms of her own payoffs.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在式(8.2)的极小极大策略 \(\widehat{y}\) 中，玩家II在假设玩家I选择最优反应 \(x\) 以实现收益 \(\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}a\left( {x,\widehat{y}}\right)\) 的前提下选择 \(\widehat{y}\) 。通过选择 \(\widehat{y}\) ，玩家II使该收益最小化。在一般收益的博弈中，这是玩家II相当恶意的行为，从她自身收益的安全考虑来看是不合理的。
\end{tcolorbox}

However, in a zero-sum game, a min-max strategy is just a max-min strategy for the minimizer. Namely, suppose the zero-sum game is(A, B)with \(B =  - A\) . Then with \(a\left( {x,y}\right)  = {x}^{\top }{Ay}\) , a min-max strategy \(\widehat{y}\) fulfills (8.2), or equivalently

\begin{tcolorbox}
然而，在零和博弈(zero-sum game)中，极小极大策略(min-max strategy)只是针对最小值点(minimizer)的极大极小策略(max-min strategy)。即，假设零和博弈为(A, B)，其中 \(B =  - A\) 。那么，对于 \(a\left( {x,y}\right)  = {x}^{\top }{Ay}\) ，极小极大策略 \(\widehat{y}\) 满足式(8.2)，或者等价地
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\min }\limits_{{x \in  X}}{x}^{\top }B\widehat{y} = \mathop{\max }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}\mathop{\min }\limits_{{x \in  X}}{x}^{\top }{By}
\]

so \(\widehat{y}\) is a max-min strategy in terms of player II’s payoff matrix \(B\) . In zero-sum games, a min-max strategy is therefore not a different concept, but merely expresses player II’s preferences in terms of her cost matrix \(A\) , which is also the payoff matrix to player I and defines the matrix game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此， \(\widehat{y}\) 是根据玩家 II 的收益矩阵 \(B\) 得出的极大极小策略。在零和博弈中，极小极大策略并非不同的概念，而仅仅是用玩家 II 的成本矩阵 \(A\) 来表达玩家 II 的偏好，该矩阵也是玩家 I 的收益矩阵，并定义了矩阵博弈(matrix game)。
\end{tcolorbox}

The minima and maxima in Definition 8.1 are assumed to exist, which holds if \(X\) and \(Y\) are compact and \(a\left( {x,y}\right)\) is continuous in \(x\) and \(y\) . This is certainly the case for our standard assumption that \(X\) and \(Y\) are the mixed-strategy sets for a matrix game \(A\) and \(a\left( {x,y}\right)  = {x}^{\top }{Ay}\) . The following proposition observes that in \(\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{x}^{\top }{Ay}\) the minimum is already obtained for pure strategies \(j\) instead

\begin{tcolorbox}
假设定义 8.1 中的最小值和最大值存在，若 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) 是紧集(compact set)，且 \(a\left( {x,y}\right)\) 关于 \(x\) 和 \(y\) 连续，则该假设成立。当我们通常假设 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) 是矩阵博弈 \(A\) 和 \(a\left( {x,y}\right)  = {x}^{\top }{Ay}\) 的混合策略集(mixed-strategy set)时，情况确实如此。下面的命题指出，在 \(\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{x}^{\top }{Ay}\) 中，最小值已经可以通过纯策略 \(j\) 而非混合策略 \(X\) 得到(例如，在考虑图 8.2 中的下包络(lower envelope)时就用到了这一点)，类似地，在 \(Y\) 中，最大值已经可以通过纯策略 \(a\left( {x,y}\right)\) 而非混合策略 \(x\) 得到。
\end{tcolorbox}

of mixed strategies \(y \in  Y\) (this has been used in considering the lower envelope in Figure 8.2, for example), and similarly that in \(\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}{x}^{\top }{Ay}\) the maximum is already obtained for pure strategies \(i\) instead of mixed strategies \(x \in  X\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
即，在 \(\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}{x}^{\top }{Ay}\) 中，最大值已经可以通过纯策略 \(i\) 而非混合策略 \(x \in  X\) 得到。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proposition 8.2. For an \(m \times  n\) matrix game \(A\) and \(x \in  X\) ,

\begin{tcolorbox}
命题 8.2. 对于一个 \(m \times  n\) 矩阵博弈 \(A\) 和 \(x \in  X\) ，
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{x}^{\top }{Ay} = \min \left\{  {{\left( {x}^{\top }A\right) }_{j} \mid  1 \leq  j \leq  n}\right\}  . \tag{8.3}
\]

For \(y \in  Y\) ,

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于 \(y \in  Y\) ，
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}{x}^{\top }{Ay} = \max \left\{  {{\left( Ay\right) }_{i} \mid  1 \leq  i \leq  m}\right\}  . \tag{8.4}
\]

Moreover, a max-min strategy \(\widehat{x} \in  X\) and a min-max strategy \(\widehat{y} \in  Y\) exist.

\begin{tcolorbox}
此外，存在一个极大极小策略 \(\widehat{x} \in  X\) 和一个极小极大策略 \(\widehat{y} \in  Y\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Let \(y \in  Y\) . The best response condition (6.10) says that a best response to \(y\) can be found among the pure strategies \(i\) of the row player, which implies (8.4). Condition (8.3) holds similarly.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。设 \(y \in  Y\) 。最优反应条件(6.10)表明，对 \(y\) 的最优反应(best response)可以在行玩家的纯策略 \(i\) 中找到，这意味着式(8.4)成立。类似地，条件(8.3)也成立。
\end{tcolorbox}

The right-hand side of (8.3) is the minimum of a finite number of terms \({\left( {x}^{\top }A\right) }_{j}\) and continuous in \(x\) . The equal left-hand side \(\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{x}^{\top }{Ay}\) is therefore also continuous in \(x\) and assumes its maximum for some \(\widehat{x}\) on the compact set \(X\) . Then \(\widehat{x}\) defines a max-min strategy. Similarly, a min-max strategy \(\widehat{y}\) exists.

\begin{tcolorbox}
式(8.3)的右边是有限项 \({\left( {x}^{\top }A\right) }_{j}\) 的最小值，且关于 \(x\) 连续。因此，相等的左边 \(\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{x}^{\top }{Ay}\) 关于 \(x\) 也连续，并且在紧集 \(X\) 上的某个 \(\widehat{x}\) 处取得最大值。那么 \(\widehat{x}\) 定义了一个极大极小策略。类似地，存在一个极小极大策略 \(\widehat{y}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Because max-min payoffs and min-max costs are (in expectation) "guaranteed", the first cannot be higher than the second: With a max-min strategy \(\widehat{x}\) and min-max strategy \(\widehat{y}\) as in Definition 8.1, we have

\begin{tcolorbox}
因为极大极小收益和极小极大成本(在期望意义下)是“有保证的”，所以前者不能高于后者:根据定义 8.1 中的极大极小策略 \(\widehat{x}\) 和极小极大策略 \(\widehat{y}\) ，我们有
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}a\left( {x,y}\right)  = \mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}a\left( {\widehat{x},y}\right)
\]

\[
\leq  a\left( {\widehat{x},\widehat{y}}\right)
\]

\[
\leq  \mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}a\left( {x,\widehat{y}}\right)  = \mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}a\left( {x,y}\right) . \tag{8.5}
\]

This is the near-obvious payoff inequality "maxmin \(\leq\) minmax", which holds for any nonempty sets \(X\) and \(Y\) , assuming that the minima and maxima exist. We have seen that for pure-strategy sets \(X\) and \(Y\) , the inequality may be strict.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这就是几乎显而易见的收益不等式“极大极小 \(\leq\) 极小极大”，对于任何非空集 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) ，假设最小值和最大值存在，则该不等式成立。我们已经看到，对于纯策略集 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) ，该不等式可能是严格不等式。
\end{tcolorbox}

If \(X\) and \(Y\) are the mixed-strategy sets and \(a\left( {x,y}\right)  = {x}^{\top }{Ay}\) , the famous minimax (short for "maxmin = minmax") theorem of von Neumann (1928) states that this inequality holds as an equality. The following proposition states that this theorem is equivalent to \(\left( {\widehat{x},\widehat{y}}\right)\) being an equilibrium of the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) 是混合策略集，且 \(a\left( {x,y}\right)  = {x}^{\top }{Ay}\) ，冯·诺伊曼(1928)著名的极小极大定理(“极大极小 = 极小极大”的简称)指出，该不等式取等号。下面的命题指出，该定理等价于 \(\left( {\widehat{x},\widehat{y}}\right)\) 是该博弈的一个均衡(equilibrium)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proposition 8.3. Consider an \(m \times  n\) matrix game \(A\) with mixed-strategy sets \(X\) and \(Y\) as in (6.6), and let \(\left( {{x}^{ * },{y}^{ * }}\right)  \in  X \times  Y\) . Then the following are equivalent:

\begin{tcolorbox}
命题8.3. 考虑一个 \(m \times  n\) 矩阵博弈 \(A\) ，其混合策略集为 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) ，如(6.6)所示，并且设 \(\left( {{x}^{ * },{y}^{ * }}\right)  \in  X \times  Y\) 。那么以下表述等价:
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) \(\left( {{x}^{ * },{y}^{ * }}\right)\) is an equilibrium of(A, - A);

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) \(\left( {{x}^{ * },{y}^{ * }}\right)\) 是(A, - A)的一个均衡；
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) \({x}^{ * }\) is a max-min strategy and \({y}^{ * }\) is a min-max strategy, and

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) \({x}^{ * }\) 是一个极大极小策略， \({y}^{ * }\) 是一个极小极大策略，并且
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{x}^{\top }{Ay} = \mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}{x}^{\top }{Ay}. \tag{8.6}
\]

Proof. Suppose (b) holds. Consider a max-min strategy \(\widehat{x}\) as in (8.1) and a min-max strategy \(\widehat{y}\) as in (8.2). Then (8.6) implies

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明. 假设(b)成立。考虑如(8.1)所示的极大极小策略 \(\widehat{x}\) 和如(8.2)所示的极小极大策略 \(\widehat{y}\) 。那么(8.6)意味着
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{\widehat{x}}^{\top }A\widehat{y} \geq  \mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{\widehat{x}}^{\top }{Ay}
\]

\[
= \mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{x}^{\top }{Ay}
\]

\[
= \mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}{x}^{\top }{Ay}
\]

\[
= \mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}{x}^{\top }A\widehat{y} \geq  {\widehat{x}}^{\top }A\widehat{y}
\]

so all these inequalities hold as equalities. Hence,

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此所有这些不等式都取等号。所以，
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}{x}^{\top }A\widehat{y} = {\widehat{x}}^{\top }A\widehat{y} = \mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{\widehat{x}}^{\top }{Ay}, \tag{8.7}
\]

which says that \(\left( {\widehat{x},\widehat{y}}\right)\) is a equilibrium because (8.7) is equivalent to

\begin{tcolorbox}
这表明 \(\left( {\widehat{x},\widehat{y}}\right)\) 是一个均衡，因为(8.7)等价于
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{x}^{\top }A\widehat{y} \leq  {\widehat{x}}^{\top }A\widehat{y} \leq  {\widehat{x}}^{\top }{Ay}\;\text{ for all }x \in  X,y \in  Y, \tag{8.8}
\]

where the left inequality in (8.8) states that \(\widehat{x}\) is a best response to \(\widehat{y}\) and the right inequality that \(\widehat{y}\) is a best response to \(\widehat{x}\) . This shows (a).

\begin{tcolorbox}
其中(8.8)中的左不等式表明 \(\widehat{x}\) 是对 \(\widehat{y}\) 的最优反应，右不等式表明 \(\widehat{y}\) 是对 \(\widehat{x}\) 的最优反应。这证明了(a)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Conversely, suppose (a) holds, that is, as in (8.7),

\begin{tcolorbox}
反之，假设(a)成立，即如(8.7)所示，
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}{x}^{\top }A{y}^{ * } = {x}^{*\top }A{y}^{ * } = \mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{x}^{*\top }{Ay}.
\]

Using (8.5), this implies

\begin{tcolorbox}
利用(8.5)，这意味着
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{x}^{*\top }A{y}^{ * } = \mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{x}^{*\top }{Ay}
\]

\[
\leq  \mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{x}^{\top }{Ay}
\]

\[
\leq  \mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}{x}^{\top }{Ay}
\]

\[
\leq  \mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}{x}^{\top }A{y}^{ * } = {x}^{*\top }A{y}^{ * }
\]

so we also have equalities throughout, which state that \({x}^{ * }\) is a max-min strategy and \({y}^{ * }\) is a min-max strategy, and (8.6) holds, which shows (b).

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此我们同样处处取等号，这表明 \({x}^{ * }\) 是一个极大极小策略， \({y}^{ * }\) 是一个极小极大策略，并且(8.6)成立，这证明了(b)。
\end{tcolorbox}

The minimax theorem is therefore a consequence of the Theorem 6.5 of Nash (1951).

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此，极小极大定理是纳什(1951)的定理6.5的一个推论。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 8.4 (The minimax theorem of von Neumann, 1928). In a matrix game with payoff matrix \(A\) to the maximizing row player \(I\) and mixed-strategy sets \(X\) and \(Y\) for players I and II,

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理8.4(冯·诺伊曼的极小极大定理，1928)。在一个矩阵博弈中，对于最大化的行玩家 \(I\) 其收益矩阵为 \(A\) ，玩家I和玩家II的混合策略集分别为 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) ，
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}\mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{x}^{\top }{Ay} = v = \mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}\mathop{\max }\limits_{{x \in  X}}{x}^{\top }{Ay} \tag{8.9}
\]

where \(v\) is the unique max-min payoff to player I and min-max cost to player II, called the value of the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
其中 \(v\) 是玩家I的唯一极大极小收益和玩家II的唯一极小极大成本，称为博弈的值。
\end{tcolorbox}

Nash's theorem is proved with the help of Brouwer's fixed-point theorem, which has a complex proof. We give a short self-contained proof of the minimax Theorem 8.4 in the next Section 8.4.

\begin{tcolorbox}
纳什定理是借助布劳威尔不动点定理证明的，该定理的证明很复杂。我们将在接下来的8.4节中给出极小极大定理8.4的一个简短的自包含证明。
\end{tcolorbox}

Equilibrium strategies for a two-player zero-sum game have the following very desirable properties, which usually do not hold in games with general payoffs:

\begin{tcolorbox}
两人零和博弈的均衡策略具有以下非常理想的性质，而这些性质在一般收益的博弈中通常不成立:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item The equilibrium payoff to the players is unique, and called the value of the game (as payoff \(v\) in (8.9) to the maximizer and cost to the minimizer).
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 玩家的均衡收益是唯一的，称为博弈的值(如(8.9)中对最大值点的收益 \(v\) 和对最小值点的成本)。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item An equilibrium strategy is the same as a max-min strategy (for the maximizer, and min-max strategy for the minimizer). It is therefore independent of what the other player does. For that reason it is also called an optimal strategy of the player.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 均衡策略与极大极小策略(对于最大值点而言)以及极小极大策略(对于最小值点而言)相同。因此，它与其他玩家的行为无关。出于这个原因，它也被称为玩家的最优策略。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

"Optimal strategy" assumes that players play as a well as they can. Optimal strategies are often rather "defensive" strategies that give the same expected payoff to many responses of the opponent. For example, a symmetric zero-sum game necessarily has value zero (see Exercise 8.3(c)). Hence, an optimal strategy of the maximizer will guarantee him at least payoff zero for all pure responses of the opponent, even if the opponent does not play optimally. Bad play of the opponent is thereby not exploited, but it can only help the player who plays optimally.

\begin{tcolorbox}
“最优策略”假定玩家会尽可能地发挥出最佳水平。最优策略通常是相当“保守”的策略，对于对手的许多应对方式，这些策略都能带来相同的期望效用。例如，一个对称的零和博弈的价值必然为零(见练习8.3(c))。因此，最大值点的最优策略将保证他在对手采用所有纯策略应对时，至少能获得零收益，即使对手没有采用最优策略。这样一来，对手的糟糕表现并不会被利用，但这只会对采用最优策略的玩家有利。
\end{tcolorbox}
