Because an optimal strategy does not depend on the other player, we also have:

\begin{tcolorbox}
因为最优策略不依赖于其他玩家，所以我们还有:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item In a zero-sum game, equilibrium strategies are exchangeable: If(x, y)and \(\left( {\bar{x},\bar{y}}\right)\) are two equilibria of a zero-sum game, then \(\left( {\bar{x},y}\right)\) and \(\left( {x,\bar{y}}\right)\) are also equilibria.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 在零和博弈中，均衡策略是可交换的:如果(x, y)和 \(\left( {\bar{x},\bar{y}}\right)\) 是零和博弈的两个均衡，那么 \(\left( {\bar{x},y}\right)\) 和 \(\left( {x,\bar{y}}\right)\) 也是均衡。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item As stated in Proposition 8.3, in a zero-sum game an equilibrium strategy is the same as a max-min (respectively, min-max) strategy. For example, if the zero-sum game has an equilibrium with a unique pure best response, that pure strategy is also the unique max-min strategy of the maximizer or min-max strategy of the minimizer. The reason is that if the min-max strategy (say) was not unique, it would be a non-unique strategy in any equilibrium. An example is the game in Figure 8.3: The max-min strategies are given by \(\left( {1 - \widehat{p},\widehat{p}}\right)\) for \(p \in  \left\lbrack  {\frac{1}{5},\frac{2}{3}}\right\rbrack\) ; for any value of \(\widehat{p}\) in the interior of that interval (for example \(\widehat{p} = \frac{1}{2}\) ), the unique best response of player II is the pure strategy \(w\) , which is therefore also the unique equilibrium strategy of player II and min-max strategy.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 如命题8.3所述，在零和博弈中，均衡策略与极大极小(或极小极大)策略相同。例如，如果零和博弈存在一个具有唯一纯最优反应的均衡，那么该纯策略也是最大值点的唯一极大极小策略或最小值点的唯一极小极大策略。原因在于，如果(比如说)极小极大策略不是唯一的，那么它在任何均衡中都将是一个非唯一的策略。图8.3中的博弈就是一个例子:极大极小策略由 \(\left( {1 - \widehat{p},\widehat{p}}\right)\) 给出，其中 \(p \in  \left\lbrack  {\frac{1}{5},\frac{2}{3}}\right\rbrack\) ；对于该区间内部的任何 \(\widehat{p}\) 值(例如 \(\widehat{p} = \frac{1}{2}\) )，玩家II的唯一最优反应是纯策略 \(w\) ，因此这也是玩家II的唯一均衡策略和极小极大策略。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

Further properties are discussed in Section 8.5, namely the convexity of the set of optimal strategies (Proposition 8.6), and that weakly dominated strategies can be removed without affecting the value of the game (Proposition 8.7); however, this removal may affect the set of optimal strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
第8.5节将讨论更多性质，即最优策略集的凸性(命题8.6)，以及可以移除弱被占优策略而不影响博弈的价值(命题8.7)；然而，这种移除可能会影响最优策略集。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{8.4 A Short Proof of the Minimax Theorem}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{8.4 极小极大定理的简短证明}
\end{tcolorbox}

The minimax Theorem 8.4 of von Neumann (1928) is, via Proposition 8.3, a special case of Nash's Theorem 6.5 on the existence of an equilibrium in a finite game. However, von Neumann's theorem predates Nash's result, and can be proved on its own. The following proof by induction is particularly simple.

\begin{tcolorbox}
冯·诺伊曼(1928)的极小极大定理8.4，通过命题8.3，是纳什关于有限博弈中均衡存在性的定理6.5的一个特例。然而，冯·诺伊曼的定理早于纳什的结果，并且可以独立证明。以下的归纳证明特别简单。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 8.5. For an \(m \times  n\) matrix game \(A\) with mixed-strategy sets \(X\) and \(Y\) in (6.6) there are \(x \in  X,y \in  Y\) and \(v \in  \mathbb{R}\) so that

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理8.5。对于一个 \(m \times  n\) 矩阵博弈 \(A\) ，其混合策略集为(6.6)中的 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) ，存在 \(x \in  X,y \in  Y\) 和 \(v \in  \mathbb{R}\) 使得
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{Ay} \leq  \mathbf{1}v,\;{x}^{\top }A \geq  v{\mathbf{1}}^{\top }.
\]

Then \(v\) is the unique value of the game and (8.9) holds, and \(x\) and \(y\) are optimal strategies of the players.

\begin{tcolorbox}
那么 \(v\) 是该博弈的唯一价值，并且(8.9)成立， \(x\) 和 \(y\) 是玩家的最优策略。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Consider the two optimization problems (with \(v,u \in  \mathbb{R}\) )

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。考虑两个优化问题(其中 \(v,u \in  \mathbb{R}\) )
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\operatorname{minimize}}\limits_{{y,v}}v\;\text{ subject to }{Ay} \leq  \mathbf{1}v,\;y \in  Y. \tag{8.10}
\]

and

\begin{tcolorbox}
以及
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\operatorname{maximize}}\limits_{{x,u}}u\text{ subject to }{x}^{\top }A \geq  u{\mathbf{1}}^{\top },\;x \in  X. \tag{8.11}
\]

The inequalities \({Ay} \leq  {1v}\) state that by playing \(y\) player II pays at most \(v\) in every row of the game. Similarly, \({x}^{\top }A \geq  u{\mathbf{1}}^{\top }\) states that by playing \(x\) player I gets at least \(u\) in every column of the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
不等式 \({Ay} \leq  {1v}\) 表明，玩家II采用 \(y\) 策略时，在博弈的每一行中支付最多为 \(v\) 。类似地， \({x}^{\top }A \geq  u{\mathbf{1}}^{\top }\) 表明，玩家I采用 \(x\) 策略时，在博弈的每一列中至少获得 \(u\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Let \(y,v\) be optimal for (8.10). Then at least one of the \(m\) inequalities \({Ay} \leq  \mathbf{1}v\) holds as equality because if all inequalities were strict then \(v\) could be reduced further. Hence, \(v\) is the largest entry \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) of \({Ay}\) (as in any pure best response \(i\) to \(y\) ) which by (8.4) fulfills

\begin{tcolorbox}
设 \(y,v\) 是 (8.10) 的最优解。那么 \(m\) 个不等式 \({Ay} \leq  \mathbf{1}v\) 中至少有一个取等号，因为如果所有不等式都是严格不等式，那么 \(v\) 可以进一步减小。因此， \(v\) 是 \({Ay}\) 的最大元素 \({\left( Ay\right) }_{i}\) (就像对 \(y\) 的任何纯最优反应 \(i\) 一样)，根据 (8.4) 满足
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
v = \mathop{\max }\limits_{{1 \leq  i \leq  m}}{\left( Ay\right) }_{i} = \mathop{\max }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{x}^{\top }{Ay}
\]

and an optimal choice of \(y\) in (8.10) is a min-max strategy. Similarly, for an optimal pair \(x,u\) in (8.11) we have by (8.3)

\begin{tcolorbox}
并且 (8.10) 中 \(y\) 的最优选择是一个极小极大策略(min-max strategy)。类似地，对于 (8.11) 中的最优对 \(x,u\) ，根据 (8.3) 我们有
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
u = \mathop{\min }\limits_{{1 \leq  j \leq  n}}{\left( {x}^{\top }A\right) }_{j} = \mathop{\min }\limits_{{y \in  Y}}{x}^{\top }{Ay}
\]

where \(x\) is a max-min strategy.

\begin{tcolorbox}
其中 \(x\) 是一个极大极小策略(max-min strategy)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Consider feasible (not necessarily optimal) solutions \(y,v,x,u\) to the problems (8.10) and (8.11), that is,

\begin{tcolorbox}
考虑问题 (8.10) 和 (8.11) 的可行(不一定是最优)解 \(y,v,x,u\) ，即
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{Ay} \leq  \mathbf{1}v,\;y \in  Y,\;{x}^{\top }A \geq  u{\mathbf{1}}^{\top },\;x \in  X. \tag{8.12}
\]

Then

\begin{tcolorbox}
那么
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
u = u{\mathbf{1}}^{\top }y \leq  {x}^{\top }{Ay} \leq  {x}^{\top }\mathbf{1}v = v. \tag{8.13}
\]

This implies that the objective functions in (8.10) and (8.11) are bounded and (without changing their optimum values) can be restricted to nonempty compact domains, namely to \(\left( {y,v}\right)  \in  Y \times  \left\lbrack  {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right\rbrack\) and to \(\left( {x,u}\right)  \in  X \times  \left\lbrack  {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right\rbrack\) for some \(\bar{y},\bar{v},\bar{x},\bar{u}\) that fulfill (8.12). Hence, the optimum values \(v\) and \(u\) in (8.10) and (8.11) exist (and are unique). The claim is that \(u = v\) , which proves "maxmin \(=\) minmax" and (8.9).

\begin{tcolorbox}
这意味着 (8.10) 和 (8.11) 中的目标函数是有界的，并且(在不改变其最优值的情况下)可以限制在非空紧域上，即对于满足 (8.12) 的某个 \(\bar{y},\bar{v},\bar{x},\bar{u}\) ，限制在 \(\left( {y,v}\right)  \in  Y \times  \left\lbrack  {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right\rbrack\) 和 \(\left( {x,u}\right)  \in  X \times  \left\lbrack  {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right\rbrack\) 上。因此，(8.10) 和 (8.11) 中的最优值 \(v\) 和 \(u\) 存在(并且是唯一的)。结论是 \(u = v\) ，这证明了“极大极小 ≤ 极小极大”以及 (8.9)。
\end{tcolorbox}

We prove this claim by induction on \(m + n\) . It is trivial for \(m + n = 2\) . Consider a matrix \(A\) of any dimension \(m \times  n\) and let \(y,v\) and \(x,u\) be optimal solutions to (8.10) and (8.11), which are feasible, that is, (8.12) holds. If all inequalities in (8.12) hold as equalities, that is, \({Ay} = \mathbf{1}v\) and \(u{\mathbf{1}}^{\top } = {x}^{\top }A\) , then we also have equality in (8.13) and \(u = v\) as claimed. Hence, we can assume that at least one inequality in \({Ay} \leq  \mathbf{1}v\) is strict (or in \({x}^{\top }A \geq  u{\mathbf{1}}^{\top }\) , where the same reasoning applies). Suppose this is the \(k\) th row, that is, with matrix entries \({a}_{ij}\) of \(A\) ,

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们通过对 \(m + n\) 进行归纳来证明这个结论。对于 \(m + n = 2\) 来说这是显然的。考虑任意维度 \(m \times  n\) 的矩阵 \(A\) ，并设 \(y,v\) 和 \(x,u\) 分别是 (8.10) 和 (8.11) 的最优解，它们是可行的，即 (8.12) 成立。如果 (8.12) 中的所有不等式都取等号，即 \({Ay} = \mathbf{1}v\) 和 \(u{\mathbf{1}}^{\top } = {x}^{\top }A\) ，那么 (8.13) 也取等号，并且如所声称的 \(u = v\) 。因此，我们可以假设 \({Ay} \leq  \mathbf{1}v\) 中至少有一个不等式是严格的(或者在 \({x}^{\top }A \geq  u{\mathbf{1}}^{\top }\) 中，同样的推理适用)。假设这是第 \(k\) 行，即，对于矩阵 \(A\) 的元素 \({a}_{ij}\) ，
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{j = 1}}^{n}{a}_{kj}{y}_{j} < v \tag{8.14}
\]

Let \(\bar{A}\) be the matrix \(A\) with the \(k\) th row deleted (clearly \(m > 1\) ). Let \(\bar{y},\bar{v}\) be the optimal solution to (8.10) with \(\bar{A}\) instead of \(A\) , that is, to

\begin{tcolorbox}
设 \(\bar{A}\) 是去掉 \(k\) 行后的矩阵 \(A\) (显然 \(m > 1\) )。设 \(\bar{y},\bar{v}\) 是用 \(\bar{A}\) 代替 \(A\) 时 (8.10) 的最优解，即
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\operatorname{minimize}}\limits_{{\bar{y},\bar{v}}}\bar{v}\text{ subject to }\bar{A}\bar{y} \leq  \mathbf{1}\bar{v},\;\bar{y} \in  Y. \tag{8.15}
\]

The maximization problem that corresponds to (8.11) with \(\bar{A}\) instead of \(A\) can be written as

\begin{tcolorbox}
用 \(\bar{A}\) 代替 \(A\) 时，与 (8.11) 对应的最大化问题可以写成
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\operatorname{maximize}}\limits_{{\bar{x},\bar{u}}}\bar{u}\text{ subject to }{\bar{x}}^{\top }A \geq  \bar{u}{\mathbf{1}}^{\top },\;\bar{x} \in  X,\;{\bar{x}}_{k} = 0. \tag{8.16}
\]

Let \(\bar{x},\bar{u}\) be an optimal solution to (8.16). Then

\begin{tcolorbox}
设 \(\bar{x},\bar{u}\) 是 (8.16) 的最优解。那么
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\bar{u} \leq  u,\;\bar{v} \leq  v, \tag{8.17}
\]

because \(\bar{x},\bar{u}\) is feasible for (8.11) (so \(\bar{u} \leq  u\) for the maximum \(u\) in (8.11)) and because \(y,v\) is feasible for (8.15). Intuitively,(8.17) holds because the game \(\bar{A}\) is at least as favorable for player II as game \(A\) . By inductive hypothesis, \(\bar{u} = \bar{v}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
因为 \(\bar{x},\bar{u}\) 是 (8.11) 的可行解(所以对于 (8.11) 中的最大值 \(u\) 有 \(\bar{u} \leq  u\) )，并且因为 \(y,v\) 是 (8.15) 的可行解。直观地说，(8.17) 成立是因为博弈 \(\bar{A}\) 对玩家 II 至少和博弈 \(A\) 一样有利。根据归纳假设， \(\bar{u} = \bar{v}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

We claim (this is the main point) that \(\bar{v} = v\) . Namely, if \(\bar{v} < v\) , let \(\varepsilon  \in  (0,1\rbrack\) , so \(v\left( {1 - \varepsilon }\right)  + \bar{v}\varepsilon  = v - \varepsilon \left( {v - \bar{v}}\right)  < v\) . Consider the strategy \({y}^{\prime } = y\left( {1 - \varepsilon }\right)  + \bar{y}\varepsilon\) , which belongs to \(Y\) because \(Y\) is convex. Because \(\bar{A}y \leq  \mathbf{1}v\) and by (8.15), \({y}^{\prime }\) fulfills

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们声称(这是关键点) \(\bar{v} = v\) 。即，如果 \(\bar{v} < v\) ，设 \(\varepsilon  \in  (0,1\rbrack\) ，那么 \(v\left( {1 - \varepsilon }\right)  + \bar{v}\varepsilon  = v - \varepsilon \left( {v - \bar{v}}\right)  < v\) 。考虑策略 \({y}^{\prime } = y\left( {1 - \varepsilon }\right)  + \bar{y}\varepsilon\) ，它属于 \(Y\) ，因为 \(Y\) 是凸集。由于 \(\bar{A}y \leq  \mathbf{1}v\) 且根据 (8.15)， \({y}^{\prime }\) 满足
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\bar{A}{y}^{\prime } = \bar{A}\left( {y\left( {1 - \varepsilon }\right)  + \bar{y}\varepsilon }\right)  \leq  \mathbf{1}\left( {v\left( {1 - \varepsilon }\right)  + \bar{v}\varepsilon }\right)  < \mathbf{1}v.
\]

Similarly,(8.14) implies for sufficiently small \(\varepsilon\)

\begin{tcolorbox}
类似地，(8.14) 意味着对于足够小的 \(\varepsilon\)
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
\mathop{\sum }\limits_{{j = 1}}^{n}{a}_{kj}{y}_{j}^{\prime } = \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{j = 1}}^{n}{a}_{kj}{y}_{j} + \varepsilon \mathop{\sum }\limits_{{j = 1}}^{n}{a}_{kj}\left( {{\bar{y}}_{j} - {y}_{j}}\right)  < v
\]

which implies that there are \({y}^{\prime } \in  Y\) and \({v}^{\prime } < v\) so that \(A{y}^{\prime } \leq  \mathbf{1}{v}^{\prime }\) , in contradiction to the minimality of \(v\) in (8.10). This shows \(v = \bar{v}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
这意味着存在 \({y}^{\prime } \in  Y\) 和 \({v}^{\prime } < v\) 使得 \(A{y}^{\prime } \leq  \mathbf{1}{v}^{\prime }\) ，这与 (8.10) 中 \(v\) 的极小性相矛盾。这表明 \(v = \bar{v}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

With (8.17) this gives \(\bar{u} \leq  u \leq  v = \bar{v} = \bar{u}\) and therefore \(u = v\) , which was to be shown.

\begin{tcolorbox}
结合 (8.17) 可得 \(\bar{u} \leq  u \leq  v = \bar{v} = \bar{u}\) ，因此 \(u = v\) ，这正是我们要证明的。
\end{tcolorbox}

The idea behind this induction proof can be summarized as follows: If the players have optimal strategies that equalize the payoffs \(v\) and \(u\) for all rows and columns, then \(u = v\) . Otherwise (if needed by exchanging the players), there is at least one row with lower payoff than \(v\) , which will not be chosen by the row player, and by omitting this row from the game the theorem holds by the inductive hypothesis.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这个归纳证明背后的思想可以总结如下:如果玩家拥有能使所有行和列的收益 \(v\) 和 \(u\) 相等的最优策略，那么 \(u = v\) 。否则(如有必要，通过交换玩家)，至少有一行的收益低于 \(v\) ，行玩家不会选择这一行，并且通过从游戏中去掉这一行，根据归纳假设该定理成立。
\end{tcolorbox}

The optimization problem (8.10) is a special case of a linear program which asks to optimize a linear function subject to linear inequalities and equations. Here the variables are \(\left( {y,v}\right)  \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n} \times  \mathbb{R}\) with the linear objective function \(v\) , to be minimized, and the linear constraints are \({Ay} - \mathbf{1}v \leq  \mathbf{0},y \geq  \mathbf{0},{\mathbf{1}}^{\top }y = 1\) . The maximization problem (8.11) is the so-called dual linear program to (8.10), which uses the same data of the problem but processed "vertically" rather than "horizontally", that is, using the transposed matrix and with the roles of right-hand side and objective function exchanged (we omit the precise definition here; see the references in Section 8.6).

\begin{tcolorbox}
优化问题 (8.10) 是线性规划(linear programming)的一个特殊情况，线性规划要求在满足线性不等式和方程的条件下优化一个线性函数。这里的变量是 \(\left( {y,v}\right)  \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n} \times  \mathbb{R}\) ，线性目标函数是 \(v\) ，需要求极小值，线性约束是 \({Ay} - \mathbf{1}v \leq  \mathbf{0},y \geq  \mathbf{0},{\mathbf{1}}^{\top }y = 1\) 。最大化问题 (8.11) 是 (8.10) 的所谓对偶线性规划，它使用问题的相同数据，但“垂直”处理而非“水平”处理，即使用转置矩阵(transposed matrix)，并且交换了右侧和目标函数的角色(这里我们省略精确的定义；见 8.6 节的参考文献)。
\end{tcolorbox}

The inequality (8.13) is a special case of the weak duality of linear programming and, like (8.5), nearly immediate. The strong duality theorem of linear programming states that if both the original linear program and its dual are feasible, then their optimal values are not just mutual bounds but in fact equal. This immediately implies the minimax theorem.

\begin{tcolorbox}
不等式 (8.13) 是线性规划的弱对偶性(duality of linear programming)的一个特殊情况，并且和 (8.5) 一样，几乎是显而易见的。线性规划的强对偶定理表明，如果原始线性规划及其对偶规划都是可行的，那么它们的最优值不仅是相互的界，实际上是相等的。这立即推出了极小极大定理(minimax theorem)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Linear programming duality is a beautiful and very useful theory, for which the proof of Theorem 8.5 is a nice starting point. Moreover, algorithms for solving linear programs (which now work for hundreds of thousands, even millions, of variables and constraints) can be used to find optimal strategies of zero-sum games.

\begin{tcolorbox}
线性规划对偶(linear programming duality)是一个优美且非常有用的理论，定理 8.5 的证明是一个很好的起点。此外，求解线性规划的算法(现在可以处理数十万甚至数百万个变量和约束)可以用来寻找零和博弈(zero-sum game)的最优策略(optimal strategy)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{8.5 Further Notes on Zero-Sum Games}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{8.5 关于零和博弈的进一步说明}
\end{tcolorbox}

A max-min strategy, and the max-min payoff, only depends on the player's own payoffs. It can therefore also be defined for a game with general payoffs which are not necessarily zero-sum, and even for an \(N\) -player game. In that case, a max-min strategy represents a rather pessimistic view of the world: Player I chooses a strategy \(\widehat{x}\) that maximizes his payoff against an opponent (or opponents) who only want to harm player I. (In a zero-sum game, this coincides with the opponent's own objectives.) A min-max strategy in (8.2) can also be defined for a general game, with a different interpretation: The opponent chooses a strategy \(\widehat{y}\) with the intent to punish player \(\mathrm{I}\) , who can defend himself against this by subsequently choosing \(x\) . The min-max payoff is the maximum punishment that can be inflicted on player I that way. This also makes sense in a game with more than two players, where the opponents agree on a partial mixed-strategy profile \(\widehat{y}\) that maximally punishes player I. (Such "punishment strategies" are used to enforce certain equilibria in repeated games, which we do not cover in this book.)

\begin{tcolorbox}
极大极小策略(max - min strategy)以及极大极小收益仅取决于玩家自身的收益。因此，它也可以为具有不一定是零和的一般收益的博弈定义，甚至可以为一个 \(N\) 玩家博弈定义。在这种情况下，极大极小策略代表了一种相当悲观的世界观:玩家 I 选择一种策略 \(\widehat{x}\) ，该策略能在面对只想伤害玩家 I 的对手(或多个对手)时最大化其收益。(在零和博弈中，这与对手自身的目标一致。)式(8.2)中的极小极大策略(min - max strategy)也可以为一般博弈定义，但有不同的解释:对手选择一种策略 \(\widehat{y}\) 以惩罚玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) ，玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 随后可以通过选择 \(x\) 来进行自我防御。极小极大收益是通过这种方式可以施加给玩家 I 的最大惩罚。这在有两个以上玩家的博弈中也有意义，在这种博弈中，对手们就一个部分混合策略组合(mixed - strategy profile) \(\widehat{y}\) 达成一致，该组合能最大程度地惩罚玩家 I。(这种“惩罚策略”用于在重复博弈中维持某些均衡，本书不涉及这部分内容。)
\end{tcolorbox}

Here, we consider max-min and min-max strategies only for zero-sum games, which define the same concept and are based on the rational assumption that the opponent plays a best response, against which the player wants to play optimally.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在这里，我们仅考虑零和博弈(zero - sum game)中的极大极小策略和极小极大策略，它们定义了相同的概念，并且基于对手会采取最优反应(best response)这一理性假设，玩家希望针对此做出最优应对。
\end{tcolorbox}

We state some further observations on the max-min strategies of the maximizer and min-max strategies of the minimizer in a zero-sum game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们对零和博弈中最大值点(maximizer)的极大极小策略和最小值点(minimizer)的极小极大策略做一些进一步的观察。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proposition 8.6. In a zero-sum game, the set of optimal strategies of each player is convex.

\begin{tcolorbox}
命题8.6. 在零和博弈中，每个参与者的最优策略集是凸集。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. This follows from Propositions 8.3 and 6.9.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明. 这可由命题8.3和命题6.9推出。
\end{tcolorbox}

A weakly dominated strategy in a zero-sum game can be disregarded without affecting the value of the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在零和博弈中，可以忽略弱被占优策略而不影响博弈的值。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proposition 8.7. Consider two matrix games \(A\) and \({A}^{\prime }\) , where \({A}^{\prime }\) is obtained from \(A\) by removing a weakly dominated strategy of one of the players. Then any (mixed) equilibrium of \({A}^{\prime }\) is an equilibrium of \(A\) , and \(A\) and \({A}^{\prime }\) have the same game value.

\begin{tcolorbox}
命题8.7. 考虑两个矩阵博弈 \(A\) 和 \({A}^{\prime }\) ，其中 \({A}^{\prime }\) 是通过移除其中一个参与者的弱被占优策略从 \(A\) 得到的。那么 \({A}^{\prime }\) 的任何(混合)均衡都是 \(A\) 的均衡，并且 \(A\) 和 \({A}^{\prime }\) 具有相同的博弈值。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Consider a mixed equilibrium of \({A}^{\prime }\) , which defines a mixed strategy pair in \(A\) . By the best-response condition, it is also an equilibrium in \(A\) if there is no profitable deviation to a pure strategy. The only profitable deviation not available in \({A}^{\prime }\) would be to the weakly dominated strategy, but then deviating to the strategy that dominates it would also be profitable and available in \({A}^{\prime }\) . Hence, the equilibrium of \({A}^{\prime }\) is also an equilibrium of \(A\) (Proposition 3.3(a) is similar but about pure equilibria). By Proposition 8.3, the equilibrium payoff in a zero-sum game is the value of the game, which is therefore the same in both games.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明. 考虑 \({A}^{\prime }\) 的一个混合均衡，它在 \(A\) 中定义了一个混合策略对。根据最优反应条件，如果不存在向纯策略的有利偏离，那么它也是 \(A\) 中的一个均衡。在 \({A}^{\prime }\) 中不可行的唯一有利偏离是转向弱被占优策略，但此时转向占优该策略的策略也将是有利的，并且在 \({A}^{\prime }\) 中是可行的。因此， \({A}^{\prime }\) 的均衡也是 \(A\) 的均衡(命题3.3(a)类似，但针对的是纯均衡)。根据命题8.3，零和博弈中的均衡收益就是博弈的值，因此这两个博弈中的值是相同的。
\end{tcolorbox}

The following game, with its pure best responses shown on the right, has two pure equilibria(T, l)and(T, r). Hence, any convex combination of \(l\) and \(r\) (which is an arbitrary mixed strategy of the minimizer) is a min-max strategy. The only max-min strategy is \(T\) , as the unique best response to \(l\) . The game has value 1 . This is also the case when omitting the weakly dominated strategies \(B\) and \(r\) from the game. However, \(r\) is thereby lost as a possible min-max strategy. Consequently, if we are only interested in the value of a zero-sum game (which is often the case), then we can remove weakly dominated strategies (and iterate this), but the set of optimal strategies may become smaller that way.

\begin{tcolorbox}
下面这个博弈，其纯最优反应显示在右侧，有两个纯均衡 (T, l) 和 (T, r)。因此， \(l\) 和 \(r\) 的任何凸组合(这是最小值点的任意混合策略)都是极小极大策略。唯一的极大极小策略是 \(T\) ，因为它是对 \(l\) 的唯一最优反应。该博弈的值为1。当从博弈中剔除弱被占优策略 \(B\) 和 \(r\) 时，情况也是如此。然而， \(r\) 因此失去了作为可能的极小极大策略的资格。因此，如果我们只对零和博弈的值感兴趣(通常是这种情况)，那么我们可以移除弱被占优策略(并重复此操作)，但最优策略集可能会因此变小。
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\min {}_{1}\)  \(r\) Max

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\min {}_{1}\)  \(r\) 最大值
\end{tcolorbox}

T1 1

B0 1

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.2\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_230_872_1871_379_312_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

\section*{8.6 Further Reading}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{8.6 进一步阅读}
\end{tcolorbox}

The history of the minimax theorem is discussed in von Neumann and Fréchet (1953). In earlier articles in the same issue of Econometrica, Fréchet explains how the mathematician Emile Borel first suggested the use of active randomizations (that is, mixed strategies) in games, and posed this theorem (for symmetric zero-sum games) as an open problem. The theorem was first proved by von Neumann (1928), without knowledge of Borel's papers and question. Its proof was later substantially simplified.

\begin{tcolorbox}
极小极大定理的历史在冯·诺伊曼和弗雷歇(1953)的文献中有所讨论。在《计量经济学》同一期的早期文章中，弗雷歇解释了数学家埃米尔·博雷尔如何首先提出在博弈中使用主动随机化(即混合策略)，并将该定理(针对对称零和博弈)作为一个开放问题提出。该定理最早由冯·诺伊曼(1928)证明，当时他并不知道博雷尔的论文和问题。后来该定理的证明得到了大幅简化。
\end{tcolorbox}

The short inductive proof of Theorem 8.5 is due to Loomis (1946) (for a more general statement, which we have specialized to zero-sum games). It is very close to the proof by Owen (1967) who removes only one strict inequality to apply the induction hypothesis, not all such inequalities like Loomis (1946).

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理8.5的简短归纳证明归功于卢米斯(1946)(针对一个更一般的陈述，我们将其专门应用于零和博弈)。它与欧文(1967)的证明非常接近，欧文只消除一个严格不等式来应用归纳假设，而不像卢米斯(1946)那样消除所有此类不等式。
\end{tcolorbox}

A geometric proof of the minimax theorem, which is also used in the book of von Neumann and Morgenstern (1947), is based on the theorem of the separating hyperplane (see Corollary 7.16 and Figure 7.13). This theorem applies to the following situation, where it is known as Farkas’s Lemma: Let \(A \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d \times  n}\) and \(x \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) and assume that \(x \notin  D = \left\{  {{Ay} \mid  y \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n},y \geq  \mathbf{0}}\right\}\) . The lemma states that then there is some \(v \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) so that \({v}^{\top }A \geq  {\mathbf{0}}^{\top }\) and \({v}^{\top }x < 0\) . The set \(D\) is the "convex cone" of nonnegative linear combinations of the columns of \(A\) , and if the vector \(x\) is not in that cone then there is a "separating hyperplane" with normal vector \(v\) so that all columns of \(A\) are on one side of the hyperplane \(\left( {{v}^{\top }A \geq  {\mathbf{0}}^{\top }}\right)\) and \(x\) is strictly on the other side \(\left( {{v}^{\top }x < 0}\right)\) . This is a consequence of Corollary 7.16 if one proves (which is the only missing piece) that \(D\) is closed. Because \(D\) is closed, one can choose \(c\) as the closest point to \(x\) in \(D\) and let \(v = c - x\) (which has opposite sign to \(v\) as described following Corollary 7.16). Then \(D \subseteq  \left\{  {z \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d} \mid  {v}^{\top }z \geq  \alpha }\right\}\) where \(\alpha  = {v}^{\top }c =  - {\left( x - c\right) }^{\top }c = 0\) because, if not already \(c = \mathbf{0}\) , then similarly to Lemma 7.17 one can see that \(x - c\) is orthogonal to the ray \(\{ {c\beta } \mid  \beta  \geq  0\}\) which is a subset of \(D\) ; this also implies \({v}^{\top }\left( {x - c}\right)  =  - \parallel v{\parallel }^{2} < 0\) and thus \({v}^{\top }x < {v}^{\top }c = 0\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
极小极大定理(minimax theorem)的一个几何证明(冯·诺伊曼和摩根斯坦在1947年的著作中也使用了该证明)基于分离超平面(separating hyperplane)定理(见推论7.16和图7.13)。该定理适用于以下情况，在此情况下它被称为法卡斯定理(Farkas’s Lemma):设 \(A \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d \times  n}\) 且 \(x \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) ，并假设 \(x \notin  D = \left\{  {{Ay} \mid  y \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n},y \geq  \mathbf{0}}\right\}\) 。该引理表明，存在某个 \(v \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d}\) 使得 \({v}^{\top }A \geq  {\mathbf{0}}^{\top }\) 且 \({v}^{\top }x < 0\) 。集合 \(D\) 是矩阵 \(A\) 各列的非负线性组合构成的“凸锥”，如果向量 \(x\) 不在该锥内，那么存在一个以 \(v\) 为法向量(normal vector)的“分离超平面”，使得矩阵 \(A\) 的所有列都在超平面 \(\left( {{v}^{\top }A \geq  {\mathbf{0}}^{\top }}\right)\) 的一侧，而向量 \(x\) 严格位于另一侧 \(\left( {{v}^{\top }x < 0}\right)\) 。如果能证明(这是唯一缺失的部分) \(D\) 是闭集，那么这就是推论7.16的一个结果。因为 \(D\) 是闭集，所以可以选择 \(c\) 作为 \(D\) 中距离 \(x\) 最近的点，并令 \(v = c - x\) (根据推论7.16后的描述，它与 \(v\) 符号相反)。那么 \(D \subseteq  \left\{  {z \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{d} \mid  {v}^{\top }z \geq  \alpha }\right\}\) ，其中 \(\alpha  = {v}^{\top }c =  - {\left( x - c\right) }^{\top }c = 0\) ，因为如果还不满足 \(c = \mathbf{0}\) ，那么类似于引理7.17可以看出， \(x - c\) 与射线 \(\{ {c\beta } \mid  \beta  \geq  0\}\) 正交(orthogonal)，而该射线是 \(D\) 的一个子集；这也意味着 \({v}^{\top }\left( {x - c}\right)  =  - \parallel v{\parallel }^{2} < 0\) ，从而 \({v}^{\top }x < {v}^{\top }c = 0\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The strong duality theorem of linear programming, and therefore the minimax theorem, can be proved with some further algebraic manipulation from Farkas's Lemma (see Gale, 1960, p.78f). A classic text on linear programming is Dantzig (1963). Accessible introductions are Chvátal (1983) and, in particular for the geometric aspects, Matoušek and Gärtner (2007). A scholarly book with superb historical references is Schrijver (1986). The minimax theorem is easy to obtain from linear programming duality; the converse direction has been studied by Adler (2013).

\begin{tcolorbox}
线性规划(linear programming)的强对偶定理，进而极小极大定理，可以通过对法卡斯定理进行一些进一步的代数运算来证明(见Gale，1960，第78页及以后)。关于线性规划的经典著作是Dantzig(1963)。通俗易懂的介绍有Chvátal(1983)，特别是从几何角度介绍的有Matoušek和Gärtner(2007)。一本具有出色历史参考文献的学术著作是Schrijver(1986)。从线性规划对偶(linear programming duality)很容易得到极小极大定理；相反的方向已由Adler(2013)进行了研究。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{8.7 Exercises for Chapter 8}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{8.7 第8章习题}
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 8.1. Consider the following Penalty game. It gives the probability of scoring a goal when the row player (the striker) adopts one of the strategies \(L\) (shoot left), \(R\) (shoot right), and the column player (the goalkeeper) uses the strategies \(l\) (jump left), \(w\) (wait then jump), \(r\) (jump right). The row player is interested in maximizing and the column player in minimizing the probability of scoring a goal.

\begin{tcolorbox}
习题8.1. 考虑以下点球博弈。当行玩家(射手)采用策略 \(L\) (向左射门)、 \(R\) (向右射门)之一，而列玩家(守门员)采用策略 \(l\) (向左扑)、 \(w\) (等待后再扑)、 \(r\) (向右扑)时，该博弈给出了进球的概率。行玩家希望最大化进球概率，而列玩家希望最小化进球概率。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{tcolorbox}

\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Now suppose that player I has an additional strategy \(M\) (shoot down the middle), so that the payoff matrix is

Find an equilibrium of this game, and the equilibrium payoff. [Hint: The result from (a) will be useful.] Exercise 8.2.

\begin{tcolorbox}
找出该博弈的一个均衡以及均衡收益。[提示:(a) 部分的结果会有用。] 练习 8.2。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Consider a \(2 \times  n\) zero-sum game where the best response to every pure strategy is unique. Suppose that the top row and leftmost column define a pure-strategy equilibrium of the game. Show that this equilibrium can be found by iterated elimination of strictly dominated strategies. [Hint: Consider first the case \(n = 2\) .]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 考虑一个零和博弈，其中每个纯策略的最优反应是唯一的。假设最上面一行和最左边一列确定了该博弈的一个纯策略均衡。证明这个均衡可以通过迭代消除严格被占优策略来找到。[提示:首先考虑 \(n = 2\) 的情况。]
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Does the claim in (a) still hold if some pure strategy has more than one best response (and the game is therefore degenerate)?

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 如果某些纯策略有不止一个最优反应(因此该博弈是退化的)，(a) 中的论断是否仍然成立？
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) Give an example of a \(3 \times  3\) zero-sum game, where the best response to every pure strategy is unique, which has a pure-strategy equilibrium that cannot be found by iterated elimination of strictly dominated strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) 给出一个零和博弈的例子，其中每个纯策略的最优反应是唯一的，但存在一个纯策略均衡无法通过迭代消除严格被占优策略来找到。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 8.3. Consider the following simultaneous game between two players: Each player chooses an integer between 1 and 5 (e.g., some number of fingers shown with your hand), and the higher number wins, except when it is just one larger than the lower number, in which case the lower number wins. So 4 beats 2 and 5 but it loses against 3. Equal numbers are a draw.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 8.3。考虑两个玩家之间的以下同时博弈:每个玩家选择一个介于 1 到 5 之间的整数(例如，用手展示的手指数量)，较大的数字获胜，除非它只比较小的数字大 1，在这种情况下较小的数字获胜。所以 4 胜过 2 和 5，但输给 3。数字相等则平局。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Write down the matrix of payoffs to the row player of this zero-sum game. A winning player gets payoff 1, a draw gives payoff 0 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 写出这个零和博弈中行玩家的收益矩阵。获胜的玩家获得收益 1，平局获得收益 0。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Show all pairs of strategies of the row player where one strategy weakly or strictly dominates the other, and indicate the type of domination.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 找出行玩家的所有策略对，其中一个策略弱占优或严格占优另一个策略，并指出占优类型。
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) Argue carefully, without calculations, why the value of this game has to be zero.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) 不进行计算，仔细论证为什么这个博弈的值必须为零。
\end{tcolorbox}

(d) Find an equilibrium of this game in mixed (including pure) strategies, and explain why it is an equilibrium.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(d) 找出这个博弈在混合(包括纯)策略下的一个均衡，并解释为什么它是一个均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 8.4. Consider the following bimatrix game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习 8.4。考虑以下双矩阵博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.3\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_233_719_1303_486_357_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

(a) Find an equilibrium of this game in mixed (including pure) strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 找出这个博弈在混合(包括纯)策略下的一个均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Find a max-min strategy of player I, which may be a mixed strategy, and the resulting max-min payoff to player I. [Hint: Consider the game as a zero-sum game with payoffs to player I.]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 找出玩家 I 的一个极大极小策略(可能是混合策略)，以及玩家 I 由此获得的极大极小收益。[提示:将该博弈视为一个给玩家 I 带来收益的零和博弈。]
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) Find a max-min strategy of player II, which may be a mixed strategy, and the resulting max-min payoff to player II. [Hint: Consider the game as a zero-sum game with payoffs to player II; note the reversed roles of maximizer and minimizer.]

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) 找出玩家 II 的一个极大极小策略(可能是混合策略)，以及玩家 II 由此获得的极大极小收益。[提示:将该博弈视为一个给玩家 II 带来收益的零和博弈；注意最大值点和最小值点的角色相反。]
\end{tcolorbox}

(d) Compare the max-min payoffs to player I and II from (b) and (c) with their payoffs in any equilibrium from (a).

\begin{tcolorbox}
(d) 将 (b) 和 (c) 中玩家 I 和玩家 II 的极大极小收益与 (a) 中任何均衡下他们的收益进行比较。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Geometry of Equilibria in Bimatrix Games}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{双矩阵博弈中均衡的几何性质}
\end{tcolorbox}

This chapter is about the geometric structure of equilibria in two-player games in strategic form. It shows how to quickly identify equilibria with qualitative "best-response diagrams". These diagrams (for example, Figure 9.4) are subdivisions of the two mixed-strategy simplices into labeled "best-response regions", and suitable labels of the outsides of each simplex, so that an equilibrium is a "completely labeled" pair(x, y)of points. The power of this method is that it easily allows studying \(3 \times  3\) games, which have a lot more variety than the rather restricted \(2 \times  2\) games and their intersecting best-response lines as in Figure 6.8.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本章讨论策略形式的两人博弈中均衡的几何结构。它展示了如何用定性的“最优反应示意图”快速识别均衡。这些示意图(例如，图 9.4)将两个混合策略单纯形细分为有标签的“最优反应区域”，并对每个单纯形的外部进行合适的标记，这样一个均衡就是一个“完全标记”的点对 (x, y)。这种方法的强大之处在于它可以轻松研究零和博弈，与图 6.8 中相当受限的矩阵博弈及其相交的最优反应线相比，零和博弈有更多的变化。
\end{tcolorbox}

Best-response diagrams have been introduced by Shapley (1974) to illustrate the algorithm of Lemke and Howson (1964) that finds one equilibrium of a bimatrix game. Moreover, the algorithm shows that every nondegenerate bimatrix game has an odd number of equilibria. In this chapter, we explain the best-response regions in Section 9.2 and the principle behind the Lemke-Howson algorithm in Section 9.3. Even a reader who is not particularly interested in the computation of equilibria should look at these two sections, which are one of the things in this book that every game theorist should know.

\begin{tcolorbox}
沙普利(1974 年)引入了最优反应示意图，以阐释莱姆基和豪森(1964 年)提出的算法，该算法可找出双矩阵博弈的一个均衡。此外，该算法表明，每个非退化双矩阵博弈都有奇数个均衡。在本章中，我们将在 9.2 节解释最优反应区域，并在 9.3 节阐述莱姆基 - 豪森算法背后的原理。即使是对均衡计算不太感兴趣的读者，也应该阅读这两节内容，这是本书中每个博弈论学者都应该了解的内容之一。
\end{tcolorbox}

The remainder of this chapter gives a more detailed introduction to the computation of equilibria in two-player games based on this geometric approach. In the introductory \(3 \times  2\) example (9.1), the mixed strategy simplex \(X\) of player \(\mathrm{I}\) is a triangle, and the mixed strategy simplex \(Y\) of player II is a line segment, where one would normally apply the upper envelope method (see Section 6.7). Section 9.4 explains the best-response diagrams for \(3 \times  3\) games, where they are subdivided triangles. Section 9.5 explains strategic equivalence of games, which leaves best responses and hence the diagrams unchanged, which is generally useful.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本章的其余部分将基于这种几何方法，更详细地介绍两人博弈中均衡的计算。在介绍性的 \(3 \times  2\) 示例(9.1)中，玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 的混合策略单纯形 \(X\) 是一个三角形，而玩家 II 的混合策略单纯形 \(Y\) 是一条线段，通常会应用上包络法(见 6.7 节)。9.4 节解释了 \(3 \times  3\) 博弈的最优反应示意图，它们是细分的三角形。9.5 节解释了博弈的策略等价性，它不会改变最优反应，因此也不会改变示意图，这通常很有用。
\end{tcolorbox}

While the partition of \(X\) and \(Y\) into best-response regions is convenient to draw in low dimension, it is in general dimension better to keep the payoff to the other player as an additional real variable, as described in Section 9.6. The resulting "best-response" polyhedra represent the upper envelope of expected payoffs in general dimension. The mathematically simpler, equivalent "best-response polytopes" are the basis for an algebraic implementation of the Lemke-Howson algorithm known as "complementary pivoting", described in Section 9.7. In the last Section 9.8, we describe how to extend complementary pivoting to degenerate games. This completes an alternative proof of the existence of an equilibrium in a two-player game that is independent of Brouwer's fixed-point theorem.

\begin{tcolorbox}
虽然将 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) 划分为最优反应区域在低维度下便于绘制，但在一般维度下，如 9.6 节所述，将对另一个玩家的效用作为一个额外的实变量会更好。由此产生的“最优反应”多面体代表了一般维度下期望效用的上包络。数学上更简单、等价的“最优反应有界多面体”是莱姆基 - 豪森算法的代数实现(称为“互补换基法”)的基础，这在 9.7 节中有所描述。在最后一节 9.8 中，我们描述了如何将互补换基法扩展到退化博弈。这完成了一个独立于布劳威尔不动点定理的两人博弈中均衡存在性的替代证明。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{9.1 Prerequisites and Learning Outcomes}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{9.1 先决条件和学习成果}
\end{tcolorbox}

This chapter is a continuation of Chapter 6, and assumes the knowledge of the upper-envelope (also known as "goalpost") method and of degenerate games. All geometric definitions will be recalled here. For example, the concept of a simplex has been defined in Section 7.3, but we will only consider the mixed-strategy simplices as in Figure 6.5. Undirected graphs have been defined as background material early in Chapter 7.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本章是第 6 章的延续，假设读者已经了解上包络法(也称为“门柱”法)和退化博弈。这里将回顾所有几何定义。例如，单纯形的概念已在 7.3 节中定义，但我们仅考虑如图 6.5 所示的混合策略单纯形。无向图在第 7 章开头作为背景材料进行了定义。
\end{tcolorbox}

After studying this chapter, you should be able to:

\begin{tcolorbox}
学习本章后，你应该能够:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item draw best-response diagrams for bimatrix games with up to three strategies per player;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 为每个玩家最多有三种策略的双矩阵博弈绘制最优反应示意图；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item identify the equilibria of the game with the help of the labels used in these diagrams, and explain the connection of labels and the best-response condition;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 借助这些示意图中使用的标签识别博弈的均衡，并解释标签与最优反应条件之间的联系；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item show the computation of one equilibrium with the Lemke-Howson algorithm using the diagram;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 使用示意图展示莱姆基 - 豪森算法计算一个均衡的过程；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item explain strategic equivalence, for two- and three-player games;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 解释两人和三人博弈的策略等价性；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item see the connection between best-response polyhedra, the upper envelope of expected payoffs, and the best response-regions;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 理解最优反应多面体、期望效用的上包络和最优反应区域之间的联系；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item be able to perform pivoting on small dictionaries with the complementary pivoting rule.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 能够根据互补换基规则对小型字典进行换基操作。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{9.2 Labeled Best-Response Regions}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{9.2 带标签的最优反应区域}
\end{tcolorbox}

Our leading example will be the \(3 \times  2\) bimatrix game(A, B)with

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们的主要示例将是 \(3 \times  2\) 双矩阵博弈 (A, B)，其中
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
A = \left( \begin{array}{ll} 2 & 0 \\  1 & 2 \\  0 & 3 \end{array}\right) ,\;B = \left( \begin{array}{ll} 2 & 0 \\  0 & 1 \\  4 & 1 \end{array}\right) . \tag{9.1}
\]

which is very similar to the right game Figure 6.7 and shown in Figure 9.1.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这与图 6.7 右侧的博弈非常相似，如图 9.1 所示。
\end{tcolorbox}

For an \(m \times  n\) bimatrix game, we number the pure strategies of the two players consecutively,

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于 \(m \times  n\) 双矩阵博弈，我们依次对两个玩家的纯策略进行编号，
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_236_248_211_1197_677_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 9.1 Left: The game in (9.1) with pure strategies numbered as in (9.2). Right: The upper envelope of expected payoffs to player I for a mixed strategy \(y \in  Y\) of player II. The mixed strategy set \(Y\) (bottom right) is subdivided into best-response regions, which are labeled with player I's pure best responses.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图9.1 左:式(9.1)中的博弈，纯策略编号如式(9.2)所示。右:对于玩家II的混合策略 \(y \in  Y\) ，玩家I的期望效用上包络。混合策略集 \(Y\) (右下角)被细分为最优反应区域，这些区域用玩家I的纯最优反应进行标记。
\end{tcolorbox}

pure strategies of player \(\mathrm{I}\) (rows) \(\; : \;1,\ldots ,m\) ,

\begin{tcolorbox}
玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 的纯策略(行) \(\; : \;1,\ldots ,m\) ,
\end{tcolorbox}

(9.2)pure strategies of player II (columns) : \(m + 1,\ldots ,m + n\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
玩家II的纯策略(列): \(m + 1,\ldots ,m + n\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

In drawings we typically show them as circled numbers, here (1, 2), 3) for player I and ④, ⑤ for player II. In the text, we usually omit the circles.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在绘图中，我们通常将它们表示为带圆圈的数字，这里玩家I是(1, 2, 3)，玩家II是④, ⑤。在文本中，我们通常省略圆圈。
\end{tcolorbox}

The mixed strategy sets \(X\) and \(Y\) of the two players are defined in (6.6). Using (9.2), we subdivide them into best-response regions of the other player according to

\begin{tcolorbox}
两个玩家的混合策略集 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) 在式(6.6)中定义。利用式(9.2)，我们根据以下规则将它们细分为对方玩家的最优反应区域
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
Y\left( i\right)  = \{ y \in  Y \mid  i\text{ is a best response to }y\} \;\left( {1 \leq  i \leq  m}\right) , \tag{9.3}
\]

\[
X\left( j\right)  = \{ x \in  X \mid  j\text{ is a best response to }x\} \;\left( {m + 1 \leq  j \leq  m + n}\right) .
\]

The best-response regions are not disjoint because a mixed strategy may have more than pure best response, but this typically happens only at the "boundary" of a best-response region. Every mixed strategy has at least one best response, so the union of the best-response regions is the respective set of mixed strategies. In Figure 9.1, the subdivision of \(Y\) can be seen from the upper envelope diagram:

\begin{tcolorbox}
最优反应区域并非不相交，因为一个混合策略可能有多个纯最优反应，但这种情况通常只发生在最优反应区域的“边界”上。每个混合策略至少有一个最优反应，因此最优反应区域的并集就是相应的混合策略集。在图9.1中， \(Y\) 的细分可以从期望效用上包络图中看出:
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
Y\left( 1\right)  = \left\{  {\left( {{y}_{1},{y}_{2}}\right)  \in  Y \mid  0 \leq  {y}_{2} \leq  \frac{1}{3}}\right\}  ,
\]

\[
Y\left( 2\right)  = \left\{  {\left( {{y}_{1},{y}_{2}}\right)  \in  Y \mid  \frac{1}{3} \leq  {y}_{2} \leq  \frac{1}{2}}\right\}  ,
\]

\[
Y\left( 3\right)  = \left\{  {\left( {{y}_{1},{y}_{2}}\right)  \in  Y \mid  \frac{1}{2} \leq  {y}_{2} \leq  1}\right\}  \text{ . }
\]

Figure 9.2 demonstrates the subdivision of \(X\) into best-response regions. The strategy simplex \(X\) is a triangle with the unit vectors \({e}_{1},{e}_{2}\) , and \({e}_{3}\) as vertices, which are the pure strategies of player I. A "goalpost" is erected on each vertex, shown in the perspective drawings in Figure 9.2. In the top-left diagram, the heights2,0, and 4 are marked on these posts, which are the payoffs to player II for her left column ④. The resulting expected payoffs define a plane above the triangle through these height markings, which defines the expected payoff to player II for this column as a function of \(x = \left( {{x}_{1},{x}_{2},{x}_{3}}\right)\) , because the expected payoff is just the convex combination of the corresponding "heights", located above the point \(x\) in the triangle \(X\) below. Similarly, the top-right diagram shows for each goalpost the expected payoffs 0, 1, and 1 for the right column (5) of player II, which span the plane for the expected payoff. The bottom-left diagram shows the upper envelope as the maximum of the two expected-payoff planes.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图9.2展示了将 \(X\) 细分为最优反应区域的情况。策略单纯形 \(X\) 是一个以单位向量 \({e}_{1},{e}_{2}\) 和 \({e}_{3}\) 为顶点的三角形，这些顶点是玩家I的纯策略。在每个顶点上竖起一个“球门柱”，如图9.2的透视图所示。在左上图中，在这些柱子上标记了高度2、0和4，这是玩家II选择左列④时的效用。由此产生的期望效用定义了一个位于三角形上方且穿过这些高度标记的平面，该平面将玩家II选择此列的期望效用定义为 \(x = \left( {{x}_{1},{x}_{2},{x}_{3}}\right)\) 的函数，因为期望效用只是相应“高度”的凸组合，这些“高度”位于下方三角形 \(X\) 中 \(x\) 点的上方。类似地，右上图展示了玩家II右列(5)的每个球门柱的期望效用0、1和1，这些期望效用构成了期望效用平面。左下图展示了期望效用上包络，即两个期望效用平面的最大值。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_237_333_195_1258_1214_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 9.2 Expected payoffs to player II as a function of the mixed strategy of player I for the \(3 \times  2\) game in Figure 9.2. Top left: plane of expected payoffs for the left column ④, with payoffs2,0,4against the pure strategies \({e}_{1},{e}_{2},{e}_{3}\) . Top right: plane of expected payoffs for the right column (5), with payoffs0,1,1. Bottom left: upper envelope (maximum) of expected payoffs. Bottom right: Resulting subdivision of \(X\) into best-response regions \(X\left( 4\right)\) and \(X\left( 5\right)\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图9.2 对于图9.2中的 \(3 \times  2\) 博弈，玩家II的期望效用作为玩家I混合策略的函数。左上方:左列④的期望效用平面，针对纯策略 \({e}_{1},{e}_{2},{e}_{3}\) 的效用为2、0、4。右上方:右列(5)的期望效用平面，效用为0、1、1。左下方:期望效用的上包络(最大值)。右下方:将 \(X\) 细分为最优反应区域 \(X\left( 4\right)\) 和 \(X\left( 5\right)\) 的结果。
\end{tcolorbox}

Finally, the bottom-right diagram in Figure 9.2 shows the "projection" of the upper envelope diagram onto \(X\) , by ignoring the vertical coordinate, to represent the subdivision of \(X\) into the best-response regions \(X\left( 4\right)\) and \(X\left( 5\right)\) . Against \(\left( {{x}_{1},{x}_{2},{x}_{3}}\right)\) , the expected payoffs for the left and right column are \(2{x}_{1} + 4{x}_{3}\) and \({x}_{2} + {x}_{3}\) , respectively, so the equation

\begin{tcolorbox}
最后，图9.2的右下图展示了期望效用上包络图在 \(X\) 上的“投影”，即忽略垂直坐标，以表示将 \(X\) 细分为最优反应区域 \(X\left( 4\right)\) 和 \(X\left( 5\right)\) 的情况。针对 \(\left( {{x}_{1},{x}_{2},{x}_{3}}\right)\) ，左列和右列的期望效用分别为 \(2{x}_{1} + 4{x}_{3}\) 和 \({x}_{2} + {x}_{3}\) ，因此方程
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
2{x}_{1} + 4{x}_{3} = {x}_{2} + {x}_{3}\;\text{ or equivalently }\;2{x}_{1} + 3{x}_{3} = {x}_{2} \tag{9.4}
\]

shows where both columns have equal payoff. This defines a line segment in the triangle that belongs to both \(X\left( 4\right)\) and \(X\left( 5\right)\) . The endpoints \(a\) and \(b\) of this line segment, also shown in the diagram, are obtained by setting in equation (9.4) either \({x}_{3} = 0\) , which gives \(a = \left( {\frac{1}{3},\frac{2}{3},0}\right)\) , or \({x}_{1} = 0\) , which gives \(b = \left( {0,\frac{3}{4},\frac{1}{4}}\right)\) . The equation holds also for all convex combinations of \(a\) and \(b\) , which define the line segment itself.

\begin{tcolorbox}
显示了两列具有相等收益的位置。这在三角形中定义了一条线段，该线段同时属于 \(X\left( 4\right)\) 和 \(X\left( 5\right)\) 。此线段的端点 \(a\) 和 \(b\) (图中也有显示)是通过在方程 (9.4) 中令 \({x}_{3} = 0\) (得到 \(a = \left( {\frac{1}{3},\frac{2}{3},0}\right)\) )或 \({x}_{1} = 0\) (得到 \(b = \left( {0,\frac{3}{4},\frac{1}{4}}\right)\) )得到的。该方程对于 \(a\) 和 \(b\) 的所有凸组合也成立，这些凸组合定义了该线段本身。
\end{tcolorbox}

In Figures 9.1 and 9.2, the best-response regions \(Y\left( i\right)\) and \(X\left( j\right)\) in (9.3) are labeled with the respective pure strategies \(i\) of player I and \(j\) of player II. We now introduce a second set of labels for unplayed own strategies via

\begin{tcolorbox}
在图 9.1 和 9.2 中，(9.3) 中的最优反应区域 \(Y\left( i\right)\) 和 \(X\left( j\right)\) 分别用玩家 I 的纯策略 \(i\) 和玩家 II 的纯策略 \(j\) 进行了标注。我们现在通过以下方式为未采用的自身策略引入第二组标签
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
X\left( i\right)  = \left\{  {\left( {{x}_{1},\ldots ,{x}_{m}}\right)  \in  X \mid  \;{x}_{i} = 0}\right\}  \;\left( {1 \leq  i \leq  m}\right) , \tag{9.5}
\]

\[
Y\left( j\right)  = \left\{  {\left( {{y}_{1},\ldots ,{y}_{n}}\right)  \in  Y \mid  {y}_{j - m} = 0}\right\}  \;\left( {m + 1 \leq  j \leq  m + n}\right) .
\]

We refer to \({y}_{j - m}\) because \(j\) denotes a pure strategy in \(\{ m + 1,\ldots ,m + n\}\) of player II, but the components of \(y\) still have subscripts \(1,\ldots ,n\) . Similarly, the pure strategies of player II, which are the vertices of \(Y\) , are the unit vectors \({e}_{1},\ldots ,{e}_{n}\) in \({\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们提及 \({y}_{j - m}\) 是因为 \(j\) 表示玩家 II 在 \(\{ m + 1,\ldots ,m + n\}\) 中的一个纯策略，但 \(y\) 的分量仍有下标 \(1,\ldots ,n\) 。类似地，玩家 II 的纯策略(即 \(Y\) 的顶点)是 \({\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) 中的单位向量 \({e}_{1},\ldots ,{e}_{n}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 9.1. Consider an \(m \times  n\) bimatrix game(A, B)with mixed strategy sets \(X\) and \(Y\) in (6.6). Let \(k \in  \{ 1,\ldots ,m + n\}\) be any pure strategy of player I or II with \(X\left( k\right)\) and \(Y\left( k\right)\) defined as in (9.3) and (9.5). Then \(x \in  X\) is said to have label \(k\) if \(x \in  X\left( k\right)\) , and \(y \in  Y\) is said to have label \(k\) if \(y \in  Y\left( k\right)\) . If every \(k \in  \{ 1,\ldots ,m + n\}\) is a label of \(x\) or \(y\) (or both), then(x, y)is called \({completelylabeled.\ The\ sets}X\) and \(Y\) annotated with these labels define the best-response diagram for the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义 9.1。考虑一个 \(m \times  n\) 双矩阵博弈 (A, B)，其混合策略集为 (6.6) 中的 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) 。设 \(k \in  \{ 1,\ldots ,m + n\}\) 是玩家 I 或玩家 II 的任意纯策略， \(X\left( k\right)\) 和 \(Y\left( k\right)\) 如 (9.3) 和 (9.5) 所定义。那么，如果 \(x \in  X\left( k\right)\) ，则称 \(x \in  X\) 具有标签 \(k\) ；如果 \(y \in  Y\left( k\right)\) ，则称 \(y \in  Y\) 具有标签 \(k\) 。如果每个 \(k \in  \{ 1,\ldots ,m + n\}\) 都是 \(x\) 或 \(y\) (或两者)的标签，那么 (x, y) 称为 \({completelylabeled.\ The\ sets}X\) ，用这些标签标注的 \(Y\) 定义了该博弈的最优反应示意图。
\end{tcolorbox}

For the game (9.1), the labels for the unplayed own strategies as defined in (9.5) are shown in Figure 9.3. For example, \(X\left( 1\right)\) is the side of the triangle given by the points \(\left( {{x}_{1},{x}_{2},{x}_{3}}\right)\) in \(X\) where \({x}_{1} = 0\) , which are all the convex combinations of the unit vectors \({e}_{2} = \left( {0,1,0}\right)\) and \({e}_{3} = \left( {0,0,1}\right)\) . In general, \(X\left( i\right)\) is the facet of the triangle that is opposite to the vertex given by the unit vector \({e}_{i}\) , for \(1 \leq  i \leq  m\) . The mixed strategy set \(Y\) of player II is a line segment with endpoints \({e}_{1} = \left( {1,0}\right)\) (which is the left column ④ of the game) and \({e}_{2} = \left( {0,1}\right)\) (which is the right column ⑤). Each"side" or facet \(Y\left( 4\right)\) or \(Y\left( 5\right)\) of this low-dimensional simplex \(Y\) is just one of these endpoints, where \({e}_{1}\) has label 5 because player II’s second strategy 5 is played with probability zero (and this is the only element of \(Y\) with that property), and the other endpoint \({e}_{2}\) has label 4 because player II’s first strategy 4 has probability zero.

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于博弈 (9.1)，如 (9.5) 中所定义的未采用的自身策略的标签如图 9.3 所示。例如， \(X\left( 1\right)\) 是由 \(X\) 中 \(\left( {{x}_{1},{x}_{2},{x}_{3}}\right)\) 这些点所构成的三角形的边，其中 \({x}_{1} = 0\) ，这些点都是单位向量 \({e}_{2} = \left( {0,1,0}\right)\) 和 \({e}_{3} = \left( {0,0,1}\right)\) 的所有凸组合。一般来说，对于 \(1 \leq  i \leq  m\) ， \(X\left( i\right)\) 是三角形中与单位向量 \({e}_{i}\) 所对应的顶点相对的极面。玩家 II 的混合策略集 \(Y\) 是一条线段，其端点为 \({e}_{1} = \left( {1,0}\right)\) (即博弈的左列 ④)和 \({e}_{2} = \left( {0,1}\right)\) (即博弈的右列 ⑤)。这个低维单纯形 \(Y\) 的每个“边”或极面 \(Y\left( 4\right)\) 或 \(Y\left( 5\right)\) 就是这些端点之一，其中 \({e}_{1}\) 的标签为 5，因为玩家 II 的第二个策略 5 被采用的概率为零(并且这是 \(Y\) 中具有该性质的唯一元素)，而另一个端点 \({e}_{2}\) 的标签为 4，因为玩家 II 的第一个策略 4 被采用的概率为零。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.3\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_239_346_382_495_330_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_239_1013_441_571_128_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 9.3 The mixed strategy simplices \(X\) and \(Y\) for the game in Figure 9.1 with best-response regions labeled with pure strategies of the other player, and sides labeled with unplayed own strategies. This defines the best-response diagram of the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 9.3 展示了图 9.1 中博弈的混合策略单纯形 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) ，其中最优反应区域用另一个玩家的纯策略进行标记，边则用未采用的自身策略进行标记。这就定义了该博弈的最优反应示意图。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_239_350_951_1237_306_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 9.4 The same picture as Figure 9.3 with all annotations omitted except for the labels, which fully define the best-response diagram. The equilibria as completely labeled vertex pairs are shown with symbols: a white circle for the pure equilibrium \(\left( {{e}_{1},{e}_{1}}\right)\) , a black square for(a, c), and a white square for(b, d).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 9.4 与图 9.3 相同，只是省略了除标签之外的所有注释，这些标签完全定义了最优反应示意图。作为完全标记的顶点对的均衡用符号表示:白色圆圈表示纯策略均衡 \(\left( {{e}_{1},{e}_{1}}\right)\) ，黑色方块表示 (a, c)，白色方块表示 (b, d)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Any mixed strategy \(x\) or \(y\) may have several labels according to Definition 9.1. The inclusion of the unplayed own strategies among these labels is needed for the following, very useful theorem.

\begin{tcolorbox}
根据定义 9.1，任何混合策略 \(x\) 或 \(y\) 可能有多个标签。在这些标签中包含未采用的自身策略对于以下非常有用的定理是必要的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 9.2. Under the assumptions of Definition 9.1, a mixed strategy pair(x, y)is an equilibrium of(A, B)if and only if(x, y)is completely labeled.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理 9.2。在定义 9.1 的假设下，混合策略对 (x, y) 是 (A, B) 的一个均衡，当且仅当 (x, y) 是完全标记的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. The theorem is essentially another way of stating the best-response condition Proposition 6.1. Let \(\left( {x,y}\right)  \in  X \times  Y\) , let \(u\) be the best-response payoff to player I against \(y\) , and let \(v\) be the best-response payoff to player II against \(x\) , as defined in (6.9). Suppose(x, y)is an equilibrium and therefore \(x\) a best response to \(y\) as in (6.11), and \(y\) a best response to \(x\) as in (6.12). These conditions can be rewritten as

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。该定理本质上是陈述最优反应条件(命题 6.1)的另一种方式。设 \(\left( {x,y}\right)  \in  X \times  Y\) ，设 \(u\) 是玩家 I 针对 \(y\) 的最优反应收益，设 \(v\) 是玩家 II 针对 \(x\) 的最优反应收益，如 (6.9) 中所定义。假设 (x, y) 是一个均衡，因此如 (6.11) 中所示 \(x\) 是对 \(y\) 的最优反应，并且如 (6.12) 中所示 \(y\) 是对 \(x\) 的最优反应。这些条件可以重写为
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{x}_{i} = 0\;\text{ or }\;{\left( Ay\right) }_{i} = u\;\left( {1 \leq  i \leq  m}\right) , \tag{9.6}
\]

\[
{y}_{j} = 0\;\text{ or }\;{\left( {x}^{\top }B\right) }_{j} = v\;\left( {1 \leq  j \leq  n}\right) ,
\]

where the first condition says that \(x\) has label \(i\) (because \(x \in  X\left( i\right)\) ) or \(y\) has label \(i\) (because \(i\) is a best response to \(y\) and therefore \(y \in  Y\left( i\right)\) ), and the second condition that \(y\) has label \(m + j\) or \(x\) has label \(m + j\) (because \(m + j\) is a best response to \(x\) , with the numbering of pure strategies in (9.2)). In other words, (9.6) states that every label \(i \in  \{ 1,\ldots ,m\}\) or \(m + j \in  \{ m + 1,\ldots ,m + n\}\) appears as a label of \(x\) or \(y\) , and(x, y)is therefore completely labeled.

\begin{tcolorbox}
其中第一个条件表明 \(x\) 有标签 \(i\) (因为 \(x \in  X\left( i\right)\) )或者 \(y\) 有标签 \(i\) (因为 \(i\) 是对 \(y\) 的最优反应，因此 \(y \in  Y\left( i\right)\) )，第二个条件表明 \(y\) 有标签 \(m + j\) 或者 \(x\) 有标签 \(m + j\) (因为根据 (9.2) 中纯策略的编号， \(m + j\) 是对 \(x\) 的最优反应)。换句话说，(9.6) 表明每个标签 \(i \in  \{ 1,\ldots ,m\}\) 或 \(m + j \in  \{ m + 1,\ldots ,m + n\}\) 都作为 \(x\) 或 \(y\) 的标签出现，因此 (x, y) 是完全标记的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Conversely, if(x, y)is completely labeled, then (9.6) holds and \(x\) and \(y\) are mutual best responses, so that(x, y)is an equilibrium, as claimed.

\begin{tcolorbox}
相反，如果 (x, y) 是完全标记的，那么 (9.6) 式成立，且 \(x\) 和 \(y\) 是相互的最优反应，因此 (x, y) 是一个均衡，正如所声称的那样。
\end{tcolorbox}

Another way of quickly seeing Theorem 9.2 is that a missing label from(x, y) would be a pure strategy of a player that is played with positive probability (because it is not an unplayed pure strategy as in (9.5)) and is also not a best response, which is precisely not allowed according to the best-response condition, and violates (9.6). The players' pure strategies are numbered separately as in (9.2) so that they can appear as labels of either \(x\) or \(y\) , and in order to distinguish the two sets of conditions in (9.6).

\begin{tcolorbox}
另一种快速理解定理 9.2 的方法是，(x, y) 中的一个缺失标签意味着某个参与者以正概率采用的一个纯策略(因为它不像 (9.5) 式中那样是一个未采用的纯策略)，并且它也不是一个最优反应，而根据最优反应条件，这恰恰是不允许的，并且违反了 (9.6) 式。参与者的纯策略像 (9.2) 式那样单独编号，这样它们就可以作为 \(x\) 或 \(y\) 的标签出现，并且是为了区分 (9.6) 式中的两组条件。
\end{tcolorbox}

As Figure 9.3 shows, only the points \({e}_{1},{e}_{2},{e}_{3},a,b\) in \(X\) have three labels (which is the maximum number of labels of a point in \(X\) ), and only the points \({e}_{1},{e}_{2},c,d\) in \(Y\) have two labels (which is the maximum number of labels of a point in \(Y\) ). Because an equilibrium(x, y)needs all five labels for the players’ pure strategies, one can quickly identify the equilibria of the game. These are:

\begin{tcolorbox}
如图 9.3 所示，只有 \(X\) 中的点 \({e}_{1},{e}_{2},{e}_{3},a,b\) 有三个标签(这是 \(X\) 中一个点的最大标签数)，只有 \(Y\) 中的点 \({e}_{1},{e}_{2},c,d\) 有两个标签(这是 \(Y\) 中一个点的最大标签数)。因为一个均衡 (x, y) 需要参与者纯策略的所有五个标签，所以可以快速识别出该博弈的均衡。这些均衡是:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item The pure equilibrium \(\left( {{e}_{1},{e}_{2}}\right)\) with labels 2,3,4 in \(X\) (which means 2 and 3 are not played and 4 is a best response) and 5,1 in \(Y\) (which means 5 is not played and 1 is a best response). This equilibrium is marked by white circles in Figure 9.4.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 纯策略均衡 \(\left( {{e}_{1},{e}_{2}}\right)\) ，在 \(X\) 中有标签 2、3、4(这意味着 2 和 3 未被采用，4 是最优反应)，在 \(Y\) 中有标签 5、1(这意味着 5 未被采用，1 是最优反应)。这个均衡在图 9.4 中用白色圆圈标记。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item The mixed equilibrium(a, c)with labels3,4,5for \(a = \left( {\frac{1}{3},\frac{2}{3},0}\right)\) in \(X\) (which means 3 is not played and 4 and 5 are best responses) and 1,2 for \(c = \left( {\frac{2}{3},\frac{1}{3}}\right)\) in \(Y\) (which means both 1 and 2 are best responses). This is marked by black squares in Figure 9.4.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 混合均衡 (a, c)，对于 \(X\) 中的 \(a = \left( {\frac{1}{3},\frac{2}{3},0}\right)\) 有标签 3、4、5(这意味着 3 未被采用，4 和 5 是最优反应)，对于 \(Y\) 中的 \(c = \left( {\frac{2}{3},\frac{1}{3}}\right)\) 有标签 1、2(这意味着 1 和 2 都是最优反应)。这在图 9.4 中用黑色方块标记。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item The mixed equilibrium(b, d)with labels1,4,5for \(b = \left( {0,\frac{3}{4},\frac{1}{4}}\right)\) in \(X\) (which means 1 is not played and 4 and 5 are best responses) and 2,3 for \(c = \left( {\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}}\right)\) in \(Y\) (which means both 2 and 3 are best responses). This is marked by white squares in Figure 9.4.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 混合均衡 (b, d)，对于 \(X\) 中的 \(b = \left( {0,\frac{3}{4},\frac{1}{4}}\right)\) 有标签 1、4、5(这意味着 1 未被采用，4 和 5 是最优反应)，对于 \(Y\) 中的 \(c = \left( {\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}}\right)\) 有标签 2、3(这意味着 2 和 3 都是最优反应)。这在图 9.4 中用白色方块标记。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

The quickest way to find these equilibria as completely labeled pairs of points is to check the points in \(Y\) that have two labels, where the three other labels required to complete the full set of labels are unique, and may or may not appear as labels of points in \(X\) . The three points \({e}_{1},c,d\) in \(Y\) are part of an equilibrium, but \({e}_{2}\) is not because it has labels 3 and 4 and the required three other labels1,2,5do not appear in \(X\) , for example because the sets \(X\left( 2\right)\) and \(X\left( 5\right)\) have no point in common. This suffices to find all equilibria. A similar, slightly longer method is to check \({e}_{2}\) in \(X\) with labels1,3,5where no point in \(Y\) has the other two labels 2 and 4, and \({e}_{3}\) in \(X\) with labels1,2,4where no point in \(Y\) has the other two labels 3 and 5 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
将这些均衡作为完全标记的点对来找到的最快方法是，检查 \(Y\) 中具有两个标签的点，其中完成完整标签集所需的另外三个标签是唯一的，并且可能会也可能不会作为 \(X\) 中点的标签出现。 \(Y\) 中的三个点 \({e}_{1},c,d\) 是一个均衡的一部分，但 \({e}_{2}\) 不是，因为它有标签 3 和 4，而所需的另外三个标签 1、2、5 没有出现在 \(X\) 中，例如因为集合 \(X\left( 2\right)\) 和 \(X\left( 5\right)\) 没有公共点。这足以找到所有的均衡。一种类似但稍长的方法是检查 \(X\) 中具有标签 1、3、5 的 \({e}_{2}\) ，其中 \(Y\) 中没有点具有另外两个标签 2 和 4，以及检查 \(X\) 中具有标签 1、2、4 的 \({e}_{3}\) ，其中 \(Y\) 中没有点具有另外两个标签 3 和 5。
\end{tcolorbox}

The advantage of the best-response diagram for the game is that it is a qualitative diagram that allows identifying the equilibria, in a much more informative way than the payoffs do. In Figure 9.4 we have only kept the labels as annotations, which nevertheless encode all the necessary information. In particular, \(X\) is the strategy set of player I because its sides are labeled with the pure strategies of player I, and the inside labels are the pure best responses of player II; the reverse holds for \(Y\) . The vertices of \(X\) are unit vectors that are identified by the incident labels for the unplayed pure strategies; for example, \({e}_{1} = \left( {1,0,0}\right)\) is the vertex with labels 2 and 3 . In \(Y\) this holds for \({e}_{1} = \left( {1,0}\right)\) as the first strategy of player II where the unplayed pure strategy is \(5 = m + 2\) as the label of that endpoint of \(Y\) , and for \({e}_{2} = \left( {0,1}\right)\) as the second endpoint of \(Y\) with label \(4 = m + 1\) . The equilibria themselves are identified by their complementary sets of labels.

\begin{tcolorbox}
该博弈的最优反应示意图的优点在于，它是一种定性的示意图，能够以比收益更具信息量的方式识别均衡。在图9.4中，我们仅将标签作为注释保留，不过这些标签编码了所有必要的信息。特别地， \(X\) 是玩家I的策略集，因为其边用玩家I的纯策略标记，而内部标签是玩家II的纯最优反应；对于 \(Y\) ，情况则相反。 \(X\) 的顶点是单位向量，通过未采用的纯策略的关联标签来识别；例如， \({e}_{1} = \left( {1,0,0}\right)\) 是带有标签2和3的顶点。在 \(Y\) 中，对于玩家II的第一个策略 \({e}_{1} = \left( {1,0}\right)\) ，未采用的纯策略是 \(5 = m + 2\) ，它是 \(Y\) 该端点的标签，而对于 \(Y\) 的第二个端点 \({e}_{2} = \left( {0,1}\right)\) ，其标签为 \(4 = m + 1\) 。均衡本身通过其互补的标签集来识别。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{9.3 The Lemke-Howson Algorithm}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{9.3 莱姆基 - 豪森算法}
\end{tcolorbox}

Identifying equilibria as completely labeled pairs(x, y)according to Theorem 9.2 does not yet prove that there is such a pair. Figure 9.5 is an extension of Figure 9.3 that illustrates such an existence proof, which is the topic of this section. In general, it provides an algorithm for finding an equilibrium of a nondegenerate \(m \times  n\) bimatrix game, due to Lemke and Howson (1964). In Section 9.7 below, we give a more specific (and more rigorous) description of this algorithm that can also be applied to degenerate games. This provides a new, elementary proof of the existence of an equilibrium in a two-player game, which does not require Brouwer's fixed point theorem. Nevertheless, this proof will have some similarity to the techniques used for proving the fixed point theorem, in particular the "parity argument" used in the proof of Sperner's lemma.

\begin{tcolorbox}
根据定理9.2将均衡识别为完全标记的对(x, y)，尚未证明存在这样的一对。图9.5是图9.3的扩展，它说明了这样一个存在性证明，这是本节的主题。一般来说，它提供了一种用于找到非退化 \(m \times  n\) 双矩阵博弈均衡的算法，该算法由莱姆基和豪森(1964年)提出。在下面的9.7节中，我们将给出该算法更具体(且更严格)的描述，该算法也可应用于退化博弈。这为两人博弈中均衡的存在性提供了一个新的、基本的证明，该证明不需要布劳威尔不动点定理。然而，这个证明将与用于证明不动点定理的技术有一些相似之处，特别是在斯佩纳引理的证明中使用的“奇偶性论证”。
\end{tcolorbox}

The mixed-strategy set \(X\) is a subset of \({\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) , and \(Y\) is a subset of \({\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) . Neither \(X\) nor \(Y\) contains the origin0of \({\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) or \({\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) , respectively. We now add0to \(X\) and connect it with a line segment to each vertex \({e}_{1},\ldots ,{e}_{m}\) of \(X\) , and denote this extension of \(X\) by \(\widetilde{X}\) . We similarly add0to \(Y\) and call the resulting extension \(\widetilde{Y}\) . Figure 9.5 shows \(\widetilde{X}\) and \(\widetilde{Y}\) for our example (9.1).

\begin{tcolorbox}
混合策略集 \(X\) 是 \({\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) 的一个子集，而 \(Y\) 是 \({\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) 的一个子集。 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) 分别不包含 \({\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) 或 \({\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) 的原点0。我们现在将0添加到 \(X\) 中，并通过线段将其连接到 \(X\) 的每个顶点 \({e}_{1},\ldots ,{e}_{m}\) ，并将 \(X\) 的这个扩展记为 \(\widetilde{X}\) 。我们类似地将0添加到 \(Y\) 中，并将得到的扩展称为 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 。图9.5展示了我们的示例(9.1)中的 \(\widetilde{X}\) 和 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Then0in \(\widetilde{X}\) has all labels \(1,\ldots ,m\) , and0in \(\widetilde{Y}\) has all labels \(m + 1,\ldots ,m + n\) , according to (9.5). Because the pair(0,0)is therefore completely labeled, we call it the artificial equilibrium of \(\widetilde{X} \times  \widetilde{Y}\) . It does not represent a mixed strategy pair.

\begin{tcolorbox}
然后，根据(9.5)， \(\widetilde{X}\) 中的0具有所有标签 \(1,\ldots ,m\) ， \(\widetilde{Y}\) 中的0具有所有标签 \(m + 1,\ldots ,m + n\) 。因为对(0, 0)因此是完全标记的，我们称其为 \(\widetilde{X} \times  \widetilde{Y}\) 的人工均衡。它不代表一个混合策略对。
\end{tcolorbox}

We now consider \(\widetilde{X}\) and \(\widetilde{Y}\) as graphs, rather like they are drawn in Figure 9.5. The nodes of the graph \(\widetilde{X}\) are the points that have \(m\) labels, and they are connected by an edge if they share \(m - 1\) labels, which are the labels of that edge. For example, the nodes0(with labels1,2,3) and \({e}_{2}\) (with labels1,3,5) are connected by the edge that has labels 1 and 3 . The nodes of the graph \(\widetilde{Y}\) are the points that have \(n\) labels, and are connected by an edge if they have \(n - 1\) labels in common (which in Figure 9.5 is just a single label because \(n - 1 = 1\) ), which are the labels of that edge.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们现在将 \(\widetilde{X}\) 和 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 视为图，就像图 9.5 中所绘制的那样。图 \(\widetilde{X}\) 的节点是具有 \(m\) 个标签的点，如果它们共享 \(m - 1\) 个标签，则通过一条边相连，这些标签就是该边的标签。例如，节点 0(标签为 1、2、3)和 \({e}_{2}\) (标签为 1、3、5)由具有标签 1 和 3 的边相连。图 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 的节点是具有 \(n\) 个标签的点，如果它们有 \(n - 1\) 个共同标签(在图 9.5 中由于 \(n - 1 = 1\) 所以只有一个标签)，则通过一条边相连，这些标签就是该边的标签。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_242_200_209_1214_550_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 9.5 The labeled mixed-strategy sets \(X\) and \(Y\) in Figure 9.3, each extended by an extra point0with all components zero, so that(0,0)is a completely labeled "artificial equilibrium". The gray arrows show the two Lemke-Howson paths for the missing label 2. The end nodes of these paths are the artificial equilibrium and the equilibria of the game, so the number of equilibria is odd.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 9.5 图 9.3 中标记的混合策略集 \(X\) 和 \(Y\) ，每个都通过一个所有分量都为零的额外点 0 进行扩展，使得 (0, 0) 是一个完全标记的“人工均衡”。灰色箭头表示缺失标签 2 的两条莱姆基 - 豪森路径。这些路径的端点是人工均衡和博弈的均衡，因此均衡的数量是奇数。
\end{tcolorbox}

Next, we choose a label in \(\{ 1,\ldots ,m + n\}\) that is allowed to be missing, which is kept fixed throughout. We consider all pairs(x, y)in \(\widetilde{X} \times  \widetilde{Y}\) that have all labels except possibly this missing label, and call such a pair(x, y)almost completely labeled. The almost completely labeled pairs(x, y)belong to two types of sets, which are either of the form \(\{ x\}  \times  E\) where \(x\) is a node of \(\widetilde{X}\) and \(E\) is an edge of \(\widetilde{Y}\) , with \(m\) labels for \(x\) and \(n - 1\) labels for \(y \in  E\) , except for the endpoints of \(E\) which have \(n\) labels; or these sets are of the form \(E \times  \{ y\}\) where \(E\) is an edge of \(\widetilde{X}\) , with \(m - 1\) labels for \(x \in  E\) , except for the endpoints of \(E\) which have \(m\) labels, and a node \(y\) of \(\widetilde{Y}\) . (Technically, these sets are the edges of the product graph of the two graphs \(\widetilde{X}\) and \(\widetilde{Y}\) .)

\begin{tcolorbox}
接下来，我们在 \(\{ 1,\ldots ,m + n\}\) 中选择一个允许缺失的标签，该标签在整个过程中保持固定。我们考虑 \(\widetilde{X} \times  \widetilde{Y}\) 中所有除了可能缺少这个缺失标签之外具有所有标签的对 (x, y)，并将这样的对 (x, y) 称为几乎完全标记的。几乎完全标记的对 (x, y) 属于两种类型的集合，要么是 \(\{ x\}  \times  E\) 的形式，其中 \(x\) 是 \(\widetilde{X}\) 的一个节点， \(E\) 是 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 的一条边， \(x\) 有 \(m\) 个标签， \(y \in  E\) 有 \(n - 1\) 个标签，除了 \(E\) 的端点有 \(n\) 个标签；或者这些集合是 \(E \times  \{ y\}\) 的形式，其中 \(E\) 是 \(\widetilde{X}\) 的一条边， \(x \in  E\) 有 \(m - 1\) 个标签，除了 \(E\) 的端点有 \(m\) 个标签，以及 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 的一个节点 \(y\) 。(从技术上讲，这些集合是两个图 \(\widetilde{X}\) 和 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 的乘积图的边。)
\end{tcolorbox}

In Figure 9.5, the missing label is chosen to be 2. Any completely labeled pair(x, y)is by definition also almost completely labeled. It serves as the unique starting pair of a path of almost completely labeled pairs(x, y). We start with the artificial equilibrium(0,0). With label 2 allowed to be missing, we drop label 2, which means changing \(x\) along the unique edge of \(\widetilde{X}\) that connects 0 to \({e}_{2}\) (shown by an arrow in \(\widetilde{X}\) ), while keeping \(y = \mathbf{0}\) fixed. The endpoint \(x = {e}_{2}\) of that arrow in \(\widetilde{X}\) has a new label 5 which is picked up. Because \(x\) has three labels1,3,5and \(y = \mathbf{0}\) has two labels 4,5 but label 2 is missing, the label 5 that has just been picked up in \(\widetilde{X}\) is now duplicate. Because \(y\) no longer needs to have the duplicate label 5, the next step is to drop label 5 in \(\widetilde{Y}\) , that is, change \(y\) from 0 to \({e}_{2}\) along the edge which has only label 4 . The endpoint \({e}_{2}\) of that edge of \(\widetilde{Y}\) has labels 4 and 3, where label 3 has been picked up. Therefore, the current pair \(\left( {x,y}\right)  = \left( {{e}_{2},{e}_{2}}\right)\) has the duplicate label 3. Correspondingly, we can now drop label 3 in \(\widetilde{X}\) , that is, move \(x\) along the edge with labels 1 and 5 to point \(b\) , where label 4 is picked up. At the current pair \(\left( {x,y}\right)  = \left( {b,{e}_{2}}\right)\) , label 4 is duplicate. Next, we drop label 4 in \(\widetilde{Y}\) by moving \(y\) along the edge with label 3 to reach point \(d\) , where label 2 is picked up. Because 2 is the missing label, the reached pair \(\left( {x,y}\right)  = \left( {b,d}\right)\) is completely labeled. This is the equilibrium that is found at the end of the path. In summary, the path is given by the sequence of points in \(\widetilde{X} \times  \widetilde{Y}\) with corresponding label sets

\begin{tcolorbox}
在图9.5中，缺失的标签被选为2。根据定义，任何完全标记的对 (x, y) 同时也是几乎完全标记的。它作为几乎完全标记的对 (x, y) 路径的唯一起始对。我们从人工均衡 (0,0) 开始。由于允许标签2缺失，我们去掉标签2，这意味着沿着连接0到 \({e}_{2}\) 的 \(\widetilde{X}\) 的唯一边(在 \(\widetilde{X}\) 中用箭头表示)改变 \(x\) ，同时保持 \(y = \mathbf{0}\) 固定。 \(\widetilde{X}\) 中该箭头的端点 \(x = {e}_{2}\) 获得了一个新标签5。因为 \(x\) 有三个标签1、3、5，而 \(y = \mathbf{0}\) 有两个标签4、5，但标签2缺失，所以在 \(\widetilde{X}\) 中刚获得的标签5现在是重复标签。因为 \(y\) 不再需要重复标签5，下一步是在 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 中去掉标签5，即沿着只有标签4的边将 \(y\) 从0改变到 \({e}_{2}\) 。 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 中该边的端点 \({e}_{2}\) 有标签4和3，其中标签3已被获取。因此，当前对 \(\left( {x,y}\right)  = \left( {{e}_{2},{e}_{2}}\right)\) 有重复标签3。相应地，我们现在可以在 \(\widetilde{X}\) 中去掉标签3，即沿着带有标签1和5的边将 \(x\) 移动到点 \(b\) ，在那里获取标签4。在当前对 \(\left( {x,y}\right)  = \left( {b,{e}_{2}}\right)\) 处，标签4是重复标签。接下来，我们通过沿着带有标签3的边移动 \(y\) 在 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 中去掉标签4，以到达点 \(d\) ，在那里获取标签2。因为2是缺失的标签，所以到达的对 \(\left( {x,y}\right)  = \left( {b,d}\right)\) 是完全标记的。这就是在路径末尾找到的均衡。总之，路径由 \(\widetilde{X} \times  \widetilde{Y}\) 中具有相应标签集的点序列给出
\end{tcolorbox}

(9.7)

\begin{center}
\adjustbox{max width=\textwidth}{
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
(x, y) & labels & drop label \\
\cline{1-3}
(0,0) & \(\bullet\) 123,45 & 2 in \(\widetilde{X}\) \\
\cline{1-3}
\(\left( {{e}_{2},0}\right)\) & 135,45 & 5 in \(\widetilde{Y}\) \\
\cline{1-3}
\(\left( {{e}_{2},{e}_{2}}\right)\) & 135, 43 & 3 in \(\widetilde{X}\) \\
\cline{1-3}
\(\left( {b,{e}_{2}}\right)\) & 145, 43 & 4 in \(\widetilde{Y}\) \\
\cline{1-3}
(b, d) & \(\square\) 145, 23 & (found 2 in \(\widetilde{Y}\) ). \\
\cline{1-3}
\hline
\end{tabular}
}
\end{center}

\begin{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\adjustbox{max width=\textwidth}{
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
(x，y) & 标签 & 删除标签 \\
\cline{1-3}
(0,0) & \(\bullet\) 123,45 & 在 \(\widetilde{X}\) 中有2个 \\
\cline{1-3}
\(\left( {{e}_{2},0}\right)\) & 135,45 & 在 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 中有5个 \\
\cline{1-3}
\(\left( {{e}_{2},{e}_{2}}\right)\) & 135, 43 & 在 \(\widetilde{X}\) 中有3个 \\
\cline{1-3}
\(\left( {b,{e}_{2}}\right)\) & 145, 43 & 在 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 中有4个 \\
\cline{1-3}
(b，d) & \(\square\) 145, 23 & (在 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 中找到2个)。 \\
\cline{1-3}
\hline
\end{tabular}
}
\end{center}

\end{tcolorbox}

Hence, by starting from the artificial equilibrium(0,0), the sequence of almost completely labeled node pairs of \(\widetilde{X} \times  \widetilde{Y}\) terminates at an actual equilibrium of the game. In general, this defines an algorithm for finding an equilibrium of a bimatrix game, due to Lemke and Howson (1964). The path in (9.7) is therefore also called the Lemke-Howson path for missing label 2. Note that it alternates between \(\widetilde{X}\) and \(\widetilde{Y}\) by keeping in each step one of the components \(x\) or \(y\) in(x, y)fixed, and changing the other component by traversing an edge in the respective graph \(\widetilde{Y}\) or \(\widetilde{X}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此，从人工均衡(0,0)开始， \(\widetilde{X} \times  \widetilde{Y}\) 的几乎完全标记的节点对序列在博弈的一个实际均衡处终止。一般来说，这定义了一种由莱姆基和豪森(1964年)提出的用于寻找双矩阵博弈均衡的算法。因此，(9.7)中的路径也被称为缺失标签2的莱姆基 - 豪森路径。请注意，它通过在每一步中固定(x, y)中的一个分量 \(x\) 或 \(y\) ，并通过遍历相应图 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 或 \(\widetilde{X}\) 中的一条边来改变另一个分量，从而在 \(\widetilde{X}\) 和 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 之间交替。
\end{tcolorbox}

A Lemke-Howson path can be started at any completely labeled pair(x, y), not just at the artificial equilibrium. For example, if started from(b, d)in Figure 9.5, then the path (for missing label 2) just traverses the path (9.7) backwards, which will then go back to(0,0). However, the game has further equilibria, for example the pure-strategy equilibrium \(\left( {{e}_{1},{e}_{1}}\right)\) , indicated by the white circles. The Lemke-Howson path for missing label 2 that starts at this equilibrium is also shown in Figure 9.5 and consists of only two steps, which end at the third equilibrium(a, c):

\begin{tcolorbox}
莱姆基 - 豪森路径可以从任何完全标记的对(x, y)开始，而不仅仅是从人工均衡开始。例如，如果从图9.5中的(b, d)开始，那么(缺失标签2的)路径只是反向遍历路径(9.7)，然后会回到(0,0)。然而，该博弈还有其他均衡，例如由白色圆圈表示的纯策略均衡 \(\left( {{e}_{1},{e}_{1}}\right)\) 。从这个均衡开始的缺失标签2的莱姆基 - 豪森路径也在图9.5中显示，它只包含两步，最终到达第三个均衡(a, c):
\end{tcolorbox}

(9.8)

\begin{center}
\adjustbox{max width=\textwidth}{
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
(x, y) & \phantom{X} & labels & drop label \\
\cline{1-4}
\(\left( {{e}_{1},{e}_{1}}\right)\) & O & 234, 15 & 2 in \(\widetilde{X}\) \\
\cline{1-4}
\(\left( {a,{e}_{1}}\right)\) & \phantom{X} & 345,15 & 5 in \(\widetilde{Y}\) \\
\cline{1-4}
(a, c) & - & 345,12 & (found 2 in \(\widetilde{Y}\) ). \\
\cline{1-4}
\hline
\end{tabular}
}
\end{center}

\begin{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\adjustbox{max width=\textwidth}{
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
(x，y) & \phantom{X} & 标签 & 删除标签 \\
\cline{1-4}
\(\left( {{e}_{1},{e}_{1}}\right)\) & O & 234, 15 & \(\widetilde{X}\) 中的 2 \\
\cline{1-4}
\(\left( {a,{e}_{1}}\right)\) & \phantom{X} & 345,15 & \(\widetilde{Y}\) 中的 5 \\
\cline{1-4}
(a，c) & - & 345,12 & (在 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 中找到 2)。 \\
\cline{1-4}
\hline
\end{tabular}
}
\end{center}

\end{tcolorbox}

As a consequence, for a given missing label, the equilibria of the game plus the artificial equilibrium are the end nodes of the Lemke-Howson paths. Because each path has two end nodes, their total number is even and therefore the number of equilibria of the game is odd. This is a parity argument similar to that used for Sperner's lemma in Section 7.4.

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此，对于给定的缺失标签，博弈的均衡加上人工均衡是莱姆基 - 豪森路径的端点。由于每条路径有两个端点，它们的总数是偶数，因此博弈的均衡数量是奇数。这是一个奇偶性论证，类似于第7.4节中用于斯珀纳引理的论证。
\end{tcolorbox}

In order to make this parity argument for a general bimatrix game, we have to assume that the game is nondegenerate (see Definition 6.7) because a degenerate game does not necessarily have a odd number of equilibria; for example, the degenerate game in Figure 6.12 has an infinite set of equilibria (which is very well understood with the help of the best-response diagram, see Exercise 9.2).

\begin{tcolorbox}
为了对一般的双矩阵博弈进行这种奇偶性论证，我们必须假设该博弈是非退化的(见定义6.7)，因为退化博弈不一定有奇数个均衡；例如，图6.12中的退化博弈有无限个均衡集(借助最优反应示意图可以很好地理解这一点，见练习9.2)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Lemma 9.3. An \(m \times  n\) bimatrix game as in Definition 9.1 is nondegenerate if and only if no point in \(X\) has more than \(m\) labels, and no point in \(Y\) has more than \(n\) labels.

\begin{tcolorbox}
引理9.3。如定义9.1中的 \(m \times  n\) 双矩阵博弈是非退化的，当且仅当 \(X\) 中没有点的标签超过 \(m\) 个，且 \(Y\) 中没有点的标签超过 \(n\) 个。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. Let \(x = \left( {{x}_{1},\ldots ,{x}_{m}}\right)  \in  X\) and consider the sets \(I = \left\{  {i \mid  {x}_{i} = 0,1 \leq  i \leq  m}\right\}\) and \(J = \{ j \mid  j\) is a best response to \(x,m + 1 \leq  j \leq  m + n\}\) , so that \(I \cup  J\) is the set of labels of \(x\) . The number of pure strategies played with positive probability by \(x\) is \(m - \left| I\right|\) , and nondegeneracy requires \(\left| J\right|  \leq  m - \left| I\right|\) or equivalently \(\left| I\right|  + \left| J\right|  \leq  m\) , that is, \(x\) has no more than \(m\) labels. Similarly, in a nondegenerate game no \(y \in  Y\) has more than \(n\) labels. In a degenerate game, for some \(x\) or \(y\) this fails to hold.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。设 \(x = \left( {{x}_{1},\ldots ,{x}_{m}}\right)  \in  X\) ，考虑集合 \(I = \left\{  {i \mid  {x}_{i} = 0,1 \leq  i \leq  m}\right\}\) 和 \(J = \{ j \mid  j\) 是对 \(x,m + 1 \leq  j \leq  m + n\}\) 的最优反应，因此 \(I \cup  J\) 是 \(x\) 的标签集。 \(x\) 以正概率采用的纯策略数量是 \(m - \left| I\right|\) ，非退化性要求 \(\left| J\right|  \leq  m - \left| I\right|\) ，或者等价地 \(\left| I\right|  + \left| J\right|  \leq  m\) ，即 \(x\) 的标签不超过 \(m\) 个。类似地，在非退化博弈中，任何 \(y \in  Y\) 的标签都不超过 \(n\) 个。在退化博弈中，对于某些 \(x\) 或 \(y\) ，这一条件不成立。
\end{tcolorbox}

For a nondegenerate \(m \times  n\) game, the graphs \(\widetilde{X}\) and \(\widetilde{Y}\) have the following properties (which we state without proof, because we will give an alternative, more rigorous description of the Lemke-Howson method in Section 9.7 below):

\begin{tcolorbox}
对于非退化的 \(m \times  n\) 博弈，图 \(\widetilde{X}\) 和 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 具有以下性质(我们不给出证明，因为我们将在下面的第9.7节中给出莱姆基 - 豪森方法的另一种更严格的描述):
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item The nodes of \(\widetilde{X}\) are the finitely many points in \(X\) that have \(m\) labels, plus \(\mathbf{0} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) . The nodes of \(\widetilde{Y}\) are the finitely many points in \(Y\) that have \(n\) labels, plus \(\mathbf{0} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) .
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item \(\widetilde{X}\) 的节点是 \(X\) 中具有 \(m\) 个标签的有限个点，再加上 \(\mathbf{0} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{m}\) 。 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 的节点是 \(Y\) 中具有 \(n\) 个标签的有限个点，再加上 \(\mathbf{0} \in  {\mathbb{R}}^{n}\) 。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item Any two nodes of \(\widetilde{X}\) are joined by an edge if they have \(m - 1\) labels in common, which are the labels of that edge. (As a geometric object, the edge is the set of points that have at least these labels; as an edge of the graph \(\widetilde{X}\) , the edge is just a pair \(\left\{  {x,{x}^{\prime }}\right\}\) of nodes of \(\widetilde{X}\) .) Any two nodes of \(\widetilde{Y}\) are joined by an edge if they have \(n - 1\) labels in common, which are the labels of that edge of \(\widetilde{Y}\) .
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 如果 \(\widetilde{X}\) 的任意两个节点有 \(m - 1\) 个共同的标签，那么它们由一条边连接，这些共同标签就是该边的标签。(作为一个几何对象，这条边是至少具有这些标签的点的集合；作为图 \(\widetilde{X}\) 的一条边，这条边只是 \(\widetilde{X}\) 的一对节点 \(\left\{  {x,{x}^{\prime }}\right\}\) 。)如果 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 的任意两个节点有 \(n - 1\) 个共同的标签，那么它们由一条边连接，这些共同标签就是 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 中该边的标签。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item For any node \(x\) in \(\widetilde{X}\) and a label \(k\) of \(x\) , there is a unique edge of \(\widetilde{X}\) with endpoint \(x\) that has all the labels of \(x\) except \(k\) . We say this edge is traversed from \(x\) by dropping label \(k\) (the other endpoint \({x}^{\prime }\) of this edge \(\left\{  {x,{x}^{\prime }}\right\}\) has some other label than \(k\) that is picked up, as described for Figure 9.5 above). The same holds for any node in \(\widetilde{Y}\) .
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 对于 \(\widetilde{X}\) 中的任意节点 \(x\) 以及 \(x\) 的一个标签 \(k\) ， \(\widetilde{X}\) 中存在一条唯一的以 \(x\) 为端点的边，该边包含 \(x\) 除 \(k\) 之外的所有标签。我们称这条边是通过去掉标签 \(k\) 从 \(x\) 遍历得到的(这条边 \(\left\{  {x,{x}^{\prime }}\right\}\) 的另一个端点 \({x}^{\prime }\) 会获取除 \(k\) 之外的其他标签，如上文图 9.5 所述)。同样的情况适用于 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 中的任意节点。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

Let \(\ell  \in  \{ 1,\ldots ,m + n\}\) . Using the above properties, we define the \({LH}\) graph for missing label \(\ell\) as a certain subset of the "product graph" of \(\widetilde{X}\) and \(\widetilde{Y}\) . The nodes of the LH graph are the node pairs(x, y)in \(\widetilde{X} \times  \widetilde{Y}\) that are almost completely labeled, that is, every label in \(\{ 1,\ldots ,m + n\}  - \{ \ell \}\) appears as a label of \(x\) or \(y\) . Any edge of the LH graph is of the form \(\left\{  {\left( {x,y}\right) ,\left( {x,{y}^{\prime }}\right) }\right\}\) where \(\left\{  {y,{y}^{\prime }}\right\}\) is an edge of \(\widetilde{Y}\) , or of the form \(\left\{  {\left( {x,y}\right) ,\left( {{x}^{\prime },y}\right) }\right\}\) where \(\left\{  {x,{x}^{\prime }}\right\}\) is an edge of \(\widetilde{X}\) . If(x, y)is completely labeled, then it has a unique neighbor in the LH graph reached by dropping label \(\ell\) , that is, by following the edge which does not have label \(\ell\) (via the edge in \(\widetilde{X}\) if \(\ell  \in  \{ 1,\ldots ,m\}\) , and the edge in \(\widetilde{Y}\) otherwise). If(x, y)is almost completely labeled and does not have label \(\ell\) , then there is exactly one duplicate label \(k\) that appears as a label of both \(x\) and \(y\) . Such a node in the LH graph has exactly two neighbors, reached by dropping the duplicate label \(k\) in either \(\widetilde{X}\) or \(\widetilde{Y}\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
设 \(\ell  \in  \{ 1,\ldots ,m + n\}\) 。利用上述性质，我们将缺失标签 \(\ell\) 的 \({LH}\) 图定义为 \(\widetilde{X}\) 和 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 的“乘积图”的某个子集。LH 图的节点是 \(\widetilde{X} \times  \widetilde{Y}\) 中几乎完全标记的节点对 (x, y)，即 \(\{ 1,\ldots ,m + n\}  - \{ \ell \}\) 中的每个标签都作为 \(x\) 或 \(y\) 的标签出现。LH 图的任意边的形式为 \(\left\{  {\left( {x,y}\right) ,\left( {x,{y}^{\prime }}\right) }\right\}\) ，其中 \(\left\{  {y,{y}^{\prime }}\right\}\) 是 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 的一条边，或者形式为 \(\left\{  {\left( {x,y}\right) ,\left( {{x}^{\prime },y}\right) }\right\}\) ，其中 \(\left\{  {x,{x}^{\prime }}\right\}\) 是 \(\widetilde{X}\) 的一条边。如果 (x, y) 是完全标记的，那么它在 LH 图中有一个唯一的邻居，通过去掉标签 \(\ell\) 到达，即沿着不包含标签 \(\ell\) 的边(如果 \(\ell  \in  \{ 1,\ldots ,m\}\) ，则通过 \(\widetilde{X}\) 中的边；否则通过 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 中的边)。如果 (x, y) 几乎完全标记且不包含标签 \(\ell\) ，那么恰好存在一个重复标签 \(k\) ，它同时作为 \(x\) 和 \(y\) 的标签出现。LH 图中的这样一个节点恰好有两个邻居，通过在 \(\widetilde{X}\) 或 \(\widetilde{Y}\) 中去掉重复标签 \(k\) 到达。
\end{tcolorbox}
