The moves of the other players have fixed probabilities, so all that matters is the sequence \({\sigma }^{i}\left( u\right)\) of moves of player \(i\) , which is some sequence \(\sigma\) , which has the same probability no matter whether player \(i\) uses \(\beta\) or \(\mu\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
因为所有中间项都抵消了，并且根据 (10.5)。(通过考虑图 10.11 中像 \(\sigma  = {lbc} = {c}_{1}{c}_{2}{c}_{3}\) 这样的例子可以最好地理解这一点。)因为对于玩家 \(i\) 的所有序列 \(\sigma\) 都有 \(\beta \left\lbrack  \sigma \right\rbrack   = \mu \left\lbrack  \sigma \right\rbrack\) ，根据定理 10.4 之前的解释，策略 \(\beta\) 和 \(\mu\) 是实现等价的；原因是博弈树(game tree)的任何节点(node)都是由所有玩家和机会的唯一行动序列到达的。其他玩家的行动有固定的概率，所以重要的是玩家 \(i\) 的行动序列 \({\sigma }^{i}\left( u\right)\) ，它是某个序列 \(\sigma\) ，无论玩家 \(i\) 使用 \(\beta\) 还是 \(\mu\) ，该序列的概率都是相同的。
\end{tcolorbox}

It is instructive to reproduce the preceding proof for the examples after Definition 10.3 or Figure 10.10 above, which gives also some practice in finding behavior strategies in small games.

\begin{tcolorbox}
对定义 10.3 之后的例子或上面的图 10.10 重现前面的证明是有启发性的，这也能让我们在小型博弈中寻找行为策略(behavior strategy)方面得到一些练习。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{10.9 Behavior Strategies in the Monty Hall Problem}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{10.9 蒙提霍尔问题中的行为策略(behavior strategy)}
\end{tcolorbox}

We illustrate the concept of behavior strategies for a game-theoretic, non-standard version of the Monty Hall problem. The original version has been posed as a problem in probability in the context of an early television game show with the showmaster named "Monty Hall". A contestant can win a prize by choosing one of three closed doors. Behind one of these doors is the prize (a car), and behind each of the other two doors is something the contestant does not want (a goat). Once the contestant has selected one of the doors, Monty Hall opens one of the remaining doors, which reveals a goat, and offers the contestant the option to switch to the other closed door. The question is, should she switch doors? It seems that the contestant is now facing equal chances between two options, the originally chosen door and the other unopened door, so that there is no point in switching. However, the correct solution is that the original probability \(\frac{1}{3}\) that the contestant has chosen the correct door has not changed by Monty Hall's action of revealing a goat behind another door, because Monty Hall knows where the prize is and can always choose such a door. That probability stays at \(\frac{1}{3}\) , so that the probability that the other unopened door has the prize is \(\frac{2}{3}\) , and therefore the contestant should indeed switch.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们通过博弈论中一个非标准版本的蒙提·霍尔问题(Monty Hall problem)来说明行为策略(behavior strategy)的概念。最初的版本是作为一个概率问题提出的，它源于一档早期的电视游戏节目，节目主持人名为“蒙提·霍尔”。参赛者可以通过选择三扇关闭的门之一来赢得奖品。其中一扇门后面是奖品(一辆汽车)，另外两扇门后面是参赛者不想要的东西(一只山羊)。一旦参赛者选择了其中一扇门，蒙提·霍尔会打开剩下两扇门中的一扇，露出一只山羊，然后给参赛者一个机会，让其选择换到另一扇未打开的门。问题是，她应该换门吗？看起来参赛者现在面临着两个选项(最初选择的门和另一扇未打开的门)的同等机会，所以换门似乎没有意义。然而，正确的答案是，参赛者最初选择正确门的概率 \(\frac{1}{3}\) 并不会因为蒙提·霍尔打开另一扇门露出山羊的行为而改变，因为蒙提·霍尔知道奖品在哪里，并且总能选择这样一扇门。这个概率仍然是 \(\frac{1}{3}\) ，所以另一扇未打开的门后面有奖品的概率是 \(\frac{2}{3}\) ，因此参赛者确实应该换门。
\end{tcolorbox}

This analysis runs counter to the spontaneous reaction by many people who rather see the opened door with the goat as confirmation that they had chosen the correct door in the first place (this is incorrect). Moreover, they are suspicious that Monty Hall only tries to lure them away from their correct choice. That suspicion is correct if Monty Hall would only sometimes open another door with a goat behind it and offer the switch to the remaining door. Rather obviously, if Monty Hall only offered the switch when the contestant has chosen the correct door, then switching would mean that she loses the prize, and therefore she should never switch. In short, the original "solution" to the Monty Hall problem is only valid when Monty Hall always offers the switch.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这种分析与许多人的直觉反应相悖，他们更倾向于将打开有山羊的门视为自己最初选择正确门的确认(这是错误的)。此外，他们怀疑蒙提·霍尔只是试图诱使他们放弃正确的选择。如果蒙提·霍尔只是有时打开另一扇有山羊的门并提供换门的机会，那么这种怀疑是正确的。很明显，如果蒙提·霍尔只在参赛者选择了正确的门时才提供换门机会，那么换门就意味着她会失去奖品，因此她永远不应该换门。简而言之，蒙提·霍尔问题的原始“解决方案”仅在蒙提·霍尔总是提供换门机会时才有效。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_299_337_1408_658_724_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 10.12 The (modified) Monty Hall problem as a game between player I as the contestant who has chosen the correct door with probability \(\frac{1}{3}\) (left chance move), and the television show host Monty Hall as player II who can keep the doors closed (moves \(C\) and \(c\) ), or can open another non-prize door (moves \(O\) and \(o\) ). When a door is opened, player I can keep her original choice(K)or switch to the remaining closed door(S). Payoff 1 means that player I gets the prize, paid by player II, payoff 0 means no prize.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图10.12 将(修改后的)蒙提·霍尔问题视为玩家I(参赛者)和玩家II(电视节目主持人蒙提·霍尔)之间的博弈。参赛者选择正确门的概率为 \(\frac{1}{3}\) (左侧的随机行动)，蒙提·霍尔可以选择保持门关闭(行动 \(C\) 和 \(c\) )，或者打开另一扇没有奖品的门(行动 \(O\) 和 \(o\) )。当一扇门被打开时，玩家I可以保持她最初的选择(K)或换到剩下的未打开的门(S)。收益为1意味着玩家I获得奖品，由玩家II支付；收益为0意味着没有奖品。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 10.12 shows a new game between the contestant as player I and Monty Hall as a player II who has choices after the contestant has chosen one of the three doors. We assume that the prize is hidden behind each door with equal probability. Monty Hall knows whether or not the contestant has chosen the correct door, shown by his two decision nodes with the two moves \(C\) and \(O\) at the left node and \(c\) and \(o\) at the right node, which he can choose independently. The moves \(C\) and \(c\) mean that Monty keeps the doors closed, moves \(O\) and \(o\) that he opens another door with a goat behind it. In that case the contestant (who does not know if she has chosen the correct door) can keep to her choice(K)or switch to the other unopened door(S), and then gets the prize or not. We assume that the hosting television station prefers not to the pay the prize, so that this is zero-sum game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图10.12展示了参赛者作为玩家I和蒙提·霍尔作为玩家II之间的一个新博弈，在参赛者选择了三扇门之一后，蒙提·霍尔有多种选择。我们假设奖品以相等的概率隐藏在每扇门后面。蒙提·霍尔知道参赛者是否选择了正确的门，这由他的两个决策节点表示，左节点有两个行动 \(C\) 和 \(O\) ，右节点有两个行动 \(c\) 和 \(o\) ，他可以独立地做出选择。行动 \(C\) 和 \(c\) 意味着蒙提保持门关闭，行动 \(O\) 和 \(o\) 意味着他打开另一扇后面有山羊的门。在这种情况下，参赛者(不知道自己是否选择了正确的门)可以坚持自己的选择(K)或换到另一扇未打开的门(S)，然后决定是否获得奖品。我们假设主办电视台更不愿意支付奖品，所以这是一个零和博弈(zero-sum game)。
\end{tcolorbox}

This game has the same structure as the introductory game in Figure 10.1 of this chapter, although with reversed roles of the players, and different payoffs and chance probabilities. It has the following strategic form:

\begin{tcolorbox}
这个博弈与本章图10.1中的介绍性博弈具有相同的结构，尽管玩家的角色相反，并且收益和随机概率不同。它具有以下策略形式(strategic form):
\end{tcolorbox}

(10.8)Although the game is zero-sum, we show the expected payoffs to both players because the best responses are easier to see. If the contestant chooses \(K\) , which is the top row in (10.8), then her expected payoff is always \(\frac{1}{3}\) , and all pure strategies of player II are best responses. This is therefore a degenerate game. Payoff \(\frac{1}{3}\) also results when Monty Hall never opens a door (strategy \({Cc}\) ), where player I does not even have the opportunity to switch doors. Hence, \(K\) and \({Cc}\) are mutual best responses with the pure equilibrium(K, Cc), and \(\frac{1}{3}\) as the value of the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
尽管这个博弈是零和博弈，但我们展示了两个玩家的期望效用(expected payoff)，因为这样更容易看出最优反应(best response)。如果参赛者选择 \(K\) ，即在(10.8)中的顶行，那么她的期望效用总是 \(\frac{1}{3}\) ，玩家II的所有纯策略都是最优反应。因此，这是一个退化博弈(degenerate game)。当蒙提·霍尔从不打开门(策略 \({Cc}\) )时，也会得到收益 \(\frac{1}{3}\) ，此时玩家I甚至没有机会换门。因此， \(K\) 和 \({Cc}\) 是具有纯策略均衡(pure equilibrium)(K，Cc)的相互最优反应，并且 \(\frac{1}{3}\) 是博弈的值。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_300_484_893_690_395_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

We now study the set of all equilibrium strategies of the players. A second pure equilibrium is(K, Oc), where Monty Hall only opens another door if the contestant has chosen the correct door in the first place (what we called "trying to lure her to switch"). In this equilibrium, the unique best response to \({Oc}\) is \(K\) . Hence, player I’s unique max-min strategy (and strategy in every equilibrium) is \(K\) , by Proposition 8.3 about zero-sum games.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们现在研究参与者的所有均衡策略集合。第二个纯策略均衡是 (K, Oc)，即蒙提·霍尔仅在参赛者首次选择了正确的门时才打开另一扇门(我们称之为“试图诱使她换门”)。在这个均衡中，对 \({Oc}\) 的唯一最优反应是 \(K\) 。因此，根据关于零和博弈(zero - sum game)的命题 8.3，参与者 I 的唯一极大极小策略(max - min strategy)(也是每个均衡中的策略)是 \(K\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

On the other hand, there are many equilibrium strategies of player II, because all of his pure strategies are best responses to \(K\) , and even the strategies \({Co}\) and \({Oo}\) can potentially have positive probability in a mixed equilibrium. One such mixed strategy is \(y = \left( {0,0,\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}}\right)\) , that is, to play \({Oc}\) and \({Oo}\) with probability \(\frac{1}{2}\) each. Then the top row \(K\) has expected payoff \(- \frac{1}{3}\) to player II (it always has), and the bottom row \(S\) has also expected payoff \(- \frac{1}{3}\) , so \(y\) is also a max-min strategy (in terms of his own payoffs) of player II. A second max-min strategy is \({y}^{\prime } = \left( {0,\frac{1}{3},\frac{2}{3},0}\right)\) where the same applies. With \({y}^{\prime }\) , Monty Hall plays with probability \(\frac{1}{3}\) the strategy \({Co}\) , with the seemingly strange offer to the contestant to switch only if she initially chose the wrong door, so that switching would give her the prize for sure; however, \({y}^{\prime }\) also chooses with probability \(\frac{2}{3}\) the "opposite" strategy \({Oc}\) . The strategy \({Oo}\) , chosen with probability \(\frac{1}{2}\) by \(y\) , is the strategy of always opening another door with a goat behind it, as in the original Monty Hall problem, and payoff \(\frac{2}{3}\) when player \(\mathrm{I}\) chooses \(S\) . Nevertheless, in the game the contestant should never switch, because doing so with any positive probability is not a max-min strategy (with a worse payoff than \(\frac{1}{3}\) against \({Oc}\) ).

\begin{tcolorbox}
另一方面，参与者 II 有许多均衡策略，因为他的所有纯策略都是对 \(K\) 的最优反应，甚至策略 \({Co}\) 和 \({Oo}\) 在混合均衡(mixed equilibrium)中都可能有正概率。一种这样的混合策略是 \(y = \left( {0,0,\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}}\right)\) ，即分别以概率 \(\frac{1}{2}\) 采用 \({Oc}\) 和 \({Oo}\) 。那么，顶行 \(K\) 给参与者 II 的期望效用(expected payoff)是 \(- \frac{1}{3}\) (始终如此)，底行 \(S\) 的期望效用也是 \(- \frac{1}{3}\) ，所以 \(y\) 也是参与者 II 的一个极大极小策略(就他自己的收益而言)。第二个极大极小策略是 \({y}^{\prime } = \left( {0,\frac{1}{3},\frac{2}{3},0}\right)\) ，同样适用上述情况。对于 \({y}^{\prime }\) ，蒙提·霍尔以概率 \(\frac{1}{3}\) 采用策略 \({Co}\) ，即只有当参赛者最初选错门时才向她提出换门的看似奇怪的提议，这样换门肯定能让她获得奖品；然而， \({y}^{\prime }\) 也以概率 \(\frac{2}{3}\) 选择“相反”的策略 \({Oc}\) 。策略 \({Oo}\) ， \(y\) 以概率 \(\frac{1}{2}\) 选择它，是始终打开另一扇后面有山羊的门的策略，就像原始的蒙提·霍尔问题一样，并且当参与者 \(\mathrm{I}\) 选择 \(S\) 时期望效用为 \(\frac{2}{3}\) 。然而，在这个游戏中，参赛者永远不应该换门，因为以任何正概率换门都不是极大极小策略(与 \({Oc}\) 对抗时，收益比 \(\frac{1}{3}\) 更差)。
\end{tcolorbox}

It can be shown that all optimal mixed strategies of player II in this game are convex combinations of the described max-min strategies \({Cc},{Oc},y\) , and \({y}^{\prime }\) . Except for \({y}^{\prime }\) , these are all behavior strategies. As pure strategies, \({Cc}\) and \({Co}\) are trivially behavior strategies. With the strategy \(y\) , the behavior of player \(\Pi\) is to always choose \(O\) at the left decision node and to randomize between \(c\) and \(o\) with probability \(\frac{1}{2}\) at the right decision node. The strategy \({y}^{\prime }\) is not a behavior strategy, as its distribution on the move pairs \({Cc},{Co},{Oc},{Oo}\) in the following table shows:

\begin{tcolorbox}
可以证明，在这个游戏中，参与者 II 的所有最优混合策略(mixed strategy)都是所描述的极大极小策略 \({Cc},{Oc},y\) 和 \({y}^{\prime }\) 的凸组合(convex combination)。除了 \({y}^{\prime }\) ，这些都是行为策略(behavior strategy)。作为纯策略， \({Cc}\) 和 \({Co}\) 显然是行为策略。采用策略 \(y\) 时，参与者 \(\Pi\) 的行为是在左决策节点(node)始终选择 \(O\) ，在右决策节点以概率 \(\frac{1}{2}\) 在 \(c\) 和 \(o\) 之间随机选择。策略 \({y}^{\prime }\) 不是行为策略，如下表中它在行动对 \({Cc},{Co},{Oc},{Oo}\) 上的分布所示:
\end{tcolorbox}

(10.9)

\begin{center}
\adjustbox{max width=\textwidth}{
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
\multirow{3}{*}{left node \(C\) Og} & right node \(C\) & 0 & left node & right node \(C\) & 0 \\
\cline{2-6}
 & 0 & \(\frac{1}{3}\) & \(C\) & OIN & ON \\
\cline{2-6}
 & WIN & 0 & Og & one & ON \\
\cline{1-6}
\hline
\end{tabular}
}
\end{center}

\begin{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\adjustbox{max width=\textwidth}{
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
\multirow{3}{*}{左节点 \(C\) 奥格(Og)} & 右节点 \(C\) & 0 & 左节点 & 右节点 \(C\) & 0 \\
\cline{2-6}
 & 0 & \(\frac{1}{3}\) & \(C\) & OIN & ON \\
\cline{2-6}
 & WIN & 0 & 奥格(Og) & 一 & ON \\
\cline{1-6}
\hline
\end{tabular}
}
\end{center}

\end{tcolorbox}

The right table in (10.9) shows the behavior strategy \({\beta }^{\prime }\) that, by Kuhn’s theorem, is realization equivalent to \({y}^{\prime }\) . It randomizes between \(C\) and \(O\) with probabilities \(\frac{1}{3}\) and \(\frac{2}{3}\) , and between \(c\) and \(o\) with probabilities \(\frac{2}{3}\) and \(\frac{1}{3}\) . Translated back to probabilities for the pure strategies \({Cc},{Co},{Oc},{Oo}\) , this behavior strategy \({\beta }^{\prime }\) is the mixed strategy \(\left( {\frac{2}{9},\frac{1}{9},\frac{4}{9},\frac{2}{9}}\right)\) , which gives expected payoff \(- \frac{1}{3}\) to player II in row \(S\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
根据库恩定理，(10.9)中的右表展示了与混合策略 \({y}^{\prime }\) 实现等价的行为策略 \({\beta }^{\prime }\) 。该行为策略以概率 \(\frac{1}{3}\) 和 \(\frac{2}{3}\) 在 \(C\) 和 \(O\) 之间进行随机选择，以概率 \(\frac{2}{3}\) 和 \(\frac{1}{3}\) 在 \(c\) 和 \(o\) 之间进行随机选择。将此行为策略 \({\beta }^{\prime }\) 转换回纯策略 \({Cc},{Co},{Oc},{Oo}\) 的概率，它就是混合策略 \(\left( {\frac{2}{9},\frac{1}{9},\frac{4}{9},\frac{2}{9}}\right)\) ，该混合策略为第 \(S\) 行的参与者 II 带来期望效用 \(- \frac{1}{3}\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Figure 10.13 gives a geometric view of these max-min strategies of player II. The left picture shows the mixed-strategy simplex, and the right picture the square that represents the pairs of behavior strategy probabilities. If \(p\) is the probability that he plays \(O\) (vertical coordinate of the square, going downwards) and \(q\) is the probability that he plays \(o\) (horizontal coordinate), then his four pure strategies \({Cc},{Co},{Oc},{Oo}\) are played with probabilities \(\left( {1 - p}\right) \left( {1 - q}\right) ,\left( {1 - p}\right) q,p\left( {1 - q}\right)\) , and \({pq}\) , respectively. For \(p,q \in  \left\{  {\frac{1}{4},\frac{1}{2},\frac{3}{4}}\right\}\) , they define a grid of lines which is shown in both pictures. However, as a subset of the mixed-strategy simplex, this grid is "twisted" and not convex because a convex combination of behavior strategies is a general mixed strategy and usually not a behavior strategy. The square is convex, and so is the set of max-min behavior strategies as a subset of that square. The behavior strategy \({\beta }^{\prime }\) belongs to that set, and is a convex combination of \({Cc}\) and \(y\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 10.13 从几何角度展示了参与者 II 的这些极大极小策略。左图展示了混合策略单纯形，右图展示了代表行为策略概率对的正方形。如果 \(p\) 是他选择 \(O\) 的概率(正方形的垂直坐标，向下)， \(q\) 是他选择 \(o\) 的概率(水平坐标)，那么他的四种纯策略 \({Cc},{Co},{Oc},{Oo}\) 分别以概率 \(\left( {1 - p}\right) \left( {1 - q}\right) ,\left( {1 - p}\right) q,p\left( {1 - q}\right)\) 和 \({pq}\) 被选择。对于 \(p,q \in  \left\{  {\frac{1}{4},\frac{1}{2},\frac{3}{4}}\right\}\) ，它们定义了一个线网格，在两幅图中均有展示。然而，作为混合策略单纯形的一个子集，这个网格是“扭曲的”且非凸的，因为行为策略的凸组合是一般的混合策略，通常不是行为策略。正方形是凸的，作为该正方形子集的极大极小行为策略集也是凸的。行为策略 \({\beta }^{\prime }\) 属于该集合，并且是 \({Cc}\) 和 \(y\) 的凸组合。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_302_196_202_1261_521_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 10.13 Left: Simplex of mixed strategies (always for player II) for the game (10.8), and in gray its subset of max-min strategies with vertices \({Cc},{Oc},y,{y}^{\prime }\) . The "twisted grid" shows the behavior strategies for move probabilities that are multiples of \(\frac{1}{4}\) . Right: The square of behavior strategies for the extensive game in Figure 10.12, with the behavior strategy \({\beta }^{\prime }\) that is realization equivalent to \({y}^{\prime }\) , and the same grid. The shaded triangle is the set of max-min behavior strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图 10.13 左图:博弈 (10.8) 的混合策略单纯形(始终针对参与者 II)，灰色部分是顶点为 \({Cc},{Oc},y,{y}^{\prime }\) 的极大极小策略子集。“扭曲的网格”展示了移动概率为 \(\frac{1}{4}\) 倍数的行为策略。右图:图 10.12 中扩展式博弈的行为策略正方形，其中行为策略 \({\beta }^{\prime }\) 与 \({y}^{\prime }\) 实现等价，并且有相同的网格。阴影三角形是极大极小行为策略集。
\end{tcolorbox}

The pure strategies are the four corners of the square (in the same positions as the cells in (10.9)), and are also the vertices of the mixed-strategy simplex.

\begin{tcolorbox}
纯策略是正方形的四个角(与 (10.9) 中的单元格位置相同)，也是混合策略单纯形的顶点。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{10.10 Subgames and Subgame-Perfect Equilibria}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{10.10 子博弈与子博弈完美均衡}
\end{tcolorbox}

In an extensive game with perfect information as studied in Chapter 4, a subgame is any subtree of the game, given by a node of the tree as the root of the subtree and all its descendants. This definition has to be refined when considering games with imperfect information, where every player has to know that they have entered the subtree.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在第 4 章研究的具有完美信息的扩展式博弈中，子博弈是博弈树的任意子树，由树中的一个节点作为子树的根节点及其所有后代节点构成。在考虑具有不完美信息的博弈时，这个定义需要细化，此时每个参与者都必须知道他们已经进入了子树。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 10.5. In an extensive game, a subgame is any subtree of the game tree so that every information set is either a subset of or disjoint from the nodes of the subtree.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义 10.5:在扩展式博弈中，子博弈是博弈树的任意子树，使得每个信息集要么是子树节点的子集，要么与子树节点不相交。
\end{tcolorbox}

In other words, if any information set \(h\) contains some node of the subtree, then \(h\) must be completely contained in the subtree. This means that the player to move at \(h\) knows that he or she is in the subgame. Information sets that have no nodes in the subtree do not matter because they are disjoint from the subtree.

\begin{tcolorbox}
换句话说，如果任意信息集 \(h\) 包含子树的某个节点，那么 \(h\) 必须完全包含在子树中。这意味着在 \(h\) 处行动的参与者知道他或她处于子博弈中。信息集中在子树中没有节点的情况无关紧要，因为它们与子树不相交。
\end{tcolorbox}

In Figure 10.14, the subtree with player I's decision node as root defines a subgame of the game. On the other hand, a node in player II's second information set is not the root of a subgame, as shown in the figure for the node that follows move \(B\) of player \(\mathrm{I}\) , because this information set is neither contained in nor disjoint from the subtree with that root.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在图 10.14 中，以参与者 I 的决策节点为根的子树定义了该博弈的一个子博弈。另一方面，参与者 II 的第二个信息集中的一个节点不是子博弈的根节点，如图中参与者 \(\mathrm{I}\) 选择行动 \(B\) 之后的节点所示，因为这个信息集既不包含在以该节点为根的子树中，也不与该子树不相交。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=1.0\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_303_332_216_1273_609_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 10.14 Extensive game, with its subgame, and the reduced strategic form of the game. Only one equilibrium of the game is subgame-perfect, see Figure 10.15.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图10.14 扩展式博弈及其子博弈，以及该博弈的精简策略型。该博弈只有一个均衡是子博弈完美均衡，见图10.15。
\end{tcolorbox}

A trivial subgame is either the game itself, or any leaf of the game tree. Some games have no other subgames, for example the games in Figure 10.1 or 10.5.

\begin{tcolorbox}
平凡子博弈要么是博弈本身，要么是博弈树的任意叶节点。有些博弈没有其他子博弈，例如图10.1或图10.5中的博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

If every player has perfect recall, then any mixed strategy can be replaced by a realization equivalent behavior strategy by Kuhn's Theorem 10.4. The strategies in any equilibrium can therefore be assumed to be behavior strategies. A behavior strategy can easily be restricted to a subgame, by ignoring the behavior probabilities for the information sets that are disjoint from the subgame. This restriction of a strategy would be much more difficult to define for a profile of mixed strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
如果每个参与者都有完美记忆，那么根据库恩定理10.4，任何混合策略都可以用一个实现等价的行为策略来替代。因此，任何均衡中的策略都可以假定为行为策略。通过忽略与子博弈不相交的信息集的行为概率，行为策略可以很容易地限制在子博弈中。对于混合策略组合来说，定义策略的这种限制要困难得多。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 10.6. In an extensive game with perfect recall, a subgame-perfect equilibrium (SPE) is a profile of behavior strategies that defines an equilibrium for every subgame of the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义10.6。在具有完美记忆的扩展式博弈中，子博弈完美均衡(SPE)是一个行为策略组合，它为博弈的每个子博弈定义了一个均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

In Figure 10.14, the reduced strategic form of the extensive game is shown on the right. Figure 10.15 shows the non-trivial subgame that starts with the move of player I, and its strategic form. This subgame has a unique mixed equilibrium, where player I chooses \(T,B\) with probabilities \(\frac{2}{3},\frac{1}{3}\) , and player II chooses \(a,b\) with probabilities \(\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}\) . The resulting payoffs to players I and II are 2 and 4, which are substituted for the subgame to obtain a smaller game where player II chooses between \(l\) and \(r\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
在图10.14中，扩展式博弈的精简策略型显示在右侧。图10.15显示了从参与者I的行动开始的非平凡子博弈及其策略型。这个子博弈有一个唯一的混合均衡，其中参与者I以概率 \(\frac{2}{3},\frac{1}{3}\) 选择 \(T,B\) ，参与者II以概率 \(\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}\) 选择 \(a,b\) 。参与者I和II的最终收益分别为2和4，用这些收益替代子博弈，得到一个较小的博弈，其中参与者II在 \(l\) 和 \(r\) 之间进行选择。
\end{tcolorbox}

This smaller game is shown on the right in Figure 10.15. Because this small game has perfect information, an SPE is found by backward induction, which here means that player II chooses \(l\) with certainty. In the overall resulting behavior strategies, player I mixes \(T,B\) with probabilities \(\frac{2}{3},\frac{1}{3}\) , and player II chooses \(l\) for sure and mixes \(a,b\) with probabilities \(\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}\) . This profile of behavior strategies is an SPE.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这个较小的博弈显示在图10.15的右侧。因为这个小博弈具有完美信息，所以通过逆向归纳法可以找到一个子博弈完美均衡，这里的逆向归纳法意味着参与者II肯定会选择 \(l\) 。在最终的整体行为策略中，参与者I以概率 \(\frac{2}{3},\frac{1}{3}\) 混合 \(T,B\) ，参与者II肯定会选择 \(l\) ，并以概率 \(\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}\) 混合 \(a,b\) 。这个行为策略组合是一个子博弈完美均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_304_197_216_1249_465_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 10.15 The subgame of the game in Figure 10.14, and its unique mixed equilibrium with payoffs 2 and 4. In the full game, the subgame is replaced by these payoffs, as shown on the right, so that the optimal move of player II at the root is \(l\) . This defines the SPE.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图10.15 图10.14中博弈的子博弈，以及其唯一的混合均衡，收益为2和4。在整个博弈中，如图右侧所示，子博弈被这些收益所替代，因此参与者II在根节点的最优行动是 \(l\) 。这就定义了子博弈完美均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

The step of computing an equilibrium of a subgame and substituting the resulting equilibrium payoffs for the subgame generalizes the backward induction step in games with perfect information. In games with perfect information, this backward induction step amounts to finding an optimal move of a single player in a very simple subgame where each move leads directly to a payoff. The optimal move defines an equilibrium in that subgame. In a game with imperfect information, the subgame may involve more than one player, and the equilibrium is usually more difficult to find.

\begin{tcolorbox}
计算子博弈的均衡并用得到的均衡收益替代子博弈这一步骤，推广了具有完美信息博弈中的逆向归纳步骤。在具有完美信息的博弈中，这个逆向归纳步骤相当于在一个非常简单的子博弈中找到单个参与者的最优行动，在这个子博弈中每个行动都直接导致一个收益。最优行动定义了该子博弈中的一个均衡。在具有不完美信息的博弈中，子博弈可能涉及多个参与者，并且通常更难找到均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

The described SPE of the extensive game in Figure 10.10 is the mixed-strategy equilibrium \(\left( {\left( {\frac{2}{3},\frac{1}{3}}\right) ,\left( {\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2},0}\right) }\right)\) of the reduced strategic form, which also defines a pair of behavior strategies. Another equilibrium is the pair \(\left( {B,r * }\right)\) of reduced pure strategies. The strategy \(r *\) is only a reduced behavior strategy for player II, because it does not specify the probabilities for the moves \(a\) and \(b\) at her second information set (which is unreachable when she makes move \(r\) ). However, this equilibrium is not subgame-perfect, because we have already determined the SPE, which in this game is unique.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图10.10中扩展式博弈所描述的子博弈完美均衡是精简策略型的混合策略均衡 \(\left( {\left( {\frac{2}{3},\frac{1}{3}}\right) ,\left( {\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2},0}\right) }\right)\) ，它也定义了一对行为策略。另一个均衡是精简纯策略对 \(\left( {B,r * }\right)\) 。策略 \(r *\) 只是参与者II的一个精简行为策略，因为它没有指定她在第二个信息集(当她采取行动 \(r\) 时该信息集无法到达)的行动 \(a\) 和 \(b\) 的概率。然而，这个均衡不是子博弈完美均衡，因为我们已经确定了该博弈中唯一的子博弈完美均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

In general, just as for games with perfect information, an equilibrium of an extensive game can only be called subgame-perfect if each player's behavior (or pure) strategy is not reduced, but is fully specified and defines a probability distribution (or deterministic move) for each information set of the player.

\begin{tcolorbox}
一般来说，就像具有完美信息的博弈一样，只有当每个参与者的行为(或纯)策略不是精简的，而是完全确定的，并且为参与者的每个信息集定义了一个概率分布(或确定性行动)时，扩展式博弈的一个均衡才能被称为子博弈完美均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

We described for the example in Figure 10.14 how to find an SPE, which generalizes the backward induction process. We describe this generally in the proof of the following theorem.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们针对图10.14中的例子描述了如何找到子博弈完美均衡，这推广了逆向归纳过程。我们将在下面定理的证明中对其进行一般性描述。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 10.7. Any extensive game with perfect recall has an SPE in behavior strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理10.7。任何具有完美记忆的扩展式博弈在行为策略中都存在子博弈完美均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. If the game tree consists of a single node, which is simultaneously the root of the game tree and its only leaf with a payoff to each player, then the theorem holds trivially because every player has only one strategy (doing nothing) and this defines an equilibrium of the game, which is also trivially subgame-perfect.

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。如果博弈树仅由一个节点组成，该节点同时是博弈树的根节点和唯一的叶节点，且每个玩家都有一个收益，那么该定理显然成立，因为每个玩家只有一种策略(什么都不做)，这就定义了博弈的一个均衡，并且这个均衡显然也是子博弈完美均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

Hence, assume that the game tree does not just consist of a single node. A SPE of the game is then found inductively as as follows. First, find a subgame of the game with root \(u\) that is not just a leaf and that has itself no further nontrivial subgames. Such a subgame exists, because if this does not hold for the root of the entire game, then consider a nontrivial subgame with root \(u\) instead, and continue in that manner until a subgame that has no further subgames is found.

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此，假设博弈树不仅仅由一个节点组成。然后按如下方式归纳地找到该博弈的一个子博弈完美均衡(SPE)。首先，找到该博弈的一个以 \(u\) 为根节点的子博弈，该子博弈不仅仅是一个叶节点，并且它自身没有进一步的非平凡子博弈。这样的子博弈是存在的，因为如果整个博弈的根节点不满足这个条件，那么考虑一个以 \(u\) 为根节点的非平凡子博弈，然后继续这样操作，直到找到一个没有进一步子博弈的子博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

Second, consider an equilibrium of this subgame, which exists by Nash's Theorem 6.5, and which can be represented using behavior strategies by Kuhn's Theorem 10.4. This is an SPE of the subgame because the subgame has itself no subgames. In the entire game, replace the root \(u\) of the subgame with a leaf whose payoffs are the expected payoffs in the equilibrium of the subgame. The resulting game tree then has fewer nodes, so that this process can be repeated, until \(u\) is the root of the original game. The resulting behavior strategies are then combined to define a profile of behavior strategies for the original game that by construction is subgame-perfect.

\begin{tcolorbox}
其次，考虑这个子博弈的一个均衡，根据纳什定理6.5，这个均衡是存在的，并且根据库恩定理10.4，可以用行为策略来表示。这是该子博弈的一个子博弈完美均衡，因为这个子博弈自身没有子博弈。在整个博弈中，用一个叶节点替换该子博弈的根节点 \(u\) ，该叶节点的收益是子博弈均衡中的期望效用。这样得到的博弈树的节点数就会减少，因此这个过程可以重复进行，直到 \(u\) 是原博弈的根节点。然后将得到的行为策略组合起来，为原博弈定义一个行为策略组合，根据构造，这个组合是子博弈完美的。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_305_390_1273_1145_551_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 10.16 Left: Zero-sum game, with payoffs to player I, and its nontrivial sub-games. Right: Strategic form of the left subgame, and its optimal mixed strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
图10.16 左:零和博弈，显示玩家I的收益，以及它的非平凡子博弈。右:左子博弈的策略形式，以及它的最优混合策略。
\end{tcolorbox}

We illustrate the use of subgames for the example in Figure 10.16, which is a zero-sum game; payoffs are to player I. The game has two nontrivial subgames, each of which starts with a move of player II. In the right subgame, in an SPE player II as the minimizer clearly chooses move \(c\) with payoff 0 to player II. The left subgame is a \(2 \times  2\) game with the strategic form shown on the right in Figure 10.16. This game has a unique mixed equilibrium, found with the "difference trick" as \(\left( {\left( {\frac{3}{5},\frac{2}{5}}\right) ,\left( {\frac{1}{5},\frac{4}{5}}\right) }\right)\) and payoff \(\frac{12}{5} = {2.4}\) to player I, and -2.4 to player II. It describes the optimal behavior of the players in the left subgame, and is part of a behavior strategy for the whole game. At the root of the game tree, player I will choose \(L\) , as this gives him the higher payoff 2.4, which is the unique value of the game because the game is zero-sum.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们以图10.16中的例子来说明子博弈的使用，这是一个零和博弈；收益是针对玩家I的。该博弈有两个非平凡子博弈，每个子博弈都从玩家II的一次行动开始。在右子博弈中，在一个子博弈完美均衡中，作为最小值点的玩家II显然会选择行动 \(c\) ，此时玩家II的收益为0。左子博弈是一个 \(2 \times  2\) 博弈，其策略形式如图10.16右侧所示。这个博弈有一个唯一的混合均衡，使用“差分技巧”得到为 \(\left( {\left( {\frac{3}{5},\frac{2}{5}}\right) ,\left( {\frac{1}{5},\frac{4}{5}}\right) }\right)\) ，玩家I的收益为 \(\frac{12}{5} = {2.4}\) ，玩家II的收益为 -2.4。它描述了玩家在左子博弈中的最优行为，并且是整个博弈行为策略的一部分。在博弈树的根节点，玩家I会选择 \(L\) ，因为这会给他带来更高的收益2.4，由于该博弈是零和博弈，这是该博弈的唯一值。
\end{tcolorbox}

It is also possible to find all equilibria of this game, including those that are not subgame-perfect, by considering the game tree and without creating the strategic form. Because the game is zero-sum, its payoff is the max-min payoff to each player in any equilibrium, and therefore 2.4 for player I and -2.4 for player II. If \(R\) is played with positive probability, then the minimizing player II has to play \(c\) because otherwise her expected payoff would be lower. With player II choosing \(c\) , this would lower player I’s payoff compared to playing \(L\) and then the max-min strategy that mixes \(S\) and \(T\) as described above. Hence, \(L\) is played for certain in any equilibrium, with player I's behavior strategy as described before. However, this means that the right decision node of player II is never reached, and player II can mix between \(c\) and \(d\) as long as the expected payoff to player I does not exceed 2.4, that is, as long as \(\operatorname{prob}\left( d\right)  \leq  {0.6}\) . This behavior at the right node, plus the behavior for mixing \(a,b\) with probabilities \(\frac{1}{5},\frac{4}{5}\) as described above, is the set of behavior strategies of player II in an arbitrary equilibrium of the game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
也可以通过考虑博弈树而不构建策略形式来找到这个博弈的所有均衡，包括那些不是子博弈完美的均衡。因为该博弈是零和博弈，在任何均衡中，它的收益是每个玩家的极大极小收益，因此玩家I的收益是2.4，玩家II的收益是 -2.4。如果以正概率选择 \(R\) ，那么作为最小化者的玩家II必须选择 \(c\) ，否则她的期望效用会更低。如果玩家II选择 \(c\) ，与选择 \(L\) 然后采用上述混合 \(S\) 和 \(T\) 的极大极小策略相比，这会降低玩家I的收益。因此，在任何均衡中一定会选择 \(L\) ，玩家I的行为策略如前所述。然而，这意味着玩家II的右决策节点永远不会被到达，玩家II可以在 \(c\) 和 \(d\) 之间进行混合，只要玩家I的期望效用不超过2.4，即只要 \(\operatorname{prob}\left( d\right)  \leq  {0.6}\) 。在右节点的这种行为，加上上述以概率 \(\frac{1}{5},\frac{4}{5}\) 混合 \(a,b\) 的行为，就是该博弈任意均衡中玩家II的行为策略集。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{10.11 Further Reading}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{10.11 进一步阅读}
\end{tcolorbox}

The example of the competition between software firms in Section 10.2 is adapted from Turocy and von Stengel (2002).

\begin{tcolorbox}
10.2节中软件公司之间竞争的例子改编自图罗西和冯·施滕格尔(2002年)的研究。
\end{tcolorbox}

Information sets were introduced by Harold Kuhn (1953). Realization equivalent strategies are just called "equivalent" by Kuhn. His Definition 17 states that player \(i\) has perfect recall if for every pure strategy \(\pi\) of player \(i\) and every information set \(h\) of player \(i\) that is reachable ("relevant") when playing \(\pi\) , all nodes in \(h\) are reachable ("possible") when playing \(\pi\) . This is equivalent to our Definition 10.1 of perfect recall, which we have adapted from Selten (1975, p. 27).

\begin{tcolorbox}
信息集(information set)由哈罗德·库恩(Harold Kuhn，1953 年)引入。库恩将实现等价策略简称为“等价”策略。他的定义 17 指出，如果对于玩家 \(i\) 的每个纯策略 \(\pi\) 以及玩家 \(i\) 的每个在采用 \(\pi\) 策略时可达(“相关”)的信息集 \(h\) ，在采用 \(\pi\) 策略时， \(h\) 中的所有节点都是可达(“可能”)的，那么玩家 \(i\) 具有完美记忆(perfect recall)。这与我们从塞尔滕(Selten，1975 年，第 27 页)改编而来的完美记忆的定义 10.1 等价。
\end{tcolorbox}

The concept of a reduced behavior strategy is not standard, but we have introduced it here because it corresponds to a behavior strategy in the same way as a reduced pure strategy corresponds to a pure strategy.

\begin{tcolorbox}
约简行为策略(reduced behavior strategy)的概念并非标准概念，但我们在此引入它，是因为它与行为策略的对应关系，就如同约简纯策略与纯策略的对应关系一样。
\end{tcolorbox}

Other textbook treatments of extensive games with imperfect information are Osborne and Rubinstein (1994, Chapter 11) and Gibbons (1992, Section 2.4).

\begin{tcolorbox}
其他关于不完美信息扩展式博弈的教科书处理方式可参考奥斯本和鲁宾斯坦(Osborne and Rubinstein，1994 年，第 11 章)以及吉本斯(Gibbons，1992 年，第 2.4 节)的著作。
\end{tcolorbox}

Our proof of Kuhn's Theorem 10.4 is based on sequences of moves. These sequences play a central role in the sequence form of von Stengel (1996). This is a strategic description that instead of pure strategies considers sequences \(\sigma\) , which are played with probabilities \(\mu \left\lbrack  \sigma \right\rbrack\) that fulfill the equations (10.6) for all \(h \in  {H}_{i}\) and (10.5). Whereas the strategic form may be exponentially larger than the extensive game, the sequence form has the same size. A zero-sum game, for example, can then be solved with the help of a linear program of the same size as the game tree.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们对库恩定理 10.4 的证明基于行动序列。这些序列在冯·施滕格尔(von Stengel，1996 年)的序列形式(sequence form)中起着核心作用。这是一种策略性描述，它不考虑纯策略，而是考虑序列 \(\sigma\) ，这些序列以概率 \(\mu \left\lbrack  \sigma \right\rbrack\) 被采用，且对于所有 \(h \in  {H}_{i}\) 满足方程(10.6)和(10.5)。虽然策略形式可能比扩展式博弈大指数倍，但序列形式的规模与扩展式博弈相同。例如，一个零和博弈(zero - sum game)可以借助与博弈树(game tree)规模相同的线性规划来求解。
\end{tcolorbox}

The Monty Hall problem was posed in Selvin (1975). Its behavioral assumptions are discussed and tested in Friedman (1998). Even though our game-theoretic analysis suggests that "not switching doors" is very justifiable when Monty Hall does not always offer a switch, this is still considered a "choice anomaly".

\begin{tcolorbox}
蒙提·霍尔问题由塞尔文(Selvin，1975 年)提出。弗里德曼(Friedman，1998 年)对其行为假设进行了讨论和检验。尽管我们的博弈论分析表明，当蒙提·霍尔并非总是提供换门选项时，“不换门”是非常合理的选择，但这仍然被视为一种“选择异常”。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{10.12 Exercises for Chapter 10}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{10.12 第 10 章习题}
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 10.1. Explain the two ways, with either player I or player II moving first, of representing an \(m \times  n\) bimatrix game as an extensive game that has the given game as its strategic form. Giving an example may be helpful. In both cases, how many decision nodes do the players have? What is the number of terminal nodes of the game tree? Why do we need information sets here?

\begin{tcolorbox}
习题 10.1. 解释将一个 \(m \times  n\) 双矩阵博弈(bimatrix game)表示为扩展式博弈的两种方式，即玩家 I 先行动或玩家 II 先行动，使得该扩展式博弈的策略形式就是给定的双矩阵博弈。举例说明可能会有所帮助。在这两种情况下，玩家各有多少个决策节点(decision node)？博弈树的终端节点(terminal node)数量是多少？为什么这里需要信息集？
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 10.2. Which of the following extensive games have perfect recall and if not, why not? For each extensive game with perfect recall, find all its equilibria in mixed (including pure) strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
习题 10.2. 以下哪些扩展式博弈具有完美记忆？如果不具有，原因是什么？对于每个具有完美记忆的扩展式博弈，找出其所有混合策略(包括纯策略)均衡(equilibrium)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_307_334_1232_1260_726_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Exercise 10.3. Which of the following extensive games have perfect recall and if not, why not? For each extensive game with perfect recall, find all its equilibria in mixed (including pure) strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
习题 10.3. 以下哪些扩展式博弈具有完美记忆？如果不具有，原因是什么？对于每个具有完美记忆的扩展式博弈，找出其所有混合策略(包括纯策略)均衡。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_308_197_216_1246_590_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Exercise 10.4. Consider the following zero-sum game, a simplified version of Poker adapted from Kuhn (1950). A deck has three cards (of rank High, Middle, and Low), and each player is dealt a card. All deals are equally likely, and of course the players get different cards. A player does not know the card dealt to the other player. After seeing his hand, player I has the option to Raise(R)or Fold(F). When he folds, he loses one unit to player II. When he raises, player II has the option to meet(m)or pass(p). When player II chooses "pass", she has to pay one unit to player I. When player II chooses "meet", the higher card wins, and the player with the lower card has to pay two units to the winning player.

\begin{tcolorbox}
习题 10.4. 考虑以下零和博弈，这是一个改编自库恩(Kuhn，1950 年)的简化版扑克(Poker)游戏。一副牌有三张牌(高、中、低等级)，每个玩家发一张牌。所有发牌情况的可能性相同，当然玩家拿到的牌不同。玩家不知道对方拿到的牌。玩家 I 看到自己的牌后，有加注(R)或弃牌(F)的选项。当他弃牌时，他输给玩家 II 一个单位。当他加注时，玩家 II 有跟注(m)或弃牌(p)的选项。当玩家 II 选择“弃牌”时，她要付给玩家 I 一个单位。当玩家 II 选择“跟注”时，牌面大的玩家获胜，牌面小的玩家要付给获胜玩家两个单位。
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) Draw a game in extensive form that models this game, with information sets, and payoffs to player I at the leaves.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 绘制一个具有信息集的扩展式博弈来模拟这个游戏，并在叶节点(leaf)标注玩家 I 的收益。
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) Simplify this game by assuming that at an information set where a player's move is always at least as good as the other move, no matter what the other player's move or the chance move were or will be, then the player will choose that move. Draw the simplified extensive form game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) 通过假设在某个信息集上，无论另一个玩家的行动或随机行动是什么，一个玩家的某个行动至少和另一个行动一样好，那么该玩家会选择那个行动，来简化这个游戏。绘制简化后的扩展式博弈。
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) What does the simplification in (b) have to do with weakly dominated strategies? Why is the simplification legitimate here?

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) (b) 中的简化与弱被占优策略(weakly dominated strategy)有什么关系？为什么这里的简化是合理的？
\end{tcolorbox}

(d) Give the strategic form of the simplified game from (b) and find an equilibrium of that game. What are the behavior probabilities of the players in that equilibrium? What is the unique payoff to the players in equilibrium?

\begin{tcolorbox}
(d) 给出 (b) 中简化后游戏的策略形式，并找出该游戏的一个均衡。在该均衡中，玩家的行为概率是多少？均衡时玩家的唯一收益是多少？
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 10.5. For an extensive game with perfect recall, show that the information set that contains the root (starting node) of any subgame is always a singleton (that is, contains only that node). [Hint: Use Proposition 10.2.]

\begin{tcolorbox}
习题 10.5. 对于一个具有完美记忆的扩展式博弈，证明包含任何子博弈(subgame)的根节点(起始节点)的信息集总是单元素集(即只包含该节点)。[提示:使用命题 10.2。]
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 10.6. Find all equilibria in (pure or) mixed strategies of this extensive game. Which of them are subgame-perfect?

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习10.6。找出这个扩展式博弈的所有(纯策略或)混合策略均衡。其中哪些是子博弈完美均衡？
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_309_942_210_652_1243_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Exercise 10.7. Consider this three-player game in extensive form. At a terminal node, the top, middle, and bottom payoffs are to player1,2,3, respectively. Find all subgame-perfect equilibria (SPE) in mixed strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习10.7。考虑这个扩展式三人博弈。在终端节点处，顶部、中部和底部的收益分别属于玩家1、2、3。找出所有混合策略的子博弈完美均衡(SPE)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Exercise 10.8. Consider this three-player game in extensive form. Find all subgame-perfect equilibria (SPE) in mixed strategies.

\begin{tcolorbox}
练习10.8。考虑这个扩展式三人博弈。找出所有混合策略的子博弈完美均衡(SPE)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.5\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_309_952_1473_650_617_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

\section*{11}

\section*{Bargaining}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{讨价还价}
\end{tcolorbox}

This chapter presents models of bargaining. Bargaining occurs in real life between the buyer and seller of a house, for example. This is a game-theoretic problem because it is useful to think about the situation of the other player. Both players have something to gain by reaching an agreement.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本章介绍讨价还价模型。讨价还价在现实生活中很常见，比如房屋买卖双方之间的讨价还价。这是一个博弈论问题，因为考虑对方的处境很有必要。双方都能通过达成协议获得收益。
\end{tcolorbox}

A basic bargaining model is that of "splitting a pie" in a way that is acceptable to both players, but where within that range a player can only gain at the expense of the other. The question is how to split the pie fairly. In his undergraduate thesis, later published in Nash (1950), John Nash proposed a system of "axioms", or natural conditions, for arriving at a fair "bargaining solution". Nash showed that these axioms always entail a unique solution, which is also easy to calculate. Section 11.2 shows how a bargaining set arises from a bimatrix game, here a variant of the Prisoner's Dilemma, where the players can benefit from cooperation. General bargaining sets and corresponding axioms are presented in Sections 11.3 and 11.4, with a geometric characterization of the bargaining solution in Section 11.5.

\begin{tcolorbox}
一个基本的讨价还价模型是“分馅饼”，要以双方都能接受的方式进行分配，但在这个范围内，一方的收益必然意味着另一方的损失。问题是如何公平地分割馅饼。约翰·纳什(John Nash)在他后来发表于《纳什(1950)》的本科论文中，提出了一套“公理”，即自然条件，以达成公平的“讨价还价解”。纳什证明，这些公理总能得出唯一解，而且这个解也很容易计算。第11.2节展示了如何从双矩阵博弈(这里是囚徒困境的一个变体)中产生讨价还价集，在这个博弈中，玩家可以从合作中受益。第11.3节和第11.4节介绍了一般的讨价还价集和相应的公理，第11.5节对讨价还价解进行了几何刻画。
\end{tcolorbox}

Nash's bargaining model is not a non-cooperative game in strategic or extensive form that we have considered so far. Rather, it belongs to cooperative game theory, where players are assumed to be able to agree in some way and then adhere to their agreement. The model describes which agreements are possible and how the players benefit from them, but does not describe individual actions that lead to this agreement, nor an enforcement mechanism. The typical approach of cooperative game theory is to propose solutions to the game (ideally a unique solution, such as a specific agreement) that have to fulfill certain axioms. The Nash bargaining solution is of this kind.

\begin{tcolorbox}
纳什的讨价还价模型不是我们目前所考虑的策略式或扩展式非合作博弈。相反，它属于合作博弈论，在该理论中，假设玩家能够以某种方式达成协议，然后遵守协议。该模型描述了哪些协议是可行的，以及玩家如何从中受益，但没有描述达成该协议的个体行动，也没有描述执行机制。合作博弈论的典型方法是提出满足某些公理的博弈解(理想情况下是唯一解，如特定协议)。纳什讨价还价解就是这种类型。
\end{tcolorbox}

The later sections of this chapter go back to a non-cooperative bargaining model, due to Rubinstein (1982). This model uses a game tree with perfect information where the players make alternating offers that the other player can accept or reject. The analysis of this game uses the study of commitment games from Section 4.7. The most basic form, described in Section 11.7, is the "Ultimatum game" that consists of a single take-it-or-leave-it offer which can only be accepted or rejected.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本章后面的章节回到了鲁宾斯坦(Rubinstein，1982)提出的非合作讨价还价模型。该模型使用具有完美信息的博弈树，玩家轮流提出报价，另一方可以接受或拒绝。对这个博弈的分析运用了第4.7节中对承诺博弈的研究。第11.7节描述的最基本形式是“最后通牒博弈”，它由一个要么接受要么放弃的单一报价组成，对方只能选择接受或拒绝。
\end{tcolorbox}

With alternating offers over several rounds, described in Sections 11.8 and 11.9, there is a chance at each stage that the game does not proceed to the next round so that players wind up with nothing because they could not agree. The effect of this is a "discount factor" which quantifies to what extent an earlier agreement is better that a later one. By thinking ahead, the optimal strategies of the players define sequences of alternating offers, with agreement in the first round. This can be done with a fixed number of rounds, or with "stationary strategies" for an infinite number of bargaining rounds where, in essence, the game repeats every two rounds when it comes back to the same player to make an offer (see Section 11.10). The nice conclusion of this alternating-offers bargaining model, shown in Section 11.11, is that a discount factor that represents very patient players leads to the same outcome as Nash's axiomatic bargaining solution. Nash's "cooperative" solution concept is thereby justified with a model of self-interested behavior.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在第11.8节和第11.9节描述的多轮交替报价中，每一轮都有可能因为双方无法达成协议而导致博弈无法进入下一轮，最终双方一无所获。这产生了一个“贴现因子”，它量化了早期达成协议比后期达成协议好的程度。通过前瞻性思考，玩家的最优策略定义了交替报价的序列，并在第一轮就达成协议。这可以在固定轮数的情况下实现，也可以通过“平稳策略”在无限轮次的讨价还价中实现，本质上，当轮到同一玩家报价时，博弈每两轮重复一次(见第11.10节)。第11.11节展示的这个交替报价讨价还价模型的一个很好的结论是，代表非常有耐心的玩家的贴现因子会导致与纳什公理讨价还价解相同的结果。这样，纳什的“合作”解概念就可以通过自利行为模型得到证明。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{11.1 Prerequisites and Learning Outcomes}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{11.1 先决条件和学习成果}
\end{tcolorbox}

Prerequisites for this chapter are: two-player games in strategic form (Chapter 3), the concept that utility functions admit positive-affine transformations (Section 5.6), concave utility functions (Section 5.9), commitment games (Section 4.7), and max-min strategies (Section 8.2).

\begin{tcolorbox}
本章的先决条件包括:策略式两人博弈(第3章)、效用函数允许正仿射变换的概念(第5.6节)、凹效用函数(第5.9节)、承诺博弈(第4.7节)和极大极小策略(第8.2节)。
\end{tcolorbox}

After studying this chapter, you should be able to:

\begin{tcolorbox}
学习本章后，你应该能够:
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item explain the concepts of bargaining sets, bargaining axioms, Nash bargaining solution, threat point, and Nash product;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 解释讨价还价集、讨价还价公理、纳什讨价还价解、威胁点和纳什积的概念；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item draw bargaining sets derived from bimatrix games, and for bargaining over a "unit pie" when players have concave utility functions;
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 绘制从双矩阵博弈导出的讨价还价集，以及当玩家具有凹效用函数时对“单位馅饼”进行讨价还价的讨价还价集；
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{itemize}
\item explain, and solve specific examples of, the model of bargaining over multiple rounds of demands and counter-demands, and of stationary SPE for infinitely many rounds.
\end{itemize}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\begin{itemize}
\item 解释并解决多轮需求和反需求的讨价还价模型的具体例子，以及无限轮次的平稳子博弈完美均衡。
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{11.2 Bargaining Sets}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{11.2 讨价还价集}
\end{tcolorbox}

Consider the variant of the Prisoner's Dilemma game on the left in Figure 11.1. Player I’s strategy \(B\) dominates \(T\) , and \(r\) dominates \(l\) , so that the unique equilibrium of the game is(B, r)with payoff 1 to both players. The outcome(T, l)would give payoffs 2 and 3 to players I and II, which is clearly better.

\begin{tcolorbox}
考虑图11.1左侧的囚徒困境博弈的变体。玩家I的策略 \(B\) 优于 \(T\) ，且 \(r\) 优于 \(l\) ，因此该博弈的唯一均衡是(B，r)，两个玩家的收益均为1。结果(T，l)将使玩家I和玩家II的收益分别为2和3，这显然更好。
\end{tcolorbox}

Suppose now that the players can talk to each other and have agreed on a certain course of action that they intend to follow. We do not model how the players can talk, agree, or enforce their agreement, but instead try to give guidelines about which agreement they should reach.

\begin{tcolorbox}
现在假设玩家可以相互交流，并就他们打算遵循的某种行动方案达成了一致。我们不建模玩家如何交流、达成协议或执行他们的协议，而是试图给出他们应该达成何种协议的指导原则。
\end{tcolorbox}

The first step is to model what players can achieve by an agreement, by describing a "bargaining set" of possible payoff pairs that they can bargain over. Suppose that the players can agree to play any of the cells of the payoff table in Figure 11.1. The resulting utility pairs(u, v)are points in a two-dimensional diagram with horizontal coordinate \(u\) (utility to player I) and vertical coordinate \(v\) (utility to player II). These points are shown on the right in Figure 11.1, marked with the respective strategy pairs. For example, \(T,l\) marks the utility pair(2,3).

\begin{tcolorbox}
第一步是通过描述一个玩家可以讨价还价的可能收益对的“讨价还价集”，来建模玩家通过协议可以实现的目标。假设玩家可以就图11.1收益表中的任何单元格的策略达成一致。由此产生的效用对(u，v)是二维图中的点，其中横坐标 \(u\) (玩家I的效用)，纵坐标 \(v\) (玩家II的效用)。这些点显示在图11.1的右侧，用相应的策略对标记。例如， \(T,l\) 标记效用对(2，3)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_312_186_677_1245_606_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 11.1 Left: \(2 \times  2\) game similar to the Prisoner’s Dilemma. Right: corresponding bargaining set \(S\) (dark gray), and its bargaining solution(U, V).

\begin{tcolorbox}
图11.1 左侧: \(2 \times  2\) 类似于囚徒困境的博弈。右侧:相应的讨价还价集 \(S\) (深灰色)及其讨价还价解(U，V)。
\end{tcolorbox}

The convex hull (set of convex combinations) of these utility pairs is shown in light and dark gray in Figure 11.1. Any such convex combination is obtained by a joint lottery that the players have decided to play as a possible result of their agreement. For example, the utility pair(1.5,2)would be the expected utility if the players have decided to toss a fair coin, and, depending on the outcome, to either play(T, l)or else(B, r); we assume that this coin-toss and subsequent joint action is part of the players' agreement and they will follow through with it.

\begin{tcolorbox}
这些效用对的凸包(凸组合的集合)在图11.1中以浅灰色和深灰色显示。任何这样的凸组合都是通过玩家作为协议的可能结果而决定进行的联合彩票获得的。例如，如果玩家决定抛一枚公平的硬币，并根据结果选择玩(T，l)或(B，r)，那么效用对(1.5，2)将是期望效用；我们假设这次抛硬币以及随后的联合行动是玩家协议的一部分，并且他们会执行该协议。
\end{tcolorbox}

Making the bargaining set convex with the help of lotteries is not as strange as it sounds. For example, if two people find something that both would like to have but that cannot be divided, tossing a coin to decide who gets it is often accepted as a fair solution.

\begin{tcolorbox}
借助彩票使讨价还价集成为凸集并不像听起来那么奇怪。例如，如果两个人发现了一件他们都想要但无法分割的东西，抛硬币决定谁得到它通常被认为是一种公平的解决方案。
\end{tcolorbox}

Not every point in the convex hull is "reasonable" because we always assume that the players have the option not to agree to it in advance. For example, player I would not agree that both players choose with certainty the strategy pair(T, r), where player I gets payoff zero. The reason is that without any agreement, player I can use his max-min strategy \(B\) that guarantees him a payoff of 1 irrespective of what the other player does. As shown in Chapter 8 on zero-sum games, a max-min strategy may be a mixed strategy; in the Prisoner's Dilemma game in Figure 11.1 it is a pure strategy. We ask that every agreed outcome of the bargaining situation should give each player at least his max-min payoff (which is, as usual, defined in terms of that player's own payoffs).

\begin{tcolorbox}
并非凸包中的每个点都是“合理的”，因为我们总是假设玩家有权事先不同意某个点。例如，玩家I不会同意两个玩家确定地选择策略对(T，r)，在这种情况下玩家I的收益为零。原因是在没有任何协议的情况下，玩家I可以使用他的极大极小策略 \(B\) ，无论另一个玩家做什么，该策略都能保证他获得1的收益。正如第8章关于零和博弈中所示，极大极小策略可能是一种混合策略；在图11.1的囚徒困境博弈中，它是一种纯策略。我们要求讨价还价情况的每个商定结果都应至少给每个玩家提供他的极大极小收益(通常根据该玩家自己的收益来定义)。
\end{tcolorbox}

The bargaining set \(S\) derived from a two-player game in strategic form is thus defined as the convex hull of utility pairs (as specified in the game), with the additional constraint that \(u \geq  {u}_{0}\) and \(v \geq  {v}_{0}\) for all(u, v)in \(S\) , where \({u}_{0}\) is the max-min payoff to player I and \({v}_{0}\) is the max-min payoff to player II. With \({u}_{0} = {v}_{0} = 1\) , the resulting set \(S\) is shown in dark gray in Figure 11.1.

\begin{tcolorbox}
因此，从策略形式的两人博弈中导出的讨价还价集 \(S\) 被定义为效用对的凸包(如博弈中所指定)，并附加约束条件:对于 \(S\) 中的所有(u，v)，有 \(u \geq  {u}_{0}\) 和 \(v \geq  {v}_{0}\) ，其中 \({u}_{0}\) 是玩家I的极大极小收益， \({v}_{0}\) 是玩家II的极大极小收益。通过 \({u}_{0} = {v}_{0} = 1\) ，得到的集合 \(S\) 在图11.1中以深灰色显示。
\end{tcolorbox}

In this example, the "Nash bargaining solution" (which is not to be confused with "Nash equilibrium", a very different concept) will give the utility pair \(\left( {U,V}\right)  = \left( {{2.5},{2.5}}\right)\) , as we will explain (the fact that \(U = V\) is a coincidence). This expected payoff pair is a convex combination of the utility pairs(2,3)and(5,0). It is achieved by a joint lottery that chooses(T, l)with probability \(\frac{5}{6}\) and(B, l)with probability \(\frac{1}{6}\) . In effect, player II always plays \(l\) and player I chooses between \(T\) and \(B\) with probabilities \(\frac{5}{6}\) and \(\frac{1}{6}\) . However, this is not a "mixed strategy" but rather a lottery performed by an independent device, with an outcome that both players have in advance decided to accept.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在这个例子中，“纳什讨价还价解”(不要将其与“纳什均衡”相混淆，这是一个截然不同的概念)将给出效用对 \(\left( {U,V}\right)  = \left( {{2.5},{2.5}}\right)\) ，我们稍后会解释( \(U = V\) 只是巧合)。这个期望效用对是效用对 (2,3) 和 (5,0) 的凸组合。它是通过一个联合彩票实现的，该彩票以概率 \(\frac{5}{6}\) 选择 (T, l) ，以概率 \(\frac{1}{6}\) 选择 (B, l) 。实际上，玩家 II 总是选择 \(l\) ，而玩家 I 以概率 \(\frac{5}{6}\) 和 \(\frac{1}{6}\) 在 \(T\) 和 \(B\) 之间进行选择。然而，这不是“混合策略”，而是由一个独立设备进行的彩票，其结果是两个玩家事先就决定接受的。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{11.3 Bargaining Axioms}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{11.3 讨价还价公理}
\end{tcolorbox}

We consider bargaining situations modeled as bargaining sets, and try to obtain a "solution" for any such situation. The conditions about this model are called axioms and concern both the bargaining set and the solution one wants to obtain.

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们将讨价还价情形建模为讨价还价集，并尝试为任何此类情形找到一个“解”。关于这个模型的条件被称为公理，它们既涉及讨价还价集，也涉及我们想要得到的解。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 11.1 (Axioms for bargaining set). A bargaining set \(S\) with threat point \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) is a nonempty subset of \({\mathbb{R}}^{2}\) so that

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义 11.1(讨价还价集的公理)。具有威胁点 \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) 的讨价还价集 \(S\) 是 \({\mathbb{R}}^{2}\) 的一个非空子集，使得
\end{tcolorbox}

(a) \(u \geq  {u}_{0},v \geq  {v}_{0}\) for all \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) ;

\begin{tcolorbox}
(a) 对于所有 \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) ， \(u \geq  {u}_{0},v \geq  {v}_{0}\) ；
\end{tcolorbox}

(b) \(S\) is compact;

\begin{tcolorbox}
(b) \(S\) 是紧致的；
\end{tcolorbox}

(c) \(S\) is convex.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(c) \(S\) 是凸的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Each pair(u, v)in a bargaining set \(S\) represents a utility \(u\) for player \(\mathrm{I}\) and a utility \(v\) for player II, which the players can achieve by reaching a specific agreement. At least one agreement is possible because \(S\) is nonempty. The threat point \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) is the pair of utilities that each player can (in expectation) guarantee for himself. Axiom (b) in Definition 11.1 states that \(S\) is bounded and closed, with the aim of defining a "bargaining solution" by some sort of maximization process. The convexity axiom (c) means that players have, if necessary, access to joint lotteries as part of their agreement.

\begin{tcolorbox}
讨价还价集 \(S\) 中的每一对 (u, v) 代表玩家 \(\mathrm{I}\) 的效用 \(u\) 和玩家 II 的效用 \(v\) ，玩家们可以通过达成特定协议来实现这些效用。因为 \(S\) 是非空的，所以至少有一种协议是可能的。威胁点 \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) 是每个玩家(在期望意义上)可以为自己保证的效用对。定义 11.1 中的公理 (b) 表明 \(S\) 是有界且闭的，其目的是通过某种最大化过程来定义一个“讨价还价解”。凸性公理 (c) 意味着，如果有必要，玩家们可以在他们的协议中使用联合彩票。
\end{tcolorbox}

In Section 11.2, we have constructed a bargaining set \(S\) from a bimatrix game (A, B). We show that this set fulfills the axioms in Definition 11.1. This is clear for (b) and (c). By construction,(a) holds, but we have to show that \(S\) is not empty. The threat point \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) is obtained with \({u}_{0}\) as the max-min payoff to player I (in terms of his payoffs) and \({v}_{0}\) as the max-min payoff to player II (in terms of her payoffs). Thus, if player I plays his max-min strategy \(\widehat{x}\) and player II plays her max-min strategy \(\widehat{y}\) , then the payoff pair \(\left( {u,v}\right)  = \left( {{\widehat{x}}^{\top }A\widehat{y},{\widehat{x}}^{\top }B\widehat{y}}\right)\) that results in the game is an element of \(S\) because \(u \geq  {u}_{0}\) and \(v \geq  {v}_{0}\) , so \(S\) is not empty.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在第 11.2 节中，我们从一个双矩阵博弈 (A, B) 构建了一个讨价还价集 \(S\) 。我们证明这个集合满足定义 11.1 中的公理。对于 (b) 和 (c) 这是显然的。根据构造，(a) 成立，但我们必须证明 \(S\) 是非空的。威胁点 \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) 是这样得到的: \({u}_{0}\) 是玩家 I 的极大极小收益(就他的收益而言)， \({v}_{0}\) 是玩家 II 的极大极小收益(就她的收益而言)。因此，如果玩家 I 采用他的极大极小策略 \(\widehat{x}\) ，玩家 II 采用她的极大极小策略 \(\widehat{y}\) ，那么博弈中得到的收益对 \(\left( {u,v}\right)  = \left( {{\widehat{x}}^{\top }A\widehat{y},{\widehat{x}}^{\top }B\widehat{y}}\right)\) 是 \(S\) 的一个元素，因为 \(u \geq  {u}_{0}\) 和 \(v \geq  {v}_{0}\) ，所以 \(S\) 是非空的。
\end{tcolorbox}

However, in the construction of a bargaining set from a bimatrix game, the threat point \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) need not itself be an element of \(S\) , as shown in Exercise 11.2(ii). For this reason, the threat point \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) is explicitly stated together with the bargaining set. If the threat point belongs to \(S\) and (a) holds, then there can be no other threat point. The axioms in Definition 11.2 below would be easier to state if the threat point was included in \(S\) , but then we would have to take additional steps to obtain such a bargaining set from a bimatrix game. In our examples and drawings we will typically consider sets \(S\) that include the threat point.

\begin{tcolorbox}
然而，在从双矩阵博弈(bimatrix game)构建讨价还价集时，威胁点 \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) 本身不必是 \(S\) 的元素，如练习11.2(ii)所示。出于这个原因，威胁点 \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) 会与讨价还价集一起明确给出。如果威胁点属于 \(S\) 且条件(a)成立，那么就不可能有其他威胁点。如果威胁点包含在 \(S\) 中，下面定义11.2中的公理表述起来会更简单，但那样我们就必须采取额外的步骤才能从双矩阵博弈中得到这样一个讨价还价集。在我们的示例和图示中，我们通常会考虑包含威胁点的集合 \(S\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

From now on, we simply assume that the bargaining set \(S\) fulfills these axioms. We will later consider bargaining sets for other situations, which are specified directly and not from a bimatrix game.

\begin{tcolorbox}
从现在起，我们简单地假设讨价还价集 \(S\) 满足这些公理。我们稍后会考虑其他情况的讨价还价集，这些情况是直接指定的，而非从双矩阵博弈得出。
\end{tcolorbox}

In the following definition, the axioms (d)-(h) continue (a)-(c) from Definition 11.1. We discuss them afterwards.

\begin{tcolorbox}
在下面的定义中，公理(d) - (h)是定义11.1中(a) - (c)的延续。我们随后会对它们进行讨论。
\end{tcolorbox}

Definition 11.2 (Axioms for bargaining solution). For a given bargaining set \(S\) with threat point \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) , a bargaining solution \(N\left( S\right)\) is a pair(U, V)so that

\begin{tcolorbox}
定义11.2(讨价还价解(bargaining solution)的公理)。对于给定的具有威胁点 \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) 的讨价还价集 \(S\) ，讨价还价解 \(N\left( S\right)\) 是一个二元组(U, V)，使得
\end{tcolorbox}

(d) \(\left( {U,V}\right)  \in  S\) ;

(e) the solution(U, V)is Pareto-optimal, that is, for all \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) , if \(u \geq  U\) and \(v \geq  V\) , then \(\left( {u,v}\right)  = \left( {U,V}\right)\) ;

\begin{tcolorbox}
(e) 解(U, V)是帕累托最优(Pareto - optimal)的，即对于所有 \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) ，如果 \(u \geq  U\) 且 \(v \geq  V\) ，那么 \(\left( {u,v}\right)  = \left( {U,V}\right)\) ；
\end{tcolorbox}

(f) it is invariant under utility scaling, that is, if \(a,c > 0\) and \(b,d \in  \mathbb{R}\) and \({S}^{\prime }\) is the bargaining set \(\{ \left( {{au} + b,{cv} + d}\right)  \mid  \left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\}\) with threat point \(\left( {a{u}_{0} + b,c{v}_{0} + d}\right)\) , then \(N\left( {S}^{\prime }\right)  = \left( {{aU} + b,{cV} + d}\right)\) ;

\begin{tcolorbox}
(f) 它具有效用缩放不变性(invariant under utility scaling)，即如果 \(a,c > 0\) 且 \(b,d \in  \mathbb{R}\) ，并且 \({S}^{\prime }\) 是具有威胁点 \(\left( {a{u}_{0} + b,c{v}_{0} + d}\right)\) 的讨价还价集 \(\{ \left( {{au} + b,{cv} + d}\right)  \mid  \left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\}\) ，那么 \(N\left( {S}^{\prime }\right)  = \left( {{aU} + b,{cV} + d}\right)\) ；
\end{tcolorbox}

(g) it preserves symmetry, that is, if \({u}_{0} = {v}_{0}\) and \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) implies \(\left( {v,u}\right)  \in  S\) , then \(U = V\)

\begin{tcolorbox}
(g) 它保持对称性，即如果 \({u}_{0} = {v}_{0}\) 且 \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) 意味着 \(\left( {v,u}\right)  \in  S\) ，那么 \(U = V\)
\end{tcolorbox}

(h) it is independent of irrelevant alternatives: If \(S,T\) are bargaining sets with the same threat point and \(S \subset  T\) , then either \(N\left( T\right)  \notin  S\) or \(N\left( T\right)  = N\left( S\right)\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(h) 它与无关选项(irrelevant alternatives)无关:如果 \(S,T\) 是具有相同威胁点的讨价还价集，且 \(S \subset  T\) ，那么要么 \(N\left( T\right)  \notin  S\) ，要么 \(N\left( T\right)  = N\left( S\right)\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The notation \(N\left( S\right)\) is short for "Nash bargaining solution". The solution is a pair(U, V)of utilities for the two players that belongs to the bargaining set \(S\) by axiom (d).

\begin{tcolorbox}
符号 \(N\left( S\right)\) 是“纳什讨价还价解(Nash bargaining solution)”的简写。根据公理(d)，该解是属于讨价还价集 \(S\) 的两个参与者的效用二元组(U, V)。
\end{tcolorbox}

The Pareto-optimality stated in (e) means that it should not be possible to improve the solution for one player without hurting the other player. Graphically, the solution is therefore on the upper-right (or "north-east") border of the bargaining set, which is also called the Pareto-frontier of the set.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(e)中所述的帕累托最优性意味着，不可能在不损害另一个参与者的情况下改善一个参与者的解。从图上看，解因此位于讨价还价集的右上角(或“东北”)边界上，该边界也称为该集合的帕累托前沿。
\end{tcolorbox}

Invariance under utility scaling as stated in (f) means the following. The utility function of a player can be changed by changing its "scale" and "origin" without changing its meaning, just like a temperature scale (see Chapter 5). Such a change for player I means that instead of \(u\) we consider \({au} + b\) where \(a\) and \(b\) are fixed real numbers and \(a > 0\) . Similarly, for player II we can replace \(v\) by \({cv} + d\) for fixed \(c,d\) and \(c > 0\) . When drawing the bargaining set \(S\) , this means that (by adding \(b\) ) the first coordinate of any point in that set is moved by the amount \(b\) (to the right or left depending on whether \(b\) is positive or negative), and stretched by the factor \(a\) (if \(a > 1\) , and shrunk if \(a < 1\) ). In (f), the set \({S}^{\prime }\) and the new threat point are obtained from \(S\) by applying such a "change of scale" for both players.

\begin{tcolorbox}
(f)中所述的效用缩放不变性意味着以下内容。玩家的效用函数可以通过改变其“刻度”和“原点”来改变，而不改变其含义，就像温度刻度一样(见第5章)。对于玩家I，这样的改变意味着我们考虑的不是 \(u\) ，而是 \({au} + b\) ，其中 \(a\) 和 \(b\) 是固定的实数，且 \(a > 0\) 。类似地，对于玩家II，我们可以用 \({cv} + d\) 替换 \(v\) ，其中 \(c,d\) 和 \(c > 0\) 是固定的。在绘制讨价还价集 \(S\) 时，这意味着(通过加上 \(b\) )该集合中任何点的第一个坐标移动了 \(b\) 的量(根据 \(b\) 的正负向右或向左移动)，并按 \(a\) 的因子拉伸(如果 \(a > 1\) )，或者收缩(如果 \(a < 1\) )。在(f)中，集合 \({S}^{\prime }\) 和新的威胁点是通过对两个玩家应用这样的“刻度变化”从 \(S\) 得到的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Because this scaling does not change the meaning of the utility values (they still represent the same preference), the bargaining solution should not be affected either, as stated in axiom (f). In a sense, this means the bargaining solution should not depend on a direct comparison of the players' utilities. For example, if you want to split a bar of chocolate with a friend you cannot claim more because you like chocolate "ten times more than she does" (which you could represent by changing your utility from \(u\) to \({10u}\) ), nor can she claim that you should get less because you already have 5 bars of chocolate in your drawer (represented by a change from \(u\) to \(u + 5\) ).

\begin{tcolorbox}
因为这种缩放不会改变效用值的含义(它们仍然代表相同的偏好)，所以如公理(f)所述，讨价还价解也不应受到影响。从某种意义上说，这意味着讨价还价解不应依赖于对玩家效用的直接比较。例如，如果你想和一个朋友分一块巧克力，你不能因为你“比她喜欢巧克力多十倍”就要求多分(你可以通过将你的效用从 \(u\) 改变为 \({10u}\) 来表示)，她也不能因为你抽屉里已经有5块巧克力就要求你少分(通过从 \(u\) 改变为 \(u + 5\) 来表示)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Preservation of symmetry as stated in (g) means that a symmetric bargaining set \(S\) , where for all(u, v)we have \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S \Leftrightarrow  \left( {v,u}\right)  \in  S\) , and with a threat point of the form \(\left( {{u}_{0},{u}_{0}}\right)\) , should also have a bargaining solution of the form \(N\left( S\right)  = \left( {U,U}\right)\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(g)中所述的对称性保持意味着，一个对称的讨价还价集 \(S\) (对于所有的(u, v)，我们有 \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S \Leftrightarrow  \left( {v,u}\right)  \in  S\) )，且威胁点的形式为 \(\left( {{u}_{0},{u}_{0}}\right)\) ，其讨价还价解的形式也应该为 \(N\left( S\right)  = \left( {U,U}\right)\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The independence of irrelevant alternatives stated in (h) is the most complicated axiom. As illustrated in Figure 11.2, it means that if a bargaining set \(S\) is enlarged to a larger set \(T\) that has additional points(u, v), but without changing the threat point, then either \(T\) has a bargaining solution \(N\left( T\right)\) that is one of these new points, or else it is the same solution as the solution \(N\left( S\right)\) . It is not allowed that the solution for \(T\) moves to a different point, like \(P\) in Figure 11.2, that still belongs to \(S\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
(h)中所述的无关选项独立性是最复杂的公理。如图11.2所示，这意味着如果一个讨价还价集 \(S\) 扩展为一个更大的集合 \(T\) ，该集合有额外的点(u, v)，但不改变威胁点，那么要么 \(T\) 的讨价还价解 \(N\left( T\right)\) 是这些新点之一，要么它与解 \(N\left( S\right)\) 相同。不允许 \(T\) 的解移动到另一个不同的点，如图11.2中的 \(P\) ，该点仍然属于 \(S\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.4\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_315_355_1577_597_578_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 11.2 Bargaining sets \(S\) (dark gray) and \(T\) (dark and light gray). Independence of irrelevant alternatives states that if \(S\) is a subset of \(T\) , then \(N\left( T\right)\) is either not in \(S\) , like here, or equal to \(N\left( S\right)\) , but cannot be another point in \(S\) , like \(P\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图11.2 讨价还价集 \(S\) (深灰色)和 \(T\) (深灰色和浅灰色)。无关选项独立性表明，如果 \(S\) 是 \(T\) 的子集，那么 \(N\left( T\right)\) 要么不在 \(S\) 中，就像这里一样，要么等于 \(N\left( S\right)\) ，但不能是 \(S\) 中的另一个点，如 \(P\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

The stated axioms (a)-(h) for a bargaining set and a possible bargaining solution are reasonable requirements that one can defend as general principles.

\begin{tcolorbox}
所陈述的讨价还价集和可能的讨价还价解的公理(a)-(h)是合理的要求，可以作为一般原则来辩护。
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{11.4 The Nash Bargaining Solution}

\begin{tcolorbox}
\section*{11.4 纳什讨价还价解}
\end{tcolorbox}

This section shows that the bargaining axioms (a)-(h) in Definitions 11.1 and 11.2 imply that a bargaining solution exists, which is, moreover, unique.

\begin{tcolorbox}
本节表明，定义11.1和11.2中的讨价还价公理(a)-(h)意味着存在一个讨价还价解，而且这个解是唯一的。
\end{tcolorbox}

Theorem 11.3. Under the Nash bargaining axioms, every bargaining set \(S\) that contains a point(u, v)with \(u > {u}_{0}\) and \(v > {v}_{0}\) has a unique solution \(N\left( S\right)  = \left( {U,V}\right)\) , which is obtained as the unique point(u, v)that maximizes the product (also called the "Nash product") \(\left( {u - {u}_{0}}\right) \left( {v - {v}_{0}}\right)\) for \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
定理11.3. 在纳什讨价还价公理下，每个包含点(u, v)且 \(u > {u}_{0}\) 和 \(v > {v}_{0}\) 的讨价还价集 \(S\) 都有一个唯一的解 \(N\left( S\right)  = \left( {U,V}\right)\) ，该解是使乘积(也称为“纳什乘积”) \(\left( {u - {u}_{0}}\right) \left( {v - {v}_{0}}\right)\) 在 \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) 时达到最大值的唯一的点(u, v)。
\end{tcolorbox}

Proof. First, consider the set \({S}^{\prime } = \left\{  {\left( {u - {u}_{0},v - {v}_{0}}\right)  \mid  \left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S}\right\}\) , which is the set \(S\) translated so that the threat point \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) is the origin(0,0), as shown in Figure 11.3(a) and (b). Maximizing the Nash product then amounts to maximizing \({uv}\) for \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  {S}^{\prime }\) . Let(U, V)be a utility pair(u, v)where this happens, where by assumption \({UV} > 0\) ; Figure 11.3(b) shows the hyperbola \({uv} = c\) for the maximum number \(c\) so that the hyperbola still intersects \({S}^{\prime }\) . Next, we re-scale the utilities so that \(\left( {U,V}\right)  = \left( {1,1}\right)\) , by replacing \(S\) with the set \({S}^{\prime \prime } = \left\{  {\left. \left( {\frac{1}{U}u,\frac{1}{V}v}\right) \right| \;\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  {S}^{\prime }}\right\}\) , shown in Figure 11.3(c). To simplify notation, we rename \({S}^{\prime \prime }\) to \(S\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
证明。首先，考虑集合 \({S}^{\prime } = \left\{  {\left( {u - {u}_{0},v - {v}_{0}}\right)  \mid  \left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S}\right\}\) ，它是集合 \(S\) 经过平移后得到的，使得威胁点 \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) 为原点(0,0)，如图11.3(a)和(b)所示。那么，最大化纳什积就相当于在 \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  {S}^{\prime }\) 的条件下最大化 \({uv}\) 。设(U, V)是出现这种情况时的一个效用对(u, v)，根据假设 \({UV} > 0\) ；图11.3(b)展示了对应最大数值 \(c\) 的双曲线 \({uv} = c\) ，使得该双曲线仍与 \({S}^{\prime }\) 相交。接下来，我们对效用进行重新缩放，使得 \(\left( {U,V}\right)  = \left( {1,1}\right)\) ，方法是用集合 \({S}^{\prime \prime } = \left\{  {\left. \left( {\frac{1}{U}u,\frac{1}{V}v}\right) \right| \;\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  {S}^{\prime }}\right\}\) 替换 \(S\) ，如图11.3(c)所示。为简化符号，我们将 \({S}^{\prime \prime }\) 重命名为 \(S\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

Next, consider the set \(T = \{ \left( {u,v}\right)  \mid  u \geq  0,v \geq  0,u + v \leq  2\}\) , see Figure 11.3(d). For the bargaining set \(T\) , the solution is \(N\left( T\right)  = \left( {1,1}\right)\) , because \(T\) is a symmetric set, and(1,1)is the only symmetric point on the Pareto-frontier of \(T\) . We claim that \(S \subseteq  T\) . Then \(N\left( T\right)  = \left( {1,1}\right)  \in  S\) , so that the independence of irrelevant alternatives implies \(N\left( S\right)  = N\left( T\right)\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
接下来，考虑集合 \(T = \{ \left( {u,v}\right)  \mid  u \geq  0,v \geq  0,u + v \leq  2\}\) ，见图11.3(d)。对于讨价还价集 \(T\) ，解为 \(N\left( T\right)  = \left( {1,1}\right)\) ，因为 \(T\) 是一个对称的集合，并且(1,1)是 \(T\) 的帕累托前沿上唯一的对称点。我们断言 \(S \subseteq  T\) 。那么 \(N\left( T\right)  = \left( {1,1}\right)  \in  S\) ，因此无关选项的独立性意味着 \(N\left( S\right)  = N\left( T\right)\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

We prove \(S \subseteq  T\) using the convexity of \(S\) . Namely, suppose there was a point \(\left( {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right)\) in \(S\) which is not in \(T\) . Because \(\bar{u} \geq  0,\bar{v} \geq  0\) , this means \(\bar{u} + \bar{v} > 2\) . Then, as shown in Figure 11.3(d), even if the Nash product \(\bar{u} \cdot  \bar{v}\) is not larger than 1 (which is the Nash product \({UV}\) ), then the Nash product \({uv}\) of a suitable convex combination \(\left( {u,v}\right)  = \left( {1 - \varepsilon }\right) \left( {U,V}\right)  + \varepsilon \left( {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right)\) for some small positive \(\varepsilon\) is larger than 1 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
我们利用 \(S\) 的凸性来证明 \(S \subseteq  T\) 。具体来说，假设 \(S\) 中存在一个点 \(\left( {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right)\) 不在 \(T\) 中。因为 \(\bar{u} \geq  0,\bar{v} \geq  0\) ，这意味着 \(\bar{u} + \bar{v} > 2\) 。然后，如图11.3(d)所示，即使纳什积 \(\bar{u} \cdot  \bar{v}\) 不大于1(这是纳什积 \({UV}\) )，那么对于某个小的正数 \(\varepsilon\) ，一个合适的凸组合 \(\left( {u,v}\right)  = \left( {1 - \varepsilon }\right) \left( {U,V}\right)  + \varepsilon \left( {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right)\) 的纳什积 \({uv}\) 也会大于1。
\end{tcolorbox}

This is illustrated in Figure 11.3(d): The hyperbola \(\{ \left( {u,v}\right)  \mid  {uv} = 1\}\) touches the set \(T\) at the point(1,1). The tangent to the hyperbola is the line through points (2,0)and(0,2), which is the Pareto-frontier of \(T\) . Because the point \(\left( {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right)\) is to the right of that tangent, the line segment that connects(1,1)and \(\left( {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right)\) intersects the hyperbola and therefore contains points(u, v)to the right of the hyperbola, where \({uv} > 1\) . This is a contradiction, because \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) since \(S\) is convex, but the maximum Nash product of a point in \(S\) is 1 .

\begin{tcolorbox}
这在图11.3(d)中有所说明:双曲线 \(\{ \left( {u,v}\right)  \mid  {uv} = 1\}\) 在点(1,1)处与集合 \(T\) 相切。双曲线的切线是过点(2,0)和(0,2)的直线，该直线是 \(T\) 的帕累托前沿。因为点 \(\left( {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right)\) 在那条切线的右侧，连接(1,1)和 \(\left( {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right)\) 的线段与双曲线相交，因此包含位于双曲线右侧的点(u, v)，其中 \({uv} > 1\) 。这是一个矛盾，因为 \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) ，由于 \(S\) 是凸集，但 \(S\) 中一点的最大纳什积为1。
\end{tcolorbox}

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[max width=0.9\textwidth]{images/bo_d2e5cmbef24c73b03tn0_317_325_206_1260_1124_0.jpg}
\end{center}
\hspace*{3em} 

Figure 11.3 Proof steps for Theorem 11.3. The dashed line in (d) shows that the point \(\left( {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right)\) , which is outside the triangle \(T\) (dark and light gray), cannot belong to \(S\) (dark gray), which shows that \(S \subseteq  T\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
图11.3 定理11.3的证明步骤。(d)中的虚线表明，位于三角形 \(\left( {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right)\) (深灰色和浅灰色)外部的点 \(T\) ，不可能属于 \(S\) (深灰色)，这表明 \(S \subseteq  T\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

To prove this formally, consider the Nash product for the convex combination (u, v)of \(\left( {U,V}\right)  = \left( {1,1}\right)\) and \(\left( {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right)\) :

\begin{tcolorbox}
为了正式证明这一点，考虑 \(\left( {U,V}\right)  = \left( {1,1}\right)\) 和 \(\left( {\bar{u},\bar{v}}\right)\) 的凸组合(u, v)的纳什积:
\end{tcolorbox}

\[
{uv} = \left\lbrack  {\left( {1 - \varepsilon }\right) U + \varepsilon \bar{u}}\right\rbrack  \left\lbrack  {\left( {1 - \varepsilon }\right) V + \varepsilon \bar{v}}\right\rbrack
\]

\[
= \left\lbrack  {U + \varepsilon \left( {\bar{u} - U}\right) }\right\rbrack  \left\lbrack  {V + \varepsilon \left( {\bar{v} - V}\right) }\right\rbrack
\]

\[
= {UV} + \varepsilon \left\lbrack  {\bar{u} + \bar{v} - \left( {U + V}\right)  + \varepsilon \left( {\bar{u} - U}\right) \left( {\bar{v} - V}\right) }\right\rbrack  . \tag{11.1}
\]

Now, because \(\bar{u} + \bar{v} > 2\) , but \(U + V = 2\) , the term \(\bar{u} + \bar{v} - \left( {U + V}\right)\) is positive, and \(\varepsilon \left( {\bar{u} - U}\right) \left( {\bar{v} - V}\right)\) can be made smaller than that term in absolute value, for suitably small \(\varepsilon  > 0\) , even if \(\left( {\bar{u} - U}\right) \left( {\bar{v} - V}\right)\) is any negative number. Consequently, \({uv} > 1 = {UV}\) for sufficiently small positive \(\varepsilon\) , which shows that \({UV}\) was not the maximum Nash product in \(S\) , which contradicts the construction of(U, V). This proves that, indeed, \(S \subseteq  T\) , and hence \(N\left( S\right)  = N\left( T\right)\) as claimed.

\begin{tcolorbox}
现在，因为 \(\bar{u} + \bar{v} > 2\) ，但 \(U + V = 2\) ，项 \(\bar{u} + \bar{v} - \left( {U + V}\right)\) 为正，并且对于足够小的 \(\varepsilon  > 0\) ，即使 \(\left( {\bar{u} - U}\right) \left( {\bar{v} - V}\right)\) 是任意负数， \(\varepsilon \left( {\bar{u} - U}\right) \left( {\bar{v} - V}\right)\) 的绝对值也可以小于该项。因此，对于足够小的正 \(\varepsilon\) ， \({uv} > 1 = {UV}\) ，这表明 \({UV}\) 不是 \(S\) 中的最大纳什积，这与(U, V)的构造相矛盾。这证明了，实际上， \(S \subseteq  T\) ，因此如所声称的 \(N\left( S\right)  = N\left( T\right)\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

In order to show that the point \(\left( {U,V}\right)  = \left( {1,1}\right)\) is unique, we can again look at the hyperbola, and argue similarly as before. Namely, suppose that \(\left( {u,\frac{1}{u}}\right)\) is another point in \(S\) on the hyperbola with \(u \neq  1\) . Because \(S\) is convex, the line segment that connects(1,1)and \(\left( {u,\frac{1}{u}}\right)\) is also in \(S\) , but its interior points have a larger Nash product. For example, the mid-point \(\frac{1}{2} \cdot  \left( {1,1}\right)  + \frac{1}{2} \cdot  \left( {u,\frac{1}{u}}\right)\) has the Nash product \(\left( {1 + u + \frac{1}{u} + u\frac{1}{u}}\right) /4\) , which is larger than 1 if and only \(u + \frac{1}{u} > 2\) , which holds because this is equivalent to \({\left( u - 1\right) }^{2} > 0\) , which holds because \(u \neq  1\) . Hence, again because \(S\) is convex, the Nash product has a unique maximum in \(S\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
为了证明点 \(\left( {U,V}\right)  = \left( {1,1}\right)\) 是唯一的，我们可以再次观察双曲线，并进行与之前类似的论证。即，假设 \(\left( {u,\frac{1}{u}}\right)\) 是双曲线 \(S\) 上的另一点，且 \(u \neq  1\) 。因为 \(S\) 是凸集，连接(1,1)和 \(\left( {u,\frac{1}{u}}\right)\) 的线段也在 \(S\) 中，但其中间点的纳什积更大。例如，中点 \(\frac{1}{2} \cdot  \left( {1,1}\right)  + \frac{1}{2} \cdot  \left( {u,\frac{1}{u}}\right)\) 的纳什积为 \(\left( {1 + u + \frac{1}{u} + u\frac{1}{u}}\right) /4\) ，当且仅当 \(u + \frac{1}{u} > 2\) 时，该值大于1，这成立是因为这等价于 \({\left( u - 1\right) }^{2} > 0\) ，而这成立是因为 \(u \neq  1\) 。因此，再次因为 \(S\) 是凸集， \(S\) 中的纳什积有唯一的最大值。
\end{tcolorbox}

The assumption that \(S\) has a point(u, v)with \(u > {u}_{0}\) and \(v > {v}_{0}\) has the following purpose: Otherwise, for \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) , all utilities \(u\) of player I or \(v\) of player II (or both) fulfill \(u = {u}_{0}\) or \(v = {v}_{0}\) , respectively. Then the Nash product \(\left( {u - {u}_{0}}\right) \left( {v - {v}_{0}}\right)\) for \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) would always be zero, so it has no unique maximum unless \(S\) is just the singleton \(\left\{  \left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right) \right\}\) . Then the bargaining set \(S\) is a singleton or a line segment \(\left\{  {u}_{0}\right\}   \times  \left\lbrack  {{v}_{0},V}\right\rbrack\) or \(\left\lbrack  {{u}_{0},U}\right\rbrack   \times  \left\{  {v}_{0}\right\}\) . In this rather trivial case, the Pareto-frontier consists of a single point(U, V), which defines the unique Nash solution \(N\left( S\right)\) .

\begin{tcolorbox}
假设 \(S\) 有一个点 (u, v)，且满足 \(u > {u}_{0}\) 和 \(v > {v}_{0}\) ，其目的如下:否则，对于 \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) ，玩家 I 的所有效用 \(u\) 或玩家 II 的所有效用 \(v\) (或两者)分别满足 \(u = {u}_{0}\) 或 \(v = {v}_{0}\) 。那么对于 \(\left( {u,v}\right)  \in  S\) ，纳什积 \(\left( {u - {u}_{0}}\right) \left( {v - {v}_{0}}\right)\) 总是为零，所以除非 \(S\) 只是单元素集 \(\left\{  \left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right) \right\}\) ，否则它没有唯一的最大值。那么议价集 \(S\) 是一个单元素集或一条线段 \(\left\{  {u}_{0}\right\}   \times  \left\lbrack  {{v}_{0},V}\right\rbrack\) 或 \(\left\lbrack  {{u}_{0},U}\right\rbrack   \times  \left\{  {v}_{0}\right\}\) 。在这种相当简单的情况下，帕累托前沿由一个单点 (U, V) 组成，该点定义了唯一的纳什解 \(N\left( S\right)\) 。
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\Rightarrow\) If the bargaining set is a line segment as discussed in the preceding paragraph, why is its Pareto-frontier a single point? Is this always so if the line segment is not parallel to one of the coordinate axes?

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\Rightarrow\) 如果议价集是上一段中讨论的线段，为什么它的帕累托前沿是一个单点？如果线段不平行于任何一个坐标轴，情况总是如此吗？
\end{tcolorbox}

It can be shown relatively easily that the maximization of the Nash product as described in Theorem 11.3 gives a solution(U, V)that fulfills the axioms in Definitions 11.1 and 11.2.

\begin{tcolorbox}
可以相对容易地证明，定理 11.3 中描述的纳什积最大化给出了一个满足定义 11.1 和 11.2 中公理的解 (U, V)。
\end{tcolorbox}

\(\Rightarrow\) In Exercise 11.1, you are asked to verify this for axiom (f).

\begin{tcolorbox}
\(\Rightarrow\) 在练习 11.1 中，要求你验证公理 (f) 的情况。
\end{tcolorbox}

We describe how to find the bargaining solution(U, V)for the bargaining set \(S\) in Figure 11.1. The threat point \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) is(1,1). The Pareto-frontier consists of two line segments: a first line segment that joins(1,3.5)to(2,3), and a second line segment that joins(2,3)to(4,1), where the latter is part of a longer line segment that extends from(2,3)to(5,0)(the first line segment is also part of the longer segment that connects(4,0)to(2,3), but this will not be needed). The Nash product \(\left( {u - {u}_{0}}\right) \left( {v - {v}_{0}}\right)\) for a point(u, v)on the Pareto-frontier is the size of the area of the rectangle between the threat point \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)\) and the point(u, v). In this case, the diagram shows that the second line segment creates the larger rectangle in that way. A point on the second line segment is of the form \(\left( {1 - p}\right) \left( {2,3}\right)  + p\left( {5,0}\right)\) for \(0 \leq  p \leq  1\) , that is, \(\left( {2 + {3p},3 - {3p}}\right)\) . Because \(\left( {{u}_{0},{v}_{0}}\right)  = \left( {1,1}\right)\) , the resulting Nash product is \(\left( {1 + {3p}}\right) \left( {2 - {3p}}\right)\) or \(2 + {3p} - 9{p}^{2}\) . The unique maximum over \(p\) of this expression is obtained for \(p = \frac{1}{6}\) because this is the zero of its derivative \(3 - {18p}\) , with negative second derivative, so this is a maximum. The point(U, V) is therefore given as(2.5,2.5). (In general, such a maximum can result for \(p < 0\) or \(p > 1\) , which implies that the left or right endpoint of the line segment with \(p = 0\) or \(p = 1\) , respectively, gives the largest value of the Nash product. Then one has to check the adjoining line segment for a possibly larger Nash product, which may occur at the corner point.) This value of \(p\) allows representing the Nash bargaining solution as a convex combination of payoff pairs that correspond to cells in the bimatrix game, namely via a lottery that chooses(T, l)with payoff pair(2,3)with probability \(1 - p = \frac{5}{6}\) and(B, r)with payoff pair(5,0)with probability \(p = \frac{1}{6}\) . Both players have agreed in advance to accept the outcome of this lottery (even though with probability \(\frac{1}{6}\) the pair(5,0)gives payoff zero to player II, which is less than at the threat point where she gets \({v}_{0} = 1\) ; however, her expected payoff 2.5 for the lottery is higher than \({v}_{0}\) ).

\begin{tcolorbox}
